title: AFK Check
content:
{{Removed}}
<choose uncached>
<option>[[File:AFK Check (Common).png|thumb|The image of a {{Rarity|Common}} AFK Check.|link=]]</option>
<option>[[File:AFK Check (Unusual).png|thumb|The image of an {{Rarity|Unusual}} AFK Check.|link=]]</option>
<option>[[File:AFK Check (Rare).png|thumb|The image of a {{Rarity|Rare}} AFK Check.|link=]]</option>
<option>[[File:AFK Check (Epic).png|thumb|The image of a {{Rarity|Epic}} AFK Check.|link=]]</option>
<option>[[File:AFK Check (Legendary).png|thumb|The image of a {{Rarity|Legendary}} AFK Check.|link=]]</option>
<option>[[File:AFK Check (Mythic).png|thumb|The image of a {{Rarity|Mythic}} AFK Check.|link=]]</option>
<option>[[File:AFK Check (Ultra).png|thumb|The image of an {{Rarity|Ultra}} AFK Check.|link=]]</option>
<option>[[File:AFK Check (Super).png|thumb|The image of a {{Rarity|Super}} AFK Check.|link=]]</option>
</choose>
The '''AFK Check''' mechanic is a popup that shows up randomly in a user's session to make sure the player is active, in which they must drag a circle, colored after one of the 9 different rarities, to the end of a randomly generated path. If the user doesn't do so, they will be kicked out of the game a minute after it shows up. This is to prevent players from leaving the game running while doing other things (going AFK). You have 60 seconds to complete an AFK check and are not required to complete it in one motion.

It will appear in any place of your monitor, in order to prevent players AFKing for too long. This is mainly done to prevent farmers using summons like [[Ant Egg]], [[Beetle Egg]], or [[Mysterious Stick]] to AFK, but can also be used against other cases.

There's a chance that an AFK notice that would appear when there's no movement on the screen for long time, which reads the following:
{{AFK Check}}
This usually appears when you open the game and then got kicked from the game immediately after the notice appears. The box is generated with random font kerning, aliasing and vertical alignment in order to frustrate attempts at auto-targeting clicker scripts.

However unlike them, the button has equal chances for all rarities.[[File:Super AFK check.jpg|thumb|A Super AFK check before the update]][[File:UniqueCkeckButton.jpg|thumb|Another Unique AFK check]]

title: AFK Farming
content:
{{Stub}}
=== What It Is ===
AFK farming is when you stay in a place to obtain huge amounts of low rarity petals, by constantly killing mobs that are easily killable for your level.

=== Places you can farm in ===
Many famous farm places are as followed:
* Ladybug zone
* [[Ant Hell|Ant Hel]] (all zones)
* Ultra mob boxes
* Bee zone
* Jellyfish Zone
* [[East Waters/Crab Kingdom|Crab Kingdom]]

(plz add links for each one that is missed)

=== Petals Used for farming ===
* [[Jelly]]: Farmed from [[jellyfish]], jelly is a invaluable asset in farming, because the principle of farming is that mobs die before reach your flower, damaging you, but jelly makes sure they go back instead of advancing.
* [[Web]]: Web is a necessity in farming, ESPECIALLY desert farm sites like sandstorm zone, webs dramatically slow down enemies so that they will not reach you easily. But web can affect many enemies at once, the other slowing petal, pincer, slows one mob. This is dropped by [[Spider|spiders]], which can be found in abundance at the [[Sewers]].
* [[Pincer]]: Pincer is great as it slows down a mob to almost a stop for a second, it can only affect one mob at a time, but it is still useful when farming sewers, for roaches. This can be taken from [[Scorpion|Scorpions]] found in the [[Desert]].
* [[Lightning]]: This petal's damage chains to multiple mobs, so a person with lots of lightning can contribute to afk farming by zapping every mob. This is dropped by [[jellyfish]] in the [[Ocean]] and can be obtained by [[Assembler|assembling]] [[Battery|batteries]].
* [[Magnet]]: Helps you collect drops, NEEDED if not using magnetism [[Talents/Magnetism|talent]]. Get this from [[Fire Ant Burrow|Fire Ant Burrows]] in the [[desert]].
* Summoning petals (this includes ones like [[Ant Egg|ant egg]], [[Beetle Egg|beetle egg]], [[Mysterious Stick|mysterious stick]], [[Mysterious Stick/Magic|magic stick]], and [[square]] (don't use squares unless you want to flex).: Very important as ants, beetles, and sandstorms all do INSANE dps via body damage when alive, and they (except for sandstorms) target and kill every last mob, they led to very high farm rates. Square is great for... well it has a 60 sec reload time, just about the slowest, low health, doesn't attack mobs-it's only good for bragging.
* [[Yggdrasil]]: When someone fights with you during a farm session, you can now revive them with [[Yggdrasil|ygg]], but the issue is, do you have it?
* [[Soul Splitter|Soul splitter]]: the ability to have 18 petals instead of 10 is a pretty broken one (TBC)

title: Absorbing
content:
'''Absorbing''' is an action that can be performed on [[petals]]. While [[crafting]] can make stronger [[petals]], absorbing will grant experience, allowing the player to [[Level|level up]] which gives [[Talents|Talent Points (TPs)]].

Each rarity of petal gives a certain amount of experience. Absorbed petals will be permanently destroyed and no longer accessible. 

Absorbing was removed in the April 21, 2024 update due to a shift from getting XP from absorbing petals into killing mobs, but it was reintroduced in December 12-13, 2025 with some changes and now have its separate area: [[Crystal Room]]. 

==Overview==
Absorbing can be accessed by going to the [[Crystal Room]], which replaces [[Crafting]] and pressing "C", or clicking the purple button.  

Your XP bar is shown below your selected petal(s), which represents how many XP is required to get a level. There is also a display at the top right of the bar, saying 'Next level +1 TP', which you will get a Talent Point after your XP bar is filled up.

Like [[Crafting]], you can hold Shift on your keyboard to put the every petals of the same type and rarity, but unlike Crafting which restricts you to putting 5 of the same petals of rarity and type, absorbing allows you to put many petals as possible. After you chose every petal you want to absorb, you can click on the 'Absorb' button, which would permanently destroy the selected petal(s) and gain XP on the said rarity.

<gallery>
File:Absorb.png|This is the absorb menu.

File:Screenshot 2025-12-13 142613 (absorb menu).png|The new absorb menu after it was readded with the crystal
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*Absorbing is likely based from deleting petals in the legacy florr.io.
*Absorbing will give you TP (Talent Points), which can be used for [[Talents]].
*When Absorbing was released, the text at the top used to said 'Crafting'.
*Some people who quit the game chose to absorb all of their petals. However, this can be a questionable decision due to the fact that they might come back later. Absorbing is now removed entirely, and mobs give XP when killed.
**Actually, the new XP obtaining mechanic still has something to do with Absorbing. The mob's XP depends on how many petals it drops and how good are there drop rates.
*Absorbing used to be accessible from Crafting by switching it. Unlike the new absorbing, it did not have the crystal charge mechanic.

title: Achievements
content:
{{LongPage}} [[File:Achievements.webp|thumb|200px|Achievements menu.]] '''Achievements''' are tasks given to users that accomplished by performing any feats in the game. They were added in the May 8th, 2024 update. As of February 11th, 2026, there are 133 achievements. ==Interface== People can access Achievements by clicking on the red button at the top of the screen with a rosette icon. Upon performing any specific task, you will complete an achievement. Once clicked on it, you will view the achievements that are available to complete, When you complete any achievement, the achievement would be darkened with a text saying "Done", but when you didn't complete it, there's a button below the rewards named "Track". Upon pressing it, that achievement would go next to the minimap, and will disappear once you complete it. Clicking on it again hides the achievement at the top right. There is a checkbox button named "Track automatically" which will automatically track the achievements that goes from up-to-down. There's a progress bar at the top, which displays how many achievements you completed. There are rarities for achievements, which go from {{Rarity|Common}} to {{Rarity|Unique}}. {{Rarity|Common}} has 2 achievements, {{Rarity|Unusual}} has 16 achievements, {{Rarity|Rare}} has 16 achievements, {{Rarity|Epic}} has 13 achievements, {{Rarity|Legendary}} has 24 achievements, {{Rarity|Mythic}} has 18 achievements, {{Rarity|Ultra}} has 22 achievements, {{Rarity|Super}} has 20 achievements and both {{Rarity|Eternal}} and {{Rarity|Unique}} only have a single achievement each. Some of which achievments correspond with/are related to a mob, accomplishment, or petal of that rarity or previous rarity (e.g. "Conquerer" requires destroying a mob of that rarity, "Unkillable" requires killing 100 mobs of the previous rarity in one life, and "Gear Up" requires equipping a number of petals of that rarity) *On May 12, 2024, [[M28]] added more new additional achievements, which are "Racecar", "Bad in every way", "Touched", and "Collector" (re-added with different requirements to get it). * On May 13-14, 2024, M28 added another few additional achievements. *On May 26, 2024, M28 added trading update where [[Coin]] and [[Trader]] was added to the game, which the achievements related to the update was added as well. *On May 28, 2024. M28 added Gambling and [[Plinko]], which adds 3 new achievements. *On June 7, 2024. M28 added [[Oracle]], it is an NPC that was added in [[Ocean]], which adds 2 new achievements. *On December 5, 2024. M28 added mana and magic that adds one new achievement: "Magician". *On Feburary 9, 2025, M28 added one new achievement: "I'm feeling lucky". *On October 7, 2025, M28 added one new achievement: "Blood Sacrifice". ==Achievements ==

content:
{{Petal Template Infobox
| image = <gallery>

</gallery>
| description = It's literally nothing.
| first-rarity = {{Rarity|Common}}
| extra-radius = {{Infobox Colors Empty|8|16|24|32|40|48|56|64|72}}
| stackability = Unstackable (stackable with [[Soil]])
| dropped-by = *[[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble]]
| special = Increases the flower's size and hitbox.
}}
'''Air''' is a [[petal]] from [http://florr.io florr.io]. Its function consists of increasing the size of your flower. Air is dropped by [[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble mobs]].

==Appearance==
Air consists of thin air, or, as its description makes clear, Air appears to be literally nothing.

==Function==
{|class="article-table"
|+Petal Stats
![[Rarity]]
!Obtainable?
!Extra Radius
|-
|{{Rarity|Common}}
|rowspan="9"|Yes
|8
|-
|{{Rarity|Unusual}}
|16
|-
|{{Rarity|Rare}}
|24
|-
|{{Rarity|Epic}}
|32
|-
|{{Rarity|Legendary}}
|40
|-
|{{Rarity|Mythic}}
|48
|-
|{{Rarity|Ultra}}
|56
|-
|{{Rarity|Super}}
|64
|-
|{{Rarity|Unique}}
|72
|}
At first, Air appears to be literally nothing. However, once equipped, it increases the flower's size (and consequently its hitbox) by a certain amount. Technically, it also increases petal attack radius (due to the increased body pushing out the petals), as well as the distance of petals between each other (as it won't appear in your petal orbit). It doesn't stack, meaning that more Airs won't further increase the size of your flower. However, [[Soil]]'s own flower size buff is able to stack with Air's buff.

==Obtainment==
===[[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble]]===
{{:Bubble (Mob)/Drop Air}}

===Purchasing===
On certain days, Air can be purchased from the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|0.5}}

== Obtaining ==

* It can only be farmed in ocean by defeating [[Bubble|Bubbles]]. Air has a notoriously high drop rate, so farming it is easy. 

==Strategies==
*Air's main use is as a trolling petal, usually working in tandem with a [[Bubble]] build to knock players around.
*Air can also be [[Coin|Coined]] if needed via the [[Trader]] due to it's negligible properties.
*Air can be used to increase your size drastically, allowing you to hit more mobs at once if you are using a ramming build.
*Air can also be used to mitigate the suction effect of {{Rarity|Super}}+ [[Sandstorm]]s during their special attack. It is advised to use {{Rarity|Epic}} Air to save space and to be less vulnerable to [[Scorpion]]'s missiles.

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">
File:Air (Common).png|{{Rarity|Common}} Air.
File:Air (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|Unusual}} Air.
File:Air (Rare).png|{{Rarity|Rare}} Air.

File:Air (Unique).png|{{Rarity|Unique}} Air.
File:Air.png|Air.
File:IMG 2372.webp|Player using air at [[Ant Hell]] portal.
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*Using Air allows you to move "inside" a [[Wax]], instead of pushing it around.
*FREEDOM008 is the first player to forge the {{R|Unique}} Air.
*Air is the subject of one [[Achievement]]: the "Breathe In, Breathe Out" {{Rarity|Epic}} Achievement (which is one of the seven secret Achievements in the game), granted to you once you rapidly equip and unequip an Air of any rarity multiple times.
**This Achievement, Air's "appearance" and Air's description are a reference to real-life air, invisible to the human eye, but extremely necessary for humans (especially due to the presence of O2, the molecular Oxygen, in the atmosphere).
*Air's flower size buff values are all multiples of eight.
*Prior to the April 21st, 2024 update, people jokingly classified Air as the "number one" petal. 
**Although it was an obvious lie and/or only a silly joke, Air did have some pretty niche uses. Air could be used to fill up petal slots to prevent from automatically equipping any petals. However, the niche it fills could still easily be replaceable by [[Soil]], [[Magnet]], using a secondary build and other similar cases. After the update, the niche uses became obsolete due to Air making you larger.
**This joke may also refer that Air is the first petal in alphabetical order.
**"FEAR THE SUPER AIR" was the title of a video by CorruptX, about how he crafted a {{Rarity|Super}} Air, and pretended it was the best petal. This was back when it did nothing and could be absorbed.
*Before the update that removed the [[Absorbing]] mechanic, Air used to do nothing, thus simply "removing" a petal slot. Now, it increases your flower's radius, like [[Soil]]. However, unlike Air, Soil stacks, increases radius by 10 per petal on all rarities, increases the user's HP and decreases the user's speed, while Air increases by a set amount each rarity, is unstackable and doesn't affect the user's HP and speed. Soil is still able to stack with Air.
*Air, [[Ankh]], [[Battery]], [[Beetle Egg]], [[Rose]] and [[Square]] are the only petals that all rarities of a mob that drops the petal can drop with a 100% chance ([[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble mob]] for Air, [[Tomb]] for Ankh, [[Firefly/Magic|Magic Firefly]] for Battery, [[Beetle/Nazar|Nazar Beetle]] for Beetle Egg, [[Shiny Ladybug]] for Rose and [[Square (Mob)|Square mob]] for Square). These petals are also tied for the highest droprate in the game.
*Air is tied with [[Bur]], [[Poo]] and [[Wax]] for the feature with the shortest name in the whole game, with only 3 letters.
*Air and [[Mimic]] are the only petals to be literally nothing in appearance. Air also used to be literally nothing in functions before the April 21st, 2024 update.
*Air is tied with [[Ankh]], [[Bandage]], [[Battery]], [[Compass]], [[Corruption]], [[Dahlia]], [[Fragment]] and [[Rose]] for the highest drop chance of an {{Rarity|Ultra}} petal in the game (64.3%).
**All mentioned petals plus [[Missile/Mecha|Mecha Missile]], [[Missile]] and [[Pollen]] are the only petals to have a higher chance to drop at {{Rarity|Ultra}} rarity (64.3% for said petals, 58.4% for Pollen and 53.8% for Mecha Missile and Missile) than at {{Rarity|Mythic}} rarity (35.7% for said petals minus Compass and Corruption, 41.6% for Pollen and 46.2% for Mecha Missile and Missile) by the {{Rarity|Super}} rarity variation of the mob that drops this ([[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble mob]] for Air, [[Tomb]] for Ankh, [[Beetle/Pharaoh|Pharaoh Beetle]] for Bandage, [[Firefly/Magic|Magic Firefly]] for Battery, [[Termite Overmind]] for Compass, Shiny Ladybug for Dahlia and Rose, [[Barrel]] and [[Spider/Mecha|Mecha Spider]] for Fragment, [[Wasp/Mecha|Mecha Wasp]] for Mecha Missile, [[Hel Wasp]] and [[Wasp]] for Missile and [[Bumble Bee]] for Pollen).
**In Air's case, it's yet again due to the fact it's useless, and the extra radius only makes you more vulnerable.
*Since, once again, Air brings no major benefit to you and it mostly detriments you, the {{Rarity|Legendary}} rarity variant of Air is the cheapest petal available to be sold in the [[Shop]], having a 0.5x [[Stars|star]] price multiplier of a petal's base star price: 10 stars compared to the base 20. However, it used to be even cheaper, being sold at 5 stars instead (0.25x multiplier).
**On April 21st, 2023, the cheapest petal ever sold in the Shop was present: a {{Rarity|Legendary}} Air with a 20% discount, making it cost only 4 stars (since at the time its multiplier was 0.25x).
***First of all, {{Rarity|Legendary}} Petals on 1x multiplier costs 20 Stars - 20 stars.
***Second of all, Air had 0.25x multiplier then - 5 stars.
***Third of all, 20% discount is 1.25x cheaper - <math>20/1.25=16</math> stars.
***Fourth of all, {{Rarity|5}} Air got 1.25x cheaper cost (20% discount) - <math>5/1.25=4</math> stars.
***Result: 4 stars.
*Air can be classified as a wearable petal, because it doesn't rotate around your flower like others, and modifies your flower's appearance, but also can't be classified as one, as it doesn't appear as an accessory for your flower (as it doesn't even appear at all.)
*There was a glitch for a couple minutes where air was stackable. This caused the servers to lag a lot, and it was fixed shortly after.

title: Amulet
content:
{{Petal Template Infobox |image = File:Amulet (Common).png|C File:Amulet (Unusual).png|U File:Amulet (Rare).png|R File:Amulet (Epic).png|E File:Amulet (Legendary).png|L File:Amulet (Mythic).png|M File:Amulet (Ultra).png|Ul File:Amulet (Super).png|S File:Amulet (Unique).png|Uq |description = Converts a percentage of overheal into shields. |first-rarity = {{Rarity|Common}} |health = {{Petal Scale|10}} |overheal-conversion = {{Infobox Colors Empty|+5%|+10%|+15%|+20%|+25%|+30%|+35%|+40%|+45%}} |reload-time = 2.0s |stackability = Stackable |dropped-by = *[[Termite Egg]] |special = Converts the flower's overheal done by healing petals into a Shield.
Indirectly affected by [[Basil]] and Medic. }} '''Amulet''' is a [[petal]] from [https://florr.io florr.io]. Its function consists of converting a certain percentage of overheal from healing petals into Shield. Amulet is dropped by [[Termite Egg]]s. ==Appearance== Amulet consists of an hexagonal shape with a pink color. Inside it, there's a darker pink (magenta) pentagon exactly in the hexagon's center, matching the colors of an Amulet's borders. The shades of pink present in an Amulet are equal to the [[Rose]]'s pink colors. ==Function== {|class="article-table" |+Petal Stats ![[Rarity]] !Obtainable? !Health !Respawn !Overheal rate |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |rowspan="9"|Yes |10 HP |rowspan="9"|2.0 seconds | +5% |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |30 HP | +10% |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |90 HP | +15% |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |270 HP | +20% |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |810 HP | +25% |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |2,430 HP | +30% |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |7,290 HP | +35% |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |21,870 HP | +40% |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} |65,610 HP | +45% |} Amulets convert a percentage of excessive heal into a Shield with a set percentage, instead of completely wasting it. This excessive heal is called by the game overheal, which is simply when your burst healing petal ([[Dahlia]], [[Fangs]] or Rose) heals you beyond your max HP, or when your passive healing petal ([[Leaf]] and [[Yucca]]) keeps healing you beyond your max HP. The percentage of the Shield given by overheal increases as the rarity of Amulet increases. This effect is also stackable, meaning that more Amulets further boost the overheal Shield conversion percentage, while more healing petals further increase overheal Shield at once. This conversion can be indirectly affected by both [[Basil]] and the Medic [[Talents|Talent]], as they increase all healing petals' abilities, giving more overheal Shield conversion to Amulet. ==Obtainment== ===[[Termite Egg]]=== {{:Termite_Egg/Drop_Amulet}} ===Purchasing=== On certain days, Amulet can be purchased from the [[Shop]]. ''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]'' {{Petal Price|3}} == Obtaining == * It can only be obtained by defeating [[Termite Egg|Termite Eggs]]. With Termite Eggs having different spawning mechanics, moving from chamber to chamber is advised to efficiently farm eggs. Staying in one area too long will lower the egg's rarities. ==Strategies== * Amulet can be a secondary method of getting shields if you do not have a high rarity [[Shell]]. Mythic Amulet is recommended for use. ** Using petals such as [[Rose]] and [[Dahlia]] are much faster at charging shields than [[Leaf]] and [[Yucca]]. [[Starfish]] can't trigger Amulet's shielding. [[Fang]] and [[Totem]] are usable, though inconsistent. * If you are entering a harder area, using full heals with Amulet can quickly heal you and double your health in case you are outnumbered. ** Use 2 Amulets for faster shield charge. * Do not use Amulet in a Ramming build as the petal needs to be unbroken for the effect to work. ==Gallery== File:Amulet (Common).png|{{Rarity|Common}} Amulet. File:Amulet (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|Unusual}} Amulet. File:Amulet (Rare).png|{{Rarity|Rare}} Amulet. File:Amulet (Epic).png|{{Rarity|Epic}} Amulet. File:Amulet (Legendary).png|{{Rarity|Legendary}} Amulet. File:Amulet (Mythic).png|{{Rarity|Mythic}} Amulet. File:Amulet (Ultra).png|{{Rarity|Ultra}} Amulet. File:Amulet (Super).png|{{Rarity|Super}} Amulet. File:Amulet (Unique).png|{{Rarity|Unique}} Amulet. File:Amulet.png|Amulet. File:Amulet (Old).png|Amulet's old look, which had a brown color palette instead of a pink one. ==Trivia== *Amulet is the first and (currently) only petal to convert healing (specifically its excess) into a Shield. **Amulet is one of the 2 petals that give you a Shield. The other is [[Shell]]. *Amulet is one of the six petals to rely on other petals to work (in this case, all healing petals). The others are [[Basil]] and [[Dandelion]] (which also rely on all healing petals), [[Clover]] (which relies on [[Dice]]) and [[Lotus]] and [[Privet]] (which rely on poisonous petals, although each have different reasons from each other). *As [[Starfish]] will only heal you when you're below 75% of your HP, thus not fully healing you, Starfish is the only healing petal that can't give an overheal Shield when Amulet is active, although it theoretically could do so. *When Amulet was first released, it used to have a brown color before being changed into pink on January 23, 2024. The brown color palette is reminiscent of an [[Ant Hole]]'s color palette, just like the current color palette (of pink) is just like the Rose. **Amulet's new color palette is likely directed towards newer players, hinting to its main function or at least to what petals ([[Dahlia]] and [[Rose]]) majorly benefit (from) this (although [[Fang]]s, [[Leaf|Leaves]], [[Totem]]s and [[Yucca]]s can also be used with them). *Amulet, Basil and Dandelion are the only petals to alter a healing petal's stats. This and Basil do so positively: Amulet creates a Shield for overheal (rather than completely wasting it) caused by healing petals, while Basil directly buffs them. **Curiously, Basil can indirectly buff Amulet: by buffing the petals that can cause overheal, Basil can give more overheal Shield for Amulet. This is also applicable to the interaction between Amulet and the Medic [[Talents|Talent]]. {{Petals}} [[zh:护身符]] [[Category:Petals]] [[Category:Health-Based]] [[Category:Shield Petals]] [[Category:Termite Hell]] [[Category:Common+]] [[Category:Buffs]] [[Category:Droppable Petals]] [[Category:Stackable Petals]] [[Category:Ant Eggs]]

'''Ankh''' is a [[petal]] from [https://florr.io florr.io]. Its function consists of allowing players to place the petal on the ground. However, once Ankh's HP reaches zero or it disappears by having its slot occupied by another petal, the player teleports to Ankh's place prior to the petal's disappearance. Ankh is dropped by [[Beetle/Mummy|Mummy Beetles]], [[Beetle/Nazar|Nazar Beetles]], [[Beetle/Pharaoh|Pharaoh Beetles]] and [[Tomb]]s.

==Appearance==
Ankh consists of a cross shape, but the upper handle is oval in shape. This appearance makes Ankh identical to the Egyptian symbol for life. The petal also shares the same color palette of the [[Relic]] and the [[Yggdrasil]] petals, bearing a brown color with the edges having a darker shade of brown.

==Function==

Upon a left or a right click of your mouse (going into offensive/defensive modes), Ankh will be dropped on the ground and stay there indefinitely on its own. However, if Ankh is destroyed by either dying to any mob, having its place in the petal loadout changed or unequipping it, the player is teleported to the petal's exact position before it disappeared.

===Purchasing===
On certain days, Ankh can be purchased from the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|3}}

== Obtaining ==

* Ankh is obtained from defeating [[Beetle/Mummy|Mummy Beetles]], [[Beetle/Nazar|Nazar Beetles]], [[Beetle/Pharaoh|Pharaoh Beetles]] and [[Tomb]]s. Mummy Beetles have a timed life after reaching zero health based on the rarity of the beetle, so use plenty of slowing and knockback petals such as [[Web]], [[Pincer]] and [[Jelly]] to prevent them from cornering you.

==Strategies==
*Ankh is used as a sort of "teleport" petal. Placing it drops the petal on the ground. When broken, the player is teleported to the Ankh's position.
**It can be broken in 2 ways, either by it being damage or the petal being swapped out. 
**{{Rarity|Mythic}} Ankh, although weak is the lowest rarity recommended for use.

* Ankh is useful in Hel, primarily in Gambler fights in where players use the [[Talents/Second Chance|Second Chance]] talent to safely back out of the fighting area via breaking their Ankh to regenerate health and wait for Second Chance to be off of cooldown.
* Ankh is also used to help players safely complete the Biologist challenge by placing them near a portal, saving it for when they're finished with an area.
==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">

File:Ankh (Super).png|{{R|8}} Ankh.
File:Ankh (Unique).png|{{R|9}} Ankh.
File:Ankh.png|Ankh.
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*Ankh is one of the many petals to be added unannounced in the changelog.
*Ankh seems to be made from (or has the same mythical properties as) the same materials that [[Relic]] is made from (including its colors), as Ankh's description implies with "an ancient relic" (although that is a pretty vague statement, since "relic" is a highly used noun).
*On May 26th, every player got one {{Rarity|Common}} Ankh, given by the developer himself, [[M28]].
*On its release, many people initially mistook Ankh for another type of [[Yggdrasil]].
*Ankh is one of the only petals to be placed down on the ground, with the others being [[Honey]], [[Pearl]], [[Pollen]], [[Uranium]], and [[Web]].
*Currently, Ankh has the highest respawn timer out of all petals, with 256 seconds at {{Rarity|Common}} rarity.
**The {{Rarity|Common}} rarity variation of Mark used to hold that record (960 seconds, or 16 whole minutes) until its special ability cooldown and reload time were swapped.
**Depending on what factors you're counting, three other petals take its spot: Yggdrasil, whose {{Rarity|Common}} rarity variant has a cooldown of 320 seconds but can't be dropped from anything, thus technically not in the game, and [[Moon Rock]] and [[Square]], which have the longest cooldown of any constant (which means it doesn't change since {{Rarity|Common}} Rarity or the lowest Rarity possible for the Petal) respawn time petal, at 60 seconds.
*Ankh is currently the only petal whose recharge cooldown is halved with each rarity, thus, the only petal whose respawn rates changes consistently.
**This is a fact that used to be shared with Mark, until its special ability cooldown and reload time were swapped.
*[[Beetle/Mummy|Mummy Beetles]] and [[Beetle/Pharaoh|Pharaoh Beetles]] can drop Ankh, while [[Hel Beetle]]s drop Mark. This serves as a parallel of "life and death" or specially since mummies and pharaohs are known to be mummified on death after living a good life, while Hel Beetles imply destruction, hunt, anger. It can also be a parallel to "heaven and hell" giving that mummies and pharaohs are meant to be preserved corpses, while Hel Beetles represent damned souls.
*Ankh used to drop from regular [[Beetle|Beetles]].

title: Ant Egg
content:
{{Outdated}}{{DISPLAYTITLE:Egg}} {{Petal Template Infobox | image = File:Ant Egg (Common).png|C File:Ant Egg (Unusual).png|U File:Ant Egg (Rare).png|R File:Ant Egg (Epic).png|E File:Ant Egg (Legendary).png|L File:Ant Egg (Mythic).png|M File:Ant Egg (Ultra).png|Ul File:Ant Egg (Super).png|S File:Ant Egg (Unique).png|Uq | description = Something interesting might pop out of this. | first-rarity = {{Rarity|Common}} | health = 1 | contents = 4x Soldier Ant | reload-time = 60s | dropped-by = *[[Ant Egg (Mob)|Ant Egg]] *[[Fire Ant Egg]] *[[Queen Ant]] *[[Queen Fire Ant]] *[[Termite Egg]] | special = Spawns four friendly [[Soldier Ant]]s.
Affected by Duplicator and Relic. }} ''Not to be confused with the [[Beetle Egg]], which shares the same shortened in-game name, but spawns one [[Beetle]] instead.'' ''This page is about the Ant Egg petal. For the mob which shares its in-game name, see [[Ant Egg (Mob)]].'' '''Ant Egg''' (in-game: ''Egg'') is a legacy [[Petals|petal]] in ''florr.io''. The petal contains four fragile eggs. Each egg deals no direct damage, but when an egg breaks it immediately spawns an allied [[Soldier Ant]] that automatically attacks hostile entities near the petal’s bearer. Ant Eggs are dropped by [[Ant Egg (Mob)|Ant Egg Mobs]], [[Fire Ant Egg|Fire Ant Eggs]], [[Termite Egg|Termite Eggs]], [[Queen Ant|Queen Ants]], and [[Queen Fire Ant|Queen Fire Ants]]. To avoid confusion, every instance mentioning the petal as a whole will be '''Ant Egg''', while the four separate Eggs that form this petal will be called '''Egg'''. ==Appearance== *A singular Egg petal is shaped like a perfect circle with the same color scheme as the [[Beetle Egg]], much like the Ant Egg mob. *The icon for the Ant Egg petal displays it as all four Eggs occupying one spot like the [[Sand]], but in-game, before the eggs hatch, they squish other petals together like {{Rarity|Unusual}}+ Lights. *The Soldier Ants spawned from this appear the same as regular Soldier Ants, but their body (including its borders) is yellow-colored rather than gray, indicating it's a friendly mob. The yellow color is close to the flowers' yellow colors to represent this. ==Function== {| class="article-table" |+Petal Stats ![[Rarity]] !Obtainable? !Health !Respawn !Contents !Mob Health !Mob Damage |- |{{Rarity|Common}} | rowspan="9" |Yes | rowspan="9" |1 | rowspan="9" |60 seconds | rowspan="9" |4x Soldier Ant |250 |6 |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |750 |18 |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |2,250 |54 |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |6,750 |162 |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |20,250 |486 |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |60,750 |1,458 |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |182,250 |4,374 |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |546,750 |13,122 |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} |1,640,250 |39,366 |} The Ant Egg contains 4 separate Eggs. These Eggs decrease the distance between petals until they break. Each Egg spawns 1 Soldier Ant, for a total of 4 Soldier Ants for one Ant Egg. Eggs present in the petal (and therefore Soldier Ants hatched) can be increased by one with the Duplicator [[Talents|Talent]]. The Soldier Ants spawned from this will wander around the flower if there's no entities nearby, but will follow the flower if they go too far away from them. If they spot hostile entities, they will prioritize aggressive mobs over neutral mobs, then neutral mobs over passive mobs, then passive mobs over flowers, and finally other flowers. This petal has a secret interaction with the [[Relic]] petal in which the mobs hatched during a Relic's usage will be [[Soldier Termite]]s instead. ==Obtaining== ===[[Ant Egg (Mob)|Ant Egg]]=== {{:Ant Egg (Mob)/Drop Egg}} ===[[Fire Ant Egg]]=== {{:Fire Ant Egg/Drop Egg}} ===[[Queen Ant]]=== {{:Queen Ant/Drop Egg}} ===[[Queen Fire Ant]]=== {{:Queen Fire Ant/Drop Egg}} ===[[Termite Egg]]=== {{:Termite Egg/Drop Ant Egg}} ===Purchasing=== On certain days, Ant Egg can be purchased in the [[Shop]]. ''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]'' {{Petal Price|2}} == Obtaining == * Ant Eggs are obtained after defeating Ant Queens and Eggs. ** In Normal Ant Hell, Eggs can spawn against the walls with their rarity scaling with the area they're in. Queen Ants infrequently spawn. ** In Fire Ant Hell, Eggs tend to spawn in the center of each chamber, scaling with the area they're in. Fire Queen Ants also spawn infrequently here. ** In Termite Hell, their spawn rates and rarities are determined on how long the area has been occupied by players. The longer players are in an area, the less often and lower rarity they spawn. Move frequently in order to farm more effectively. ==Strategy== *Generally, you can circle around your target, dodging its attacks while you wait for the Ant Eggs to spawn and take care of the enemy quickly. *{{Rarity|Super}} Ant Egg has very high yet inconsistent DPS, even above {{Rarity|Super}} Iris on some {{Rarity|super}} Mobs. This makes some players load it on their way to the {{Rarity|Super}} Mob and only hatch it once into the target, dealing millions of damage. *Soldier Ants hatched from an Ant Egg of a certain rarity will take away mobs of the same rarity, though they'll die together in the process. *Their high damage output can further be boosted with [[Bur]], while you can try to reduce respawn time with the Reload Talent Tree. This makes Ant Egg's damage output and DPS vastly superior to Beetle Egg's on mobs that apply (all except Leafbug and Shell). *As a secret mechanic, Relic actually changes this petal: Ant Eggs will spawn friendly Soldier Termites instead. This mechanic was responsible for the Termites' HP nerf, as players would bubble away from their Soldier Termites and let them damage a mob, dealing dozens of millions of damage. [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8ympOsYVD8Y Video]. *Using batteries and relic with Epic Ant Eggs, you can deal high lightning damage since Ant Eggs and Relic deal damage to you. {{Zone|Garden}} *Ant Egg is particularly useful in Garden. You can use this to distract [[Hornet]]s and block its powerful missiles, or to defeat [[Bee]]s without engaging in close combat and risking ram damage. In the Hornets' case, you should try using other high damaging petals together with Eggs, as the "pets" will die very quickly from them while do little damage due to the Hornets' high body damage and moderate HP, as well as the fired Missiles. *The Soldier Ants will also be good at distracting a [[Spider]] so you don't risk dying to them. Use this to your advantage if you're going to use other attacking petals with you. {{Zone|Desert}} *The Ant Egg is useful for blocking any poisonous missiles [[Scorpion]]s may shoot. They also kill Beetles rather quickly. **Try to use it against {{Rarity|Legendary}}+ Scorpions, as they have unlimited ammunition but can easily be blocked by the Soldier Ants. Just remember that they might be killed in the process. * Soldier Ants are great at distracting the aforementioned mob as well as the [[Centipede (Desert)|Desert Centipede]], but also can take [[Soldier Fire Ant]]s spawned from a [[Fire Ant Burrow]] with relative ease. Soldier Fire Ants can take two friendly Soldier Ants of the same rarity due to their double damage, so take note of that. *[[Sandstorm]]s can also be dealt with ease when using Ant Eggs, as you can avoid risking ramming them (due to their unpredictable movements) to kill them to get [[Glass]]/[[Sand]]. However, be careful, as {{Rarity|Mythic}}+ Sandstorms have big hitboxes. {{Zone|Ocean}} * The spawned Soldier Ants can attack and distract [[Crab]]s and [[Leech]]es from far away, thus avoiding dying quickly. Avoid using against [[Shell (Mob)|Shells]], since their high armor will nullify damage from ants of a rarity equal or below to theirs. * It can be suggested against [[Jellyfish|Jellyfishes]], as the amount of Soldier Ants will prevent the lightning attacks from reaching you as long as you stay a safe distance away from the Soldier Ants in contact with the Jellyfish. {{Zone|Ant Hell}} *Ant Hell is a good place for Ant Eggs. One Soldier Ant can take another Soldier Ant of the same rarity, while it only takes around two to kill a Soldier Fire Ant, then, they can severely cripple all Termites, and finally, they're decently good against all bosses there, whether it's [[Queen Ant]], [[Queen Fire Ant|her fire version]] or [[Termite Overmind]]. ==Gallery== File:Ant Egg (Common).png|{{R|1}} Ant Egg. File:Ant Egg (Unusual).png|{{R|2}} Ant Egg. File:Ant Egg (Rare).png|{{R|3}} Ant Egg. File:Ant Egg (Epic).png|{{R|4}} Ant Egg. File:Ant Egg (Legendary).png|{{R|5}} Ant Egg. File:Ant Egg (Mythic).png|{{R|6}} Ant Egg. File:Ant Egg (Ultra).png|{{R|7}} Ant Egg. File:Ant Egg (Super).png|{{R|8}} Ant Egg. File:Ant Egg (Unique).png|{{R|9}} Ant Egg. File:Ant Egg.png|Ant Egg. File:Ant Egg with Duplicator.png|Ant Egg when the Duplicator [[Talents|Talent]] is active. ==Trivia == *The name '''Ant Egg''' wasn't actually used in the game until around 2024, despite the petal being (re)added in 2023. *On 06/02/2025, Ant Egg mechanic were changed. Now Ant Egg only spawns friendly Soldier Ants upon collision with a mob. *Many people think this update was an egg nerf. *The Ant Egg, the Beetle Egg, the [[Mysterious Stick]], the [[Mysterious Stick/Magic|Magic Stick]], the [[Pharaoh's Crown]], and the [[Square]] are the only petals who spawn friendly mobs. Curiously, all of those petals spawn friendly variations of the mobs that drops said petals, except the Ant Egg itself (though [[Soldier Ant]]s can be hatched from an Ant Egg created by a [[Queen Ant]]). *Since mobs cannot go into walls unless there happens to be a glitch, the petals of the Ant Egg, the Beetle Egg and the Stick won't go past the wall. The same is true for the [[Carrot]] (for a different reason). * This was the first petal to have a hidden feature: having [[Relic]] in another slot will transform the Soldier Ants into Soldier Termites. *This was the first petal to spawn a friendly mob, with the others being the Beetle Egg, the Stick, the Square and lastly Magic Stick. *This petal was inspired from legacy florr: there, it was a petal simply called the [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Egg Egg] which was a singular Egg that hatched into a friendly Soldier Ant. It was dropped at {{Rarity|Epic}} rarity by Ant Holes (whereas it can't drop from there now) and Queen Ants pretty rarely. It was also shaped like the Beetle Egg nowadays. ** The Beetle Egg itself also followed this, maintaining the shape and singular Egg from the [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Egg_(Legendary) {{Rarity|Legendary}} Egg], except it dropped from an old, huge version of the Beetle called the [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Massive_Beetle Massive Beetle]. ***Both of those spawned friendly and normal-colored variations of their respective mobs as opposed to yellow-colored variants of this. **This is one of the many petals that are dropped by a mob of the same name. The others are: [[Bubble]], [[Cactus]], [[Dandelion]], [[Jelly]] (partially), [[Rock]], [[Shell]], [[Sponge]], [[Square]] and [[Starfish]]. In this, it's the [[Ant Egg (Mob)|Ant Egg Mob]]. ***However, the mob variations of this, Bubble and Dandelion only came with the new florr.io (Dungeons/Maze map in the case of Dandelion), with the Ant Egg mob taking even longer to be in the game until Ant Hell 2 was released. *The Ant Egg-Relic interaction, the Clover-Dice interaction and the [[Compass]]-[[Magnet]] interaction are the only interactions in the entire game to happen between only two petals. All other interactions are more generalized (for example, Basil and healing petals). *Ant Egg now spawns Ants of the same rarity as the petal, but the stats is completely different from original health. *Starting at {{Rarity|Rare}} rarity, all Soldier Ants hatched from Ant Eggs will be one rarity lower than the petal. This is shared with the Beetle Egg starting at {{Rarity|Legendary}} rarity. **Surprisingly, Ant Egg and Beetle Egg have interesting stats: the Ant Egg has a cooldown of 30 seconds at {{Rarity|Common}} rarity and spawns {{Rarity|Common}} Soldier Ants, the {{Rarity|Unusual}} rarity has a cooldown of 38 seconds and spawns {{Rarity|Unusual}} Ants and the Rare rarity has a cooldown of 2 seconds and spawns {{Rarity|Unusual}} Ants. This means that its cooldown rises, then falls to 2 seconds and then begins raising again to up to 84.1 seconds. Beetle Egg also shares this, as its cooldown timers go from 15, to 19.2, to 23.5, then to 32.0 at {{Rarity|Epic}} rarity and then 1.8 seconds at {{Rarity|Legendary}} rarity, then increasing again as normal, spawning {{Rarity|Common}}, {{Rarity|Unusual}}, {{Rarity|Rare}}, {{R|4}} and {{R|4}} [[Beetle]]s. They are the only two petals to have this weird oscillation in the respawn timer, as not even the Stick or the Square have this. ** At {{Rarity|Unique}} Rarity, Ant Egg still spawns {{Rarity|Ultra}} Soldier Ants. {{Petals}} [[zh:蚁卵]] [[Category:Petals]] [[Category:Summoner Petals]][[Category:Summon]] [[Category:Buffs]] [[Category:Common+]] [[Category:Ant Hell]] [[Category:Fire Ant Hell]] [[Category:Termite Hell]] [[Category:Ant Hole]] [[Category:Garden]] [[Category:Eggs]] [[Category:Droppable Petals]] [[Category:Stackable Petals]] [[Category:Quick-Respawn]] [[Category:Queen Ants]] [[Category:Ant Eggs]] [[Category:Affected by Duplicator Talent]] [[Category:Luring Petals]]

''Not to be confused with the petal, [[Ant Egg]].''

''For other uses, see [[Ant (Disambiguation)]] or [[Egg (Disambiguation)]].''

The '''Ant Egg''' is a mob in florr.io. It hatches an aggressive [[Baby Ant]] or [[Soldier Ant]] depending on how it was spawned. Ant Eggs spawned naturally will sometimes spawn Baby Ants, whereas ones created by [[Queen Ant]]s will always hatch Soldier Ants.

==Appearance==
Looks exactly like the [[Ant Egg|petal version]], but bigger and single.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Stationary.

'''Behavior:''' Passive by itself, but has a small chance to spawn an aggressive [[Baby Ant]] or [[Soldier Ant]] when killed.

When hatched from a [[Queen Ant]], it spawns a Soldier Ant regardless of being damaged or not. Breaking an Ant Egg from a Queen Ant will hatch it instantly and very likely have the Soldier Ant aggro on the player who broke the Ant Egg.

When broken from an Ant Egg cluster, it has a small chance to spawn an aggressive Baby Ant with the same rarity as the Ant Egg.

==Spawning==
The Ant Egg only spawns in the [[Ant Hell]] zone, either naturally or by Queen Ants.

==Drops==
===[[Egg (Ant)]]===
{{:Ant Egg (Mob)/Drop Egg}}

==Strategy==
*When created naturally, this is a great source of Ant Eggs. They are not really hard to kill. The best place to do this is at the area just before the Fire Ant Hell passage, where {{R|5}} Eggs spawn frequently. This is also somewhat a source of the petals the Baby Ant drops, however, it's RNG-based, and the Baby Ant is aggressive, which means it could put you in potential danger.
*When created from Queen Ants, not only does she herself pose natural threat, the Soldier Ants she constantly spawns cause huge trouble as well, but are a great source of [[Glass]] and [[Clover]] whenever you need them. Still, maintaining distance is key to not get aggros.
*[[Bone|Bones]] of the same rarity can kill Ant Eggs without losing health. (Example: {{Rarity|Mythic}} Bone can kill a {{Rarity|Mythic}} Ant Egg.)

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185">
File:Common egg.png|{{Rarity|Common}} {{PAGENAME}}.

File:Ant Egg (Mob).png|{{PAGENAME}}.
File:Superegggg.jpeg|A {{R|8}} Ant Egg Has Just Spawned!
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*It is one of three hatching mobs and a mob that works like a petal, the others are [[Fire Ant Egg|Fire Ant Egg]] and [[Termite Egg]]. All of these drop the normal [[Ant Egg]].
*It is the most effective way to get Ant Eggs.
*Breaking Ant Eggs created by Queen Ants would not drop the Ant Egg petal, but will give you the gallery for the mob.
*Ant Egg, Fire Ant Egg, Termite Egg and [[Tomb]] have the lowest body damage out of any mob.
*Before the Ant Hell rework, high rarity Ant Eggs were incredibly rare. It was estimated that one {{R|8}} Ant Egg would spawn every 3 years.
**This accidentally made the {{R|8}} Ant Egg one of the rarest mobs in the game. However, this was corrected with the new Ant Hell, turning the rate to one every 3-4 decades.

'''Ant Hell''' is an area in florr.io. Its presence in the game indicates that every variant of Ants in the game spawn here.

==Location==
Ant Hell is in the left region of the world map. It is not a spawn point, but it is 1 zone away from the [[Garden]], [[Desert]] and [[Jungle]] spawns, and 2 zones away from the [[Ocean]] spawn. 

===Connections===
This area connects to 3 areas.
{| class="article-table"
!'''Area'''
!'''Connection Location'''
|-
|Garden
|Center
|-
|Desert
|Bottom Left
|-
|Jungle
|Bottom Right
|}

===Special Zones===
*Ants spawn in the top left region of the map.
*Fire ants spawn in the bottom left region of the map.
*Termites spawn in the entire right region of the map.

== Mobs ==
A total of 16 mobs spawn in this area.                                                 

'''[[Baby Ant]]''': Passive mob that has fairly low health and wanders around randomly. Drops [[Light]], [[Leaf]], and [[Rice]].

'''[[Worker Ant]]''': Neutral mob that wanders around until it gets damaged, then it attacks the attacker. Drops [[Leaf]] and [[Corn]].

'''[[Soldier Ant]]''': Aggressive mob that chases players if they get close enough. Drops [[Glass]] and [[Clover]].

'''[[Queen Ant]]''': Aggressive mob that has above-average health and spawns Ant Eggs that spawn Soldier Ants 1 rarity below its rarity (exception of {{RarityTest|Common}} Queen Ants and {{RarityTest|Unique}} Queen Ants). Chases players if they get close to it. Spawnable in all rarities in this zone. Drops [[Ant Egg]], and [[Basil]].

[[Ant Egg (Mob)|'''Ant Egg''']]: Static mob that spawns in the middle of each "bulb" in the top left region of the area (except for the top left bulb) and have a 30% chance to spawn an aggressive baby ant the same rarity as the egg upon destruction. Drops [[Ant Egg]].

'''[[Baby Fire Ant]]''': Passive mob that has fairly low health and wanders around randomly, but has double the body damage of its normal variant. Drops [[Light]], [[Broccoli]], and [[Yucca]].

'''[[Worker Fire Ant]]''': Neutral mob that wanders around until it gets damaged, then attacks the attacker. It has double the body damage of its normal variant. Drops [[Yucca]] and [[Corn]].

'''[[Soldier Fire Ant]]''': Aggressive mob that chases players if they get close enough. It has double the body damage of its normal variant. Drops [[Yucca]] and [[Triangle]]. Once dropped bone.

'''[[Queen Fire Ant]]''': Aggressive mob that has above-average health and spawns Ant Eggs that spawn Soldier Fire Ants 1 rarity below its rarity. Chases players if they get close to it. Spawnable in all rarities in this zone. It has double the body damage of its normal variant. Drops [[Ant Egg]] and [[Basil]].

'''[[Fire Ant Egg]]''': Static mob that only spawns in special zones in the bottom-left region of the area. It has a 30% chance to spawn an aggressive Baby Fire ant the same rarity as the egg upon destruction. Despite being the Fire Ant variant, it does not have doubled body damage compared to its normal variant. Drops [[Ant Egg]].

'''[[Baby Termite]]''': Passive mob that has fairly low health and wanders around randomly. It has Psyonic Connection (if it takes damage, it will be spread it between all the other Termites in a certain radius). Drops [[Relic]] and [[Carrot]].

'''[[Worker Termite]]''': Neutral mob that wanders around until it gets damaged, then attacks the attacker. It has a Psyonic Connection. Drops [[Relic]] and [[Plank]].

'''[[Soldier Termite]]''': Aggressive mob that chases players if they get close enough. It has a Psyonic Connection. Drops [[Relic]] and [[Bone]]. Once also dropped triangle.

'''[[Termite Overmind]]''': Aggressive mob that will chase you very slowly if you get near it. Unlike Queen Ants and Queen Fire Ants, it does not spawn any sort of eggs at all. Spawnable in all rarities in this zone. It has the highest health of all mobs, while also having a Psyonic Connection. Drops [[Relic]], [[Tomato]], and [[Compass]].

'''[[Termite Egg]]''': Static mob that only spawns in bulbs in middle-right region of the area. It has a 30% chance of spawning a Baby Termite upon destruction. Despite being a Termite, it does not have a Psyonic Connection. Nearby Termites will chase you if you damage it. Drops [[Ant Egg]] and [[Amulet]]. 

'''[[Worm]]:''' Aggressive mob that burrows undergorund, following anything it detects, then pops up from underneath its prey to attack. At Super, it also inflicts poison and swallows the flower to the Worm area. Drops [[Shovel]] and [[Soil]]. 

==Strategy==
See ''[[Builds - Ant Hell]]''
*Getting to the top-left of Ant Hell is not really hard, as [[Soldier Ant|Soldier Ants]] are not really fast and strong. You only need to use [[Bubble|Bubbles]] to get there quickly.
**Now there's {{Rarity|Ultra}} spam and even the lowest of 3 chambers has far larger {{Rarity|Ultra}} spawnrate than the past. 
**{{R|Ultra}} mobs now have 6x the health they used to as well, making surviving the hardest zones nearly impossible. 
**It is now easier to go to the {{R|Mythic}}-{{R|Ultra}} zone because of two shortcuts.
*So far, [[Glass]] and [[Leaf]] are the best early-game petals, which means farming regular Ants is vital for improvement. [[Ant Egg]]s can also be dropped there frequently, so try that as well.
*This mean that the [[Mob Diversity]] is low, so it is good for farming.

==Gallery==
<gallery>
File:Ant Hell-.png|Old Ant Hell, changed by January 1st, 2025.
File:Ant Hell look.png|Lucky {{Rarity|Ultra}} [[Clover]] drop.
File:A player in Ant Hell.png|[[Flower|Flowers]] in Normal Ant Hell
File:Pehiley 2025-07-02 at 19.12.44.png|Pehiley in chat after using {{Rarity|Unique}} [[Corruption]] to troll [[Flower|Flowers]] farming there in {{Rarity|Mythic}} Fire Ant Hell
File:IMG 2441.webp|Manfred in AH
</gallery>

==Tiles==
<gallery>
File:dirt2_c_0.svg
File:dirt2_c_1.svg
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*In the old, combined Ant Hell, if one ant type met the another type, both of them would attack and deal damage. A major example of this is when a {{Rarity|Mythic}} Soldier Fire Ant wandered into the Normal area and came face-to-face with a {{Rarity|Mythic}} normal Soldier Ant. This was no longer true after the January 1, 2025 update. Back when this was true, it was common to lure {{R|Ultra}} [[Queen Fire Ant|Queen Fire Ants]] into {{R|Ultra}} [[Termite Overmind|Termite Overminds]].
*This area used to be 3 separate zones: one for [[Ant Hell 2|Normal Ants]], one for [[Ant Hell 1|Fire Ants]], and one for [[Ant Hell 3|Termites]], but on April 12, 2024, they were merged into one Ant Hell.
*Termites used to have double the health of their standard Ant counterparts, but this got changed in October 22, 2024. Now, they just have the exact same stats.
*This is the zone that has the ability to spawn the most types of different mobs (16 total) out of any other zone. The second one is [[Garden]] with 14 different mobs.
*The only mobs that aren't actual Ants in this zone are Termites, and they're supposed to be Ant counterparts anyway. The Worm is also a mob that isn't an Ant.
**In real life, termites are actually related more to [[Roach]]es and [[Mantis]]es.
*This is one of four areas that you cannot spawn directly in, the others being [[Sewers]], [[Factory]] and [[Pyramid]].
*On January 1, 2025 update, the map was reworked and changed to a bigger area.
*Before, soldier fire ants dropped triangle and soldier termites dropped bone. It later got swapped. Now, [[Soldier Fire Ant]]'s Bone and [[Soldier Termite]]'s Triangle drops got swapped again to the original.
*On December 16, 2025, shortcuts were added. They use to be normal pathways but became tunnels in the next day.

'''Ant Hell 1''' was an area in florr.io. It was the only zone that spawns only fire ant variants. Players may still refer to this area as A.H.1 or Fire Ant Hell.

This area is copied into the Maze update on 12 April 2024 in the southwest corner of the Ant Hell merge area. The connections to Desert and the other parts of Ant Hell still remain. 

==Appearance==
It looked like an [[Ant Hole]], but much bigger and with lesser bulbs. It had a special [[portal]]. The ground and walls were dirt texture.

==Location==
{{PAGENAME}} was located in the southern region of the [[World Map]]. The zone was 4 zones from [[Centralia]], the old standard spawn location. Assuming no interruptions, it took 4 and a ¼ minutes when using the [[South Desert/Southwest]] route, without using [[Bubble|Bubbles]] or [[Powder]].

===Connections===
The area connects to 3 areas.
{| class="article-table"
|+Connections

!Connection Location
|-

|Top Left
|-
|Ant Hell 2
|Top Center
|-
|Ant Hell 3
|Top Right
|-
|}
==Spawning==
The average rarity in {{PAGENAME}} was between {{Rarity|Rare}} and {{Rarity|Mythic}}. The further southeast you go, the rarer the mobs were. Mobs near the portal were usually {{Rarity|Rare}} to {{Rarity|Legendary}}. The bottom right corner was almost entirely {{Rarity|Mythic}} mobs. This region was also where {{Rarity|Ultra}} mobs were most likely to spawn.

A total of 5 mobs spawned in {{PAGENAME}}.

|Only in special zones
|-
|}

===Special Zones===
There were five [[Fire Ant Egg]] special zones, or nurseries. There was a [[Worker Fire Ant]] special zone near the bottom of the map. In the bottom right corner of the map, only [[Soldier Fire Ant]]s and [[Queen Fire Ant]]s spawned. Soldier Fire Ants used to spawn at an increased rate in the chamber directly above.

===Ultra/Super Mobs===
Queen Fire Ants spawned in as {{Rarity|Ultra}} and {{Rarity|8}} in the bottom right corner of Ant Hell 1. All of the mobs in Ant Hell were elegible to spawn as {{Rarity|7}}/{{Rarity|8}}.
[[File:Probably Inaccurate Special Zones.png|thumb|The special zones and their standard rarities.]]

==Strategy (outdated)==
*This zone is a great place to farm for [[Yucca|Yuccas]] and [[Privet Berry|Privet Berries]], since Soldier Fire Ants drop them at a high rate.
*Be careful of running into large swarms of Soldier Fire Ant or aggressive [[Worker Fire Ant]]s that are {{Rarity|Legendary}}+, as in swarms, they can easily overwhelm and kill a flower.
*When traveling through, try to go in a group, as it is much safer together. 
*This area can be useful for farming [[Ant Eggs]], as little people travel here for them, leaving many for you. However, it is not advised to go AFK, as the bulbs in which the eggs spawn are quite spaced apart, needing {{Rarity|Ultra}}+ [[Magnet]] in order to be able to pick them up from all around.
*Recently, toxic players and trolls have entered this area. They like to use [[Jelly]] and [[Heavy]] to throw mobs into other players, so be prepared to use Jelly and Heavy too if you encounter trolls.

==Gallery==
<gallery>
File:FireAntEggZoneEx.png|One of the Fire Ant Egg special zones.
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*This was one of the 7 areas with only one connection out of a total of 24 areas (2023-01-23).
*This was one of the most complex areas. There were 2 other complex areas which are Ant Hell and East Waters East.
*This was the first Ant Hell - as in it inspired the [[Ant Hell (Ant)|other two]] [[Ant Hell (Termite)|Ant Hells]], as well as the merged [[Ant Hell]].
*This still exists, as the bottom left region of the merged Ant Hell.

'''Ant Hell 2''' was an area in florr.io. It looked like [[Ant Hell 1]], except it spawns normal ants instead of ants from the Fire Ant branch.

==Appearance==
The walls and background were exactly like Ant Hell 1.

==Location ==
Ant Hell 2 was located in the South-West region of the world map. It was located 2-4 zones from [[Centralia]], the old standard spawn location. Assuming no interruptions, it took around 2 minutes and a half to reach this zone, without using [[Bubble|Bubbles]] or [[Powder]].

===Connections===

==Spawning==
The average rarity in Ant Hell 2 was {{Rarity|Epic}}. Mobs typically used to spawn from {{Rarity|Common}}  to {{Rarity|Legendary}}. The further from the entrance in Centralia Fields, the rarer the mobs would get.

===Ultra/Super Mobs===
All types of mobs that spawned in Ant Hell 2 would then spawn {{Rarity|Ultra}}/{{Rarity|Super}}.

==Strategy==
*'''For beginners:''' Don't try to go for the top right bulbs straight away. There are Legendary+ [[Soldier Ant]]s that will kill you. You should probably follow pros who will protect you to the center of the top right. From there, you can farm Legendary [[Ant Egg|Eggs]] and get a full loadout of Eggs. If you can, find a squad with [[Yggdrasil]] to respawn you if you die and farm in the top middle. 
*'''For intermediate players:'''  As you get more comfortable with defeating Rare and Epic anAnts, head to the top right or bottom left to farm Legendaries. Legendary [[Baby Ant]]s are the easiest to kill and [[Worker Ant]]s won't attack unless provoked, but Soldier Ants attack if they see you. Take advantage of any AFK players with a large hoard to lure the ants attacking you to die easily.  
*'''For experienced players:''' Assuming you have all the Legendary Eggs you need, you can probably safely farm in the bottom left or the top middle, or even the top left bulb. You can farm with [[Clover]]s to try to get even more Lgendary Eggs, and then craft those into Mythics. The best way to farm is with Eggs of your own, but they can be killed by other people's Sggs. In this case, use [[Wing]]s instead to get egg drops from the loot share mechanic.
*'''For long-time players:''' You are probably bored with the easier bulbs, and mostly farm in the top left or top middle. You can farm efficiently in the top middle with Ultra Eggs, and most likely no one will contest you with their own Eggs. Going to get loot share from the Ultra [[Queen Ant]] that spawns in the top left is also a good way to get very rare drops. 

==Trivia==
*It was one of the 3 areas to have the name [[Ant Hell]].
*The area was removed on [[Changelog#April_12th_2024|April 12, 2024 update]] and replaced with the current [[Ant Hell]] area at the top-left part of Ant Hell. It still spawns normal Ants as usual.
*This area was once famous due to its high Wing rate.
*Currently, the Fire Ant section of Ant Hell looks like AH2.

It is one of the only zone that spawns only one specific branch of mobs, Termites (Ants with higher health than usual and are connected through Psyonic Connection). ==Appearance== It had a special [[portal]] in [[Ant Hell (Ant)|Ant Hell 2]]. The ground and walls were dirt texture, just like [[Ant Hell 1]]. ==Location== Ant Hell 3 was located next to [[Ant Hell (Ant)|Ant Hell 2]] and [[Ant Hell|Ant Hell 1]]. The zone was 4-5 zones from [[Centralia]], the old standard spawn location. Assuming no interruptions, it took (unconfirmed) minutes, without using [[Bubble|Bubbles]] or [[Powder]]. It was closest to the Ellne spawnpoint. ===Connections=== The area connected to 3 areas. {| class="article-table" |+Connections !Area !Connection Location |- |Ant Hell 2 |Top-left |- |Ant Hell 1 |Bottom-left |- |Ellne Jungle 1 (Southeast) |Bottom-right |} ==Spawning== The average rarity in Ant Hell 3 used to be {{Rarity|Epic}}. Mobs typically spawned from {{Rarity|Rare}}to Mythic. The further toward East you went, the rarer the mobs got. Mobs near the portal were usually {{Rarity|Legendary}}, but could sometimes spawn as {{Rarity|Epic}}or lower. The top right corner was almost entirely {{Rarity|Mythic}} mobs. This region is also where {{Rarity|Ultra}} mobs were most likely to spawn. {| class="article-table" |+Mobs !Mob !Spawn Rate |- |Termite Overmind |Very Low |- |Termite Egg |High |- |Baby Termite |Medium |- |Soldier Termite |High |- |Worker Termite |High |} [[File:Screenshot 2023-02-28 7.58.09 PM.png|thumb|The special zones and their standard rarities.]] ===Special Zones=== There were no special zones. ===Ultra/Super Mobs=== All types of mobs that spawned in Ant Hell 3 got to spawn {{Rarity|Ultra}}/{{Rarity|Super}}. ==Strategy (outdated)== *To wipe out an entire area full of Mythic mobs, use at least 2 or 3 Ul+ Bones and you're good to go! Remember to be careful to not be near a Termite Overmind. ==Gallery== ==Trivia== *This used to be the third area to share the name "Ant Hell". *It was most of the times hard to get to because of the {{Rarity|Legendary}}+ [[Soldier Termite]]s meaning that you needed to have a good [[Builds - General|Build]] if you really wanted to survive in this area. *In real life, Termites are more related to [[Roach]]es and [[Mantis]] than Ants. *This area got removed on January 2, 2025, and got replaced by a new termite hell. * Surprisingly enough, the bottom of Ant Hell 3 actually contained lower rarities of Termites ({{Rarity|Common}}- {{Rarity|Epic}}), while the top contained higher and more difficult rarities ({{Rarity|Epic}}- {{Rarity|Ultra}}). {{Removed Areas}} [[Category:Areas]] [[Category:Ant Areas]]

The '''Ant Hole''' is a legacy [[Mobs|mob]] in florr.io. It spawns up to four different types of Ants when attacked. 

==Appearance==
Three concentric circles, with the innermost being dark brown, the next one being a bit lighter, and the outermost being the lightest.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Stationary.

'''Behavior:''' Upon Ant Holes spawning, three [[Baby Ant]]s, two [[Worker Ant]]s, and a [[Soldier Ant]] spawn around it. When attacked, more ants come out, those being five Baby Ants, eight Worker Ants, twenty-six Soldier Ants, and one [[Queen Ant]] the same rarity of the Ant Hole, in total. The Queen Ant comes out when the Ant Hole is low on health. Baby Ants and Worker Ants tend to come out before Soldier Ants. When attacked, all Worker Ants that are part of the Ant Hole become aggressive. This applies to all rarities of the Ant Hole.

==Spawning==
Ant Holes spawn rarely in [[Garden]]. 

==Drops==

===[[Soil]]===
{{:Ant Hole/Drop Soil}}

===[[Shovel]]===
{{:Ant Hole/Drop Shovel}}

==Strategy== 
*Be careful when attacking higher rarity variants, since they can spawn lots of ants that can easily overwhelm you. In particular, the [[Queen Ant]] can deal significant damage.
*After the Queen Ant spawns, the Ant Hole is low on health. Notice how 5 to 10 more Soldier Ants will come out of the hole.
*[[Iris]]es are great for lower rarity Ant Holes. Try to hit the Ant Hole with the Iris and move away while the Ant Hole is taking damage.
*However, high damage petals (like Stinger) and high durability petals (like Rock, Heavy and Corn) can cause more ants to come out at once, so take extra caution when you have these petals.

=== {{Rarity|8}} Ant Hole ===

* In [[Garden]], the main danger when fighting a {{Rarity|8}} mob is not the actual mob, but the many {{Rarity|7}} mobs that spawn around the mob. When bubbling to the mob, it is advised to use [[Sponge]] and multiple {{Rarity|7}} healing petals, and have a [[Lotus]] equipped in case a [[Spider]] hits you. It is also advised to use Sponge when actually fighting the mob so you don't die if anything hits you.
* If the mob spawns deep in Garden, it is advised to form a "megasquad", where a large group of players stay near each other and clear mobs. This increases the chance for everyone to survive.
* Going to a {{Rarity|8}} Ant Hole is not advised unless you have over 200k dps, as it is one of the most sought after {{Rarity|8}} mobs. It has around 1 billion health, so you will need to deal 10 million damage to even have a chance of getting loot. 
* Avoid staying in corners as the Queen Ant that spawns may pin you to the wall.

==Trivia==  
*The Ant Hole has the most health out of any mob, with {{Rarity|9}} Ant Hole having almost 15 billion health, Thats more than a Target dummy.
*Ant Holes, [[Fire Ant Burrow|Fire Ant Burrows]], [[Termite Mound|Termite Mounds]], Queen Ants, Queen Fire Ants and the 3 types of [[Ant Egg (Mob)|An]][[Fire Ant Egg|t E]][[Termite Egg|ggs]] are the only mobs that can spawn other mobs at {{Rarity|6}}- rarities.
*Before January 19th 2023, the insides of {{Rarity|3}}+ Ant Holes were circular and while the Queen Ant matched the Ant Hole's rarity, the other Ants were always {{Rarity|3}}.
*After January 19th 2023 until an unknown date, every rarity of Ant Hole you could enter had the exact same map (old {{Rarity|3}}, current {{Rarity|4}} Ant Hole map).
*On an unknown date, the insides of {{Rarity|3}}+ Ant Holes were shifted forward by one rarity, resulting in a completely new, albeit tiny map for the Rare Ant Hole. Along with the absurdly huge {{Rarity|6}} Ant Hole map being replaced with the old {{Rarity|5}} Ant hole's map, making it unused until December 12th 2023 when M28 Spawned a {{Rarity|8}} Ant Hole and eventually on January 5th 2024 when {{Rarity|7}} and {{Rarity|8}} ant holes were able to spawn naturally the same map was used with more connections.
*On January 5th 2024, {{Rarity|7}} and {{Rarity|8}} spawning changed allowing {{Rarity|7}}/{{Rarity|8}} Ant Holes to spawn naturally.
*On April 29, 2024, all rarities of Ant Holes can now be attacked. This means that mobs that were inside will come out as it was for {{Rarity|1}} and {{Rarity|2}} Ant Holes before this change, but it now no longer depends on the rarity.
**Because of this, they now deal damage on direct contact.

title: Antennae
content:
''Not to be confused with the [[Talents/Antennae|talent of the same name]] and the [[Antennae/Mecha|Mecha]] version of this petal.''
{{Petal Template Infobox |image = File:Antennae (Rare).png|R File:Antennae (Epic).png|E File:Antennae (Legendary).png|L File:Antennae (Mythic).png|M File:Antennae (Ultra).png|Ul File:Antennae (Super).png|S File:Antennae (Unique).png|Uq |description = Allows your flower to sense foes farther away. |first-rarity = {{Rarity|Rare}} |extra-vision = {{Infobox Colors Empty|||+25%|+33.3%|+42.9%|+100%|+185.7%|+400%|+900%}} |stackability = Unstackable |dropped-by = *[[Hornet]] *[[Roach]] *[[Wasp]] |special = Increases the flower's FoV. }} '''Antennae''' are a legacy [[petal]] from [https://florr.io florr.io]. Unlike other petals, they will not orbit around the player, instead, they will attach themselves to the flower and will increase the user's FoV (Field of View, what the user can see on the monitor currently playing the game) by a certain amount. Antennae are dropped by [[Hornet]]s, [[Roach]]es and [[Wasp]]s. ==Appearance== Antennae consist of two black thick and slightly curved lines, resembling antennae on the following mobs: Hornet, [[Mantis]], Roach and Wasp. When equipped, Antennae will go on the top of the flower, making the flower look like it has its own pair of antennae. ==Function== {|class="article-table" |+Petal Stats ![[Rarity]] !Obtainable? !Extra Vision |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |rowspan="2"|No | +11.1% |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} | +16.6% |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |rowspan="7"|Yes | +25% |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} | +33.3% |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} | +42.9% |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} | +100% |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} | +185.7% |- |{{Rarity|Super}} | +400% |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} | +900% |} Unlike other petals, Antennae will not rotate around the flower. Instead, they will install themselves on top of the user's head, just like a Hornet's antennae. This means that they do not have the normal stats that other petals have, like damage, health, and reload time. Antennae's utility is to increase the flower's FoV significantly, allowing the user to see more areas of the map at the same time. As they don't count as a petal orbiting you, Antennae will also indirectly increase the distance between your petals in the petal orbit radius. Their effect isn't stackable, which means that using more Antennae at once in your build will not reward you with further FoV boosts. This is also applicable to the Antennae [[Talents|Talent]]. Antennae's FoV buff can override the Talent's buff if its value is higher, and vice-versa. ==Obtainment== ===[[Hornet]]=== {{:Hornet/Drop Antennae}} ===[[Roach]]=== {{:Roach/Drop Antennae}} ===[[Wasp]]=== {{:Wasp/Drop Antennae}} ===Purchasing=== On certain days, Antennae can be purchased from the [[Shop]]. ''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]'' {{Petal Price|2}} == Obtaining == * Antennae are dropped by [[Hornet|Hornets]], [[Roach|Roaches]], and [[Wasp|Wasps]]. The safest way to obtain them would be by farming [[Garden]], as farming Roaches without a good squad is tedious and [[Jungle]] requires more [[Plank|Planks]] to survive than Garden. ==Strategies== *Antennae can be added to a bubble build to spot areas to avoid running into mobs as well as to help find rare mobs such as [[Ladybug (Desert)|Shiny Ladybug]] or [[Square|Squares]]. **It is recommended for players with weaker/older devices to not use Antennae all the time as it can cause unwanted lag. **When farming high density areas such as the box in [[Sewers]] or the ultra zone in [[Ant Hell 1|Fire Ant Hell]], it is discouraged to use any antennae as mob aggressiveness is based off of the player's sight. * Antennae should only be used when you need to see more around you, as you are using a slot that could be used for healing or damage. You can equip a [[Third Eye]] or [[Disc]] under the Antennae for instant swapping. ==Gallery== File:Antennae (Common).png|{{Rarity|Common}} Antennae. (Unobtainable) File:Antennae (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|Unusual}} Antennae. (Unobtainable) File:Antennae (Rare).png|{{Rarity|Rare}} Antennae. File:Antennae (Epic).png|{{Rarity|Epic}} Antennae. File:Antennae (Legendary).png|{{Rarity|Legendary}} Antennae. File:Antennae (Mythic).png|{{Rarity|Mythic}} Antennae. File:Antennae (Ultra).png|{{Rarity|Ultra}} Antennae. File:Antennae (Super).png|{{Rarity|Super}} Antennae. File:Antennae (Unique).png|{{Rarity|Unique}} Antennae. File:Antennae.png|Antennae. File:Antennae (Old).png|Old Antennae. ==Trivia== *Antennae were added on the February 19th, 2020 update. Thus, they belong to the legacy florr.io era. *Antennae are the subject of one [[Achievement]]: the "All-seeing" {{Rarity|Unusual}} Achievement, granted to you once you equip Antennae of any rarity for the first time. *Antennae's description implies that you can "''sense'' foes farther away". This is a reference to in-real life insects, as some of them use their antennae to sense touch, air motions, heat, vibrations, smell, and taste. **To a lesser extent, Antennae are also a reference to parabolic antennae, who sense radio waves and can send/receive them. *Antennae are the first petal in the game which are not actually used as a weapon of any kind. Instead, they are an attachment to the flower. [[Cutter]], [[Pharaoh's Crown|Crown]], [[Disc]], [[Third Eye/Magic|Magic Eye]] and [[Third Eye]] are the only other petals to do this. *Antennae, [[Grapes]] and [[Peas]] are the only petals which are pluralized, rather than being singular. For example, Antenna is the singular form of Antennae. **Antennae are the only one out of these that come from legacy florr.io (which is the reason they're first). *Antennae are the second petal which cannot deal any damage, with the first being [[Bubble]], also a legacy florr.io petal. *Legacy florr.io [https://florr.io.fandom.com/wiki/Antennae Antennae] used to be dropped at the {{Rarity|Legendary}} rarity. Although they still are dropped by Hornets nowadays, now they are also dropped by [[Roach]]es and [[Wasp]]s ever since the addition of [[Sewers]] and [[Jungle]] in the game. **Their appearance resembling a Hornet's antennae not only references their legacy state (they were only dropped by Hornets) but also implies that, when a Hornet dies, their antennae detach from their body, being usable by flowers upon the Hornet's death. It is also implied that the same thing happens with Roaches and Wasps. *Antennae were the first petal whose {{Rarity|Super}} rarity variation was first obtained by someone who crafted it, instead of a purchase from the [[Shop]]. *It should be noted that all mobs who drop Antennae have particularly long antennae themselves (equal to the size of the petal itself). **Also, it seems that Antennae themselves are the one who give Hornets and Wasps a higher FoV at higher rarities. **For apparently the same reason, [[Bee]]s and [[Bumble Bee]]s (as well as other mobs who wield the same antennae as them) don't drop this, as their antennae are small and curved on their ends. However, [[Mantis]]es don't drop Antennae, despite having them like Hornets, Roaches and Wasps (likely because another mob in Jungle already drops Antennae, which is Wasp). {{Petals}} [[zh:触角]] [[Category:Petals]] [[Category:Buffs]] [[Category:Unstackable Petals]] [[Category:Wearable Petals]] [[Category:Rare+]] [[Category:Garden]] [[Category:Jungle]] [[Category:Sewers]] [[Category:Assembling]] [[Category:Wasps and Hornets]][[Category:Antennae]] [[Category:Roach]] [[Category:Luring Petals]]

title: Antennae/Mecha
content:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Mecha Antennae}} {{Petal Template Infobox | name = | image = File:Mecha Antennae (Rare).png|R File:Mecha Antennae (Epic).png|E File:Mecha Antennae (Legendary).png|L File:Mecha Antennae (Mythic).png|M File:Mecha Antennae (Ultra).png|Ul File:Mecha Antennae (Super).png|S File:Mecha Antennae (Unique).png|Uq | first-rarity = {{Rarity|3}} | description = Allows your flower to sense foes farther away.
And it has a blinking red light on top. | extra-vision = {{Infobox Colors Empty|7.8%|12.4%|17.5%|23.3%|30%|70%|130%|280%|630%}} | stackability = Unstackable | dropped-by = *[[Wasp/Mecha|Mecha Wasp]] | special = Increases your FoV by a percentage, and enables you to control [[Missile/Mecha|Mecha Missiles]] of the same rarity or below, but not above. }} '''Mecha Antennae''' is a [[Petals|petal]] from [https://florr.io florr.io]. Its function consists of increasing the flower's field of view, as well as enabling [[Missile/Mecha|Mecha Missiles]] of the same rarity or below to home on nearby enemies. Mecha Antennae is dropped by [[Wasp/Mecha|Mecha Wasp]]. ==Appearance== Mecha Antennae consists of two thin antennae, each with a red blinking light on the end. ==Function== If applicable, include: ''Note: The "A + B" respawn format means that the petal takes A seconds to respawn and B seconds after respawning to do its special action/have its special action enabled.'' ==Function== {|class="article-table" |+Petal Stats ![[Rarity]] !Obtainable? !Extra Vision |- |{{Rarity|Common}} | rowspan="2" |No | +7.8% |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} | +12.4% |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} | rowspan="7" |Yes | +17.5% |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} | +23.3% |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} | +30% |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} | +70% |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} | +130% |- |{{Rarity|Super}} | +280% |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} | +630% |} Mecha Antennae increase the user's field of view (FOV) like [[Antennae]] but lower. It also control [[Missile/Mecha|Mecha Missiles]], enable its ability to home on mobs. Mecha Antennae does not stack its FOV with other Mecha Antennae, so does [[Talents/Antennae|Antennae Talent]] and [[Antennae]]. However, you can use [[Antennae]] together with Mecha Antennae without getting any of them disabled. ==Obtainment== ===[[Wasp/Mecha|Mecha Wasp]]=== {{:Mecha Wasp/Drop Mecha Antennae}} Mecha Antennae can be assemble by using 1 [[Antennae]] and 1 [[Light Bulb]] of the same rarity via [[Assembler]]. ===Purchasing=== On certain days, Mecha Antennae can be purchased from the [[Shop]]. ''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]'' {{Petal Price|1}} ==Strategies== *Mecha Antennae's FoV is less than that of Antennae's, so it is only useful for using Mecha Missile. However, its drop rate is much higher than that of Antennae's, making its high rarity versions easier to obtain. ==Gallery== File:Mecha Antennae (Common).png|{{Rarity|1}} Mecha Antennae File:Mecha Antennae (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|2}} Mecha Antennae File:Mecha Antennae (Rare).png|{{Rarity|3}} Mecha Antennae File:Mecha Antennae (Epic).png|{{Rarity|4}} Mecha Antennae File:Mecha Antennae (Legendary).png|{{Rarity|5}} Mecha Antennae File:Mecha Antennae (Mythic).png|{{Rarity|6}} Mecha Antennae File:Mecha Antennae (Ultra).png|{{Rarity|7}} Mecha Antennae File:Mecha Antennae (Super).png|{{Rarity|8}} Mecha Antennae File:Mecha Antennae (Unique).png|{{Rarity|9}} Mecha Antennae File:Mecha Antennae.png|Mecha Antennae. ==Trivia== *Mecha Antennae is the 100th petal added to the game. The 100th petal was planned to be something exceedingly rare by [[M28]], but ended up being this. *If you see clearly and compare the petal with normal Antennae, Mecha Antennae is thinner than normal Antennae. *Mecha Antennae having lower FOV might be because it need power to control [[Missile/Mecha|Mecha Missile]]. **This can be counteracted by equipping a normal Antennae of at least the same rarity, as the higher bonus takes full priority. *This is one of the petals that start at Rare rarity. *When you equip both [[Antennae]] and Mecha Antennae at once, you can see that only Mecha Antennae's 2 red dots appear, and the rest is normal Antennae. {{Petals}} [[Category:Petals]] [[Category:Factory]] [[Category:Wearable Petals]] [[Category:Assembling]] [[Category:Wasps and Hornets]][[Category:Antennae]] [[Category:“Mecha” Petals]]
