title: Armor
content:
{{Stub}}
Armor is an attribute of all mobs in game, excluding [[Fire Ant Burrow]]. At base, the incremental armor to mobs goes: 1, 2, 7, 22, 65, 194, and capping off at 583 for any mob over {{Rarity|Mythic}}. Armor does not work on Poison damage or Lightning damage.

Mobs such as [[Leafbug]] and the Mecha mobs ([[Mecha Flower]], [[Crab/Mecha|Mecha Crab]], [[Spider/Mecha|Mecha Spider]], and [[Wasp/Mecha|Mecha Wasp]]) have extra armor stats which do not cap at a specific rarity, unlike base armor. For example, the extra armor of an {{Rarity|Ultra}} Mecha Flower is 4,228 (total) - 583 (base) = 3,645, whereas that of a {{Rarity|Super}} Mecha Flower is 11,518 (total) - 583 (base) = 10,935, triple the previous rarity. The extra armor scales exactly like petal damage, petal health (with some exceptions), or mob body damage.

If the damage value of an entity ([[Flower]]'s Body Damage, [[Gambler]]'s Petal, etc.) is less than or equal to another entity's Armor value, the physical damage will be cancelled, Otherwise, it will receive <math>\left(\text{Damage} - \text{Self Armor}\right)</math> damage and register that value. This is most noticeable with Leafbug, with [[Light]] and other low damage petals barely doing any damage to it, while high damage petals like [[Stinger]] can pierce easily. Armor Debuff from [[Bur]] will lower this attribute to the damage source, allowing the damager to gain a damage boost. Armor can go negative due to this.

There are currently two petals with armor applied to it, [[Bone]] and [[Root]]. As for Root, the armor does not go away if the armored petal is destroyed but not unequipped, as long as the requirement to apply armor is met. Bone reduces damage dealt to itself, making it better than the armorless version [[Coin]].{{Game Info}}

title: Assembler
content:
{{Background|图片=Factoryb.png|大小=2000px}} {{Mob Template Infobox | name = {{PAGENAME}} | image = File:Assembler(Common).png|C File:Assembler(Unusual).png|U File:Assembler(Rare).png|R File:Assembler(Epic).png|E File:Assembler(Legendary).png|L File:Assembler(Mythic).png|M File:Assembler(Ultra).png|Ul File:Assembler(Super).png|S File:Assembler(Unique).png|Uq | rarity = {{Rarity|1}} | health = Invulnerable | move = No | status = | attack = | spawn = [[Factory]] | special = Allows you to convert specific petals into different petals. }} '''Assembler''' is a [[:Category:Special Entities|Special Entity]] in [[florr.io]]. It appears scattered across [[Factory]] in rarities ranging from {{Rarity|1}} to {{Rarity|Super}}. It can be used to convert specific petals into different petals. It despawns and respawns at random locations in the same chamber with a rerolled recipe every certain time interval. Each chamber in Factory contains one Assembler. ==Appearance== The Assembler appears as a gray metallic machine with rotating gears and dials. It also has a conveyor belt, an air vent, a green light, and a hole. ==Interface== When you get close to the Assembler or touch it with your flower, the [[Crafting]] menu will turn into the [[Assembling|Assemble]] menu. Touching it with your flower, like touching any other Special Entity, automacically opens it. But it was later disabled in November 2025. The Assemble menu allows you to insert multiple petals to convert them into different petals. Assemblers only accepts petals and outputs petals of the same rarity as themselves. The Assemble interface is light gray in color, and doesn't display any of your petals other than the petals of the specific rarity that the Assembler accepts. If the player doesn't have the required petals, the displayed petal requirement would be grayed out, similar to how petals are grayed out when the player does not meet the requirements for the [[Oracle]] or the [[Titan]] to perform their crafts. The text below the interface reads:
'''{{Text shadow|Combine specific petals to assemble a new petal.}}'''
===Recipes=== All rarities of Assembler have the same selection of recipes, although sometimes they are unusable due to petals being unobtainable at their respective rarities. {| class="mw-collapsible article-table" !Input !Output |- |1x [[Glass]] |1x [[Sand]] |- |1x [[Sand]] |1x [[Glass]] |- |1x [[Battery]]
1x [[Bulb]]

1x [[Glass]]
1x [[Magnet]] |1x [[Compass]] |- |100,000x [[Rock]] |1x [[Moon Rock]] |- |1x [[Moon Rock]] |10x [[Rock]] |- |1x [[Battery]] |1x [[Fragment]] |- |1x [[Light]]
1x [[Honey]] |1x [[Pollen]] |- |10x [[Triangle]] |1x [[Cutter]] |- |20x [[Fragment]] 1x [[Cogwheel]] |1x [[Sawblade]] |} ==Gallery== File:Assembler(Common).png|{{R|1}} Assembler. File:Assembler(Unusual).png|{{R|2}} Assembler. File:Assembler(Rare).png|{{R|3}} Assembler. File:Assembler(Epic).png|{{R|4}} Assembler. File:Assembler(Legendary).png|{{R|5}} Assembler. File:Assembler(Mythic).png|{{R|6}} Assembler. File:Assembler(Ultra).png|{{R|7}} Assembler. File:Assembler(Super).png|{{R|8}} Assembler. File:Assembler(Unique).png|{{R|9}} Assembler. File:Assembler0.png|Assembler. File:Assembler.png|Assembler Asset. File:Assemble.png|An {{R|7}} Assembler in {{R|6}} zone. ==Trivia== *Assemblers will sometimes ask for petals that can't be obtained, including an {{Rarity|2}} [[Basil]] and an {{Rarity|4}} [[Monstera]] and give petals that normally don't exist like {{Rarity|Rare}} [[Fragment]] or {{Rarity|6}} [[Compass]]. These petals that don't exist normally can be ingredients for assembling other usually unobtainable petals. *Assembler is the only special entity that has no dialogue and is not a flower. Not having dialogue makes sense because it is inanimate. *Each spawning zone in the map can spawn a maximum of one Assembler, which despawns and respawns after a while with another random recipe. *The recipe that takes 3 [[Battery|Batteries]] to produce 1 Battery + 1 [[Lightning]] is the only recipe to input and output the same petal at different quantities. It discharges 2 Batteries to produce 1 Lightning, and it uses a third Battery to start the assembly and returns it to the player afterwards. *Both [[Monstera]] and [[Basil]] can be disassembled to produce 2 Leaves each. *Despite not having any copies of itself, {{Rarity|Common}} [[Light]] can convert {{Rarity|Common}} [[Rose]] into {{Rarity|Common}} [[Dahlia]]. *Assembler is useful for anyone who want to obtain {{Rarity|Ultra}} [[Compass]] easily without killing {{Rarity|Ultra}}+ [[Termite Overmind]]. *[[Moon Rock|Moon]] when assembled used to give 25 Rocks instead of 10 Rocks now. *Getting {{Rarity|Mythic}}- Compass from Assembler is not worth as they are broken now, right after a day of the released recipe. However, you can use the [[Oracle]] to turn 128 of them to get an {{Rarity|Ultra}} Compass, which works normally. *{{Rarity|Super}} and {{Rarity|Unique}} Assemblers are theoretically spawnable. However, {{Rarity|Unique}} Assemblers only give {{Rarity|Super}} recipes (unconfirmed). The reason probably is some recipes could give 2 {{R|Unique}} petals of the same petal, which is illegal. Example: when you assembled a {{R|Unique}} [[Monstera]], it would give you 2 {{R|Unique}} [[Leaf|Leaves]] at once, which might break the [[Titan]] usage for getting {{R|Unique}} petals. *There used to be a recipe when you can use a [[Rock]] and a [[Honey]] to assemble a [[Wax]]. This was removed some times later and was replaced with the current recipe: 3 Honey -> 1 Wax. *There's one {{R|Unique}} Assembler that spawned, but not confirmed yet, but someone has screenshotted it. *Super assemblers have spawn messages, just like mobs. {{Mobs}} [[Category:Mobs]] [[Category:Non-stationary mobs]] [[Category:Special Entities]] [[Category:Interactive Entities]] [[Category:Mob]] [[Category:Factory]] [[Category:Mecha Mobs]] [[Category:Assembling]]

''For other versions of the Baby Ant, see [[Baby Termite]] or [[Baby Fire Ant]].''

The '''Baby Ant''' is a mob in florr.io. Baby Ants spawn in [[Garden]] (from [[Ant Hole]]s or close to the [[Ant Hell]] portal) and Ant Hell.

==Appearance==
Two small grey circles with two slightly tilted lines representing its mouth. The outer circle is slightly darker than the inner circle.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Slow (Moderate when spawned from Ant Eggs or provoked by [[Honey]]).

'''Behavior:''' Baby Ants move a short distance slowly before pausing and eventually changing direction. Baby Ants are always passive if spawned naturally, but will attack if spawned from an [[Ant Egg (Mob)|Ant Egg]]. However, upon killing the player that spawned them, they become passive again.

==Spawning==
The {{PAGENAME}} spawns in Garden, from an [[Ant Hole]] (first half of HP drain), and regularly from [[Ant Hell]]. 
==Drops==
===[[Light]]===
{{ID 6}}

===[[Leaf]]===
{{ID 7 - U}}

===[[Rice]]===
{{ID 6}}


==Strategy== 
*Because of its slow speed and low health, any build can defeat it quickly. Just do it quickly, because Baby Ants are rarely alone.
*Killing them is a good enough task to do, as it provides not only the [[Light]] petal, very versatile and useful, but also the [[Leaf]] petal, one of the best healing petals, as well as the [[Rice]]. Light and Leaf are very important beginner petals that also have value later on.

=== Super Baby Ant ===

* The {{Rarity|Super}} Baby Ant is not really hard to kill. Mainly due to it having lower health than most {{Rarity|Super}} Mobs and it not becoming aggressive when attacked.
* For both Garden and Ant Hell, the key to getting loot from a {{Rarity|Super}} Baby Ant is speed. Typically, {{Rarity|Super}} Baby Ants die before 25 people are able to deal damage, so at least 4 Ultra Bubbles are recommended before trying to hunt a {{Rarity|Super}} Baby Ant.
* Around 330,000 damage is required for loot, so most builds will be able to get loot if you arrive early.
* In Ant Hell, the {{Rarity|Super}} Baby Ant is not the main danger, but the many {{Rarity|Ultra}} [[Soldier Ant]]s, [[Queen Ant]]s and [[Worm]]s around it.
* In Garden, the main danger when fighting a {{Rarity|Super}} mob is not the actual mob, but the many Ultra mobs that spawn around the mob. When bubbling to the mob, it is advised to use [[Sponge]] and multiple {{Rarity|Ultra}} healing petals, and have a [[Lotus]] equipped in case a [[Spider]] hits you. It is also advised to use Sponge when actually fighting the mob so you don't die if anything hits you. Using an {{Rarity|Ultra}}+ [[Poo]] is almost necessary, due to the {{Rarity|Ultra}} [[Hornet|Hornets]] there.
* If the mob spawns deep in Garden, it is advised to form a "megasquad", where a large group of players stay near each other and clear mobs. This increases the chance for everyone to survive.

*Baby Ants used to be the only mob that was passive in all rarities that could move on its own. 
**The other passive mobile mobs include [[Baby Fire Ant]], [[Baby Termite]], [[Sandstorm]], [[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble]], and [[Moth]].
*The Baby Ant has the same fill color, a shade of grey, as the {{Rarity|9}} icon. This means that the {{Rarity|9}} baby ant icon is fully grey, with a slightly darker circular outline and two black lines pointing outward to represent its mouth.

'''Baby Fire Ant''' is a mob in florr.io. It spawns in [[Ant Hell]].

==Appearance==
Baby Fire Ants look like Baby Ants, only they are much more red in hue.

== Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Slow (Moderate when spawned from [[Fire Ant Egg]]s or provoked by [[Honey]]).

'''Behavior:''' It is passive on all rarities, but it becomes hostile if it spawns from a [[Fire Ant Egg]]. However, upon killing the player that spawned them, they become passive again.

===[[Yucca]]===

=== [[Broccoli]] ===
{{:Beetle/ Drop Privet}}

==Strategy==
*Take them out. These are free loot, they don't even attack! (Unless they spawned from a Fire Ant Egg, but they are still not much of a threat)

=== Super Baby Fire Ant ===

* The key to getting loot from a {{Rarity|8}} Baby Fire Ant is speed. Typically, {{Rarity|8}} Baby Fire Ants die before 25 people are able to deal damage, so at least 4 {{Rarity|7}} Bubbles are recommended before trying to hunt a {{Rarity|8}} Baby Fire Ant.
* Around 330,000 damage is required for loot, so most builds will be able to get loot if you arrive early.

''For other variations of the Baby Termite, see [[Baby Ant]] or [[Baby Fire Ant]].''

'''Baby Termite''' is a mob in florr.io. They spawn in [[Termite Mound]]s and [[Ant Hell]] and were added on February 27th, 2023.

==Appearance==
A small orange circle with two black lines on its front.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:'''  Move randomly for a few tiles before they change direction when passive, medium speed when aggressive.

'''Behavior:''' It is weak and turns semi-aggressive when health is under 50%. However, if it was spawned from a destroyed Termite Egg, or if a player damages any nearby Termite Egg, it will be aggressive towards the player who destroyed/attacked it. However, upon killing the player that spawned them, they become passive again.

It has a Psyonic Connection with other termites, taking damage if any other termites within a certain radius of itself takes damage.

==Spawning==
The {{PAGENAME}} spawns in Ant Hell 3 and in Termite Mounds in the Jungle. It can also be spawned from Termite Eggs.
==Drops==
===[[Relic]]===
{{:Baby Termite/Drop Relic}}

===[[Carrot]]===
{{:Baby Termite/Drop Carrot}}

==Strategy==
*Although semi-aggressive when [[Termite Egg]]<nowiki/>s are hit or otherwise passive, this mob is not threatening on its own but becomes deadly when paired with termite soldiers as they act as meatshields that keep the soldiers alive for longer.
* It has more health compared to fire and normal ants and it's psyonic connection makes it take even less damage if there are other termites so do not get overconfident and ram it.

=== Super Baby Termite ===

* Around 328,000 damage is required for loot, but due to high demand you will probably need a decent DPS build to get loot.
* Baby Termites will become aggressive if you damage a Termite Egg, so stay on the lookout for any Termite Eggs that may be nearby.

== Trivia ==

* Baby Termites, along with other Termites got a health change during the following years.
** On October 22, 2024, its HP was nerfed by 50% when its original base health is 50, before being reverted on October 23, 2025 update.
** On January 22, 2026, its HP was nerfed by another 50% again.
* Baby Termites are the only Ant variant that can become hostile even if it is not from an Egg.
* Baby Termites used to have jaws that are positioned like the normal and fire versions but that was changed somewhere on March 1, 2023 to have the jaws positioned away from its body.

'''Basic''' is a legacy [[petal]] from [https://florr.io/ florr.io]. It is the default starter petal, and thus, the first ever obtained petal, but not the first picked up petal. Basic is dropped by [[Flower]]s in [[Hel]] as well as [[Ghost|Ghosts]].

==Appearance==
Basic consists of a simple, white, circular petal with a light gray outline, serving as the basic and model appearance for other petals.

==Function==

Basic is considered the most fundamental petal with relatively modest and balanced attributes, lacking any distinctive features. Every player begins with five {{Rarity|Common}} Basics (four on the player's main loadout and another one at the secondary loadout, directly below the fifth petal slot which is occupied by a [[Rose]]), which are not subject to crafting to the {{Rarity|Unusual}} rarity. Any additional {{Rarity|Common}} Basics are not accessible through regular means, and cannot be crafted into {{Rarity|Unusual}} Basics. {{Rarity|Unusual}}+ Basics can be used for crafting.

== Obtainment==
Basic can be obtained through killing flowers in [[Hel]], defeating [[Ghost|Ghosts]], and winning a Battle Royale match, which grants the player the choice between obtaining the [[Champion's Crown]] or a {{Rarity|Super}} Basic. Champion's Crown also gives players a temporary {{R|Super}} Basic.

===Flowers===
Killing flowers in Hel is one way of obtaining Basics. Not only does using [[Corruption]] not grant you any Basics if you kill players in normal zones such as Garden (or killing a Corruption user yourself), Basics that are on the ground can be collected by anybody so a player may swoop in and steal them. [[Magnet|Magnets]] and the Magnetism [[Talent]] are also incompatible with Basic drops.

The rarity of Basic depends on their score as shown below. When a flower dies with enough score (20 or more; players with less than 20 will not drop a Basic), they receive two Basics, with their rarity based on their score prior to death: One Basic is dropped on the ground for anyone to collect whilst the player that died obtains a second Basic instantly on death. If a player is skilled or lucky enough they may arrive to their place of death in time to obtain the other Basic.

It should be noted that the scores listed below are rounded values, as such, they're not entirely accurate. Note that this score makes it difficult to obtain high rarity Basics.
{| class="article-table"
![[Rarity]]
!Score
|-
|Nothing
|0-20
|-

|20-180

|{{Rarity|Rare}}
|180-2.7k
|-

|2.7k-72k
|-
|{{Rarity|Legendary}}
|72k-6.2m
|-
|{{Rarity|Mythic}}
|6.2m-790m
|-
|{{Rarity|Ultra}}
|790m-199.4b
|-
|{{Rarity|Super}}
|199.4b-324.6t
|}

=== Ghosts ===
Ghosts also drop Basics.

One {{R|Super}} Basic is given to whoever wins a Battle Royale in the Rift.

===Purchasing===
Unlike most other petals, Basic can't be purchased from the [[Shop]], meaning it's useless looking for it in [[Shop]].

== Obtaining ==

* If you wish to obtain more basics, you need to either farm in [[Hel]] and then die to a mob OR hunt players with score in Hel. You can also kill ghosts, but those are rare to encounter.

== Strategies ==
*Get new petals as quickly as possible. Almost 99% of other petals that you collect will be better than Basic in some way.
**If you are looking for good farming petals, go to [[Ant Hell]] for [[Bone]] (Termite Hell) or [[Glass]] (Normal Ant Hell), or [[Ocean]] for [[Lightning]].
**If you are in need of healing, farm [[Ocean]] for [[Starfish]]. [[Ant Hell]] is a good source of [[Leaf]] and [[Yucca]]. [[Jungle]] can also provide [[Cotton]] and [[Dahlia]]. [[Desert]] provides [[Cactus]] if you need more hit points.
**If you are in need of raw damage, farm [[Garden]] or [[Jungle]]. [[Stinger|Stingers]] (or [[Blood Stinger|Blood Stingers]] in Jungle) can be useful in the early game.
**If you need summons, farm [[Desert]], where all summoning petals are.

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">
File:Basic (Common).png|{{Rarity|1}} Basic.

</gallery>

==Trivia==
*Basic and [[Rose]] are the first petals a player can obtain, as they are your only efficient way of getting another petal (Basic) and healing you (Rose) before you progress through the game. You get 5 Basics (one in the main loadout and another one in the secondary one) and 1 Rose immediately after entering the game for the first time.
*The florr.io developer [[M28]] used to give {{Rarity|Ultra}} Basics for people who successfully reported bugs in the game's Discord server, when petals of the said rarity only lasted for one game, and the {{Rarity|Ultra}} rarity was black-colored. Afterwards, M28 decided to give {{Rarity|Common}} [[Square]]s. To accommodate the change, every {{Rarity|Ultra}} Basic was converted into a {{Rarity|Common}} Square on the day this decision was made. Nowadays, no rewards are obtained when reporting a bug.
**The only other petals that had this conversion were {{Rarity|Epic}}+ [[Peas]] which turned into [[Grapes]] of the same rarity to accommodate the petal lineage separation from normal Peas to poisonous Peas (Grapes).
*Basic, [[Faster]], [[Heavy]] and [[Light]] are petals named after adjectives, rather than substantives. They are the only petals to have that characteristic, and all four are from legacy florr.io.
**In legacy florr, the same petals have that same characteristic: [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Basic Basic], [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Fast Fast] (renamed to Light), [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Faster Faster], [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Heaviest Heaviest] (renamed to Heavy) and [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Heavy Heavy] (which is technically removed if you don't count current Heavy.
*It is possible to drop Basics of a rarity higher than {{Rarity|Mythic}}. However, it requires an absurd amount of score no one has ever reached. This is due to the fact that M28 wanted to prevent {{Rarity|Ultra}}+ Basic drops to keep their rarity.
**It should be noted that {{Rarity|Ultra}} and {{Rarity|Super}} Basics are obtainable at 837 million and 211 billion scores respectively.
*{{R|1}} Basic is the only petal in the game to not be obtainable by any means: you can only hold 5 of them for forever.
**Since they can't be used for crafting either, they are the only non-Super petals to not be available to craft. As a consequence, {{Rarity|Unusual}} Basic is the only non-{{Rarity|Common}} petal to be unable to be crafted from its previous rarity at all.
**It used to be possible for players to craft {{Rarity|Common}} Basic from {{Rarity|Mythic}} Basic before the [[Talents]] (at the time called Skills) update. It had a 100% success rate.
**In legacy florr.io, a glitch called the "Special Basic Obtaining Glitch" used to exist (and still exists in [https://static.florr.io/old/ static florr.io] servers). It consisted of dying at a high level while not collecting any petals (or a maximum of 3), then respawning. The player would get the normal 5 Basics + one for level 15, another one for level 30 and another one for level 45, if you respawned at those levels or higher. {{Rarity|OldUnique}} Basics also could've obtained with a higher chance this way.
*Basic (at {{Rarity|Ultra}} rarity), Coin (on all rarities), [[Nazar Amulet]] (at {{Rarity|Unusual}}, {{Rarity|Rare}}, {{Rarity|Legendary}} and {{Rarity|Mythic}} rarities) and Square (on all rarities except {{Rarity|Common}} rarity) are the only non-{{Rarity|Super}} and non-{{Rarity|Unique}} petals that aren't droppable by mobs, yet still obtainable by alternative means ([[Crafting]] for all petals, and [[Trader|trading]] for Coin).
*When [[Absorbing]] used to be in the game, Basic couldn't be absorbed regardless of rarity, and was the only petal that used to do so. {{Rarity|Common}}-{{Rarity|Ultra}} Basics could still be either used or wasted for Crafting.
*{{Rarity|Mythic}} and {{Rarity|Ultra}} Basics had an exclusive trait to themselves: they used to sparkle like legacy {{Rarity|OldUnique}} Basic. After the {{Rarity|Super}} update, though, it was changed so that they could no longer sparkle, and the sparkling is currently a shared feature among every {{Rarity|Super}} petal (as opposed to only {{Rarity|Super}} Basic).
*Basics you drop when dying in [[Hel]] are saved to your inventory, probably as a result of people quitting the game for losing a {{Rarity|Legendary}}+ Basic drop. 
**Due to special coding, Basic drops cannot be picked up by [[Magnet]] or the Magnetism Talent. You have to get close to them to pick them up, all by yourself. Dying also won't give you the Basic unless it's your Basic.
**The dropped Basic is not the same Basic you drop. You get a separate one for dying. You can still grab the Basic you dropped, though, letting you have two of them from one death.
*Basic's damage matches with the body damage stat of most mobs. Those mobs are: [[Baby Ant|An]][[Worker Ant|t]][[Soldier Ant|s]], [[Bush]], [[Centipede|Cent]][[Centipede (Desert)|ipe]][[Centipede (Jungle)|des]], [[Ladybug|Lad]][[Ladybug (Desert)|ybu]][[Ladybug (Jungle)|gs]], [[Leech]], [[Rock (Mob)|Rock mob]], [[Shell (Mob)|Shell mob]], [[Sponge (Mob)|Sponge mob]] and [[Baby Termite|Ter]][[Worker Termite|mit]][[Soldier Termite|es]].
*On June 17th, 2024, the developer sent players who were at the bottom right of Desert while killing 10 {{Rarity|Super}} [[Ladybug (Desert)|Shiny Ladybugs]] (spawned by M28) to Hel, and gave all of them 10 {{Rarity|Super}} Basics each. This, however, wasn't saved onto their inventories.
*Basic, [[Card]], [[Poker Chip|Chip]], Ultra [[Corruption]] and [[Dice]] are the only petals to exclusively drop in Hel.
**Basic and Corruption are the only Hel-exclusive petal not involved with any RNG in terms of damage, healing or anything else. 
***Basic, Coin and Square are the only petals to not be involved with RNG at all on their own, as they lack a drop chance from a certain mob or a chance to appear in the Shop. Basics have a 100% (guaranteed) chance of dropping from flowers (and although the higher rarity Basics need to be dropped by higher score players, their chance is still 100%). Squares also have a 100% drop chance when killing any rarity of Square (mob). Only crafting the Basic and Coin petals would include RNG, with any rarity of Square having a 100% crafting success probability.
**Additionally, Basic is the only Hel-exclusive petal not dropped from any mobs there.
**Finally, Basic is the only Hel-exclusive petal from legacy florr.io.
**Before August 27th, 2024, it was theorized that Basic could be dropped outside Hel. but since PVP was exclusive to this zone (specially when PvP in Centralia (modern-day Garden) was removed some time ago), Basics were only seen dropping there. Then, ever since Corruption was added in the update of the same date, it was confirmed that Basics are tied to Hel regardless of PvP outside of other zones, thus still making them exclusive to that area. This means that another piece of special coding was made in order to let flowers drop Basics only while in Hel, even if either of the players during combat used a Corruption to engage in PvP outside of that area.
*Basic, Chip, Coin, Corruption and Square are the only petals unable to appear in the [[Shop]] to be bought.
**In Basic's case, it's because it's the only petal to be dropped by flowers (exclusively, for that matter), thus putting it in the Shop would make it less rare.
*Basic technically changed the way it drops: while [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Basic legacy {{Rarity|Common}} Basics] never dropped (and had to be acquired with a glitch) and [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Basic_(Unique) legacy {{Rarity|OldUnique}} Basics] could only be dropped from flowers (and spawned with a 0.1% chance), Basics in new florr.io are still dropped from a flower, but never {{Rarity|Common}} (which can't be obtained), {{Rarity|Ultra}}, {{Rarity|Super}} can be obtained at certain scores after a new update suggested by EndermanMC.
*{{Rarity|Super}} Basics can be considered one of the hardest {{Rarity|Super}} petals to obtain: not only due to the hard nature of crafting {{Rarity|Super}} petals, but also due to the Basic's trait of being dropped by flowers, as well as {{Rarity|Ultra}} Basics also being exclusive to [[Crafting]]. The only {{Rarity|Super}} petals which can be considered even rarer than the Basic are: [[Cutter]] and [[Uranium]] (since high rarity [[Digger]]s will spawn from the already rare high rarity Ant/Termite houses with a 5% chance, combined with their average drop rates), [[Moon Rock]] (lowest drop rates in the game), [[Square]] (due to the [[Square (Mob)|Square mob]]'s rare nature and the fact that it only drops {{Rarity|Common}} Squares, thus, it can arguably be considered the hardest {{Rarity|Super}} petal, even though Crafting Squares is 100% guaranteed at all rarities), [[Yggdrasil]] (due to the extremely rare nature of high rarity [[Shiny Ladybug]]s combined with a decent drop rate) and [[Nazar Amulet]] (due to [[Beetle/Nazar|Nazar Beetles]] being extremely rare combined with an extremely low drop rate).
*M28 also gave out 5 {{Rarity|Super}} Basics during an event which happened in NA Pyramid. He hid 5 {{Rarity|Super}} Basics and told players to find them. One of the owners of these {{Rarity|Super}} Basics is Hot_X_Donalds confirming that M28 did not take away the {{Rarity|Super}} Basics after the events ended. 
*M28 said Basics will be used to craft [[Soul Splitter|soul splitters]]. This likely means that managing to craft super basic will give you the splitter instead, just like [[Fragment|fragments]] with [[mjölnir]]. 
*Basic is now the only petal in game that can be obtained from all the biomes (from ghosts in garden ocean and desert and players in hel)
*A {{Rarity|Super}} Basic is used as a reward for the player get the highest score in Battle Royale.
*The first {{Rarity|Super}} Basic craft has been achieved, and Skware is current owner of {{Rarity|Unique}} Basic; he has 9 {{Rarity|Super}} Basic total, according to himself.
*Basics aren't as rare now, their value has since plummeted a lot due to the fact the there is no limit on how many {{Rarity|Super}} Basics can be achieved in Battle Royale. This means players can hoards lot of {{Rarity|Super}} Basics, and also anyone can achieve {{Rarity|Super}} Basics unlike where only 5 players would have it. 

{{Petals}}

The '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is a mob in Florr.io. It spawns all around [[Garden]] as the sole provider for the [[Stinger]] petal. It also drops [[Honey]] and [[Pollen]].

==Appearance==
A yellow, oval-shaped body with black stripes running from side to side. A slightly darker yellow outlines this body. On the back end, a triangle represents the stinger (though it doesn't do anything). On the front, there are two slightly curved antennae which curve away from the center. On the ends, there are small circles on the antennae.

==Stats & Behavior==
*'''Speed: '''Slow when passive, fast when aggressive.
*'''Behavior:''' 
**Wanders around randomly. It is usually passive but deals very high damage on impact.
**Passive - Moves in slow, random curved lines for a few tiles before stopping and changing directions.
**Aggressive - Moves towards the player in a path that resembles a [https://www.google.com/search?q=sinusoidal+curve&rlz=1C1CHBD_enUS896US896&oq=sinusoidal+curve&gs_lcrp=EgZjaHJvbWUyBggAEEUYOdIBCDE3OTFqMGo3qAIAsAIA&sourceid=chrome&ie=UTF-8&safe=active&ssui=on sinusoidal curve], at a moderately high speed ([[Bubble]] and [[Bubble/Magic|Magic Bubble]] can be useful for outrunning).
**If the Bee's rarity is {{Rarity|Rare}}+, the bee will chase the player until the player dies or it is killed. It will not attack at lower rarities.

==Spawning==
The {{PAGENAME}} spawns everywhere in Garden. In the bottom right, there is a zone where it spawns more frequently, at around Legendary rarity.
==Drops==
===[[Stinger]]===
{{ID 6}}

===[[Pollen]]===
{{:Bee/Drop Pollen}}

===[[Honey]]===
{{:Bee/Drop Honey}}

==Strategy==
*The Bee has high body damage and if not taken care of from a distance, they could instantly wipe you out, especially if you are just starting out. Make sure to take on the Bee far away to not be hit by its insane body damage, and beware any other mobs that could be nearby when killing the Bee.

*They're useful for getting [[Stinger]]s, not only for PvP but also for damaging very high rarity mobs. It servers best in the OP Quick-Ripper build, while being effective in all zones.
*In mythic zones in garden, Mythic Bees spawn among other mobs. It is a great area to grind/farm Bees for their loot, but be careful of other hostile mobs such as the [[Hornet]].
** However, if you're too weak for the mythic zones, the Bee zone is also a great high rarity area to find Legendary and rarely Mythic Bees however be aware of other mobs like Hornets, especially Ultra Hornets that spawn there.
** You could also go to Bee zone and use [[Talents/Luck|Luck talent]] or [[Clover]] to turn the place into a Mythic/Ultra zone.
*The Bee has low health, so kill it with high damage petals like Stinger and ranged petals like [[Wing]] with [[Third Eye]], but be aware that Stinger has a long reload. 
*Using a a combination of DPS petals (like [[Sand]], [[Rice]], or [[Light]]) and/or [[Pincer]] and [[Web]] will also help. 
Here are the drops:
*[[Stinger]] is a high damage petal with a slow reload. It can be used to enforce large damage on high-health mobs to take them out, especially with petals like [[Faster]]. With higher rarities, Stinger gains more damage and health. At the mythic rarity, the stinger will turn into three stingers which is commonly known as "tringer". At its ultra/super/unique rarity, the three stingers will turn into five stingers which is commonly known as "pinger".
* [[Pollen]] is a petal with a fast reload that can be dropped on the ground. When dropped, any mob that runs into it will be hurt by it. Pollen is good for damaging mobs while running away from them at the same time, it can also be used to block things like Hornet missiles. With higher rarities it gains more damage and health, at the rare rarity, it turns into 2 petals, at the epic rarity, it turns into 3 petals, at the ultra rarity, the petal grows in size a little bit.
*[[Honey]] is a petal with a moderate reload that can be dropped just like Pollen and acts as a bait for mobs, when placed, it will lure any mob that is within its rarity limit and distract them until the petal dies, with each higher rarity, Honey will attract higher rarity mobs and gain more health.

=== Super Bee ===

* In Garden, the main danger when fighting a Super mob is not the actual mob, but the many Ultra mobs that spawn around the mob. When bubbling to the mob, it is advised to use Sponge and multiple Ultra healing petals, and have a Lotus equipped in case a Spider hits you. It is also advised to use Sponge when actually fighting the mob so you don't die if anything hits you.
* If the mob spawns deep in Garden, it is advised to form a "megasquad", where a large group of players stay near each other and clear mobs. This increases the chance for everyone to survive.
* Around 500,000 damage is required for loot, but a decent DPS build is advised due to extremely high demand.
* Because Bees move slightly faster than players, having bubbles in your build is recommended. 
* Third eye is also recommended due to the irregular movement of Bees, which may lead to petals missing if you don't have enough reach.

*Bees used to chase the player in straight lines, like most mobs. They also moved at a slower speed.
*Despite having antennae, Bees don't drop [[Antennae]], likely because they're a different shape, and Hornets always used to drop them instead.
*2 {{R|5}} Bees spawned on the Garden spawn, This allowed it to be a rare sight of the Garden, However it came from the nearest {{R|5}} spawn. ''We can't explain this but someone screenshotted it.''

''For its special variants, see [[Beetle/Nazar|Nazar Beetle]], [[Hel Beetle]], [[Beetle/Mummy|Mummy Beetle]], or [[Beetle/Pharaoh|Pharaoh Beetle]].''

The '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is a [[Mobs|mob]] in florr.io. It is a fairly standard mob that deals high damage on contact. It is fast and aggressive, but it turns quite slow.

== Appearance ==
A purple oval with a dark purple outline. It has a slightly curved stripe going down the middle from head to back, not quite reaching both sides. There are six dots (three on each side) surrounding the stripe. The stripe and dots are also dark purple.

On its head, there are two pincers which are crescent shaped ending in a rounded tip. They are pointed slightly towards each other. Only about half the crescent shows. When aggressive, they move towards each other and back out again repeatedly.

Beetles spawned from [[Beetle Egg|Eggs]] are yellow.

=== Asset Change ===
For a short amount of time, '''Beetles''' were called '''''Buttles''''', along with that, they had a butt on their back.

* Beetles' asset was changed on 12/26/24, then changed back at around 12/30/24.

== Stats & Behavior ==
'''Behavior:''' When passive, wanders around randomly, moving a few tiles forward before stopping and changing directions.

Upon a flower or friendly mob entering a medium range around it or being attacked, it becomes aggressive. Beetles spawned from [[Beetle Egg|Eggs]] are provoked by nearby mobs and hostile flowers.

==Spawning==
Beetles of any rarity can spawn anywhere in [[Desert]], but Mythic and Ultra Beetles will spawn mostly in the bottom of Desert.  

Beetles can also spawn from [[Beetle Egg|Eggs]](Beetle).

==Drops ==
''Note: Beetles spawned from Eggs don't drop petals.''

===[[Beetle Egg|Egg]]===

{{:Beetle/Drop_Egg}}

=== [[Privet Berry]] ===

{{:Beetle/ Drop Privet}}

==Strategy==

* After the 2023-01-21 update, beetles move faster than [[Flower|players]]. If the beetles chasing a player are of lower rarities than the player's petals, it is possible to kill them. However, if one is of the same rarity or above, it is imperative to use petals that can slow them down or knock them back, e.g. [[Pincer]] or [[Web]]. Pincer is sufficient even when a player is chased by multiple beetles of the same rarity or above, as one slowed down beetle will slow the rest down.
** A single [[Bubble]] will also work.
*A single, high-rarity Beetle is best dealt with using a Pincer and a DPS build, but swarms of weaker Beetles warrant Webs, and perhaps also [[Pollen]]. Remember they also are usually accompanied by Scorpions, which can use their missiles to force you into the Beetle(s).
*Beetles are capable of dealing high damage. One beetle is usually not a problem, but they can be if multiple are encountered at once or are with other mobs. Contact with them deals heavy damage.
**Beetles can tank the damage from more dangerous [[Scorpion]]s that deal poison damage, making them extremely dangerous if both are encountered simultaneously.
*Despite being fast, it takes around 20-30 seconds for a Beetle to outrun a player from an average distance, meaning stalling petals ([[Web]], [[Pincer]], etc.) can be very helpful in defeating or escaping a beetle.
**If you are using the Web, be in defensive position in case it draws close, as going in attacking position will throw the petal into an unpredictable direction and possibly missing the beetle.
*Try to take out beetles before they become a threat.
*Take advantage of [[Cactus|Cacti]] and [[Desert Centipede]]s to impede beetles' movement. This works especially well with rarer beetles due to their larger hitbox.
*Beetles turn slowly, so it helps sometimes to move in a circle around it, especially with more mobs around that you don't want to run into. You may need [[Mysterious Powder|Powder]] and several [[Bubble|Bubbles]] to do that.
*[[Beetle Egg]]s of one rarity higher with the Summoner Talent can also work against the Beetle.

=== Super Beetle ===

* The super beetle itself isn't hard, but the Ultra mobs around it that are a threat. It is advised to use [[Sponge]] and a few Ultra healing petals. 
* It is also advised to form a "megasquad" where players group together to kill the mob. It increases the chances of all players surviving.
* Players usually like to lure the Beetle into easier areas so that other mobs won't be as much as a threat.
* When killing the mob, use a high DPS build and a pincer, as other players most likely to use web and pincer.
* It is advised to rather stay in front or behind the beetle. Don't stay on the side as they could turn and hit you anytime. Do not go too close as they have a very large hitbox. 

==Gallery==

==Trivia== 
*Along with the [[Cactus]] and [[Shiny Ladybug]], it is one of the only mobs in [[Desert]] which originated from legacy florr.io. The remainder are from new florr.io.
*Beetles used to drop [[Talisman]] and [[Ankh]]<nowiki/>.
*The {{R|Epic}} rarity looks like the Massive Beetle from legacy florr.io. It has 18 times higher health stats though.
*Beetles used to chase in straight lines instead of turning. They also used to be slower than the current ones.
*On 26th December 2024, Beetles used to be temporarily replaced with Buttles, which were Beetles but had butts on the back. This also applied to [[Hel Beetle]]s.
**This was reverted a few days later.
**They also dropped [[Poo]] during that time.

* So far the Beetle has the second most variants.. Currently they are Normal, [[Hel Beetle|Hel]], [[Beetle/Nazar|Nazar]], [[Beetle/Mummy|Mummy]], and the [[Beetle/Pharaoh|Pharaoh]], which makes the total 5.
* The Beetle also moves slowly while turning so using summons to distract them works.

The '''Hel Beetle''' is a mob in florr.io. It is essentially the same as the [[Beetle]], except that it has higher health, has the  capability to teleport, and spawns in [[Hel]] instead of the [[Desert]]. 

==Appearance==
It looks like a regular Beetle, except that it is dark red in color and is a little bigger than a normal Beetle. The pincers on the front are replaced with a realistic mouth with five sharp black teeth that move around slightly.
<!-- ‘I miss when they had pincers and weren’t so terrifying’-CrystalKai12345/I-Like-Jadeite -->

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Like the Beetle, it is faster than base flower speed, but it turns very slowly.

'''Behavior:''' Remains passive until spotting a player. Once spotted, Hel Beetle will give chase to the player. It has recently gained the ability to teleport, with a pentacle showing where it's going to teleport.

It has the same stats as the [[Beetle]], but with 1.5x the health.

==Spawning==
The Hel Beetle spawns exclusively in [[Hel]]. It spawns alongside [[Spider/Hel|Hel Spider]], [[Centipede/Hel|Hel Centipede]], and [[Wasp/Hel|Hel Wasp]]. Beware while exploring [[Hel]], as swarms of Hel Beetles may appear which can be dangerous for low-level players (especially {{Rarity|Legendary}} and {{Rarity|Mythic}} ones).

==Drops==
===[[Dark Mark]]===
{{:Hel Beetle/Drop Dark Mark}}

=== [[Beetle Egg]] ===
{{:Hel Beetle/Drop Egg}}

===[[Dice]]===
{{:Hel Beetle/Drop Dice}}

==Strategy==
The Hel Beetle is hard to defeat due to its fast movement speed, high HP, high body damage, and ability to teleport directly on top of you. Players should use petals that can slow down mobs, such as [[Web]], [[Pincer]], [[Heavy]], and Mythic+ [[Jelly]] (not very advised to use Jelly as it has little knockback against Legendary+ Hel Beetles). When fighting Hel Beetles, players need to be vigilant of other players, since [[Hel]] is a PvP zone. On top of that, they also have to be wary of running into large groups of Hel beetles, since in swarms, they can easily overwhelm and kill a flower and also can't be slowed down, as Hel Beetles behind push them forwards. {{Rarity|Mythic|Mythic+}} [[Rubber]] is also useful as it can provide knockback and prevent multiple hits from a Hel Beetle. Since Hel is a PVP zone, it is advised to use Sponge, which can prevent you from dying instantly to other players. 

Another way is to use the "hit and run" technique, which involves a combination of [[Bubble|Bubbles]] and burst damage. You hit the mob with burst damage petals, run, and repeat. 

Its not recommended to grind for [[Beetle Egg|Beetle Eggs]] from Hel Beetles, since they have a lower drop rate that regular [[Beetle]]s. Players should only grind here if they want to get [[Mark]], [[Basic]]s or [[Dice]].

Don’t use Powder. Even at {{Rarity|Ultra}} or {{Rarity|Super}} rarity, it will disable around {{Rarity|Ultra|Ultra+}} mobs, making it useless against {{Rarity|Mythic|Mythic+}} Beetles.

Its also recommended to use {{Rarity|Mythic}}+ [[Bubble]]s to dodge teleporting Hel Beetles. But you must react quickly as it teleports quickly, and beware that the Hel Beetle's hitbox extends a bit further front than visual.

If you are really skilled(or just lucky), dash towards the wall using Bubbles exactly before the Beetle teleports will confuse it and make it teleport into the wall.

==Trivia==
*On June 16, 2024 update, Hel Beetle's stats were nerfed from having drastically higher health and damage that a regular Beetle.
**On an unaccounted date, the Beetles also became able to teleport, probably as to make up for their slightly lowered stats.
*On June 12, 2023, [[M28]] changed the name of the name of this to be "[[Beetle]]" instead, same goes with "[[Desert Centipede]]"
*The Beetle (Hel) was the only mob that spawned in [[Hel]] before the update that added the [[Hel Centipede]], [[Gambler]], [[Hel Spider]], and [[Hel Wasp]].
*The message for a Super Beetle (Hel) is '''You sense ominous vibrations coming from a different realm...'''
** It also shares this with [[Hel Spider]], [[Hel Centipede]], and [[Hel Wasp]].
*It moves as fast as a regular [[Beetle]], and is as tanky as a [[Soldier Termite]].
*The Beetle (Hel) was originally designed to look like a normal [[Beetle]] but the color is dark red.
*On December 26, 2024, Hel Beetles used to be temporarily replaced with Buttles, which were Beetles but had butts on the back. This also applies to regular [[Beetle]]s.
**This was reverted several hours later.
**They also dropped [[Poo]] during that time.
*This mob can be spawned by [[Beetle Egg]] when used with [[Corruption]].
*With the mobs health buff update, this mob is now more dangerous than ever, due to the ability of teleporting and faster speed.
*Just like Einstein Hornets, Hel Beetles can foresee where you’ll get to and place the teleport mark there.

title: Beetle/Mummy
content:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Mummy Beetle}}{{Mob Template Infobox | name = Mummy Beetle | image = File:Mummy Beetle (Common).png|C File:Mummy Beetle (Unusual).png|U File:Mummy Beetle (Rare).png|R File:Mummy Beetle (Epic).png|E File:Mummy Beetle (Legendary).png|L File:Mummy Beetle (Mythic).png|M File:Mummy Beetle (Ultra).png|Ul File:Mummy Beetle (Super).png|S File:Mummy Beetle (Unique).png|Uq | description = This one seems like it shouldn't be alive... | health = {{Infobox Colors Empty|100|375|1.4k|5.4k|32.4k|243k|2.9m|131m|Uq}} | damage = {{Infobox Colors Empty|30|90|270|810|2.4k|7.3k|21.9k|65.6k|196.8k}} | armor = {{Mob Armor Scale|0.8|0}} | xp-reward = {{Infobox Colors Empty|1|1|4|28|256|4.1k|39.2k|S|Uq}} | move = Yes, faster than the player. | status = Hostile | attack = Body Damage | spawn = [[Pyramid]], [[Desert]] | special = Turns undead upon death for a duration before dying. | drops = [[Beetle Egg]]
[[Bandage]]
[[Ankh]] }} The '''Mummy Beetle''' is a [[Mobs|mob]] from [https://florr.io florr.io]. The mob is a variation of [[Beetle]], but it appears like a mummy, and it becomes [[Undead|'''{{Text shadow|Undead|color=#83b05d}}''']] upon receiving fatal damage. ==Appearance== Mummy Beetles are a slightly pale green recolored version of [[Beetle]], with beige [[Bandage]]s wrapped around its body. It has also inspired the design of the [[Beetle/Pharaoh|Pharaoh Beetle]], a boss variant of the Mummy Beetle. ==Stats & Behavior== '''Speed:''' Same as [[Beetle]]; faster than the player. '''Behavior:''' Same as [[Beetle]]; aggroing on the nearest player and chasing them in a straight line. {| class="article-table" |+Mob Stats ![[Rarity]] !Health !Body Damage !Armor !Invulnerability period !XP Reward |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |100 |30 |1 |1s |1 |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |375 |90 |2 |2s |1 |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |1350 |270 |7 |3s |4 |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |5400 |810 |22 |4s |20 |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |32400 |2430 |65 |5s |256 |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |243000 |7290 |194 |6s |4.1k |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |2187000 |21870 | rowspan="2" |583 |7s |39.2k |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |131220000 |65610 |8s |? |} Mummy Beetle has a special ability on all rarities: when all of its health lost, it won't die instantly, instead it will remain undead (invincible) for a certain amount of time, and then automatically dies. Its undead duration scales from 1 second to 9 seconds, depending on the rarity. ==Spawning== The '''Mummy Beetle''' spawns from the entrance to the [[Pyramid]] in the [[Desert]]. It also spawns abundantly in the [[Pyramid]], along with [[Beetle/Nazar|Nazar Beetles]]. ==Drops== === [[Lentil]] === {{:Firefly/Drop Battery}} === [[Ankh]] === {{ID 6}} ===[[Bandage]]=== {| class="article-table" !Mob Rarity !{{Rarity|Mythic}} !{{Rarity|Ultra}} |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |0.5 | |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |8.9 |0.3 |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |85.6 |14.3 |} ==Strategy== *Pairing [[Bandage]] with a ram build can deal lots of extra damage, especially if you have ram talents on. This makes it a good choice once your second chance has been used up. *Mummy Beetles become '''{{Text shadow|Undead|color=#83b05d}}''' after suffering a fatal hit, which is annoying. **On low rarities, this is not problematic, and summons could be used to tank the hits, but on high rarities it can easily overwhelm squads of flowers. **This is especially annoying for {{Rarity|Super}} fights because the {{Rarity|Super}} will survive longer, giving other mobs more time to kill the flowers before it dies. **[[Pincer]] can be used to make Mummy Beetles less painful to kill. [[Jelly]] or even [[Heavy]] due to its tankiness and multi-hit capability can be used to knockback beetles, but this won't work very well on high rarities. **Be careful while bubbling around, because a small amount of any rarity (but {{Rarity|Rare|text=Rare+}} are more dangerous because they're '''{{Text shadow|Undead|color=#83b05d}}''' for enough time to kill you) can swarm you and kill you in seconds because they'll be '''{{Text shadow|Undead|color=#83b05d}}''' and invincible while they destroy you. ** === {{Rarity|Super}} Mummy Beetle === * The {{Rarity|Super}} beetle itself isn't hard, but the {{Rarity|Ultra}} mobs around it that are a threat. It is advised to use [[Sponge]] and a few {{Rarity|Ultra}} healing petals. * It is also advised to form a "megasquad" where players group together to kill the mob. It increases the chances of all players surviving. * Players usually like to lure the Beetle into easier areas so that other mobs won't be as much as a threat. * When killing the mob, use a high DPS build and a pincer, as other players most likely to use [[Web]] and [[Pincer]]. * It is advised to rather stay in front or behind the beetle. Don't stay on the side as they could turn and hit you anytime. Do not go too close as they have a very large hitbox. * It is strongly advised to clear majority of the mobs around as their '''{{Text shadow|Undead|color=#83b05d}}''' ability is a huge advantage for them. They could overwhelm you. ==Gallery== File:Mummy Beetle (Common).png|{{Rarity|1}} Mummy Beetle. File:Mummy Beetle (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|2}} Mummy Beetle. File:Mummy Beetle (Rare).png|{{Rarity|3}} Mummy Beetle. File:Mummy Beetle (Epic).png|{{Rarity|4}} Mummy Beetle. File:Mummy Beetle (Legendary).png|{{Rarity|5}} Mummy Beetle. File:Mummy Beetle (Mythic).png|{{Rarity|6}} Mummy Beetle. File:Mummy Beetle (Ultra).png|{{Rarity|7}} Mummy Beetle. File:Mummy Beetle (Super).png|{{Rarity|8}} Mummy Beetle. File:Mummy Beetle (Unique).png|{{Rarity|9}} Mummy Beetle. File:Mummy Beetle.png|Mummy Beetle. ==Trivia== * This mob used to have 7 seconds invulnerability period for all rarities before [[Pyramid]] was accessible. * There is another variant of Mummy Beetle which has a Pharaoh's crown on its body. [[Pharaoh Beetle]] only exist at {{Rarity|Super}} and {{Rarity|Unique}}rarity. * Somewhere around the [[Triangle]] update, the [[Ankh]] became a unique drop to this mob and other Pyramid mobs (Since [[Beetle]] now drops [[Privet Berry]]). {{Mobs}} [[Category:Beetles]] [[Category:Pyramid]] [[Category:Mobs]] [[Category:Non-stationary mobs]] [[Category:Desert]] [[Category:Bandage]] [[Category:Ankh]] [[Category:Eggs]] [[Category:Undead]]

The '''Nazar Beetle''' is a rare variant of [[Beetle|Beetles]] in florr.io that spawns in [[Desert]] and in [[Pyramid]].

==Appearance==
The Nazar Beetle is a Beetle, with a body which is reminiscent of the [[Nazar Amulet]] petal. The eye on its back constantly changes in size. It has blue, slightly square bodies and the Nazar pattern spins in place. 

==Stats and Behavior ==
'''Speed:''' Normal when passive, fast when provoked.

'''Behavior:''' Nazar Beetles are neutral, meaning they only attack the player once they have been attacked. {{Rarity|Ultra|text=Ultra+}} Nazar Beetles are aggressive and will chase anyone who gets close. They can resist 98% of the damage dealt by players in 3 hits. These hits recharge.

==Strategy==

* These act exactly the same as regular Beetles (with the only differences having a different look and only aggroed when attacked), so you could use the same strategies.
* The strategy for dealing with them is completely different compared to normal beetles. Because they are neutral, bulb is recommended to lure them to you.
* Beware though as other players could rush and kill them, just like [[Ladybug (Desert)|Shiny Ladybugs]].
* Nazar Beetles turn slightly slower than normal Beetles, which makes it possible to rotate around it while damaging it without dying.

=== Super Nazar Beetle ===

* The Super Nazar Beetle itself isn't hard, but the Ultra mobs around it that are a threat. It is advised to use [[Sponge]] and a few Ultra healing petals.
* It is also advised to form a "megasquad" where players group together to kill the mob. It increases the chances of all players surviving.
* Players usually like to lure the Nazar Beetle into easier areas so that other mobs won't be as much as a threat.
* When killing the mob, use a high DPS build as probably lots of other players most likely to also want to loot.

==Trivia== 
* Nazar Beetles used to look like [[Beetle]]s with a golden recolor, much like [[Ladybug (Desert)|Desert Ladybug]], and were called "Golden Beetle". However, it was changed to the current appearance later in the day it was added to be more noticeable and to reduce confusion with [[Beetle Egg|player-spawned Beetles]].
* Aside from [[Centipede|Centipedes]], Nazar Beetles are the only mob that can skip rarities in their drops.
* In early May of 2025, Nazar Beetles spawn rate was increased significantly as obtaining any rarity Nazar Amulet higher than Legendary was nearly impossible.
* Nazar Beetles spawn more often in the Pyramid than the Desert.

The '''Pharaoh Beetle''' is a [[Mobs|mob]] from [https://florr.io florr.io]. It is a special boss that spawns in the [[Pyramid]] that replaces {{Rarity|Super}} [[Beetle/Mummy|Mummy Beetles]]. It drops [[Ankh]], [[Bandage]], and [[Pharaoh's Crown]].

==Appearance==
The Pharaoh Beetle looks like a [[Beetle/Mummy|Mummy Beetle]] wearing the Nemes, or a pharaoh's crown.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed: ''' Faster than the player, but turns slowly{{Citation needed}}

'''Behavior:''' Aggressive
{| class="article-table"
|+Mob Stats
![[Rarity]]
!Health
!Body Damage
!Armor
!XP Reward
|-
|{{Rarity|Super}}
|131220000
|65610
|583
|13m
|}
The Pharaoh Beetle summons undead Mummy Beetles from underground at random places near it to fight for the Pharaoh Beetle. These Beetles' rarities are 2 rarities lower than the Pharaoh Beetle, which are at the {{Rarity|Mythic}} rarity.

Its summon ability is same as Pharaoh's Crown function.

==Spawning==
The Pharaoh Beetle spawns in the [[Pyramid]] every three hours. The spawn message comes out as a regular beetle spawn message.   

==Drops==
===[[Pharaoh's Crown]]===
{| class="article-table"
!Mob Rarity
!{{Rarity|Unique}}
|-
|{{Rarity|Super}}
|0.4%
|-
|{{Rarity|Unique}}
|1%
|}

== Strategy ==
*Sponge is advised for this fight since the Pharaoh Beetle spawns [[Beetle/Mummy|Mummy Beetle]] minions, getting damaged is very likely. Shovel is also useful when the beetle isn't slowed and is aggroed on you, so you can remove the aggro from the Pharaoh Beetle as well as its minions. Lightning or Magic Eye are also recommended because you can keep dealing damage to the Pharaoh beetle even when it has minions in front of it. It is also recommended to use Bubble or Magic Bubble so that you don't get surrounded by its minions. You can also use Light Bulb to know which location the minions spawned in. Never go to the corners of a pathway when you're fighting a Pharaoh Beetle, otherwise, you might get trapped by it pretty easily.
*The Pharaoh Beetle drops the [[Pharaoh's Crown|Pharaoh's crown]], [[Ankh]] and [[Bandage]]. Since the [[Pharaoh's Crown|Pharaoh's crown]] has a very small Crown drop chance, and will only drop if the player has at least 4 Super petal, it mostly isn't viable to farm Pharaoh Beetles for their crown. However, the Pharaoh Beetle's rate for {{Rarity|Ultra}} [[Ankh]] and [[Bandage]] is really high, with a quite high xp reward, so it is still worth it to kill Pharaoh Beetles.
*Since so many [[Beetle/Mummy|Mummy Beetle]]<nowiki/>s are spawned by the Pharaoh Beetle, it is recommended to use ultra [[lightning]] and above to ensure that at least some of your petals' damage reaches the Pharaoh Beetle. Using multiple ultra lightning can stun multiple mythic beetles spawned by the Pharaoh. Third eye should also be used along with lightning to keep a safe distance away from the pharaoh.  Do NOT lure the Pharaoh Beetle close to spawn, as you will be forced to disconnect and respawn at the Desert spawn if you press Continue too close to the spawnpoint.

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185">
File:Pharaoh Beetle (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|Unusual}} Pharaoh Beetle (Doesn't exist).
File:Pharaoh Beetle (Super).png|{{Rarity|8}} Pharaoh Beetle.
File:Pharaoh Beetle (Unique).png|{{Rarity|9}} Pharaoh Beetle.
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*Pharaoh Beetle is one of the most hunted mobs, due to their valuable drop: Pharaoh's Crown, which is also described in their description.
*The Pharaoh Beetle is the first non-{{Rarity|Common}} mob that can drop a rarity higher than itself, and the only mob that can drop a petal above {{Rarity|Ultra}} rarity.
*Pharaoh Beetle is the first and currently only mob that only spawns at {{R|8}}+ rarity.
*It is said that having at least 4 {{Rarity|Super}} petals makes you able to obtain Crown via Pharaoh Beetle. However, even with {{R|8}} petals, you will still have an unfathomably low chance of obtaining the [[Pharaoh's Crown]]. However, due to recent balancing updates, the chances were increased by x2, making it possible to get the [[Pharaoh's Crown]] on a 0.2% chance.

''Not to be confused with the [[Ant Egg]], which shares the same shortened in-game name, but spawns four [[Soldier Ant]]s instead.''

The '''Beetle Egg''', also called in-game as just the '''Egg''', is a legacy [[Petals|petal]] from [https://florr.io/ florr.io]. The petal itself is very weak and doesn't deal any damage, but once broken, it hatches a friendly [[Beetle]] mob, which targets any hostile entity close to its user. The Beetle Egg is dropped by [[Beetle|Beetles]], [[Hel Beetle]]s, [[Beetle/Nazar|Nazar Beetles]], and [[Beetle/Mummy|Mummy Beetles]].

To avoid confusion with a similar petal and similar mobs, this page will refer to this petal as the '''Beetle Egg''' rather than just being referred to as '''Egg'''. '''Egg''' will be used to refer to the Egg itself rather than the petal as a whole.

==Appearance==  
*The Beetle Egg appears to be an oval shaped egg with a pale yellow body color and a darker outline. With every increasing rarity, the egg will slightly increase in size.
*The friendly Beetle spawned from this appears the same as regular Beetles, but its body (including its borders) is yellow-colored rather than purple, indicating it's a friendly mob. The purple dots from the hostile Beetle are also colored yellow here. The yellow color is close to the flowers' yellow colors to represent this.

|10,935
|-
|{{Rarity|Unique}}
|5,904,900
|32,805
|}
The Beetle Egg will spend a prolonged time into cooldown time, but once it spawns, it will have to break to activate its ability: the Beetle Egg will hatch into a friendly Beetle. When there are no entities nearby, the Beetle will wander around the flower like a normal Beetle without any flowers nearby, but if the flower goes too far away, it will follow the mob. When other entities are nearby, the friendly Beetles will attack all mobs and hostile flowers. Beetles prioritize their targets if there's are many types of them, focusing on hostile mobs, then neutral mobs, then passive mobs, then finally flowers.

[[Hel Beetle|Hel Beetles]] can be spawned if the player is [[Corruption|corrupted]].

==Obtainment==
===[[Beetle]]===
{{:Beetle/Drop Egg}}

===[[Hel Beetle]]===
{{:Hel Beetle/Drop Egg}}

=== [[Beetle/Nazar|Nazar Beetle]] ===
{{:Template:ID_1}}
===Purchasing===
On certain days, Beetle Egg can be purchased in the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|3}}

==Strategy== 
*Beetle Eggs are a viable choice, especially in the early areas of many zones, however, the Beetle Eggs' biggest con is their very long cooldown timer, specially at {{Rarity|Ultra}} and {{Rarity|Super}} rarities, meaning it could be unreliable against crowds. Still, the friendly Beetles have plenty of HP, and you can simply use multiple Beetle Eggs or one-two of this petal alongside other petals (like [[Stinger]]s) to mitigate their long cooldown.
*Beetle Eggs can be seen as ranged petals, as while they can't be shot, the friendly Beetles will be able to move around, meaning they could attack other mobs at long distances. However, this could be detrimental to you, as hostile mobs around can lock on the Beetle, kill it and then lock on you, potentially killing you if you're unprepared. Always make sure to not draw too much attention, or to be able to counter said crowds. Neutral mobs (such as the [[Worker Ant]]s, which will lock onto you once the friendly Beetle that attacked it dies, even if there are other friendly mobs around).
*Compared to the [[Ant Egg]] and the [[Mysterious Stick]] (the other two summoner petals), the Beetle Egg takes the least time to hatch a friendly mob, meaning that it will protect yourself quicker than other summoners, whereas the Ant Eggs will take a little longer for that, and the friendly [[Sandstorm]]s from the Sticks are quite unreliable for that. The Beetle also has pretty good stats, which means it can kill most mobs and survive with some HP left, or can heavily injure the mobs after dying. Beetles do not only have higher body damage than Soldier Ants, but also have a faster speed than them. These characteristics make it a good choice for beginner summoners. However, the Beetle Egg only consists of 1 Egg, which can spawn only 1 Beetle at a time, meaning that Beetle Egg strategies should mostly include multiple of them.
*[[Bur]] can be used to further help the Beetles damage and kill the other mobs.
*Most Beetle Egg builds will have them mixed with Ant Eggs, lacking any other attacking/defensive/healing petals. It's advised to bring those type of petals in your secondary loadout to avoid risking everything with the friendly mobs.
*Generally, Beetle Eggs are good distraction petals with their Beetles, and can take pretty much anything on their own. However, specially if you are using {{Rarity|Super}} Eggs, you'll be weakened for too long before hatching more Beetles. It's best if you bring a squad with you, or use 2-3 Beetle Eggs and other petals to assist you.

===Obtaining===
*The only way to obtain Beetle Eggs is, of course, killing Beetles. Remember to always maintain distance from them, as they have high speed and can kill you if you let them. Be sure to have a [[Jelly]], a multi-hit like [[Heavy]] and/or a [[Rubber]] to constantly avoid ram damage, or [[Disc]] to mitigate it. 
**[[Hel Beetle]]s are extra dangerous, not only due to the scenario it is present, but also because they can teleport close to (or on) your current location, severely hurting or even killing you if you can't outrun them. Bring some [[Bubble]]s and maybe [[Cactus|Cacti]] to have a chance at surviving them.
*Just like the Ant Egg and [[Ant Egg (Mob)|its mob counterpart]], Beetle Eggs are good for farming themselves, as they can distract and kill Beetles and Hel Beetles of the same rarity (though they will die in the process).

{{Zone|Garden}}
*The Beetle Egg is very efficient in fending off [[Hornet]]s at a range. The Beetle Egg user should try to assist the Beetle, as it might take too long to respawn and protect you again.
*Beetles are also good for distracting and even taking out [[Bee]]s and [[Spider|Spiders]] which can otherwise cause big trouble to you.
*A handful of Beetle Eggs can quickly destroy an [[Ant Hole]] and all Ants spawned from it.

{{Zone|Desert}}
*The Beetle Egg is specially useful for killing Sandstorms with ease, which is highly beneficial for both [[Glass]]/[[Sand]] farming and for efficiency, since Sandstorms are very unpredictable, and can instantly kill you if they ram into you. However, be careful when dealing with {{Rarity|Mythic}}+ Sandstorms, as they are faster and have bigger hitboxes, plus, {{Rarity|Super}} Sandstorms can pull you into their ramming radius.
*Friendly Beetles are also good for taking [[Beetle|their hostile counterparts]] (as mentioned above) as well as [[Desert Centipede]]s and [[Soldier Fire Ant]]s (from [[Fire Ant Burrow]]s). They can kill the former and die in the process if they're of the same rarity, while the last two will be shredded due to the tanky stats of the friendly Beetle.
*This can also be used to distract [[Scorpion]]s, as they can tank both the stingers and the resulting poison damage while still having enough HP to potentially kill them.

{{Zone|Ocean}} 
*A single friendly Beetle may be able to mitigate quite a handful of lightning discharges from a [[Jellyfish]], avoiding lightning bounces and even killing the mob itself.
**Be aware that the Jellyfish's lightning discharges can potentially harm multiple friendly mobs at once. The spawned mobs can also cause the discharges to chain onto the player when they'd otherwise be out of range.
*Beetle Eggs can also be used to distract and deal with [[Crab]]s and [[Leech]]es, matching up with their HP stat and avoiding them killing you. A less viable strategy would be using this against {{Rarity|Ultra}}+ [[Shell (Mob)|Shells]] to distract them abd potentially mitigate their fired Pearls.
*Beetle Eggs can deal with [[Starfish (Mob)|Starfish]], but it may not deal enough damage to fully kill them. Pairing them with [[Dandelion]]s and assisting them with glass petals like [[Stinger]]s should be enough most of the time.

{{Zone|Ant Hell}}
*Beetle Eggs specially do very well in all Ant Hells. Beetles will be able to tank multiple Ants and Ant Egg mobs before dying, and while tanking less Fire Ants, they can still do well against them. Termites, however, will be shredded by the friendly Beetles, as they can tank a lot of damage from Termites, while dealing massive damage to all Termites due to their Psyonic Connection.
*The friendly Beetles are also quite useful for dealing with [[Queen Ant]]s, [[Queen Fire Ant]]s and [[Termite Overmind]]s.

{{Zone|Hel}}
===Using=== 
*Slow down chasing enemies by unleashing Beetles against them, dealing knockback and massive damage.
*Despite being good in PvE, players are much more skilled (and also stronger), so unless you're using slowing petals, weakening petals, or higher rarity Beetle Eggs, they're pretty much useless on PvP, specially due to the petal's massive respawn time.
*The spawned Beetles target other mobs over flowers, so try to fight in areas without any distractions, or deal with them yourself.
*If you have {{Rarity|Mythic}}+ Beetles, there are a couple strategies you could try using:
**One strategy is to use [[Pincer]] or [[Web]]s to slow down players, which puts them in the perfect position to be killed by the Beetles and your supporting petals.
**Another strategy is to use [[Bubble]]s to push the Beetles into players. Just know that ramming builds can withstand getting hit by the Beetle multiple times and can heal regularly, so take them out carefully.
*On PvE, Beetle Eggs are useful against all Hel mobs here being able to distract and tank them with ease while killing them alone.
*{{Rarity|Ultra}}+ Beetle Eggs can take out the {{Rarity|Super}} [[Bone]] as the {{Rarity|Mythic}} Beetle's damage is higher than the Super Bone's Armor.

===Against===
*One of the most viable strategies is to not kill the friendly Beetles. Instead, let them chase you, so you don't need to waste your time killing them, but rather waste full power against the Beetle Egg user. 
**Killing the Beetles will cause more to respawn in the perfect position to ram the player, dealing heavy damage and knockback.
*Since friendly Beetles prioritize all mobs over flowers, try to fight them on places where there are a lot of mobs to avoid being targeted as well.
*If the Beetles spawned are of {{Rarity|Legendary}} rarity or above and you can handle killing them, it's best to do so, since then the Beetle Egg will take very long to respawn, thus opening a weak spot on the player where you can kill them.
*Beware for any slowing petals a Beetle Egg user may have, as they might cause you to ram into the Beetles and kill you. Also beware of Bubble users bouncing the Beetle until it rams you.
*Use [[Jelly]] or multi-hit petals like the [[Heavy]] to keep them from dealing massive damage at you.
*Try to also bring [[Lightning]] (or [[Battery]] and ram damage builds) with you, as the attack has the potential to bounce from the mobs to the player who summoned them, specially if there are multiple Beetles.
*Treat these the same way you would treat normal Beetles in Desert.
*Do not use Bones against {{Rarity|Ultra}} Beetle Eggs since the Mythic Beetle's damage is higher than the Super Bone's Armor.

{{Zone|Jungle}}
*Beetle Eggs are very useful for distracting both [[Mantis]]es and [[Wasp]]s as well as tanking their projectiles. They can also obliterate every other mob here (specially [[Leafbug]]s despite their Armor stat), including Termites from [[Termite Mound]]s.

{{Zone|Sewers}} 
*Friendly Beetles are also highly useful on Sewers, as they can obliterate [[Fly|Flies]], [[Moth]]s and Spiders alike, and although [[Roach|Roaches]] might have a chance on aggroing onto you, Beetles will most likely kill them before that happens, as Beetles are stronger than Roaches.

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">
File:Beetle Egg (Common).png|{{rarity|1}} Beetle Egg.
File:Beetle Egg (Unusual).png|{{rarity|2}} Beetle Egg.
File:Beetle Egg (Rare).png|{{rarity|3}} Beetle Egg.

File:Screenshot 2025-01-05 162941.png|An example of summoned mobs from a Beetle Egg. This type of [[Beetle]] is only achieved by using a [[Corruption]] while summoning a Beetle.
File:Image (12).png|Another example of summoned mobs from a Beetle Egg. This type of [[Beetle]] is only achieved by using a [[Corruption]] while summoning a Beetle. 
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*On 06/02/2025, Beetle Egg mechanic were changed. Now Beetle Egg only spawns friendly Beetle upon collision with a mob.
*The Beetle Egg, the Ant Egg, the [[Mysterious Stick]] and the [[Square]] are the only petals who spawn friendly mobs. Curiously, those petals summon the mobs that happen to drop their respective petals.
*Since mobs cannot go into walls unless there is the presence of a glitch, the petals of the Beetle Egg, the Ant Egg and the Stick won't go past the wall.
*After an update, the Beetle Egg and the Stick spawn smaller versions of their normal counterparts.
*When the Eggs are on cooldown or have hatched mobs, they do not count as a petal orbiting the flower, thus causing the distance between other petals to increase. Beetle and Ant Eggs are the only petals to do so, unlike with how many other petals decreasing distance among themselves, like [[Light]] and [[Pollen]] at {{Rarity|Unusual}} rarities.
*The legacy Florr.io and older re-released Florr.io versions did not spawn mobs that were re-colored to be yellow, but rather looked identical to their hostile counterparts.
*The Mythic Beetle Egg has gone through several reworks, first spawning 2 {{Rarity|Rare}} [[Hornet]]s per petal, then 2 {{Rarity|Epic}} Beetles, and currently, a single {{Rarity|Legendary}} Beetle.
*Summoned mobs will deal damage to other players' summoned mobs. This is to prevent huge numbers of summoned mobs from taking up entire zones.
*Beetle Eggs at higher rarities grow in size. It is actually hard to see it until you compare a Common Beetle Egg with a Super one. Thus, this is the only petal to change sizes on all rarities, though it doesn't change its other characteristics (color, shape, etc…) like the other four petals that change their appearance ([[Card]], [[Chip]], [[Dice]] and [[Glass]]). Thus, the Beetle Egg is the only petal to change its size depending on the rarity.
*Multiple players have initially bought Super Beetle Eggs from the shop, making this the first Super petal to be obtained by a player.
*Starting at {{Rarity|Legendary}} rarity, all Beetles hatched from Beetle Eggs will be one rarity lower than the petal. This is shared with the Ant Egg's multiple Eggs starting at {{Rarity|Rare}} rarity.
**Surprisingly, Beetle Egg and Ant Egg have interesting stats: the Beetle Egg has a cooldown of 17 seconds at {{Rarity|Common}} rarity and spawns Common Beetles, the {{Rarity|Unusual}} rarity has a cooldown of 21.8 seconds and spawns Unusual Beetles, the Rare rarity has a cooldown of 26.5 seconds and spawns Rare Beetles, the {{Rarity|Epic}} rarity has a cooldown of 36.0 seconds and spawns Epic Beetles and the {{Rarity|Legendary}} rarity has a 2.2 second cooldown and spawns {{Rarity|Epic}} Beetles. This means that its cooldown rises, then falls to 2.2 seconds and then begins raising again to up to 82.4 seconds. Ant Egg also shares this, as its cooldown timers go from 30 seconds, to 38 seconds and then 2 seconds at {{Rarity|Rare}} rarity, then increasing again as normal, spawning {{Rarity|Common}}, Unusual and {{Rarity|Rare}} [[Soldier Ant]]s (or [[Soldier Termite]]s). They are the only two petals to have this weird oscillation, as not even the Stick has this.
**At {{Rarity|Unique}} Rarity, the Beetle Egg still spawns a {{Rarity|Ultra}} Beetle. Most likely because it would be too overpowered if it spawned a {{Rarity|Super}} Beetle.
*This petal is taken directly from legacy florr: the Beetle Egg is inspired by the old [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Egg_(Legendary) Legendary Egg] from there (with the name and the Egg's appearance exactly like the modern day Beetle Egg) which hatched into a friendly Beetle exactly like a hostile [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Beetle Beetle] from that game. It dropped from an old, massive variation of the Beetle called the [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Massive_Beetle Massive Beetle], which can be argued that technically came back as a modern day {{Rarity|Epic}}+ Beetle. 
**The Ant Egg also came from that same version: it was also called the [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Egg Egg] at that time, dropped at the {{Rarity|Epic}} rarity and hatched into a friendly Soldier Ant equal to a hostile one. 
***Both of those spawned friendly and normal-colored variations of their respective mobs as opposed to yellow-colored variants of this.
*After a month without any updates (date?), the developer [[M28]] nerfed Beetle Egg by increasing its cooldown by about 1.2 times for every Beetle Egg rarity.
*Summoned Beetles now have their own stats.
*On an unknown update, Beetle Egg, [[Ant Egg]], [[Mysterious Stick|Stick]] and it's magic counterpart [[Mysterious Stick/Magic|Magic stick]] alongside [[Square]] now spawn friendly mobs of equal rarity.
