title: Blood Stinger
content:
{{Petal Template Infobox | image = File:Blood Stinger (Common).png|C File:Blood Stinger (Unusual).png|U File:Blood Stinger (Rare).png|R File:Blood Stinger (Epic).png|E File:Blood Stinger (Legendary).png|L File:Blood Stinger (Mythic).png|M File:Blood Stinger (Ultra).png|Ul File:Blood Stinger (Super).png|S File:Blood Stinger (Unique).png|Uq | dropped-by = * [[Wasp]] | damage = {{Petal Scale 2|100|1|1|1|1|1|3|5|5}} | health = 1 | self-damage = {{Infobox Colors Empty|5|15|45|135|405|405|420.9|729|1262.7}} | reload-time = 7.5s | special = Does self-damage upon spawning. | description = It really hurts, but it's very fragile.
Deals damage to self when spawned. | first-rarity = {{Rarity|Common}} }} '''Blood Stinger''' is a petal in florr.io. It is similar to [[Stinger]] with a lower reload time but deals self-damage as soon as it spawns. Blood Stinger is dropped by [[Wasp]]s. ==Appearance== Its appearance is nearly identical to that of Stinger, but instead the petal uses a more reddish tint. It also appears that the edges of the petal curve inward, much like a [[w:c:diepio:Traps|Trap in diep.io]]. ==Function== Its base reload time is 7.5s, or 75% of Stinger's reload time. It functions like Stinger but deals a certain amount of damage to the flower upon spawning. It cannot lower the user's HP below 1. {|class="article-table" |+Petal Stats ![[Rarity]] !Obtainable? !Damage !Health !Reload !Self Damage |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |rowspan="9"|Yes |100 |1 |rowspan="9"|7.5 seconds |5 |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |300 |1 |15 |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |900 |1 |45 |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |2,700 |1 |135 |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |8,100 |1 |405 |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |8,100 (x3) |1 (x3) |405 (x3) |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |14,580 (x5) |1 (x5) |420.9 (x5) |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |43,740 (x5) |1 (x5) |729 (x5) |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} |131,220 (x5) |1 (x5) |1,262.7 (x5) |} ==Obtainment== ===[[Wasp]]=== {{:Wasp/Drop Blood Stinger}} ===Purchasing=== On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]]. ''Note: This table shows the number of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]'' {{Petal Price|3}} == Obtaining == * Blood Stinger is dropped from [[Wasp]]s. Unless you have enough skill to dodge the multiple projectiles from both [[Mantis]]es and [[Wasp]]s, it is recommended to bring [[Plank]]s to [[Jungle]] before engaging with any Wasp higher than the {{Rarity|Legendary}} rarity. * Do not equip [[Mana Orb]], as it will replace the Blood Stinger drop with a [[Stinger/Magic|Magic Stinger]] drop instead. ==Strategy== * Blood Stinger needs your HP to reload, so it is required to have healing petals when using it. ** [[Starfish]] is a good healing petal due to its high heal rate. [[Leaf]] can be used, but it requires {{Rarity|Epic}}+ [[Talents/Medic|Medic Talents]] to sustain a Blood Stinger of the same rarity with the {{Rarity|Super}} [[Talents/Reload|Reload Talent]]. [[Fang]] is an exceptional petal when used with Blood Stinger, due to the instant regeneration when you hit an enemy with it. *** [[Rose]] and [[Dahlia]] can be used as well, but they are less effective when it comes to healing. [[Yucca]] is not advised, as it is counter-active to what Blood Stinger is designed for. *** [[Sponge]] with [[Cotton]] can also nullify Blood Stinger's self-damage; however, you still need healing due to Cotton's reload and Sponge's spread damage. ** Shield can also be used to reload Blood Stinger as well since it counts to your HP. *** A very niche way of reloading Blood Stinger is equipping [[Cotton/Magic|Magic Cotton]] and some mana regeneration petals. Since the player would be dealt damage by Blood Stinger reloading, Magic Cotton can nullify all of its damage. * Blood Stinger is designed to be a damage petal using your health as the fuel. Bursting down mobs is its specialty due to its high damage and faster reload than its normal counterpart. Aggression is key when using this petal. ** Using Blood Stinger in a bubble build with some healing petals can help clear mobs that would be in your way. The faster reload means you don't have to worry about being left open for long without damage. *** In Hel, it is recommended to use a build similar to this as it allows both mobility and damage without the fear of [[Beetle/Hel|Hel Beetles]] teleporting into you. ** This is a double-edged sword in PvP if you don't use [[Sponge]]. If you don't have a lot of health, the self-damage will kill you faster. * Recommended farming areas with Blood Stinger are [[Ocean]], [[Sewers]], [[Ant Hell]], and [[Desert]] due to the lack of projectile-based mobs in those areas. * When you have any form of invincibility, Blood Stinger will spawn as usual without dealing self-damage. Blood Stinger can be used with [[Bandage]] against Super+ mobs if you are sure that you had already dealt enough damage to loot them. ==Gallery== File:Blood Stinger (Common).png|{{R|1}} Blood Stinger. File:Blood Stinger (Unusual).png|{{R|2}} Blood Stinger. File:Blood Stinger (Rare).png|{{R|3}} Blood Stinger. File:Blood Stinger (Epic).png|{{R|4}} Blood Stinger. File:Blood Stinger (Legendary).png|{{R|5}} Blood Stinger. File:Blood Stinger (Mythic).png|{{R|6}} Blood Stinger. File:Blood Stinger (Ultra).png|{{R|7}} Blood Stinger. File:Blood Stinger (Super).png|{{R|8}} Blood Stinger. File:Blood Stinger (Unique).png|{{R|9}} Blood Stinger. File:Blood Stinger with Duplicator 3.png| 3 parts of {{Rarity|Mythic}} Blood Stinger when applied with the [[Talents/Duplicator|Duplicator Talent]]. File:Blood Stinger with Duplicator.png| 5 parts of Blood Stinger at {{Rarity|Ultra}}+ when applied with the [[Talents/Duplicator|Duplicator Talent]]. ==Trivia== * On May 20, 2024, Blood Stinger's cooldown was increased from 5 seconds (its original duration) to 7.5 seconds. * {{Rarity|Ultra}}+ Blood Stingers are referred as "Bingers". * In game, the Blood Stinger is slightly concave compared to the regular [[Stinger]]. This is easiest to see with an {{Rarity|Ultra}}+ Blood Stinger compared to a regular [[Stinger]], becoming even more evident if you add the [[Duplicator]] talent. * This and [[Uranium]] are the only petals to be detrimental to the player. ([[Powder]] used to, but then it got reworked). * Because of the self-damage, it is like a [[Salt]] and [[Stinger]] combination, but the reflected damage is only done to the player using Blood Stinger. * Blood Stinger used to be able to kill the player with its self-damage. When this happened, it would say "You were destroyed by: A mysterious entity" on the death screen. * On January 17, 2025, Blood Stinger's self-damage ability was changed. Now it deals self-damage upon spawning and is unable to directly kill the player using it. {{Petals}} [[zh:血刺]] [[Category:Petals]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Stinger}} [[Category:Common+]][[Category:Same Health All Rarities]][[Category:Same Reload All Rarities]] [[Category:Wasps and Hornets]] [[Category:Affected by Duplicator Talent]] [[Category:Debuffs]][[Category:Blood Petals]]

'''Bone''' is a [[petal]] in florr.io. It has a special stat called [[Armor]], reducing damage dealt to the petal, but loses half of armor after each hit. Bone is dropped by [[Soldier Termite]]s.

==Appearance==
Bone consists of a typical, cartoonish portrait of a real-life bone's appearance: it is a vertically long and curved rectangle with heart-shaped figures in both ends of the petal. Its color palette is white with gray edges surrounding it.

==Function==

Bone possesses the Armor attribute which reduces any physical damage dealt to it by the Armor value, but loses half of its Armor each hit. For example, if a player uses a {{Rarity|Super}} Bone on a {{Rarity|Super}} [[Ladybug]], the Bone will hit the Ladybug three times: Bone receives 21,870 - 21,870 = 0 damage on the first hit, 21,870 - (21,870 / 2) = 10,935 damage on the second hit, leaving it with 21,870 - 10,935 = 10,935 health, and finally 21,870 - (21,870 / 4) = 16,402.5 damage on the third hit, depleting the remaining health of the Bone.

While lightning damage bypasses Bone's armor, poison damage (e.g., from [[Scorpion]]'s missiles and [[Spider]]s) does not because it cannot inflict poison effect on petals.

==Obtainment==
===[[Soldier Termite]]===
{{:Soldier Termite/Drop Bone}}

===Purchasing===
On certain days, Bone can be purchased from the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|3}}

== Obtaining ==

*Defeat [[Soldier Termite]]s to obtain Bone. Be extra careful when farming in [[Ant Hell|Termite Hell]], as Soldier Termites have an extremely high base health stat and share damage with nearby Termites.

==Strategies==
*Bone is a good petal, with powerful farming capabilities allowing you to defeat mobs fairly quickly due to the Armor. Places recommended to use Bone in are:
**[[Ant Hell 2|Normal Ant Hell]]
**[[Ant Hell 3|Termite Ant Hell]]
**[[Ocean]]

* A general rule of thumb for Bone is that it can easily defeat any mob one tier below the petal's rarity (Ex: {{Rarity|Ultra}} Bone can solo most {{Rarity|Mythic}} mobs).
** {{Rarity|Super}} Bone can hit some {{Rarity|Super}} Mobs twice or more, meaning it can be used as somewhat reliable damage in a DPS build.
* {{Rarity|Ultra|Ultra+}} Bones can melt players in PvP who don't have the [[Talents/Sharp Edges|Sharp Edges]] [[Talents|talent]] or [[Plank]] or any defensive methods.

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">
File:Bone (Common).png|{{Rarity|Common}} Bone.
File:Bone (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|Unusual}} Bone.
File:Bone (Rare).png|{{Rarity|Rare}} Bone.
File:Bone (Epic).png|{{Rarity|Epic}} Bone.

File:Bone (Mythic).png|{{Rarity|Mythic}} Bone.
File:Bone (Ultra).png|{{Rarity|Ultra}} Bone.
File:Bone (Super).png|{{Rarity|Super}} Bone.
File:Bone (Unique).png|{{Rarity|Unique}} Bone.
File:Bone.png|({{Rarity|9b}}) Bone.
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*Bone used to be dropped by [[Soldier Fire Ant|Solder Fire Ants]] instead of Soldier Termites. This change was made in order to redirect players to grind Soldier Fire Ants instead. More importantly, the change was made to avoid self inflation, as {{Rarity|Super}} Bone was highly effective against {{Rarity|Ultra}} Soldier Termites. This was reverted in one of the balance updates in October 2025.
**With Termites' base health stats and {{Rarity|Ultra}} mob health scaling buffed, Termite Hell becomes arguably one of the most difficult zones to farm in. 
*Bone's Armor stat is similar to the Armor stat of [[Leafbug]]. However, Leafbug does not drop Bone, although it does drop [[Root]], which gives its user Armor. Soldier Fire Ant drops Bone, but they don't have their armor buffed.
*The fact that Bone has an Armor stat that reduces damage is a reference to real-life chemistry behind real-life bones: elements such as Calcium make them strong and sturdy (like in-game Bone's description), which is reflected by the Armor stat.
*Bone used to completely tank most mobs one rarity below. However, it got nerfed twice so that Bone currently only tanks most mobs two rarities below. Said nerfs happened in unknown dates, as they were unannounced in the [[Changelog]].
**Bone's Armor value of all rarities was reduced by 20% in the first nerf and 25% in the second nerf, meaning that {{Rarity|Common}} Bone had 5 Armor, {{Rarity|Unusual}} Bone had 15 Armor, and so on.
*Bone, [[Bur]] and Root are the only petals who work around the Armor attribute. More specifically, Bone has an Armor attribute itself, Bur decreases inflicted player/mob Armor attrubute once hits with a duration, and Root gives you an Armor attribute only when you are in the defensive position.
**Bone is the only petal with an Armor statistic itself.
*Since petals don't suffer from status effects, Bone's Armor isn't affected by Bur or a [[Mantis]] pea once both of them manage to hit the petal.
*Bone is simply a weaker [[Coin]] with [[armor]].
**This means that you should not trade a Bone at all since it will lose the Armor value for a slight damage increase. Coin despite being slightly better on high body damage mobs like wasp, Bone is agreed to shine better in general.
*Bone is one of the highest DPS petal that can be used in [[Ant Hell]].
*Due to the addition of Petal Health talent, Bone though got buffed damage and 3.33x armor, Bone armor splits in half whenever it takes damage. This means Bone is no longer indestructible against low rarity mobs for a long time, though it's not really worth the TPs spent. This doesn't make up for spending TPs on Petal health talents.

title: Bubble
content:
{{Petal Template Infobox | image = File:Bubble (Common).png|C File:Bubble (Unusual).png|U File:Bubble (Rare).png|R File:Bubble (Epic).png|E File:Bubble (Legendary).png|L File:Bubble (Mythic).png|M File:Bubble (Ultra).png|Ul File:Bubble (Super).png|S File:Bubble (Unique).png|Uq | description = Physics are for the weak. | first-rarity = {{Rarity|Common}} | health = 1 HP | reload-time = {{Infobox Colors Empty|2.0s + 0.7s|1.75s + 0.6s|1.5s + 0.5s|1.25s + 0.4s|1.0s + 0.3s|0.75s + 0.2s|0.5s + 0.1s|0.25s + 0.1s|0.1s}} | stackability = Stackable or Cancelable, depending on the position of multiple Bubbles | dropped-by = *[[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble]] *[[Soldier Ant/Diver|Scuba Soldier Ant]] | special = When activated with a right-click in the mouse, propels the flower forwards, to the opposite direction of Bubble prior to the petal being popped.
Affects [[Moon Rock]]. }} :''This page is about the petal. For the mob which shares its in-game name, see [[Bubble (Mob)]].'' :''This page is about normal Bubble. For the [[magic petal]] variant, see [[Bubble/Magic|Magic Bubble]].'' '''Bubble''' is a legacy [[petal]] from [https://florr.io florr.io]. Its function consists of propelling the player forwards when activated. Bubble is dropped by [[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble mobs]] and [[Soldier Ant/Diver|Scuba Soldier Ants]]. ==Appearance== Bubble consists of a very bright shade of a transparent white. An even brighter ring surrounds the outside of the petal, as well as a lighter dot in the upper right of the petal, both having the same color and opacity. ==Function== ''Note: The "A + B" respawn format means that the petal takes A seconds to respawn and B seconds after respawning to do its special action/have its special action enabled.'' {|class="article-table" |+Petal Stats ![[Rarity]] !Obtainable? !Health !Respawn |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |rowspan="9"|Yes |rowspan="9"|1 HP |2.0 + 0.7 seconds |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |1.75 + 0.6 seconds |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |1.5 + 0.5 seconds |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |1.25 + 0.4 seconds |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |1.0 + 0.3 seconds |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |0.75 + 0.2 seconds |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |0.5 + 0.1 seconds |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |0.25 + 0.1 seconds |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} |0.1 seconds |} Upon right-clicking your mouse (going into defensive mode), Bubble propels the player in the opposite direction of where it was (prior to popping) relative to the flower. This effect stacks with other Bubbles, but only if they are next to each other in the build. In other words, Bubbles close together will propel the user even further, while Bubbles exactly on the opposite sides of each other will fully cancel out themselves. It also pushes the movement of other petals forward, albeit a bit delayed when compared to the flower itself. When [[Moon Rock]] is influencing the flower's petal orbit, it is susceptible to all Bubbles revolving around it, being treated as the flower itself and being propelled with the same velocity and direction as a flower would. ==Obtainment== ===[[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble]] and [[Soldier Ant/Diver|Scuba Ant]]=== {{:Bubble (Mob)/Drop Bubble}} ===Purchasing=== On certain days, Bubble can be purchased from the [[Shop]]. ''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]'' {{Petal Price|4}} == Obtaining == * Defeat [[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble mobs]] to obtain Bubble. ** One effective method to obtain ultra bubbles once you already have a few ultras is to place an ankh in a zone where mobs do not spawn in crab kingdom, and then clear mobs to reach ultra bubbles(mobs). Then, if there are other ultra mobs blocking the path, you can lure them out, then swap your ankh to teleport to saftey. You can also teleport away if you are about to die, as ankh automatically resets your sponge. ** It should be noted that getting to this zone can be challenging depending on your petals. * Alternatively, you can [[Assembler|Assemble]] Bubble at an Assembler in [[Factory]] for 10 of the same rarity [[Air]]. ==Strategies== *Bubble is solely a Utility Petal, allowing quick movement in the opposite direction the Bubble is facing. **In order to use multiple Bubbles effectively, use two to three {{Rarity|Legendary}} or one to two {{Rarity|Mythic}}+ [[Light]]s in addition to [[Yin Yang]]. [[Yin Yang]] is swapped out with another petal under it in order to allow fairly linear movement. Timing with swapping out is necessary. ***Feel free to use "Q" to lower the rotation speed of your petals to better adjust to [[Yin Yang]] bubbling. * Bubble can also be synergized with [[Mysterious Powder|Powder]] to allow even faster movement. ==Gallery== File:Bubble (Common).png|{{Rarity|Common}} Bubble. File:Bubble (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|Unusual}} Bubble. File:Bubble (Rare).png|{{Rarity|Rare}} Bubble. File:Bubble (Epic).png|{{Rarity|Epic}} Bubble. File:Bubble (Legendary).png|{{Rarity|Legendary}} Bubble. File:Bubble (Mythic).png|{{Rarity|Mythic}} Bubble. File:Bubble (Ultra).png|{{Rarity|Ultra}} Bubble. File:Bubble (Super).png|{{Rarity|Super}} Bubble. File:Bubble (Unique).png|{{Rarity|Unique}} Bubble. File:Bubble.png|Bubble. ==Trivia== *Bubble's ability consisting of popping itself refers to real-life bubbles being frail and easily able to be popped. *Bubble is one of the eight petals that have a [[magic petal]] variant. The others are [[Cactus]], [[Cotton]], [[Grapes]], [[Leaf]], [[Missile]], [[Peas]], [[Stick]] and [[Stinger]]. *Around the end of April 2024, Bubble's ability duration and cooldown timer were buffed on all rarity variants of the petal. *Bubble is the first petal that cannot deal damage. The second one is is [[Antennae]], which came from the same era as Bubble: legacy florr.io. *Bubble is the first petal to provide a movement boost (by propelling you). The second is [[Mysterious Powder|Powder]] (which directly boosts your movement speed). *Bubble is the first petal to be added that is currently exclusive to [[Ocean]]. *Bubble is one of the many petals that are dropped by a mob of the same name. The others are: [[Cactus]], [[Dandelion]], [[Ant Egg]], [[Jelly]] (partially), [[Leaf]] (partially), [[Rock]], [[Shell]], [[Sponge]], [[Square]] and [[Starfish]]. In the case of Bubble, it's the [[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble mob]]. **Bubble, Cactus, Dandelion, Ant Egg (as it was [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Egg {{Rarity|Epic}} Egg]), Leaf, Rock and Square all came from legacy florr.io. ***However, the mob counterparts of Bubble, Ant Egg, Dandelion and Leaf didn't exist back on legacy florr.io, only appearing after new florr.io released. In the case of Bubble, its corresponding mob was released during the Waves era, when Ocean was initially released, joining Shell, Sponge and Starfish (new florr.io petals) in that list. *[https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Bubble Legacy Bubble] used to drop from [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Bee Bees], [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Hornet Hornets] and [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Ladybug all] [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Dark_Ladybug types of] [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Shiny_Ladybug Ladybugs] (including the old [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Massive_Ladybug Massive Ladybug]) at the {{Rarity|Rare}} rarity before the Bubble mob was added in the Waves era of florr.io. *Bubble is the first petal whose secondary cooldown timer (the cooldown timer for its ability) decreases with each Rarity increase. It used to be the only one until an update caused [[Mark]]'s cooldown timers to swap between each other, meaning that Mark's secondary cooldown timer also does this now. *Bubble, {{Rarity|Common}} [[Chip]], [[Coral]] and [[Privet]] have the lowest durability in the game: 1 HP. **Bubble is also the weakest non-wearable petal in the game, dealing a total of 0 damage. ***Those facts are true for Bubble because they exist for the sole purpose of propelling you and letting you get to places quicker (as well as having an easier time ramming things). Thus, it doesn't need a damage stat (it wouldn't make any sense for Bubbles to even deal damage) or any amounts of durability like another petal such as [[Dahlia]] or [[Rose]] (which need durability in order to survive to heal you a bit). *Bubble, its mob version, [[Glass]] and [[Jellyfish]] share the same color palette, and are the most transparent things in the game. Jelly comes to a close second in terms of transparency (as it appears to be just a little more translucent); however, its color palette is purple/magenta instead. *On the November 4th, 2024 update, there was a change to Bubbles surrounding [[Moon Rock]]: they can propel the petal forwards at the same velocity and distance as a flower would be propelled by them, having the same rules as when revolving a flower. This allows Moon Rock to be treated like a ranged petal, or even a stronger flower itself, although it cannot be healed. Bubbles orbiting Moon Rock are still able to cancel each other. *Bubble's description was changed from "You can right click to pop it and propel your flower." during legacy to "Physics are for the weak.". **This makes Bubble one of the legacy petals to have their description changed, alongside [[Peas]], [[Grapes]], [[Dahlia]], [[Rock]], [[Dandelion]], [[Heavy]], [[Honey]], [[Yin Yang]], [[Rice]] and [[Salt]]. {{Petals}} [[zh:泡泡]] [[Category:Petals]] [[Category:Buffs]] [[Category:Common+]] [[Category:Ocean]] [[Category:Aiming]] [[Category:Stackable Petals]][[Category:Soldier Ants]] [[Category:Droppable Petals]] [[Category:Assembling]] [[Category:Bubbles]]

title: Bubble/Magic
content:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Magic Bubble}} {{Petal Template Infobox |name = Magic Bubble |image = File:Magic Bubble (Common).png|C File:Magic Bubble (Unusual).png|U File:Magic Bubble (Rare).png|R File:Magic Bubble (Epic).png|E File:Magic Bubble (Legendary).png|L File:Magic Bubble (Mythic).png|M File:Magic Bubble (Ultra).png|Ul File:Magic Bubble (Super).png|S File:Magic Bubble (Unique).png|Uq |first-rarity = {{Rarity|Common}} |description = Physics are for the magicless. |reload-time = {{Infobox Colors Empty|2.0s + 0.7s|1.8s + 0.6s|1.5s + 0.5s|1.2s + 0.4s|1s + 0.3s|0.8s + 0.2s|0.5s + 0.1s|0.2s + 0.1s|0.1s}} |stackability = Unstackable |dropped-by = *[[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble]] |mana = {{Infobox Colors Empty|5|10|20|40|80|160|320|640|1280}} |special = Once triggered, propels the flower to the direction it's currently moving, regardless of position.
Affects [[Moon Rock]]. }} :''This page is about Magic Bubble. For the normal petal variant, see [[Bubble]].'' '''Magic Bubble''' is a [[Petals|petal]] from [https://florr.io florr.io]. Its function consists of propelling the player in the direction that the player is currently moving. Magic Bubble is dropped by [[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble]] with [[Mana Orb]] equipped. ==Appearance== Magic Bubble consists of a blue recolor of [[Bubble]]. Its transparent part is also more blue. ==Function== ''Note: The "A + B" respawn format means that the petal takes A seconds to respawn and B seconds after respawning to do its special action/have its special action enabled.'' {|class="article-table" |+Petal Stats ![[Rarity]] !Obtainable? !Mana !Respawn |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |rowspan="9"|Yes |5 MP |2.0 + 0.7 seconds |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |10 MP |1.8 + 0.6 seconds |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |20 MP |1.5 + 0.5 seconds |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |40 MP |1.2 + 0.4 second |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |80 MP |1 + 0.3 second |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |160 MP |0.8 + 0.2 second |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |320 MP |0.5 + 0.1 seconds |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |640 MP |0.2 + 0.1 seconds |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} |1280 MP |0.1 seconds |} When going into defensive mode, Magic Bubble will propel the flower to the direction it's currently moving, unlike [[Bubble]], which propel the flower to Bubble's opposite direction. Unlike other most magic petals which consumes mana to spawn immediately, Magic Bubble consumes mana to spawn but has the same reload as [[Bubble|its normal variant]], and so does its activating time, but never goes down 0.1 second. Magic Bubble also affects [[Moon Rock|Moon]] movement. ==Obtainment== ''Note: Magic Bubble can only be obtained via [[Mana Orb]], regardless of rarity.'' ===[[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble]] === {{:Bubble (Mob)/Drop Magic Bubble}} ===Purchasing=== On certain days, Magic Bubble can be purchased from the [[Shop]]. ''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]'' {{Petal Price|1}} ==Strategies== *Magic Bubble, for the most part, actions as Bubble. However, since it doesn't require positioning them correctly, one can use the same amount of Magic Bubbles as you would with normal Bubbles, without the need of [[Light]]s to clump them together. This means that more defensive/offensive petals can be used for survivability. *What makes Magic Bubble worse than its base version is its cost, specially when multiple are used. They cost a lot to be used, which means that one must use high rarity [[Magic Cactus|Magic Cacti]] and [[Magic Leaf|Magic Leaves]] in what should have been Lights, or even sacrifice a spot or two for more of them, in order to maintain a high MP storage for Magic Bubbles. *Using Magic Bubbles instead of normal Bubbles may help you in getting the four Racecar [[Achievements]] easier. *Besides MP cost and a slightly different function, Magic Bubble strategies can pretty much sum to what strategies you would use for Bubble. {{Zone|Garden}} *Along with Magic Cacti and Magic Leaves, [[Lotus]] and [[Plank]] can be used in tandem with Magic Bubbles, letting you be safe from [[Hornet]]s and [[Spider]]s (most dangerous Garden enemies) with more ease. *[[Antennae]] can also be used to see if [[Ant Hole]]s or [[Centipede]]s are in your way, avoiding taking considerable damage from both of them. {{Zone|Desert}} *Due to Desert's density spawning a lot of [[Beetle]]s, [[Cactus (Mob)|Cacti]] and [[Sandstorm]]s, which are very dangerous mobs, using Magic Bubbles is very problematic, unless DPS petals, defensive petals or healing petals manage to outheal everything. Dodging abilities and Antennae can also be of great use here. *Magic Bubble can be a pretty good tool in [[Shiny Ladybug]] hunting, as you have more freedom in choosing where to head to. {{Zone|Ocean}} *Magic Bubbles can let you have more control in escaping from [[Leech]]es, however, caution is advised, as you may run into another mob (including another Leech), making you suffer a lot of damage. *The same strategy can also be done with [[Jellyfish]], specially on places like Jellyfish Fields, letting you avoid some unnecessary damage. ===Obtaining=== *Strategies applied for farming normal [[Bubble]]s should be applied to farming Magic Bubbles, as they are dropped by the same mob ([[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble mobs]]). Just remember to bring [[Mana Orb]] with you, since Magic Bubble is a magic petal. *After you acquire your Mana Orb, good places for farming should be Ocean Trench, Ocean Maze and Crab Kingdom, as those places frequently spawn a lot of high rarity Bubble mobs. **It can be difficult to farm Bubble mobs, as they are frequently greeded, as well as strong mobs usually guard the spots they spawn. {{Zone|Jungle}} *It's advised not to use Magic Bubbles deep into Jungle, as you can end up ramming [[Wasp]] without you seeing the mob. Antennae is highly advised when using Magic Bubbles in Jungle. *Magic Bubbles can be somewhat useful when looking for the last Termites in [[Termite Mound]]s, saving some time. {{Zone|Hel}} ===PvE=== *It's strongly advised to not use Magic Bubbles in Hel, as its high density, combined with mobs with a lot of damage, could easily get you killed, even if you pay attention to your surroundings. *If they have any usage here, they can be used to outrun [[Hel Beetle]]s and [[Hel Spider]]s, having more chance to survive their dangerous abilities (teleporting, for Hel Beetle, poison, for Hel Spider, and high body damage, for both). ===PvP: Using=== *Magic Bubbles essentially play as if you were using normal Bubbles: bubble away from your opponent when you're either low on HP, arming yourself or just exhausting the opponent, then bubble back to your opponent, counterattack at the right time and kill them. *Due to Magic Bubbles giving you more control in your bubbling direction, you can deal surprise attacks with ram builds, where the opponent won't see when you Bubble to them, leading to them taking a lot of damage at once. You can also dodge ranged attacks (against projectiles, [[Third Eye]] or [[Wing]]) with more facility, and get inside the enemy's attacking position to deal a lot of damage while suffering none until the opponent reacts. *While Magic Bubbles do have a lot of potential, it should be noted that [[Magic Cactus|Magic Cacti]] and [[Magic Leaf|Magic Leaves]] have to be used in order to sustain their ability, specially if you're using multiple Magic Bubbles. This alone may reduce the possibilities you have to dominate PvP with them, unless you manage to work around this liability. ===PvP: Against=== *One should always be cautious when fighting against Magic Bubble users: you never know when they'll use the petal to retreat or deal surprise attacks. As such, try to predict their next movements in order to defeat them. *While Magic Bubbles have a lot of potential, they can quickly exhaust the user's own MP with just some activations. Due to this, the user either will become weakened very quickly, unable to use Magic Bubbles by losing all MP, or using Magic Cacti/Magic Leaves and losing offensive or defensive power, which can give you a great advantage against your enemy. {{Zone|Ant Hell}} *Magic Bubbles are somewhat useful to let you easily get out of situations where's you're swarmed by Ants/Termites from all sides, though it is trivial as the cost doesn't compensate, while it's pretty easy to avoid getting swarmed by any Ant Hell mobs. *{{Rarity|Super}} [[Termite Overmind]]s in Termite Hell can easily cut your supply of Mana Points short (by disabling Magic Cacti and Magic Leaves), while also disabling some Magic Bubbles alongside. One should be cautions when fighting with them due to their special ability. {{Zone|Sewers}} *Magic Bubbles are very complicated to use in Sewers, as this area is very narrow with a high density of mobs everywhere, which menas you'll constantly take damage if you don't kill the mobs before bubbling. ==Gallery== File:Magic Bubble (Common).png|{{Rarity|Common}} Magic Bubble. File:Magic Bubble (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|Unusual}} Magic Bubble. File:Magic Bubble (Rare).png|{{Rarity|Rare}} Magic Bubble. File:Magic Bubble (Epic).png|{{Rarity|Epic}} Magic Bubble. File:Magic Bubble (Legendary).png|{{Rarity|Legendary}} Magic Bubble. File:Magic Bubble (Mythic).png|{{Rarity|Mythic}} Magic Bubble. File:Magic Bubble (Ultra).png|{{Rarity|Ultra}} Magic Bubble. File:Magic Bubble (Super).png|{{Rarity|Super}} Magic Bubble. File:Magic Bubble (Unique).png|{{Rarity|Unique}} Magic Bubble. File:Magic Bubble.png|Magic Bubble. File:Screenshot (346).png|5 {{Rarity|Unusual}} Magic Bubble being used in deck, 5 {{Rarity|Unusual}} Magic Bubble is like 3 {{Rarity|Mythic}} Bubble and 2 {{Rarity|Ultra}} Bubble. ==Trivia== *Unlike normal [[Bubble]], Magic Bubble's secondary cooldown timer can't go beyond 0.1 seconds, while {{Rarity|Super}} and {{Rarity|Unique}} Bubbles have a secondary cooldown timer of 0.05 and 0.04 seconds, respectively. *Some people think that Magic Bubble was added for lazy people who don't want to keep Bubble in place by using [[Yin Yang]]. *Magic Bubble used to be the least durable magic petal: it only had 1 HP. On an unknown date, Magic Bubbles got reworked so now they don't have a durability stat: they are invincible, much like [[Basil]], [[Battery]] and other similar petals. **Magic Bubble is still the weakest magic petal, dealing no damage. *Magic Bubble and [[Magic Cactus]] are the only petals dropped by a mob that shares the same name as their shortened name. *Magic Bubble is the 9th magic petal to be added to the game and also the only magic petal that can be obtained in [[Ocean]]. *Magic Bubble was once stackable with itself. [[zh:魔法泡泡]] {{Petals}} [[Category:Petals]] [[Category:Magic Petals]] [[Category:Common+]] [[Category:Bubbles]] [[Category:Ocean]] [[Category:Buffs]] [[Category:Droppable Petals]] [[Category:Unstackable Petals]]

''Not to be confused with the petal, [[Bubble]].''

The '''Bubble''' is a mob that spawns in [[Ocean]]. When killed, it pushes away nearby [[Flower|Flowers]] and [[Mobs]] 

==Appearance==
It is a non-hued translucent circle with a white border and shine.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Moves very slowly.

'''Behavior:''' Moves around in various directions, and sometimes spins around, noticeable by its shine.

Bubble is immune to poison and chained lightning damage.

If you damage a {{R|8}} rarity of a Bubble, it will shoot out lower rarity bubbles. This ability is the same as [[Rock (Mob)|Rock]].

==Spawning==
Bubbles spawn in [[Ocean]]. Bubbles spawn more often in the [[Starfish]] Zone at the top-right of the Ocean, in the [[Crab]] Kingdom at the bottom-right, and in the pocket below the spawn point of the Ocean where the [[Oracle]] is.

==Drops==
===[[Air]]===
{{:Bubble (Mob)/Drop Air}}

===[[Bubble]]===
{{:Bubble (Mob)/Drop Bubble}}

===[[Bubble/Magic|Magic Bubble]]===
{{:Bubble (Mob)/Drop Magic Bubble}}

==Strategy== 
*This mob has very low health so you can destroy it very easily. However it has high Armor which means that a single {{Rarity|Common}} Basic can only kill approximately one {{R|Common}} Bubble.
**Despite the Armor, Bubbles are very easy to kill. Even an {{Rarity|Ultra}} Bubble is easy to kill.
*High-rarity Bubbles (especially {{Rarity|Ultra}}) are greeded very often because players need [[Bubble]]s to travel to other places quickly. Try to kill the Bubble as fast as possible, or if another player is with you, only do some damage to it and let the other player finish the bubble off (If the player is in your squad you don't need to do this).

==={{Rarity|Super}} Bubble===
*The {{Rarity|8}} Bubble is one of the hardest Supers to loot, as it will mostly die almost immediately after spawning due to very fast Bubble users killing the Bubble before anyone else can reach it, or by players already in ocean that are farming, killing it almost instantly.

==Gallery==
<gallery>
File:Bubble (Mob) (Common).png|{{Rarity|Common}} Bubble.
File:Bubble (Mob) (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|Unusual}} Bubble.
File:Bubble (Mob) (Rare).png|{{Rarity|Rare}} Bubble.
File:Bubble (Mob) (Epic).png|{{Rarity|Epic}} Bubble.
File:Bubble (Mob) (Legendary).png|{{Rarity|Legendary}} Bubble.
File:Bubble (Mob) (Mythic).png|{{Rarity|Mythic}} Bubble.
File:Bubble (Mob) (Ultra).png|{{Rarity|Ultra}} Bubble.
File:Bubble (Mob) (Super).png|{{Rarity|Super}} Bubble.
File:Bubble (Mob) (Unique).png|{{Rarity|Unique}} Bubble.
File:Bubble (Mob).png|Bubble.
File:Bubble in a wall..png|Game is perfect, no bugs
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*This mob has the lowest health out of every mob, with only 1 health at {{Rarity|Common}} rarity.
*Bubbles have an unspawnable counterpart named [[Acid Bubble]], which is a scrapped mob that only existed for a short period of time. It was replaced by [[Bumble Bee]].
*Its description is a reference to the fact that it can mostly be killed in just one hit.
*It is the only mob that can fling you away by touching it.
*Bubble is one of the slowest non-stationary mobs in the game.
*While other mobs had their {{R|Common}} rarity hp multiplied by 2.5 in the current version of florr.io compared to waves, Bubble's got halved.
*It is the only passive mob immune to Poison and lightning.
*It is the only mob whose description ends with an alphabetical character.
**It also has the shortest description out of any other mob, with only 3 characters.
*Bubbles lost their damage cap and got their Armor buffed to the point where they do not share the same Armor value as almost any other mob, but their Armor isn't strong enough to be like the Armor values of [[Leafbug]] and [[Shell (Mob)|Shell mob]]. This was done to "further prevent greeding".
**However, it actually lets you kill Bubbles much faster with petals that ignore Armor.
**{{Rarity|Super}} Bubble's Armor was 44k, but it was nerfed shortly after, since most {{Rarity|Super}} petals wouldn't be able to penetrate the Armor, except for [[Tomato]] and [[Dice]].

content:
{{Builds - Scenario|Ant Areas}}
__TOC__
== Builds ==


=== Average Glass Build ===

: ''"For those who don't have a Ultra yet, or dies all lot"''

* '''Petals Used:'''  (10 slots) 1st Row:  (M+) Glass/Glass/Leaf/Glass/Glass/(L+) Pincer/Glass/Glass/Glass/(L+) Root
* 2nd Row: (L+) Healing, Triangles, or Bubbles
* |
*
*
* '''How it Works:''' The damaging petals like glass does the the damage (mind blowing, right?).Leaf for a little healing. Pincer for stun when fighting ultras, if you're alone. And root helps keep you alive much longer since you can get crushed easily and can't afford things like rubber.
* |
*
*
* '''Strengths:''' Killing 3-4 ultras, or tons of mythics chasing you.
* |
*
*
* '''Weakness:''' If root stacks are lower than 4-5, you can't loot fast enough, and you get cornered/smashed.
* |
*
* '''Variations:''' Leaf for any healing except, Rose and Dahila or Totem. Pincer for another Glass or a debuff petal. Or replace any Glass with faster, if you haven't maxed rotation speed.
* |
*
*
* '''Tip:''' Max rotation speed, reach, inventory, and get magnetism (all in talent tree), spread newly acquired Ultra Glass evenly across the build, and don't touch the healing, health, body damage, poison, and luck (all in talent tree). Also, if you see me, say ''"qwerty"''
* |
*
*
* '''Author:''' Ebom (In-game name) 
* |
*
*
* If you like it put a 1 here, and if you don't like it put a two instead ----->  :  
* |
* Type what you hate: 
* |

=== '''Mr.Krabs''' ===
"Hello,I like <s>money</s> Loot"

Primary build:however many claws you have(reccemended at least 5 l+)fill in the gaps with iris and pincer.

Secondary build:In order-
{{Background|图片=AHb.png|大小=2000px}}
L+pincer/L+Damage(coin,sand,bone or anything)/L+clover/L+Damage/L+Heal/L+Jelly/L+Damage/L+Web/9th slot:Faster/10th slot:Magnet

'''How it works:''' Use primary builds till 75-50% damage and then switch to secondary.

'''Strengths:''' Single mobs at a time.Can solo M soldier ant

'''Weaknesses:'''A lot of mobs ramming you into a wall(better have a shell,heal build to switch to)

'''Variations:'''Replace somethings with disc,Cutter,Sponge pincer with web or lightning as damage for ant holes.Replace some things with web for u worker or u baby

=== '''The Best Ramming Build History Has Ever Seen''' ===
'''''"Ant: look a flower. DIE U RASCAL!! A second later… I died HOLY GREAT"'''''

* '''Petals Used:''' (Do it in order. Use mythic for everything) Disc(put antennae under it) Yuccax4(put a lotus and anything u want to add under) Talisman(switch with relic) Battery(switch with soil) Sponge(with whatever) Basil(with bulb. bulb can be legendary-) Magnet(if u don’t have magnetism,switch with basil. if u do, just use basil)
* '''How it Works:''' just ram. When low on health switch talisman and battery with relic and soil. Switch sponge and back when second chance activates. Also switch relic and soil back until ur low on health.
* '''Strengths:''' close range mobs
* '''Weaknesses:''' ranged mobs
* '''Variations: add a yucca and basil instead of battery and talisman'''
* '''Tip:''' avoid swarms

=== Boredom ===
''"I hate AFK checks"''

'''Petals:''' USticks, UWebs, maybe healing and knockback

'''Strengths:''' AFKing in Mythic/Ultra zones

'''Weaknesses:''' Actually obtaining enough USticks; AFK checks

'''Variations:''' If you cannot obtain a bunch of USticks, obtain 1 U magic stick and equip mana petals

'''How it works:''' You sit there in defensive position and web the mobs around you, sandstorms shred, you heal up/knock back the one mob that gets too close, other people in the area kill worms, you fail AFK check and get yeeted back to spawn.

=== The triangle strategy(use M+) ===
''"I hate healing"''

'''Petals:top: 9 tri + faster'''

bottom:healing+ magnet(if u don’t have magnitism)

'''Strengths:''' against single mythic or ultra. This may not be able to get loot from a super, for supers u need ultra tri. For swarms, beat them one by one

'''Weaknesses:''' swarms getting u into a corner

Variations:8 tri 1 third eye 1 faster

'''How it works:''' Move AWAY  From the m+ mob u want to kill. If ur health gets lower than 35% switch to healing. If using that variation, switch out third eye when cornered or when mobs are gaining on u

===Free Ultras===
:''"No autographs today, people."''

* '''1st row:''' Ultra Ant Egg, Ultra Beetle Egg, Ultra Ant Egg, Ultra Wing, Ultra Orange, Mythic Third Eye, Ultra Leaf, Ultra Corn, Mythic Sponge, Ultra Sand
*2nd row: Mythic Bubble, Ultra Bubble, Mythic Bubble, Ultra Antennae, Mythic Yggdrasil, Ultra Magnet, Mythic Starfish, Mythic Orb Ultra Poo, Mythic Yin Yang
*'''Strengths:''' Anything except the weakness
*'''Weaknesses:''' Literally 5000 mobs chasing you into a wall and you have no escape at all
*'''Variations:''' Replace Mythic Orb with Ultra Bone, I just don't have it :(     You don't need to have these exact rarities, they're just the ones I use. Preferably, the 2nd slot in the 2nd row is a higher rarity than the other bubbles (if slot 1 and 3 in row 2 are legendary bubbles, slot 2 must be mythic+)
*'''Tips:''' Get max reload. Equip the ultra bubble in your main loadout (row 1). This allows a quick escape from a horde of mobs not completely surrounding you. If you are at low health, replace the ultra leaf with the mythic starfish for faster heal. When at 75% health, switch back.
*'''Author:''' TheLucanLord
===Ant Hell 2 Winger===
'''''"I am invincible to you, egger."'''''
* '''Petals Used:''' All wings must be L+ and fasters E+) The order is Wing/Faster/Wing/Faster/Wing/Faster/Wing/Faster/9th slot : Wing/10th slot Faster
* '''2nd Build:''' Any type of Magnet (Recommend L+ Magnets)
*'''How it Works:''' You go Ant hell 2 and to where the egger is and attack with your wings.
*'''Strengths:''' Very good with lots of people using almost the same thing because you are untouchable and close to Queen spawning.
*'''Weaknesses:''' Less than 3 people and/or an egger using S eggs.
*'''Tip:''' Try going to areas that spawn L+ eggs and recommend area is Top Mid (and if you see my name "Power" say Ebo.
*'''Other Uses:''' Very Good with U+ Queen Ant and for Shredding Weak Mobs
*'''Tally:''' If you like this build tally a one here ->1111 Why 5 fasters more Wings bro -1
*'''Author:''' Power (Florr user)
=== '''Ant killer''' ===

* '''Build 1.1 (For Fire ant hell and Ant hell) :'''5x L+ Light, 2x L+Fasters, 1x L+ Bur, 1x Magnet, 1x Antenna, Antennae to Faster and I'd agree
* '''Build(secondary):''' a good set of healing petals
*
* '''Build 1 (for Termites hell and Ant hell):'''7x M+ bone, 1x Magnet, 1x Antenna, 1x M+ faster, Bones aren't great for M+
*
* '''How it works:''' Build 1 works by bone's Armor , bone's Armor let's it hit multiple times on the ants which are lower rarity of the bone as the ants aren't able to damage the bone. This is good to insta kill ants and is more efficient than build 1.1 in ant hell. Build 1.1 uses the fast reload of the lights (you can replace the lights with sand) the bur debuffs the mob's Armor therefore increasing the damage of the other petals. The fasters just increase the speed of the petals so they do more damage(don't use fasters above mythic). Build 2 is just for healing What about yy
* '''Strengths:''' Build 1: ants, termites; Build 1.1: fire ants and ants.
* '''Weaknesses:''' Build 1: fire ants(because they deal more damage they destroy the bone but still works); Build 1.1: termiets Termites*(because they have more hp and they spread damage) More hp doesn't help does it?
* '''Additional information:''' You can use build 1 in fire ant hell but it won't insta kill the ants; for build 1.1 get full petal rotation skill (No it can effect other rotation) and don't use fasters above mythic rarity(its to just to get more dps or damage per second)
* '''Author:''' sniperboy (discord ID: sniperboy#6277)
* _______________________________

=== '''Ant Hell 2 wings efficient grinder''' ===

* Build: 1x L+ Claw, 6x L+ Lights/Sands, 2x L+ Faster (hopefully Mythic), 1x Magnet. Second set of petals must be a decently efficient bubble set with One faster? antannae.antennae*
* How it works: Bubble around until you get to a Mythic Ant make sure you bubble beside it, not into it. With a lot of other pros around you can ensure drops with the build. Also Uboners can just greed?
* Strengths: Any type of normal ant, ultra queen if exceedingly lucky. "exceedingly lucky" It spawns in around 15 minutes bruh
* Weaknesses: Idk the weaknesses bro just don't use it outside of anthell 2 is all. Where's the space, weakness is nearly everything Mants gets melted instantly
* MegaHeckler ;) Wait, I know this guy

=== '''Termite-inator''' ===
"'''''I'm sorry, were you trying to kill me?"'''''

* '''Petals Used:''' 3x U+ Bone, and 4x U+ Stinger, in an alternating pattern. Then 1 M+ Jelly and a Magnet, as well as a Third Eye.
* '''How it Works:''' The Bones and Stingers will shred everything while the Jelly keeps any Mythic Termites from attacking you. The Third Eye increases your petal range, so you can hit enemies from further away. 
* '''Strengths:''' [[Worker Termite]], and also [[Soldier Termite]]. Possibly also Ultra/Super [[Termite Overmind]] as well. No termite can kill you with this build, unless you get into a tough situation. (sandwiched against wall, etc.)
* '''Weaknesses:'''  [[Hornet|Hornets]]. They will destroy the Stingers and kill you from the holes in your petal wall. 
* '''Variations:''' '''''The P2W/No Life:''''' Replace the Ultras with Supers!
* '''Author:''' Kitten With Headset (Fandom), Sandstorm_XD (florr.io) . LoafCat touch this again and die.

=== '''Ant 'A'blitorator''' ===
Its such a good build that I had to change the letters in obliterator(READ THE VARIATIONS BEFORE YOU SCROLL DOWN TO OTHER BUILDS)!

* <u>Primary Build: E+faster, L+Rock, L+Sand, L+Fang, L+Stinger, L+Rock, L+Fang, L+Stinger, M+Basil, E+Magnet Yomama it's me roasting more for your builds again! TheLoafCat (Ucotten is banned in PVP same for Swing)</u>
* <u>Secondary Build:</u>  NONE Really? What about the bubble build.
* <u>How It Works:</u>  The fangs heal and do damage while the basil buffs them, the stingers are EXCELLENT boosts in the damage while rocks and sand are fairly consistent DPS. U+ fangs not L+ correction
* <u>Strengths:</u> Ant hell and Fire ant hell and its OK against  a few L-M jellyfish. The build is also fairly good for maze(Read Variations). Double space meta, no way it's good for FAH
* <u>Weaknesses:</u>  Pretty much everything else. FINALLY! Someone who got a part right!
* <u>Variations:</u>  If you want to use this setup in jellyfish fields, replace the rocks with L+Fangs. If you want to use the setup in Mythic Maze, replace one or both of the fangs with L+Leafs Fangs. Are. Not. Always. Good.
* Author: Yomama101(Florr.io) or Thatoneprogamer#8785(Discord) n0tprogamer?
* ''I would love to see how many people use this build over time. If this setup helped you PLEASE go into edit mode and leave a '1' next to the word 'Tally'. Thank you!''
* '''''TALLY: 111111111111111111111 This build isn't really good, don't use Sand, Basil and Rocks, they aren't very good damage. M light with M bur is also just so much better, your build isn't even using jelly so what matters is just DPS and healing (maybe). -TheLoafCat Waaait a second why is there Basil in here the first place.'''''

note from someone who didn't make this build: ig you only have mbasil, using an extra lfang would be better. mbasil gives only +40% healing to both fangs, but adding an extra fang of the same rarity would buff it by 50% instead. you can assume an lfang heals for 10. 3 lfangs = 3×10 =30. 2 lfangs and 1 mbasil = 2×10+2×10×0.4 = 28. so evidently using an extra lfang in this situation not only increases your health, but also increases your damage output due to fangs better damage capabilities. Who wrote this, let me know, this guy actually knows how to do math. (first guy who knows how to do math, congrats!)
=== '''General Normal Ant Build (mythics not required :D) Okay, "4M+ Wings" ("Mythics not required")''' ===
[[File:Ant shred.png|thumb|523x523px]]'''"''ascend to the shadow realm!"'''''
* '''Petals used:''' '''3 L+ Bones, 4 M+ Wings (recommended), 1 L+ Fang,''' (other petals can be whatever you want[recommend(ed)（ stinger/rock])
* '''Strategy:''' The bones will respawn quite fast and deal lots of damage while the wings provide adequate range. Fangs will heal you and hopefully, if you have a rock, you can push back the ants a little more. This is dumb rock for pushing?! Use jelly or pincer goddamnit.
* '''Advantages''': great for taking on a single ant at a time or several weak ants.
* '''Tip:''' If you see a player called ric call them a rice monke. This does not relate at all bruh
* '''Weaknesses:''' doesn't do so well against stronger swarms of ants. At least to tell them to use lightning

Brought to you by MegaHeckler ;)

=== '''Crowd Controller (Jelly Version)''' ===
'''''"how to become an Ant yeeter in 9.73 seconds REAL!!!!1!11!!!!!!!!11!"'''''

* '''Petals Used:''' 8x L+ Jelly/2x of any healing petal (Fangs would be the most suggestible) Pro tip: use Uheavy 3M heavy and troll Uqueen like me :)
* '''Strategy:''' basically, you just {attack/defend} to yeet the over-protective Soldier Ants to heck, allowing everyone else to take care of them while they're "stunned" Nah go behind them bubble in and yeet the ants
* '''Advantages:''' prevention of swarming on oneself, adds backup for folks who are already being chased by swarms of aggressive ants.
* '''Disadvantages:''' Jelly Knockback backfire, Jellies not reloading quickly enough, Ultra spawnkills, etc... This is exactly why you get healing and use heavies
* '''Alternatives:''' replace some Jellies with Heavies for messing with other players...but beware that even the petal itself has consequences Great tip here!
* '''Tip:''' NEVER afk in Termite HELL, you could die instantly by being swarmed by Termites Not when you're stacking but it's a fine tip
* '''Author:''' FoldingPapers
==='''Ant Catapult'''===
'''''"Where did they all go?"'''''
*'''Petals Used:''' 3 L+ jelly next to each other, 1 faster, 3 bubbles, some [[Light|Lights]] and healing, [[Third Eye]] if you have it. Have skill and use yy boi
*'''How it Works:''' You attack the [[Soldier Ant|ant]] to send it flying, [[Light|Lights]] allow the jelly to be grouped tightly together to increase your yeet factor, Bubbles allow you to catch up with the ant if it is chasing another player, Third Eye to attack from a distance. Other players soon find anty cannonballs flying towards them, and die quickly. This by far is the best one, bet it's made by a super player
*'''Strengths:''' Defeating annoying players who steal loot
*'''Weaknesses:''' Not much DPS, as this is not a build focusing on shredding ants.
*'''Variations:''' Use more Jelly or lights.
*'''Tip:''' Launch the ant from behind at the player when the ant is chasing another player.
*'''Author:''' HCaterpillar Hehe, I knew it was someone who had ultras/supers in florr!

===Ant Egg Farmer===
'''''"ALL THE EGGS ARE MINEEEEE!!!!!!!"'''''
*'''Petals Used:''' 1 Rare+ [[Magnet]], 1 L+ [[Rose]], 8 L+ [[Ant Egg|Ant eggs]]. Why L+ people like me use Ueggs
*'''How it Works:''' Magnet sucks up items, rose heals you, ants destroy the [[Ant Egg (Mob)|eggs]]. Honestly if you're able to yoink all the eggs screw magnet drops are trash
* '''Strengths:''' Destroy the eggs, small groups of ants.
*'''Weaknesses:''' A really good player (why did you bring 6 U eggs!?!?!), large swarms of ants (That's me btw except I have 7, egg police)
* '''Variations:''' replace some [[Ant Egg|ant eggs]] with [[Beetle Egg|beetle eggs]] or [[Mysterious Stick|sticks]] for that perfect build. replace [[rose]] with another healing item if rose is too weak, or add more healing items. Beggs are recommended （for me）
*'''Tip:''' Works best if you have another player do this with you. 2 players = less chance of dying because of an [[Soldier Ant|ant]]. it is recommended that one person uses this, and the other person uses the "Crowd Controller" build from above.
* '''Author:''' My username is CewwiBoyHalo ingame. Aswell on Scratch. I'm Cewwi on Fandom. I somehow have no idea who this guy is

=== "Antshredder" ===
'''''"looks like they cANT get you anymore!"'''''

'''Petals needed:''' (use in this order)  Bulb(L+), Magnet (L+) Bubble (L+) Bubble (L+)  Basil (if you don't have a basil, use a healing petal. Bone (U+) (Ultra is required, sorry D:) Bone (U+) (Also needs to be ultra).  YESSSS SOMEHOW WHO ACTUALLY HAS SOME SKILL IN FLORR! Any leftover slots are healing petals. (leaf, starfish, or yucca is best recommended). Okay, this part made me lose a little hope In your loadout, have a yinyang in the last (any) slot to swap out with. The rest of the other loadout should be your usual build, This build is NOT meant for ultras. Agreed

'''How it works:''' Use bulb to attract the mobs; bone shreds them, healing petals heal, bubbles for quick travel, and yinyang to swap them so you don't have to wait a full petal rotation to bubble again.

'''Strengths:''' Ants and Fire Ants (all types except for the ultra queen) Ubone is actually great for Mfire ants if you didn't know

'''Weaknesses:''' Possibly termites and Ultras Hmm questionable about the termite part

'''Author:''' -Diamond- (the florr user) I know this guy again

=== Ant Hole Conqueror ===
=== '''''I came, I saw, I conquered.'''''===
'''Petals Needed:''' 2 L+Jellies, as many L+Lightnings as you have.

'''How It Works:''' Very simple. Jelly provides defense and knockback, Lightning does mass area damage. Can take down Rare Fire Ant Burrows at full Legendary. Most likely requires Mythics in order to take down Epic ones. Extend your petals when you go in to maximize damage (unless you die from the ants if you do that; in that case then don’t go in there/don’t extend your petals). Moving is optional when in Ant Holes and Fire Ant Burrows. If you notice yourself taking damage you can move, but otherwise there is no need to move during the initial swarm.

'''Variations:''' If you have at least 4 Lightnings and 2 Jellies then you can throw in anything else you want that’s useful (like Magnet for collecting drops, healing petals if they’re strong enough, Fang for heals and a bit of damage, it’s up to you). Full on Lightning spam also works.

Beetle Eggs can also be good but the egg must be Legendary or above to survive the ants amd hatch faster. Ant Eggs are not highly recommended since Epic ones only spawn Rare Soldier Ants and Legendary ones spawn Epic Soldier Ants with a longer reload time.

'''Strengths:''' Ant Holes, Fire Ant Burrows, Centipedes, Desert Centipedes.

'''Weaknesses:''' Avoiding every mob other than the build’s strengths is recommended, unless you are very confident you can take it down. And never engage in Ultra and Super battles.

'''Tips:'''
*Keep a second build for running/defense, as this build can’t even take down Epic mobs easily (assuming full Legendary set).
*This build is mostly for Rare or Epic Holes/Burrows because thats where Lightning can actually utilize the chain.
*Get Jellies and Lightnings from Jellyfish, which are ridiculously easy to solo. All you need is 2 Magnets, which drop from R+ Shells, 1 E+ healing if you don’t get instakilled by L+ Jellyfish and as many Wings as you can fit (E+ required, L+ recommended). Also at least 2 reload upgrades are needed. Then find your way to Jellyfish Fields, find a L Jellyfish and extend your petals. That’s it.
*Alternative: Light spam with 1-2 E+ Fasters if you’re willing to risk your magnets.
*Don’t trust whatever the author says since he’s like level 50 and only found this build to be useful. Take this with a pinch of [[Salt]].
*His English proficiency is also not very high so you may be unable to understand what he says. Please forgive him for that.

'''Author:''' CyberDGun (wiki name), FrostTheGrey (florr name)
==='''Nice Pattern'''===
'''''"Corn and leaf, corn and leaf, corn and leaf..."'''''
*'''Petals Used:''' A magnet required, then add a L+corn and a L+leaf basing on your slots.
*'''How it Works:''' Corns can deal some good damage if used correctly, and the leaves heal you quickly.
*'''Strengths:''' Consistent healing and good damaging combined.
*'''Weaknesses:''' Can't kill ultra queens.
*'''Variations:''' One or two leaves can be changed into L+stingers to add the damage.
* '''Tip:''' When facing M ants, try to damage them by yourself and deal enough damage to get drops. (Otherwise you may find other players annoying, always preventing you from getting drops.
*'''Author:''' (no account)
*
==='''The Armored Flower'''===
'''''"This flower is rooted to the ground."'''''

NOTE : Since root doesn't stack with itself, this build doesn't work sadly. Disc Heals sponge bur cutter RAM - TheLoafCat
*'''Petals Used:''' There is no specific number of petals used, the petals you use depend on the petals' rarity and the rarity of ants you're fighting. First, put in a cutter. Then, put in some roots. The total armor value of the roots should be greater than the damage of the most powerful mob you're fighting. If you don't have enough roots then put leaf, yucca or starfish into this build. Then put in a magnet and a bulb or uranium if you have slots remaining.
*'''How it Works:''' The roots give armor and prevent you from taking any damage. The bulb and uranium get mobs to attack you. The cutter is for directly damaging the mobs by body (hey, you take no damage, so you can just go and hit the mobs with your body, right). Magnet for getting loot, you really don't wanna spend 30 seconds to reach a piece of loot just to pick it up.
*'''Strengths:''' all melee mobs
*'''Weaknesses:''' all ranged mobs. Mantis is a greater problem as it decreases your armor, but so far I fail to find any ranged mobs in ant hell.
*'''Variations:''' do slip in a soil or cactus if you don't trust me or if you don't have enough roots, or some bones for additional armored damage. Bur perhaps against leafbugs, but bur is really unnecessary as leafbugs don't spawn in ant hells either.
* '''Tip:''' Prepare a healing/bubbles build in your secondary, you never know when a hornet will spawn in ant hell. (To be honest, nor do I.)
*'''Author:''' Same author as 'bane of the beetles' on the desert page.

=== '''<big>Meet the Heavy</big>''' ===
'''''"I'm heavy petals guy, and this is my weapon..."'''''
*'''Petals Used:''' Alternating L+ rocks and L+ heavies
*'''How it Works:''' Heavies yeet the ants away from you and rocks deal damage so the fight doesn't take forever. The longer the more trollin
*'''Strengths:''' A bunch of L+ ants and single M ants, also ridiculously high survivability.
*'''Weaknesses:''' Hordes of L+/M+ ants depending on rarity of petals, doesn't kill ant queen, low-ish damage means you might miss out on drops. Also other players will get pissed at you for yeeting ants across half the map.
*'''Variations:''' Change some of the heavies into pincers, and change some of the rocks into leaves or L+ stingers.
* '''Tip:''' If you ever get overwhelmed just lead whatever is chasing you into a stronger player.

=== '''The Ring of Death''' ===

The Best Guide For All Ant Hells

Okay, so basically I'm someone with 72+ ultras my name is TheLoafCat in both hornex.pro and florr.io. If you want to farm AH2 literally just Ubone bubbles and then charge at them with heals and sponge. (meta) If you want ultra drops make sure you have a good rotation use L+ Wings and faster if you don't have rotation talents (really depends on a lot of stuff so I can't give you the rarity) and iris if you can have yourself not one shotted. If you're a AH2 greeder (like me) Use 7 Ubones 1Uiris 1Ubur and then just greed. Second build 2,3 should be Lights so you can switch them on for Uegg greeding. If you want bones in (Ah3) use Uwings and Ubones but if you only have mythics I'm afraid you'll have to go back to farming AH1 or other stuff for ultras. Use sponge if you don't have enough Ubones and Uwings to use because you might find yourself sandwiched every time. Anyways, if you have Mbone use it on termites since they can double hit (Probably won't get drops even then) but of course Uwings are still superior to Mbones. (Also use magnet for both AH1 - 3 unless you're very nob) If you want a load of wings go to AH2 and go to EU or AS because US/NA is nearly always crowded. Don't farm in the most down chamber since there are a lot of eggs and Uwing greeders such as: freaky and shiberry. However for the people who have a lot of M+ wings and are lucky to find a nearly empty server use Missiles or Wings for a good chance of drops. Don't spam YGGGGGGGGGGGGG(like Madhatter when he dies) when you die since they'll most likely mark you using the Dark Mark. If you're inside a guild and spam YGGGGGGGGGGGGG in guild chat they may kick you out because of that. (That's the fate of Madhatter) If you somehow have a lot of Ubones but can't get AH1 drops use sponge and U+ bones in the second chamber for good drops.
