=== "Destroyer" ===
"''Someone is annoyed by mobs that he uses an outraging build to survive.''"

Petals used: Wing/Web/Missile/Faster/Wing/Web/Missile/Faster/Third Eye/Ant Egg

How it works: Wings to kill at far distance which are good for L+ Wasps, Missile to shoot mobs, Web to slow down enemies, Ant Egg to protect you, Third Eye for ranged attacking.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             Strengths: Hordes of enemies, Ul Wasp, Bush, Mantis, AND ANY OTHER ULTRA MOB IN JUNGLE.                                                                                                                                                         Weaknesses: None(Please test build and edit its weaknesses)                                                                                                                                                                                                                   Tips: Run away from mobs if you are too close to them, Third Eye is only for ranged attacking. Make sure that your eggs has spawned before attacking or it will make things worse. Use M+ ant eggs for greater range attack. You must be level 65+ and have 15k~30k hp. Keep in mind that leafbug has the highest armor of all mobs, you may swap third eye and ant egg to lightning/iris as they ignore mob armor, note that without third eye, things will get risky and you might get killed. Use ankh if needed and put it in a safe place.  Just keep in mind that if you are using ankh it somewhat disables your petals  like a petal is in reload time.                                                                                          Variations:  You may swap ant egg to beetle egg, note that beetle egg somehow has fast spawning time but it is only in a set of one.

'''"GOTTA GO FAST"'''

Petals used: rose (L-UI)/Light/Light (M-UI)/Bubble/Bubble/Bubble/bubble/bubble/light/light

How it works: The bubbles acts like a booster, and the lights acts like a shield, mostly used from all of the flowers, The light need to be mythic to Ultra, And rose is L-UI, The bubbles can be at L+, But good for speeding (unlike ultras)

Strengths: Wasps, Hordes of Mobs chasing (use your bubble), And all mobs.

Weakness: Bubble only having 1 hp and needed to be faced in the back, This also Weakens the boost, Use UI+ powder for extra speed. not having powder cant help you run away due to the lack of extra speed.

Tip: Don't get to close into the mobs while low, This will kill you, Also wasp can be dangerous for bubble since its missiles one-shot bubble, Use powder to gain speed.

Variations: Swap the second last light for powder. This does decrease light amount but you have to use powder.

The One Shot Everyone Build.

"Five players? More like one"

Petals Used: Bubble U+, Sponge L+, Iris M+, Blood Stinger U+, Cotton M+, Cotton, M+, Cotton M+, Yucca U+, Yucca U+, Ying Yang C+.                                           Second build: 3 L+ Bubbles, M+ Cotton, + Cotton, M+ Soil, U+ Yucca, M+ Starfish, M+ Starfish, M+ Cotton, U+ Bone.

How it Works: You use Ying Yang to use your DPS petals and point towards your opponent. Then you use the Ultra Bubble to start hunting them down with the Iris and Blood Stinger. The Cotton, Sponge, and Yucca prevents you from dying easily. When your opponent dies, you start switching to healing and then you move on to the next target.

Strengths: Everything but eggers with U+ Beetle Egg and Swing users with U+ Cotton (Yomama101).

Weaknesses: People with more U+ Blood Stingers than you and DPS supers. (I must kill Yomama101 even though he's using U Cotton and Swing with a ton of Ultra Blood Stingers.) If someone comes with Ultra Beetle Eggs you'll just have to leave them alone as there's nothing you can do.

Tips: If you're able to hit someone with U+ Iris try to follow them but not let them heal too much, when they do go for another attempt to wipe them off.  If they don't have much Cotton or good healing petals just follow them until they die. (If you've already done a good amount of damage to them)

=== '''Build, requires skill!!!111''' ===

* Petals required: 4 E+ Dandelions (If M+ use only 2) 3 M+Missile 2 M+Eggs(preferably beetle, make sure you can switch you eggs to a different petal if your opponent is using lightning.) Use a bubble/powder which you can switch to a healing item.
* 1 M+ dandelion is better. M+ missiles aren't the best. Also, they use bones, not lightning.
* Strategy: So you stand around at midrange taunting them. You shoot the dandelions or spam them and have you eggs give chase and they loose an immense amount of health and can't heal. The Missiles ensure their quick death and the bubble/powder just lets you chase them. 
* What if they dodge? What if they, like me, using Btringer with bubbles, kills the beetles and charges at you and one shot you. Cotton is available, so we don't need to use healing, instead, we use sponge.
* Strengths: Totally demolishes 1v1s.
* Weaknesses: A little weak to lightning due to eggs.

==='''Ant Melter (bone)''' ===
'''''"It fuses their bones!"'''''
*'''Petals Used:''' In this order: M+ Bone, L+ Faster, L+ Rose/ Dahlia, L+ Web, M+ Bone, L+ Faster, L+ Rose/ Dahlia, L+ Web, M+ Cactus, L+ Antennae.
*'''How it Works:''' The bone has more armor than Legendary ant summons, and therefore will take no damage from them while still doing max full damage to the summons. Faster ensures that bones will be consistently hitting ants. Web slows down ants (and players), making it easier to hit them with bone. Cactus lets you tank damage if you get hit by an ant. Rose/ Dahlia regenerates health if you get hit by ants. Antennae makes it easier to spot and chase targets.
*Use da YY with da bone and then one shot da summons.
*'''Strengths:''' Mythic and below ant eggs (The ones that spawn Legendary ants). Players that have less than 972 body damage.
*'''Weaknesses:''' Mythic beetle eggs. Beetles have more contact damage than ants, and can destroy bones easily. Anything that can destroy bone extremely fast. Especially well timed tomato, claw, stinger, or blood stinger.
* '''Variations:''' Replace faster with heavy. This will help slow and push ants down, and can help with defeating beetle minions.
*'''Tip:''' Try to time web so that it activates on ants, not to the side. Keep a bubble or powder in your secondary load-out to swap to if you enemy flees.
* '''Author:''' Bumbubee (Raccoonsk on the Discord server.)

* Orrrrrr you could just charge with tomato and then one shot them :)
* '''Strengths:''' Good against players who use full healing builds. 
* Doesn't work against actualy PvP players, cotton is just better.
* '''Weaknesses:''' [[Dandelion]]s are hard to aim and without sufficient healing, the build will be useless.
* '''Variations:''' Replace the healing items with [[Fangs]] or a [[Stinger]] with a Mythic [[Faster]]. 
* '''Tip:''' After using [[Pincer]], keep a safe distance away from [[Beetle]] since the cooldown for the [[Pincer]] may end suddenly and will result in your death.

=== Assasinator ===
'''(this build is based on the "assassinator" petal, a fanmade petal by Play Florr.io)'''
* '''Petals Used:''' 3x R+ Dandelion/ 3x E+ Iris/ 2 of any other Petal
* '''How it Works:''' the Dandelions prevent the victim from healing; once that it set, you can poison them with the Iris(es), guaranteeing their death
* '''Method 1:''' Carefully aim the Dandelion at the nearest flower shoot at it; if it hits the flower, advance toward it and at least TRY to hit it with an Iris. if a success, the flower will LIKELY die
* '''Method 2:''' Stay in a defensive position until you are close enough to your target; once you are close enough, attack them. this usually will kill them UNLESS they figure out a way to escape
* '''Strengths:''' with a fair strategy, you can gain a high score
* '''Weaknesses:''' without healing petals, you ''probably'' might die easily
* '''Alternatives:''' Pincers and Grapes also do poison damage. but they are rather weak, so it might not be entirely worth it

==='''The Overlord''' ===
'''''"kid from diep [[Builds - Garden#The Auto-Gunner|v2]]"'''''
*'''Petals Used:''' full Beetle/Ant eggs
*'''How it Works:''' Beetles and Ants will destroy other players' petals and are the main source of damage. 
* '''Strengths:''' Stinger/Bubble users and players with weak petals (for an example of what builds to target, take a look at the build above this one), and salt spammers, since salt doesn't reflect damage when the petal is reloading. 
* '''Weaknesses:''' Your main weakness is Lightning users, since they can chain between pets and damage you. Note that Ant Eggs are slightly better at fending off Lightnings than Beetle Eggs. Be careful around deathwheels (aka WoD) too.
*'''Variations:''' Sticks can be used, but they are more defensive and do not chase players. Ultra+ Sticks are good due to the sandstorm's large size. 
*'''Tip:''' Stay within your pets and don't get caught off guard. 
* '''Author:''' that creeper guy
==='''The Witch-Hunter v1''' ===
'''''"Give me your epic basic now, or else I will find where you live"'''''
*'''Petals Used:''' Faster / Bubble / Bubble / Stinger / Stinger / Stinger / [Stinger/Cactus] / Shell / [Cactus/Sponge] / [Cactus/Shell]
*'''How it Works:''' Mythic Bubbles, paired with a faster, allow the user to pursue players quickly. Boost toward a player and, at the same time, press the attack button. Your stingers will revolve into position and deal massive damage to the victim. Cactus and Shell allow you to tank more hits.
* '''Strengths:''' Weak/low health players. With skill, it can be used against stronger users. The user FoldingPapers also hates this build, along with any other build including Stingers. 
* '''Weaknesses:''' Deathwheels (aka WoD), Egg users, and Salt spammers. 
*'''Variations:''' A few counters to some of this build's weaknesses: Faster can be swapped out for Antennae for increased vision. Swap out Bubbles for Lightning and Powder when fighting Egg users. 
*'''Tip:''' Note that this is a build for Legendary/Mythic/Ultra petal users, so the rarity of the petals should all be '''Mythic (+)'''. Do not confront deathwheelers and only attack salt spammers when they are at low health. 
* '''Author:''' that creeper guy (who is going to get lots of hate for creating this)

Boost into players to deal high damage and take them out. Highly recommended but hard to get, Third Eye extends your Stingers' range.

==='''The Element of Surprise''' ===
'''''"Oh hello there! *kills* Bye!"'''''
*'''Petals Used:''' Primary slots: [[Antennae]]/[[Yin Yang]]/L+[[Bubble]]/L+[[Bubble]]/L+[[Bubble]]/L+[[Light]]/M+[[Missile]]/M+[[Missile]]/M+[[Missile]]/L+[[Light]]  Secondary slots: [[Faster]]/Healing petal, preferably M+ [[Rose]]/High dps petal, preferably [[Light]]/E+[[Salt]]/High dps petal, preferably [[Light]]/E+[[Salt]]/High dps petal, preferably [[Light]]/E+[[Salt]]/High dps petal, preferably [[Light]]/E+[[Salt]]
*'''Required Skills''': Epic+ Reload, Legendary Loadout and preferably Epic+ Health
*'''How it Works:''' First, right click and left click simultaneously so that your [[Bubble|Bubbles]] push you towards your enemy while you shoot your [[Missile|Missiles]] at them. Immediately after that, press R so you switch loadouts. Epic+ Reload will ensure that your High dps petals reload quickly (that's why it says preferably [[Light]]). This will surprise your enemy, minimize their time to react and tear them apart most of the time. The 4 [[Salt|Salts]] will reflect the damage dealt to you back to your enemy, making your enemy less likely to survive.
*'''Strengths:''' Enemies who have about the same amount of health as you. [[Ant Egg|E]][[Beetle Egg|gg]] users will suffer as well since the secondary build will kill their pets, thus leaving them vulnerable to your attacks because they lack both offense and defense. [[Missile]]<nowiki/>rs also struggle against this build because they lack damage in close-range combat. Players with slow reaction speed are very easy to get killed by this build.
*'''Weaknesses:''' Players with a lot more health than you can be a threat. [[Salt]]<nowiki/>ers will reflect the damage back to you so don't attack them. Deathwheelers have both defense and offense so they are a big threat to users of this build. To counter this, you can try to shoot the [[Missile|Missiles]] through their petals when they are in attack position.
*'''Variations:''' Use a M+[[Dandelion]] in the place of one of the [[Missile|Missiles]] to prevent your opponent from healing. This way if the build did not kill them, they are less likely to heal while running away. However, this variation reduces damage. Light can also be replaced by pollen to damage others while "tactical retreating" with [[Bubble|Bubbles]].
*'''Tip:''' Rapidly swapping [[Yin Yang]] with the healing petal can help you lock on to your opponent, giving you even more advantage. However, this technique is very tricky and takes lots of practice to master.
*'''Author:''' [[User:Florreryay|Florreryay]] (florr.io name: SryBadWifi)
==='''Sierra L-M dominator''' ===
'''''"AAARGH! these people are soooooo frikin annoyin!"'''''
*'''Petals Used:''' ante, stinger, binger, stinger, leaf, bubble, bubble, bubble, leaf; rubber, yucca, rubber, starfish, rubber, yucca, rubber, leaf, rubber, basil.
*'''How it Works:''' RAM into opponents using primary build! you can deal 73k damage at once! XD Of cource, the binger damages you for 2.4k so the leaves can heal you over time. (The time it takes binger to recharge is completely enough, so you don't have to worry about being killed by your own binger.) If for some reason you get really low on health, switch to the secondary layout and go into defensive position. The rubbers make you sooooo annoying to hit! :p so you can heal reasonably safely.
*'''Strengths:''' L-M pvp arenas
*'''Weaknesses:''' people who use salt, or sponge with a load of leaves
*'''Variations:''' Go even crazier and use jelly to absolutely KNOCK PEOPLE FLYING! XDDDDDDDDDDD
*'''Tip:''' stay away from spongers and salters... they are a real threat.
*'''Author:''' the 'bane of the beetles' author.
==='''Salter''' ===
'''''"Salt in pvp? HECK YEAH!"'''''
*'''Petals Used:''' (L-M) Salt x 5, yucca x 5
*'''How it Works:''' REFLECT ALL INCOMING DAMAGE!!!
*'''Strengths:''' pvp
*'''Weaknesses:''' maybe spongers
* '''Variations:''' replace yucca with starfish or leaf!
*'''Tip:''' YEET YOURSELF ONTO EVERY STINGER YOU FIND!
* '''Author:''' same as above
==='''Sponger''' ===
'''''"Damage equals to no damage!"'''''
*'''Petals Used:''' (L-M, preferably M) Sponge, leaf x 7, cactus x 2
*'''How it Works:''' Sponge spreads all incoming damage! Leaf negates them! Cactus absolutely further diminishes them!
*'''Strengths:''' pvp
*'''Weaknesses:''' maybe salters
*'''Variations:''' use yucca instead of leaf? but that would lack offence
*'''Tip:''' make sure your sponge doesn't break, or rather just get 2 sponges.
*'''Author:''' same as above
*'''Note:''' I've just looked through this page and this build and the salter can easily beat any of them.

=== '''Is it a fruit or vegetable?''' ===
'''''"its a vegetable!!1!11!!"'''''

* '''Petals Used:''' 5 M tomato, m sponge, 3 m starfish, third eye
*'''How it Works:''' Sponge and starfish prevent you from dying right away and tomato nukes players!
*'''Strengths:''' pvp
*'''Weaknesses:''' possibly salters, eggers, other sponge users, stingers, wings, missiles
*'''Variations:''' use cotton/rose/yucca/leaf/dahlia instead of starfish
*'''Tip:''' make sure your sponge doesn't break, or rather just get 2 sponges.
*'''Author:''' [[User:Ovary|Ovary]]
*'''Note:''' Tomato is actually a fruit, Idiot!
===Pearl Build ===
'''''"you live to die"'''''
*'''Petals Used:''' pearl/pearl/pearl/pearl/pearl/sponge/cotton/starfish/cotton/pollen
*Seconday: sponge/starfish/cotton/starfish/stinger/third eye/pollen/web/rubber/stinger
*'''How it Works:''' Pearl as dps against stinger users. if against a low petal damage player like salt, then swap pollen in, switch to the first sponge, and use stinger with third eye. If sponge about to break, like in an egger fight, then switch the other sponge in. after a battle, use all the healing petals with pollen, swapping the second pollen with third eye.
*'''Strengths:''' all mythic pvp builds, except full stick user. note even if you are fighting someone using this build, you should be able to beat them, or at least draw. this build heavily relies on pearl skill, and noobs will not be able to convert this build's potential dps.
*'''Weaknesses:''' full stick user, you must cheese them, because you do not have enough dps. 1 pearl ~= 1 stick; if they use more than 5 stick, u ded
*'''Variations:''' 3 pearl 2 cotton 1 star 1 sponge 2 tringer 1 third eye; assasin build, high dps, low defense. if you need defend, ie against stinger bubble user, replace tringer with pollen
*'''Tip:''' don't be a noob, you can beat everyone with this build, but you can also die to skill issue, or stick users. Against normal b/aegg users, just use pearl as dps, you should shred them fast enough. if you are at risk of dying, use rubber. if you are still at risk of dying, switch in web.
*'''Author:''' lordpearl | the pvp master
*'''PS''': this build will shred all the builds above
*
==='''Stingers shubbed in your face''' ===
'''''"OH NO HE'S COMING! GOTTA RUN!"'''''
*'''Petals Used:''' x6 Stingers or, (mythic+ required) x1 Yin Yang, and x3 bubbles (mythic+ is preffered.)
*'''How it Works:''' You use bubbles to boost and use the yin yang to keep it straight. the stingers are kept at the front to deal heavy damage. 
*'''Strengths:''' almost unpenatrateable. can kill almost anything.
*'''Weaknesses:''' 1. Salt bombers and salter users are a HUGE threat, as they launch damage back in you. even unusual salt users will SEND YOU TO HELL.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             2. if someone uses a dps build in pvp, they can destroy your stingers. leaving you to become that rare basic they worked hard for.[[File:Screenshot 2023-09-25 4.23.20 PM.png|thumb|A general look of what this should look like]]'''Variations:''' 1. you can replace a stinger with third eye for further range.                                                                                                                                                                                                  2. replace the stingers with blood stingers for faster reload at the cost for self damage.
* '''Author: Guest #0824'''
==='''The eliminator''' ===
'''''"OH NO A PINCER AND AN IRIS!”'''''
*'''Petals Used:''' L+ Pincer/L+ Iris/M+Dandelion/M+ Bubble x2/M+Fang x3/M+ Stinger
*Secondary build: Just your normal healing build.
*'''How it Works:''' The pincer slows the enemy down, the Iris makes the poison around 3x stronger, the dandy prevents heals, the bubbles are to run from salt users (even unusual salts can easily send your flower to hell), the fangs are to heal just in case you can’t switch to the secondary in time, and the stinger is able to bring the finishing blow to users who survive.
*'''Strengths:''' Deathwheels, Jelly users (occasionally), and lower level users
*'''Weaknesses:''' Salters. They use the poison and stinger as ways to kill you.
* '''Variations:''' Use a sponge in the healing build just in case a salter arrives
*'''Tip:''' Switch to the healing build if you see a salt user.
* '''Author:''' Justin1209


==='''Thing''' ===
'''''"There is no slogan"'''''
*'''Petals Used:''' Antenna, 2x faster, stinger and lightning alternating
*'''How it Works:''' Lightning stunlocks, stinger does damage
*'''Strengths:''' IDK
* '''Weaknesses:''' IDK
* '''Variations:''' Use different nuking petals for damage, one or three fasters, stinger/lightning instead of antenna, you could use for second build. 
*'''Tip:''' WARNING: This has never been tested. I just got inspiration from a video. This might be considered a joke build. 
* '''Author:''' I shall never tell you
Talisman MAN

* Petals used: full talisman, a few rice in sec loadout, and other healing petals
* How it works: Makes you immotal. Even if you do take damage heal.
* Strengths: when your enemy's petals are reloading switch to rice (no heal thou) and kill them fast.
* Weaknesses: Rice against you. Rice spawns fasst so it destroys your talisman and now you're not immortal! WOW
* Variations: Can change rice to light
* Tip: You look cool
* Author: haoxie11 (discord & florr)
===Stinger Rush ===
*'''Petals Used:''' 3x stinger, 2x bubble, powder, rose, faster, shell, antennae
*'''How it Works:''' Stingers deal high damage, rose heal you, bubbles let you rush to other players to deal damage, powder allows you to make a quick getaway in case of a mistake.
*'''Strengths:''' High damage and can even kill some players in seconds.
*'''Weaknesses:''' Long cooldown, not suitable for dealing with multiple players.
*'''Variations:''' Rose can be replaced with other healing petals.
*'''Tip:''' The stingers and bubbles must be M+, and the powder must be L+.
*'''Author:''' I won't tell you

==='''Health Steal''' ===
'''''"steals hp"'''''
*'''Petals Used:''' alternate between fang and faster
*'''How it Works:''' fangs steal hp, and faster speeds that up
*'''Strengths:''' light and rice
* '''Weaknesses:''' stinger
*'''Tip:''' to get, grind on leeches



The One Shot Everyone Build.

"Five players? More like one"

Petals Used: Bubble U+, Sponge L+, Iris M+, Blood Stinger U+, Cotton M+, Cotton, M+, Cotton M+, Yucca U+, Yucca U+, Ying Yang C+.                                           Second build: 3 L+ Bubbles, M+ Cotton, + Cotton, M+ Soil, U+ Yucca, M+ Starfish, M+ Starfish, M+ Cotton, U+ Bone.

How it Works: You use Ying Yang to use your DPS petals and point towards your opponent. Then you use the Ultra Bubble to start hunting them down with the Iris and Blood Stinger. The Cotton, Sponge, and Yucca prevents you from dying easily. When your opponent dies, you start switching to healing and then you move on to the next target.

Strengths: Everything but eggers with U+ Beetle Egg and Swing users with U+ Cotton (Yomama101).

Weaknesses: People with more U+ Blood Stingers than you and DPS supers. (I must kill Yomama101 even though he's using U Cotton and Swing with a ton of Ultra Blood Stingers.) If someone comes with Ultra Beetle Eggs you'll just have to leave them alone as there's nothing you can do.

Tips: If you're able to hit someone with U+ Iris try to follow them but not let them heal too much, when they do go for another attempt to wipe them off.  If they don't have much Cotton or good healing petals just follow them until they die. (If you've already done a good amount of damage to them)

title: Builds - Road Builds
content:
{{Builds - Road Builds}}

__TOC__


== Builds ==

=== '''Noobster''' ===
'''''"Everyone's first bubble build"'''''
* '''Petals Used: 4 * Bubbles, 1* Yin Yang, 1 * heal, and 4 * Light.'''
* '''How it Works: Bubbles push you, Yin Yang calibrate Bubbles' direction, and Light makes the velocity overlaps more.'''
* '''Strengths: Petals are easy to collect, and pretty fast.'''
* '''Weaknesses: Makes you very delicate.'''
* '''Variations: Some Heavy instead of Light.'''
* '''Tip: Turn your petal rotation speed for this build to the bottom.'''
* '''Author: [https://official-florrio.fandom.com/wiki/User:YITB YITB] (both fandom and florr)'''

=== '''Unstoppable''' ===
'''''"Actually stoppable."'''''
* '''Petals Used: 6 * Bubbles, 1 * Yin Yang, 1 * heal, 1 * Poo, maybe 1 * Sponge.'''
* '''How it Works: Bubbles push you, Yin Yang calibrates Bubbles' direction, and Poo makes you survive in high rarity zones.'''
* '''Strengths: Petals are easy to collect, and pretty fast.'''
* '''Weaknesses: Makes you very delicate.'''
* '''Variations: Some Corn.'''
* '''Tip: Turn your petal rotation speed for this build to the bottom.'''
* '''Author: [[User:Hamber99|6]]'''

=== '''Speed or Safety?''' ===
'''''"Why not both?"'''''
* '''Petals Used:'''
[[File:CalicolicaBubbleBuild V2.png|CalicolicaBubbleBuild V2.png]]
* '''How it Works: This build contains utility petals under the bubbles, which the user can swap in when necessary.'''
* '''Strengths: It serves as a regular bubble build with all bubbles on, and a safe bubble build with some or most of the utility petals equipped in place of the bubbles.'''
* '''Weaknesses: With high enough [[Talents/Reload|Reload talent]], bumping into mobs can push the user backwards or go in an unpredictable direction. [[Root]] may not be able to keep up with the flower's speed and end up being destroyed from time to time, rendering it useless sometimes.'''
* '''Variations: The [[Yin Yang]] slot is up to the user's preference. [[Cotton]] can be used in place of Root.'''
* '''Tip: Use a slower rotation speed for better control.'''
* '''Author: [https://official-florrio.fandom.com/wiki/User:Calicolica Christina]'''

title: Builds - Sewers
content:
{{Builds - Sewer|Sewer}}{{Background|图片=Sewersb.png|大小=2000px}}
__TOC__
== Builds ==

=== Lightning Wheel ===
''"Zeus"''

* '''Primary Build:''' (Preferably all M+) Lightning (x5), any combination of Pincer/Jelly/Web
* '''Secondary Build:''' Battery (x2), Lotus, Healing Petals (x7)
* '''How it works:''' Lightning is your primary source of damage due to being able to mass murder flies without missing. Your other 5 petals are very important, due to the high movement speed of sewers mobs. This will allow you to beat any mob in a 1v1 in sewers that's equal rarity as your highest rarity crippling petal. In your secondary build, it provides healing which the primary build does not have. The lotus can be swapped into primary build when taking damage from spider, while the batteries are there to punish flies that get too close to your when healing (2 Lbatteries one-shot LFlies, you can replace a battery with healing if you have M+Battery).
* '''Strengths:''' ''Flies,'' Spiders, 1v1 <Highest Rarity Petal roaches
* '''Weaknesses:''' Higher rarity roaches, armies of roaches, (I never tested this on an ultra spider yet)
* '''Variations:''' Replace 2 petals with fasters, as 2 fasters synergize well with lightning, allowing more dps. However, this is not recommended due to Lightning's main function is being able to murder flies, and flies have no hp anyway; this variation makes killing spiders and roaches more inconsistent.
* '''Tip:''' Space out the lightning, for both more consistent fly-kills and more consistent slowing. Running the web version of this build somewhat improves this build's ability to fight large hordes of mobs. Be careful of how webs require some aiming unlike pincer (unless you're running 3+ M+ webs). If you are still struggling with roaches, consider using rubber to give yourself some breathing room after getting hit.

===The Cockroach Killer===
'''''Bad cockroach'''''

* '''Petals Used:''' M+ Heavy / M+ Jelly / M+ Stinger / [L+ Powder/healing] / Heavy / Jelly / Stinger / Heavy / Jelly / Stinger. 10th slot: [L+ Jelly/Magnet]
*'''How it Works:''' Cockroaches have insane speed but slow down over time. Heavies and Jellies will push the Cockroach away until it is "tired." The Stingers will be dealing enough damage so that the Cockroach won't kill you as your petals are reloading.  
*'''Strengths:''' Mythic and below Cockroaches, Flies, and Spiders
*'''Weaknesses:''' Could potentially work against Ultra Cockroach if you have Ultra petals, but unlikely due to its health scaling. 
*'''Variations:''' Replace Heavies with Jellies or vice versa. 
*'''Tip:''' (I put this build up here because I have seen too many noobs die from mcockroaches and people need this)

*'''Author:''' that creeper guy
=== Heavy ===
'''''This flower is so heavy that nothing gets in the way'''''

'''Petals Used:''' 

* Loadout 1: L+Heavy/M+Stinger/L+Heavy/M+Stinger/L+Heavy/M+Stinger/L+Heavy/M+Stinger/L+Heavy/L+Magnet
* Loadout 2: 10x MWings

'''How it works:''' Heavy can push away mobs; Stinger does high damage; Magnet picks up loots.

'''Strengths:''' A few Mflies and Mspiders.

'''Weaknesses:''' Lots of Mflies and Mspiders chasing you together.

'''Tip:''' Stack on other flowers and AFK

'''Variations:'''  Replace some of Mwings with healing petals

'''Author:''' yblui

===Fly Sniper===
'''''Time to snipe flies!'''''
*'''Petals Used: 9''' Missiles with 1 Magnet, (mmag is best)
*'''How it Works:''' Missiles can able to destroy flies easily.
*'''Strengths:''' Mythic flies. but hard for mythic spiders
*'''Weaknesses:''' Ultra spiders
*'''Variations:''' Replace missiles with healing petals
*'''Tip:''' It is best with mythic+ missiles. because the homing still exists.
*'''Author:''' Slime
==='''The Rockwheel'''===
'''''Approved by Dwayne 'The Rock' Johnson'''''
* '''Petals Used:''' 1 [[Rose]]/[[Leaf]], 1 [[Faster]], 7 [[Rock]]. 10th slot: Magnet
*'''How it Works:''' Rocks have high health and decent damage and can land several hits on Flies and Spiders before dying. The Faster increases DPS. 
*'''Strengths:''' Sewer mobs
*'''Weaknesses:''' Generally, Rock petals will shred anything one rarity below but get clapped by anything one rarity above. A full Mythic Rockwheel will be virtually invincible, but a Legendary or lower build will struggle in the harder Sewer maps. They also leave you vulnerable when reloading. 
*'''Variations:''' Mix in some more Leaves to combat poison and have some defense while your rocks regenerate. Heavies and Stingers are good alternatives if you do not have enough Rocks. 
*'''Tip:''' Be careful not to knock mobs into other flowers with your Rocks. 
*'''Author:''' that creeper guy

=== Spike Bounce ===
'''''No, not THE Spike Bounce! and second of all, NO TRINGERS/PINGERS ALLOWED!'''''

* '''Petals Used:''' 4x L+ Jelly/5x E+ Leaf/E+ Magnet
* '''Strategy:''' The Jellies are meant to knock away mobs, while the leaves help you heal; on the contrary, the Magnet is simply just to pick up loot
* '''Strengths:''' anything that has a base speed below 130% and requires body damage to end your life. (e.g. Spiders, Flies, Aggressive Moths)
* '''Weaknesses:''' Roaches, since they have INSANE Movement Speed while aggressive.
* '''Alternatives:''' replace one of the Leaves with a Powder to run away from Spiders and chase Moths...
*'''Tip:''' okay...MAYBE you could try adding a Stinger to your build...BUT ONLY IN OFFENSIVE TERMS!
* '''Author:''' FoldingPapers

=== '''Sewer Farmer''' ===
'''''Cockroaches, Flies, and Spiders? NOT ANYMORE!'''''
* '''Petals Used:''' Tringer, tringer, tringer, m+ faster, m+ antennae, m+ lotus, m+ rose, m+ leaf, m+ pincer, and m+ heavy.
* '''How it Works:''' Tringers shred mobs and flies, fasters make the tringers come back faster, antennae so you can see the mobs, lotus to defend against spiders, rose and leaf for healing, pincer to slow cockroaches down, and heavy for knockback.
* '''Strengths:''' Sewer!''
* '''Weaknesses:''' Ultra flies (use pingers if you're grinding them)
* '''Variations:''' On the bottom, use 3 m+ bubbles, m+ missle, m+ wing, leg+ magnet, 2 m+ yucca, m+ web, and m+ jelly. 
* '''Tip:''' USE YOUR MYTHICS
* '''Author:''' [[User:Bobz99|Bobz99]]
==='''Anti-M Roach Heavy Weapon'''===
'''''Here's the Heavies, Roach!'''''
*'''Petals Used:''' M Antannae, M+ Fangs, UL Sand, Tringer, M Heavy, Blood Tringer/Tringer, M+ Fangs, L+ Pincer, M Heavy, M+(UL) Web
*'''Secondary loadout:''' L+ Magnet, Bubbles ×6, M Faster, M+ Light ×2
*'''How it Works:''' place the Web beside the Roach and then attack it. After placing the web, switch the web if you want to get more DPS or keep it if you want to keep as much HP as possible by slowing down the Roach while consistently attacking tthrowing petals on it, the Pincer help to keep the Roach at a low speed. Fangs for healing.
*'''Strengths:''' Roaches and other Sewer Mobs. Don't need a ton of Ultras to use this build.
*'''Weaknesses:''' A lot of Uraniumed Roaches or Ultra+ ones...
* '''Variations:''' change the Rose to Fangs, Sand to DPS petals
*'''Tip:''' If your HP is very low then you should probably consider swiching Rose, Yucca, Dahila, etc.
*'''Author:''' florr.io ID: Peter314
*
==='''slow kill'''===
'''''there is no slooooooooooooooooooooooowgan.'''''
*'''Petals Used:''' web, pincer, web, pincer, web, pincer, web, [cactus/pincer], [leaf/rose/dahlia/yucca], magnet
*'''How it Works:''' webs and pincers slow enemies down and pincer deals damage.
*'''Strengths:''' all spiders. all moths. most flies. all roaches at a level not higher than that of your lowest-level web or pincer.
*'''Weaknesses:''' all roaches at a level higher than that of your lowest-level web or pincer.
*'''Variations:''' use a bulb in sewers 2 if you want to die quickly. Or rather, if you don't want to die, use a lotus somewhere. If you want to farm spiders then put in a few more pincers in replacement of webs.
*'''Tip:''' put the webs around the enemy then hit it with the pincers. If the enemy survives this then just run for your life.
*'''Author:''' the same author as 'medic' in builds-general.

==='''Anti all sewer mobs'''===
'''''Eat petals mobs!'''''
*'''Petals Used:''' M+heavy, M+Rock, M+Bone, M+tringer, M+heavy, M+rose, M+heavy, E+Magnet, M+tringer, M+Rock
*'''How it Works:''' Rock does lots of damage along with tringers. bone gets rid of all things below its rarety. 
*'''Strengths:''' All mythics and some ultras.
*'''Weaknesses:''' Ultra roach :skull:
*'''Variations:''' replace heavies with jellies and pincers
*'''Tip:''' Be careful when heavies are reloading. 
*'''Author:''' (ZSlash)

=== '''Low Budget anti- sewer mob''' ===
''For the ones who have (slightly) limited inventories and no TE :(''

* '''Petals Used:''' 4x L+ Fangs, 2x L+ Pincers, 2x M+ Jellies, 1x E+ Magnet, 1x L+ Antennae. Order is: fang/pincer/fang/jelly/fang/pincer/fang/jelly/magnet/antennae
* '''How it works:''' The pincers and jellies hold off mobs (and are even enough to stop a M+ roach from crushing you), while the fangs heal and do the majority of the damage
* '''Strengths:''' All mythics, most ultras (not for ultra fly or roach), meant for s4 with friends
* '''Weaknesses:''' Ultra roach :skull: and Ultra fly
* '''Variations:''' IDK u guys can test improvements
* '''Tip:''' Take care of hostile mobs, only attack m+ roaches 1 at a time and avoid aggroing other roaches

* '''Author:''' florr.io     


==='''Jellier'''===
'''''Not a troller~'''''
* '''Petals Used:'''M+ jelly/L+ magnet/M+ jelly/Tringer or Missile/Tringer or Missile/M+ jelly/Any L+ healing/M+ jelly/Tringer or Missile/Tringer or Missile/m+ jelly
* '''How it Works:''' jelly push away mobs and Tringer or Missile destroy everything
* '''Strengths:''' Moth(use bulb),fly,spider(This can destroy Ultra mobs)
*'''Weaknesses:''' Roach,4 M jelly can't destroy M roach.
*'''Variations:''' Use bulb to replace Tringer or Missile
*'''Tip:''' DONT DISTURB ROACH!!
*'''Author:'''Peter114




=== FOR THIRD EYE''' ===
'''''I love Spiders'''''
* '''Petals Used:'''/M+jelly*2(separate)/ tringer or tomatoes or sand /L+princers(can change as pinger/tomato) /some M+leaf(use some starfish if you are using blood tringers(recommended))/1+L+faster(dont be over 2)/M+web(change web to jelly/princer when ambushed spider/roach )(use antenna when there are no enemies around you to have field of view, third eye is nor must needed)
*'''How it Works:'''jelly stops everything and tringer,tomato,sand for main damage leaf for healing+damage, web for stoping annoying flys,princers for roaches
*'''Strengths:''' moth, '''spider(this build is designed for spider at first)''',fly
*'''Weaknesses:''' Roach
*'''Variations:''' can change some tringer/leaf into sand or other DPS petals, iris is not very good for sewers
*'''Tip:''' if UR alone, run away from '''Uflys''',and '''ankh''' is good for this build(solo 2Uspider together), '''poo''' is allowed when solo,<u>'''GOOD FOR PLAYERS WHO DONT HAVE [[Third Eye]],'''</u>this build can be used in other maps (maze/J5:change several unused petal to plank ,desert use princers,change leaf into bone if U have M+, leave 1 leaf(can change as M+fangs)as heal )<u>'''''this build is not very good in OCEAN'''''</u>
* '''Author:''' JSONH

==='''leave me alone!'''===
'''''Will you stop trying to kill me? thank you.'''''
*'''Build 1:''' A lot of Jellys, preferably M+, Yuccas, Basil, Talisman, Rubber
*'''Build 2:''' Classic bubbling set but with Magnet
*'''How it Works:''' Fly punch you away (Rubber),  you punch flies away (jellys), Yucca + Basil = Good heal and Talisman because evasion. after everythig dies, switch to secondary and collect stuff. May also give the "Rolling in dough" Archievement
*'''Strengths:''' Flies
*'''Weaknesses:''' Spiders
* '''Variations:''' Change jelly for heavy 
*'''Tip:''' I wound try not to aggro any roaches 

==='''Bug Killer'''===
'''''Kills 100% of bugs!'''''
*'''Petals Used:''' L+rubber/L+disc/M+basil/M+basil/M+basil/M+yucca/M+yucca/M+yucca/M+yucca/M+pincer
*'''How it Works:''' Rubber pushes you away from mobs you ram into, while Disc reduces the damage you take, and Yuccas heal while Basils boost healing.
*'''Strengths:''' All sewers mobs.
*'''Weaknesses:''' I have only tested this in the sewers. Try it out in some different areas and see what works!
* '''Variations:''' Replace pincer with another M+yucca.

=== '''Tip:''' Make sure to run from the mob as well, and try not to get trapped in between mobs. This build does NOT work on Super mobs or Ultra Spiders. Don't try it on them unless you have this build in all ultras. ===
*Talents: Mythic Second Chance, Rare Health, Super Medic, Super Sharp Edges, Body Toxicity, Legendary Loadout. If you have more TP, prioritize these Talents in this order: Health, then Reload, after that do Poison/Concentrated Poison, next is Antennae, and finally Magnetism. The other Talents don't help in this build, but you can use TP on the other Talents too.
*'''Author:''' LockeDorian

=== '''Battery''' ===
'''''Strikes the flies with battery'''''

* '''Petal used:''' 1 relic + 1 r/e ant egg + 2u starfish + 5/6 battery (required 5+ ppl) +0/1 moon
* '''How it works:''' mobs touch the spawned soldier termites and deal limited damage to the players.  This caused all of the players strikes lightning damage to the mobs. The moon prevents the mob from colliding directly into the mobs.
* '''Strenths:''' defeat multiple flies and roaches fast and safely. The more ppl there is, the stronger the build for each player.
* Weaknesses: multi u+ battery is difficult to obtain for a lot of players. Not everyone in this megesquad can loot.
* '''Variations:''' decide by yourself.
* '''Tip:''' do not collide the mobs directly
* '''Author:''' yangheli (not original)

=== Summoning Combat ===
'''''"Let your Beetles and Sandstorms slave away!!!"'''''

(this build is optimised for levels 50-65)

* '''Petals used:''' M beetle egg / M beetle egg / M beetle egg / M beetle egg / M mysterious stick / M leaf / M lightning / M claw / M pincer / M  magnet
* '''How it works:''' This works by using the summons to multihit flies, roaches and spiders. Due to the low body stats of the sewers mobs, this build makes it easy to farm mobs. The claw is for aggroing roaches, as they will be low by the time they chase you, the lightning is for crowd control, the pincer can be swapped for web as a defence, the magnet can be swapped with rice when low, and the leaf can be swapped with other healing petals, but staying with leaf is recommended.
* '''Strengths:''' Destroying most sewers mobs easily. However, be wary of M+ roaches and spiders, as they move quickly and summons may take too long to respawn. 
* '''Weaknesses:''' <u>DON'T</u> attack non sewers mobs with this build as it doesn't have enough defence to stay alive in a different location.
* '''Variations:''' as mentioned above
* '''Tip:''' Stay with people with triangles and webs to have an easier time surviving roaches, which aggro on you due to beetles and sandstorms.
* '''Author:''' CO0L_D (username in florr) Join my guild if you meet me! - Add a 1 here if you liked this build -> 1 

=== An Average Rammer ===
'''''"Ram mobs to death"'''''

* '''Petals Used:''' M+salt / M+yucca / M+yucca / M+yucca / L+sponge / M+locus / M+yucca / M+basil / L+disc / L+cutter
* '''How it works:''' This build makes use of the weak body stats of all sewers mobs. The yuccas are for healing, basil for buffing the heal, the locus to cancel out spider poison, and disc, cutter and salt to increase dps. The sponge is for tanking out higher tier mobs. You can replace the sponge with another heal if you are not skilled at using the sponge or don't know how to sponge reset. 
* '''Strengths:''' All sewers mobs near your petals' level or under. 
* '''Weaknesses:''' Sewers mobs at higher levels than your petals or other mobs with high body stats.
* '''Variations:''' If you don't have basil, you can swap it out for more heals. The same is for salt and cutter. You can also trade out yucca for starfish but this is not recommended as it only heals you up to 70%. You can also swap out a yucca for a monstera if you have a high levelled one and are in a squad.
* '''Tip:''' Keep a 3 slot magic bubble build in 2nd loadout in case you encounter ultra mobs (except for moth, which is easy, and spider, which you should avoid). Gameplay against ultras is simply equipping 2 bubbles and magic leaf/orb to run away. You ram the ultra once, bubble away, heal up and resume the ramming cycle again. This is one of the only builds that can survive U Roach and still kill it relatively quickly.
* '''Author:''' CO0L_D (username in florr) Join my guild if you meet me! - Add a 1 here if you liked this build -> 1

=== Fly Flapper ===
:'''''"Baby bye, there's a fly"'''''
*'''Petals Used: 2 * {{Pincer}}/{{Lightning}}, 1 or 2 heal(s), and coralsssssss''' 
*'''How it Works: Coral splits, flies can't talis.'''
*'''Strengths: Some flies.''' 
*'''Weaknesses: Bunch of garbage.'''
*'''Variations: Turn pincers(lightnings) to corals.'''
*'''Tip: Reload talent pls.'''
*'''Author: YITB'''

The '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an uncommon mob in florr.io. It is a very fast mob that lays down damaging pieces of pollen. It spawns in the [[Garden]].
==Appearance==
It looks quite like the [[Bee]] but the body is rounder, tinted orange, and there is no stinger on the back.
==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Fast

'''Behavior:''' Always passive unless provoked by [[Uranium|Uranium.]] Will move around and lay [[Pollen]] in its tracks that can damage flowers.

The Bumble Bee drops [[Pollen]] when it is alive. These particles are similar to petals of the same rarity placed down by players. However, unlike the petal, the higher the rarity the Bumble Bee is, the larger the radius the pollens will be.

==Spawning==
The {{PAGENAME}} spawns anywhere in [[Garden]].

==Drops==
===[[Honey]]===
{{:Bumble Bee/Drop Honey}}

===[[Pollen]]===
{{:Bumble Bee/Drop Pollen}}

===[[Wax]]===
{{:Bumble Bee/Drop Wax}}

==Strategy==
The Bumble Bee is quite fast, and you will probably need [[Powder]] or [[Bubble]] if you want to kill it without [[Missile]] or other ranged petals. As just mentioned, with good aim or luck you can damage/kill it with ranged petals. You can also provoke it with [[Uranium]] to kill it easier. Make sure the rarity isn't too high as it is faster than you so it will deal damage to you if you do not kill it quickly.

Bumble Bee is rare but not very strong. It doesn't do a lot of damage or have a lot of health. It should be treated accordingly, but watch out because it'll often run through swarms of [[Hornet|Hornets]] while you're attacking it.

=== Super Bumble Bee ===

* In Garden, the main danger when fighting a Super mob is not the actual mob, but the many Ultra mobs that spawn around the mob. When bubbling to the mob, it is advised to use Sponge and multiple Ultra healing petals, and have a Lotus equipped in case a Spider hits you. It is also advised to use Sponge when actually fighting the mob so you don't die if anything hits you. 
* If the mob spawns deep in Garden, it is advised to form a "megasquad", where a large group of players stay near each other and clear mobs. This increases the chance for everyone to survive.
* Around 330,000 damage is required for loot, but high DPS is required for loot due to extremely high demand.
* Bubbles are recommended because Bumble Bees move faster than players. 
* Sponge may also be helpful because the Pollen laid down can one shot players with no health upgrades.

==Trivia==  
*This mob was added to the game unannounced from the changelog, along with [[Fly]].
*This mob’s stats are the same as [[Baby Fire Ant]]. It used to be the same as [[Baby Ant]], but its damage stat was later doubled.
*Before January 5, 2024, the {{Rarity|Mythic}} Bumble Bee was arguably the rarest mob in the game, due to it only spawning in [[Centralia Fields/West|Centralia Fields 5]] where {{Rarity|Mythic}} mobs are pretty rare there, it was so rare that it has only been spotted a couple of times. Nowadays, it spawns commonly all throughout [[Garden]].
*Bumble Bee's Pollen drop rate is exclusive to itself.

title: Bur
content:
{{Petal Template Infobox | image = File:Bur (Common).png|C File:Bur (Unusual).png|U File:Bur (Rare).png|R File:Bur (Epic).png|E File:Bur (Legendary).png|L File:Bur (Mythic).png|M File:Bur (Ultra).png|Ul File:Bur (Super).png|S File:Bur (Unique).png|Uq | description = Decreases armor of affected unit. Does not stack with itself. | first-rarity = {{Rarity|Common}} | damage = {{Petal Scale|5}} | health = {{Petal Scale|5}} | armor-debuff = {{Petal Scale|-1.5}} | duration = 3 seconds | reload-time = 2.0s | stackability = Stackable (Unstackable armor debuff) | dropped-by = *[[Mantis]] | special = Decreases an enemy's Armor statistic.
Armor can go into negative values. }} '''Bur''' is a [[petal]] in [https://florr.io florr.io]. Its function consists of temporarily decreasing the [[Armor]] statistic of the affected mob. The Armor decreased can also go below zero, letting your other petals deal even more damage to the affected mob. Bur is dropped by [[Mantis]]es. ==Appearance== Bur consists of a small petal, with a blood red color and dark red outlines. The petal's shape is similar to [[Lightning]]; however, Lightning is cyan, the spikes on it have are pointy and it has ten sides, while Bur is crimson, its "spikes" are actually semi-circles and there are twelve semi-circles. ==Function== {|class="article-table" |+Petal Stats ![[Rarity]] !Obtainable? !Damage !Health !Respawn !Armor Debuff !Duration |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |rowspan="9"|Yes |5 |5 |rowspan="9"|2.0 seconds | -1.5 |rowspan="9"|3 seconds |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |15 |15 | -4.5 |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |45 |45 | -13.5 |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |135 |135 | -40.5 |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |405 |405 | -121.5 |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |1,215 |1,215 | -364.5 |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |3,645 |3,645 | -1,093.5 |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |10,935 |10,935 | -3,280.5 |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} |32,805 |32,805 | -9,841.5 |} Once Bur hits an enemy, it will deal its base damage and then inflict an effect on the enemy called Armor Debuff. This effect decreases the Armor statistic of the enemy by the listed number. For comparison, {{Rarity|Ultra}} [[Leafbug]] has 7,873 Armor, and {{Rarity|Ultra}} Bur reduces it by 1,093.5, which means {{Rarity|Ultra}} Leafbug will have 7,873 - 1,093.5 = 6,779.5 Armor remaining after being debuffed. If the Armor debuffed is more than the Armor value, then the Armor is able to wander into negative values, and, instead of the Armor subtracting damage by its value, it will be a double negative: the Armor will begin adding damage to every hit on the mob (e.g. if a mob has a -200 Armor value due to Bur's effects and a {{Rarity|Common}} Basic which has 10 damage hits the debuffed mob, the final damage would be 10 - (-200) = 210, which the mob suffers from the {{Rarity|Common}} Basic). This effect can't be stacked and only lasts for 3 seconds. ==Obtainment== ===[[Mantis]]=== {{:Mantis/Drop Bur}} ===Purchasing=== On certain days, Bur can be purchased from the [[Shop]]. ''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]'' {{Petal Price|4}} == Obtaining == * Defeat Mantises to obtain Bur. Some dodging skills / [[Plank]]s can be helpful for taking them down. ==Strategies== *Bur is primarily used for DPS builds. Bur works well with petals with multiple particles such as [[Light]], [[Sand]], and [[Coral]]. It also works very well with [[Rice]] due to Rice's extremely short reload time. **Bur can also work decently with some petals that have the ability to hit multiple times, such as [[Corn]] and [[Bone]]. *Bur is ineffective at farming due to their weak base stats and anti-synergy with some useful farming petals. ==Gallery== File:Bur (Common).png|{{Rarity|Common}} Bur. File:Bur (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|Unusual}} Bur. File:Bur (Rare).png|{{Rarity|Rare}} Bur. File:Bur (Epic).png|{{Rarity|Epic}} Bur. File:Bur (Legendary).png|{{Rarity|Legendary}} Bur. File:Bur (Mythic).png|{{Rarity|Mythic}} Bur. File:Bur (Ultra).png|{{Rarity|Ultra}} Bur. File:Bur (Super).png|{{Rarity|Super}} Bur. File:Bur (Unique).png|{{Rarity|Unique}} Bur. File:Bur.png|Bur. ==Trivia== *Bur is inspired on the genus of biennial plants '''Arctium''' of the '''Asteraceae''' family. Said genus is commonly known as '''Burdock''', which implies that the name '''Bur''' is short for '''Burdock'''. However, unlike other petals whose names have been shortened (for example, '''[[Bulb]]''', from '''Light Bulb'''), the game never actually displays the name "Burdock". Instead, only "Bur" is shown. *Bur, [[Bone]] and [[Root]] are the only petals who work around the Armor statistic. More specifically, Bone has an Armor statistic itself, Bur decreases a mob's Armor statistic once it hits the mob and Root gives you an Armor statistic. **Bur is the only petal to alter other entities' Armor stat, and, consequently, the only petal to negatively do so. *Bur is tied with [[Air]], Wax and [[Poo]] for the shortest name ever, at 3 letters each. *Bur used to stack its armor debuff and was thus very strong against mobs when it was first released. *Bur also used to have an effect duration of 10 seconds, which allowed players to temporarily swap bur with another DPS petal (Light, Sand and Rice) to achieve even higher DPS outputs. **In December 2023, Bur's base armor debuff was increased from -2 to -1.5. {{Petals}} [[zh:刺果]] [[Category:Petals]] [[Category:Debuffs]] [[Category:Common+]] [[Category:Jungle]] [[Category:Unstackable Petals]] [[Category:Buffs]] [[Category:Droppable Petals]] [[Category:Mantis]] [[Category:Plant-based petals]]

==Strategy==

* Treat it like a weaker rock. Just watch out its hitbox if you intend to use melee petals.
* Though {{Rarity|Ultra}} and lower Bushes aren't much of a threat, they don't spawn very often and when getting chased by swarms of mobs they can push you into one, killing you rather quickly. This is especially dangerous for [[Leafbug|Leafbugs]], because they are melee mobs and therefore it's much easier for them to move you around.

==={{R|Super}} Bush===

* {{R|Super}} Bushes have the ability to spawn {{R|Ultra}} [[Jungle]] Mobs (other than other Bushes) when attacked.
** Although Bushes don't pose a threat, the mobs that it spawns can be dangerous.
* There is one helpful tip to loot {{R|Super}} Bushes: damage the Bush spawns. Damage to the spawned mobs will also count as damage credit towards the {{R|Super}} Bush. A lightning build will be effective for this as lightning can strike multiple mobs at once.
* To safely kill a {{R|Super}}, go in a squad of about 20 players and slowly make your way to the Bush. Once there, attack it until there is a small crowd of its summons. Move back from the {{R|Super}} Bush to clear the mobs around and kill the summons, and continue until the Bush is dead.

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185">
File:Bush (Common).png|{{Rarity|Common}} Bush.

File:Bush (Super).png|{{Rarity|Super}} Bush.
File:Bush (Unique).png|{{Rarity|Unique}} Bush.
File:Bush.png|Bush.
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*Super Bush has been known to spawn in [[Jungle]] at the very top area, making it the longest {{R|Super}} mob to stay alive due to its ability for spawning {{R|Ultra}} mobs upon damaging it, plus other {{R|Ultra}} that spawns nearby.
*It is currently one of the 9 mobs that do not move.
*Equipping [[Mana Orb|Orb]] will make this mob drop [[Leaf/Magic|Magic Leaf]], no matter what rarity the Bush is. The drop rate of it is really bad. This can be explained by the fact that [[Bush]]es of all rarities will attempt to drop [[Golden Leaf]], but since its first rarity is {{Rarity|Ultra}} lower rarities can't drop it. However, [[Leaf/Magic|Magic Leaf]]'s first rarity is {{Rarity|Unusual}} so it can drop it. The fact that [[Golden Leafbug]] drops [[Leaf/Magic|Magic Leaf]] when equipped with [[Mana Orb]] supports it.
*The description is likely a reference to a speech by George W. Bush.
*Since Bushes drop Cotton, it can be implied that the Bush is based on the '''Gossypium''' genus, from which real-life cotton is harvested, despite the mob looking like a generic Bush.
**However, the later-added Monstera drop contests this idea, as these leaves do not originate from cotton bushes.
*During one of the first {{Rarity|Unique}} mob events, a {{Rarity|Unique}} Bush used to be able to spawn {{Rarity|Super}} mobs. This was seen [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=az3SJUjrz7o here]. This was eventually changed for an unknown reason.
**It had likely revealed the fact that other mobs with minions ''(such as [[Queen Ant]]'' or with certain {{Rarity|Super}}+ specials ''(such as [[Rock]])'' were also able to spawn {{Rarity|Super}} mobs as minions, which could also use their own {{Rarity|Super}} specials.
**As of now, {{Rarity|Unique}} mobs now spawn {{Rarity|Ultra}} mobs, and the ability to spawn {{Rarity|Super}} mobs is now exclusive to the [[Ant Hole]] variants when they're {{Rarity|Super}}+.

:''This page is about the petal. For the mob which shares its in-game name, see [[Cactus (Mob)]].''
:''This page is about the regular Cactus. For the [[magic petal]] variant, see [[/Magic/|Magic Cactus]].''

'''Cactus''' is a legacy [[petal]] from [https://florr.io florr.io]. Its function consists of increasing the user's maximum Health Points. Cactus is dropped by [[Cactus (Mob)|Cactus mobs]].

==Appearance==
Cactus consists of an eight-sided green petal. Cactus's sides, while they are sharp in one end, are curvy on their beginning. Due to that, Cactus appears pointy, mimicking a real-life cactus's appearance. The petal also has a light green circle in the center, indicating a font of light shining on it, while also being surrounded by an even darker green outline.

When equipped, Cactus increases the user's maximum health by a certain amount, which is dependent on the petal's rarity. It also heals the flower so that the percentage of the remaining HP stays the same, but it doesn't actually heal the flower otherwise. This effect is stackable, meaning that its HP buff can stack with other Cacti, [[Relic]] and [[Soil]].

==Obtainment==
===[[Cactus (Mob)|Cactus]]===
{{:Cactus (Mob)/Drop Cactus}}

===Purchasing===
On certain days, Cactus can be purchased from the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|2}}

==Strategy== 
*Cactus' main synergy is certainly ram builds, since they play a key role in them by increasing your HP by a nice amount, letting you ram an entity more times due to having more health to do so. Thus, Cactus (specially multiple of them) is usually found with petals like [[Cutter]], [[Disc]] or [[Sponge]] for an efficient ramming build that can work in any situations given that the player manages to consistently ram the enemy.
*Cactus can also be useful in tank builds, whose purpose is to take the most damage you can handle over time without dying, and Cactus (specially multiple of them) will give you more HP to use to tank more hits, meaning that usually tanks will also have Cacti in their build, along with [[Basil]] and a few healing petals.
*It should be noted that Cacti have the same statistics as [[Rock]] of the previous rarity, making high rarity Cacti very useful as both offensive and defensive petals by themselves.
*If you are lucky enough to randomly obtain an {{Rarity|Ultra|Ultra+}} Cactus, you can use it in your main build as a safety mechanic to ward off death for longer. It works well with [[Sponge]].

{{Zone|Garden}}
*When Cacti are used in Garden, they allow the user to take more damage from [[Hornet]] missiles before the user needs to care for their health. Pairing this with [[Salt]] will allow the user to deal damage to the Hornets without directly attacking them via damage reflection, as well as Sponge to slowly receive damage, allowing your healing petals to heal more than you lose health.
*Cacti can also be an efficient tool in letting you soak more poison damage at once from [[Spider]]s. Using [[Lotus]] alongside your build will boost the strategy even further, having Lotus take the poison damage instead of you, while Cactus stays as a backup plan in case Lotus fails.

{{Zone|Desert}}
*Cactus can serve as a good petal against [[Scorpion]]s, letting you have more HP to tank both the stinger and the poison damage that comes with it, similarly to both Hornets and Spiders. Salt can boost this strategy by reflecting damage back at the Scorpion. Lotus may or may not be advised depending on your performance.
*Cactus will also help against [[Beetle]]s, letting you take some more hits before dying to one. This is useful as Beetles are notorious for dealing a lot of damage at once, specially at higher rarities. [[Cactus (Mob)|Cactus mobs]] and [[Sandstorm]]s, albeit not aggressive, can also be countered the same since they deal a lot of damage at once.
*Cactus' HP buff will somewhat counter double damage from [[Soldier Fire Ant]]s spawned from a [[Fire Ant Burrow]], though it isn't as notable as other strategies.
*Cactus' HP buff can also aid you in [[Desert Centipede]] fights, both if they suddenly ram onto you and when they're already ramming you. The HP buff means the Desert Centipede (and other mobs trying to kill you at the same time) will take longer to kill you, which is very helpful as the mob's speed won't let you run away very easily.

===Obtaining===
*Cactus mobs are very durable. Be prepared to spend quite a long time fighting it, and better bring [[Bur]] or high-damaging petals like [[Stinger]] to have a quicker time degrading their health. However, they're stationary, so they wont cause a lot of problems by themselves. Thus, should you not face constant danger by fighting other Desert mobs, farming for Cacti is pretty easy.
*If you're aiming to get a lot of low rarity Cacti, the Cactus mob zone just above the Desert spawn is a great place, as it has a very high frequency of {{Rarity|Common}} and {{Rarity|Unusual}} Cactus mobs that spawn there. For getting higher rarity Cacti, but having to fight other mobs, you can go into the {{Rarity|Mythic}} corridor and the {{Rarity|Ultra}} mob zones of Desert.

{{Zone|Ocean}}
*Cacti will give you extra HP to fight [[Jellyfish]] and their constant lightning discharges, much like Hornets and Scorpions, with the difference being that discharges are not physical projectiles. This is specially useful if there are swarms of Jellyfish, which could easily kill you with their multiple discharges at once.
*Cacti will also help you when fighting a {{Rarity|Super}} [[Shell (Mob)|Shell mob]], as it will fire pearls at you, thus strategies with not only Cacti but also Salt will be useful. Fighting {{Rarity|Ultra|Ultra+}} Shell mobs should be the same, except that they don't fire pearls, with Cacti still being somewhat useful.
*Cacti will give a good help when fighting [[Leech]]es, as they will require more hits to kill you once they have locked into you, even though their lifesteal mechanic could still make them harder to deal anyways.
*Cacti's HP buff can aid you if [[Crab]]s charging onto you is a frequent problem, letting you withstand more of their hits.

{{Zone|Jungle}}
*Strategies used when fighting [[Mantis]]es and [[Wasp]]s should be similar to fighting Hornets in Garden. This includes Cactus and Salt strategies. Be careful, since the projectiles from a Mantis can debuff your Armor, which makes you take more damage at once, possibly more than you can handle.
*Cacti can be handy if [[Firefly|Fireflies]] ramming into you causes a lot of trouble to you. Even though they are pretty weak in both damage and durability, they can still somewhat deal some damage to you, so be careful.

{{Zone|Hel}}

===PvE===
*Cactus is an useful tool when fighting against Hel mobs. For example, it's a good petal to have if [[Hel Wasp]] missiles are rapidly and critically chipping your HP, leaving you to almost die. This is specially true to them, as they send a barrage of missiles at once at you.
*Cactus' HP buff can also help you against both [[Hel Beetle]]s and [[Hel Spider]]s. Not only do they deal a lot of damage at once, but their special abilities (Hel Beetle's teleports and Hel Spider's stronger poison damage) could easily get you killed without extra HP.
*Cactus can also aid you when fighting [[Gambler]]s. Their petals' special abilities ([[Chip]]'s Evasion and [[Dice]]'s critical hits) could harm you a lot and even kill you, while using Cacti will reduce your chances of dying to them.

===PvP: Using===
*In general, Cactus can be worthy just with the HP buff. Generally requiring more hits to be taken down can prove to be just enough to have the edge over opponents. Plus, the petal itaelf is pretty tanky, letting it soak some damage for you while still having the HP buff active even during cooldown.
*Cactus can prove to be a good counter against [[Salt]] users. Salt can reflect 20% of all incoming damage, which makes them annoying to hit. Although healing builds are usually enough, Cactus' HP buff can aid you in mitigating the damage reflection.
*Cactus itself is a good tool to use with Salt builds, as the HP buff can be used to withstand more damage from opponents, thus reflecting more damage at once, leading to more threatening counterattacks or just chip damage.
*Extra health does not mean extra healing, so healing petals are needed to cover damage suffered.
*Cactus' HP buff can be crucial in a build relying on [[Blood Stinger]]s, letting you take more damage before their self damage detriment you enough.

===PvP: Against===
*A Cactus user with healing petals is pretty hard to take down, as they usually heal too much to your build to outdamage the heal. Healing can be lowered with [[Dandelion]]. Using poison petals such as [[Iris]] increases the efficiency of this, dealing tons of damage over time paired with less healing to mitigate poison damage.
*Cactus users will take longer to kill, but Cactus itself lacks offensive power. Knowing this, Cactus can be used as a weak point to attack the opposing flower.
*Beware of Bubble-Cactus-Cutter and Salt-Cactus builds, and always stay away from the former. Versus the latter, try bringing Salt yourself, or use a Dandelion to counter their healing, then burst them with [[Stinger]]s and similar petals.
*One can take advantage of Cactus' HP buff and bring [[Claw]] in your build, as the HP buff will increase the odds of Claw hitting two or even more bonus damage, leaving the opponent more vulnerable.

{{Zone|Ant Hell}}
*Cactus' HP buff can serve in Ant Hell battles, specially when you can't find a place to escape, since Ant Hell is a pretty dense area. Cactus mainly helps against Fire Ants' double damage, but can also help in normal Ant and Termite zones.
*[[Termite Overmind]]s will temporarily disable the extra HP gained if it disables Cactus. Take note of that.

{{Zone|Sewers}}
*Cactus can turn to be an useful tool in Sewers, as mobs will constantly chip your HP. As an example, it helps against [[Spider]]s, who deal a lot of poison damage as well as body damage.
*Cactus' HP buff can also be a last resort against [[Fly|Flies]] when it evades all of your petals' damage, letting you survive for slightly longer.
*Cactus won't be a very ideal petal against [[Roach]]es, as they are too quick and deal a lot of damage at once.

==Trivia==
*Cactus' flower health was buffed by 20% from its original amount in a certain update.
*Cactus' flower health buff is double its durability, as well as six times its damage. This means that Cactus' durability is triple its health.
**Also, starting at the {{Rarity|Unusual}} rarity, Cactus' damage and durability stats are the same as [[Rock]]'s damage and durability values of one rarity lower than Cactus.
*Cactus is one of the ten petals that have a [[magic petal]] variant. The others are [[Bubble]], [[Cotton]], [[Grapes]], [[Leaf]], [[Missile]], [[Peas]], [[Stick]], [[Third Eye]], and [[Stinger]].
*Cactus is one of the only three petals that can increase a flower's Health Points. The other ones are [[Relic]] and [[Soil]].
**This is the only legacy petal that can do so.
*In legacy florr.io, Cactus had three rarities: [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Cactus {{Rarity|Rare}}], [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Cactus_(Epic) {{Rarity|Epic}}] and [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Cactus_(Legendary) {{Rarity|Legendary}}]. All three of them were dropped by the same mob ([https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Cactus_(Mob) Cactus mob]) and had one attribute in common (the flower HP buff, with {{Rarity|Rare}} and {{Rarity|Epic}} Cacti giving you 20 extra HP and {{Rarity|Legendary}} Cacti giving you 13.3 extra HP, for a total of 40 extra HP). however, {{Rarity|Epic}}/{{Rarity|Legendary}} Cacti each had a twist: the former used to do poison damage when your flower had body contact with any entity (while also having weak poison damage by itself), while the latter was three Cactus petals in one (which is why it used to be called by the community '''Tricactus''' or '''Tricac'''), while buffing your health 2x more (but lacking {{Rarity|Epic}} Cactus' poison traits). Neither {{Rarity|Epic}} nor {{Rarity|Legendary}} Cactus have returned yet to current florr.io.
**Before the July 30th, 2022 update, {{Rarity|Rare}} Cacti used to give you the same poison ability on contact as legacy {{Rarity|Epic}} Cactus.
*A player with 10 Cacti of one rarity can give as much HP as the durability value of [[Corn]] of the same rarity. This means that, while {{Rarity|Super}} Cacti give you 656,100 HP, {{Rarity|Super}} Corn, the second most durable petal in the game, has 656,100 HP of durability.
**This statistic is so strong that a player using this can withstand being hit by 4 {{Rarity|Super}} [[Iris]]es (each dealing 612,360 HP worth of poison damage and 43,740 HP worth of contact damage, both statistics adding up to exactly 656,100 HP worth of damage) or 3 {{Rarity|Super}} [[Stinger]] or [[Blood Stinger]] petals without the Duplicator [[Talent]] (with the individual petal dealing 43,740 HP worth of damage, all five of them composing the petal dealing 218,700 HP worth of damage each and fifteen of them composing three of the petal dealing exactly 656,100 HP worth of damage each), or 2.5 of them.
***Statistically speaking, 10 Cacti of one rarity can buff your HP to the point where you can handle (to the point where the remaining HP completely nullifies the HP buff):
****One Corn of the same rarity;
****Contact damage and poison damage from four Irises of the same rarity (it is worth noting that this statistic is purely theoretical, as petals do not take poison damage), and finally;
****Fifteen Stinger/Blood Stinger individual petals of the same rarity, or fifteen whole petals ({{Rarity|Common}}-{{Rarity|Legendary}}), five whole petals ({{Rarity|Mythic}}), 10/3 whole of them ({{Rarity|Mythic}} with Duplicator), three whole of them ({{Rarity|Ultra}} and {{Rarity|Super}}) and 2.5 whole of them ({{Rarity|Ultra}} and {{Rarity|Super}} with Duplicator).
*Cactus is one of the many petals that are dropped by a mob of the same name. The others are Bubble, [[Dandelion]], [[Ant Egg]], [[Jelly]] (partially), [[Leaf]] (partially), [[Rock]], [[Shell]], [[Sponge]], [[Square]] and [[Starfish]]. In the case of Cactus, it's [[Cactus (Mob)|Cactus mob]].
**Cactus, Bubble, Dandelion, Ant Egg (as it was [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Egg {{Rarity|Epic}} Egg]), Rock and Square all come from legacy florr.io.
***However, only Cactus, Rock and Square's corresponding mobs came from legacy florr.io.
*On February 1st, 2026, a player by the name sayo crafted 4 {{R|8}} Cacti from 92 {{R|7}} Cacti.
**The approximate probability of that happening is 0.02755%.

:''This page is about Magic Cactus. For the normal petal variant, see [[Cactus]].''
'''Magic Cactus''' is a [[magic petal]] from [https://florr.io florr.io]. It increases the user max mana can be stored at once. Magic Cactus is dropped by [[Cactus (Mob)|Cactus mobs]].

==Appearance==
Magic Cactus, for the most part, is based off [[Cactus]]: it consists of an eight-sized petal with spikes on the ends. The sides slightly curve to reveal the spikes. To indicate that it's a magic petal, its body is cyan, with an even lighter blue on its center and a darker blue on its borders.

==Function==

Magic Cactus functions is same as to its base version, [[Cactus]]. However, it increases the max amount of mana can be stored, like how normal Cactus increases max health. Magic Cactus max mana is doubled throughout the rarities.

Magic Cactus make the mana bar unable to disappear if all [[Mana Orb]] or [[Leaf/Magic|Magic Leaf]] are unequipped (only if you still have mana in your mana bar).

==Obtainment==
''This is a magic petal, meaning mobs only drop this if you have [[Mana Orb]] active. It replaces [[Cactus]]' drop rates.''
===[[Cactus]]===
{{:Cactus (Mob)/Drop Magic Cactus}}

===Purchasing===
On certain days, Magic Cactus can be purchased from the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|1}}

==Strategies==
*Magic Cactus is an important tool in Mana builds, letting you have far more MP than the initial limit, allowing your MP builds to be even more effective while you take longer to exhaust your MP. 
*Magic Cacti must be used with at least one [[Mana Orb]], or your MP will be too low for a good magic petal build to work.
*Magic Cacti are a must in [[Magic Eye]] builds, seeing as the former can provide even more MP for the latter's damage output to be stronger. This also means that you'll have more MP after Magic Eye consumes half of your current value.
*Magic Cactus is also required for [[Magic Bubble]] builds, as their ability can quickly drain your MP supply.
*[[Magic Leaf]] is a very good petal to pair with Magic Cacti, as not only it regens the MP to the new limit imposed by Magic Cacti, but also they will quickly heal to the new limit, giving the user more MP to use per second.
*By itself, Magic Cactus has pretty tanky stats, meaning that it could somewhat tank some stuff.

{{Zone|Garden}}
*The Magic Cacti and Magic Eye combo cab cause chaos in Garden, with the latter being able to deal considerably immense damage to the targeted mobs due to the extra MP.
*One can also build a [[Blueberries]] spam build with some Magic Cacti and Magic Leaves to keep the MP at a high value. This will let you to have a massive area control build that also does a nice amount of damage to all enemies hit by the individual Blueberries.
*Magic Cactus can also be a good tool in [[Magic Stinger]] builds, since it's pretty fragile and can die to [[Hornet]] missiles pretty quickly. Magic Cacti will let them respawn instantly for a longer time before needing to regen MP.

{{Zone|Desert}}
*Magic Eye is a pretty prominent petal in Desert, due to the presence of [[Desert Centipede]]s, [[Fire Ant Burrow]]s and clumped up mobs whereas the petal can deal a lot of damage to some enemies with the lightning bounces. As such, Magic Cactus will further increase its value.
*For the same reason, Magic Cactus can also be used in Blueberries spam builds for a lower MP cost in exchange for a lower damage output.
*Players can also pair Magic Cacti with some [[Magic Missile]]s to snipe mobs from afar more often, including [[Sandstorm]]s and [[Scorpion]]s, which can be taken out with ranged petal strategies.

===Obtaining===
*Strategies used for farming normal Cacti can also be used when farming Magic Cacti, as they're dropped by the same mob ([[Cactus (Mob)|Cactus mob]]).
*Lower rarity Magic Cacti can be easily farmed in the Cactus zone, just a bit above the Desert spawn area. The high density of Cactus mobs allow you to get a lot of Magic Cacti at once.
*For higher rarity Magic Cacti, players can try to venture into the {{Rarity|Mythic}} mob corridor (and the {{Rarity|Ultra}} mob section in it), the {{Rarity|Ultra}} mob chamber and the Desert Split Path area.
*Cactus mobs are pretty tough, so one needs to deal a burst petal to take it out quicker, like [[Stinger]]. Armor-bypassing petals with a lot of DPS, like [[Iris]], can also be used.
*The most dangerous part of killing Cactus mobs is when another mob forces you to move near it, potentially killing you if you touch the Cactus mob as it has a high damage statistic. Be aware of your surroundings at all costs to avoid ramming them.

{{Zone|Ocean}}
*Magic Eye builds can be useful in Ocean, being used to kill [[Jellyfish]] from a safe range, outdamage [[Starfish (Mob)|Starfish mobs]]' healing and shine in high density areas. Once again, Magic Cacti serve to boost the builds' performance.
*With Magic Cacti, Blueberries and Magic Eye are also useful tools to bypass [[Shell (Mob)|Shell mobs]]' armor, which makes them pretty tough to kill.
*Magic Stinger can shine with the aid of Magic Cacti, dealing a lot of damage to all mobs nearby, letting you progress pretty easily.

{{Zone|Jungle}}
*With the high density of extremely dangerous Jungle mobs, like [[Mantis]]es and [[Wasp]]s, [[Magic Cotton]] is useful in increasing your survivability in the area. Once again, Magic Cactus boosts the magic petal in question's performance, letting Magic Cotton absorb even more damage for you.
*Magic Eye shines in dealing a lot of damage to mobs close to each other, while Blueberries take advantage in the high mob density of the area. This means that Magic Cactus will be even better in the meta.
*Magic Missile can also somewhat let you hit mobs from afar, avoiding getting close to their projectiles/body. This is specially useful against {{Rarity|Mythic}}+ Wasps, where you can avoid their missiles just by staying still (of course, this can't be done if there's any mob other than Wasps).

{{Zone|Hel}}
===PvE===
*Magic Eye and Magic Missile can snipe mobs from afar, letting you take low to no damage from them, which could save you a lot specially in this scenario. As such, Magic Cactus is also required for them.
*With Magic Cactus, Magic Stingers can help you a lot against [[Hel Beetle]]s and [[Hel Spider]]s that are too close to you but not hitting you yet, as when they start hitting you, you lose a lot of HP at once.
*Blueberries are also pretty decent in dealing with swarms of Hel mobs everywhere.
*Magic Cacti paired with Magic Cotton will help you increase survivability in this zone as well.

===PvP: Using===
*Magic Cactus paired with Magic Leaf can be useful in a lot of PvP interactions. More specifically:
**Blueberries deal decent damage to a player, while letting you damage multiple players/mobs at once;
**Magic Bubble can make you unpredictable, meaning that the opponent has to predict your opponents to have a chance of killing you in combat;
**Magic Cotton can help you survive longer, specially if your HP is being chipped or a good chunk of it is depleted per attack;
**Magic Eye can deal an immense amount of damage to an opponent at once. If they use [[Rubber]] to mitigate the lightning damage, attack the petal and then strike the user, or just chip the player's HP slowly;
**Magic Missile can strike other flowers from afar without risking close range combat;
**Magic Stinger can deal an immense amount of damage as well, but only if you open the way for the petal to directly attack the opponent, or the petal will die by other petals before hitting the enemy.
*Magic Cactus by itself is pretty strong, meaning that it will take some damage before dying. Magic Leaves also deal somewhat good damage.

===PvP: Against===
*Depending on what magic petals your opponent pairs with Magic Cactus, you must plan your strategies accordingly.
*Getting [[Cactus]] and healing petals to quickly recover from your enemy's strategies is crucial. Usually, using ranged petals can also be a good choice.
*If your opponent is using ranged magic petals, try to use something like a [[Plank]] (against Magic Missile) or Rubber (against Magic Eye). Blueberries can be ignored as long as you have good healing and deal more damage to the enemy.
*In the case of Magic Bubbles, exhaust your opponent's MP storage quickly, making them unable to use the petals and enabling you to predict and hit them with more ease.

{{Zone|Ant Hell}}
*Magic Cactus can be useful in some Ant Hell scenarios. For example, Blueberries and Magic Eye can deal with the swarms of Ants/Termites, letting you avoid getting swarmed by them with no chance to escape.
*Magic Cactus + Magic Stinger builds will allow you to deal massive damage to any of the three Ant Hell bosses, increasing your chances of getting loot from them.
*Magic Cactus + Magic Cotton builds will increase your survivability in Fire Ant Hell, as its high density and less space to travel (specially during [[Queen Fire Ant]] fights) makes you take a lot of damage, which is dangerous considering Fire Ants' double damage properties.

{{Zone|Sewers}}
*Magic Bubble builds will help you travel efficiently in this area. Be ware of the mob density and the high MP cost, though.
*[[Magic Stick]] can be a great AFK tool as well as a Sewers mob clearing tool. However, it also costs a lot of MP to respawn, summon the Sandstorms and maintain them, so Magic Cactus alongside Magic Leaf and it can help with the total MP cost.
*Once again, Magic Cactus + Magic Eye builds will allow you to deal insane damage to mobs like [[Fly|Flies]] and [[Roach]]es without letting them get too dangerous. Blueberries are not as good due to their low damage output.

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">
