title: Crab/Mecha
content:
{{Background|图片=Factoryb.png|大小=2000px}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:Mecha Crab}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:Mecha Crab}} {{Mob Template Infobox | name = Mecha Crab | image = File:Mecha Crab (Common).png|C File:Mecha Crab (Unusual).png|U File:Mecha Crab (Rare).png|R File:Mecha Crab (Epic).png|E File:Mecha Crab (Legendary).png|L File:Mecha Crab (Mythic).png|M File:Mecha Crab (Ultra).png|Ul File:Mecha Crab (Super).png|S File:Mecha Crab (Unique).png|Uq | description = Dr. Crab. | health = {{Infobox Colors Empty|75|281.2|1.0k|4.1k|24.3k|237k|14.8M|328M|2.0B|}} | damage = {{Infobox Colors Empty|15|45|135|405|1.2k|3.6k|10.9k|32.8k|98.4k|}} | armor = {{Infobox Colors Empty|3|8|25|76|227|680|2.0k|5.0k|13.7k|}} | xp-reward = {{Infobox Colors Empty|1|2|9|76|692|8.5k|466k|20.0M|200M|}} | status = Hostile | attack = Moves in a circle around the flower, and uses a laser to attack them. | spawn = [[Factory]] | drops = * [[Laser]] * [[Battery]] *[[Magnet]] *[[Fragment]] | move = Yes (Medium) | lightning = {{Infobox Colors Empty|5/s|15/s|45/s|135/s|405/s|1.2k/s|3.6k/s|10.9k/s|32.8k/s|}} }} The '''Mecha Crab''' is a [[Mobs|mob]] from [https://florr.io florr.io]. It is a hostile mob that moves around flowers and uses a laser to attack it. ==Appearance== Mecha Crab looks like a gray, metallic crab with a laser mounted on top. ==Stats & Behavior== '''Behavior:''' Moves towards the flower, and instead of rushing towards them, they will orbit around the target. The mounted laser locks and fires at the enemy, dealing continuous lightning damage over time. {| class="article-table" |+Mob Stats ![[Rarity]] !Health !Body Damage !Armor !Lightning !XP Reward |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |75 |15 |3 |5/s |1 |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |281.2 |45 |8 |15/s |2 |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |1012.5 |135 |25 |45/s |9 |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |4050 |405 |76 |135/s |76 |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |24300 |1215 |227 |405/s |692 |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |236925 |3645 |680 |1,215/s |8471 |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |14762250 |10935 |2041 |3,645/s |465978 |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |328049984 |32805 |4957 |10,935/s |20017732 |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} |1968300032 |98415 |13705 |32,805/s |200177320 |} Mecha Crab circles around the player while firing lasers that deal continuous lightning damage. Laser will continuously lock on its target until it goes out of the Mecha Crab's range. Unlike [[Crab|its native variant]], Mecha Crabs aren't able to dash its enemy. ==Spawning== Mecha Crab spawns in [[Factory]] and sometimes at the Factory entrance in [[Garden]]. ==Drops== === [[Battery]] === {{:Firefly/Drop Battery}} ===[[Laser]]=== {| class="article-table" !Mob Rarity !{{Rarity|Common}} !{{Rarity|Unusual}} !{{Rarity|Rare}} !{{Rarity|Epic}} !{{Rarity|Legendary}} !{{Rarity|Mythic}} !{{Rarity|Ultra}} |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |61.3% |38.2% | | | | | |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |14.6% |85.4% | | | | | |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} | |62.5% |37.5% | | | | |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} | | |71% |28.9% | | | |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} | | | |83.3% |12.6% | | |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} | | | | |97.7% |2% | |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} | | | | |1.8% |96.9 |1.3% |- |{{Rarity|Super}} | | | | | | rowspan="2" |6.8% | rowspan="2" |93.2% |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} | | | | | |} [[Fragment]] {| class="article-table" !Mob Rarity !{{Rarity|Mythic}} !{{Rarity|Ultra}} |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |3.1% | |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |97.8% |2% |- |{{Rarity|Super}} | rowspan="2" |1.6% | rowspan="2" |98.4% |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} |} [[Magnet]] {| class="article-table" !Mob Rarity !{{Rarity|Rare}} !{{Rarity|Epic}} !{{Rarity|Legendary}} !{{Rarity|Mythic}} !{{Rarity|Ultra}} |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |30.3% | | | | |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |76.4% |23.1% | | | |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |0.5% |89.7% |9.8% | | |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} | | |97.9% |1.5% | |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} | | |4.5% |94.5% |1% |- |{{Rarity|Super}} | | | | rowspan="2" |12.7% | rowspan="2" |87.3% |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} | | | |} ==Strategy== *Because of its ranged lightning attack, [[Rubber]] is great for negating a lot of the damage it would deal. You still need healing as usual for when the rubber breaks. *[[Sponge]] is less useful due to having lightning damage rack up quickly, making healing not be able to keep up with the damage from Sponge. === Super Mecha Crab === * The Super mob itself is not as dangerous, but the Ultra mobs around the mob that is a threat. When bubbling to the mob, it is advised to use Sponge and multiple healing petals, and have Rubber equipped in case a Spider hits you or a Crab. It is not advised to use sponge when actually fighting the mob, because the lightning damage from the mob and other mobs will just go too high and the healing cannot keep up, so you don't die if anything hits you. * If the mob spawns deep in Factory, it is advised to form a "megasquad", where a large group of players stay near each other and clear mobs. This increases the chance for everyone to survive. * In most cases, players will lure Super Crab to slightly easier areas because they are nearly impossible to kill while surrounded by Ultra mobs. This means that you do not need to rush into the ultra zone area to attack it. * When actually killing the mob, it is advised to not go to the side of the mob since it moves sideways. It would be easier to go behind the mob and attack it while the Mecha Crab is attracted to other players. It would also work if you use long ranged petals to avoid crashing into the mob or the laser. * If the crab is attracted to you, there are a few options. Use a few [[Rubber|rubbers]] or heals to counter its laser and kill it. You could also just keep on letting it attract to you and let your squad kill it (if you have one). Another option advised is just to hide behind a corner for the crab to attract to someone else and kill it.
==Gallery== File:Mecha Crab (Common).png|{{Rarity|1}} Mecha Crab. File:Mecha Crab (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|Unusual}} Mecha Crab. File:Mecha Crab (Rare).png|{{Rarity|3}} Mecha Crab. File:Mecha Crab (Epic).png|{{Rarity|4}} Mecha Crab. File:Mecha Crab (Legendary).png|{{Rarity|5}} Mecha Crab. File:Mecha Crab (Mythic).png|{{Rarity|6}} Mecha Crab. File:Mecha Crab (Ultra).png|{{Rarity|7}} Mecha Crab. File:Mecha Crab (Super).png|{{Rarity|8}} Mecha Crab. File:Mecha Crab (Unique).png|{{Rarity|9}} Mecha Crab. File:Mecha Crab.png|Mecha Crab. ==Trivia== *Mecha Crab's laser is the only Lightning ability that can theoretically damage an unlimited number of enemies due to its piercing. *Mecha Crab's laser used to be longer and bigger, causing chaos to [[Factory]] megasquads. *Like other Mecha mobs, Mecha Crab is aggressive to [[Garden]] mobs. *Mecha Crabs cannot dash into player or summoned mobs. The reason might be they have lasers to attack them. *At some point in mid December 2025, Mecha Crabs no longer drop Google but instead drop Fragment and Magnet. {{Mobs}} [[Category:Factory]] [[Category:Lightning]] [[Category:Mobs]] [[Category:Non-stationary mobs]] [[Category:Garden]] [[Category:Mecha Mobs]] [[Category:Crab]]

title: Crafting
content:
Crafting is a mechanic in [[florr.io]] that allows players to craft higher-rarity petals using petals of the same type and previous rarity. In the Crafting menu, click the petal to place five, or shift-click the petal to place all available petals into the slots. To proceed with crafting, click 'Craft' to attempt the creation process. Alternatively, alt-click the petal to attempt five, or alt-shift-click to attempt all available petals. The outcome will depend on the rarity of the petal: either the craft will succeed or 1 to 4 petals will be lost in the process per individual craft. ==Overview== The Crafting menu can be accessed by clicking the Craft button. It is to the bottom left of the interface and can also be pulled up by pressing 'C' on the keyboard. In the Crafting menu, the petals are listed in alphabetical order from top to bottom: one should have five or more of the same rarity and type of a petal in order to craft it. Depending on the craft chances below, either a petal of one higher rarity will be crafted or 1-4 of that petal will be destroyed. Shift-clicking the petal inserts the maximum number of petals that can be crafted with. If a {{Rarity|Super}} has been crafted by a player, the following message will be sent:
"'''{{Text shadow|A Super [petal] has been crafted by [user]!|color=#2BFFA3}}'''"
This message can be seen globally, and across all servers. Unlike {{Rarity|Super}} spawn messages, however, there is no level threshold on viewing {{Rarity|Super}} craft messages: everyone can view them regardless of level. If a petal gets crafted, it will either craft instantly (done if you have a lot of the same rarity petal), or it will spin 9-10 times if crafted from 5. ===Crafting chances=== *{{Rarity|Common}} to {{Rarity|un}}: 64% *{{Rarity|un}} to {{Rarity|Rare}}: 32% *{{Rarity|Rare}} to {{Rarity|Epic}}: 16% *{{Rarity|Epic}} to {{Rarity|Legendary}}: 8% *{{Rarity|Legendary}} to {{Rarity|Mythic}}: 4% *{{Rarity|Mythic}} to {{Rarity|Ultra}}: 2% *{{Rarity|Ultra}} to {{Rarity|Super}}: 1% *{{Rarity|Super}} to {{Rarity|Eternal}}: 0.1% On average, players will need the following numbers of petals of a certain rarity to craft the next rarity:These values are the statistical averages, calculated with the formula \frac{2.5}{c} + 2.5, where c is the chance for success. Individual equations that account for all aspects of crafting, without missing subtle effects, are complex. *{{Rarity|Common}} to {{Rarity|Unusual}}: 6.40625 *{{Rarity|Unusual}} to {{Rarity|Rare}}: 10.3125 *{{Rarity|Rare}} to {{Rarity|Epic}}: 18.125 *{{Rarity|Epic}} to {{Rarity|Legendary}}: 33.75 *{{Rarity|Legendary}} to {{Rarity|Mythic}}: 65 *{{Rarity|Mythic}} to {{Rarity|Ultra}}: 127.5 *{{Rarity|Ultra}} to {{Rarity|Super}}: 252.5 *{{Rarity|Super}} to {{Rarity|Eternal}}: 2502.5 To find out the expected number of petals obtained from any number of petals of the previous rarity, divide the number of petals with the average number of petals needed to craft the next rarity, i.e., 100 {{Rarity|Rare}} petals will give on average 5.5 {{Rarity|Epic}} petalsThe value "5.5" here is a simplified, approximate estimation. The accurate estimation is the mean of all probabilities of success attempts.. To find out how many petals of any rarity it takes to craft any other rarity, multiply all the numbers for all the rarities in between them. For instance, it takes on average 211,630,689,419,023 {{Rarity|Common}} petals to craft one {{Rarity|Eternal}} petal, or 631,882 {{Rarity|Ultra}} petals to craft one {{Rarity|Eternal}} petalRounded up to the nearest integer.. Keep in mind that one can only possess at most 1 million of the same petal. ==Gallery== File:CraftingFailure.png|A failed {{Rarity|Epic}} [[Light Bulb|Bulb]] crafting attempt. File:Smaller craft button.png|A smaller button. File:Screenshot 2024-05-23 12.15.33 AM.png|A failed {{Rarity|Super}} [[Wing]] crafting attempt. File:Crafted ultra glass.png|A player successfully crafted an {{Rarity|Ultra}} petal. File:CraftBoard.png|The Crafting UI. File:Uorangefrom5veryrng 2025-06-26 at 10.50.16.png|A successful {{Rarity|Ultra}} craft from 5 petals. File:IMG 2435.webp|A successful {{Rarity|Super}} [[Third Eye]] Craft. File:IMG 2437.webp|A successful {{Rarity|Super}} [[Yucca]] craft. File:Switch.png|The Switch button(outdated) File:Eternal craft.png|A crafting {{R|ET}} has 0.01% chance. ==Trivia== *There is a tool called Crafting Calculator, can be accessed by clicking [https://nulrea.github.io/#crafting_calculator here], that calculates probabilities of X petals of Y+1 rarity being crafted from Z petals of Y rarity. **The original tool that was based on Glitch was discontiuned on of July 8, 2025 due to Glitch project hosting and user profiles being shut down. *Crafting can be useful to get rid of petals you don't need and make better ones. *Each craft has their own chance, the better the rarity the lower the percentages. *Crafting is the more common way to obtain {{Rarity|Mythic|text=Mythics}} and {{Rarity|Ultra|text=Ultras}}, and the only way to get {{Rarity|Super}} petals. *There are 5 different [[achievements]] you can get with crafting: Consolation Prize (lose 4 petals in a craft attempt), Crafter (craft the first petal of the achievement rarity), The Lucky One (successfully craft 10 petals of any rarity in a row), Wasteful Crafting (lose a total of 1 million petals in crafting attempts) and High Volume Crafter (successfully craft 200 thousand petalsNeed more clarifications.). There are 2 more achievements with [[Oracle]]. *When the {{Rarity|Mythic}} rarity was first released, the craft chance was 1%. It was later buffed to 2%, then 3%, and finally 4% as of now. *The {{Rarity|Ultra}} Rarity has gone through several changes. **In November 2nd 2022, it created 1 {{Rarity|Ultra}} from 2 {{Rarity|Mythic|text=Mythics}} with a 100% success rate. The {{Rarity|Ultra}} would revert back to 1 {{Rarity|Mythic}} after one match, the other {{Rarity|Mythic}} is destroyed. **In November 3rd 2022, the recipe for {{Rarity|Ultra|text=Ultras}} was changed so that 1 {{Rarity|Mythic}} creates 3 {{Rarity|Ultra|text=Ultras}}. {{Rarity|Ultra|text=Ultras}} would be destroyed after one match. **In November 4th 2022, the recipe for {{Rarity|Ultra|text=Ultras}} was changed again so that 5 {{Rarity|Mythic|text=Mythics}} would create 50 {{Rarity|Ultra|text=Ultras}}, still lasting for only one match. **In January 1st 2023, {{Rarity|Ultra}} crafting was temporarily disabled, and every 10 {{Rarity|Ultra|text=Ultras}} in your inventory before this update were reverted back to a single {{Rarity|Mythic}}. Any remaining {{Rarity|Ultra|text=Ultras}} that weren't reverted were [[Absorbing|absorbed]]. **In January 2nd 2023, {{Rarity|Ultra|text=Ultras}} no longer get destroyed and the success chance was reduced to just 1%, and 5 {{Rarity|Mythic|text=Mythics}} would only create 1 {{Rarity|Ultra}} upon success. {{Rarity|Ultra|text=Ultras}} are also used to craft {{Rarity|Super|text=Supers}}. **In an unannounced update, the {{Rarity|Ultra}} craft success chance was increased to 2%. *Crafting used to follow a pseudo-random system, where the craft chance increased after each attempt (without showing the pseudo-chance in the crafting UI). *As of the December 24th, 2023, a server announcement is made when a {{Rarity|Super}} petal is crafted by any player in that server. *Before the May 22nd, 2024 update, a Crafting [[Talents|Talent]] existed, whereas it made you able to craft petals of higher rarity, corresponding to the max Crafting Talent rarity you had unlocked. On that day, the Talent tree was removed so that you can craft any rarity of petals anytime. That update also removed the Talented Crafting tree, which increased your crafting odds by 1.1x ({{Rarity|Legendary}} Talented Crafting) and 1.2x ({{Rarity|Mythic}} Talented Crafting). *In the May 25th, 2024 update, all crafting chances were made so that they are all under a geometric progression of 1/2, counting from the {{Rarity|Common}} rarity having a 64% chance, and then all other chances are exactly halved. This change modified only up to {{Rarity|Mythic}} craft chances, as {{Rarity|Ultra}} and {{Rarity|Super}} craft chances were unaltered. *The petal prices for crafting a petal using the Oracle are the same as the average prices for crafting petals, except rounded up. *Before the January 19, 2026 update, {{Rarity|Eternal|text=}}'s crafting chance was 0.01%, before it was buffed to be 0.1%. ==Notes== {{Game Info}} [[Category:Game information]] [[Category:User Interface Menu]] [[Category:Features]]

title: Dandelion
content:
{{Petal Template Infobox | image = File:Dandelion (Unusual).png|U File:Dandelion (Rare).png|R File:Dandelion (Epic).png|E File:Dandelion (Legendary).png|L File:Dandelion (Mythic).png|M File:Dandelion (Ultra).png|Ul File:Dandelion (Super).png|S File:Dandelion (Unique).png|Uq | description = Its interesting properties prevent healing effects on affected units.
Affected units have their healing reduced by 20%. Stacks with itself. | first-rarity = {{Rarity|Unusual}} | damage = {{Petal Scale 2|8|1|1|1|1|1|2|3|3|lowest-rarity=Unusual}} | health = {{Petal Scale 2|8|1|1|1|1|1|2|3|3|lowest-rarity=Unusual}} | duration = 30 seconds | reload-time = 1.0s | stackability = Stackable | dropped-by = *[[Dandelion (Mob)|Dandelion]] | special = Goes farther than other petals on attack mode.
Reduces healing by 20%.
Stackable with itself.
{{Rarity|Mythic}} and {{Rarity|Ultra}} Rarities add another Dandelion to itself.
Affected by Duplicator. }} :''This page is about the petal. For the mob with the same in-game name that drops this, see [[Dandelion (Mob)]].'' '''Dandelion''' is a legacy [[petal]] in [https://florr.io florr.io] added on the February 11th, 2020 update. It reduces enemy healing by 20% and stacks with itself. Dandelion is dropped by [[Dandelion (Mob)|Dandelion mobs]]. ==Appearance== Dandelion's body is almost exactly like the [[Basic]] petal, just slightly smaller. Its differential from that petal is that Dandelion has a black stick out of one side. This black part goes straight out, ending in a semicircle, and always points towards the flower when orbiting. ==Function== ''Note: The "A + B" respawn format means that the petal takes A seconds to respawn and B seconds after respawning to do its special action/have its special action enabled.'' {|class="article-table" |+Petal Stats ![[Rarity]] !Obtainable? !Damage !Health !Respawn |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |No |8 |8 |rowspan="9"|1 second |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |rowspan="8"|Yes |24 |24 |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |72 |72 |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |216 |216 |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |648 |648 |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |972 (x2) |972 (x2) |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |1,944 (x3) |1,944 (x3) |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |5,832 (x3) |5,832 (x3) |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} |17,496 (x3) |17,496 (x3) |} When Dandelion hits an enemy, it will have its healing reduced by 20% for 30 seconds. This effect stacks, which means hitting an enemy with Dandelion 5 times in quick succession stops 100% of healing for a while. The only mob this is effective against is [[Starfish (Mob)|Starfish]] due to its healing mechanic. A flower under the effect of a Dandelion will turn pale yellow / almost white and be sad regardless of whether or not it is defending. {{Rarity|Mythic}} Dandelion has 2 Dandelions in one petal slot, while {{Rarity|Ultra}}+ Dandelions have 3 (or 4 with the [[Talents/Duplicator|Duplicator Talent]]. {{Rarity|Mythic}}+ Dandelions will squish all other petals together like a [[Light]] on {{Rarity|Unusual}}+ rarities. Dandelions on the attack state go farther than normal petals. ==Obtainment== ===[[Dandelion (Mob)|Dandelion]]=== {{:Dandelion (Mob)/Drop Dandelion}} ===Purchasing=== On certain days, Dandelion can be purchased in the [[Shop]]. ''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]'' {{Petal Price|1}} ==Obtaining== *Defeat [[Dandelion (Mob)|Dandelion mobs]] to obtain Dandelion. Compared to other mobs in [[Garden]], Dandelion mobs have a lower spawn rate. Therefore, the most practical way to obtain Dandelions of high rarities is to hunt {{Rarity|Super}} Dandelion mobs. ==Strategy== *In PvE scenarios, Dandelion is only useful in [[Ocean]] to stop [[Starfish (Mob)|Starfishes]] from healing in order to make it easier to defeat them. It is suggested to use Dandelions, especially {{Rarity|Legendary}}- ones, with [[Yin Yang]] to apply the stacks faster. *In [[Hel]], Dandelion can cripple opponents it hits by disabling their healing and scare them off, which can be helpful when a {{Rarity|Super}} mob is present and you want more chance to deal damage to it. ==Gallery== File:Dandelion (Common).png|{{Rarity|Common}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Dandelion (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|Unusual}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Dandelion (Rare).png|{{Rarity|Rare}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Dandelion (Epic).png|{{Rarity|Epic}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Dandelion (Legendary).png|{{Rarity|Legendary}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Dandelion (Mythic).png|{{Rarity|Mythic}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Dandelion (Ultra).png|{{Rarity|Ultra}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Dandelion (Super).png|{{Rarity|Super}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Dandelion (Unique).png|{{Rarity|Unique}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Dandelion.png|Dandelion. File:Dandelion with Duplicator.png|{{R|7}} and {{R|8}} Dandelion when the Duplicator Talent is active. ==Trivia== *This used to be one of the two shootable petals with the other one being [[Missile]], and one of the five triggerable petals, with the others being Missile, [[Peas]], [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Peas_(Epic), Epic Peas] (modern-day [[Grapes]]) and [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Peas_(Legendary) Legendary Peas] (a bigger version of {{R|Epic}} Peas which hasn't been fully restored as of now). However, it was nerfed to being a normal, non-shooting orbiting petal, and has since then "replaced" with [[Carrot]]. **A similar case happened to [[Honey]]: it used to be just a [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Honey normal orbiting petal], but its overhaul for new florr.io allowed it to be droppable like [[Ankh]]. *It looks similar to a white and black [https://diepio.fandom.com/wiki/Tank Basic Tank] from diep.io, created by the same creator of florr.io, [[M28]]. However, Dandelion has a rounded handle instead of a rectangular one, and it's unknown if it's an intended reference or not. *[[Dandelion (Mob)|Dandelion mobs]] have 10 Dandelion petals stuck around it, distributed evenly. They also cripple your healing petals for 20%. Once hit, they're shot out like legacy florr.io Dandelions. *Missile is the first person to truly craft the {{Rarity|Super}} Dandelion. *This is one of the many petals that are dropped by a mob of the same name. The others are: [[Bubble]], [[Cactus]], [[Ant Egg]], [[Jelly]] (partially), [[Rock]], [[Shell]], [[Sponge]], [[Square]] and [[Starfish]]. In this, it's Dandelion mob. **Dandelion, Bubble, Rock, Ant Egg (as it was the old Epic Egg), Cactus and Square all come from legacy florr.io. ***However, Dandelion, Bubble and Ant Egg received their mob counterparts only after legacy florr.io ended. Dandelions couldn't even appear during the [[Waves]] period, only being added in Maze. *Dandelion, [[Basil]] and [[Amulet]] are the only petals who alter healing petals. Dandelion is the only one to do so negatively. *Before an unknown date, {{Rarity|Mythic}} Dandelion used to have 3 Dandelions, but now it only has 2. **It still has 3 if you have the [[Talents/Duplicator|Duplicator talent]]. *In legacy florr.io, Dandelion used to be dropped at {{Rarity|Rare}} rarity. However, it was originally dropped at {{Rarity|Epic}} when added before it was changed to what it was. This and Square (which went from {{Rarity|Unusual}} to {{Rarity|Legendary}} and finally to {{Rarity|Unique}} when said rarity existed) were the only petals to change rarities in legacy florr.io. **Dandelion also received a change in what mobs drop this: [[Hornet]]s used to drop this infrequently in both legacy florr.io and early new florr.io stages, but since the Dandelion mob was added (first maze florr.io), Dandelion now drops from there, while the [[Orange]] replaced its Hornet drop. *Dandelion used to disable ''all'' healing (100% debuff) before being nerfed. *The first clause of its description is reminiscent from its legacy incarnation, thus, still accounts for when the Dandelion fully disabled healing. The second clause was added with the nerf, which means both clauses contradict each other (although the 100% debuff can still be achieved due to Dandelion's stacking capacity. *{{R|Mythic}} Dandelion's extra Dandelion will be facing 180º (opposite direction) relative to the one to the right (found in {{R|Legendary}}- rarities, whose facing direction is conserved). **{{R|Ultra}} and {{R|Super}} Dandelions' previous Dandelions will be facing 60° to the East relative to their {{R|Mythic}} counterpart, while the new Dandelion will be facing 120° to the West relative to a {{R|Legendary}}- Dandelion. All of these combined form an equilateral triangle with their black sticks. ***Finally, {{R|Ultra}} and {{R|Super}} Dandelions under the effect of the [[Talents/Duplicator|Duplicator Talent]] will have the following characteristics: the bottom Dandelion will face the same direction as the one without the talent, the right Dandelion will turn another 60° to the East (totaling 120° to the East), the left Dandelion will turn around 30° to the West (totaling 90° to the West, or exactly in a perpendicular direction compared to a {{R|Legendary}}- Dandelion) and the top Dandelion (added with the Duplicator Talent) will be facing around 30° to the West relative to a {{R|Legendary}}- Dandelion. All of these combined form a Square. *When the player is on the attack state, Dandelions go farther than any other petal except [[Wing]] and shootable petals, likely to mitigate the nerf that made it not shootable anymore. {{Petals}} [[zh:蒲公英]] [[Category:Petals]] [[Category:Health-Based]] [[Category:Debuffs]] [[Category:Unusual+]] [[Category:Garden]] [[Category:Stackable Petals]] [[Category:Plant-based petals]] [[Category:Affected by Duplicator Talent]]

:''Not to be confused with the petal [[Dandelion|of the same name]].''

The '''Dandelion''' is a mob in [https://florr.io florr.io]. It fires "dandelion" missiles when attacked, which reduce the player's healing.

==Appearance==
The Dandelion mob consists of a white circle with a gray outline with 10 dandelion petals sticking out of its body.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' N/A

'''Behavior:''' If the player damages its body, it will spread out all of its dandelion missiles that remain on the body, which will reduce your healing for 30 seconds. If its killed before all of its missiles are launched, they will be shot at the same time immediately after it dies.

The missiles it shoots reduces regeneration from petals or players. This acts the same as the [[Dandelion|Dandelion petal]], with the difference: it is large and does not have regeneration.

==Spawning==
The '''Dandelion mob''' occasionally spawns in [[Garden]]. It spawns commonly at the southwest tunnel in [[Garden]].

==Drops==
===[[Dandelion]]===
{{:Dandelion (Mob)/Drop Dandelion}}


==Strategies==
=== Dandelion body===
* Dandelion is very easy to kill as it has low health, so shooting with ranged petals can easily kill them.
*Avoid the [[Dandelion]] petals that they shoot at players, or try to stay away from dandelions and shoot them out with [[Missile|Missiles]], [[Peas]], [[Grapes]] etc (basically any ranged [[petals]]).
* If you ram the [[Dandelion]] petals, your healing will be decreased by -20%. It might be dangerous if your health is low. Try waiting and avoiding [[Hornet|Hornets]] if you have low health.
*You can also try killing it with just standing nearby, firstly destroying the missile dandelions, then attacking the core.
*You can use stingers or other high-damage petals to clear out an arc of the dandelions so you don't get hit.
*Use Eggs. They can destroy the missiles,then when then dandelion shoots the remaining missiles,go behind the ants so none of the other missiles can hit you.You could also dodge them,but it's harder.You could use [[Beetle]] or [[Sandstorm]] too.
*Ramming a Dandelion is a fast and efficient way to get rid of them. However, if you do so, beware not to hit the missiles because they'll reduce your healing, a crucial component to ram builds. Use a normal build to bring it down to about 95% so its missiles get shot out, and then ram the body until it dies.

===Dandelion missiles ===
The dandelion missiles get launched when the body takes damage. There's some guides how to tank/avoid them.

*Dodge the missiles, most are tankable but M+ ones may be dangerous.
*In case the missile is bigger than a legendary one, be careful, since they deal a lot of damage and have some health. It's better do dodge them, especially the ultra's missiles.
*Don't get in range (which is normally size of using legendary [[antennae]]) to avoid get hit. The missiles despawn when they're out of their range.
*If somebody deals damage to the petals (in case the Dandelion petals are strong enough to oneshot you), stay at the petal they are with, in case they hit the core, which release all the missiles and kills you unexpectedly.
*Due to the sheer amount of players and dandelions, dodging the missiles is not easy. To make matters worse, the health regeneration is reduced, making you more vulnerable to Hornet missiles.
*Nowadays, Dandelions don't spawn as often. This means that it is unlikely you will actually find one and even more unlikely its missiles will hit you. However, beware, because groups of [[Hornet|Hornets]] and [[Spider|Spiders]] can chase you into a Dandelion... reducing your healing for when you have to fight them!

=== Super Dandelion ===
* In Garden, the main danger when fighting a Super mob is not the actual mob, but the many Ultra mobs that spawn around the mob. When bubbling to the mob, it is advised to use Sponge and multiple Ultra healing petals, and have a Lotus equipped in case a Spider hits you. It is also advised to use Sponge when actually fighting the mob so you don't die if anything hits you. 
* If the mob spawns deep in Garden, it is advised to form a "megasquad", where a large group of players stay near each other and clear mobs. This increases the chance for everyone to survive.
* Because Dandelion is rarer than other garden mobs, there will be very high demand for this mob. This means that high DPS is required for loot, but it also means that the Dandelion projectiles will not be relevant, as they will most likely be gone by the time you get there.

==Trivia==  
*The Dandelion mob was added due to a replacement from Hornet's drop from Dandelion petal into Orange.
*The Dandelion is the first stationary mob that can attack at all rarities.
**It is also one of the only 3 stationary mobs that can attack. Others are [[Rock (Mob)|Rock mob]], with its Super ability which summons Rocks based on damage dealt, and the [[Barrel]], which releases poison when attacked.
*The Dandelion is one of the only mobs that drop a single petal.
*The Dandelion used to have the same body damage as Basic, now it has 1.5x the base body damage.

title: Dark Mark
content:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Mark}} {{Petal Template Infobox | image = File:Mark_(Common).png|C File:Mark_(Unusual).png|U File:Mark_(Rare).png|R File:Mark_(Epic).png|E File:Mark_(Legendary).png|L File:Mark_(Mythic).png|M File:Mark_(Ultra).png|Ul File:Mark_(Super).png|S File:Mark_(Unique).png|Uq | description = A dark mark that binds itself to a fallen soul. | first-rarity = {{Rarity|Common}} | health = {{Petal Scale|10}} | reload-time = {{Infobox Colors Empty|1.0s + 960.0s|1.0s + 480.0s|1.0s + 240.0s|1.0s + 120.0s|1.0s + 60.0s|1.0s + 30.0s|1.0s + 10.0s|1.0s + 4.0s|1.0s + 2.0s}} | stackability = Stackable | dropped-by = *[[Hel Beetle]] *[[Hel Centipede]] *[[Hel Spider]] *[[Hel Wasp]] | special = Locks onto a dead player.
After some time, sends that player to the [[Hel]] zone. }} The '''Dark Mark''', also shortened in-game to '''Mark''', is a [[Petals|petal]] in florr.io. When it finds a dead player nearby, it marks the same player and sends them to [[Hel]] after some time. The Mark is dropped by [[Hel Beetle]]s, [[Hel Centipede]]s, [[Hel Spider]]s and [[Hel Wasp]]s. ==Appearance== The Mark appears to bear a dark red color with even darker borders. The shape of this petal is a pentagram: a five-pointed regular star with a regular pentagon in the star's central area. ==Function== ''Note: The "A + B" respawn format means that the petal takes A seconds to respawn and B seconds after respawning to do its special action/have its special action enabled.'' {|class="article-table" |+Petal Stats ![[Rarity]] !Obtainable? !Health !Respawn !Hel stuck duration |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |rowspan="9"|Yes |10 HP |1.0 + 960 seconds | rowspan="8" |5 Minutes |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |30 HP |1.0 + 480 seconds |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |90 HP |1.0 + 240 seconds |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |270 HP |1.0 + 120 seconds |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |810 HP |1.0 + 60 seconds |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |2.4k HP |1.0 + 30 seconds |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |7.3k HP |1.0 + 10 seconds |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |21.9k HP |1.0 + 4 seconds |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} |65.6k HP |1.0 + 2 seconds |1 Hour |} The Mark sends any dead player it touches to [[Hel]] after a while. The Mark affects dead players in Hel similar to how it would in other areas, sending the marked player back to Hel. Mark has an extra hidden mechanic in which it functions like [[Yggdrasil]] when the dead player is already in [[Hel]], but instead of reviving the player, it teleports them to a random spot on the map and then revives them. Players who got marked will be stuck in Hel for 5 minutes, means they can't go to other zones except Hel until timer expires. {{Rarity|9}} Mark's location lock lasts for 1 hour. ==Obtainment== ===[[Hel Beetle]]=== {{:Hel Beetle/Drop Dark Mark}} ===[[Hel Centipede]]=== {{:Hel Centipede/Drop Dark Mark}} ===[[Hel Spider]]=== {{:Hel Spider/Drop Dark Mark}} ===[[Hel Wasp]]=== {{:Hel Wasp/Drop Dark Mark}} ===Purchasing=== On certain days, Dark Mark can be purchased in the [[Shop]]. ''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]'' {{Petal Price|5}} ==Strategy== *One must immediately notice that the Mark is not your ordinary petal: as it doesn't deal damage, it can only "tank" stuff (and it has very low health for that matter). Its only uses are for forcing dead players to leave, trolling and for helping other players go to Hel. *As it's different from your normal petal, it lacks proper strategies for itself. However, it still has its own set of strategies designed pretty much just for the Mark. ===Marking=== *One intended way of using the Mark is seeing if anyone wants to go to Hel. This pretty much only happens once per player, as they cannot access Hel by themselves before they get Marked. Doing so will get an [[Achievements|Achievement]] both for you and for the player sent to Hel. *The other, less intended way is using Mark instead of Yggdrasil pretty much anywhere. Many players after dying will expect to get revived with Yggdrasil to come back for combat, specially if the player just died to a {{Rarity|Ultra}} or {{Rarity|Super}} Mob fight so they can keep damaging the mob and potentially get very good loot. However, you can suddenly mark them with the Mark and suddenly send them to Hel, making them lose their drops. It's trolling and a very toxic activity so it's not encouraged here, but {{Rarity|Ultra}}+ Marks are recommended for this as marks of earlier rarities take too long to send the dead player to Hel before a Yggdrasil user revives it. **One can stay on top of the marked player to try to prevent them from knowing you marked them. *The third way is to seek for dead flowers that is probably offline. Marking them is less toxic because they don’t literally lose anything since they are not playing. Doing this around the Target Dummy is recommended. *The fourth way is to Mark enemy players in Factory. However, this is kind of toxic too during {{Rarity|Super}} mob fights. *You can mark [[Bandage]] users while on cooldown or [[Corruption]] users as the fifth way to mark people. In the case of Bandage users, people mark them solely for Bandage users ramming when the {{Rarity|Super}} is at 100% HP. In the case of Corruption, it is the same reason as marking enemy players at Factory. ===Marked=== *This is the only way to initially go to Hel. Asking for some time to get Marked will turn into a free ticket to Hel, and afterwards you can join and leave this mysterious other plane at your will. *However, most of the time, you'll get marked against your will: instead of being properly revived with a Yggdrasil, you'll get Marked and possibly lose all of the chances you had looting the mob that killed you, and even having to go back to where you died. **You can know if someone Marked you if: they are on top of you (to try to hide the Mark), you notice something else on top of you (a Yggdrasil doesn't stay for too long before activating its ability) and noticing what petal the other player may bring to "revive" you: a dried leaf or a pentagram. ***Mark is now rendered above other players, making it easier to tell if you have been marked, as players can no longer stand on top of your flower to hide the Mark. === Marked(but not yourself) === *If your squad member got marked, the best way is to [[Yggdrasil]] that player, of course. **If you don’t have [[Yggdrasil]], you can lead mobs to the Mark and let the mob destroy it. {{Zone|Hel}} ===Using=== *The Mark won't bring any value to either PvP or PvE, serving as an useless petal in this scenario. *This can be used as a Yggdrasil sort of petal, as people dead in Hel are already in Hel, they will simply get revived. This can help if you don't have Yggdrasil yet. ===Against=== *This petal gives zero value in PvP. Killing a Mark user should be easy as you're essentially fighting a player with one less petal slot. *Other players can also revive you with the Mark in Hel. ===Obtaining=== *It can be pretty difficult to get high-rarity Marks, as all mobs that drop Mark are either hard to kill and/or have bad drop rates. **[[Hel Centipede]]s are the easiest to kill out of the 4 mobs that drop the Mark. However, their drop rates are the lowest in the game. This problem is mitigated by their multiple segments, although it still depends on luck. **[[Hel Spider]]s are the least complicated of the 3 mobs with good Mark drop rates. Bring in a [[Lotus]] with you if you have trouble with their poison, or [[Pincer]] if you have trouble with its speed. **[[Hel Wasp]]s are slightly more complicated. [[Plank]] will help you on dealing with its missiles, and use [[Wing]] and other ranged petals to deal with the range problem. **[[Hel Beetle]]s have high speed and can teleport to your location. Bringing glass petals like the [[Stinger]] to quickly deal with it is one of the best strategies. ==Gallery== File:Mark (Common).png|{{R|1}} Mark. File:Mark (Unusual).png|{{R|2}} Mark. File:Mark (Rare).png|{{R|3}} Mark. File:Mark (Epic).png|{{R|4}} Mark. File:Mark (Legendary).png|{{R|5}} Mark. File:Mark (Mythic).png|{{R|6}} Mark. File:Mark (Ultra).png|{{R|7}} Mark. File:Mark (Super).png|{{R|8}} Mark. File:Mark (Unique).png|{{R|9}} Mark. File:Mark.png|Mark. File:MarkUsed.png|A mark being used on a player. File:TheDoubleMark.jpg|Two marks laid by different flowers on the same player. (Thank you Sandwich456) ==Trivia== *[[M28]], upon release, gave everyone at spawn a temporary {{Rarity|Mythic}} Mark and sent everyone to [[Hel]], allowing people to obtain mark. *Mark in Hel can be destroyed by other players while on top of a player. Additionally, different players can mark the same person, with strange results. *Before Hel got added to the buttons on the title screen that allows quick teleportation to areas, the only way to go to Hel was for another player to use the Mark on you while you were dead. *The Mark and its users usually get the reputation of trollers because the Mark doesn't serve for anything other than to send players to Hel, whereas it usually happens on unwanted scenarios. *While Mark, [[Hel Beetle]] and [[Hel]] as a whole were added on the April Fools date, they stayed for longer (and are still in the game). This confirms that Hel and its additions were not April Fools additions. *The Mark had the longest ever respawn time, sitting at 960 seconds (plus one to activate its ability) at {{Rarity|Common}} Rarity). However, an update switched the primary and the secondary cooldown timers (so Mark couldn't be affected by the Reload [[Talents|Talent]] anymore). Now, [[Ankh]] (longest at {{Rarity|Common}} Rarity), [[Yggdrasil]] (also longest at {{Rarity|Common}} Rarity although unreleased) and [[Moon Rock]] and [[Square]] (longest constant timers) have the longest cooldown. It still holds the record for the longest secondary cooldown. *Its initial design actually lacked the pentagon, so it was a star instead of a pentagram. *A change was made to Mark so that "marked" players will be trapped in Hel for 5 minutes (1 hour with Unique) *Hel Beetles drop this, while Mummy [[Beetle/Mummy|Beetles]] drop Ankh. Not only this is a parallel of "life and death" or "heaven and hell", this also indicates both Beetles and Hel Beetles literally carry some kind of essence of life and can also give birth/give life to anything else. This can be further proved that they can drop the [[Beetle Egg]], which indicates the creation of life through the Egg (although this may just mean that both Beetles just give birth to infant Beetles, not everything else). *Mark at the {{Rarity|Ultra}} Rarity is one of the two petals that have the lowest drop rate out of anything: it reaches a whopping 0.0002 (1/500,000) chance to be dropped from {{Rarity|Ultra}} Hel Centipedes, alongside [[Mimic]] from {{Rarity|Ultra}} [[Mecha Flower]]. Mark and the Hel Centipede also break two records: not only the mob drops Mark 5 rarities below the mob's rarity ({{Rarity|Common}} Mark dropped by {{Rarity|Legendary}} Hel Centipede, {{Rarity|Unusual}} Mark dropped by {{Rarity|Mythic}} Hel Centipede and {{Rarity|Rare}} Mark dropped by Ultra Hel Centipede), the mob can also drop the most petal rarities at once: 5 petal rarities (Common-Legendary Mark for Legendary Hel Centipede, Unusual-Mythic Mark for Mythic Hel Centipede and Rare-Ultra Mark for Ultra Hel Centipede). **Those records most likely belong to the Mark and the Hel Centipede because the mob has multiple segments, and said segments each have individual drop rates, thus technically each drop rate shown in the table is 10 times more common. However, it is still dependant on RNG if it will happen or not. ***If segmented drop rates are counted, technically the [[Moon Rock]] has the second rarest drop rate (0.001 chance to be dropped from a [[Rock (Mob)|Rock Mob]]) compared to the technical 0.002 chance of an {{Rarity|Ultra}} Mark being dropped by an {{Rarity|Ultra}} Hel Centipede. *You can get marked by [[Titan|the Titan]]. When that happens, You will be stuck in Hel for 1 hour. *Mark resembles the [[Hel]] map, and this should be intended. The darker lines on the mark also correlates to how [[Hel]] is split into different zones, where the middle pentagon zone correlates to the {{Rarity|Common}} to {{Rarity|Unusual}} zone {{Petals}} [[zh:暗黑标记]] [[Category:Petals]] [[Category:Hel]] [[Category:Common+]] [[Category:Gameplay]] [[Category:Droppable Petals]] [[Category:Stackable Petals]] [[Category:Hel Mobs]] [[Category:Beetles]] [[Category:Wasps and Hornets]] [[Category:Spiders]]

'''Dead Leaf''' is a [[Petals|petal]] in [https://florr.io florr.io]. It increases petal health of all petals, but it also increases their reload by 5%. Dead Leaf is dropped by [[Soldier Ant/Diver|Scuba Soldier Ants]].

==Appearance==
Dead Leaf is a gray recolor of [[Leaf]], with some pieces ripped off.

==Function==

Dead Leaf applies a certain percentage increase in petal health and a 5% increase in reload to all petals, which stacks additively with other Dead Leaves and the [[Talents/Petal Health|Petal Health]] talent.

==Obtainment==

===Purchasing===
On certain days, Dead Leaf can be purchased from the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|4
}}

==Obtaining==
*Defeat {{Rarity|Mythic}}+ [[Soldier Ant/Diver|Scuba Soldier Ants]] to obtain Dead Leaf. Players are expected to spend a considerable amount of time in [[Ocean]] if they wish to obtain the petal, as Scuba Soldier Ants are said to be as rare as [[Ladybug (Desert)|Shiny Ladybugs]] in [[Desert]] or [[Golden Leafbug]]s in [[Jungle]].

==Strategies==
*Dead Leaf can be paired with petals with high petal health stats, including [[Corn]], [[Pearl]], [[Rock]] and [[Sawblade]], to increase their DPS by a certain amount.
*Dead Leaf may be used in tandem with the [[Talents/Petal Health|Petal Health]] talent, but it only stacks additively with the talent. Therefore, for players using high rarity Petal Health talents, this petal may not be entirely beneficial.

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">

==Trivia==
*Dead leaf is the fourth variant of [[Leaf]].
*When released, Dead Leaf had better petal health increase than it has now.
*Dead Leaf actually acts in the same way throughout the rarities, with the only difference being changed stats.
*This petal can actually reduce DPS of certain WoD. This is in because deathwheel based builds, health is sort of irrelevant.
*Golden Leaf and Dead Leaf are the only petals that can attribute to reload stat.

'''Desert''' is an area in florr.io. It is the area that features Desert mobs and Trader, a special entity that wanders around the map.

== Appearances ==
The map is a modern version of the old Desert map, however flipped 180 degrees. The [[Developer Statue|developer statue]] is located at the bottom right area of the map, and there is a pathway densely packed with low-rarity [[Cactus|Cacti]] and [[Sandstorm|Sandstorms]] towards the top of spawn that leads to [[Pyramid]].

==Location==
'''Desert''' is located in the bottom left region of the world map. The zone is a spawn, but it is one zone away from [[Jungle]] and [[Garden]] spawns, and two zones away from [[Ocean]] spawn. 

=== Connections ===
This zone connects to 4 areas.
{| class="article-table"
|+'''Connections'''
!'''Area'''
!'''Connection Location'''
|-
|Garden
|Top
|-
|Jungle
|Top Right
|-
|Ant Hell
|Left
|-
|Pyramid
|Left but closer to Garden portal.
|}

=== Special Zones===
In the top left area, a lot of sandstorms spawn at {{Rarity|Legendary}}/{{Rarity|Mythic}} rarity.

In the the narrow path to the [[Pyramid]], many [[Cactus (Mob)|Cacti]] and [[Sandstorm|Sandstorms]] of {{Rarity|Unusual}}/{{Rarity|Rare}} rarity spawn along it, frequently obstructing it.

In the top right area, Desert mobs spawn frequently at {{Rarity|Legendary}} rarity.

In the bottom tunnel, Desert mobs spawn at {{Rarity|Mythic}}/{{Rarity|Ultra}} rarity, and has the highest [[Yggdrasil]] rate when farming with {{Rarity|Ultra}} petals. 

In the bottom right area, the [[Developer Statue|Dead Developer Statue]] spawns there. Going to it will grant you the "Skill Issue" achievement. 

The large open area at the bottom right is referred to as the box. It is the place most {{R|Super}} mobs spawn. 

'''Spawning'''

A total of 10 mobs spawn in Desert.

'''[[Beetle]]''': An aggressive mob that chases you when you get close and deals high damage on contact. Drops [[Beetle Egg]] and [[Privet Berry|Privet]].

'''[[Scorpion]]''': An aggressive mob that chases you and shoots poison missiles at you when you get close. {{Rarity|Epic|Epic-}} shoot one missile then chase you at a fast speed, but {{Rarity|Legendary|Legendary+}}  have unlimited missiles and shoot one at you every 5 seconds. Drops [[Pincer]] and [[Iris]].

'''[[Desert Centipede]]''': Moves around randomly at a very fast speed, occasionally burrowing underground and resurfacing. {{Rarity|Rare|Rare-}} are passive and {{Rarity|Epic|text=Epic+}} chase you if you hit their head segment. Drops [[Salt]] and [[Mysterious Powder|Powder]].

'''[[Ladybug (Desert)]]''': Very rare mob that is neutral and chases you when hit. Is well known for its prized drop, [[Yggdrasil]]. It has the highest drop rates out of any mob in the game. Drops [[Rose]], [[Dahlia]], and [[Yggdrasil]].

'''[[Cactus (Mob)|Cactus]]''': Static mob that deals high damage upon contact with it. Drops [[Cactus]].

'''[[Sandstorm]]''': Passive mob that moves around randomly and has high health and damage. Drops [[Sand]], [[Glass]] and [[Mysterious Stick|Stick]].

'''[[Fire Ant Burrow]]''': Mob that releases a lot of [[Soldier Fire Ant|Soldier Fire Ants]] when someone gets close and has a 2% chance to spawn a [[Digger]] when all the ants have been released. Drops [[Disc]] and [[Magnet]]. 

[[Beetle/Mummy|'''Mummy Beetle''']]: Mob that is similar to a normal beetle but can stay alive for a certain duration after their health bar is drained. Drops [[Bandage]], [[Ankh]], and [[Beetle Egg]]

[[Soldier Fire Ant|'''Soldier Fire Ant''']]: Mob that has similar stats to a regular [[Soldier Ant]] but with increased damage.

'''[[Digger]]''': A mob that is friendly to players and attacks other mobs. Has a 0.2 to appear in a [[Fire Ant Burrow]], [[Ant Hole]] or [[Termite Mound]] under different circumstances for very one of them. Diggers can be corrupted if it's owned by the player (who got the kill credit for it).

==Strategy==
*Be careful, as most of the mobs here move faster than you, so you will need to either kill them quickly or use [[Pincer]] or [[Web]]. Make sure you have a Pincer or Web in your secondary slot in case you run into a high rarity beetle or centipede.
*Don't use a ram build here, as most of the mobs here have above-average body damage.

==Gallery ==

File:Desert map have a new locate.png|New location: [[Pyramid]].
File:Desert.png|Desert map before Pyramid got added.

==Tiles==

==Trivia==
*The [[Trader]] can be found in the bottom left area. The Trader looks like a flower with a white [[Cotton]] body. The Trader could be outside from its original area since it is always moving around.
*The map is similar to the old map but upside down.
*The only water body in this map is an oasis located below the spawn.

title: Dice
content:
{{Petal Template Infobox | image = File:Dice (Common).png|C File:Dice (Unusual).png|U File:Dice (Rare).png|R File:Dice (Epic).png|E File:Dice (Legendary).png|L File:Dice (Mythic).png|M File:Dice (Ultra).png|Ul File:Dice (Super).png|S File:Dice (Unique).png|Uq | description = Has a 5% chance of dealing 35x damage.
Chance increases by 4% per point in luck. | first-rarity = {{Rarity|Common}} | damage = {{Petal Scale|10}} | health = {{Petal Scale|10}} | reload-time = 2.5s | dropped-by = *[[Gambler]] *[[Hel Beetle]] | special = Has a 5% base chance to deal 35x its normal damage.
The chance is affected by Clover and Luck talent. }} '''Dice''' is a [[petal]] in [https://florr.io florr.io]. It has a 5% chance of dealing 35x its base damage, and the chance increases by 4% per point in luck, either by [[Clover]] or [[Talents/Luck|Luck talent]]. Dice is dropped by [[Gambler]]s and [[Hel Beetle]]s. ==Appearance== Dice is a white square with a variable number of pips (the black dots) on it, which varies between 1-6 pips around every 0.6 seconds. When changing, the number of pips will twitch and circle before changing to another number of pips (e.g., from 3 to 4). If the game rolls the same number of pips twice, Dice won't change appearance nor the pip number will twitch and circle, and a new pip number will happen in the next 0.6 seconds as usual. ==Function== {|class="article-table" |+Petal Stats !rowspan="2"|[[Rarity]] !rowspan="2"|Obtainable? !colspan="2"|Damage !rowspan="2"|Health !rowspan="2"|Respawn !rowspan="2"|Special |- !Base Damage !Crit Damage (35x Base) |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |rowspan="9"|Yes |10 |350 |10 |rowspan="9"|2.5s |rowspan="9"|Has a 5% chance of dealing 35x damage.
Chance increases by 4% per point in luck. |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |30 |1,050 |30 |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |90 |3,150 |90 |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |270 |9,450 |270 |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |810 |28,350 |810 |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |2,430 |85,050 |2,430 |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |7,290 |255,150 |7,290 |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |21,870 |765,450 |21,870 |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} |65,610 |2,296,350 |65,610 |} Dice has the same stats as [[Basic]] and a 5% chance to deal 35x its base damage upon hitting a mob. The chance increases by 4% per point in luck, either by [[Clover]] or [[Talents/Luck|Luck talent]]. For example, with {{Rarity|Unique}} Luck talent and {{Rarity|Super}} Clover, Dice has a 5% + (2.9 + 0.8) × 4% = 19.8% chance to land a critical hit. ==Obtainment== ===[[Gambler]]=== {{:Gambler/Drop Dice}} ===[[Hel Beetle]]=== {{:Hel Beetle/Drop Dice}} ===Purchasing=== On certain days, Dice can be purchased in the [[Shop]]. ''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]'' {{Petal Price|4}} ==Obtaining== *Defeat [[Gambler]]s or [[Beetle/Hel|Hel Beetles]] to obtain Dice. Gamblers have better drop chances, but spawn less frequently than Hel Beetles. Given the PvP nature and the difficulty of [[Hel]], it is not recommended to farm Dice in early-game and mid-game. ==Strategy== *Dice can be a robust DPS option when used in a dedicated build. **A full {{Rarity|Ultra}} Dice build which consists of 8 Dice, 1 [[Clover]] and 2 [[Talents/Duplicator|duplicated]] [[Faster]]s does an average of 653,523 DPS on {{Rarity|Ultra}}+ mobs with base [[armor]] stats, assuming ideal conditions with {{Rarity|Super}} [[Talents/Reload|Reload talent]], {{Rarity|Legendary}} [[Talents/Petal Rotation|Petal Rotation talent]] (can be adjusted to match the optimal rotation of Dice) and {{Rarity|Unique}} [[Talents/Luck|Luck talent]]. However, it should be noted that Dice's DPS output can fluctuate greatly as it is luck-based after all. Other consistent DPS builds should be considered if one expects a {{Rarity|Super}} mob to be defeated in a short amount of time. **Against {{Rarity|Super}} mobs with high armor stats, for example [[Leafbug|Leafbugs]], [[Golden Leafbug|Golden Leafbugs]] and [[Mecha Flower|Mecha Flowers]], Dice can be a decent choice as its critical hit can effectively bypass their armor thresholds. The player might have to be lucky on critical hits in order to secure loot from said mobs, as newer and stronger DPS strategies such as [[Iris]]-[[Privet]], [[Pincer]]-Privet and {{Rarity|Super}} [[Triangle]] WoD have become increasingly popular in the current meta. *While Dice can be used for farming, it requires Clover and/or Luck talent to maximize its performance. If one does not own high-rarity Dice (especially {{Rarity|Super}}) or cannot afford the entire talent branch, it may be better to not use Dice at all. *In player-player interactions, high-rarity Dice could be a huge threat not only because of its extreme critical damage but also its ability to multi-hit if the player's body damage is low (which can be increased through [[Talents/Sharp Edges|Sharp Edges talent]]). ==Gallery== File:Dice (Common).png|{{R|1}} Dice. File:Dice (Unusual).png|{{R|2}} Dice. File:Dice (Rare).png|{{R|3}} Dice. File:Dice (Epic).png|{{R|4}} Dice. File:Dice (Legendary).png|{{R|5}} Dice. File:Dice (Mythic).png|{{R|6}} Dice. File:Dice (Ultra).png|{{R|7}} Dice. File:Dice (Super).png|{{R|8}} Dice. File:Dice (Unique).png|{{R|9}} Dice. File:Dice.png|Dice. ==Trivia== *The florr.io Dice has 6 faces: each individual face has a certain number of pips (one face has one pip, another face has two pips, and so on until the last face has all six pips). The player can't visually see the other five faces without the aid of the constantly changing pips. **Despite the presence of dice with even more faces (or even less), in-game Dice just shows a singular face implying it has 6 total faces, likely due to simplification issues as florr.io is a 2D game with a cartoonish and simple style, not being able to show 3D forms or an in-game Dice with more faces. **The Dice's pip numbers don't affect its critical hit chance. *Dice originally had a 10% chance of dealing 10x damage, until [[M28]] halved the chance and buffed critical hit by 3.5 times. *This petal is the first petal to have its stats (in this case, DPS) affected by RNG. The other petals, also Hel petals, are [[Card]], which randomly spawns on other suits (each having different damage/durability stats), and [[Domino]], which has a random damage value each time it spawns. **Dice, Card, Domino, [[Glass]] and [[Rock]] are the only petals to always change appearance, regardless of rarity (although Dice changes faces every 0.5 seconds, while Card changes suits every respawn, Domino spawns with a random number of pips and finally, Glass and Rock change appearances every time they are equipped). The petal closest to meet this condition is [[Poker Chip|Chip]] (also a Hel petal) which changes appearance every time it reaches a new rarity. ***All four Hel petals are associated with RNG: Dice has a chance to land critical hits as well as a random chance to change to a certain number of pips, Card gets a random suit upon respawn, Chip is known for the luck associated with [[Plinko]] and Domino spawns with a random number of pips which determines its damage. *Dice has the highest damage potential due to its critical hit mechanic, outclassing [[Claw]] by about 3 times. *This petal was added unannounced in the Changelog. *The first {{R|8}} Dice was acquired by Manfred, who bought it for 1.5 million stars. *Dice has one of the highest theoretical DPS, even equaling to ones found from [[Rice]] + [[Yin Yang]] or ram builds. With a fully upgraded Luck talent tree and an {{Rarity|Ultra}} Clover, a single {{Rarity|Mythic}} Dice will deal on average 10,935.2 damage per hit on an {{Rarity|Ultra}} mob. *This is the only petal to be buffed by another petal which only boosts a single petal, which is [[Clover]]. **The Clover-Dice interaction, the [[Ant Egg]]-[[Relic]] interaction and the [[Compass]]-[[Magnet]] interaction are the only interactions in the entire game to happen between only two petals. All other interactions are more generalized (for example, [[Basil]] and healing petals). *At some point, [[Hel Wasp]]s were able to drop Dice, until it was changed to drop Chips instead. *This, Card, Chip, [[Corruption]], Domino and [[Mark]] are the only petals exclusive to Hel. *The first {{Rarity|Unique}} Dice was acquired by Pehiley, who bought 5 {{R|8}} Dice for a total of 15 million stars. *Dice was used to be dropped by all Hel mobs when Gamblers weren't added. {{Petals}} [[zh:骰子]] [[Category:Petals]] [[Category:Luck Based]] [[Category:Hel]] [[Category:Common+]] [[Category:Stackable Petals]] [[Category:Droppable Petals]] [[Category:Quick-Respawn]] [[Category:Hel Mobs]] [[Category:Beetles]] [[Category:Gambler]]

The '''Digger''' is a rare [[Mobs|mob]] in florr.io. It is an ally, meaning you cannot damage it, nor can it damage you. They spawn from [[Ant Hole]]s, [[Fire Ant Burrow]]s, and [[Termite Mound]]s.

==Appearance==
The '''Digger''' mob represents the player character from [https://Digdig.io digdig.io], appearing as a cameo for florr.io, having a gray body and black sharp ridges around it.

==Stats & Behavior ==
'''Speed:''' Normal

'''Behavior:''' Unlike other mobs, the Digger will target other mobs instead of players. When there are no available mobs to attack, the Digger's face will change to a smile. If the Digger's health is presumably low (around 5%), its face will change to a sad face and it will run away to an edge and stay there until killed by mobs. If there are no mobs nearby while low on health, the Digger will stand still.<!--(maybe flees at 5% hp and goes aggro at 10%? values are approximate please verify).--> Its health will drain over time until it dies even when not attacked, lasting around 3 minutes. If the player who spawns the Soldier Fire Ants and Digger equips [[Corruption]], the Digger will turn red, and target players over mobs, and can be attacked, as is the corrupted player.

==Spawn==
The Digger may spawn with a 5% chance from [[Fire Ant Burrow]]s and 10% from [[Ant Hole]]s and [[Termite Mound]]s with the Digger matching the hole's rarity. It is an ally mob, which means it harms hostile mobs and can't harm players nor ally spawned mobs.

==Drops==
The digger drops [[Cutter]] and [[Uranium|Uranium.]]
===[[Cutter]]===
{{ID 7}}

===[[Uranium]]===
{{:Digger/Drop Uranium}}

==Strategy==
*Diggers' HP drains over time even when no mobs are attacking them, so reach them as soon as possible if you are informed by other players about their spawning.
*You want to be the main one to kill the [[Ant Hole]] that the '''Digger''' is in, then you will get the loot.
===Pre-update===
*As the player who found the digger, you can protect it by killing mobs when it is at low health. However, doing this is not advised, as these kinds of players that protect the diggers are known as "Digger Police". They are usually there to troll other players who want the loot, however '''Digger''' now takes damage if not in a Burrow, Mound, or Hole, so even if people protect it, you still get the loot.

*As the other players, use [[Honey]], [[Bulb]], and [[Clover]] to attract mobs. There also may be other utility petals to kill a '''Digger''', such as [[Uranium]]. If you don't kill the '''[[Digger]]''' that doesn't belong to you, it will be difficult to get any loot from other mobs. 
**A way to get the '''Digger''' out of the corners of the map is to push it with non-hostile mobs, aka ally mobs, similar to the Digger, which can be spawned with [[Ant Egg]]s, [[Beetle Egg]]s, and [[Stick]]s, This is quite difficult due to the '''Digger''' trying to push its way back and your mobs protecting the Digger, however [[Mysterious Stick|Stick]] no longer works because of a new update.

== Gallery ==
[[File:Mythic digger in garden.png|thumb|{{Rarity|6}} Digger in Garden (ignore the apps or battery warning)]]

==Trivia==
*The first ever {{R|Super}} Digger was spawned in the EU Server at May 4, 2024.
*The Digger is one of the few non-player entities featuring a face, others being [[Gambler]], [[Target Dummy]], [[Titan]], [[Mecha Flower]] and [[Ghost]].
*The Digger is one of the few mobs that is an ally, but it cannot be spawned by eggs or petals unlike [[Sandstorm]], [[Soldier Ant]], or [[Beetle]].
**The Ants and Beetles spawned from Eggs are considered allies. The same goes for the Sandstorms spawned from Sticks.
*The Digger is an obvious reference to [https://Digdig.io digdig.io], a game made by the same developer of florr.io, commonly known as [[M28]]. The mob's description supports this, as it's not supposed to appear here, because it's from another game.
**The [[Uranium]] petal is a reference to an ore in that same game that players can mine and use to their advantage.
*Diggers can be slowed by a [[Spider|Spider's]] laid webs.
*Diggers flee when at low health. The code does not account for the borders of the map, meaning that diggers often continuously bounce off of the map’s border when at low health.
*It is one of 2 mobs referencing one of M28's games, the other being the [[Square (Mob)|Square]]. The Square is from Diep.io and Arras.io.
*Diggers spawn in the most areas out of any mob, at three. ([[Garden]], [[Desert]], [[Jungle]])
**[[Spider|Spiders]], [[Triangle|Crabs]], and several [[Ant Egg|ant]] variants spawn in two different biomes.
*This is the third mob to have the highest health out of every mob in the game, the second is [[Termite Overmind]] and first is [[Ant Hole]].
*On September 9, 2023, there was a {{Rarity|Mythic}} Digger in [[Centralia Fields/North|Centralia Fields 3]] in EU server nicknamed "Jared". It survived for around 15 hours, then it died to a {{Rarity|Mythic}} [[Bee]] making it one of the longest lasting Diggers in Florr.io history.
**After several days, a second one spawned in NA CF3. After many hours of protecting the digger to protect it from mobs (much to the annoyance of many), it wound up getting pushed outside of the map. It never died and only despawned when the game updated days later.
*On December 29, 2023, Diggers were given health drain which made them slowly lose health. this made it so that {{Rarity|Ultra}} and {{Rarity|Super}} Diggers didn't last forever.
**After this change, {{Rarity|Super}} Diggers went from the longest living {{Rarity|Super}} mobs to one of the shortest living Super mobs.
*When a Digger kills a mob, you will get drops from it even if you did not damage it as long  as you got kill credit for the [[Ant Hole]], [[Fire Ant Burrow]], or [[Termite Mound]] it spawned from.
*It is possible to imitate a Digger by using [[Air]] and [[Cutter]]. Being in [[Factory]] makes you appear gray to other players, making you look like a Digger.
*Using a [[Corruption]] with your Digger will turn the Digger red, turning it against other players, and allowing them to attack it and loot.
*Unlike other mobs with faces, it looks to its right in the [[Mob Gallery]].

| first-rarity = {{Rarity|Common}}
| collision-damage-resistance = {{Infobox Colors Empty|+9%|+17.2%|+24.6%|+31.4%|+37.6%|+43.2%|+48.3%|+53%|+57.2%}}

'''Disc''' is a [[petal]] from [https://florr.io florr.io] that was released on January 10th 2024. This is a wearable petal, which reduces all collision damage received between mobs and flowers by a certain percentage. Disc is dropped commonly by [[Fire Ant Burrow]]s upon all [[Soldier Fire Ant]]s that have spawned from it being destroyed.

==Appearance==
The Disc looks like a large, thick and black ring, similar to the Naked [[w:c:digdigio:Skins|skin]] in digdig.io, except the Disc's line appears to be thicker. Disc will also make [[Cutter]] larger when both are equipped at the same time.

==Function==
{|class="article-table"
|+Petal Stats
![[Rarity]]
!Obtainable?
!Collision Damage Resistance 
|-
|{{Rarity|Common}}
|rowspan="9"|Yes
| +9%
|-
|{{Rarity|Unusual}}
| +17.2%
|-
|{{Rarity|Rare}}
| +24.6%
|-
|{{Rarity|Epic}}
| +31.4%
|-
|{{Rarity|Legendary}}
| +37.6%
|-
|{{Rarity|Mythic}}

|-

|-

|}
Disc reduces all incoming collision damage received between mobs and flowers by a certain percentage. Effect does not stack with each other. Highest rarity and the 1st slot in the build will be prioritised.

Disc is an alternative petal to petals that can reduces damage taken such as Cotton.

Note that Disc ability don't work with mob projectiles or flowers petals.

==Obtainment==
===[[Fire Ant Burrow]]===
{{:Fire Ant Burrow/Drop Disc}}

===Purchasing===
On certain days, Disc can be purchased in the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|5}}

==Strategy==
*The Disc's most reliable place to be used is in ram builds: it allows you to take less damage, letting you land more hits on mobs before having to retreat and heal. Pairing this with [[Basil]] and passive heals can possibly outheal most enemies, with Cutter will give you more body damage per hit while you take less damage, while this and [[Cactus]] will give you more HP to let you deal more hits, and finally, this and Cutter will further decrease the amount of hits you need to land in order to kill a mob.
*Disc can also work well with [[Relic]], [[Root]] and [[Shell]]. Relic will further spread damage taken if other mobs are nearby, or use [[Ant Egg]]s with this and Relic instead to share damage taken with the spawned [[Soldier Termite]]s. Root will give you armor while you're in the defensive state, allowing you to take an even smaller amount of damage per ram. Shell will give you a shield meter for you to hit even more times.
*Another great petal for using alongside Disc is [[Salt]], as you'll take damage from the enemy entity while dealing damage to them, thus Salt will reflect some percentage of the damage taken to you back to the enemy, further damaging them.
*Using this with [[Amulet]] is pretty bad, since constantly ramming enemies will usually destroy your burst heals like [[Rose]], meaning Amulet won't have time to shield you like the Shell does. To heal yourself after ramming, use Basil with passive heals like the [[Leaf]].

{{Zone|Garden}}
*[[Spider]]s will oftentimes ram you if you're not careful, as well as [[Bee]]s and specially [[Bumble Bee]]s.
*Another viable strategy is constantly ramming a [[Ladybug]], escaping its hitbox, healing and then going back to ramming. It's preferred to go into DPS mode for higher rarities, though.
*This is also viable when fighting [[Centipede]]s, as you'll ram your way into destroying all of its segments.
*Using this against lower rarity [[Ant Hole]]s will let you shred through it and all of the spawned Ants.

{{Zone|Desert}}
*[[Beetle]]s can prove to be deadly, specially with the higher speed they have. It is advised to use this petal to help you survive.
*Another good contender for Disc is [[Sandstorm]]. They're notorious for being very dangerous for their high body damage combined with a high speed and unpredictable movements. If you happen to accidentally ram into one with a Disc, chances of survival greatly increase.
*[[Desert Centipede]]s, while rammable, might get you killed if you try to kill them with ram damage, because they're even faster than a Beetle and might not let you escape, unless you use [[Jelly]] or [[Rubber]].

===Obtaining===
*[[Fire Ant Burrow]]s appear pretty commonly around Desert. You shouldn't have a hard time killing all of the [[Soldier Fire Ant]]s spawned for a chance at getting the Disc.
*Beware of high rarity Fire Ant Burrows. Their Soldier Fire Ants might kill you quickly. Also try to not get killed by the other mobs in Desert while running from the Ants.

{{Zone|Ocean}}
*Disc is pretty useful with Rubber against [[Crab]]s, as if you happen to ram an {{Rarity|Ultra}}+ Crab speeding onto you, you'll have less chances to die, while Rubber will let you escape. The same can be said about [[Leech]]es.
*It is also useful against [[Shell (Mob)|Shells]], even more so at Ultra/{{Rarity|Super}} rarities. If it manages to catch you, or if it fires multiple deadly Pearls to you, Disc will greatly reduce damage taken, reducing chances of death.

{{Zone|Ant Hell}}
*Disc is a pretty good contender for all Ant Hells: while it can just be a general saver if you happen to get cornered, swarmed or caught, it can help tank the Fire Ants' extra damage (Super Disc completely negates it), as well as tank more hits from Termites while all of them get shared ram damage.

{{Zone|Hel}}
===Using===
*The Disc is viable in Hel as well, not only because it suits ram builds (who are pretty popular in the meta) very well, but also because it can mitigate opposing ram builds, as you'll take less damage if enemies [[Bubble]] up to you and ram you.
*Disc's also good in PvE, as it can help fighting any other mobs here: [[Gambler]]s (if you manage to get close to them), [[Hel Beetle]]s, [[Hel Centipede]]s, [[Hel Spider]]s (beware of all three's speed and Hel Beetle's teleportation, though) and [[Hel Wasp]]s and their projectiles.

===Against===
*Countering ram builds with Disc is simple as long as you can keep a distance from them, hit their Bubbles, deplete their HP slowly and be cautious around your surroundings. Countering Disc users who want to counter ram builds is pretty much the same thing.
*Keep in mind Disc only reduces body damage, so attacking them with petals will go as normal. Use burst petals like [[Stinger]] to quickly kill them.
*A less viable strategy would be using [[Cutter]] and [[Salt]] yourself to try to negate the reduced body damage. It will work better with other typical ram builds.

{{Zone|Jungle}}
*[[Ladybug (Jungle)|Dark Ladybugs]] and [[Evil Centipede]]s are great contenders for Disc ramming. This can also take decent care of [[Leafbug]]s and their Armor stat (which can be further downgraded with [[Bur]]).

{{Zone|Sewers}}
*As all mobs will mainly try to ram you, Disc is very good for increased survival, specially since [[Roach]]es are too fast to reliably escape from them. Don't forget to bring a [[Lotus]] as Spiders are pretty common here.

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">
File:Disc (Common).png|{{R|1}} Disc.
File:Disc (Unusual).png|{{R|2}} Disc.
File:Disc (Rare).png|{{R|3}} Disc.
File:Disc (Epic).png|{{R|4}} Disc.
File:Disc (Legendary).png|{{R|5}} Disc.
File:Disc (Mythic).png|{{R|6}} Disc.
File:Disc (Ultra).png|{{R|7}} Disc.
File:Disc (Super).png|{{R|8}} Disc.
File:Disc (Unique).png|{{R|9}} Disc.
File:Disc.png|Disc.
File:Flower wearing Disc.png|A flower wearing a Disc.
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*Disc's addition on the January 10th, 2024 update was added unmentioned in the [[Changelog]].
*Disc is an exclusive drop of Fire Ant Burrow.
*This is the third [https://digdig.io digdig.io]-themed petal, with the first two being Cutter and [[Uranium]]. It's also the fourth reference to said game, with the others being Cutter, [[Digger]] and Uranium.
**The Disc is based on the "Naked" skin from the same game.
*When used with Cutter, the latter gains a thicker border. This is the first petal to visually change another petal's appearance.
*Disc is one of the seven wearable petals in the game, with the others being Antennae, Mecha Antennae, Cutter, [[Third Eye]], Relic, [[Third Eye/Magic|Magic Eye]] and lastly Bandage.
* Collision Damage Resistance stats can be calculated with the following formula<math>1-0.91^{r+1}</math>where r is the rarity index ({{Rarity|Common}} starts at 0, {{Rarity|Unusual}} starts at 1, etc.)

'''{{SUBPAGENAME}}''' was an ocean area in florr.io. It is home to [[Crab|Crabs]] and other ocean mobs. It is now at the bottomright area of [[Ocean]] and also spawns [[Jellyfish]].

==Appearance==
The map is a much narrower looking map that has rock walls similar to [[Centralia]]. Some of the map's features indicate shapes such as faces, crab arms, and etc. 

==Location==
{{SUBPAGENAME}} was located in the top right region of the world map in the [[Ocean]]. The zone is {5} zones from [[Centralia]], the standard spawn location. Assuming no interruptions, it takes around {?} minutes and {?} seconds to reach this zone, without using [[Bubble|Bubbles]] or [[Powder]].

===Connections To===
Crab Kingdom has only one connection, that being [[East Waters/North|East Waters/Northwest]].
{| class="article-table"
|+Connections
!Area
!Connection Location
|-
|East Waters 1 (North)
|Middle Bottom
|}

==Spawning==
The average rarity in {{SUBPAGENAME}} is Legendary. Mobs typically spawn from Epic to Ultra. At the start of the area, you will see Epic or Legendary Shells outside the walls. Epic and Legendary Crabs will spawn everywhere throughout the area, along with bubbles with the same rarity. At the top of the map, you will find Mythic crabs and bubbles that seem to be guarding a throne that looks to belong to the Ultra Crab.

A total of 3 types of mobs spawn in {{SUBPAGENAME}}.
{| class="article-table"
|+Mobs
!Mob
!Spawn Rate
|-
|[[Crab]]
|Very High
|-
|[[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble]]
|Moderate
|-
|[[Shell (Mob)|Shell]]
|Moderate (Bottom of the map)
|}

===Special Zones===
Shells spawn in an area at the bottom of the area.

=== Ultra/Super Mobs ===
{{Rarity|7}}/{{Rarity|8}} Crab and Bubble used to spawn mostly in this area.

== Mob Rarity ==
{| class="article-table"
!Zone
!Bubble
!Shell
!Crab
|-
|The Throne Room
|Legendary - Mythic (Ultra rarely spawns here)
| rowspan="2" |N/A
|Mythic - Ultra
|-
|The Keep
|Legendary
|Legendary
|-
|The Ward
|Epic - Legendary (Rare rarely spawns here)
|Epic
|Rare - Epic
|-
|The Outskirts
|Rare - Epic
|Rare - Epic
(Legendary rarely spawns here)
|Rare - Epic
|}

==Strategy==
*It is recommended you deal with Crabs carefully as they will be the main mob you see in the area, if there are any Mythic crabs chasing you, lead them away from other mobs and deal with it as quickly as you can. 
*The Ultra Crab should spawn infrequently at the top of the map on the "throne". When it spawns, try to keep a distance away from it with [[Web|Webs]] and such and find an area the ultra crab cannot access like the crab arm structures, then you can take care of the Ultra Crab from there, slowly but surely.
*Crab Kingdom is one of the most challenging zones due to the overpowered crabs. Be sure to have a couple of [[soil]] or [[cactus]] because the crabs deal massive damage.

==Gallery==
<gallery>
File:Crab (Ultra).png|Ultra Crab
File:Super Bubble.jpg|An Ultra Crab
File:Shell Zone.png|Shell Zone
</gallery>

== Trivia ==
*As the name implies, this is where the [[Crab|Crabs]] live, the Crab Kingdom.
*This is the only zone where two ultras can spawn at, those being the [[Crab]] and the [[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble]].
*has a bubble zone in the armpits of the claw picture
*Crab kingdom is the only place that spawns two Ultra mobs/Super mobs
*This area was removed on April 12, 2024 update along with other East Waters areas and replaced with [[Ocean]]. Despite its removal, a smaller version of this zone appears in the bottom-right corner of Ocean.
{{Removed Areas}}

'''{{SUBPAGENAME}}''' was an area in florr.io. It is the area that spawns jellyfishes.

==Appearance==
The background is water and the walls are made of dirt.

The map of the area itself resembles a jellyfish, with portals at the southernmost points of the tentacles and mobs further north.

==Location==
{{SUBPAGENAME}} was located in the top right region of the world map. The zone is 6 zones from [[Centralia]], the standard spawn location. Assuming no interruptions, it takes around {number} minutes and {number} seconds to reach this zone, without using [[Bubble|Bubbles]] or [[Powder]].

It is accessible through entering the northernmost portals in [[East Waters/East]] (Ocean maze).

===Connections===
The area has 5 portals but all connected to East Water 3.
{| class="article-table"
|+Connections
!Area
!Connection Location
|-
|East Waters 3 (Northwest)
|Down (5 paths from left to right)
|}
==Spawning==
The average rarity in {{SUBPAGENAME}} is {{Rarity|Mythic}}. Mobs typically spawn from {{Rarity|5}} to {{Rarity|8}}.

A total of 4 mobs spawn in {{SUBPAGENAME}}.
{| class="article-table"
|+Mobs
!Mob
!Spawn Rate 
|-
|Jellyfish
|High
|-
|Bubble
|Low
|-
|Crab
|Low
|-
|Shell
|Low
|}
===Special Zones===
There are no special zones in this area. 

===Ultra Mobs ===
{{Rarity|7}}/{{Rarity|8}} Jellyfish and {{Rarity|7}}/{{Rarity|8}} Bubble used to spawn mostly in this area.

==Strategy==
*Jellyfish are large and tanky, and their damage can be reduced with [[Rubber]].
**Players should use healing petals to out-heal their weak attacks.
**Ironically, Lightning is effective against Jellyfish, since they often arrive in swarms if not quickly killed. 
**Summoners are only effective if the petal is Ultra or above. Anything below only serves as food for the Jellyfish to chain its lightning on. 
**Ultra Jellyfish is extremely dangerous . Stick with a group of players and retreat if low on health. Watch out of its wave that deals a lot of damage. Alternatively, you can also lure to the bottom of the map so it can't attack anyone.
**Use Ranged petals such as missile and wing or any petal with third eye as you can stay away from the range(especially mythic jellyfish since they deal 1.7k damage(which is fatal to people who don't have enough hp.)
**Use [[Rubber]] to resist the lightning damage.
*Shells are tanky but drop valuable loot. Most players are gunning for them and Bubbles so be quick about killing them. 
*Bubbles drop Bubbles, the best petal for getting around fast. If you see one, immediately kill it for its good loot. 
*Crabs are the most dangerous mob in Jellyfish Fields. They are unpredictable, fast, and deal lots of damage. 
**Use ranged petals like Wing and Missile. 
**Get up close, hit it with burst-damage petals like Stinger and Iris, and retreat. 

==Gallery==
<gallery>
File:Eggy.png|Relevant image or screenshot
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*When it was first released, it used to be called "East Waters".
*It is the only area to have 5 portals.
*Jellyfish Fields is based on the place of the [[w:c:spongebob:Jellyfish_Fields|same name]] from SpongeBob.
*This area was removed on April 12, 2024 update along with other East Waters areas and replaced with [[Ocean]].
{{Removed Areas}}
