'''Electric Web''' is a [[Petals|petal]] in [https://florr.io florr.io]. It is capable of slowing down mobs while dealing lightning damage to them over time. Electric Web is dropped by [[Spider/Mecha|Mecha Spiders]].

==Appearance==
Electric Web looks exactly like normal [[Web]], but is tinted blue. When placed on the ground, it shows an alternating yellow-blue tint with blue being most noticeable.

==Function==
''Note: The "A + B" respawn format means that the petal takes A seconds to respawn and B seconds after respawning to do its special action/have its special action enabled.''

Electric Web shoots away (much like [[Missile]] and [[Dandelion]]) for a short distance and turns into a web, at which point it goes on respawn. The web slows enemy flowers or mobs (except [[Spider|Spiders]], [[Hel Spider|Hel Spiders]] and [[Shell (Mob)|Shells]]) on it down by a certain amount, depending on the rarity, and deals lightning damage. The slow effect can't stack with other Electric Webs, but can stack with other slowing petals.

Electric Web also does not stack with itself or other Electric Webs from the same player. However, the damage does stack across other [[Flower|Flower's]] Electric Webs. This makes using multiple Electric Webs only useful to affect a larger area.

===Purchasing===
On certain days, Electric Web can be purchased from the [[Shop]]. The current data may not be accurate.

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|2}}

==Strategies==
*Electric Web is essentially regular [[Web]] with AoE lightning damage, which is a niche option for dealing with mob swarms. Notable biomes or places where this petal could be useful are [[Ant Hell|Fire Ant Hell]] (for AFK farming) and [[Sewers]] (against [[Fly|Flies]] as lightning bypasses their evasion).
*Do be wary that Electric Web's slow is not as effective as regular Web.

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">
File:Electric web rare.webp|{{Rarity|Rare}} {{PAGENAME}}.
File:Electric web epic.webp|{{Rarity|Epic}} {{PAGENAME}}.
File:Electric web legendary.webp|{{Rarity|Legendary}} {{PAGENAME}}.
File:Electric web mythic.webp|{{Rarity|Mythic}} {{PAGENAME}}.
File:Electric web ultra.webp|{{Rarity|Ultra}} {{PAGENAME}}.
File:Electric web super.webp|{{Rarity|Super}} {{PAGENAME}}.
File:Electric web unique.webp|{{Rarity|Unique}} {{PAGENAME}}.
File:Basic.png|{{PAGENAME}} yet.
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*Electric Web used to stack with itself when it was first released. This was patched shortly after.
*Electric Webs spawned by Mecha Spiders used to deal no lightning damage when first released, but it was fixed shortly after.
*M28 spawned {{Rarity|Unique}} Mecha Spiders which despawned due to closing servers shortly after. They were spawned back a few hours later.
*Electric Web used to only have a blue tint.
*Unlike Web, Electric Web's first rarity is {{Rarity|Rare}}.
*Electric Web's slowing down effect is not as effective as normal Web, which is likely because it also provides AoE damage.
*Electric Web is abbreviated as "Eweb", which can be confusing as {{Rarity|Epic}} Web is also abbreviated like that.
*The first {{Rarity|Super}} Electric Web was crafted by Utilisateur on September 26, 2025.
*This petal addition actually makes Mecha Spider worth killing, and also more dangerous than ever. That's because Electric Web trails they leave can deal immense damage if you are not prepared well.

'''Ellne''' was an area in the [[Jungle#Ellne Jungle|Ellne Jungle]] scenario.

==Overview==
When a player enters Ellne, they will default to respawning in Ellne instead of [[Centralia]] or [[Centralia Fields/South|Centralia fields 1]] until they make it back to Centralia itself, where players will respawn in the latter area. In short, Ellne mainly acts as a spawn point where players can sit back and relax without needing to worry.

The middle of Ellne is a jungle hut with a yellow-brown floor with a bamboo basket texture. One can enter or exit the jungle hut through the top and bottom entrances. The top entrance would lead to Ellne Jungle 1.[[File:Target dummy ultra bones.png|thumb|A player attacking a Target Dummy in Ellne]]Also, there are 8 [[Target Dummy|Target Dummies]] in Ellne, each one spaced out equally in a semicircle-like pattern. The super target dummy is on the right, and the rarities decrease as we move clockwise, so players can test the DPS of their builds here.

==Location==
{{PAGENAME}} was located in the bottom right region of the world map. The zone is 7 zones from [[Centralia]] spawn location. Assuming no interruptions, it takes around ?? minutes and ?? seconds to reach this zone, without using [[Bubble|Bubbles]] or [[Powder]].

===Connections===
The area connects to 1 area.
{| class="article-table"
|+Connections
!Area
!Connection Location
|-
| Ellne Jungle 1 (Southeast)
|Top Right
|}

==Mob Spawns==
Most mobs that spawn in Ellne are {{Rarity|C}}, {{Rarity|UN}} or {{Rarity|R}}. {{Rarity|E}} mobs also spawn uncommonly. There is a small chance for {{Rarity|L}} mobs to spawn. Along with that, all rarities possible spawn as Target Dummies.

A total of 10 mobs spawn in {{PAGENAME}}.
{| class="article-table"
|+Mobs
!Mob
!Spawn Rate

|Medium

|-
|Termite Overmind
| Very low, only spawns with Termite Mounds
|-
|Digger
| Extremely low, only spawns with Termite Mounds
|}
==Special Areas==
This area was the main spawnpoint for Jungle area.

==Trivia==
*In 26th December 2023, M28 accidentally spawned 1,000 ultra termite eggs in there.
*{{Rarity|R}}+ [[Termite Mound]]s can spawn rarely, which means [[Digger]]s can spawn, but the chance of a Digger's spawning is nearly impossible, especially at higher rarities such as {{Rarity|M}}.
*This is the only zone where [[Target Dummy|Target Dummies]] can spawn.
* This is a good place to farm for low-rarity cotton,as you don’t have to worry about mobs.
* Only two other spawn points exist, being [[Centralia]] and [[Centralia Fields/South|Centralia Feilds 1]], because Ellne Jungle is so far from Centralia.
*Many people have [[Dark Mark|Marks]] here, which means that this is a good place to get into [[Hel]].
*The [[Target Dummy|Target Dummies]] are impossible to kill, having 40,000 health that regenerates every second.
*No mobs can spawn inside the hut.
*This area was removed on April 12, 2024 update along with other Ellne Jungle areas and replaced with [[Jungle]]. Despite its removal, the part of the hut is still present on the current Jungle area, along with [[Target Dummy|Target Dummies]].

==Gallery==
<gallery>
Digger and target dummy.png|A [[Digger]] next to a Target Dummy in Ellne
EllneHutInside.png|The appearance of the inside of the Ellne Hut.
</gallery>

'''Ellne Pass''' was a desert/jungle area in florr.io that was released few days after the Jungle update, which was in May 1st 2023. It was the area that was first passed through before reaching other Jungle areas, if you were reaching Ellne Jungle by Centralia Fields 4.

==Appearance==
The Ellne Pass has a sandy floor, along with the dirt walls that uses, Despite having the name "Ellne" in it, it may be related to Desert zone because of the sand, or the Jungle zone, or Ocean. The shape of the map resembles the "Y" shape.

==Location==
Ellne Pass was located in the desert-jungle region of the map. This place is 3 zones away from [[Centralia]]. Assuming no interruptions, it takes 2 minutes and 5 seconds to reach, without using [[Bubble|Bubbles]] or [[Powder]].

===Connections===
Ellne Pass connects to 2 areas, The above portal leads to Centraila Fields/Southeast, while the bottom portal leads to Ellne Jungle 3.

==Spawning==
The average rarity in Ellne Pass is Common. Mobs typically spawn from Common to Unusual.

A total of 2 mobs spawn in Ellne Pass.
{| class="fandom-table"
|+Mobs
!'''Mob'''
!'''Spawn Rate'''
|-
|[[Crab]]
|High
|-
|[[Square]]
|Very Low
|}

=== Ultra/Super Mobs ===
{{Rarity|7}}/{{Rarity|8}} Square used to spawn rarely in this area.

==Strategy==
*If the player wants to go to Ellne Jungle quickly, this area would be the shortcut for it.

== Gallery ==
<gallery>
File:Ellne Pass.png|Ellen pass 
File:Ladybug (Desert).png|Light Ladybug Sometimes spawns! Does this reminds of Yggdrasil?
File:Crab.png|I hate Crabs! But they spawn here
File:Desert.png|Believe it or not! But it is in desert! The only place in desert which spawns Crabs.
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*When this zone was released, M28 decided to make its knowledge known by starting a hide and seek event. After he was found, he spawned a super crab on top of the winner, attracting many players to this zone.
*This was currently the quickest and safest way to go from Centralia to the Ellne Jungle, since the Leech Zone was very deadly.
*On May 21st 2023, a screenshot was posted of an Ultra Shiny Ladybug in this zone, but it is not confirmed whether they spawn here.
*M28 used to spawn squares in the area. On 12th December 2023, M28 spawned a {{Rarity|Super}} square that appears to be a shiny variant that's hostile to players, while on 18th December 2023, M28 spawned the passive one, and on 25th December 2023, M28 spawned the {{Rarity|Ultra}} square.
*This area was removed on April 12, 2024 update along with other Ellne Jungle areas and replaced with [[Jungle]].

The map appears to look like flipped version of East Waters 6.

Ellne Jungle 4 was located in the center region of the world map. The zone is 4 zones from [[Centralia]] and 3 zones away from [[Ellne]], the spawn locations. Assuming no interruptions, it takes around 4 minutes and 5 seconds (?) to reach this zone, without using [[Bubble|Bubbles]] or [[Powder]].

*This area was removed on April 12, 2024 update along with other Ellne Jungle areas and replaced with [[Jungle]].

title: Factory
content:
'''Factory''' is a dungeon in [[Florr.io]].
{{Background|图片=Factoryb.png|大小=2000px}}
{{Area Template Infobox
 |mobs = * [[Mecha Flower]]
* [[Spider/Mecha|Mecha Spider]]
* [[Wasp/Mecha|Mecha Wasp]]
* [[Crab/Mecha|Mecha Crab]]
* [[Barrel|Barrel]]
 |connections = [[Garden]]
 |distance_from_spawn = 1
 |special_entity = [[Assembler]]
 |name = Factory
 |image = Updated factory-Dec21,2025.png
}}

== Location ==
Factory is located in the middle right of [[Garden]].

===Connections===
The area connects to 1 area, as shown on the [[World Map]].
{| class="article-table"
!Area
!Connection Location
|-
|[[Garden]]
|Middle Right
|}

==Mobs==
A total of 6 mobs spawn in Factory. All of them are capable of dropping Fragments.

'''[[Mecha Flower]]:''' A mob that uses [[Cogwheel|Cogwheels]] as petals and keep a proper distance to players to attack players, similar to [[Gambler]]s. Mecha Flowers drop [[Battery]], [[Cogwheel]], [[Fragment]], and [[Mimic]] ({{R|Ultra}}+ only).

'''[[Spider/Mecha|Mecha Spider]]:''' A mob that is a variant of [[Spider]], but instead of dealing body and poison damage, it deals lightning body damage. Mecha Spiders drop [[Battery]], [[Fragment]], ({{R|Mythic}}+ only), [[Electric Web]], and [[Goggles|Paranormal Goggles]]. Mecha Spiders don't discharge Batteries.

'''[[Wasp/Mecha|Mecha Wasp]]:''' A mob that is a variant of [[Wasp]] that shoots [[Mecha Missile]]s, which directly track the player's position and must be destroyed until the Wasp gets distracted from the player or it is killed. Mecha Missiles also change its target to another one if the Wasp aggro on something else. Mecha Wasps drop [[Battery]], [[Fragment]], [[Antennae/Mecha|Mecha Antennae]] ({{R|Rare}}+) and [[Missile/Mecha|Mecha Missile]] ({{R|Mythic}}+).

'''[[Crab/Mecha|Mecha Crab]]:''' A mob that is a variant of [[Crab]] that attacks players using a mounted [[Laser]] on its shell. It doesn't ram the player. Mecha Crabs drop [[Battery]], [[Fragment]], [[Magnet]], and [[Laser]]. Its laser does not discharge Batteries.

'''[[Barrel]]:''' Barrels are stationary mobs that release poison damage in an AoE effect when attacked. Barrels drop [[Uranium]] ({{R|Rare}}+) and [[Fragment]] ({{R|Mythic}}+).

'''[[Assembler]]:''' Assemblers are scattered around the map, one in each section. They allow you to convert certain petals into other petals. There is always only one Assembler in each Factory chamber. A new Assembler spawns as soon as an Assembler despawns. 

==Strategy==
''See also: [[Builds - Factory]]''

*This area is good for [[Mecha Missile]] as long as you have good enough petals to beat {{Rarity|Mythic|text=Mythic+}} [[Wasp/Mecha|Mecha Wasp]].
*[[Plank]] or even [[Jelly]] are recommended while fighting [[Wasp/Mecha|Mecha Wasp]] and even [[Mecha Flower]].
*[[Pincer]], [[Jelly]] and even [[Heavy]] can be used to stop [[Spider/Mecha|Mecha Spider]] from ramming you.
*[[Rubber]] or good healing petals are recommended to fight [[Crab/Mecha|Mecha Crab]] or [[Spider/Mecha|Mecha Spider]] (if you ram it).
*[[Lotus]] should be used to absorb most or all of [[Barrel]] poison.
*Take advantage of [[Assembler]] blocking mobs way to kill mobs more easily or escape them. They are also can used to block {{R|Super}}+ [[Mecha Flower]] and [[Spider/Mecha|Mecha Spider]], prevent them from damaging you. Be aware of Mecha Flowers's [[Cog]], which it is using.
*A [[Triangle]] build can be used to pick off any {{R|Mythic}}- mobs. However, it is also recommended that you use [[Pincer]] for fighting {{R|Ultra}} [[Spider/Mecha|Mecha Spiders]] and [[Mecha Flower|Mecha Flowers]], [[Rubber]] for fighting {{R|Ultra}} [[Crab/Mecha|Mecha Crabs]], and [[Plank]] for Fighting [[Wasp/Mecha|Mecha Wasps]].
*[[Rubber]] and [[Bandage]] can be used to lure mobs away from the shortcut to the {{R|Ultra}} zone so others can enter.

==Tiles==
<gallery>
File:factory_entrance.svg
File:factory_exit.svg
File:factory_floor_c_0.svg
File:factory_floor_c_1.svg
File:factory_floor_c_2.svg
File:factory_floor_c_3.svg
File:factory_wall_c_0.svg
File:factory_wall_l_0.svg
File:factory_wall_tl_0.svg
File:factory_wall_tri_0.svg
</gallery>

==Gallery==
<gallery>
File:PlayerInFactory.png|A player in Factory
File:PlayerInFactory2.png|Another player in Factory.
File:Factoryraritysomehowrhymes 2335.jpeg|Full detailed rarity map of Factory (Old).
File:IMG 2347.webp|Factory’s previous location in the mini spiral (top right of [[Garden]], where the Battle Ground was going to be)
File:Updated factory-Dec21,2025.png|Updated Factory map. Where is a maze of maps in the center.
File:Factory with zones.png|The previous map with the difficulty zones.
</gallery>

==Trivia==
* All mobs in Factory only spawn in Factory, except a few mentioned below, like how all mobs in [[Hel]] only spawn in Hel.
* [[Battle Ground]] was briefly planned to serve as the '''Factory''' area, but was eventually replaced with '''Factory'''. The map no longer exists in the game files.
* After a few weeks of release, guildmates on the same guild no longer in the same team. They will join either left team or right team and this lasts for 1 day and it will be changed on the next day.
* Mecha mobs can spawn outside Factory, near the Factory entrance within 1 minute interval. They are hostile against mobs in [[Garden]]. These mobs have rarities ranging from {{R|Common}}-{{R|Epic}}.
* Despite having 2 spawns for each team, the connection to [[Garden]] is still the same.
* Ever since the December 13, 2025 update, the centre part of Factory became a shortcut and {{R|4}} mobs no longer spawn commonly in Factory.
* The left spawn now was set to be the player spawn, and there are no more player teams.

'''Faster''' is a legacy [[petal]] in [https://florr.io florr.io] added on the February 7th, 2020 update. It makes the user's other petals spin faster, hence the name. Faster is dropped by [[Leech]]es and [[Spider]]s.

==Appearance==
Faster looks nearly identical to [[Light]], except that it is slightly more yellow: It is a small, yellowish-white circle with a yellowish-gray outline.

==Function==

![[Rarity]]

Faster increases the rotation speed of your other petals. Multiple Fasters further increase it, which means it stacks with itself. Faster also appears to be "twitching", never staying still on its orbit but making strange movements.

==Obtainment==
===[[Leech]]===
{{:Leech/Drop Faster}}
===[[Spider]]===
{{:Spider/Drop Faster}}

===Purchasing===
On certain days, Faster can be purchased in the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|2}}

==Obtaining==
*[[Spider|Spiders]] drop Fasters at an average rate but are harder to kill due to their speed and, at higher rarities, webs. Bring [[Jelly|Jellies]] and [[Pincer]]s to slow them down. If necessary, a [[Lotus]] helps negate their Poison damage.
*[[Leech|Leeches]] are a secondary method of obtaining Fasters. Although they are easier to defeat with the use of [[Lightning]] and [[Bone]], their spawn frequency makes farming them less efficient than Spiders.

== Strategies ==

* Fasters are useful with fast reloading petals such as [[Sand]], [[Coral]] and [[Light]] since it reduces the amount of time the petals rotate around the flower. 
** Wheel of Death (WOD) builds require specific numbers of Fasters for optimal rotation. With {{Rarity|Super}} [[Talents/Reload|Reload talent]], a [[Sand]]/[[Coral]] WOD requires 2 {{Rarity|Ultra}} Fasters, while a [[Light]] WOD requires 4 {{Rarity|Ultra}} Fasters. [[Bur]] is required when using any WOD for a significant DPS increase.
***When paired with {{Rarity|Mythic}}+ [[Talents/Reach|Reach talents]] and/or [[Third Eye]], use the Q/E controls to slightly reduce your petal rotation speed in order to avoid over-rotation caused by increased flyout time<ref>Flyout time refers to the time it takes for petals to travel from the flower to the petal orbit, which is proportional to the distance between the flower and the target.</ref>.
***Coral WOD operates at a slower rotation speed than Sand WOD as Coral takes a few in-game ticks to break apart.
* Fasters don't work as effectively with slow reload petals such as [[Heavy]] and [[Stinger]] due to potential over-rotation, causing a loss of DPS.
* Using an {{Rarity|Ultra}}+ Faster in any build that mainly uses base reload petals<ref>Petals with a reload time of 2.5 seconds.</ref> with {{Rarity|Super}} Reload talent can be a replacement for the [[Talents/Petal Rotation|Petal Rotation talent]], saving up to 15 TPs (Talent Points).

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">
File:Faster (Common).png|{{Rarity|1}} Faster.
File:Faster (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|2}} Faster.
File:Faster (Rare).png|{{Rarity|3}} Faster.
File:Faster (Epic).png|{{Rarity|4}} Faster.
File:Faster (Legendary).png|{{Rarity|5}} Faster.
File:Faster (Mythic).png|{{Rarity|6}} Faster.
File:Faster (Ultra).png|{{Rarity|7}} Faster.
File:Faster (Super).png|{{Rarity|8}} Faster.
File:Faster (Unique).png|{{Rarity|9}} Faster.
File:Faster.png|Faster.
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*Faster, [[Basic]], [[Heavy]] and [[Light]] are petals named after adjectives, rather than substantives. They are the only petals to have that characteristic, and all four are from legacy florr.io.
**In legacy florr.io, the same petals have that same characteristic: [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Basic Basic], [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Fast Fast] (renamed to Light), Faster, [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Heaviest Heaviest] (renamed to Heavy) and [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Heavy Heavy] (which is technically removed if you don't count the new Heavy).
*Faster and Heavy are the only petals to alter the rotation speed of your petals. Faster speeds it, while Heavy slows it. [[Moon Rock]] has a totally different mechanic, but still revolves around the flower slowly.
*Faster was inspired from the old Fast, and its description technically predicted the Fast renaming to Light.
*Eight legacy [[Yin Yang]] petals, known as the Taijitu (太極圖) [[Builds|Build]], are the only other petal that can increase rotation speed. Using 8 Yin Yangs boosts rotation speed to about 15.6 radians a second. Compared to the legacy default 3 radians per second, this is a boost of about 12.6 (4π) radians per second. Now, using 1 {{Rarity|Unique}} [[Mimic]], 1 {{Rarity|Unique}} Faster and 8 {{Rarity|Super}} Fasters boosts rotation speed by 19.4 radians per second, giving a total of 23.4 radians per second and granting the fastest rotation speed in the game with the {{Rarity|Legendary}} Petal Rotation Talent unlocked.
*The {{Rarity|Mythic}} variant consisted of three Fasters in early [[Waves]]. It was then changed to two Fasters and later a [[Basic]]-sized Faster with the same stats as a {{Rarity|Mythic}} Basic. Currently, {{Rarity|Mythic}}+ Faster no longer has any visual changes.
*Before the January 29th update on legacy florr.io, it had the second shortest respawn in game together with Fast (0.8 seconds), whereas the first was [[Rice]] with 0.04 seconds (although current Rice has a reload of 0.1 seconds).
*On legacy florr.io, Fasters used to drop at {{Rarity|Rare}} Rarity by [[Queen Ant]]s, [[Soldier Ant]]s and [[Spider|Spiders]] before waves florr.io was added and made it only droppable by [[Leech]]es and Spiders.
*Faster and [[Talents/Petal Rotation|Petal Rotation Talent]] can greatly increase the DPS of most builds, especially WOD builds.
*Using full Fasters makes your petals spin weirdly, and makes them stay close to you. However, a cool mechanic is that if you get close enough to a mob or any target, the Fasters will swing towards the mob when on the side that the target is on from your flower, and it's pretty fun to use. Pairing with [[Third Eye]] makes them reach even further.

}}
'''Fire Ant Burrow''' is a mob that was added along the Desert scenario. it will spawn a lot of [[Soldier Fire Ant|Soldier Fire Ants]] when players are near it, and shrinking in size. Then it will despawn when all the spawned [[Soldier Fire Ant|Soldier Fire Ants]] are defeated. [[Digger]] can spawn when all the ants are released. It drops [[Magnet]] and [[Disc]] upon despawn.

==Appearance==
A simple 3 Circles with a brighter red on the outside, darker red in the second circle, and the darkest in the middle circle. It is smaller than a regular Ant Hole.
==Stats & Behavior==
The XP Reward will be given when all mobs that come out of the Fire Ant Burrow are destroyed.

|+Mob Stats
![[Rarity]]

When a player goes near a Common or Unusual Burrow, it spawns around 30 Soldier Fire Ants, then is destroyed.

Rare Fire Ant Burrows will have 8-13 Rare Soldier Fire Ants, 14-17 Unusual Soldier Fire Ants and 0-2 Common Soldier Fire Ants. (confirmation needed)
==Drops==
NOTE: The Fire Ant Burrow will drop these when all of the Soldier Fire Ants that come out of it are destroyed.

===[[Magnet]]===
{{:Fire Ant Burrow/Drop Magnet}}

===[[Disc]]===
{{:Fire Ant Burrow/Drop Disc}}

==Strategy==

* If you're farming in Desert and you're surrounded by mobs, do not try to come close near the mob as this can decrease the chances of survival, depending on what rarity. If you have stronger petals and are close to a high rarity Fire Ant Burrow, this isn't a problem as if the mobs that spawn have higher rarities you will probably die from the ants. 
* Only pop a burrow if you don't have tons of mobs chasing you. These burrows can be very dangerous if you're forced near one and a lot of people die due to this. Use [[Bubble|Bubbles]] to keep away from these if you're being chased.

=== Super Fire Ant Burrow ===

* In Desert, the main danger when fighting the {{Rarity|8}} Fire Ant Burrow is not the {{Rarity|8}} Fire Ant Burrow itself, but the many {{Rarity|7}} mobs plus the {{Rarity|8}} Soldier Fire Ant that spawn around it. When bubbling to the {{Rarity|8}} Soldier Fire Ant, it is advised to use [[Sponge]] and multiple {{Rarity|7}} healing petals. It is also advised to use Sponge when actually fighting the Soldier Fire Ants, so you don't die if anything hits you.
* If the mob spawns deep in Normal Ant Hell, it is advised to form a "megasquad", where a large group of players stay near each other and clear mobs. This increases the chance for everyone to survive.
* People mostly just try to pop the Fire Ant Burrow before anything. After, they mostly just care about the Super that it spawns, not the ultras. It is often lured to easier areas. It is advised to use high DPS petals to kill the Soldier Fire Ants. But along with a few healing petals in case a Scorpion kills you. 
* If you kill the {{Rarity|8}} Soldier Fire Ant or the {{Rarity|7}} mobs and die, it is advised to not respawn since your damage counted to the {{Rarity|8}} Fire Ant Burrow would reset.
* If the Fire Ant Burrow is deep, the hardest part is not actually killing the mob, but reaching the loot. If not reached, the loot would despawn. This is a very common problem with {{Rarity|8}} Fire Ant Burrows. If there is no way of reaching, you could try to use Rubber and Bandage with [[Talents/Magnetism|Magnetism Talent]] and pray for you to bounce towards the loot. Note that this way is the last thing you want to do to reach the loot.

</gallery>

==Trivia== 
*This is one of the only mobs with no hitbox so it can't be damaged. The others are [[Trader]], [[Titan]], [[Termite Mound]] and [[Oracle]].
* This, [[Ant Hole]] and [[Termite Mound]] are the only mobs that can spawn [[Digger|Diggers]].
* This mob used to have a high chance of spawning in old South Desert 4 map.
* Fire Ant Burrow used to not spawn in old South Desert 5 map until {{Rarity|Ultra}} mobs update went live.
* {{Rarity|Rare}}+ Fire Ant Burrows used to be able to be entered. The inside looks like like any other Ant Hole with many Soldier Fire Ants inside and a small chance to spawn a [[Digger]], but now they are the same as {{Rarity|Common}} and {{Rarity|Unusual}}s.
*Although {{Rarity|Ultra}}/{{Rarity|Super}} Fire Ant Burrows don't spawn a [[Queen Fire Ant|Qu]]<nowiki/>[[Queen Fire Ant|een Fire Ant]], they spawn one {{Rarity|Ultra}}/{{Rarity|Super}} [[Soldier Fire Ant]].
**This likely to make {{R|7}} and {{R|8}} Fire Ant Burrows doesn't be overpowered.
*The Fire Ant Burrows will be smaller when the Fi<nowiki/>re Ants comes out.
*{{Rarity|Common}} and {{Rarity|Unusual}} Fire Ant Burrow used to drop nothing u<nowiki/>ntil [[Disc]] got added to the game.

==Appearance==
Fire Ant Egg is a slight red recolor of the [[Ant Egg (Mob)|Ant Egg mob]].

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed: '''Stationary.

'''Behavior:''' Much like the normal variant, it is passive and has a chance of spawning an aggressive [[Baby Fire Ant]] or [[Soldier Fire Ant]].

==Spawning==
The {{PAGENAME}} spawns in [[Ant Hell]]. They only spawn in the center of each area in Fire Ant Hell, and from [[Queen Fire Ant|Queen Fire Ants]].

==Drops==
===[[Egg (Ant)]]===
{{:Fire Ant Egg/Drop Egg}}

==Strategy==
Fire Ant Eggs are passive, immobile mobs that pose no threat. However, they are found only in the center zones in Fire [[Ant Hell]]. There are often many players in these areas, so it is often difficult to obtain the drops.
*Use [[Stick|Sticks]], [[Beetle Egg|Beetle Eggs]] or [[Ant Egg|Ant Eggs]] to fill up these special zones with friendly mobs. Note that this will only work well if no one else is using summoned mobs.
*{{R|6}}+ [[Missile|Missiles]] can be used to destroy Fire Ant Eggs from a distance.
**[[Peas]] or [[Grapes]] are decent substitutes if you do not have many Mythic+ Missiles.
*Sometimes the eggs will spawn an aggro [[Baby Fire Ant]], beware when afk.
*[[Bone|Bones]] of the same rarity can kill Fire Ant Eggs without losing health. (Example: {{Rarity|Mythic}} Bone can kill a {{Rarity|Mythic}} Fire Ant Egg.)
*During early game, these areas are one of the best ways to get many eggs within a short amount of time.
*Ramming these eggs is a good idea, because they have very low body damage and will not do much damage to you while you kill them.

*On April 2nd 2023, M28 spawned a {{R|8}} Fire Ant Egg in the EU server of Ant Hell 1.

The '''Firefly''' is a mob in florr.io that spawns in [[Jungle]]. When the firefly hits a player, it discharges lightning. 
==Appearance==
The front of the mob is almost identical to that of the [[Fly]], whilst its back, composed out of a glowing yellow circle, with a line.
==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' It is slower than the Fly, but moves in the same way.

'''Behavior:''' Neutral, chases the flowers when hit, can be outran.

Their back glows, as well as they are to emit lightning, that chains upon flowers.

==Spawning==
The firefly spawns in [[Jungle]]. It can spawn anywhere in Jungle except for the beach part on top of the map. Higher rarities can be found in the right side of Jungle.
==Drops==
===[[Wing]]===
{{:Firefly/Drop Wing}}

=== '''[[Bulb]]''' ===
{{:Firefly/Drop Bulb}}

=== [[Battery]]===
{{:Firefly/Drop Battery}}

==Strategy==
It doesn't pose a threat, because it has low health and it's slower than the flower. It is advised to not ram it, if you don't want to risk to get exposed to the effect similar to the battery. With higher rarities, summons are extremely dangerous to use because of all of the lightning you'll have to endure. To get around this, use a lot of high rarity [[Ant Egg|Ant Eggs]] so you have enough summons not to be included in the Lightning bounce. Or, [[Rubber]] also works.

<gallery>

==Trivia == 
*In real life, male Fireflies use their glowing back to attract females. This could explain, why they aggro on you when you use a [[bulb]], which is very similar in terms of the effect on the back of the firefly.
*Fireflies are the replacement of moths in terms of dropping [[battery]] (before they were added, the only mob to drop battery were [[Moth|moths]], and before the Moth, it was the [[Leech]]). 
* Firefly was planned to be released on the [[Jungle]] Update but was delayed for months.
* The Firefly's description is a reference to the advancement's name for distracting a Piglin with gold in Minecraft.
* Firefly's ability to electrocute enemies might be from the misconception that Fireflies have actual electric bulbs inside.

'''Magic Firefly,''' shortened in-game as just '''Firefly''' is a mob in florr.io, a rare variant of [[Firefly]] that spawns in random intervals in the place of regular Fireflies. They drop [[Mana Orb]] and [[Battery]].

==Appearance==
Magic Fireflies are a cyan recolored version of [[Firefly]] and they emit a blue glow instead of a yellow one.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Yes, slower than the Flower.

'''Behavior:''' Neutral, chases flowers sinusoidally when hit.

==Spawning==
Magic Fireflies spawn in waves, with each wave lasting 3 minutes. During the wave, magic fireflies spawn everywhere except the top left of Jungle (near ocean portal). After a wave ends, another wave will appear randomly within the next three hours. This sometimes makes it harder to get for newer players with lesser time on their hands.   

==Drops==
===[[Mana Orb]]===
{{:Magic Firefly/Drop Mana Orb}}

===[[Battery]]===
{{:Magic Firefly/Drop Battery}}

==Strategy==
Magic Fireflies don't pose a major threat, because they have low health and are slower than the flower. Do not ram the Magic Firefly, as ramming causes it to discharge large amounts of lightning damage. With higher rarities, summons are extremely dangerous to use against all Firefly variants.

All Magic Firefly rarities spawn with intervals of randomized minutes in between, so it is advised not to wait for the next wave straight after the last. Additionally, Magic Fireflies can only spawn if the mob density is low enough: this means that if there are too many mobs, higher rarity Magic Fireflies cannot spawn.

The best strategy to farm them is to use [[Bubble]] and [[Uranium]] around the Firefly zone which can usually guarantee {{Rarity|Rare}} and {{Rarity|Epic}} [[Mana Orb|Mana Orbs]]. For more experienced players, Bubble around the Mythic zone with petals that multihit like corn, do high damage like claw, or with a good squad.

Magic Fireflies are often greeded. To get around this, go to areas players don't normally visit, such as the right edge of the right side of [[Jungle]] or the [[Wasp]] zone. Doing this should find you a few more than you otherwise would normally find.

</gallery>

==Trivia== 
* Most people refer to it as Shiny Firefly or Blue Firefly.
* Magic Firefly used to spawn intervals of 79 minutes as waves.
* Farming Orb from Magic Fireflies has been quite inefficient due to Garden Ladybug ability to drop Orb by equipping Orb.
* Unlike the normal variant, Magic Fireflies do not have a special {{Rarity|Super}} spawn message.
** This allows players to distinguish a {{Rarity|Super}} Magic Firefly from a normal {{Rarity|Super}} Firefly.

The '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is a mob in florr.io. It is an aggressive mob that moves very fast, even faster than [[Spider]] and has the ability to evade damage.

==Appearance==
The Fly looks like a larger body of [[Baby Ant]] (without the pincers) with 2 smaller wings. 
==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Mid-Fast until sees a flower. When a flower gets in range, it moves at the same speed (and in the same pattern) as an aggressive [[Bee]]. They can evade hits. 

'''Behavior:''' Aggressive

==Spawning==
The Fly spawns in Sewers. The {{PAGENAME}} also spawns from [[Garbage]], which also spawns in [[Sewers]].

==Drops==
===[[Poo]]===
{{ID 6 U}}

===[[Wing]]===
{{ID 6}}

===[[Talisman of Evasion]]===
{{:Fly/Drop Talisman}}

==Strategy==

*{{Rarity|1}}-{{Rarity|5}} Flies are most likely easy to kill, you can use some [[Missile|Missiles]] or some good damage [[petals]] to handle them.
*Use high-reload low-damage petals like [[Rice]] or [[Light]] or even better, poisonous petals such as [[Iris]] instead of high-damage low-reload petals like [[Stinger]].
*{{Rarity|Mythic}} Flies are pretty strong and must be treated with [[Heavy|heavies]] or {{Rarity|Ultra}} [[Bone|bones]].
*Since Flies team with [[Spider|Spiders]], you may need Anti-[[Spider]] strategy.
**This strategy is by killing many Flies as possible to prevent them from helping Spiders, and avoiding death by touching Spiders.
**If the Anti-Spider strategy fails because of [[Roach]] crashing into you, try not to attack the roach.
*When being chased, stick to the walls as you flee in a straight line. Flies move in a wavy pattern and will bump into the wall, which slows them down to manageable speeds. Don't actively press yourself against the wall, as that will slow you down.
** This wall trick alone is not enough to make Flies slower than your speed (without Powder), so use repelling petals like [[Jelly]] and [[Heavy]], or slowing petals like [[Pincer]]s and [[Web]]s.
*Since Flies can evade hits, you need to pray your attacks hit or you’ll have an even harder time getting rid of them. It is not recommended ramming them, as they can evade collisions, too. Lightning and Poison damage can bypass fly evasion.
*Flies can be annoying when using [[Mysterious Stick|Sticks]], because before the ally [[Sandstorm|Sandstorms]] spawn the Flies can destroy your sticks and can end up killing you. It’s best to use something to prevent them from destroying the [[Mysterious Stick|Sticks]] such as [[Jelly]], [[Pincer]] or even [[Honey]]. 
*If you are mainly using summons, it's best to use [[Ant Egg|Ant Eggs]] as more ants can hit the fly and, therefore, increase chance of actually hitting the Fly. 
*When using a ramming build against Flies, [[Talents|Body Toxicity]] is absolutely essential as the poison will bypass Flies' evasion. But don't ram straight at the Fly, instead constantly move away from it to maximize poison damage and minimize damage taken to the flower, though this is only really useful in Mythic or less dense Ultra [[Sewers]] tunnels where mob density isn't nearly as overwhelming as the box ({{Rarity|Ultra}}/{{Rarity|Super}} zone).
*Flies have low health but high body damage and only take 10% of the hits you give them, effectively decupling their maximum health. However, ramming builds with Body Toxicity work well and petals such as [[Pincer]], [[Iris]], [[Lightning]], and [[Battery]] can take them down quickly and efficiently. Alternatively, a deathwheel or a [[Rice]] build will take them out eventually.
*A Battery ram build including Battery and Totem is recommended to farm Flies.
*Magic Eyes are good for looting Super Flies

<gallery>

</gallery>

==Trivia==
*On May 8, 2024 update, [[Wing]] drop rates were temporarily removed from the Fly, but it was added back ever since.
*The Fly is the first new [[Mobs|mob]] in the [[Sewers]] area.
*Despite looking somewhat like a [[Soldier Ant]] and having the same body damage, the Fly is not an Ant mob and does not spawn in [[Ant Hole|Ant Holes]], rather being another species.
*Flies are the second mobs to have the lowest health, with the first being [[Bubble]].
*The {{Rarity|8}} Fly spawn message is now "A {{Rarity|8}} Fly has spawned (somewhere)!", but it used to be "A buzzing noise echoes through the sewer tunnels".
**This is the only {{Rarity|8}} spawn message that doesn't end with an ellipsis symbol.
*As part of an update that occurred on December 21, 2024, the HP of the Fly was reduced by 80%, while its evasion stat was increased from 50% to 90%.
*Its recent stats are same as Hornet/Wasp missile stats, but with armor.
*Flies have an ability to dodge your petals to not take damage from them with a 90% chance. This is called their Evasion ability.
**It could be a reference that Flies move fast in real life, and has about 6,000 ommatidia on each eye.
**Fly, [[Talisman of Evasion]] and [[Poker Chip]] all have Evasion stats, with the Fly evasion applying to mob itself, Talisman's to the user and Poker Chip's to the petal itself. The Fly and Poker Chip has the same evasion stat (90%), target effect and is always active unlike Talisman.
*Flies used to spawn naturally directly in [[Sewers]] but now on the November 9, 2025 update, they only spawn from [[Garbage]], making them the only mob that can't spawn naturally.

The '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is a somewhat rare mob that spawns in [[Hel]].

==Appearance==
The Gambler appears as a dark red flower with huge eyes, likely from too much gambling in such a dark place, without knowledge of combinatorics and the Central Limit Theorem.

== Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Normal.

'''Behavior:''' Hostile to any entity it sees, whether other mobs or players. Gamblers will even attack each other. It uses [[Card|Cards]], [[Poker Chip|Chips]], [[Dice]], [[Domino]] and a [[Clover]] to attack, much like a petal. It will try to remain at the right distance from the enemy for its petals to hit it, rather than ramming. {{Rarity|Common}} Gamblers use 5 petals, and each higher rarity has an extra petal used, maxing at 10 with the {{Rarity|Mythic}} Gambler.

|}Sometimes, the Gambler says something random while exploring the map. Its chat bubble is green, like the [[Trader]]. The Gambler has around 10 identified lines and this list currently shows all of confirmed lines that they say:

*Thirty-seventh time's the charm
*I LOVE GAMBLING
*Where are the slots?
*Where are the blackjack tables?
*Winner winner!
*I'm feeling lucky
*99% of gamblers quit before they win big
*I'M ALL IN
*Double or nothing?
*Just one more game...
{{Rarity|Super}}+ Gamblers have ability to corrupt nearby flowers. Corrupted player will appear in the same design as Gamblers. For the most part, this doesn't affect anything, since PvP is already enabled for [[Hel]]. However, any [[Beetle Egg|Beetle Eggs]] will spawn [[Hel Beetle|Hel Beetles]] instead of normal [[Beetle|Beetles]].

When Corrupted, you will get the same flower status as when you have [[Corruption]] equipped: a dark gray skull, which is identical to Corruption symbol, in a red circle with darker outline.

==Spawning==
The {{PAGENAME}} infrequently spawns in [[Hel]].
==Drops==
===[[Poker Chip]]===
{{:Gambler/Drop Poker Chip}}

===[[Card]]===
{{:Gambler/Drop Card}}

===[[Dice]]===
{{:Gambler/Drop Dice}}

===[[Domino]]===
{{:Gambler/Drop Domino}}

===[[Corruption]]===
{{:Gambler/Drop Corruption}}

==Strategy==
*Gamblers have higher drop rates than other mobs in Hel, alongside with their XP reward.
*You can use the [[Hel]] mobs to your advantage, by luring them to the Gambler. But be careful as this can backfire on you.
*You can also use Gamblers to kill other Hel mobs, such as [[Hel Beetle|Hel Beetles]] and [[Hel Spider|Hel Spiders]].
*Gamblers are one of the best ways to get Hel petals (excluding [[Dark Mark]]) due to its high drop rates. You should prioritize killing these over other players and mobs if its loot is worth it. However, the Gambler's high health and a presence of other players also trying to get loot makes it dangerous.
*Gamblers are valuable, but the higher-rarity ones spawn in high-rarity zones, and then they get destroyed by Hel mobs. Unfortunately now, you need to deal enough damage as if multiple people are looting the Gambler, even when the only things attacking it are you and the Hel mobs.

==={{Rarity|8}} Gambler===
*{{Rarity|8}} Gamblers can be dangerous due to the petals it uses to attack, so it is recommended to refer the strategies above.
**Do not try to come close to a Gambler, since that will easily overwhelm you by using its petals. 
**Since PvP is enabled in Hel, players can attack and kill you for their high drops than most mobs in Hel.

==Trivia==
* Gamblers are the only mobs to spawn in Hel that do not drop Mark.
* Gamblers are not allies with each other, so you can find a fight between themselves. This is identical to Corruption users, as they still can damage each other despite both corrupted. Now it's very hard to see these fights as Hel mobs will likely to overwhelm the Gamblers at first.
* After Domino petal was added, it replaced the {{Rarity|Mythic}} Chip (purple) position in Gambler gallery.
* After the Domino and Corruption petal addition, this became the first mob to ever have 5 petal drops in the game since Legacy.
** Gamblers are the only mobs to have 5 petal drops.
** It's also the first and only mob which has 4 different drops (magic petals don't count).
* Somewhere in March 2025 update, a change was made that Gamblers had lower XP gain and drop rates. However, the [[Corruption]] drop rate remained unchanged.
** Back then, many people came to high rarity Gamblers to obtain Ultras and XP.
* When Gamblers were released, people could kill it to get the ''Devil's Advocate'' achievement as they were used to include for this list. However, it was no longer possible to obtain by Gamblers as of mid-2024.
* Before the 29th May 2024 update, the Gambler was a [[:Category:Special Entities|Special Entity]] that did not attack but wander around the map and say its speeches.
* Gamblers and [[Mecha Flower]] are one of the only mobs in the game that has the ability to use petals the same way that a flower does.
* Its description references the [[Corruption]] petal as you can become this mob with it.
** Gamblers are the currently only mobs that you can actually become.
** Corruption implies that Gamblers were flowers before their souls have been corrupted beyond repair (implied by their lock-on ability) by gambling. It also implies the existence of skulls, at least for flowers and/or Gamblers. The existence of skulls can further be proved by [[Bone]].
*** {{Rarity|Unique}} Corruption implies that the corruption is contagious, possibly meaning that everything in [[Hel]] may be under the influence of Corruption.
* Gamblers are one of two mobs that isn't a Special Entity to have speech lines and the other being [[Target Dummy]].
* Despite having Clover, Gambler do not drop [[Clover]]. Clover is only used to buff Dice's crit damage chance, and also Clover itself don't do much damage. Other petals is also RNG-based like Dice, but somehow don't get its chance buffed.
* Gamblers used to have the same health as original Soldier Termite's health (608k at {{Rarity|Mythic}}). Now it's reduced to the same health as old Leech's health (425k at {{Rarity|Mythic}}).
* Other mobs in Hel now no longer aggro on Gamblers, unless they attack them.
* M28 once spawned a Unique Gambler in Ocean trenches area.
* Gamblers have the most gallery picture versions, which is 4 as of November 30th, 2025.
**In the first version no petals surround the Gambler.
**In The second version five petals (being [[Card]] ,[[Dice]], [[Clover]] and two [[Chip]]s) surround it.
**In the third version a [[Chip]] was replaced by a [[Domino]].
**In the fourth version the Gambler is in a attacking state (has an angry face) rather than the previous normal state (smiling).

'''Garden''' is the original area in florr.io. It has a green background and features primarily mobs from only legacy florr.io, although there are still mobs that are not from legacy florr.io such as [[Bumble Bee|Bumble Bees]] and [[Dandelion (Mob)|Dandelions]].
==Locations==
After players leave [[Training Grounds]], Garden will be the default spawn location for them. The spawn is located in the center of the map, above the [[Sewers]] portal.

===Connections===
Garden connects to 8 areas, as shown on the [[World Map]].
{| class="article-table"
!Area
!Connection Location
|-
|Sewers
|Center
|-

|Bottom center

|Desert
|Bottom center

|Right center
|}

==Mobs==
A total of 14 mobs spawn in Garden.

'''[[Ant Hole]]''': Static mob that spawns Ants ([[Baby Ant|Baby]], [[Worker Ant|Worker]], [[Soldier Ant|Soldier]], and [[Queen Ant|Queen]]) when hit. Drops [[Soil]] and [[Shovel]].

'''[[Baby Ant]]''': Passive mob that wanders around, spawns both naturally and from [[Ant Hole]]s. Drops [[Light]], [[Rice]] and [[Leaf]].

'''[[Bee]]''': Passive mob that wanders around, dealing high contact damage when touched, {{R|Rare}}+ becomes aggressive when damaged. Drops [[Stinger]], [[Pollen]] and [[Honey]].

'''[[Bumble Bee]]''': A rare passive mob that wanders around quickly while trailing pollen, drops [[Pollen]], [[Honey]] and [[Wax]].

'''[[Centipede]]''': Passive mobs that moves slowly with multiple segments, each counting as an individual Centipede. The frontmost segment will always be a head. {{R|Epic}}+ becomes aggressive when damaged. Drops [[Peas]] and [[Leaf]].

'''[[Dandelion (Mob)|Dandelion]]''': A static mob which shoots Dandelion missiles in all directions upon taking damage. Drops [[Dandelion]].

'''[[Digger]]''': A friendly mob that has a 10% chance of spawning after any kind of [[Ant Hole]] is defeated. Can attack hostile mobs, and cannot harm players and other friendly mobs (such as Eggs) unless players uses [[Corruption]]. Drops [[Cutter]] and [[Uranium]]. Those who did sufficient damage to the ant hole will get loot from the Digger.

'''[[Hornet]]''': Aggressive Mob that wanders around, until it detects a [[Flower]], in which case it starts shooting [[Missile]]s at the Flower (with a slight recoil) while keeping its distance. {{R|Rare}}-  Hornets simply shoot at the Flower's current location, but {{R|Epic}}+ Hornets shoot at where the player will be upon collision, necessitating a change in direction. Drops [[Missile]], [[Antennae]], and [[Orange]].

'''[[Ladybug]]''': Passive Mob that wanders around. {{R|Rare}}+ become aggressive when damaged. Drops [[Rose]] and [[Light]].

'''[[Queen Ant]]''': Aggressive Mob that will follow a Flower until it gets out of range, in which case it wanders around its Ant Hole. Drops [[Ant Egg]] and [[Basil]].

'''[[Rock (Mob)|Rock]]''': Static Mob. Drops [[Rock]], [[Heavy]] and [[Moon Rock]].

'''[[Soldier Ant]]''': Aggressive Mob that wanders around until it detects a Flower, in which case it starts following it. Can spawn both naturally and from Ant Holes. If it spawns by the latter reason, it can not go far away from its Ant Hole. Drops [[Glass]] and [[Clover]].

'''[[Spider]]''': Aggressive Mob that wanders around until it detects a [[Flower]], in which case it runs towards the Flower at a high speed. Drops [[Web]], [[Faster]] and [[Third Eye]].

'''[[Worker Ant]]''': Neutral Mob that wanders around, becoming aggressive when damaged. Can spawn both naturally and from Ant Holes. Drops [[Leaf]] and [[Corn]].

'''[[Mecha Flower]]:''' A mob that uses [[Cogwheel|Cogwheels]] as petals and keep a proper distance to players to attack players, similar to [[Gambler|Gamblers]]. Drops [[Battery]], [[Cogwheel]] and [[Mimic]] ({{R|Ultra}} only).

'''[[Spider/Mecha|Mecha Spider]]:''' A mob that is a variant of [[Spider]], but instead of dealing body and poison damage, it deals lightning body damage. Drops [[Battery]] and [[Fragment]] (Mythic+ only)

'''[[Wasp/Mecha|Mecha Wasp]]:''' A mob that is a variant of [[Wasp]] that shoots [[Mecha Missile|Mecha Missiles]], which directly track the player's position and must be destroyed until the Wasp unaggro on the player or it is killed. Mecha Missiles also change its target to another one if the Wasp aggro on something else. Drops [[Battery]], [[Antennae/Mecha|Mecha Antennae]] ({{R|Rare}}+) and [[Missile/Mecha|Mecha Missile]] ({{R|Mythic}}+).

'''[[Crab/Mecha|Mecha Crab]]:''' A mob that is a variant of [[Crab]] that attacks players using a mounted [[Laser]] on its shell. It doesn't ram the player. Drops [[Battery]] and [[Laser]].

===Special Zones===
The bottom right circular room is an area where {{R|Legendary}} [[Bee|Bees]] spawn frequently.

The top right circular room is an area where {{R|Legendary}} and {{R|Mythic}} [[Ladybug|Ladybugs]] spawn frequently.

The bottom left wall corridor is an area where {{R|Legendary}} [[Hornet|Hornets]] and [[Dandelion (Mob)|Dandelions]] spawn frequently.

The long corridor from bottom left to top left is a {{R|Mythic}} zone.

The top left spiral is an {{R|Ultra}} zone. It is one of the most difficult zones in the entire world map.

The top middle contains the [[Factory]], which spawns [[Mecha Flower|Mecha Mobs]].
==Strategies==
''See also: [[Builds - Garden]]''

== Trivia ==
*All mobs that are in Garden were in Legacy Florr.io except for [[Bumble Bee]], [[Dandelion (Mob)|Dandelion]] and [[Digger]].
*[[Spider]] spawning was removed in an unknown date but was re-added back in April 12, 2024.
*Before the February 21, 2025 update, a portal that leads to Battle Ground was there at the top right corner of the map.
*This is the second area to have the most mobs spawning at 14, only being beaten by [[Ant Hell]] with 16 unique mobs.
*The [[Factory]] entrance used to be in the mini spiral before opening and being moved to the {{R|Epic}} area.
*On December 25, 2024, the Garden map was changed for a week due to M28 throwbacks. As it was a temporary map, there were no checkpoints.
**The path to Ant Hell used to be located at the top right of the map.
**There was a secret part in the temporary Garden map, and was impossible to enter, due to walls blocking players path (you can use Carrots or your speed decreases to know the walls). It was near to Ladybug zone entrance.
***This secret part might've been only accessed by players being teleported by M28.
***This zone had been confirmed to be a new area, which was the old [[Battle Ground]] (now Factory).
**It was then reverted back to the old map on January 2, 2025.
*On December 15, 2025, Garden received a map update which added a tunnel connecting the spawn and the upper-right corner, and it was quickly turned into [[Shortcuts|shortcut]] in the following day. Players are unable to use petals there.
**There are also a few places with shortcuts that lead to nowhere, which are referred to as "resting points" by M28.

title: Glass
content:
{{Petal Template Infobox |name = Glass |image = File:Glass (Common).png|C File:Glass (Unusual).png|U File:Glass (Rare).png|R File:Glass (Epic).png|E File:Glass (Legendary).png|L File:Glass (Mythic).png|M File:Glass (Ultra).png|Ul File:Glass (Super).png|S File:Glass (Unique).png|Uq |first-rarity = {{Rarity|Common}} |description = Phases through enemies. Cannot damage enemies more often than a certain interval. |damage = {{Petal Scale|22}} |interval = 1s |reload-time = 2.5s |stackability = Stackable |dropped-by = *[[Sandstorm]] *[[Soldier Ant]] |special = Cannot be damaged or killed by other enemies.
Cannot damage an individual enemy for 1 second after it deals damage to it. }} '''Glass''' is a [[petal]] in [https://florr.io florr.io]. It phases through mobs upon collision and cannot damage the same mob more often than once per second. Glass is dropped by [[Sandstorm]]s and [[Soldier Ant]]s. ==Appearance== As its name implies, Glass is a shard of a glass. While in-game, it can morph into many different shapes: it can be assumed that the game draws 5 different points, then joins them and fills in the outline with a transparent white color. Its petal icon displays one of its various forms: an irregular pentagonal shape. There might be an infinite amount of Glass shapes. ==Function== {|class="article-table" |+Petal Stats ![[Rarity]] !Obtainable? !Damage !Respawn !Interval |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |rowspan="10"|Yes |22 |rowspan="10"|2.5 seconds |rowspan="10"|1 second |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |66 |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |198 |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |594 |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |1,782 |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |5,346 |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |16,038 |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |48,114 |- |{{Rarity|Eternal}} |rowspan="2"|144,342 |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} |} Glass lacks a health attribute, thus being invincible, and it passes through any and all enemies. However, in return, if it hits an enemy, it can't damage the same enemy again for 1 second . ==Obtainment== ===[[Sandstorm]]=== {{:Sandstorm/Drop Glass}} ===[[Soldier Ant]]=== {{:Soldier Ant/Drop Glass}} ===Purchasing=== On certain days, Glass can be purchased in the [[Shop]]. ''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]'' {{Petal Price|1}} ==Obtaining== *Defeat [[Soldier Ant]]s and [[Sandstorm]]s to obtain Glass. [[Ant Hell|Normal Ant Hell]] has the highest spawn rate of Soldier Ant, thus making it the most efficient place to farm this petal. For players that are unable to get to Ant Hell yet, try damaging [[Ant Hole]]s and killing the Soldier Ants they spawn. ==Strategy== *Glass is an outstanding petal in early game. **It being indestructible eliminates the need for the [[Talents/Reload|Reload talent]] to optimize its damage, allowing players to invest in the more important talents as soon as possible, such as [[Talents/Loadout|Loadout]] and [[Talents/Reach|Reach]]. **It phases through mobs and therefore can damage multiple mobs at once, which is particularly useful against mob swarms. However, unlike most other petals, it cannot push mobs away via physical collision. **It can be obtained with ease and in large amounts in Ant Hell. *Glass still keeps its utility beyond early game, especially in [[Garden]] and [[Jungle]] where it can weaken projectiles (by damaging them). When paired with [[Third Eye]] and/or the Reach talent, Glass can extend even further, enabling it to potentially hit more mobs. **In [[Ocean]], it is a cheap alternative to [[Lightning]] against [[Leech]]es as they are multi-segmented. **Glass can be [[Assembler|assembled]] into [[Sand]], which is a decent DPS petal for {{Rarity|Super}} hunting. ==Gallery== File:Glass (Common).png|{{R|1}} Glass. File:Glass (Unusual).png|{{R|2}} Glass. File:Glass (Rare).png|{{R|3}} Glass. File:Glass (Epic).png|{{R|4}} Glass. File:Glass (Legendary).png|{{R|5}} Glass. File:Glass (Mythic).png|{{R|6}} Glass. File:Glass (Ultra).png|{{R|7}} Glass. File:Glass (Super).png|{{R|8}} Glass. File:Glass (Unique).png|{{R|9}} Glass. File:Glass.png|Glass. ==Trivia== *Glass is the first indestructible petal that is non-wearable. It's also the only petal that can't damage enemies for a certain amount of time besides a cooldown timer. *Glass is not to be confused with glass petals: in video-game terminology, something that is glass (also referred to as glass cannons for itself or its users) has very low durability but deals immense damage (specially compared to their durability), referencing how real life objects made of glass can shatter easily, but can cause lots of very harmful cuts on you. In florr.io, there are some examples of glass petals, like [[Stinger]] and its [[Blood Stinger|Blood counterpart]]. Glass is rather a literal shard of glass whose ability to phase through mobs references a real life glass shard's cutting capacity. *This petal was added unmentioned in the June 20th, 2024 update, along with [[Privet Berry]]. *Glass is the only petal that deals damage but cannot take damage, in complete contrast with petals that take damage but cannot deal damage such as [[Bubble]], [[Yggdrasil]] and [[Wax]]. *This is one of the only petals to change its appearance, since it spawns as a random shape. **The only other petals to do so are [[Poker Chip|Chip]], [[Card]], [[Domino]], [[Dice]] and [[Rock]]. *There is an uncountable amount of Glass shard formations. The one found in the petal image is just one of them. **When Live icons setting is enabled, the different formations will be shown on the petal icon. {{Petals}} [[zh:玻璃]] [[Category:Petals]] [[Category:Common+]] [[Category:Desert]] [[Category:Ant Hole]] [[Category:Ant Hell]] [[Category:Desert]] [[Category:Garden]] [[Category:Droppable Petals]] [[Category:Stackable Petals]] [[Category:Ants]] [[Category:Assembling]] [[Category:Soldier Ants]] [[Category:Sandstorm]]

:''This page is about Golden Leaf. For the regular and [[Magic Petals|Magic]] variants, see [[Leaf]] and [[Leaf/Magic|Magic Leaf]] respectively.''

'''Golden Leaf''' is a [[petal]] in [https://florr.io florr.io] added on 17th February 2025. Its function consists of reducing reload time of all petals. Golden Leaf is dropped by [[Bush]]es and [[Golden Leafbug]]s.

==Appearance==
Golden Leaf looks like Leaf, but is recolored to look (as the name suggests) golden.

==Function==

Golden Leaf's special ability is reducing the reload time of all petals in the loadout, which stacks multiplicatively. For example, using two {{Rarity|Ultra}} Golden Leaves reduces petal reload by 1 - (1 - 17.5%)<sup>2</sup> ≈ 31.9%. 

==Obtainment==
''Note: When [[Mana Orb]] is equipped, Golden Leaf is unobtainable and it will be replaced with [[Leaf/Magic]].''

=== [[Bush]] ===
{{:Bush/Drop Golden Leaf}}

=== [[Golden Leafbug]] ===
{{:Golden Leafbug/Drop Golden Leaf}}

===Purchasing===


''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|10|Ultra}}

==Strategy==
*Golden Leaf can notably increase a build's DPS output if used properly.
**For rotation builds that mainly consist of base reload petals, use one Golden Leaf.
**For shotgun builds that involve [[Yin Yang]] and quick-reload petals such as [[Rice]] and [[Privet]] (which is typically paired with [[Iris]]), use one Golden Leaf in place of a Rice and Privet respectively. More Golden Leaves can be used if the build consists of multiple high rarity, quick-reload petals that are worth optimizing over using other petals.
**Burst damage petals (like [[Blood Stinger|Blood Stingers]] and [[Stinger|Stingers]]) and petals with long reload can greatly benefit from using a few Golden Leaves.
**For {{Rarity|Super}} summoner builds (except [[Mysterious Stick|Stick]] and [[Mysterious Stick/Magic|Magic Stick]]), use multiple Golden Leaves.
*Other than damage petals, Golden Leaf can also improve the efficiency of utility petals, especially [[Bubble|Bubbles]].

==Trivia==
*This petal is not obtainable when equipping [[Mana Orb]], as it is replaced with [[Leaf/Magic|Magic Leaf]]. It can be seen in Bush's drops.
*This petal shares drop rates with [[Moon Rock]], making it one of the rarest petals in the game.
*It has the same price as [[Moon Rock]].
*Just like Leaf and its magic variant, Golden Leaf has balanced stats.
*This petal has nerfed reload attribute stat in an unknown update.

The '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is a mob in florr.io. It is a rare variant of the Leafbug.

==Appearance==
The '''{{PAGENAME}}''' looks like a regular [[Leafbug]], with its colors switched to be golden.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Slow, medium when provoked.

'''Behavior:''' Neutral, attacks the player if provoked.

==Spawning==
The {{PAGENAME}} rarely spawns in [[Jungle]].

==Drops==
===[[Golden Leaf]]===
{{:Golden Leafbug/Drop Golden Leaf}}

=== [[Golden Leaf|Magic Leaf]] ===
{{ID 6 U}}

=== [[Root]] ===
{{ID 1}}

==Strategy==
*This one has an insanely large hitbox. This factor, combined with its [[Beetle]]-like damage and a high flat damage reduction make this mob dangerous to provoke. The best way to deal with it would be by using [[Sponge]] to tank its damage and using Lightning and Poison damages to bypass its armor.
*Using [[Egg (Disambiguation)|Eggs]] on the Golden Leafbug will usually fail to deal damage (If [[Ant Egg|Ant Eggs]] spawning ants of the same rarity as the leafbug, or [[Beetle Egg|Beetle Eggs]] spawning [[Beetle|Beetles]] of one rarity lower than the leafbug). [[Mysterious Stick|Sticks]] spawn [[Sandstorm|Sandstorms]] with high body damage and could be considered using.
*Ram builds are not as effective against this mob as they are against many others, this is because of the Golden Leafbug's insane armor and high body damage (triple the average) If you do ram, make sure to use the [[Talents/Body Toxicity|Body Toxicity Talent]] so that your ramming will also deal Poison damage, which bypasses Armor. Even with this, ramming is highly not recommended
*Because of its high armor, using petals that deal much damage is almost required to deal damage to the mob.
*Bur can decrease its Armor, though its armor debuff is barely noticeable because of the Golden Leafbug's insane armor.
*Its Armor is useless against poison and lightning, meaning that petals such as [[Iris]] and [[Lightning]] will always damage it regardless of the Armor. [[Dice|Dice's]] critical hit will also bypass the Armor if its rarity is high enough.
*[[Salt]] can damage it, as its body damage is always higher than the Armor. But due to the fact that Salt don't stack anymore, this strategy was overall useless.
*Do not use a [[Mana Orb|Orb]] when fighting an {{R|7}}+ Golden Leafbug, as the [[Golden Leaf]] will be replaced with a [[Magic Leaf]].

title: Heavy
content:
{{Petal Template Infobox | image = File:Heavy (Rare).png|R File:Heavy (Epic).png|E File:Heavy (Legendary).png|L File:Heavy (Mythic).png|M File:Heavy (Ultra).png|Ul File:Heavy (Super).png|S File:Heavy (Unique).png|Uq | description = This thing is so heavy that nothing gets in the way.
Slows down petal rotation speed when equipped. | first-rarity= {{Rarity|Rare}} | damage = {{Petal Scale|9|lowest-rarity=Rare}} | health = {{Petal Scale|50|lowest-rarity=Rare}} | reload-time = 10.0s | stackability = Stackable | dropped-by = *[[Rock (Mob)|Rock]] | special = High cooldown time.
High density.
High durability.
High knockback.
Reduces the petal orbit speed of the flower. }} '''Heavy''' is a legacy [[petal]] from [https://florr.io/ florr.io]. It has a high durability stat but also carries the curse of a long respawn timer. The Heavy is dropped by [[Rock (Mob)|Rock]]s. ==Appearance== The Heavy looks like a black billiard ball, but without a number on it. It also appears to be made of a metallic element (like Lead due to its color). About its shape, it's a large, dark gray circle with an even darker outline. It has a white spot in the upper right section of it, indicating that it's shining. ==Function== {|class="article-table" |+Petal Stats ![[Rarity]] !Obtainable? !Damage !Health !Respawn |- | {{Rarity|Common}} |rowspan="2"|No |9 |50 |rowspan="9"|10 seconds |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |27 |150 |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |rowspan="7"|Yes |81 |450 |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |243 |1,350 |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |729 |4,050 |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |2,187 |12,150 |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |6,561 |36,450 |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |19,683 |109,350 |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} |59,049 |328,050 |} Heavy's main gimmick is its long cooldown timer due to its massive health and moderate damage. Its secondary gimmick is reducing the rotation speed of the flower's petals. Its third gimmick is going less far or less close than other petals when the flower is on attack/defense state. Finally, Heavy resists being pushed around by other things (not suffering as much knockback as other petals), while having a great knockback itself due to multi-hitting. ==Obtainment== ===[[Rock (Mob)|Rock]]=== {{:Rock (Mob)/Drop Heavy}} ===Purchasing=== On certain days, Heavy can be purchased in the [[Shop]]. ''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]'' {{Petal Price|4}} ==Strategy== *Heavy's huge health makes it useful as a shield, pushing other things away from the user and their petals. Since it also deals good damage against other things (higher than a [[Corn]]), it can be treated as a hybrid petal used for both attack and defense strategies. Weak petals such as the [[Iris]] or [[Stinger]] can be placed behind a Heavy to protect them. Heavy's damage is decent, but its long respawn makes it kind of unreliable. *Due to its damage and health stats, Heavy (specially at higher rarities) end up being better than [[Jelly]] in a lot of scenarios. *Heavy is a good contender for trolling people, as such, it's advised to stay aware of any Heavy users trying to push mobs onto you. Such activities are toxic and not encouraged here. *While its long respawn does hurt it, upgrading your Reload [[Talents|Talent]] can reduce it by a lot, making Heavy more viable on PvE. *This can be used as a slightly more aggressive [[Plank]] on PvE, so similar strategies apply. {{Zone|Garden}} *Unlike other petals, Heavies are unlikely to die to a barrage of [[Hornet]] missiles. They can shield or divert their projectiles so you don't have to worry about getting killed by them. Heavies matching the Hornet's rarity are preferred for this, though, and be sure to bring supporting petals in case it ends up failing. *This can easily destroy [[Ant Hole]]s, [[Centipede]]s and [[Dandelion (Mob)|Dandelions]] of lower rarities and can prove helpful for the same rarity of them, specially due to each mob's special ability (summoning Ants, multiple segments and shielding [[Dandelion]]s). [[Spider]]s can also be kept at bay since the Heavy's high health gives it the multi-hitting effect, letting it push anything on its hitbox every time it hurts a mob. *Do not rely fully on Heavies to kill other mobs. Instead, bring something like [[Rock]] if you want a more viable tank petal, or support the Heavy accordingly. ===Obtaining=== *They're slightly rarer than [[Rock]]s but way more common than [[Moon Rock]]s. You'll be able to get Heavies pretty frequently. *Defeating [[Rock (Mob)|Rock mobs]] isn't that hard at all, unless it's a {{Rarity|Super}} Rock, in which case you may want to dodge the smaller Rocks, bring in [[Disc]] or [[Relic]] or burst petals like the [[Stinger]] to avoid/deal with them. **Killing Super Rocks is beneficial due to the smaller Rocks it spawn, thus increasing the frequency of Rock and Heavy petals dropped. {{Zone|Desert}} *Garden strategies should apply similarly here, except that the mobs are slightly different. Heavy also helps killing a [[Cactus (Mob)|Cactus]] easier. {{Zone|Ocean}} *Heavy can help push and slowly damage [[Crab]]s, [[Leech]]es and [[Shell (Mob)|Shells]], as they will most likely kill you when they reach too close to your location. *This isn't as useful against [[Jellyfish]] because their ranged attack is not something physical, but rather, electrical discharges (countered with [[Rubber]]). Still, this can deal enough damage to kill them. {{Zone|Ant Hell}} *This can help you push back Ants/Termites that are too close to you to avoid getting cornered or ambushed by all sides. This can also help you deal moderately good damage against the Termites' Psyonic Connection as well. {{Zone|Hel}} ===Using=== *Iris and Stinger are more useful in PvP than anywhere else, so Heavy does have its uses to protect these petals. It's best used to kill a [[Light]]/Deathwheel by nullifying their shield. *Heavy can be used to inflict knockback on an advancing player, allowing the user to escape comfortably. *Using multiple of these petals creates an effective shield against any attack, but be aware that the long respawn time may cause giant holes in your defense. *Due to its knockback by multi-hitting and high HP, Heavies will do good in killing any Hel mob, but specially killing [[Hel Wasp]] projectiles. *Sometimes, its knockback may detriment PvP interactions as it may push the opponent away from your attacking radius. Also, Heavy naturally has a low attacking radius, so using [[Bubble]]s or [[Third Eye]] to have a better position might help it. ===Against=== *Heavy's weakness is its long respawn timer. Even though it may be mitigated with the Reload Talent tree, it still is a pretty reliable place to attack once it dies. *Try to use ranged petals to deal with this without getting harmed by the Heavy. *Use [[Faster]]s, Lights, and/or [[Sand]] petals to quickly wear out this petal and open a weak spot in the Heavy user's defenses. **As an alternative, use [[Plank]] to wear out this even quicker, or [[Salt]] to reflect any damage the Heavy deals to you. {{Zone|Jungle}} *This petal is good, not only for shredding [[Ladybug (Jungle)|Dark Ladybugs]], [[Centipede (Jungle)|Evil Centipedes]][[Firefly|Fireflies]] and [[Termite Mound]]s and the inside Termites, but also for deflecting [[Mantis]] and [[Wasp]] projectiles with ease, although the Wasps' missiles will be harder to deflect because of their zigzag movement. *This can also be used to deal a good chunk of damage to [[Leafbug]]s without worrying about its Armor (though using [[Bur]] can still be beneficial). {{Zone|Sewers}} *[[Fly|Flies]], [[Roach]]es and Spiders can be deflected with ease without worrying about dying. However, Roaches are still a lot dangerous even with Heavy, so you're better off using [[Jelly]] or Rubber. *This isn't as good against [[Moth]]s as Heavies may push Moths away from your attack radius, thus letting them escape. ==Gallery== File:Heavy (Common).png|{{rarity|1}} Heavy. File:Heavy (Unusual).png|{{rarity|2}} Heavy. File:Heavy (Rare).png|{{rarity|3}} Heavy. File:Heavy (Epic).png|{{rarity|4}} Heavy. File:Heavy (Legendary).png|{{rarity|5}} Heavy. File:Heavy (Mythic).png|{{rarity|6}} Heavy. File:Heavy (Ultra).png|{{rarity|7}} Heavy. File:Heavy (Super).png|{{rarity|8}} Heavy. File:Heavy (Unique).png|{{rarity|9}} Heavy. File:Heavy.png|Heavy. ==Trivia== *The Heavy has the third highest durability of any petal in the game, and is tied with [[Corn]] and the [[Stinger]] for second highest constant respawn time. All of these are legacy petals (although this petal has a different name there). The longest constant respawn timer goes to [[Moon Rock]] and [[Square]] at 60 seconds each. **A constant respawn timer is one that doesn't change between any rarity. [[Ankh]] at Common rarity has the longest respawn timer out of anything, at 256 seconds, while [[Ant Egg]]s and [[Beetle Egg]]s can reach a cooldown beyond 60 seconds at {{Rarity|Super}} rarity. However, petals like those have variable respawn timers: Ankh's cooldown decreases with each rarity increase, while the Eggs' cooldown increase. Meanwhile, Corn, Heavy and Stinger all stay at 10 seconds through every rarity, and Moon Rock and Square stay at 60 seconds. Since they don't change on rarity, it's a constant number. **On legacy florr, between February 20th, 2020 (date of its addition to legacy florr) until November 2nd, 2020 (date of Corn's addition to legacy florr), it had the highest durability and the longest respawn timer. *Heavy, [[Faster]] and [[Light]] are petals named after adjectives, rather than substantives. They are the only petals to have that characteristic, and all three are from legacy florr.io. **In legacy florr, the same petals have that same characteristic: [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Heaviest Heaviest] (renamed to Heavy), [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Fast Fast] (renamed to Light), Faster and [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Heavy Heavy] (which has ever since been removed). *Heavy and Faster are the only petals to mess with the speed of your petals' orbit. Faster speeds it, while Heavy slows it. Moon Rock has a totally different mechanic, but still revolves around the flower slowly. *This version of the Heavy is inspired on the '''Heaviest''', an {{Rarity|Epic}} rarity legacy florr with not very different properties from this (sharing the same appearance and first clause of the description, and being renamed during new florr.io). It used to be rarely dropped from [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Boulder Boulders] (a bigger and more durable Rock). However, a {{Rarity|Common}} legacy petal called '''Heavy''' also used to exist, and was erased after legacy florr was discontinued. It used to be dropped from both Rocks and Boulders. **On legacy florr.io, Heavy was a decent common petal with decently high HP and 4-5 respawn time (in seconds). It was usually prefered over the [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Heavy Basic] and the Fast (which is now called Light). *The first clause of this is directly from legacy florr, while the second clause is brand new. **Heavy's gimmick of reducing the petal rotation speed is a reference to how people believed that the old Heaviest used to do the same (albeit this wasn't the case until new florr.io). *Newb was the first player to obtain the {{Rarity|8}} Heavy. {{Petals}} [[zh:重]] [[Category:Petals]] [[Category:Rocks]] [[Category:Rare+]] [[Category:Garden]] [[Category:Stackable Petals]] [[Category:Droppable Petals]]

title: Hel
content:
{{Background|图片=Helb.png|大小=2000px}}
{{Area Template Infobox

 |mob_diversity = about 2.6
}}
'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an area in florr.io. It is the only area that spawns [[Hel Beetle|Hel Beetles]], [[Hel Spider|Hel Spiders]], [[Hel Centipede|Hel Centipedes]], [[Hel Wasp|Hel Wasps]] and [[Gambler|Gamblers]]. It is also the only place in the game where '''[[PvP]]''' is enabled (except in the old [[PvP]] and [[Factory]], but it's every flower for themselves here in the second case), unless someone is using [[Corruption]] outside Hel.

[[Crafting]] is disabled here, as the menu is replaced with [[Plinko]], where the Central Limit Theorem shows its true form. Players used to be able to [[Squads|Squad]] here, but an update at some point in late 2024 removed that ability. Players are completely on their own here, and [[Yggdrasil]] is useless.

==Appearance==
The background is similar to the sand texture, but tinted red. The walls are simply dirt. The map resembles an upside down star where in each sides has their own rarity of spawning mobs.

==Overview==
Hel cannot be accessed through portals. To enter Hel for the first time, you must die and be revived with a [[Dark Mark]].

The area has 5 special zones on each sides of the star shaped area, where each zones would spawn mobs at certain rarities.
*{{Rarity|1}} and {{Rarity|2}} mobs spawn in the center of the map.
*{{Rarity|3}} mobs spawn at the top-left side of the map.
*{{Rarity|4}} mobs spawn at the bottom side of the map.
*{{Rarity|5}} mobs spawn at the top-right side of the map.
*{{Rarity|6}} mobs spawn at the left side of the map.
*{{Rarity|7|text=Ultra+}} mobs spawn at the right side of the map.

The only mobs that spawn here are [[Hel Beetle]]s, [[Hel Spider]]s, [[Hel Centipede]]s, [[Hel Wasp]]s and [[Gambler]]s.

== Score ==
Score given now can be gained through mobs XP Reward. Killing another player gives you 50% of their score, while killing mobs give a predetermined amount of score.

== Strategy==
[[Hel Beetle|Hel Beetles]] have high speed, body damage, and HP, but what makes them deadly is that they can teleport to your position. This is telegraphed by a red pentagram appearing where the Beetle will spawn, so it is wise to use [[Bubble]] or [[Shovel]] in your builds to avoid them.

[[Hel Spider|Hel Spiders]] have decently high speed, though with high poison damage (double that of regular Spiders) and spawn frequently, It is recommended to use [[Pincer|Pincers]], because [[Web|Webs]] don't work on Spider variations. Beside having much health, damage and poison damage, strategies applied to normal Spiders also applied here.

[[Hel Centipede|Hel Centipedes]] behave much like [[Centipede (Jungle)|Jungle Centipedes]]. They are relatively easy to kill, but try not to be overwhelmed by other mobs or other segments. They seem to be quite fast.

[[Hel Wasp|Hel Wasps]] are arguably the most dangerous mob in Hel, as they have many missiles that are spread out like a shotgun, which makes them hard to attack without being pelted by Missiles. As you approach a Hel Wasp, it will start to recoil away as it attacks, which can make them hard to get to, and they don't turn around to shoot as their counterpart [[Wasp]], which makes them less predictable. It is recommended to use [[Plank]] to protect against projectiles. Going in a straight line at the Hel wasp when it attacks you also somewhat lessen your chances of getting hit by its projectiles for some reason.

[[Gambler|Gamblers]] have high health and body damage, and use [[Dice]], [[Card|Cards]], [[Clover]] and [[Poker Chip|Chips]]. They are the most targeted mobs in Hel because they have very good drop rates and a very high XP reward. Though that also makes looting a {{Rarity|Super|Super+}} Gambler extremely hard as fights are prolonged and players will kill each other by accident or deliberately to increase their chance of obtaining loot.

Global [[PvP]] is on, so beware of other players, although the vast majority can't get into an actual fight without dying to {{Rarity|Legendary|Legendary+}} Hel Mobs or other players. Only strong players with [[Sponge]] and {{Rarity|Super}} petals, or skilled players with {{Rarity|Ultra}} petals can survive for long here.

Sometimes, there aren’t many players (or even none at all) so one has to be careful, as the Hel variants mentioned can swarm you, very big {{Rarity|Mythic}}/{{Rarity|Ultra}} swarms in fact as many players won't clear the already present swarm which only accumulates over time and only gets noticed by {{R|Super}} hunters if the swarm becomes too much of a nuisance, even then it's not guaranteed that hunters will clear them as it's very hard even with collaborative effort.

Using [[Jelly]], it is possible to AFK in Hel due to Jelly's knockback function. [[Poo]] is also useful because it reduces mob aggro, increasing the range where you can move around without getting aggravated. But generally as Hel mobs are way stronger than their normal counterparts and players sometimes just kill you, you won't be able to AFK here for that long before dying, beside AFK here doesn't really means anything since the entire map is connected in a way that makes it very easy to traverse and it is really easy to get back to where you are after respawning. AFK farming here is also a terrible idea.

Once every 0 to 24 hours, a {{R|Super}} [[Blood Sacrifice]] spawns somewhere on the map, and stays until someone picks it up.

For builds suitable for this area, see [[Builds - Hel]].
