==Trivia==
*All mobs in Hel only spawn in Hel, so Hel is the only area where [[Poker Chip]], [[Card]], [[Dice]], [[Corruption]] and [[Dark Mark]] can be obtained, along with [[Basic]] from player.
*Hel mobs also have much higher health and damage stat compared to normal mob they're based off.
*Previously, [[Magnet]] did not work in Hel. It now functions as supposed to, but [[Basic]] and [[Blood Sacrifice]] is not affected by it, death petal attraction and [[Talents/Magnetism|Magnetism Talent]].
*This currently is the only area except [[Worm (Area)|Worm]], [[PvP]], and [[Termite Mound|Termite Mounds]] in the game without any portal connections to other areas or maps.
*This is also the only area other than [[PvP]] where full PvP is enabled.
**However, since PvP was removed, Hel is now the only area with full PvP enabled. 
**[[Factory]] separates players in two teams, enabling PvP against each other, but not against everybody.

*This area is likely based on gambling due to the Gamblers, [[Plinko]] and the petals that can be dropped here.
*[[Yggdrasil]] and spamming "YGG" doesn't work in Hel because since Hel squads were removed, people cannot revive you.
*The word "Hel" originates from [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Norse_mythology Norse mythology] where the world under the Yggdrasil tree is called Hel, same as their purpose.
*This area includes these drops: [[Basic]], [[Dark Mark|Mark]], [[Dice]], [[Third Eye]], [[Poker Chip]], [[Card]], [[Missile]] and [[Beetle Egg]].
*You can afk via using [[Jelly]] or [[Ant Egg|Eggs]] as it can avoid the mobs from being able to reach you, Though, when using just [[Jelly|Jelly,]] there is a risk of players killing you while you afk.
*On May 27, 2024, 2 new petals have been added, [[Poker Chip]] and [[Card]]. This update also made it possible to spawn in Hel without being Marked.
*On May 28, 2024, the map was changed to a star shape of [[Dark Mark]].
*Gamblers spawn in Hel, possibly a reference to "Gamblers belong in hell."
*Mark can revive you in Hel.
*Hel may be under the influence of [[Corruption]], due to the fact that it turns Flowers into Gamblers and [[Beetle|Beetles]] into Hel Beetles.
*One more Hel-exclusive petal was added: [[Blood Sacrifice]], and it spawns every 0-24 hours and it will not despawn till it gets picked up. This got added around 10/8/2025. The petal encourages players to go to Hel frequently.

title: Honey
content:
{{Petal Template Infobox | image = File:Honey (Rare).png|R File:Honey (Epic).png|E File:Honey (Legendary).png|L File:Honey (Mythic).png|M File:Honey (Ultra).png|Ul File:Honey (Super).png|S File:Honey (Unique).png|Uq | description = Mobs love this. Attracts mobs to this in a large radius.
Mobs will prioritize this over flowers.
Affected units develop immunity to its effects after some time. | first-rarity= {{Rarity|Rare}} | health = {{Petal Scale|50|lowest-rarity=Rare}} | max-mob-rarity = {{Infobox Colors Empty|||{{R|2}}|{{R|3}}|{{R|4}}|{{R|5}}|{{R|6}}|{{R|7}}|{{R|8}}}} | reload-time = 2.0s + 0.5s | stackability = Stackable | dropped-by = *[[Bee]] *[[Bumble Bee]] | special = Attracts mobs to its location when thrown.
Ability efficiency decays over time. }} The '''Honey''' is a legacy [[Petals|petal]] in florr.io added on March 4th, 2020. It can be thrown away from the flower, and once it sticks to the ground, it attracts mobs to itself. The Honey is dropped by [[Bee]]s and [[Bumble Bee]]s. ==Appearance== The Honey is a yellow rounded hexagon with a dark yellow outline. It resembles a honeycomb but it doesn't have any internal lines. ==Function== ''Note: The "A + B" respawn format means that the petal takes A seconds to respawn and B seconds after respawning to do its special action/have its special action enabled.'' {|class="article-table" |+Petal Stats ![[Rarity]] !Obtainable? !Health !Max Rarity Attraction !Respawn |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |rowspan="2"|No |50 |rowspan="2"|{{Rarity|Common}} |rowspan="9"|2.0s + 0.5s |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |rowspan="7"|Yes |450 |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |1350 |{{Rarity|Rare}} |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |4050 |{{Rarity|Epic}} |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |12150 |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |36450 |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |109350 |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} |328050 |{{Rarity|Super}} |} The Honey can be triggered by a left or a right click, which causes it to stick to the ground. When it enters that state, it will attract most mobs, but the more it attracts, the more immunity mobs develop to it. A Honey of one rarity always attracts a mob of the same rarity minus one. ==Obtainment== ===[[Bee]]=== {{:Bee/Drop Honey}} ===[[Bumble Bee]]=== {{:Bumble Bee/Drop Honey}} ===Purchasing=== On certain days, Honey can be purchased in the [[Shop]]. ''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]'' {{Petal Price|3}} ==Strategy== *The Honey draws mobs to itself, which is good for both luring mobs you need to kill and distracting other mobs so that they don't aggro on you and end up killing you. However, be sure to be able to counter the mobs or the Honey might die easily and the mobs will shred you anyways. *It's more viable than [[Bulb]] due to its property of attracting mobs away from your location, but less viable than [[Uranium]] because it doesn't damage them in the process (even though Uranium deals self damage). {{Zone|Garden}} *This can be used to lure [[Centipede]]s and [[Spider]]s away from you, or spawned Ants from an [[Ant Hole]] if you're attacking it from a distance. However, [[Hornet]]s can't be pulled by it, so take care. *A good strategy should be using [[Ant Egg]], [[Beetle Egg]] or [[Mysterious Stick|Stick]] near the location of the Honey, so all aggroed mobs will end up ramming the friendly summons. ===Obtaining=== *While [[Bee]]s are dangerous when you aggro them, [[Bumble Bee]]s have a quick and unpredictable movement which could kill you instantly if you don't take care. Just avoid getting on a Bee's zigzag, and you'll be good. Use [[Jelly]] if all else fails. {{Zone|Desert}} *Honey will temporarily turn [[Beetle]]s and [[Scorpion]]s away from you so you don't need to flee from them or dodge their stingers. [[Desert Centipede]]s will also be pulled and won't immediately turn to fight you. It can also help in finding [[Ladybug (Desert)|Shiny Ladybugs]] for a chance at getting [[Yggdrasil]]. *[[Sandstorm]]s are immune to this due to their random movements (and because they're not sentient beings). {{Zone|Ocean}} *Like Desert, Honey can be used to distract [[Crab]]s and [[Leech]]es so their special abilities won't bother you as much. *[[Jellyfish]] are immune to this, and their lightning discharges may still hit you. {{Zone|Ant Hell}} *Honey will die pretty quickly because this area has many Ant/Termite swarms, but it can still give you some distraction time against them. {{Zone|Hel}} ===Using=== *This has no effect on PvP, as flowers aren't bots, but rather, controlled by real humans. *This can be used to either distract the Hel mobs (whose special abilities are very dangerous, including [[Hel Beetle]]s) or create a shield of mobs against other players, but be sure to not die by said "shield". ===Against=== *This has no effect on you. Ignore it accordingly. *Try to avoid the mob shield that the Honey may generate, or deal with it if you can't avoid. {{Zone|Jungle}} *This can be used to distract [[Mantis]]es or [[Wasp]]s before their projectiles can hurt you severely, as well as attracting weak [[Evil Centipede]]s and [[Firefly|Fireflies]]. {{Zone|Sewers}} *It's not advised to bring Honey onto Sewers, because [[Roach]]es are too quick (despite being neutral) and could do serious harm to you. It still could help bringing [[Moth]]s to your location before they run away. ==Gallery== File:Honey (Common).png|{{rarity|1}} Honey. File:Honey (Unusual).png|{{rarity|2}} Honey. File:Honey (Rare).png|{{rarity|3}} Honey. File:Honey (Epic).png|{{rarity|4}} Honey. File:Honey (Legendary).png|{{rarity|5}} Honey. File:Honey (Mythic).png|{{rarity|6}} Honey. File:Honey (Ultra).png|{{rarity|7}} Honey. File:Honey (Super).png|{{rarity|8}} Honey. File:Honey (Unique).png|{{rarity|9}} Honey. File:Honey.png|Honey. File:Old Honey.png|Old Honey. File:Honey on floor.jpg|Honey on the floor. ==Trivia== *Like how [[Ant Egg|Eg]][[Beetle Egg|gs]] and a [[Stick]] generally summon a friendly mob of one rarity less, Honey attracts mobs of one rarity less mobs. *[[Sandstorm]]s not being able to be pulled by it might be because they aren't sentient beings. The same is valid for [[Bubble (Mob)|Bubbles]]. *Honey is the third petal to stick to the ground, with the first two being the [[Web]] (both new and [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Web Old Web] as well as the [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Web_(Legendary) old Legendary Web]) and the [[Pollen]], despite not having said ability on legacy florr. *The Honey petal was originally shaped as a circle (slightly larger than Pollen), but on March 6th, 2020 the developers turned it into a Hexagon, resembling a honeycomb. **This was likely to prevent confusion between it, [[Faster]] and Pollen, all of them looking pretty much the same on legacy florr.io. **The shape was changed again on 13/02/25, now having rounded corners instead of sharp ones. This was likely to avoid confusion with [[Wax]]. *Mobs will eventually develop immunity to Honey. This is the only petal that suffers from this. A few mobs also are completely immune to it, for example, [[Hornet]]. *Although it is from legacy florr, it and [[Uranium]] are tied for the first petal to aggravate all mobs. The [[Light Bulb]] also followed said mechanic. Honey is the only one out of these to not make passive or neutral mobs aggressive towards you, even if the Honey has been destroyed. *On legacy florr, [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Honey Honey] used to slow down everything it hit by 20% for 5 seconds, which included petal rotation speed. Its old description was “''This is so sticky it slows down whatever it hits.''" Abilities and description were changed to current Honey when florr.io rereleased. **Only it, Fasters and 8 [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Yin_Yang Yin Yangs] could mess with petal rotation speed, while only Honey debuffed (decreased) it. **Honey used to drop from [[Bee]]s and Hornets before it was changed to Bees and [[Bumble Bee]]s. *The petal continues to spin around itself even after it has been dropped somewhere, as if it's still orbiting the player. *AwStormy was the first person obtained a {{Rarity|8}} Honey with crafting. {{Petals}} [[zh:蜂蜜]] [[Category:Petals]] [[Category:Debuffs]] [[Category:Rare+]] [[Category:Garden]] [[Category:Droppable Petals]] [[Category:Stackable Petals]] [[Category:Assembling]] [[Category:Bee]] [[Category:Luring Petals]]

[[File:Super Hornet using Second Attack.png|thumb|289x289px|{{R|8}} Hornet]]
The '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an original mob in florr.io. They have the ability to shoot [[Missile|Missiles]] at the player. 

== Appearance==
The Hornet looks like an oval with black and yellow stripes and a dark yellow outline. It has two antennae at the front, which appear as slightly curved black lines. It also has a [[Missile]] at the back.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Slow when passive, Normal when aggressive.

'''Behavior:''' When firing at the player, hornets turn 180 degrees and fire the "missile" attached to their body.

{{Rarity|Epic|Epic+}} Hornets can predict your movement. {{Rarity|Legendary|Legendary+}} Hornets recieve a firing rate buff. {{R|Super}} Hornets have 2 additional special attacks. The first summons five {{Rarity|Ultra}} Hornets. To indicate this attack, the Hornet will shake its [[Antennae]]. The second makes the Hornet move in a large clockwise circle, shooting large amounts of {{Rarity|Ultra}} Hornet Missiles. To indicate this attack, the Hornet shakes its missile.

==Spawning==
The Hornet spawns exclusively in [[Garden]].

==Drops==

===[[Missile]]===
{{ID 6 U}}

===[[Orange]]===
{{ID 6 U}}

===[[Antennae]]===
{{:Hornet/Drop Antennae}}

==Strategy==  
*Since Hornets have more health than [[Bee|Bees]], you can try to use stingers to quickly take down against Hornets.
**DPS petals may be more reliable against swarms.
*Move in a circle around the Hornet to avoid its missile but don't get to close to it cause the hornets missile can hit you that way too when they turn around.
*If you find a swarm of Hornets (2 or more) move to one side and after most of the Hornets have fired, move in the opposite direction because if you have a particularly large swarm the hornets will likely block you off from circling them.
*Hornets are one of the most annoying mobs in the game and a serious hazard to deal with. Their ranged attack means they can hurt you while still being at a safe distance from your petals. Certain petals are better than others at dealing with Hornets, but generally, more range is better for Hornets.
*Ironically, the Hornet's weapon, the [[Missile]], is also one of the best counters, dealing high damage. Missiles {{Rarity|Rare}}+ will home in on nearby Hornets. Against swarms of Hornets, missiles will get quickly overwhelmed.
*[[Wing|Wings]] are good for attacking Hornets from a distance. However, their movement is somewhat unpredictable.
*[[Peas]] and [[Grapes]] are good against swarms of low-rarity Hornets, but they do not have enough DPS to take out high-rarity Hornets. They are also hard to aim.
*[[Beetle Egg|Eggs]] are one of the best petals to use against Hornets: they allow the user to deal damage without aiming or getting too close. Against high-rarity hornets, weaker eggs won't make a dent in their health though. If you have an {{Rarity|Ultra}}+ Beetle Egg, it can spawn a {{Rarity|Mythic}} [[Beetle]], which can serve as a shield against missiles.
**{{Rarity|Super}} Beetle Eggs are even better, as the {{Rarity|Ultra}} Beetle will most likely kill the Hornet and survive.
**[[Stick]]s could have a similar function to Eggs. However, the [[Sandstorm]]s do not target mobs, making them less useful along with the petal's long reload. They can be useful when spammed along with teammates, but using this strategy would take lots of grinding. The Sandstorms can be used as shields.
*[[Orange]], [[Third Eye]] and [[Yin Yang]] and Wings can help you hit Hornets from far away.
*[[Lightning]] can deal good damage against a swarm of Hornets.
*Builds with lots of Tank-like petals such as [[Heavy]], [[Rock]], or [[Corn]] are a good idea to use by "boxing in" hornets or rushing at them with your petals. Just beware of their lengthy respawn, Hornets do a lot of damage to your petals.
*[[Jelly]] can be used to deflect missiles.
*[[Bubble]] can help you speed away from getting hit from a missile quickly.
*If you're using close-range petals, move the opposite direction your petals are rotating around the hornet, to ensure maximum damage (in most cases, move counterclockwise). While dealing with normal hornets, circle around them and keep hitting them. Make sure to quickly deal with a hornet that is following the player, as the player has a high chance of being overwhelmed if there are many hornets following.
**With Einstein hornets, move back and forth to dodge their missiles. Hornets pause for a split second between turning around and firing, so use this short window of time to your advantage.
*It's important to keep ranged petals/eggs in your inventory in case you run into a swarm, where close ranged combat becomes extremely dangerous.
*Deathwheel builds can overwhelm Hornets with sheer DPS, but only do this if you have a lot of health because the missiles will likely hit you.
*You can also go behind a [[Centipede]], making the Hornets stuck in the segments. Be careful not to hit {{Rarity|Epic}}+ Centipedes.
*Hitting a Hornet with an [[Iris]] petal will deal lots of damage, but it takes a few seconds.
*Against high-rarity hornets, [[Leaf|Leaves]] are superior to [[Rose]]s and [[Dahlia]]. The Hornet's bulky missiles will shred apart your roses before they have a chance to heal you.
*To escape hornet swarms, lay down some [[Web]]s to trap them. Then, equip a [[Powder]] or [[Bubble]], or simply ride the knockback of the hornets' missiles away.
*Hornets can't see you if you hide behind a wall. You can hide and kill them with summons
*Surprisingly, {{Rarity|Mythic}}+ [[Claw]] can serve as a shield for {{Rarity|Mythic}} or below Hornets' missile as the extra damage works on missiles. 
*Don't be intimidated by Hornets: running away almost will never help. The more flowers run away, the more hornets will stack up, and with the help of other mobs such as [[Centipede|Centipedes]] and [[Spider|Spiders]], it will be extremely difficult to fight hornets by then.
*In case you get overwhelmed by the insane amount of Hornets that spawn all throughout the garden, either try to kill them all, or make a run for where you are trying to go.
**If you have a low level and are swarmed by {{Rarity|Epic}}+ Hornets, it is just best to give up and die to them.
*Hornets have a large weakness, they can only target one player at once. If you are in a small group, the rest of the group can attack the Hornet while you try not to die.
*If you do face one alone, swerve around and avoid the missiles. This gets exponentially harder the more Hornets there are.
*Using a high-rarity [[Wax]] can shield you from missiles. 

=== {{Rarity|Super}} Hornet ===

* While {{Rarity|Super}} Hornets are extremely dangerous, the various {{Rarity|Ultra}} mobs that spawn in garden may be just as deadly as the Hornet itself. When bubbling to the mob, it is advised to use Sponge and multiple {{Rarity|Ultra}} healing petals, and have a Lotus equipped in case a Spider hits you. It is also advised to use Sponge when actually fighting the mob so you don't die if anything hits you.
* If the mob spawns deep in Garden, it is advised to form a "megasquad", where a large group of players stay near each other and clear mobs. This increases the chance for everyone to survive.
* In most cases, players will lure {{Rarity|Super}} Hornets to slightly easier areas because they are nearly impossible to kill while surrounded by {{Rarity|Ultra}} mobs. This means that you do not need to rush into the center of the Spiral area to attack it.
* Sponge is essentially required for this mob, as it can kill you quickly in many different ways. Also, using bubbles and burst damage, such as Stingers or Iris, will make fighting it less risky because you can attack it, then move away to heal.
* Make sure that you observe the Hornet, as its attacks can be indicated by movements of its antennae or missile.
* If the Hornet is targeting you, try to escape from it using Bubbles or Shovel. It is very difficult to land attacks while the hornet is shooting at you.
* Try to stay on the left side of the Hornet. When it does its missile spin attack, it will always move clockwise, meaning that players on its right will be in danger of being hit by the Hornet's body. This attack can also be dodged with high rarity Bubbles, but this is risky.
** The {{Rarity|Ultra}} Hornet missiles that are released during this attack are also dangerous, as they can pin you and deal heavy damage. It is advised to seek a large group of players, as they will be able to destroy the missiles before being hit. Alternatively, you can move out of the missiles' range using bubbles.
** Almost directly after doing its missile spin attack, the {{Rarity|Super}} Hornet will spawn five {{Rarity|Ultra}} Hornets. These pose little threat to players in front of the {{Rarity|Super}} Hornet, but they make it risky to attack the {{Rarity|Super}} Hornet from the side.
** If you are skilled enough, you can stand on the right of the Hornet and spin with the Hornet when it spins. This you won’t be hit by any missiles.

==Gallery==
<gallery>

File:Ad.png|This is what is known in the community as "Hornet spam."
</gallery>


==Trivia== 
*Around at the end of April 2024, Super Hornet now summons Ultra Mobs and shoots out Ultra Missiles as its special ability: this makes it impossible to kill alone and it requires a large crowd of players in order to kill it.
*Hornet is the first mob added to the game to feature ranged attacks
*On 24/2/20 (during Legacy florr), the Hornet AI was improved so they could predict the movement of players, using calculus to predict where the player would go: these were referred to as Einstein hornets. This was done to prevent herding of Hornets. Later that day, most Hornets were changed back to normal, but a few hornets would spawn as Einstein Hornets. Epic+ Hornets are always Einstein Hornets, while Rare- Hornets are common Hornets.
*The Hornet's attack and behavior are the complete opposite of its [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hornet real life counterpart], as real-life hornets must get close to you to sting you whereas florr hornets keep their distance and fire extremely high recoil missiles that make it very hard to get near.
*In early January 2023, the hornet's firing animation was changed. Hornets now turn around and shoot the missile on their back, making for hilarious moments when players get 180-ed by a hornet from across the screen. They used to face the enemy and shoot the missile before, but still getting recoil.
*If you hit a Hornet's Missile, it only does damage to the Missile rather than the entire Hornet.
*Hornets can shoot their [[Missile|missiles]] through walls and mobs.
*On January 4th 2024, [[M28]] updated the hornet's missile health to scale with petal health. This also applied to mobs that can shoot projectiles.
*In real life, Hornets love honey and steal them from honeybees but in florr.io they don’t get affected by [[Honey]].
*In Karlos's video, it is shown that a Super Hornet despawned while Karlos was soloing it. This is one of the weird bugs that happened.
*Hornets spawn less, which can allow other mobs to spawn. This reduced hornet spam.

title: Iris
content:
{{Petal Template Infobox | image = File:Iris (Unusual).png|U File:Iris (Rare).png|R File:Iris (Epic).png|E File:Iris (Legendary).png|L File:Iris (Mythic).png|M File:Iris (Ultra).png|Ul File:Iris (Super).png|S File:Iris (Unique).png|Uq | description = Very poisonous, but takes a little while to do its work.
-50% damage against other flowers. | first-rarity= {{Rarity|Unusual}} | damage = {{Petal Scale|5|lowest-rarity=Unusual}} | health = {{Petal Scale|5|lowest-rarity=Unusual}} | poison = {{Infobox Colors Empty||210 (70/s)|630 (210/s)|1890 (630/s)|5670 (1890/s)|17010 (5670/s)|51030 (17010/s)|153090 (51030/s)|459270 (153090/s)}} | reload-time = 4.0s | stackability = Unstackable | dropped-by = *[[Evil Centipede]] *[[Scorpion]] | special = Poisons mobs and enemy flowers. }} '''Iris''' is a [[Petals|petal]] in florr.io. Its function consists of poisoning all mobs and flowers it hits, dealing immense damage over time. It deals 50% less damage against flowers and is dropped by [[Scorpion]]s and [[Centipede (Jungle)|Jungle Centipedes]]. ==Appearance== The Iris petal consists of a small circle, smaller than the [[Basic]] and has the same size as the [[Light]]. However, instead of being white-colored, it wields a lavender purple color with a darker shade of it on its borders. Flowers hit by an Iris will turn lavender purple (same color as an Iris) and will frown, regardless if they are defending or not. ==Function== {|class="article-table" |+Petal Stats ![[Rarity]] !Obtainable? !Damage !Health !Respawn !Poison |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |No |5 |5 |rowspan="9"|4 seconds |70 (23.3/s) |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |rowspan="8"|Yes |15 |15 |210 (70/s) |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |45 |45 |630 (210/s) |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |135 |135 |1890 (630/s) |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |405 |405 |5670 (1890/s) |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |1215 |1215 |17010 (5670/s) |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |3645 |3645 |51030 (17010/s) |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |10935 |10935 |153090 (51030/s) |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} |32805 |32805 |459270 (153090/s) |} The Iris poisons any enemy that touches it. The enemy will take the amount of damage shown by the petal's poison stat. Despite only flashing every second, the poisoned entity will actually receive damage every tick. The poison effect does not stack, however, hitting a poisoned enemy again with Iris (or any other poisonous petal) prior to the previous poison debuff endeding will reset the poison value to either the current one or the one of the petal that hit it, whichever is higher. It will not add poison onto the pre-existing value. Iris poison is affected by Privet Berry, which will increase in a short time, and it's only visible when multiple are used together. ==Obtainment== ===[[Centipede (Jungle)]]=== {{:Centipede (Jungle)/Drop Iris}} ===[[Scorpion]]=== {{:Scorpion/Drop Iris}} ===Purchasing=== On certain days, Iris can be purchased in the [[Shop]]. ''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]'' {{Petal Price|2}} ==Strategy== *Irises are one of the petals with the highest damage output of pretty much any other petal, even higher than that of a singular [[Stinger]], only being slightly outclassed by [[Tomato]]. However, the poison does not deal damage immediately, so therefore other petals have higher burst damage and functionality than the Iris. Furthermore, the Iris has low health and a high respawn timer (though 4 seconds still isn't too much), and even worse, the poison does not stack with itself unless you are using [[Privet Berry]], so it is further hindered. Iris is generally good against targets with high body damage, and DPS is maximized against multiple enemies, if it manages to hit between those without repeating on one mob before the poison wears off. *Try to use petals like the [[Heavy]] to protect your Iris from something that could destroy it before it gets to be used. *An advantage of Iris is that poison damage bypasses Armor, so while it may not deal damage by itself, it can still deal a lot of damage with its poison stat. *Iris can be used in tandem with [[Privet]], so the poison deals more damage and effects lasts for a bit longer and may help you even more. However, Privet is very weak and unreliable, so this strategy is not recommended. *The Iris' major drawbacks are its stats and single-hit characteristic: [[Grapes]] can hit 4 targets at once and can be effectively crowd-control petals, [[Pincer]]s, while having less poison damage, slow down enemies so they don't catch up to you easily, and [[Uranium]] can deal quite a lot of damage as well as being a crowd-control petal if the user outheals its self-damage. ===Obtaining=== *Fending off [[Evil Centipede]]s is far easier than fending off [[Scorpion]]s, not only due to their stats, but also specially due to the scenarios they are found: Desert has [[Beetle]]s, [[Fire Ant Burrow]]s and [[Sandstorm]]s, while Jungle mobs can be mitigated if you are careful enough. *If you still want to get Irises from a Scorpion, be on the lookout for the many dangerous mobs here, use something like [[Plank]] to destroy its stingers (or avoid them yourself), try to tank its poison damage with [[Lotus]] and engage in close combat when you're sure it's safe to. {{Zone|Garden}} *Landing an Iris onto an [[Ant Hole]] will slowly deal damage to it. Although this means Ants will gradually corner you and make you run, it is still better than a [[Stinger]] in this case which will go into a lengthy cooldown after dealing massive damage to the Ant Hole and spawning lots of Ants. Remember that the Iris can't deal with multiple Ants, nor can it fend off multiple [[Centipede]] segments. *Pincer should be used instead of Iris when fending off [[Bee]]s, [[Bumble Bee]]s and [[Spider]]s since they are quick and unpredictable, being able to catch you in no time if you don't take care. {{Zone|Desert}} *Irises are most effective against Beetles and Sandstorms here, since they will wear down those mobs and kill them before they do much harm to you. However, both of them can still surprise you, whether it's by quick speed or by strong body damage. Pincer is better against Beetles, while try to use [[Disc]] against Sandstorms or maintain a safe distance from them with [[Wing]]s and the likes. *Its lack of AoE (Area of Effect) will severely hurt you against [[Desert Centipede]]s and Fire Ant Burrows, meaning you'll get overwhelmed very quickly if you don't replace this petal. {{Zone|Ocean}} *The Iris works best against [[Crab]]s once they sprint onto your petal orbit radius, as they will be punished with poison damage once they try to ram you. This also does decent against [[Shell (Mob)|Shells]] despite not being the best counter. *[[Lightning]] is a better counter to [[Leech]] while something like [[Missile]] is better against [[Jellyfish]]. Iris isn't a good pick in Ocean overall. {{Zone|Ant Hell}} *On Ant Hell, Iris is a bad pick. There are far better choices such as [[Ant Egg]] or Glass to deal with the Ants there. *On Fire Ant Hell, Iris is a mildly decent pick. While still being heavily outclassed like in Ant Hell, at least it won't suffer much from the Fire Ants' double damage. *On Termite Hell and [[Termite Mound]]s, Iris is a decent pick. While still being weak, it will chip down multiple Termites due to their Psyonic Connection. There are still other far better choices, though, such as Grapes and Uranium themselves. {{Zone|Hel}} ===Using=== *Iris is one of the best petals in this zone, against both mobs and players, as its ability to deal damage over time allows you to hit an enemy and then move back to reload petals and heal. *Once an Iris lands a hit on another player, specially at {{Rarity|Mythic}}+rarity, it will put the poisoned flower in severe risk, since it has the best poison stat out of all poisoning petals. **Combining Iris with a {{Rarity|Mythic}} Dandelion will create one of the deadliest combos ever: The Dandelion reduces the effectiveness of an enemy's healing petals by 40% (20% for each Dandelion), allowing the Iris to strike and deal massive damage overtime while the poisoned enemy can't heal with maximum efficiency. Using {{Rarity|Ultra}}+ Dandelions, specially one affected by the Duplicator [[Talents|Talent]], will further cripple healing by 60% (no Duplicator), 80% (Duplicated) *A Mythic+ Iris by itself will most likely kill the poisoned enemy, as they're very strong and can quickly wear out any flowers poisoned. [[Privet]] may further secure your victory. If the opponent has a lot of [[Cactus|Cacti]] or other petals that increase the opponent's survivability, hit them multiple times. *A [[Faster]] or [[Yin Yang]] makes the petal harder to destroy and more likely to land a hit. *Iris users should be careful of [[Salt]] users since attacking one will cause the attacker to take some damage too. *If the opponent is using [[Sponge]], Iris is one of its best counters. Conversely, if the opponent is using [[Lotus]], maybe you should try to change your Iris, or just exhaust the Lotus with poison overload. *Irises are very frail. Defend them accordingly with [[Heavy]] and the likes so your opponents don't take advantage of your weak spot. *The Iris is mildly good against [[Gambler]]s because they are close to being flowers (albeit AI-controlled). However, this struggles against any other mob, so you're better off using something else. ===Against=== *Irises can be easily destroyed by pretty much any other petal in the game. Attack the Iris and use the hole as a way to damage its user. *Lotus can be used to mitigate poison damage, letting the petal take all poison damage instead of you. However, it can easily be exhausted, so do not fully rely on it. *Iris + Privet will barely do any harm to you. *If the opponent is running Dandelion + Iris builds, make sure to not get hit by the Dandelions (specially if there are 3 or 4 of them), as getting your healing crippled will greatly benefit the Iris and you won't be able to heal from the poison damage, specially if it's a Mythic+ Iris. *Make sure to not use [[Root]] against this, as any Armor stat is ignored. {{Zone|Jungle}} *While no mobs here run faster than you, [[Pincer]] may still be favored over Iris. [[Grapes]] can also end up as a more reliable choice due to their AoE. *The Iris does shine against two instances: it may not deal base damage to [[Leafbug]]s, but its poison damage will bypass its Armor stat and deal a lot of damage to them anyways; on a [[Termite Mound]], the Iris may deal a lot of damage to many Termites at once, however, be sure to not get cornered by them. {{Zone|Sewers}} *Pincer gets the most ground over Iris due to [[Fly|Flies]], [[Moth]]s and [[Roach]]es as well as Spiders. Iris should almost never be considered on Sewers. ==Gallery== File:Iris (Common).png|{{Rarity|1}} Iris. (unobtainable) File:Iris (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|2}} Iris. File:Iris (Rare).png|{{Rarity|3}} Iris. File:Iris (Epic).png|{{Rarity|4}} Iris. File:Iris (Legendary).png|{{Rarity|5}} Iris. File:Iris (Mythic).png|{{Rarity|6}} Iris. File:Iris (Ultra).png|{{Rarity|7}} Iris. File:Iris (Super).png|{{Rarity|8}} Iris. File:Iris (Unique).png|{{Rarity|9}} Iris. File:Iris Petal.png|Iris. ==Trivia== *Iris, Grapes, Pincer, [[Privet]] (technically) and [[Uranium]] are the only poisonous petals in the game. **Out of all of them, Iris has the strongest poison damage and the longest poison duration. *The Iris shares the same color code as the Grapes, and it can be argued that the latter petal (and all four of the smaller Grapes) is a bigger variation of the former. *On legacy florr.io, [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Iris Irises] were dropped at {{Rarity|Unusual}} Rarity (like current Iris' first Rarity), from [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Beetle Beetles] (and their Massive Variation), [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Evil_Centipede Evil Centipedes] and [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Spider Spiders]. However, as of the World Map florr.io, Iris no longer drops from Beetles or Spiders (which is ironic as Spiders now deal poison damage), rather being dropped by [[Scorpion]]s instead (since their stingers poison flowers). Evil Centipedes still get to drop Iris once they returned to the game. *Iris used to deal less poison damage: the first is 4.5x base damage, the second is 6x base damage, and the last one is the current. *Iris is the only petal whose name starts with the letter "I." {{Petals}} [[zh:鸢尾]] [[Category:Debuffs]] [[Category:Desert]] [[Category:Jungle]] [[Category:Poison]] [[Category:Unusual+]] [[Category:Droppable Petals]] [[Category:Petals]] [[Category:Stackable Petals]] [[Category:Jungle Centipede]] [[Category:Scorpion]]

The '''Jelly''' is a petal in florr.io. It deals huge knockback to anything it touches. The Jelly is dropped by [[Jellyfish]].

==Appearance==
The Jelly's appearance is similar to the [[Bubble]]. However, instead of being transparent-white, it's purple-colored and looks more translucent. It also has five smaller purple circles on its body instead of only one.

==Function==

The Jelly is average in stats, which are identical to a [[Basic]]. However, the Jelly has a knockback stat in which it will push mobs away by an X distance (indicated by the knockback value) once they are touched by this petal. The knockback stat differs from multi-hitting knockbacks.

==Obtainment==
===[[Jellyfish]]===
{{:Jellyfish/Drop Jelly}}

===Purchasing===
On certain days, Jelly can be purchased in the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|2}}

==Strategy==
*Jelly is generally used for pushing away mobs when you're either cornered or the mob is too close (the latter case usually happens with a mob faster than you). However, it deals the same damage (and has the same health) as the Basic, so this can take the mobs by itself a bit while bouncing them.
*Another common usage of the Jelly is trolling: a Jelly might push something very valuable (like a {{Rarity|Super}} [[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble]]) away from someone who wants it, or it could push a very dangerous mob into another player. Such strategies are toxic and not encouraged here. For trolling purposes, [[Heavy]] works well with this petal.
*Jelly's best pairs are [[Faster]] and [[Third Eye]]. Faster lets you push back mobs faster, while Third Eye lets you push them farther away.

{{Zone|Garden}}
*Jelly is useful against [[Bee]]s, [[Bumble Bee]]s and [[Spider]]s: all three of them have dangerous movements that can quickly kill you if they are not pushed back, respectively: a zigzag movement, quick movement speed and unpredictability and quick movement speed with poison. Jelly would be a great petal to use.
*In general, it can also serve against {{Rarity|Mythic}}+ mobs close to you, to avoid accidentally ramming onto them.
*Jellies are not as good against [[Hornet]]s, as pushing them away will make it harder to reach and kill them. However, it could push them annd their missiles away from their aggro range, which leads to them not attacking you anymore unless you get close to it again.
*Jelly is quite useful against {{Rarity|Super}} [[Rock (Mob)|Rocks]] because it can push them away, preventing the smaller Rock summons from hurting you.

{{Zone|Desert}}
*Jelly is a nice petal to have in Desert, as [[Beetle]]s and [[Desert Centipede]]s are very quick and can end up killing you in no time, while it can also divert [[Sandstorm]]s too close to you or [[Scorpion]] stingers, avoiding  massive damage to your flower. Even against the [[Soldier Fire Ant]]s from a [[Fire Ant Burrow]], the Jelly can help you not get overwhelmed. Take note that this won't handle everything, so support yourself and the petal accordingly.
*Switch out this petal if you need to kill something in close combat, specially if it's a [[Ladybug (Desert)|Shiny Ladybug]], to not risk losing getting important loot.

{{Zone|Ocean}}
* Jelly is pretty useful in Ocean, as it can fend off [[Crab]]s charging onto your location, as well as helping in keeping [[Leech]]es at bay. Leeches are particularly very fast and could very much kill you easily.
**However, using Jelly against Leeches is particularly risky as you may knock its body segments to yourself, dealing yourself a lot of damage especially in higher rarities.
*Jelly is also good to use against {{Rarity|Ultra}}+ [[Shell (Mob)|Shell]] fights to divert both a close-up Shell and the Pearls it might fire onto you.
*Jelly is less useful against [[Jellyfish]] and [[Starfish (Mob)|Starfish]], as pushing them farther from you will cause more trouble to you. Bringing a [[Dandelion]] against Starfish and something like [[Rubber]] against Jellyfish will be far more beneficial.

===Obtaining===
*Jellyfish, specially on swarms, will be very dangerous. Rubber is a viable choice to absorb their lightning discharges, or fight fire with fire and bring [[Battery]] to counterdischarge them.
*Another viable strategy would be using [[Basil]] and [[Leaf]] to mitigate or outheal them completely.
*Running away from them and using [[Carrot]]s or [[Missile]]s maybe will work in avoiding their discharges. However, you have high chances of being caught anyways, so be sure to use another strategy or have a healing build on your secondary build.

{{Zone|Ant Hell}}
*Jelly is useful if you're getting cornered or swarmed on all directions on either Ant Hell. This won't be as effective against the Bosses there, since they will likely be locked on other players.

{{Zone|Hel}}
===Using===
*Using Jellies will make you hard to be hurt by other players. They will constantly push away them and their petals away, so Jellies will be good if you don't want to PvP.
*They work best against ram builds, as Jellies will keep the rammer away from touching you, pushing the other player to somewhere the Hel mobs may end up being a nuisance, unlike with Rubber where you would be the one put at risk of dying.
*[[Hel Beetle]]s, [[Hel Centipede]]s and [[Hel Spider]]s have high speed and a high chance of killing you, so using Jellies against them will keep them away from you. [[Hel Wasp]] missiles will also be easily deflected and might even harm someone else for you to finish.

===Against===
*Watch out for Jelly users: they may put you in a detrimental position, specially if you end up surrounded by mobs, or have just rammed into a Hel Spider or a Hel Wasp.
*You can try using [[Air]] or [[Soil]] to reduce knockback taken, or [[Shovel]] to completely avoid it (if you time well and the cooldown doesn't affect you too much). Alternatively, you can bring [[Bubble]] or [[Powder]] to quickly reposition yourself.
*Another viable strategy would be using ranged petals like the [[Wing]], or buffing your attack range with [[Third Eye]], so you can damage the Jelly use from afar without being pushed yourself.

{{Zone|Jungle}}
*None of the mobs here are particularly quick, however, this could save you when you're cornered or swarmed, specially by Termites once you enter a [[Termite Mound]]. This can also help you fighting [[Mantis]] and [[Wasp]] projectiles, though don't push them too far away if you want to kill them.

{{Zone|Sewers}}
*Jelly is good since three of the four mobs here will get close to you and may kill you. [[Roach]]es are specially fast and can deplete your health in no time, but [[Fly|Flies]] and [[Spider]]s can still harm you a lot.
*You should try to avoid using this against [[Moth]]s since the Jelly's knockback will make it harder to kill them.

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">
File:Jelly (Common).png|{{R|1}} Jelly.
File:Jelly (Unusual).png|{{R|2}} Jelly.
File:Jelly (Rare).png|{{R|3}} Jelly.
File:Jelly (Epic).png|{{R|4}} Jelly.
File:Jelly (Legendary).png|{{R|5}} Jelly.
File:Jelly (Mythic).png|{{R|6}} Jelly.
File:Jelly (Ultra).png|{{R|7}} Jelly.
File:Jelly (Super).png|{{R|8}} Jelly.
File:Jelly (Unique).png|{{R|9}} Jelly.
File:Jelly.png|Jelly.
</gallery>

==Trivia== 
*This petal has the same damage and health as a [[Basic]].
*The Jelly is the only petal with a knockback status. Other knockback petals (such as [[Corn]] and [[Heavy]]) only do it because of their high durability, thus letting them stay in a mob's radius for longer and hit them multiple times. This is known as multi-hitting and all high durability petals benefit from this.
*The Jelly is the first translucent thing in the game, followed by [[Dust]]. Other things are either transparent or fully opaque.
*On the secret January 14th, 2024 update, the Jelly's knockback stat was reduced to half its original. Thus, the nerf essentially made all Jelly rarities one tier lower, as {{Rarity|Super}} Jellies now have the knockback of {{Rarity|Ultra}} Jellies, {{R|Ultra}} Jellies have the knockback of {{Rarity|Mythic}} Jellies and so on.
*Its description is both a reference to the in-game petal's ability (once touched, mobs will get pushed away), a reference to how many species of Jellyfishes in real life may kill you due to their poisonous tentacles (thus, touching them would likely kill you).
*This is one of the many petals that are dropped by a mob of the same name. The others are: [[Bubble]], [[Cactus]], [[Dandelion]], [[Ant Egg]], [[Rock]], [[Shell]], [[Sponge]], [[Square]] and [[Starfish]]. In this, it's the [[Jellyfish]].
**This, Shell, Sponge and Starfish are all from new florr.io, thus their mobs were only added after its rerelease.
***All four are also curiously exclusive to the [[Ocean]] map.
**Jelly's trivia is a partial case, since the name of the mob is Jellyfish, which fully contains the name of the petal, but also has '''fish''' in it.
**Jelly is the only petal whose name starts with a "J".

The '''Jellyfish''' is a mob in florr.io that spawns in the Ocean. It strikes lightnings when you are close to it. 

==Appearance==
Jellyfish is a translucent white circle with 8 white lines near the circle's rim.

==Stats & Behavior ==
'''Speed:''' Slow.

'''Behavior:''' It attacks by using lightning strikes that bypass any petal you have. [[Rubber]] is the main counter for Jellyfish because they absorb lightning.

Jellyfish’s body damage is dealt as lightning damage, meaning it cannot be reduced with [[Root]] and can be absorbed using [[Rubber]].

{{Rarity|Super}} Jellyfishes have the ability to shoot an entire shockwave that will disable your petals. This can be known by it waying its tentacles faster.

==Spawning==
The {{PAGENAME}} spawns only in Ocean.
==Drops==

===[[Lightning]]===
{{:Jellyfish/Drop Lightning}}

===[[Jelly]]===
{{:Jellyfish/Drop Jelly}}

==Strategy==
*[[Rubber]] were extremely useful against Jellyfish as they attracted and absorbed the lightning bolt. The lightning bolts use the rubber durability but if you have multiple or it respawns fast enough, you will be fine. 
*Rubber is now the best petal to counter Jellyfish's lightning. Rubbers of the same rarity or higher will nullify it completely. 
*Try to take them out as quickly as you can, as they can annoy you when you are trying to dodge other mobs.
*Use [[Stinger]] to dispatch efficiently, otherwise use [[Salt]] to repel it.
**Don't try to think you can dodge the lightning bolts too, as the lightning bolts have 100% accuracy. See above.
*[[Wing]] is very effective against Jellyfish since they have further range than the Jellyfish lightning bolts. Other ranged petals (like [[Missile]]) work too.
*Players often use [[Sponge]] in Ocean, taking it off once they are taking too much damage from it and then not dying because of Second Chance. However, Jellyfish often kill them before they can heal or re-equip the Sponge. This makes Jellyfish very obnoxious.

=== {{Rarity|Super}} Jellyfish ===
*{{Rarity|Super}} Jellyfishes can disable petals with the special attack, so you will have to hit in time before it can make shockwaves.
*It is advised to use {{Rarity|Ultra}}+ Rubber to absorb the lightning damage. Also use Sponge in case.
*It is advised to use burst damage, such as stingers. When it shakes its tentacles, immediately [[Bubble]] away or use [[Shovel]], while the damage petals reload.

</gallery>

==Trivia==
*In May 18, 2024 update, {{PAGENAME}} health was nerfed by 1.67x.
*Jellyfish is not actually hostile. It just strikes around it passively, it's not an "attack".
*Its tentacles are a reused texture from Digdig.io where there is a skin of the same name.
*This is one of the more worthwhile mobs to farm in ocean, as it gives you [[Lightning]], one of the most valuable petals in the game.
*Jellyfish in real life do not produce electricity if their microscopic-barbed stingers touch anything.
*After an update somewhere in 2024, magnets no longer absorb the lightning bolt. It was replaced by [[Rubber]] instead.

title: Jungle
content:
{{Background|图片=Jungleb.png|大小=2000px}} {{Area Template Infobox |name = Jungle |image = Jungle.png |caption-image = |mobs = *[[Baby Termite]] *[[Bush]] *[[Centipede (Jungle)|Centipede]] *[[Firefly]] *[[Firefly/Magic|Firefly (magic)]] *[[Ladybug (Jungle)|Ladybug]] *[[Leafbug]] *[[Golden Leafbug]] *[[Mantis]] *[[Soldier Termite]] *[[Termite Mound]] *[[Termite Overmind]] *[[Termite Egg]] *[[Wasp]] *[[Worker Termite]] *[[Crab]] |special_entity = [[Target Dummy]]
[[Titan]] |connections = * [[Ant Hell]] *[[Desert]] *[[Garden]] *[[Ocean]] |distance_from_spawn = It is a spawn, but a zone away from all spawn areas, except Hel. }} '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is an area in florr.io. It includes all types of Jungle mobs. ==Location== It is a spawn, but a zone away from all spawn areas except [[Hel]]. ===Connections=== The area connects to 4 areas, as shown on the [[World Map]]. {| class="article-table" !Area !Connection Location |- |[[Ant Hell]] |Bottom |- |[[Desert]] |Slight Mid Bottom Left |- |[[Garden]] |Top Left |- |[[Ocean]] |Top |} ===Special Zones=== At the top right, there is the most crowded area that is full of {{R|Ultra}} mobs. Near the [[Ant Hell]] entrance, there is a Termite spawning zone. Near the [[Ocean]] entrance, there is a beach that spawns low rarity [[Crab|Crabs]]. The bottom left corner only spawns [[Mantis|Mantises]] and [[Firefly|Fireflies]]. The bottom right corner spawns mostly [[Wasp|Wasps]]. The left center part of the map spawns only low-rarity [[Leafbug|Leafbugs]]. The top portion of the area around the lake at the bottom center of the map spawns [[Termite Mound|Termite Mounds]]. ==Mobs== A total of 12 mobs spawn in the area. [[Baby Termite]]: Passive, like a [[Baby Ant]], unless a Termite Egg is attacked. Only spawns in [[Termite Mound]]. Drops [[Relic]] and [[Carrot]]. [[Soldier Termite]]: An aggressive termite like the [[Soldier Ant]]. Drops [[Relic]] and [[Bone]] [[Worker Termite]]: A neutral termite like the [[Worker Ant]]. Drops [[Relic]] and [[Plank]]. [[Termite Overmind]]: Extremely slow, but has lots of health. Drops [[Relic]], Compass, and [[Tomato]]. [[Leafbug]]: A bug that resembles a hybrid of a [[Leaf]] and a [[Scorpion]]. Drops [[Leaf]] and [[Root]]. Has a massively increased [[Armor]] stat. [[Golden Leafbug]]: Same as a normal [[Leafbug]], but it has a golden color, and drops [[Golden Leaf]] instead of [[Leaf]]. [[Termite Mound]]: Just like an [[Ant Hole]] but can be entered. Contains [[Soldier Termite|Ter]][[Worker Termite|mit]][[Baby Termite|es]] and a [[Termite Overmind]]. Drops [[Soil]], [[Rubber]], and [[Relic]]. [[Mantis]]: Moves similar to Scorpion, and shoots small bursts of Peas. Drops [[Peas]] and [[Bur]]. [[Bush]]: A static mob. Drops [[Cotton]], Golden Leaf, and [[Monstera]]. [[Wasp]]: It's like Hornet but a darker orange. It reloads faster and its missiles move in a wavy curved line. Drops [[Antennae]], [[Missile]], and [[Blood Stinger]]. [[Firefly]]: A Neutral mob that moves like [[Fly]], drops [[Wing]], [[Bulb]], and [[Battery]]. Releases lightning upon contact. [[Firefly/Magic|Magic Firefly]]: A Neutral mob that moves like [[Fly]], drops [[Mana Orb]] and [[Battery]]. Releases lightning upon contact. Spawns only in periodic waves. [[Ladybug (Jungle)]]: Neutral mob that drops [[Dahlia]] and [[Yin Yang]]. [[Crab]]: A mob that charges to attack players. Drops [[Claw]] and [[Sand]]. Only spawns at low rarities on the beach near the [[Ocean]] portal. ==Strategies== ''See [[Builds - Jungle|Builds - Jungle.]]'' * Jungle isn't hard if you are strong enough, but tons of neutral mobs may knock you out of place. * It is advised to use a [[Sponge]]. * [[Wasp|Wasps]] are weaker on their left side because of the curved paths their missiles take. * [[Mantis|Mantises]] have slow projectiles. Strafing slightly can easily cause them to aim too far out and miss. * You can enter [[Termite Mound|Termite Mounds]] to escape dangerous situations, but beware the possibility of exiting directly into a horde of mobs. * [[Plank|Planks]] are very useful here because of the projectile spam. * It is not recommended to use Eggs here, as [[Mantis|Mantises]]' projectiles can cause summons to die very quickly, [[Firefly|Fireflies]] will release lightning when summons ram them, and they do little to no damage against [[Leafbug|Leafbugs]]. * This biome is extremely difficult for solo players. Consider bringing teammates. ==Trivia== *Jungle bears a resemblance to both the cancelled [[Swamp]] scenario and [[Garden]]. *The Jungle biome is the only scenario to contain two projectile throwing mobs. ([[Wasp]] and [[Mantis]]). *Jungle is one of the few areas to not have shortcuts. *Mobs used to be able to spawn in the Jungle Hut. Now they cannot. == Tiles== File:grass2_b_0.svg File:grass2_c_0.svg File:grass2_c_1.svg File:grass2_c_2.svg File:grass2_c_3.svg File:bush_c_0.svg File:bush_t_0.svg File:bush_tl_0.svg File:bush_tri_0.svg File:hut_b_0.svg File:hut_bl_0.svg File:hut_bli_0.svg File:hut_br_0.svg File:hut_bri_0.svg File:hut_c_0.svg File:hut_l_0.svg File:hut_r_0.svg File:hut_t_0.svg File:hut_tl_0.svg File:hut_tri_0.svg File:hut_tr_0.svg File:hut_tri_0.svg File:wood_c_0.svg == Gallery == File:Jungle In florr.io..jpg|https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BS50OSPaLsc File:Leafbug image.png|M28 spawning an {{Rarity|Ultra}} Leafbug in [[Centralia]]. File:Screenshot 2023-02-21 08.47.30.png|Concept art for Jungle. File:Old jungle 2343.jpeg|Jungle spawnpoint in the April 27, 2024 update. Note that Jungle mobs are in the hut. {{Areas}} [[Category:Scenarios]] [[Category:Areas]]

{{Mob Template Infobox | name = {{PAGENAME}} | image = File:Ladybug (Common).png|C File:Ladybug (Unusual).png|U File:Ladybug (Rare).png|R File:Ladybug (Epic).png|E File:Ladybug (Legendary).png|L File:Ladybug (Mythic).png|M File:Ladybug (Ultra).png|Ul File:Ladybug (Super).png|S File:Ladybug (Unique).png|Uq | description = Cute and harmless. | drops = * [[Light]] * [[Rose]] | health = {{Mob HP Scale|62.5}} | damage = {{Mob Damage Scale|10}} | armor = {{Mob Armor Scale|0.8|0}} | xp-reward = {{Infobox Colors Empty|1|1|5|41|378|5.2k|42.4k|4.7m}} | move = Yes. | status = Below Unusual = Passive Above Rare = Aggressive when provoked. | attack = Body Damage | spawn = * [[Garden]] | special = }} ''For other variations of ladybug, see [[Ladybug (Jungle)]] or [[Ladybug (Desert)]].'' '''Ladybug''' is an original mob in florr.io. It has no special attacks, instead just wandering like a normal mob. ==Appearance== A black circle in the front makes up the head and the body is a red circle with a dark red outline. The body has black spots on it, the pattern of which is random. ==Stats & Behavior== '''Speed:''' Normal. '''Behavior:''' {{R|Common}} and {{R|Unusual}} Ladybugs are completely passive, whereas {{R|Rare}} Ladybugs attack the player when attacked. {| class="article-table" |+Mob Stats ![[Rarity]] ! Health !Body Damage !Armor !XP Reward !Spots |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |62.5 |10 |0.8 |1 |3 |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |234.375 |30 |2.4 |1 |3 |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |843.75 |90 |7.2 |5 |3 |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |3,375 |270 |21.6 |41 |4 |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |25,312.5 |810 |64.8 |378 |5 |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |151,875 |2,430 |194.4 |5.2k |6 |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |1,822,500 |7,290 | rowspan="2" |583.2 |42.4k |8 |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |82,012,500 |21,870 |4.7m |9+ |} ==Drops== ===[[Light]]=== {{:Ladybug/Drop Light}} ===[[Rose]]=== {{:Ladybug/Drop Rose}} ==Strategy== *Ladybug drops [[Light]] and [[Rose]]. Light is good for a variety of reasons, while Rose is a good early game healer. However, when using [[Mana Orb|Orb]], it drops [[Mana Orb|Orb]] instead of [[Rose]]. *As Ladybugs only attack by ramming, the best strategy to defeat them is to either attack them from far away or slow them down with [[Pincer]] or [[Web]]. *You can also ram them, because they have average body damage and not a lot of health. *A [[Bone]] of one rarity higher than the Ladybug will take it down very quickly. === Super Ladybug === * In [[Garden]], the main danger when fighting a {{R|Super}} mob is not the actual mob, but the many {{R|Ultra}} mobs that spawn around the mob. When bubbling to the mob, it is advised to use [[Sponge]] and multiple Ultra healing petals, and have a [[Lotus]] equipped in case a [[Spider]] hits you. It is also advised to use Sponge when actually fighting the mob so you don't die if anything hits you. * If the mob spawns deep in Garden, it is advised to form a "megasquad", where a large group of players stay near each other and clear mobs. This increases the chance for everyone to survive. * Around 820,000 damage is required to get loot, so a good DPS build is recommended.
==Gallery== File:Ladybug (Common).png|{{Rarity|1}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Ladybug (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|2}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Ladybug (Rare).png|{{Rarity|3}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Ladybug (Epic).png|{{Rarity|4}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Ladybug (Legendary).png|{{Rarity|5}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Ladybug (Mythic).png|{{Rarity|6}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Ladybug (Ultra).png|{{Rarity|7}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Ladybug (Super).png|{{Rarity|8}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Ladybug (Unique).png|{{Rarity|9}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Ladybug.png|{{PAGENAME}}. File:Ladybug no spots.png|This Ladybug has no spots ==Trivia== *The rarest Ladybug possibly would have no spots on its shell, since spots on the shell are completely random. **This makes it one of the few mobs where every mob of its kind has a different design, the others being [[Hel Centipede]] and [[Worm]]. *Two other variants of Ladybug spawn, in the [[Ladybug (Desert)|Desert]] and [[Ladybug (Jungle)|Jungle]] respectively. All three of them are legacy florr mobs. *At one point, [[M28]] spawned one of these at the {{R|Super}} rarity in the [[Desert]] to troll players into believing that it was a Shiny Ladybug. (The difference is not noticeable in chat). *The more higher rarity of the Ladybug. More black spots are seen on the shell and some of the black spots appear to be bigger. This applies to all Ladybug variants (its variants are [[Ladybug (Jungle)|Dark Ladybug]] and [[Ladybug (Desert)|Yellow Ladybug]]). ** If the player equips Orb, mobs that drop Roses will be replaced by Orb drop. The highest rarity of Orb drops depends on the highest Orb rarity the player've put in their build. {{Mobs}} [[Category:Mobs]] [[Category:Ladybugs]] [[Category:Non-stationary mobs]] [[Category:Garden]] [[Category:Battle Ground]]

'''Desert Ladybug''' is a mob in florr.io that spawns in the [[Desert]]. It is a variant of [[Ladybug]] and is widely known for its prized drop, [[Yggdrasil]].

==Appearance==
The Shiny Ladybug looks like a normal [[Ladybug]], only with a yellow fill and dark yellow outline instead of the red theme of other Ladybugs.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Moves at the same speed as other variants of Ladybugs.

'''Behavior:''' Neutral, attacks when provoked.

==Spawning==
The Shiny Ladybug can spawn in [[Desert]]. There is no known Shiny Ladybug zone as of now (unlike the old world map where the Shiny Ladybug zone was D4). It spawns most frequently at the player spawn area (1%), however it mainly spawns at low rarities.

==Drops==

===[[Rose]]===
{{:Template:ID 1}}

===[[Dahlia]]===
{{:Template:ID 1 - U}}

===[[Yggdrasil]]===
{{:Template:ID 4 - L}}

==Strategy==
*The Shiny Ladybug is a very sought-after mob due to it's very rare drop, the [[Yggdrasil]].
*Use [[Antennae]] and [[Bubble|Bubbles]] to cover more ground.
*Kill mobs so new mobs spawn, with a higher chance of one being a Shiny Ladybug.
*If a {{Rarity|Legendary}}+ Shiny Ladybug spawns, hit it once with a {{Rarity|Mythic}}+ Missile and go around a corner to lose its aggro. If a {{Rarity|Mythic}}+ one spawns, hit it 3 times with a {{Rarity|Mythic}}+ Missile or once with an {{Rarity|Ultra}}+ Missile to guarantee loot. This makes it so others have a chance to get Yggdrasil as well.
*The best place to grind for Yggdrasil would be the Desert Box, as {{Rarity|Mythic}} Shiny Ladybugs have a better chance to spawn doe to the higher density. Also, it is 99.4% chance to drop at least {{Rarity|Legendary}} Yggdrasil.
*Moving around is recommended because you are more likely to find a Shiny Ladybug if you use [[Antennae]] or use the Antennae [[Talents|Talent]].

=== Super Shiny Ladybug ===

* The Super mob itself isn't as dangerous, but the Ultra mobs around it are. When bubbling to the mob, it is advised to use [[Sponge]] and multiple healing petals so that if something hits you, you won't die immediately.
* There most likely will  be a ton of people there, trying to get a high rarity [[Yggdrasil]].  
* It also most likely won't be lured as people would try to rush to it.
* To be able to loot, you obviously need a very high DPS, since only 25 people could loot.
* Follow the main group of people to ensure that you don't die.
* When killing the mob, only focus about dealing damage.
* Be aware that someone is going to troll, like using corruption,pushing people into the mob, luring Ultras over, or marking people. Be prepared.

==Trivia==
*Most people refer this mob as "Shiny Ladybug" or "Yellow Ladybug", either by Discord or in-game.
**Its description explains why this mob is referred as just "shiny".
*A Super Shiny Ladybug has spawned in the NA server in May 20, 2024, making it the very first, and real Super Shiny Ladybug to spawn.
*Shiny Ladybugs have the second highest XP Reward in the entire game, with a total of 17m at {{Rarity|Super}} rarity. [[Gambler]] has the highest, at 26m at {{Rarity|Super}} rarity.
*An {{Rarity|Ultra}} Shiny Ladybug has been found before in [[Ellne Pass]], the map that connected [[Centralia Fields/South|Centralia Fields 4]] to [[Ellne Jungle/Ellne Pass|Ellne Jungle 4]] in World Map before it was removed. The most common theory is that it was spawned by the developer, [[M28]], and didn't spawn naturally.
*On an unknown date, M28 made {{Rarity|Ultra}} and {{R|Super}} Shinies spawn naturally, but rarely. This made it much easier for players to obtain high rarities of [[Yggdrasil]].
*On June 17, 2024, M28 spawned 10 {{R|Super}} Shinies in the NA server, but none of them dropped any loot.

'''Ladybug''' is a mob in florr.io that spawns in the [[Jungle]]. It is a variant of [[Ladybug]] and is passive unless attacked.

==Appearance==
The Jungle variant of Ladybugs look like [[Ladybug]]s with a darker fill and lighter spots.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Movement speed is slow until it is attacked, before speeding up.

'''Behavior:''' Moves around slowly, moving a few tiles before changing directions until it gets provoked. When provoked, it targets the player.

==Spawning==
The Jungle variant of Ladybug spawns in [[Jungle]]. It used to spawn in [[PvP]] before it was removed.

==Drops==
===[[Dahlia]]===
{{:Ladybug (Jungle)/Drop Dahlia}}

===[[Yin Yang]]===
{{:Ladybug (Jungle)/Drop Yin Yang}}

==Strategy ==
It's best to attack this one when you are clear of any aggressive mobs which are nearby. These aren't much of a threat though, with a lower body damage than [[Leafbug]] so that even if one gets pushed into one of these by enemy projectiles they wouldn't take much damage. The best counter is using [[Bone]] of a higher rarity to attack.

You can also use the same strategies like the [[Ladybug|Garden Ladybug]]. You can use [[Iris|Irises]], [[Stinger|Stingers]], or standard DPS petals to take it down, but this may take more time.

==Trivia==
* Jungle Ladybugs, along with [[Centipede (Jungle)|Jungle Centipedes]], were the only mobs in [[Jungle]] that existed before it was added.
* Jungle Ladybugs used to spawn in [[Centralia Fields]], specifically [[Centralia Maze]], before the addition of the Jungle scenario on May 1st, 2023. Since then, they have moved to Jungle and exclusively spawn there.
* Jungle Ladybugs used to spawn in the ? zone in legacy florr. After the game was re-released, it was put in [[Garden]].

==Appearance==

You can use 1 [[Battery]], 1 [[Light Bulb]] and 1 [[Glass]] to assemble 2 Lasers via [[Assembler]] at any rarities.

===Purchasing===
On certain days, Laser can be purchased from the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|3}}
==Strategies==
*Laser is a very interesting and unique petal, good for use in all areas. Keep in mind that it cannot damage projectiles.
*Laser is best when paired with multiple others and a few [[Light|Lights]] to condense the Lasers together. This will make an extremely effective wall of death destroying all in its path. [[Yin Yang]] can also be equipped and unequipped to make this even better. 
*Be aware that Laser is breakable, so make sure to keep Laser from the mob a certain distance.
*Using a Laser DPS build can help you loot all Super mobs in Factory, as long as you can the Lasers manage to survive the harsh condition of the subbiome. (Lasers must be Ultra+ rarity)
===Obtaining===
*Use [[Sponge]] with [[Rubber]] to completely nullify the laser from [[Crab/Mecha|Mecha Crabs]]. Shred the Mecha Crabs with your attacking petals.
{{Zone|Garden}}
*It can be used to fight mobs such as [[Spider|Spiders]] to deal as much damage as possible, but it is inefficient to [[Hornet|Hornets]] due to being a ranged mob. However, it can perform well if the Hornets are alrady aggro on someone else.
*You can use Laser to keep dealing damage to [[Ant Hole]] without getting distracted by Ants spawned from it.
{{Zone|Desert}}
*[[Scorpion]] has fast movement while aggro on you or other players, which is hard to keep the Laser beam on it. However, this is easy if you are skilled.
*[[Beetle]], [[Sandstorm]] and [[Centipede (Desert)|Desert Centipede]] are actually doable if you can manage them.
{{Zone|Ocean}}
*Laser can't do the job against [[Bubble]] like other lightning petals, but its physical damage can damage them.
*Using this against anything else in here is a bad idea, since they attack either from very far or very near.
{{Zone|Jungle}}
*Laser will ignore the Armor of [[Leafbug]]s.
*Using this and Light is quite good and safe here, since nothing here is faster than you. As said above, do not use this against [[Wasp|Wasps]] and [[Mantis|Mantises]].
{{Zone|Hel}}
===PvE===
*This petal can be simply shredded by mobs. Don't use it here.
===PvP: Using===
*Lasers work badly against other players as Laser don't have enough time to deal significant damage to most players. Also, it being breakable is an advantage for them to damage the user.etal.
===PvP: Against===
*Use [[Sponge]] with [[Rubber]] to completely nullify the laser's damage.
{{Zone|Ant Hell}}
*Strategies exclusively for the Ant Hell zone.
*This section can also be used to describe [[Ant Hole]], [[Fire Ant Burrow]] and [[Termite Mound]] fights.
{{Zone|Sewers}}
*Pair Laser with [[Cogwheel]] and [[Yin Yang]] to hold it in place, and use [[Pincer]] with [[Jelly]] to prevent [[Fly|Flies]] from breaking Laser.
==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">

==Trivia==
*The laser line at the {{R|Legendary}} and {{R|Ultra}} Laser icons are poorly visible due to having a similar color as the background.
*Laser is one of the few lightning petals that doesn't bounce, the others being [[Blueberries]] and [[Mjölnir]].
*Laser has the same reload as [[Root]].
*Laser is the only petal that has its icon pass through the petal frame.
*It's the only petal that can deal both lightning and physical damage.
*Laser does the same damage as Mjölnir (lightning damage) but with a far less reload and health.
*Laser can also do normal damage but that itself isn't very good.
*Effectively, Laser is stronger than Mjölnir since its damage does not depend on reload making it one of the strongest petals.
*Laser basically doesn't need any support if it can remain unbroken and other damage petals can be added alongside it. 
*Duped Laser can do 656100 damage per second (lightning damage) making it one of the strongest duped {{Rarity|9}} petals there is.  Only Mjölnir can compare to it.

:''This page is about the normal Leaf. For the [[magic petal]] variant, see [[/Magic/|Magic Leaf]].''

'''Leaf''' is a legacy petal in florr.io added on the May 14th, 2020 update. It heals the user passively, healing a set amount of HP each second. The Leaf is dropped by [[Baby Ant]]s, [[Centipede]]s, [[Leafbug]]s and [[Worker Ant]]s.

==Appearance==
Leaf's appearance is true to its name: it's a leaf shaped petal, somewhat resembling an oval, but is pointed on one end. It is green with a darker green outline. In addition, a dark green stripe is located in the middle that runs down most of the length of the leaf. A dark green rectangle (the stem) is located at the rounded end of the oval.

==Function==

Leaf's special ability is healing the user, much like a [[Rose]]. However, instead of healing in bursts (instantly), it heals passively, which means that it will heal the user a set amount per second. The Heal stat shows how much HP the Leaf heals every second: for example, {{Rarity|Mythic}} Leaves will heal you by 243 HP every second.

==Obtainment==
Note: Regular leaf may be unobtainable from certain mobs while [[Mana Orb]] is equipped.

===[[Baby Ant]]===
{{:Baby Ant/Drop Leaf}}

===[[Centipede]]===
{{:Centipede/Drop Leaf}}

===[[Leafbug]]===
{{:Leafbug/Drop Leaf}}

===[[Worker Ant]]===
{{:Worker Ant/Drop Leaf}}

===Purchasing===
On certain days, Leaf can be purchased in the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|3}}

==Strategy==
*The Leaf is the weakest but most reliable healing petal. Simply put, despite its healing stat not being as useful as any other healing petal, it has good stats all around.
*In [[Garden]], it will do very well against [[Spider|Spiders]], [[Centipede|Centipedes]] and [[Ladybug|Ladybugs]], but struggle a lot against [[Hornet|Hornets]]. It's not recommended in this biome, as all the mobs Leaf clears with ease, [[Bone]] does too (and better at it), the healing from Leaf is outclassed by every other healing petal, and [[Wing]] does considerably better against Hornets as well. The same could be said about [[Jungle]], but with its mob counterparts.
*It's a good choice in [[Desert]], as Leaf will multi-hit [[Beetle|Beetles]] of rarities below it, and it will deal a lot of damage against [[Scorpion|Scorpions]], [[Soldier Fire Ant|Fire Ants]], and [[Centipede (Desert)|Centipedes]]. The same goes for [[Ocean]], just with its mobs instead. All of the mobs there have relatively low body damage, so Leaf has no problems in [[Ocean]].

===Obtaining===
*[[Baby Ant]]s are the easiest mob to kill due to their passiveness on any rarity. They spawn very frequently from [[Ant Hole]]s and in [[Ant Hell]].
*[[Centipede]]s also aren't hard to kill, their only difficulty is having multiple segments that can swarm you if you're unprepared. Their segments can be used to get more Leaves at once. [[Battery]] and [[Lightning]] will help you damage multiple segments.
*[[Leafbug]]s are the hardest, because Jungle is a pretty hard scenario to thrive in without the right strategies, especially due to [[Mantis]]es and [[Wasp]]s. They also have Armor, which means you either need to bring [[Bur]] or hard-hitting petals to kill them.
*[[Worker Ant]]s are a bit more troublesome than Centipedes, but the scenarios they spawn in are usually pretty easy to deal with.

==Gallery==
<gallery spacing="small" widths="185">
File:Leaf (Common).png|{{R|1}} Leaf. (unobtainable)
File:Leaf (Unusual).png|{{R|2}} Leaf.
File:Leaf (Rare).png|{{R|3}} Leaf.

File:Leaf (Super).png|{{R|8}} Leaf.
File:Leaf (Unique).png|{{R|9}} Leaf.
File:Leaf.png|Leaf.
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*Before the December 29th, 2022 update, {{Rarity|Mythic}} and {{Rarity|Ultra}} Leaves used to have a larger size visually.
*It is possible that this petal was inspired [https://www.reddit.com/r/florrio/comments/fd9wq1/leaf_petal_suggestion/ by this idea].
*Leaf was the second healing petal added to the game, after [[Rose]]. Both petals were the only healing petals on legacy florr.io.
*Its healing stat appears to be the weakest among all other healing petals, likely to balance the constant healing and excellent offensive capabilities.
*The [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Leaf Leaf] petal was added to the game after [[M28]]'s activity on legacy florr was scarce. This made its use skyrocket instantly after it was added.
*Leaf and [[Fangs]] are the only healing petals that can both attack and defend effectively. Every other healing petal is purely defensive or useless for offense, like [[Yucca]]..
*Leaf used to be able to heal friendly mobs (observed on [[Ant Egg]], [[Beetle Egg]], [[Stick]] and [[Digger]]s). This let the Leaf act like a [[Rose]] petal, but only towards mobs. Healing was the same as a Rose. This was removed in an update, likely to prevent players from healing Diggers constantly to prevent their death. This made it viable in DPS scenarios as now it wouldn't be consumed by friendly mobs from another player.
*Legacy florr.io's Leaf used to be dropped at {{Rarity|Unusual}} Rarity (like current Leaf's first Rarity) by the same mobs that drop this currently (except the [[Leafbug]], which didn't exist back then).
*This petal has the most variations in the game, 4. Leaf's variations are this, [[Leaf/Magic|Magic Leaf]], [[Dead Leaf]] and [[Golden Leaf]]. 
**The only runner-up to this petal is [[Stinger]], with 3 variations.

Magic Leaf is dropped by [[Baby Ant]]s, [[Bush]]es, [[Centipede]]s, [[Golden Leafbug]]s, [[Leafbug]]s and [[Worker Ant]]s when [[Mana Orb]] is equipped.

On certain days, Magic Leaf can be purchased in the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''{{Petal Price|3}}

==Obtaining==
*Defeat [[Baby Ant]]s, [[Bush]]es, [[Centipede]]s, [[Golden Leafbug]]s, [[Leafbug]]s or [[Worker Ant]]s while having [[Mana Orb]] equipped to obtain Magic Leaf. Baby Ants and Worker Ants are commonly found in [[Ant Hell|Normal Ant Hell]], which is the most efficient place to farm this petal.

==Strategy==
*Despite having a lower Mana regeneration rate than Mana Orb of the same rarity (assuming {{Rarity|Legendary}}+ [[Talents/Reload|Reload talent]]), Magic Leaf not only generates Mana consistently, which works well with low rarity [[Bubble/Magic|Magic Bubbles]], but also doubles as a damage petal, giving it some offense value in combat.

The '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is a mob in florr.io. It spawns frequently in [[Jungle]] and has high armor stat, much like [[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble]]. 

==Appearance==
The '''Leafbug''' uses a similar body to the [[Scorpion]] except its body is changed into a leaf, and has eight legs instead of six and uses the pincers from Beetle.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Slow, medium when provoked.

'''Behavior:''' Neutral, attacks the player if provoked.

Along with [[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble mobs]], and previously shell mobs, Leafbugs have a much higher armor stat than all mobs, make it able to tank more damage than normal.

==Spawning==
The {{PAGENAME}} spawns anywhere in [[Jungle]].

==Drops==
===[[Leaf]]===
{{ID 6 U}}

===[[Root]]===
{{ID 6}}

==Strategy==
*This one has great armor. This factor, combined with its [[beetle]]-like damage make this mob dangerous to provoke. The best way to deal with it would be by hitting it with long-ranged petals, and then retreating with [[Mysterious Powder|Powder]] or [[Bubble|Bubbles]]. 
**Using Eggs on the Leafbug will make it come after your pets, and possibly making it go into your teammates, killing them if they don't have enough damage. Beware that only [[Beetle Egg|Beetle Eggs]] and [[Stick|Sticks]] work, [[Ant Egg|Ant Eggs]] of the same rarity as the Leafbug deals no damage to it.
**If one is scared on provoking it, you can use low damage petals to still do damage to others, but not Leafbug. However, this may cause complications with other mobs
*Ram builds are not as effective against this mob as they are against many others, this is because of the Leafbug's insane armor and high body damage (triple the average) If you do ram, make sure to use [[Talents/Body Toxicity|the Body Toxicity Talent]] so that your ramming will also deal Poison damage, which bypasses Armor.
*Because of its high armor, using petals that deal much damage is almost required to deal damage to the mob.
**[[Bur]] can decrease its Armor.
**Its Armor is useless against poison and lightning, meaning that petals such as [[Iris]] and [[Lightning]] will always damage it regardless of the Armor. [[Dice]]'s critical hit will also bypass the Armor. Also note [[Claw]] but it will stop bypassing armor after its hp is low.
*[[Salt]] can damage it, as its body damage is always higher than the Armor. But due to the fact that [[Salt]] don't stack anymore, this strategy isn't as useful as it used to be.

*Leafbugs have the highest armor in the game, with {{R|Super}} variants having over 22k.

*When you encounter a Leafbug, it has 8 legs but in the gallery it has 6 legs presented.

The '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is a mob in florr.io that spawns in the Ocean. They ram into flowers with alarming speeds, dealing damage every second until the flower is dead.

==Appearance==
They have a similar appearance to [[Baby Ant|Baby Ants]], except their body is lengthened. Like every other mob, leeches grow bigger with every rarity.

== Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Leeches are extremely fast, moving much faster than players when they are aggroed. They slowly move around until a flower is in sight. Then, Leeches will speedily move to them.

'''Behavior:''' Moves slowly until it spots a player. When it spots a player, it will move towards the player with extreme speed, attaching onto the player and dealing damage every second, while gaining health. 

*

Leeches ram into flowers and "suck their blood", draining flower health. Even though leeches can only "suck blood" once per second, ramming into the side of the {{R|Legendary}}+ leech can deal extreme damage. 

==Spawning==
The Leech spawns everywhere in [[Ocean]]. It spawns the most in the area linked to the entrance of the Crab Kingdom, in the bottom right corner.

==Drops==

===[[Fangs]]===
{{:Leech/Drop Fang}}

===[[Faster]]===
{{:Leech/Drop Faster}}

==Strategy==
*Leeches have segments that share health, [[Lightning]] petals can strike a part of the leech and chain between them, dealing vastly more damage than a regular petal would. (For example, an {{R|Ultra}} Lightning would deal nearly 70k damage in one hit) [[Third Eye/Magic|Magic Eye]] can one-shot Leeches if the user saves sufficient mana.
*When Leeches are attacking a flower, the flower can equip [[Cutter]] and/or [[Battery]] and ram to fight back. However, healing petals are required as the leech will still damage the flower.
*Even though {{R|Common}} - {{R|Mythic}} Leeches are not very tough, {{R|Ultra}} Leeches can kill anyone alone that is using a regular Ocean build; In return, it would be best to use mass-healing petals that are capable of being used in the defense position, alongside a few [[Fangs]] to heal back some of the massive amounts of health that would be lost in the fight. Using a [[Disc]] also helps the flower taking less damage and therefore less healing required for recovery.
*If there aren't any other mobs (beside Leeches) chasing you, it would be best to stay still and let the damage be done. This works best if there is another player nearby to help you destroy the leech(es).
*Using egg petals can be good when a lot of leeches are chasing you as it can make some of the leeches targeting them for a moment. Also, it can attack the leech from a far distance.
* Do not use any petals that deal knockback, as you will knock the body segments of the leech onto yourself. Unlike the head, which is attached to the player, the body segments have no delay for damage and can deal damage extremely quickly.
*Do not use Blood Stingers, or the self-damage will kill you faster.
*For Ultra Leech, use [[Basil|Basils]] and {{Rarity|Mythic}} Fangs to defeat it. Use at least 4 Mythic Fangs. Using Yucca/Starfish can also help.
*Try to avoid the random mobs when killing {{Rarity|Mythic}}+ Leeches. They can knock the body segments onto you as well. A good mob to avoid is Shell, as they spawn in the same zone as Leeches do. This is no longer true when mobs can go through Leeches now.
*Sponge and Fangs are a good counter against Leeches.
*Leech’s sucking attack counts as physical damage, hence a high amount of [[Root]] stacks are a counter to it (a stack is regenerated every 2 seconds and leeches suck every second)
