==Trivia==
* When the world map was introduced, Leeches did not spawn until the 14th February 2023 update.
* Leeches used to drop loot for all of their segments, like [[Centipede]].
* Leeches are the [[Ocean]] equivalent to [[Evil Centipede|Jungle Centipedes]], in terms of behavior.
*Its speed when attacking is slightly faster than that of the [[Desert Centipede]].
*Leeches have one of the farthest aggro ranges. This can be dangerous as an {{R|Ultra}} Leech could spot you from far away and launch a surprise attack on you. You can use [[Antennae]] to be prepared for incoming leeches, or [[Poo]] for reducing leech aggro range.
*{{R|Common}} and {{Rarity|Unusual}} Leeches used to be very rare, but after the April 13th 2024 update, they now can spawn frequently.

title: Level
content:
'''Levels''' are a measure of how much XP you have accumulated. 

When you gain levels, you gain [[Talents|Talent Points]]. 1 TP is given each time a player reaches a new level. If you reach a level that ends with 5, you will get 2 TP instead and if it ends with 0, you get 10 TP. Your base health and body damage also increases as your level increases. Beside TP, your base stats are also slightly increase with each level and scales exponentially like the XP required to level up. 

To gain levels, you have to kill mobs to gain XP, which, when ascending to the next level, rewards you with [[Talents|Talent Points]]. 
Levels scale exponentially, meaning you will have to kill more mobs to advance to the next level. The better drop rates a mob has, the more XP it will give. A mob that has a rarity of Mythic may give around 30 times the XP of a mob of rarity Legendary in certain cases. Note that mobs of the rarity common and unusual both give 1 or 2<ref>[[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble]], [[Gambler]] and [[Termite Mound]] mob</ref> XP. You can also absorb petals using [[Crystal]], with the number of XP gained increases how high is the petal's rarity.

Currently, the level cap is level 311. However, there are no players at that level or even near that level. [[File:Level Colors.png|alt=A florr showcasing the way level coloring works via an alt. The petal slots on the bottom are the petals being used. In one, it shows one mythic petal and 9 common petals yet the level is still mythic. In another, it shows a legendary air and 9 epic petals yet the level is still legendary. |thumb|A player showcasing the way level coloring works via an alt. The petal slots on the bottom are the petals being used.]]
Somewhere around the January 28th update, Levels thankfully started being displayed next to a the player's name. This shows as a small indicator below the health bar. The color of the indicator is dictated via [[petals]]. The highest rarity petal in a player's equipped slot dictates the player's level color. (E.g. Epic Petal = Purple). However, this does not apply if said petal is in only your inventory.

Level colors cannot be viewed by the player themselves, only by other players. However, since your level color is just the highest rarity petal you currently have equipped, your level color will be the same as your best petal.

You can check your level below the player name and health UI, which is at the top left corner of the screen.

There are also several milestones for levels. The different rarities of the achievement "Growing Up" can be obtained at level 5, 10, 20, 35, 55, 75, and 100. These achievements start from Unusual rarity and make their way up to Super. 
Another milestone for leveling up is at level 75. Starting from level 75, the player will be able to see when a Super mob spawns. Text messages like "A Super Soldier Fire Ant has [[Builds - Joke Builds|spawned]]!" or special spawn messages like "You hear lightning strikes coming from a far distance..." (for [[Jellyfish]]) tells the player where the Super mob is. Super mob texts can also appear if a Super mob spawns in a different area than the player. The text will then say "somewhere" at the end of it to let the player know it isn't in that area (e.g. the player is in Desert and a Super Bubble spawns).

Some of the best places to gain levels can be debated about. Ant Hell is an easy way to accumulate levels and petals. You won't be able to get kill credit on a Queen for some time, but after a bit of grinding, it shouldn't be a problem. If [[Antennae|Ant]] Hell is too easy and you feel you aren't moving forward anymore, go to Fire Ant Hell where all the mobs have double the Damage, as well as it being a place to farm Fire Ant drops.

==Health==
The amount of health a player has is dependent on their level. The game uses this formula for calculating health:
200*(243^0.01)^(min(n,75)-1), where n is the level

*A level 1 flower has 200 hp
*A level 75 flower with no health talent has 11651 hp
*You do not gain extra hp from levels past lvl 75

The graph below shows how much hp you have with no health talent at a certain level, where the x axis is the level and the y axis is the health.

[[File:HP_Scaling_Graph.jpeg|900px]]


== Trivia ==

* Levels were originally obtained by absorbing [[petals]] until April 21, 2024, where it was removed, but was later re-added on December 13, 2025 with a new absorbing mechanic.
* The amount of XP a mob gives is 20% of what you get from absorbing the average drops of a mob.
* Until the February 10th 2023 update, levels ending in 0 awarded 5 SP (Skill Points, the initial name for Talent Points), and levels ending in 5 awarded 3 SP.
**When the Skills update was originally released, levels ending in 0 awarded 3 SP.
*You could fool players into thinking you are stronger than you are by using any petal of a higher rank than the rest of your petals. This also works vice-versa, by equipping low rarity petals in your equip slots, then putting your powerful petals in your inventory slots. Note that your level color changes '''immediately''' after swapping, regardless of reload time. After an unknown update, petals can now show their rarities on other players by holding "G" on your keyboard and Air now makes you larger.
*Back in 2022 before a world map and when you could only do waves, equipping a common petal gave you a yellow player level color even though common was green. This was quickly patched days later.

title: Light
content:
{{Petal Template Infobox | image = File:Light (Common).png|C File:Light (Unusual).png|U File:Light (Rare).png|R File:Light (Epic).png|E File:Light (Legendary).png|L File:Light (Mythic).png|M File:Light (Ultra).png|Ul File:Light (Super).png|S File:Light (Unique).png|Uq | description = Weaker than most petals, but recharges very quickly. | first-rarity = {{Rarity|Common}} | damage = {{Petal Scale 2|13|1|2|2|3|3|5|5|5}} | health = {{Petal Scale 2|5|1|2|2|3|3|5|5|5}} | reload-time = 0.8s | stackability = Stackable | dropped-by = *[[Baby Ant]] *[[Baby Fire Ant]] *[[Ladybug]] | special = Weaker than normal, but recharges quicker.
Higher rarities add more Lights to itself.
Affected by Duplicator. }} '''Light''' is a legacy petal in florr.io. It's a petal weaker than [[Basic]] but has a lower respawn timer. The Light is dropped by [[Baby Ant]]s, [[Baby Fire Ant]]s and [[Ladybug]]s. ==Appearance== Light identical to the Basic and Faster petal. However, its size is half as small as the Basic. Light itself is a small, white circle with a light gray outline with almost no details in it. The number of Lights composing the whole petal can increase depending on the Rarity. They can go from a single one ({{Rarity|Common}} Rarity) to two of them ({{Rarity|Unusual}} and {{Rarity|Rare}} Rarities) to three of them ({{Rarity|Epic}} and {{Rarity|Legendary}}) to finally five of them ({{Rarity|Mythic}} and above). The Duplicator [[Talents|Talent]] also works with this, adding up to three of them ({{Rarity|Unusual}} and {{Rarity|Rare}}), four of them ({{Rarity|Epic}} and {{Rarity|Legendary}}) and finally six of them ({{Rarity|Mythic}} and above). ==Function== {|class="article-table" |+Petal Stats ![[Rarity]] !Obtainable? !Damage !Health !Respawn !Petal Count |- |{{Rarity|1}} |rowspan="9"|Yes |13 |5 |rowspan="9"|0.75 seconds |1 |- |{{Rarity|2}} |19.5 (x2) |7.5 (x2) |2 |- |{{Rarity|3}} |58.5 (x2) |22.5 (x2) |2 |- |{{Rarity|4}} |117 (x3) |45 (x3) |3 |- |{{Rarity|5}} |351 (x3) |135 (x3) |3 |- |{{Rarity|6}} |631.8 (x5) |243 (x5) |5 |- |{{Rarity|7}} |1895.4 (x5) |729 (x5) |5 |- |{{Rarity|8}} |5686.2 (x5) |2187 (x5) |5 |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} |17058.6 (x5) |6561 (x5) |5 |} Light's function is pretty much like the Basic's, except that it sacrifices damage and durability in favour of a quicker respawn. As shown above, higher rarities of Light will add another Light for the whole petal. The number of Light petals in a rarity is represented by the multiplier number in the Function table, with (x2) being 2 Light petals, (x3) being 3 Light petals, (x5) being 5 Light petals and no multipliers being 1 Light petal. The Duplicator Talent will add another Light petal to those with 2 or more multipliers. ==Obtainment== ===[[Baby Ant]]=== {{:Baby Ant/Drop Light}} ===[[Baby Fire Ant]]=== {{:Baby Fire Ant/Drop Light}} ===[[Ladybug]]=== {{:Ladybug/Drop Light}} ===Purchasing=== On certain days, Light can be purchased in the [[Shop]]. ''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]'' {{Petal Price|4}} ==Strategy == *Light is an all-around petal that can serve for practically anything. It might look weaker than [[Sand]] at first, but Light will actually hold their ground better than Sand on a multitude of occasions. Alternatively, they can be used together (especially with [[Faster]] and other petals to make the infamous Deathwheel build) as another good strategy. Lights will mostly be found on such builds with such petals, but in some cases, they may appear on their own, generally at {{Rarity|Super}} tier as by then the petal is strong enough to be used by itself. * All mobs have Armor. Light will deal pretty low damage, lower than most mobs' Armor. Try to bring [[Bur]] with you so that the Light can deal damage, specially against [[Leafbug]]s. *Lights can be used to clump up petals for more DPS. This can be helpful for aiming with shootable petals like [[Missile]], or [[Yin Yang]] strategies. *Lights are far more useful when they're at {{Rarity|Mythic}}+ Rarity, especially with the Duplicator Talent, having up to 6 Lights in one single petal. Lower rarities are not advised to use early on. *You can also use Lights in [[Bubble]] builds, because they will condense your Bubbles together resulting in more of a boost. Additionally, you can use them to kill weak mobs in your path! ===Obtaining=== *[[Baby Ant]]s and [[Baby Fire Ant]]s are fully passive if they don't hatch from their [[Ant Egg (Mob)|respective]] [[Fire Ant Egg|eggs]]. Still, they're pretty easy to fend off, but Baby Fire Ants may be harder to take since they do double damage against you and your petals. *[[Ladybug]]s are arguably one of the easiest passive-aggressive mobs in the game, as it doesn't have any special abilities or segments, or pretty much anything. They're a bit durable, but that's it. You also get [[Rose]]s from them. *Try looking after [[Ant Hole]]s. They spawn Baby Ants frequently on their early stages, which could easily give you Lights. {{Zone|Garden}} *Lights on their own are not recommended for Garden, as they offer no defense to the player. Fasters are advised to increase their efficiency, alongside strong healing. *Light, unless on a Deathwheel build (which is still not very reliable in this case) struggles to go against [[Hornet]] missiles. Bringing [[Plank]] with you is advised. *The Deathwheel build will quickly shred through any Ant Holes, [[Centipede]]s and [[Spider]]s with ease. It's also a decent ground for killing [[Bee]]s, [[Bumble Bee]]s and [[Rock (Mob)|Rocks]], despite other petals and builds being better for that stuff. Caution is always advised for {{Rarity|Epic}}+ Bees and any rarity Bumble Bees, as their behavior is pretty harmful to you. {{Rarity|Super}} Rocks may also eject smaller Rocks onto you which could end your run. {{Zone|Desert}} *Light is fairly usable in Desert. However, caution is needed, as [[Beetle]]s and [[Desert Centipede]]s are very quick and [[Scorpion]]s have strong stingers with strong poison. Having good dodging skills is strongly advised, just as bringing good healing to prevent Scorpions from depleting your HP in an instant. *Lights can shred through [[Soldier Fire Ant]], as they won't suffer much from their double damage statistics. *[[Sandstorm]]s, while pretty deadly (specially if they ram you without a [[Disc]]), can also be taken out quickly if you manage to stay away from it. [[Third Eye]] further improves the strategy. * This can help killing [[Cactus (Mob)|Cacti]] easily, as their DPS can shred through its high hitpoints. {{Zone|Ocean}} *The Light's presence in Ocean mainly consists of Deathwheel as usual. This is primarily useful for shredding [[Starfish (Mob)|Starfish]] before they're able to heal (though it's still extremely hard to do without [[Dandelion]]s), but can also be used against [[Leech]]es once they get too close to you. They are mildly good against [[Crab]]s. Do not use against [[Shell (Mob)|Shells]], as their armor is too high for Light to deal any damage, even with [[Bur]]. *[[Jellyfish]] will give you a hard time if you don't use something like [[Rubber]] or [[Salt]] against them. However, Lights will clump up shootable petals like [[Carrot]] and [[Missile]] so that the reduced distance between each other will help them hit the same target (which is great against Jellyfish). {{Zone|Ant Hell}} *Lights can easily shred Ant Holes, [[Fire Ant Burrow]]s and [[Termite Mound]]s as well as all Ant Hells. Their extreme DPS if used correctly won't give any Ants a chance to fight, and many Termites will be easily killed at once due to their Psionic Connection spreading damage against any close Termites. *Their DPS is also pretty good for looting the Ant Hell bosses for a chance at getting their respective rare loots (or just XP). *Light works well enough in Ant Hell. There are better options, but this petal is good. {{Zone|Hel}} ===Using === *Lights are a very solid DPS petal, specially when they're found with Deathwheel builds. However, since they lack burst damage or being able to shield you from petals, [[Heavy]] or [[Stinger]] could be used to deal with other players better. * Due to both Lights and Deathwheels' quick respawn and high DPS characteristics, they can be used to exhaust durable petals, glass petals and absorbers like [[Sponge]] - but this is not suggested due to their small hitbox and the existence of many better petals for this purpose. * They can be used to wear out the dangerous [[Hel Beetle]]s, [[Hel Centipede]]s and [[Hel Spider]]s specially due to their close combat characteristic. [[Gambler]]s and [[Hel Wasp]]s may resist more against this petal due to their other abilities. *Be warned that to make efficient use of Light for DPS, you would need many [[Faster|Fasters]] and a [[Bur]], which would cripple the amount of healing slots available to the player. === Against=== *Countering the Light and the Deathwheel with a Salt is pretty efficient as the Light user is likely to get pretty damaged from damaging you. Be sure to bring [[Basil]]s and [[Yucca]]s to better resist their DPS. *Heavies, Missiles and other petals that can bypass it and deal good damage to the flower are also advised to put the Light user in risk. *Ram builds may struggle to deal with this build due to them being constantly hit by the Deathwheel. {{Zone|Jungle}} *Lights won't function as well as other places due to the presence of three pretty annoying mobs: [[Leafbug]], [[Mantis]] and [[Wasp]]. **Leafbugs are known for their Armor stat. This can specially hurt your Lights' performance, so either switch this petal or bring in [[Bur]] with you. ** Mantises shoot 3 Pea projectiles in a rapid succession. They usually pierce through your Lights and heavily damage you. [[Jelly]] or [[Plank]] is advised. ** Wasps are highly dangerous, not only because their missiles are strong, but they are also more unpredictable. Try using [[Third Eye]] to combat them. *Despite said mobs, Lights can also hold their ground, whether it's against [[Ladybug (Jungle)|Dark Ladybug]]s, [[Evil Centipede]]s, [[Firefly|Fireflies]] or [[Termite Mound]]s. Their DPS will do enough to shred through all of the above. {{Zone|Sewers}} *Lights can prove to be very effective since the mobs are pretty frail, but [[Roach]]es and [[Spider]]s can prove to be major dangers to you due to their speed.However, a pincer and light deathwheel can counter this, as pincers will slow the mob down, the light will hit roaches so much they become slow, as their speed scales with HP, and spiders are fast but not as much as roaches, this way you can easily out speed it by hitting it with pincer, and lights dps will shred the mob. *They can also help killing a [[Moth]] before they escape from your petal orbit range. ==Gallery== File:Light (Common).png|{{Rarity|Common}} Light. File:Light (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|Unusual}} Light. File:Light (Rare).png|{{Rarity|Rare}} Light. File:Light (Epic).png|{{Rarity|Epic}} Light. File:Light (Legendary).png|{{Rarity|Legendary}} Light. File:Light (Mythic).png|{{Rarity|Mythic}} Light. File:Light (Ultra).png|{{Rarity|Ultra}} Light. File:Light (Super).png|{{Rarity|Super}} Light. File:Light (Unique).png|{{Rarity|Unique}} Light. File:Light.png|Light. File:Light with Duplicator 3.png|{{Rarity|Epic}} and {{Rarity|Legendary}} Light when the Duplicator Talent is active. File:Light with Duplicator.png|{{Rarity|Mythic}}+ Light when the Duplicator Talent is active. == Trivia== *Light WOD used to be one of the most powerful WOP followed by Sand WOD. However, after the armor on all mobs update, the build is seemed useless. *Light, [[Faster]] and [[Heavy]] are petals named after adjectives, rather than substantives. They are the only petals to have that characteristic, and all three are from legacy florr.io. **In legacy florr, the same petals have that same characteristic: [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Fast Fast] (renamed to Light), Faster, [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Heaviest Heaviest] (renamed to Heavy) and [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Heavy Heavy] (technically removed if you don't count the new Heavy). *Light is one of the petals have the most petals of any petal in the game, with 5 Lights at {{Rarity|Mythic}}+ Rarity (up to 6 with the Duplicator [[Talents|Talent]]), Alongside the [[Stinger]], which has 5 Stingers at {{Rarity|Ultra}}+ Rarity (6 with the Duplicator talent) ** The only petal to technically break this record is [[Clover]], which has 6 clover leaves at {{Rarity|Ultra}}+ Rarity without the Duplicator Talent, though they are in one unit. *In legacy florr.io, Lights were three petals that were functionally identical to Light: Fast (dropped at {{Rarity|Common}} Rarity), Twin (dropped at {{Rarity|Unusual}} Rarity) and Triplet (dropped at {{Rarity|Epic}} Rarity). They acted the same as modern {{Rarity|Common}}, {{Rarity|Unusual}}-{{Rarity|Rare}} and {{Rarity|Epic}}-{{Rarity|Legendary}} Rarity Lights, and were eventually merged into one lineage (Light) once florr was rereleased. Fasters acted as a Rare version of Fasts. **2-in-1 Lights starting at {{Rarity|Unusual}} Rarity and 3-in-1 Lights starting at {{Rarity|Epic}} Rarity is reminiscent of said Twin and Triplet petals. **Fasts used to drop from [[Baby Ant]]s, [[Bee]]s, [[Centipede]]s, [[Ladybug]]s, [[Rock (Mob)|Rocks]] (ironically) and [[Worker Ant]]s. Twins used to drop from all of the above, minus Rocks and plus [[Queen Ant]]s, [[Ladybug (Desert)|Shiny Ladybugs]] and [[Soldier Ant]]s. Triplets used to drop from Baby Ants, [[Beetle]]s, Centipedes, [[Evil Centipede]]s, [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Massive_Beetle Massive Beetles] (old and big variations of Beetles), [[Ladybug (Jungle)|Dark Ladybugs]], Queen Ants, Shiny Ladybugs, Soldier Ants and Worker Ants. **'''Twin''' and '''Triplet''' were references to how many Fasts (currently Lights) said petal currently had, with Twin being two and Triplet being three. The Community sometimes still refers to {{Rarity|Unusual}}-{{Rarity|Rare}} and {{Rarity|Epic}}-{{Rarity|Legendary}} Lights as Twins and Triplets due to that, even going as far to call '''Pentuplet''' or '''Quintuplet''' {{Rarity|Mythic}}+ Lights, '''Quadruplet''' for Duplicated {{Rarity|Epic}}-{{Rarity|Legendary}} Lights and '''Hexuplet''' or '''Sextuplet''' for Duplicated {{Rarity|Mythic}}+ Lights. {{Petals}} [[zh:轻]] [[Category:Petals]] [[Category:Quick-Respawn]] [[Category:Common+]] [[Category:Garden]] [[Category:Ant Hole]] [[Category:Ant Hell]] [[Category:Fire Ant Hell]] [[Category:Assembling]] [[Category:Fire Ants]] [[Category:Ladybugs]][[Category:DPS Petals]] [[Category:Baby Ants]] [[Category:Affected by Duplicator Talent]] [[Category:Petals from Legacy Florr]]

title: Light Bulb
content:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Bulb}} {{Petal Template Infobox |name = Light Bulb |image = File:Bulb (Common).png|C File:Bulb (Unusual).png|U File:Bulb (Rare).png|R File:Bulb (Epic).png|E File:Bulb (Legendary).png|L File:Bulb (Mythic).png|M File:Bulb (Ultra).png|Ul File:Bulb (Super).png|S File:Bulb (Unique).png|Uq |description = A shiny lightbulb. Draws aggro from mobs. |first-rarity = {{Rarity|Common}} |damage = {{Petal Scale|5}} |health = {{Petal Scale|10}} |radius = {{Infobox Colors Empty|300|600|900|1200|1500|1800|2100|2400|2700}} |reload-time = 1.0s + 0.5s |stackability = Unstackable |dropped-by = *[[Firefly]] *[[Moth]] |special = Attracts mobs to the location of its user, regardless of their behavior.
Doesn't aggro stationary mobs, [[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble mob]] or [[Sandstorm]]. }} '''Light Bulb''', shortened in-game to just '''Bulb''', is a [[petal]] in [https://florr.io florr.io]. Its function consists of attracting most nearby mobs. Their behavior doesn't matter, only if they're sentient or not. Bulb is dropped by [[Firefly|Fireflies]] and [[Moth]]s. ==Appearance== Light Bulb consists of a cartoonish lightbulb appearance: it is circular with a dark gray handle with even darker borders. The lightbulb is yellow-colored, indicating that it's emitting light, and there are white lines zigzagging around inside the lightbulb, starting and ending on the base. During usage, Light Bulb will emit a yellow glow around its center, constantly increasing and decreasing in radius, bearing the same radius as [[Uranium]]'s green glow. ==Function== {|class="article-table" |+Petal Stats ![[Rarity]] !Obtainable? !Damage !Health !Respawn !Radius |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |rowspan="9"|Yes |5 |10 |rowspan="9"|1.0 + 0.5 seconds |300 |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |15 |30 |600 |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |45 |90 |900 |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |135 |270 |1,200 |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |405 |810 |1,500 |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |1,215 |2,430 |1,800 |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |3,645 |7,290 |2,100 |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |10,935 |21,870 |2,400 |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} |32,805 |65,610 |2,700 |} Light Bulb serves to attract mobs that are in a certain radius from the petal. The effect radius is dependent on Light Bulb's rarity. Attracted mobs will then follow you. It is able to provoke neutral and aggressive mobs, but it can't attract passive, non-sentient or stationary ones like [[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble mob]], [[Cactus (Mob)|Cactus mob]] or [[Sandstorm]]. The aggro effect doesn't stack, meaning using more Bulbs won't aggro mobs from further away. The aggro range pull works independently from the glow that Light Bulb emits, as the glow is only a visual effect. ==Obtainment== ===[[Firefly]]=== {{:Firefly/Drop Bulb}} ===[[Moth]]=== {{:Moth/Drop Bulb}} ===Purchasing=== On certain days, Light Bulb can be purchased from the [[Shop]]. ''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]'' {{Petal Price|2}} ==Obtaining== *Defeat [[Firefly|Fireflies]] in [[Jungle]] or [[Moth]]s in [[Sewers]] to obtain Light Bulb. Farming Jungle for Light Bulb is not recommended, as it is a mob-dense and projectile-dense biome which requires considerably stronger supports than Sewers. ==Strategies== *Light Bulb's main purpose is to attract mobs without having to damage or come close to them. **This is particularly useful for AFK farming in [[Ant Hell]], where one player lures and the other players stay in a safe spot using DPS or summoner petals. Although [[Baby Ant]]s cannot be aggroed by Light Bulb, they can still be pushed by the [[Worker Ant]]s and [[Soldier Ant]]s behind them. **In Sewers, Light Bulb can be used to lure Moths, which are technically a neutral mob, though their behavior makes them not seem so. It is advised to use {{Rarity|Common}} Light Bulb to avoid over-luring. *Another utility of Light Bulb is to increase the visible range inside the [[Pyramid]]. ==Gallery== File:Bulb (Common).png|{{Rarity|Common}} Bulb. File:Bulb (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|Unusual}} Bulb. File:Bulb (Rare).png|{{Rarity|Rare}} Bulb. File:Bulb (Epic).png|{{Rarity|Epic}} Bulb. File:Bulb (Legendary).png|{{Rarity|Legendary}} Bulb. File:Bulb (Mythic).png|{{Rarity|Mythic}} Bulb. File:Bulb (Ultra).png|{{Rarity|Ultra}} Bulb. File:Bulb (Super).png|{{Rarity|Super}} Bulb. File:Bulb (Unique).png|{{Rarity|Unique}} Bulb. File:Bulb.png|Bulb. File:Flower with bulb.png|A flower with a Bulb emitting light. ==Trivia== *Bulb's damage value is always half of its health value. *Bulb seems to be shining yellow, indicating that it's an incandescent bulb. *Mobs are attracted to Bulb's light. This is a reference to real life, where light bulbs are known for attracting real-life insects (because they are attracted by light), especially moths, flies, beetles and mosquitoes. **As a reference to the attracted insects, [[Firefly|Fireflies]] and [[Moth]]s drop it, although other mobs, like [[Beetle]]s and [[Fly|Flies]], don't drop the petal. *Bulb, [[Honey]], [[Poo]] and [[Uranium]] are the only petals to work around the aggro mechanic. Only Bulb and Poo change it. **Bulb and Uranium attract mobs towards you, while Honey attracts mobs towards the petal (only while it's on the ground). **Bulb essentially functions like an inverse version of Poo: Bulb increases your aggro range, while Poo decreases it. ***Do NOT use Bulb and Poo together, which would make Poo completely useless. *Bulb emits a yellow glow around itself, similar to Uranium's green glow. Those glow effects match with those petals' respective main colors (yellow and green). *Since the petal gallery of mobs, the inventory and the [[Crafting]] UI are based on the alphabetical order of each petal's displayed name, Bulb is the first petal whose name is shortened in the order of petals (from '''Light Bulb''' to simply '''Bulb'''). However, based on the true alphabetical order of the petals' names (which organizes petals based on their extended name), [[Ant Egg]] (to simply '''Egg'''), [[Beetle Egg]] (also to simply '''Egg''') and [[Blood Stinger]] (to simply '''Stinger''') all come before Light Bulb. *When the Pyramid update came out, Bulb no longer gets hidden by the "Hide other petals" setting. {{Petals}} [[zh:灯泡]] [[Category:Petals]] [[Category:Buffs]] [[Category:Common+]] [[Category:Jungle]] [[Category:Sewers]] [[Category:Unstackable Petals]] [[Category:Droppable Petals]] [[Category:Assembling]] [[Category:Fireflies]] [[Category:Moth]] [[Category:Luring Petals]]

title: Lightning
content:
{{Petal Template Infobox | image = File:Lightning (Unusual).png|U File:Lightning (Rare).png|R File:Lightning (Epic).png|E File:Lightning (Legendary).png|L File:Lightning (Mythic).png|M File:Lightning (Ultra).png|Ul File:Lightning (Super).png|S File:Lightning (Unique).png|Uq | description = Strikes several nearby enemies. | first-rarity = {{Rarity|Unusual}} | health = {{Petal Scale|20|lowest-rarity=Unusual}} | lightning = {{Petal Scale|12|lowest-rarity=Unusual}} | bounces = {{Infobox Colors Empty||3|4|5|6|7|8|9|10}} | reload-time = 2.5s | stackability = Stackable | dropped-by = *[[Jellyfish]] | special = Discharges bouncy lightning discharges when it hits something.
Affected units will be briefly paralyzed. }} The '''Lightning''' is a petal in florr.io. Whenever it strikes an entity, the petal releases a lightning discharge that damages several nearby enemies in a chain, while also briefly stunning them completely. Lightning is dropped by [[Jellyfish]]. ==Appearance== The Lightning looks like a small light blue star, with 10 pointy edges evenly distributed around itself. ==Function== {|class="article-table" |+Petal Stats ![[Rarity]] !Obtainable? !Health !Respawn !Lightning !Bounces |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |Assembler Exclusive |20 |rowspan="9"|2.5 seconds |12 |2 |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |rowspan="8"|Yes |60 |36 |3 |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |180 |108 |4 |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |540 |324 |5 |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |1620 |972 |6 |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |4860 |2916 |7 |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |14580 |8748 | 8 |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |43740 |26244 |9 |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} |131220 |78732 |10 |} The Lightning activates upon contact with enemy mobs, flowers or [[Assembler|Assemblers]]. Once activated, the Lightning discharges a lightning bolt which can bounce to nearby enemies up to the indicated number of times by the bounce stat, damaging every entity struck by the lightning in the process. All entities struck by it have their velocity set to 0 for an instant, although they are still moving due to inertia. Lightnings actually do not activate on contact with projectiles such as missiles. An interesting thing to note is that although Lightning has a health stat, it instantly destroys itself upon contact with mobs, preventing multihitting. However, this doesn't apply to projectiles so it can still block them. Also, [[Bubble (Mob)|Bubbles]] are completely immune to Lightning. Projectiles are affected by Lightning's bounce stat. ==Obtaining== ===[[Jellyfish]]=== {{:Jellyfish/Drop Lightning}} ===Purchasing=== On certain days, Lightning can be purchased in the [[Shop]]. ''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]'' {{Petal Price|3}} ==Strategy== *Lightning is a pretty decent overall petal that you could use in a lot of moments. Usually, you'll find yourself swarmed by many mobs at once, and they could be very close to each other, which means Lightning will strike several of them at once and help you killing every mob in said swarm. *Lightning has a slightly larger hitbox than most petals, allowing it to actually be a good defense against projectiles. This has proven effective against {{R|7}} and {{R|6}} Scorpions, as well as Mantis Peas, though it has yet to be tested on Hornet missiles. *Any given primary zone also has mobs with multiple segments like [[Centipede|Centipede,]] letting Lightning damage all of their segments at once. ===Usage=== *The Lightning is decent against players, but remember it won't connect to the player if it hits a petal and/or projectile. *Lightning is pretty ambiguous against Rubber: while it doesn't bounce the user, the petal will absorb damage taken from it (unless the Lightning deals damage above what Rubber can absorb). Choose another petal over this against Rubber users. *A Lightning user actually won't have to worry about aggroing mobs to itself: all mobs are aggressive and their aggro ranges are pretty big. *All Hel mobs should have their base versions' strategies considered (like how [[Hel Wasp]] projectiles won't activate the Lightning). [[Gambler]]s can be considered enemy flowers, specially with their own petal usage. ===PVP/Offensive Strategies=== *Rubber won't make you bounce with each Lightning hit, but it will absorb damage dealt to you. *Salt will deal some damage back at the flower who discharged their Lightning onto you. *Using ranged petals like the Missile will help you deal damage to the Lightning user without risking getting hit by it. *Overall, consider fighting Lightning users like fighting Jellyfish who can't discharge at you from a range. == Strategies In certain areas == {{Zone|Garden}} *Lightning's performance in Garden may or may not be hurt by [[Hornet]]s. Their projectiles don't activate the Lightning's ability at all. *Lightning is most useful against [[Ant Hole]]s and [[Centipede]]s as the multiple Ants or multiple Centipedes will cause the discharges to bounce among each other, thus damaging them all at once. *Lightning could also somewhat help against {{Rarity|Super}} [[Rock (Mob)|Rocks]] as the petal might chain on all of the spawned mini Rocks. {{Zone|Desert}} *Although [[Beetle]]s and [[Desert Centipede]]s are faster than you, the "stun" from lightning can slow them down slightly. *The Lightning isn't affected by the double damage of the [[Soldier Fire Ant]]s from a [[Fire Ant Burrow]], because it's weak and its damage output (lightning discharges) is independent of the petal's HP, as HP doesn't really affect anything as said above. *Be cautious with [[Scorpion]]s. On top of their general dangers, their stingers don't activate the Lightning, making it pretty useless. {{Zone|Ocean}} *Lightning is ambiguous here as well. Swarms will usually form, however, the mobs are quite strong here, specially [[Starfish (Mob)|Starfish]] with their HP regen ability (which requires [[Dandelion]]). *[[Leech]]es will take multiple damage from Lightning if they happen to be long, as long Leeches are considered Centipede-like segments composing the mob. *Bubbles are completely immune to Lightning, so be sure to have some other petal to pop them. *Counter-intuitively, you can use the bubbles as a shield and strike it with lightning over and over to keep damaging the mobs nearby the bubble without risk of being damaged. It isn't encouraged as the mobs might swarm and force the bubble unto you. ===Obtaining=== *[[Jellyfish]] are annoying due to their own electrical discharges. [[Rubber]] or outhealing them is recommended. *[[Salt]] can also help damaging back the Jellyfish once they start discharging you. *Running and using ranged petals like [[Missile]] will let you damage them without suffering from their discharges, but this requires more skill and the density is too high for this to be viable. *Through Assemblers, 3 Batteries can be assembler into 1 Lightning petal of the same rarity and 1 Battery left. {{Zone|Ant Hell}} *Lightning is pretty good on all Ant Hells, as their chaining characteristic can easily damage all clumped Ants, Fire Ants, Termites (dealing more damage to all of them at once) and even Eggs, since they will be close to each other pretty frequently. *Lightning is also good against [[Queen Ant]]s and [[Queen Fire Ant]]s as it not only damages the boss itself, but also its spawned minions (and other nearby Ants). {{Zone|Hel}} * Useful when being chased by Hel mobs, since they usually attack in hordes {{Zone|Jungle}} *Lightning will chain on multiple mobs as well as [[Evil Centipede]]s. They should be considered despite their inability to activate against [[Mantis]] and [[Wasp]] projectiles. *The [[Leafbug]]'s Armor can't affect the Lightning's discharges (or lightning/poison in general). This is a key point in Lightning's usefulness in here as well as having an easier time dealing with its Armor. {{Zone|Sewers}} *It's pretty easy for Sewers to have frequently clumped up mobs. This means that Lightning will be useful dealing with multiple mobs at once. *Lightning is effective against Flies. Flies have lower HP compared to other mobs, but is compensated with a high evasion rate. Lightning bypasses the Fly's evasion rate, which makes it much more easier to deal with. *Using Lightning, however, could turn against you, as you don't want to aggro [[Roach]]es without caution. Doing so will likely result in your death. **A far less dangerous but more annoying interaction would be aggroing [[Moth]]s, as they will begin running away from you. This can be countered with cornering them, using [[Bulb]] or speeding petals like [[Bubble]]. ==Gallery== File:Lightning (Common).png|{{R|1}} Lightning. (Only obtainable via [[Assembler]]) File:Lightning (Unusual).png|{{R|2}} Lightning. File:Lightning (Rare).png|{{R|3}} Lightning. File:Lightning (Epic).png|{{R|4}} Lightning. File:Lightning (Legendary).png|{{R|5}} Lightning. File:Lightning (Mythic).png|{{R|6}} Lightning. File:Lightning (Ultra).png|{{R|7}} Lightning. File:Lightning (Super).png|{{R|8}} Lightning. File:Lightning (Unique).png|{{R|9}} Lightning. File:Lightning.png|Lightning. ==Trivia== *At one point, Lightning was proximity-activated and would strike several nearby enemies by itself without contact needed. **Later it was changed to only activating when destroyed, including when projectile and enemy flower’s petals hit it. Eventually this function was removed to only when hitting a mob or enemy flower. *The petal used to have only 1 health, similarly to [[Bubble]], however, it was soon changed to have health to compensate for the removal of activation when destroyed. *Even though it is able to do damage, Lightning has no damage stat. The damage it does is caused by a special stat, named "Lightning". *Lightning's hitbox is far bigger than its visual size, similar to [[Ankh]]. *Lightning, [[Blueberries]], [[Magic Eye]], [[Mjölnir]], [[Laser]], [[Electric Web]], and [[Battery]] are the only five petals who can release lightning discharges. They also have to be activated on specific circumstances and only damage mobs and enemy flowers. *The Lighting's shape is similar to the [[Bur]] petal, however, the latter is a circle with round edges, and the former is a pointy star. *The Lightning is also similar to [[Lotus]] in appearance, however, it has 10 sharp edges as opposed to 8, and it's cyan-colored instead of purple. *The {{Rarity|Common}} version of this petal can only be obtained via [[Assembler]]. *Although Lightning has twice the health of the average mob damage equal to its rarity (For example, {{R|Ultra}} [[Soldier Ant]] deals 7.3k damage and {{R|Ultra}} Lightning has 14.6k health), it still breaks on impact. *Despite affected by Petal health talents, it doesn't matter for Lightning. It just makes Lightning much more durable against projectiles. {{Petals}} [[zh:闪电]] [[Category:Petals]] [[Category:Unusual+]] [[Category:Ocean]] [[Category:Lightning]] [[Category:Droppable Petals]] [[Category:Stackable Petals]] [[Category:Assembling]] [[Category:Jellyfish]]

title: Lotus
content:
{{Petal Template Infobox
| image = <gallery>
File:Lotus (Common).png|C

'''Lotus''' is a [[Petals|petal]] in florr.io. It negates a certain amount of poison taken by the user. Lotus is an exclusive drop from [[Roach]]es.

==Appearance==
Lotus appears to be a pointy star with eight sharp edges pointing away from the center, much like [[Lightning]] (which has ten sharp edges). Its color is lavender and its border is closer to a purple shade. It also has a small purple circle in its center.

==Function==
{|class="article-table"
|+Petal Stats
![[Rarity]]
!Obtainable?
!Poison Resistance
!Respawn
!Special
|-

|rowspan="9"|Negates poison damage
|-

|10,206/s
|-

|30,618/s
|-
|{{Rarity|Unique}}
|91,854/s
|}
Lotus negates a certain amount of poison taken by the user. The poison damage taken while equipping Lotus are calculated by this formula: poison damage taken - Lotus's poison resistance. Ex: equipping an {{R|Ultra}} Lotus (10.206 poison damage/second) while taking 10.935 poison damage/second, you will only take 0.729 damage/second. If the value equals 0 or below, the user will not take damage.

Lotus is stackable, means you can use multiple petals at once, increase your chance of surviving immense poison damage from mobs/other players.

==Obtainment==
===[[Roach]]===
{{:Roach/Drop Lotus}}

===Purchasing===
On certain days, Lotus can be purchased in the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|2}}

==Obtaining==
*Defeat [[Roach]]es to obtain Lotus. Use [[Jelly|Jellies]], [[Pincer]]s and [[Web]]s along with DPS petals to slowly take them down.

==Strategy==
*Lotus is the most effective petal for negating poison damage.
**In [[Desert]], Lotus is indispensable to survival as [[Scorpion|Scorpions]] make up a big portion of mob swarms. Lotus can negate 93.3% of poison DPS inflicted by a Scorpion's missile of the same rarity, and 31.1% of poison DPS inflicted by that of one rarity above.
**Lotus can also be useful for countering [[Spider|Spiders]]' poison in [[Garden]] and [[Sewers]] and [[Barrel|Barrels]]' poison in [[Factory]]. 
**During {{Rarity|Super}} [[Hel]] mob fights, use a Lotus or two to defend against [[Uranium]] users as well as [[Spider/Hel|Hel Spiders]].
*Lotus is required for builds involving Uranium. To fully negate Uranium's poison DPS (without [[Talents/Poison|Poison talent]]), at least three Lotuses of the same rarity and one Lotus of one rarity below are needed.
**For {{Rarity|Super}} [[Worm|Worms]], use seven {{Rarity|Ultra}} and two {{Rarity|Mythic}} Lotuses at minimum.

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">
File:Lotus (Common).png|{{Rarity|1}} Lotus.
File:Lotus (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|2}} Lotus.
File:Lotus (Rare).png|{{Rarity|3}} Lotus.

File:Lotus (Legendary).png|{{Rarity|5}} Lotus.
File:Lotus (Mythic).png|{{Rarity|6}} Lotus.
File:Lotus (Ultra).png|{{Rarity|7}} Lotus.
File:Lotus (Super).png|{{Rarity|8}} Lotus.
File:Lotus (Unique).png|{{Rarity|9}} Lotus.
File:Lotus.png|Lotus.
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*With the [[Sponge]] nerf that removed its ability to absorb poison damage, Lotus is the only petal that can effectively absorb poison damage as of now. The other two are [[Cotton]] and [[Cotton/Magic|Magic Cotton]].
*Lotus, Cotton, Magic Cotton, [[Rubber]] and Sponge are the only petals which can absorb damage. While Cotton, Magic Cotton and Rubber divert their respective absorbed damage to the petals (which take damage), Lotus negates a portion of it, and Sponge absorbs it but slowly deals said damage to the player.
*On January 1, 2025, Lotus's ability was changed from absorbing poison damage to negating a certain amount of poison damage.
**Lotus still had its damage and health stats after said change, but eventually those were removed. The petal no longer can be destroyed.
**Before the update, Lotus was risky to use, because mobs/players can destroy it, make you receive the base poison damage. This was an advantage of players who uses Iris (especially {{R|Ultra}}+) along with decent petals like Rocks and Stingers.
*This is one of the petals that reduces [[Salt]] effectiveness.

title: Magic Petals
content:
'''Magic Petals''' are weapons used by the players in [https://florr.io florr.io]. They are almost like the normal [[Petals]], however, they have an extra touch of magic that change their abilities a bit. They can only be obtained by killing mobs while equipping [[Mana Orb]] in your loadout. They revolve around Mana and either require Mana to spawn or regenerate mana. You can only obtain dropped Magic Petals of up to the same rarity as your [[Mana Orb]], for example, if you have an epic [[Mana Orb]], you can only obtain up to an epic magic petal as a drop.

title: Magnet
content:
{{Petal Template Infobox | image = File:Magnet (Rare).png|R File:Magnet (Epic).png|E File:Magnet (Legendary).png|L File:Magnet (Mythic).png|M File:Magnet (Ultra).png|Ul File:Magnet (Super).png|S File:Magnet (Unique).png|Uq | description = Increases drop pickup range. | first-rarity = {{Rarity|Rare}} | damage = {{Petal Scale|1|lowest-rarity=Rare}} | health = {{Petal Scale|30|lowest-rarity=Rare}} | pick-up-range = {{Infobox Colors Empty|||+240|+420|+600|+780|+960|+1140|+1320}} | reload-time = 1.5s | stackability = Unstackable (stackable with [[Talents/Magnetism|Magnetism talent]]) | dropped-by = *[[Fire Ant Burrow]] *[[Crab/Mecha|Mecha Crab]] *[[Shell]] | special = Increases the flower's petal pickup radius.
Disrupts nearby {{Rarity|Ultra}} Compasses. }} '''Magnet''' is a [[Petals|petal]] in [https://florr.io florr.io]. During its usage, it increases the range from which drops can be picked up, and affects nearby {{Rarity|Ultra}} [[Compass]]es. Magnet is dropped by [[Fire Ant Burrow]]s, [[Mecha Crab|Mecha Crabs]], and [[Shell (Mob)|Shells]]. ==Appearance== Magnet appears to be an U-shaped magnet with slight curves on the edges. The left half is red and the right half is blue (indicating North and South respectively). ==Function== {| class="article-table" |+Petal Stats ![[Rarity]] !Obtainable? !Damage !Health !Respawn !Pickup Range !Disruption Range |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |rowspan="2"|No |1 |30 |rowspan="9"|1.5 | +0 |rowspan="9"|2k |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |3 |90 | +60 |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |rowspan="7"|Yes |9 |270 | +240 |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |27 |810 | +420 |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |81 |2,430 | +600 |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |243 |7,290 | +780 |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |729 |21,870 | +960 |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |2,187 |65,610 | +1,140 |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} |6,561 |196,830 | +1,320 |} Magnet allows the user to pick up dropped petals from farther away. This effect is only active when the petal is alive, meaning pickup range will be reverted to normal when it is destroyed or is into recharge. Magnet does not expand upon attacking, and does not stack with itself; however, it stacks with [[Talents/Magnetism|Magnetism talent]]. For example, using {{Rarity|Ultra}} Magnet with Magnetism talent increases pickup range by 1,000 + 960 = 1,960. Regardless of its rarity, Magnet disrupts nearby {{Rarity|Ultra}} Compasses, causing them to point to the petal. ==Obtainment== ===[[Fire Ant Burrow]]=== {{:Fire Ant Burrow/Drop Magnet}} ===[[Shell (Mob)|Shell]]=== {{:Shell (Mob)/Drop Magnet}} ===Purchasing=== On certain days, Magnet can be purchased in the [[Shop]]. ''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]'' {{Petal Price|10}} ==Obtaining== *Defeat [[Fire Ant Burrow]]s, [[Crab/Mecha|Mecha Crabs]], [[Shell (Mob)|Shell mobs]] to obtain Magnet. For early-game players, defeating Fire Ant Burrows or Shells is the easier option. For players aiming for high rarity Magnets for [[Assembler|assembling]] [[Compass]], it is better to farm Mecha Crabs in [[Factory]], where [[Fragment]] can also be obtained simultaneously. ==Strategy== *During early game when players are usually in severe lack of [[Talents|Talent Points]], Magnet serves as an important utility petal to pick up drops, and should be made swappable, as it is only needed when there are drops accumulating on the ground. *In most cases, Magnet becomes obsolete if the player has already unlocked [[Talents/Magnetism|Magnetism talent]], which grants the passive effect of 1,000 extra pickup range. There are, however, a few exceptions: **If the drops are located in a spot that is covered by mob swarms (usually after a {{Rarity|Super}} mob was defeated), one may use Magnet in tandem with the talent to pick them up. **When farming high rarity zones with [[Moon Rock|Moons]] and summoner petals, mobs can die outside of Magnetism's range, and the player might lose drops as a result if they do not have a Magnet equipped. ==Gallery== File:Magnet (Common).png|{{R|1}} Magnet. (Unobtainable) File:Magnet (Unusual).png|{{R|2}} Magnet. (Unobtainable) File:Magnet (Rare).png|{{R|3}} Magnet. File:Magnet (Epic).png|{{R|4}} Magnet. File:Magnet (Legendary).png|{{R|5}} Magnet. File:Magnet (Mythic).png|{{R|6}} Magnet. File:Magnet (Ultra).png|{{R|7}} Magnet. File:Magnet (Super).png|{{R|8}} Magnet. File:Magnet (Unique).png|{{R|9}} Magnet. File:Magnet.png|Magnet. File:Screenshot (348).png|Side-By-Side Comparison of 1 {{Rarity|Legendary}} Magnet, and 9 inactive {{Rarity|Epic}} Magnet. ==Trivia== *At one point in the game, Magnets used to stack with each other. It was then nerfed to become unstackable with itself (though Magnetism talent can still further buff petal pickup range). *Magnets also used to absorb lightning damage. When [[Jungle]] was added to the game, Magnet lost said ability, which was given to [[Rubber]]. Its price was also halved in the [[Shop]]. **This effect is (or was) a reference to how real life magnets can pull another magnet (or any electrical discharges) if they are facing themselves but their poles have opposite charges (positive for protons and negative for electrons). This phenomenon involves energy, and that's how it relates to florr.io: lightning discharges (including Jellyfish discharges) are the result of electric energy. *{{Rarity|Ultra}} [[Compass]]es can be attracted to a Magnet. This interaction is one of the the only three interactions in the game which are specific to only two petals, with the other ones being the [[Clover]] boosting the chance of a critical hit from a [[Dice]] and [[Relic]] changing the hatched mobs from an [[Ant Egg]]'s Egg (from friendly [[Soldier Ant]]s to friendly [[Soldier Termite]]s). All other interactions involve a petal and general game mechanics (for example, [[Lotus]] being able to absorb poison damage from all poisonous petals, mobs and projectiles, or [[Dandelion]] degrading healing petals). **This is also a reference for the real-life phenomenon known as magnetism. Magnets in real life are known for attracting metallic objects, and a real life compass' pointy needles are always made of metal (which is why they're attracted to the Earth's poles, although it's more of the Electro-magnetism phenomenon at this point), which will cause any compasses to show the localization of the closest magnet present in a certain radius, just like in-game. The Electro-magnetism phenomenon is also referenced in the Compass' primary ability. **{{Rarity|Ultra}} Compasses are attracted to one's own Magnets. This is the only interaction where a petal from one's loadout negatively affects another petal from the loadout. All other interactions either happen between a petal from one's loadout and a petal from another one's loadout (for example, Lotus and poisonous petals) or are beneficial ([[Basil]] and healing petals). *Magnet is one of the few petals that can be dropped in both Desert and Ocean, with the others being [[Sand]] and [[Salt]]. In the case of Magnet, it is shared by Fire Ant Burrow and Shell mob. * Pick up range stats can be calculated with the formula 180r-120, where r is the rarity index ({{Rarity|Common}} starts at 0, {{Rarity|Unusual}} starts at 1, etc.). * Magnet has become easier to farm due to [[Crab/Mecha|Mecha Crabs]] dropping it at good rates, making it no longer a rare petal. {{Petals}} [[zh:磁铁]] [[Category:Petals]] [[Category:Rare+]] [[Category:Desert]] [[Category:Ocean]] [[Category:Buffs]] [[Category:Fire Ant Burrow]] [[Category:Unstackable Petals]] [[Category:Assembling]] [[Category:Fire Ants]] [[Category:Shell]]

'''Mana Orb''', shortened in-game to just '''Orb''', is a [[Petals|Petal]] in [https://florr.io florr.io] added on December 5th, 2024. Its function consists of creating [[Mana]] to use [[Magic petal|Magic Petals]] and obtaining them. Orb is dropped by [[Magic Firefly|Magic Fireflies]], and while it is equipped, from [[Ladybug|Ladybugs]].

==Appearance==
Orb consists of a cyan circle with a darker outline, similar to the color of [[Lightning]].

==Function==
''Note: The "A + B" respawn format means that the petal takes A seconds to respawn and B seconds after respawning to do its special action/have its special action enabled.''

When equipped, Orb adds an attribute called base mana (which causes the mana bar to appear) to the user, allowing mana to be generated, stored and consumed. Orb is consumed by the user like [[Rose]], granting a fixed amount of mana until the mana bar is full. it also allows the user to find magic petals, which are only accessible through the petal. There are some petals selected to have their magic variants, including: [[Peas]] and [[Grapes]] -> [[Blueberries]], [[Bubble]] -> [[Bubble/Magic|Magic Bubble]], [[Cactus]] -> [[Cactus/Magic|Magic Cactus]], [[Cotton]] -> [[Cotton/Magic|Magic Cotton]], [[Leaf]] and [[Golden Leaf]] -> [[Leaf/Magic|Magic Leaf]], [[Third Eye]] -> [[Third Eye/Magic|Magic Eye]], [[Missile]] and [[Missile/Mecha|Mecha Missile]] -> [[Missile/Magic|Magic Missile]], [[Mysterious Stick]] -> [[Mysterious Stick/Magic|Magic Stick]], [[Rose]] -> [[Mana Orb]] [[Stinger]] and [[Blood Stinger]] -> [[Stinger/Magic|Magic Stinger]].

==Obtainment==
===[[Firefly/Magic|Magic Firefly]]===
{{:Magic Firefly/Drop Mana Orb}}

===Purchasing===
On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]]. 

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''{{Petal Price|5}}

==Obtaining==
*Defeat [[Firefly/Magic|Magic Fireflies]] to obtain Orb. They only spawn in waves that occur at random intervals (0 to 3 hours between waves)
*You could also go to ladybug zone and equip orb. Since ladies dorp orb when orb is equipped stay in ladybug zone until your desired orb rarity is achieved.

==Strategy==
* Orb can be used to obtain magic petals from mobs.
* With {{Rarity|Legendary}}+ [[Talents/Reload|Reload talent]], Orb is better than [[Leaf/Magic|Magic Leaf]] of the same rarity in terms of mana regeneration, with the only downside of being less consistent than Magic Leaf as it can be destroyed.

==Gallery==

</gallery>

==Trivia==

* xiraa was the first player who got a {{Rarity|Mythic}} Orb. When he was searching for [[Magic Firefly|Magic Fireflies]], he accidentally killed a {{Rarity|Mythic}} one which dropped a {{Rarity|Mythic}} Orb.
* Equipping an Orb of any rarity grants the Magician [[Achievements|achievement]].
* Before on the December 16th, 2024, Orb used to always give 10 mana at a time at all rarities, making it almost useless for Mana generation. Magic Leaves were the better option
* Orb is the first petal that can affect mob drops.
** The second is [[Clover]], which gives a chance for double drops at {{Rarity|Legendary}} rarity.
** Mana Orb turns certain drops into their mana counterpart.
* After Orb was released for a few days, its price increased from 1.5k to 2.5k Stars at {{Rarity|Mythic}} rarity.
* Magic petals caused by Orb used to be additional drops and not replace the normal petals of certain mobs. Now, magic petals replace normal petals as drops when equipping Orb.
* Mana Orb now have better drop rates due to the update.
* If the player equips Orb, mobs that drop Roses will be replaced by Orb drop. The highest rarity of Orb drops depends on the highest Orb rarity the player've put in their build.

The {{PAGENAME}} is a mob in florr.io. It fires a burst of 3 non-splitting pea missiles at its target(s).

==Appearance==
'''Mantis''' consists of a green oval with a "[[Mysterious Stick|stick]]"-like shape as a pattern. It has the same antennae as [[Hornet]], but larger. (It does not drop [[Antennae]], however) Also, it has 6 legs that moves around when it moves.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Moves at a moderate speed, it is slower than a player.

'''Behavior:''' Aggressive, will shoot pea missiles at it's targets in a similar fashion to the old florr.io Hornet. The [[Peas]] inflict an armor debuff, similar to that of [[Bur]]. which lowers the amount of armor you have, and can lower the armour value below 0, effectively making you take more damage than normal. The armor debuff effect lasts for up to 10 seconds.

==Spawning==
The {{PAGENAME}} spawns quite often in [[Jungle]], around as much as the [[Wasp]].

==Drops==
===[[Bur]]===
{{:Mantis/Drop Bur}}

===[[Peas]]===
{{:Mantis/Drop Peas}}

==Strategy==
The Mantis is slower than you, so you can maintain a distance without needing any speed-boosting petals. The peas it shoots are slower than [[Hornet]] missiles, so they should be easy to dodge, just be wary that the Mantis shoots three of them at a time. You especially don't touch them as they will remove your armor and make you more vulnerable to attacks.

Alone, a single Mantis isn't a threat because, as mentioned above, its peas are easy to dodge. However, with two Mantises or more, or a Mantis along with a mob or many mobs, they will become potential supporter mobs, even if they accompany lower rarity mobs. Therefore, try to focus on the mobs coming with the Mantises, or directly on the Mantises if they have lower health. Avoid dealing with both at the same time as each map in the Jungle can spawn much more mobs each square meter than most maps, which makes it harder to dodge. Density is a huge problem in Jungle, and often you'll face swarms of Wasps and Mantises, which will barrage you with projectiles, damaging you and making you more and more vulnerable to the projectiles yet to come. Pile on [[Leafbug|Leafbugs]], neutral mobs with high body damage, you could easily be knocked or forced into, and [[Firefly|Fireflies]], weak but damaging mobs that often fly into you, and [[Bush|Bushes]], which, like Leafbugs, you can easily be knocked or forced into, but also can straight-up block your path completely, results in an unpleasant experience for you.

Any {{Rarity|Ultra}}+ Mantis should be taken seriously as the armor reduction is high, making even a tank build survive not very long when hit by it because of the highly increased damage you take each hit. There is (seemingly) no limit to how much of your armor these mobs can reduce.

[[Plank]] is a good counter against Mantises as their peas have weaker damage and lower health, making them easy to destroy. It can prevent you from taking armor debuff from Mantises, and, as a bonus, reduce damage from [[Wasp|Wasps]]. A little-known fact is that a [[Bone]] of the same rarity as Mantis will take no damage from its peas.

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185">

</gallery>

==Trivia== 
*The Mantis is the fourth mob in florr.io to have a ranged attack, the previous being [[Hornet]], [[Jellyfish]], and [[Scorpion]].
*In real life, mantises wait in places like flowers or tall grass and then ambush their prey with their spiky front legs and kill them, which is different from florr.io where they fire [[Peas]]. Mantises are also much bigger than most other insects, with some species growing up to 7 inches (18 cm) long, which is not shown in florr.io either. Mantises aren't oval too, instead the have a slim body (in most scenarios) and liik like a part of a plant.
*On April 26th 2023, a Youtuber with the name of "[GT] Darkstar" posted a video that showcased the EXACT concept for the Mantis, as seen HERE: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0fgktJGdXPk.
*A {{R|Unique}} Mantis has spawned under the Ellne hut in NA Jungle shortly after the addition of the rarity.

title: Mimic
content:
{{Petal Template Infobox
| name = 
| image = <gallery>
File:Mimic (Ultra).png|Ul
File:Mimic (Super).png|S
File:Mimic (Unique).png|Uq
</gallery>
| first-rarity = {{Rarity|7}}
| description = Copies the petal to the left of this, with the same rarity as this.
| damage = Depends on mimicked petal
| health = Depends on mimicked petal
| reload-time = Depends on mimicked petal
| stackability = Stackable (For unstackable petals, see [[Mimic#Function|Function]].
| dropped-by = *[[Mecha Flower]]
| special = Copies the damage, health, reload, and special properties of the petal to the left of itself in the loadout, but with the same rarity of itself. Its description matches the petal it mimics, of its own rarity.
}}

'''Mimic''' is an extremely rare [[Petals|petal]] in [https://florr.io florr.io]. Its function consists of mimicking the properties of the petal to its left, of its own rarity. Mimic is dropped by [[Mecha Flower]].

==Appearance==
The Mimic has no actual appearance and replicates the appearance of the petal it mimics in the petal orbit. In the petal box, it appears as a black, transparent question mark when it is in the inventory or when no petal is directly to its immediate left. When a petal occupies that slot, the Mimic changes appearance to match it, but retains its rarity and name.

==Function==
Mimics will replicate the damage, healing, reload speed, and special properties of the petal it mimics, like mana cost, lightning, or poison. If the Mimic mimics an unstackable petal and it is below or the same rarity as the petal it mimics, it will not function: otherwise, if the Mimic's rarity is above the petal, the unstackable petal becomes unusable and the Mimic functions. If the Mimic is placed in the furthest left spot, it will copy the petal on the rightmost slot.

Mimics cannot replicate [[Corruption]] or other Mimics. Thus, the maximum amount you can have equipped at a time is 5 (5 for the mimics to copy, although one is extremely rare itself.)

==Obtainment==
===[[Mecha Flower]]===
{| class="article-table"
|+Drop Rates
!Mob Rarity
!{{Rarity|Ultra}}
|-
|{{Rarity|Ultra}}
|0.003%
|-
|{{Rarity|Super}}
| rowspan='2' |0.8%
|-
|{{Rarity|Unique}} (10x)
|}

=== Purchasing===
On certain days, Mimic can be purchased from the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|30|Ultra}}

==Strategies==
*Use Mimics to replicate petals of specific rarities that you do not yet have, like mimicking Ultra Ant Egg with Super Mimic for Super Ant Egg.
*Use Mimics to duplicate petals that are rare for you and you need, like Basil in a body ramming load out, or Golden Leaf to further decrease reload time for nuking petals without having to grind for another one.
*For extensive strategies, view the strategy sections of the petal you are mimicking.

===Obtaining===
* Mimic is currently exceptionally rare because [[Mecha Flower|Mecha Flowers]] will almost never drop it.
* {{Rarity|Super}} Mecha Flowers have a higher chance of dropping Mimic, and are your best chance, but it's still extremely difficult to obtain as it only has a 0.2% chance of dropping {{Rarity|Ultra}} Mimic, even if you had 3x bonus on, it would be quite hard to obtain one. Never mind that Factory mobs, Mecha Flowers included spawn at Super rarity very rarely, which is why the Mecha Missile, Laser, and Fragment drops are high. Mimic seems to be the only notable exception. (Battery is dropped by Fireflies, and Uranium is dropped by Diggers)

</gallery>

== Trivia==
*Mimics have the second lowest drop chances out of any petal, with 0.004% (1/25,000) from {{Rarity|Ultra}} [[Mecha Flower]], only behind {{Rarity|Ultra}} [[Dark Mark]] from {{Rarity|Ultra}} [[Hel Centipede]] per segment, with only 0.002% (1/50,000).
*Mimics are one of the 5 petals that start at {{Rarity|Ultra}} rarity, the others are [[Compass]], [[Corruption]], [[Golden Leaf]] and [[Moon Rock]].
*Mimics are the second petal to have their appearance consist of a transparent icon: the first was [[Corruption]]. Now many petals that are wearable have transparent icons.
*Mimics can make spawning Ultra Squares possible. That is why M28 changed the petal Square's release time at higher rarities.
*Unique Mimic can replicate Unique petals, but the owner of the Unique being replicated does not lose it, as the Mimic can replicate any petal, except itself (There can only be one unique mimic) and Corruption.
*Mimic can't replicate Corruption because Mimic will also disappear once you die, due to the fact that Mimic replicates all of the mimicked petal usages. Since Mimic itself has no disappear function, and it is extremely rare, it does not duplicate Corruption.
*Mimic acts as a temporary petal for the petal you don't have yet at a certain rarity.
*Since {{R|8}} Mecha Flowers spawn rarely, no one has obtained {{R|9}} Mimic, making it one of the few petals that the {{R|9}} rarity of does not exist yet.
*Mimic's drop rate got buffed by 4x(from {{R|7}} [[Mecha Flower]]s) and 2x (from {{R|8}} [[Mecha Flower]]s) in August 2025.

title: Mob Diversity
content:
Mob Diversity is a statistic that shows how many kinds of [[Mobs]] can spawn in the same area. 

== Formula ==
Known that the mobs are a, b, c...(a has the highest spawnrate) 

Their spawnrates are a%, b%, c%... 

Mob Diversity=1+b%/a%+c%/a%+... 

For example, the mobs and their spawnrates in Fire Ant Hell are approximately: 

* Soldier Fire Ant - 90%
* Worker Fire Ant - 5%
* Baby Fire Ant - 3%
* Queen Fire Ant - 0%
* Worm 2%

Its Mob Diversity is 1+5/90+3/90+0/90+2/90='''1.111'''.

For example, the mobs and their spawnrates in Sewers box are approximately: 

* Spider - 35%
* Fly - 35%
* Moth - 15%
* Roach - 15%

Its Mob Diversity is 1+35/35+15/35+15/35='''2.857'''.

title: Mob Gallery
content:
The '''Mob Gallery''' is a menu in [[florr.io]]. It shows every mob you have defeated on your account, including rarity and amount. The gallery is organized in alphabetical order from top to bottom. The button to open the Mob Gallery is located on the top left.

[[File:Mob Gallery.png|thumb|Mob Gallery interface.]]

== Overview ==
When you kill a mob, its entry will appear in the Mob Gallery, alongside a background that shows what rarity the mob you killed was at. When you hover over a mob, you can see its name, the amount of XP given upon killing the mob, a brief description, and its stats. Every mob has a set amount of Health, Damage, and Armor, but some mobs may have additional stats. You can also see the drop rates of a mob, and what petals it drops.

{{Rarity|Unique}} mobs do not spawn naturally at the moment, so they do not appear in the Mob Gallery.

As of February 12th 2025, the Mob Gallery can also display how many times you killed a specific mob of a specific rarity. However, if you killed more than one of any mob before the update, then it will only show it as one killed as the game didn't store that info before the update.

== Gallery ==
<gallery>
File:Mob Gallery.png|Current Mob Gallery.
File:Legacy Mob Gallery.png|Legacy Mob Gallery.
</gallery>

== Trivia ==
* There is a script that shows every mob in the Mob Gallery in all of their rarities.
* Before April 18th 2024, the Mob Gallery button was below the [[Crafting]] button and used to be opened by pressing "V."
{{Game Info}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Mob Gallery}}

title: Mobs
content:
{{Stub}} {{LongPage}} '''Mobs''' are the main antagonists of florr.io. They are also the main form of progressing through the game by dropping [[Petals]]. Each mob has a unique appearance and Petal drops. Some mobs also have special abilities, such as firing projectiles towards you. Mobs will also give you XP, which may level you up which will then give you [[Talents|Talent Points]] for game progression. All mobs also have an [[Armor]] stat (reduces damage taken by the value of the Armor). Mobs spawn in one [[World Map|Area]] and might have a specific small area where they are more frequently spawned. Some mobs may get special attacks at the {{Rarity|Super}} and {{Rarity|Unique}} rarity. Mobs scale health and damage by the example of calculation below (with a very small inaccuracy for a high enough value): {{RarityTest|Common}} x 3.75 = {{RarityTest|Unusual}} {{RarityTest|Unusual}} x 3.6 = {{RarityTest|Rare}} {{RarityTest|Rare}} x 4 = {{RarityTest|Epic}} {{RarityTest|Epic}} x 6 = {{RarityTest|Legendary}} {{RarityTest|Legendary}} x 9.75 = {{RarityTest|Mythic}} {{RarityTest|Mythic}} x 810 \over 13 = {{RarityTest|Ultra}} {{RarityTest|Ultra}} x 200 \over 9 = {{RarityTest|Super}} {{RarityTest|Super}} x 15 = {{Rarity|9}} == Mobs ==

content:

The '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is a mob in florr.io. It is a passive mob and will flee when hit.
==Appearance==
The Moth uses the same body as Fly, but its outline is less thick, and its colored slightly brown. Its wings are moved front and is bigger. It also has 2 antennaes on the front with two circles, much like the [[Bee]].
==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' When unprovoked, it is slow, but quicker when it gets hit.

'''Behavior:''' Passive, randomly wandering slowly until it gets hit. After which, it will run away from the player.

==Spawning==
The {{PAGENAME}} sometimes spawns in Sewers.

==Drops==
===[[Wing]]===
{{ID 6}}

===[[Bulb]]===
{{ID 6}}

==Strategy==
Try using [[Mysterious Powder|Powder]] to catch up with it, [[Pincer]] to slow it down, [[Stinger]] to one-hit it or a poison petal like [[Iris]] to kill it. If possible, corner it so it couldn’t run.

You can also use [[Light Bulb]] to aggro the Moth. Be careful while using the bulb, as you can get killed by other mobs that get aggroed.

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185">
File:Moth (Common).png|{{Rarity|Common}} {{PAGENAME}}.
File:Moth (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|Unusual}} {{PAGENAME}}.

File:Moth (Unique).png|{{Rarity|Unique}} {{PAGENAME}}.
File:Moth.png|{{PAGENAME}}.
</gallery>

== Trivia==
*Moths are the only mobs to flee when attacked while still having line of sight with the player.
*This mob has the same drop rates for both petals it drops.
*This mob has the second lowest health out of any mob, only behind the [[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble]].
*Even though the mob is passive, it becomes provoked when a flower is using a [[Light Bulb|Bulb]]. This is a reference on how bugs get attracted to light.
*An {{Rarity|7}} Moth is actually easier to fight against than some {{Rarity|6}}s, like [[Hel Beetle]].
*Moth dropped [[Battery]] for the couple days before [[Firefly]] was added, and then the Battery drop got shifted to the Firefly.
*At first, this mob was coded to just walk backwards all the time, but the model made it look like it was running away.

title: Mysterious Powder
content:
{{Petal Template Infobox | image = File:Powder (Rare).png|R File:Powder (Epic).png|E File:Powder (Legendary).png|L File:Powder (Mythic).png|M File:Powder (Ultra).png|Ul File:Powder (Super).png|S File:Powder (Unique).png|Uq | description = Increases movement speed.
Disabled if the flower takes damage or if near a mob higher than a certain rarity. | first-rarity = {{Rarity|Rare}} | health = {{Petal Scale|10|lowest-rarity=Rare}} | movement-speed = {{Infobox Colors Empty|||+75%|+100%|+125%|+150%|+175%|+200%|+225%}} | max-mob-rarity = {{Infobox Colors Empty|||{{R|1}}|{{R|2}}|{{R|3}}|{{R|4}}|{{R|5}}|{{R|6}}|{{R|7}}}} | reload-time = 4.0s | stackability = Unstackable | dropped-by = * [[Desert Centipede]] | special = Increases movement speed }} '''Mysterious Powder''', shortened in-game to '''Powder,''' is a [[Petals|petal]] in [https://florr.io florr.io]. It increases user's movement speed, and stops working when the user takes damage or is near a mob higher than the max mob rarity. Powder is dropped from [[Desert Centipede]]s. ==Appearance == Powder consists of a white splatter of paint. While in-game, it animates and shifts around like it is being blown apart. ==Function== {|class="article-table" |+Petal Stats ![[Rarity]] !Obtainable? !Health !Respawn !Speed !Maximum Mob Rarity |- |{{R|1}} |rowspan="2"|No |10 |rowspan="9"|4 seconds | +25% |rowspan="3"|{{R|Common}} |- |{{R|2}} |30 | +50% |- |{{R|Rare}} |rowspan="7"|Yes |90 | +75% |- |{{R|Epic}} |270 | +100% |{{R|Unusual}} |- |{{R|Legendary}} |810 | +125% |{{R|Rare}} |- |{{R|Mythic}} |2,430 | +150% |{{R|Epic}} |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |7,290 | +175% |{{R|Legendary}} |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |21,870 | +200% |{{R|Mythic}} |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} |65,610 | +225% |{{R|Ultra}} |} Powder gives you more movement speed, and its speed bonus does not stack with other Powders. It destroys itself when you approach a mob with a rarity higher than the specified rarity or when you take damage. ==Obtainment== ===[[Centipede (Desert)]]=== {{:Centipede (Desert)/Drop Powder}} ===Purchasing=== On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]]. ''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]'' {{Petal Price|2}} ==Obtaining== *Defeat {{R|3}}+ [[Centipede (Desert)|Desert Centipedes]] to obtain Powder. Try to attack the head segment in order to make them target you, and use [[Bone]]s or [[Lightning]]s along with [[Jelly|Jellies]], [[Pincer]]s and [[Web]]s to defeat them safely. ==Strategy== * Powder is intended for fast travel between different areas and can be used with [[Bubble|Bubbles]] for a faster speed. However, it has a one downside: it gets destroyed if you walk over a mob that's higher than the maximum rarity of Powder. It is recommended to use [[Cotton]] if you are using this with a Bubble build since low rarity mobs can destroy your Powder if not careful. * Powder can also be used for farming low rarity areas such as the [[Cactus]] zone in [[Desert]] and the [[Ant Hell]] portal area in [[Garden]]. * Don't use this petal unless you have {{Rarity|Legendary}} Powder, as it is rendered essentially useless because the Powder will destroy itself and then it has no purpose except to waste a slot. * Use Powder when bubbling near a wall and/or use Antennae to see mobs ahead. ==Gallery== File:Powder (Common).png|{{R|1}} Powder. (unobtainable) File:Powder (Unusual).png|{{R|2}} Powder. (unobtainable) File:Powder (Rare).png|{{R|3}} Powder. File:Powder (Epic).png|{{R|4}} Powder. File:Powder (Legendary).png|{{R|5}} Powder. File:Powder (Mythic).png|{{R|6}} Powder. File:Powder (Ultra).png|{{R|7}} Powder. File:Powder (Super).png|{{R|8}} Powder. File:Powder (Unique).png|{{R|9}} Powder. File:Powder.png|Powder. ==Trivia== * Powder used to increase movement speed less than it does now, but it wouldn't be destroyed upon walking near a mob of a certain rarity or higher. * Around 1/7/2024, Powder's effect was buffed up to +100% and will be destroyed if you approach a mob of the same rarity as your Powder: it was then buffed a day later by increasing the buff for each rarity. * Powders used to decrease your maximum health when equipped. This debuff decreased per rarity until the effect was removed at higher rarities. * {{Rarity|Super}} and {{Rarity|Unique}} Powder's max mob rarity used to be {{Rarity|Legendary}} and {{Rarity|Mythic}}, but both were buffed a rarity ahead. * None of the players have obtained the {{Rarity|Unique}} Powder yet despite its great potential. {{Petals}} [[zh:神秘的粉末]] [[Category:Petals]] [[Category:Unstackable Petals]] [[Category:Rare+]] [[Category:Desert]] [[Category:Droppable Petals]] [[Category:Buffs]][[Category:Mysterious]]

title: Mysterious Relic
content:
{{Petal Template Infobox | name = Relic | image = File:Relic_(Unusual).png|U File:Relic_(Rare).png|R File:Relic_(Epic).png|E File:Relic_(Legendary).png|L File:Relic_(Mythic).png|M File:Relic_(Ultra).png|Ul File:Relic_(Super).png|S File:Relic_(Unique).png|Uq | description = A strange relic. Significantly increases maximum health.
Damage taken is spread among other wearers. | first-rarity = {{Rarity|Unusual}} | flower-health = {{Infobox Colors Empty||+20%|+30%|+40%|+50%|+60%|+70%|+80%|+90%}} | stackability = Unstackable | dropped-by = * [[Baby Termite]] * [[Termite Overmind]] * [[Termite Mound]] * [[Soldier Termite]] * [[Worker Termite]] | special = Spreads damage between wearers of the petal. }} '''Mysterious Relic''', shortened in-game to '''Relic,''' is a petal in florr.io. It spreads the damage taken by the player among other flowers that also wear it and increases your maximum health. The Relic is dropped from Termites and Termite Mounds. ==Appearance== The Relic looks like a brown star with a light brown interior and a slightly darker brown border. When equipped, it makes the flower look more orange and does not circle the flower, attaching to the flower, like the [[Antennae]]. ==Function== When equipped, the Relic will increase the flower's health and spread the damage (100% for all rarities) taken by the flower to all flowers (including the flower that took the damage first) with the Relic petal equipped. {|class="article-table" |+Petal Stats ![[Rarity]] !Obtainable? !Flower Health !Special |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |rowspan="9"|Yes | +20% | rowspan="7" |Spreads damage between wearers of the petal |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} | +30% |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} | +40% |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} | +50% |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} | +60% |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} | +70% |- |{{Rarity|Super}} | +80% |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} | +90% |Spreads damage between wearers of the petal. '''Affects flowers around you.''' |}It works only while it's equipped. Damage taken spreads around all the users that have it equipped in their loadout. ==Obtaining== ===[[Termite Overmind]]=== {{:Termite Overmind/Drop Relic}} ===[[Baby Termite]]=== {{:Baby Termite/Drop Relic}} ===[[Worker Termite]]=== {{:Worker Termite/Drop Relic}} ===[[Soldier Termite]]=== {{:Soldier Termite/Drop Relic}} ===[[Termite Mound]]=== {{:Termite Mound/Drop Relic}} ===Purchasing=== On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]]. ''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]'' {{Petal Price|1}} ==Strategy == This petal is best used in teaming expeditions to prevent other team members from taking all of the damage. The damaged can be neutralized if more players who own this petal actually use it alongside each other. [[Cotton]] can finish off the tiny bits of damage. Since Relics increase maximum health, it may be useful sometimes. Using it with ant eggs can summon soldier termites, but the termite's damage will also spread to you. The best way to use this feature is by using battery so the ants conduct lightning to mobs. ===[[Garden]]=== *If you are with a team of people, only then is it worth it to use this petal. Using it with any good healing petal is the best way to use the relic petal in Garden. ===[[Desert]]=== *Best used the same way as in Garden. ===[[Ocean]]=== *Beware of jellyfish because they can hit many flowers at once with their lightning, rendering relics useless. ===[[Centralia Sewers|Sewer]]=== *Best used the same way as in Garden. ===[[PvP]]=== ====Using==== *This petal can be used with squad members. ==== Against==== *You will need to use higher damaging petals against squads with Relics. ==Gallery== File:Relic (Common).png|{{R|1}} Relic. (unobtainable) File:Relic (Unusual).png|{{R|2}} Relic. File:Relic (Rare).png|{{R|3}} Relic. File:Relic (Epic).png|{{R|4}} Relic. File:Relic (Legendary).png|{{R|5}} Relic. File:Relic (Mythic).png|{{R|6}} Relic. File:Relic (Ultra).png|{{R|7}} Relic. File:Relic (Super).png|{{R|8}} Relic. File:Relic (Unique).png|{{R|9}} Relic. File:Relic.png|Relic. File:RelicPl.png|Player with Relic. ==Trivia== * This is the second petal to spread damage taken by the bearer. the others being [[Sponge]] and [[Cotton]]. ** However, this petal does more damage to the bearer than the other petals mentioned above. * You can spawn Soldier Termites from an Ant Egg if you are using Relic in your loadout. Soldier Termites are reset when Relic is unequipped. {{Petals}} [[zh:神秘的遗物]] [[Category:Petals]] [[Category:Unstackable Petals]] [[Category:Wearable Petals]] [[Category:Ant Hell]] [[Category:Termite Hell]] [[Category:Termite Mound]] [[Category:Unusual+]][[Category:Mysterious]] [[Category:Soldier Ants]] [[Category:Worker Ants]] [[Category:Baby Ants]]
