:''This page is about the normal Mysterious Stick. For the [[magic petal]] variant, see [[/Magic/|Magic Stick]].''

'''Mysterious Stick,''' shortened in-game to '''Stick''', is a [[Petals|petal]] in [https://florr.io florr.io]. It summons up to four ally [[Sandstorm]]s which each lasts for 20 seconds. Sticks are dropped by Sandstorms.

==Appearance==
Stick's appearance resembles a cartoonish wooden stick.

==Function==
''Note: The "A + B”  spawn format means that the petal takes A seconds to respawn and B seconds after respawning to do its special action/have its special action enabled.''

==Obtaining==
===[[Sandstorm]]===
{{:Sandstorm/Drop Stick}}

=== Purchasing ===
On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|5}}

==Strategy==
The Stick is a bit worthy in different conditional ways to use this petal.

Compared to the other two summoning petals, [[Beetle Egg|Beetle Eggs]] and [[Ant Egg|Ant Eggs]], the Sticks summon much more powerful mobs. 2 Sandstorms deal much more total damage than both Eggs, while their total health is higher than Beetle Eggs but lower than Ant Eggs.                                                                                                                                      -Correction- After the balancing update sandstorms were nerfed but spawn much faster and spawn more. 

Just one stick, once both Sandstorms are spawned, has over 3x the burst damage compared to a Beetle Egg. This makes it very powerful for AFK as it can build up Sandstorms for a lurer. When using Sticks, it is recommend to use them against a wall to minimize the effect of the Sandstorm’s random movement. Stick excels in AFK areas such as [[Sewers]] and Fire Ant Hell.

To obtain Sticks, the fastest way is by grinding the sandstorm zone in the top left of desert, which can yield around 200 {{Rarity|Legendary}} Sticks per hour after crafting. The box (bottom right) can also be farmed for Sticks, though the rates are lower. When grinding the box you can also get numerous other useful petals such as Beetle Egg, [[Iris]], [[Pincer]], [[Disc]] and [[Cactus]]. {{Rarity|Super}} Sandstorms are also a great way to obtain Sticks. They have around a 5% chance to drop an {{Rarity|Ultra}} stick. 

===[[Garden]]===
* The allied sandstorm is a bit fragile, but it is great for [[Hornet]] swarms, except in harder mobs. Make sure to dodge their projectiles since they can destroy the Sticks easily.
* You can also use with [[Beetle Egg|Egg]] to survive harder mobs because beetles have more strength than Sandstorms.

===[[Desert]]===
* The sandstorms can easily destroy [[Scorpion|Scorpions]]; however, it is not recommended to have Epic sticks since its rare sandstorms are very weak.
* Mythic or Ultra sticks are a must for hardest areas.

===[[Ocean]]===  
* If you are using sticks, Jellyfish might easily destroy them using their lightning, [[Crab]]s can chase sandstorms, and [[Leech]]es destroy low-tier sandstorms easily.
** This is because Eggs have more defensive ways, so they are easier to survive with.
* Do not use this petal with a crab swarm, since crabs have higher HP than sandstorms.

===[[Ellne Jungle|Jungle]]===
* Can be useful as it can damage an equal rarity [[Leafbug]] unlike the Soldier Ants from an [[Ant Egg]].
* Can act as a Meatshield towards [[Wasp]]s.

===PvP===
====Using====
* The sandstorm allies will protect you, but it is only great with Mythic or Ultra sticks.
* Pair this with rubber to avoid electrical attacks from lightning users.

====Against====
* Since Mythic+ Sticks spawn the highest rarities possible, they still have one weakness to it: its usage respawn time. Non-Stick users can use this weakness to their advantage as they take a long time to summon up to 2 sandstorms at a time (unless they're using ultra Sticks).
** However, if they do spawn, they have a lot of health, but it is best to stay away from it and use any ranged petals, [[Orange|Oranges]], [[Yin Yang|Yin Yangs]], and/or any high DPS petals to take them out one at a time.

==Gallery==
<gallery>
File:Mysterious Stick (Common).png|{{R|1}} Stick. (Unobtainable)

</gallery>

==Trivia==
* All types of summoner mobs besides Square, such as [[Ant Egg]] and [[Beetle Egg]] will now have a smaller size on all rarities.
* It is one of the 4 summoning petals. The others are [[Beetle Egg]], [[Egg (Ant)|Ant Egg]] and [[Square]].
** Stick and Square are the only summoner petals that aren't eggs.
* Stick is the only petal to start at Epic rarity.
* In an unknown date during 2023, there was a secret change to the stick petal as ally sandstorms can only last for certain amount of seconds before they respawn, forcing the stick to go on usage respawn cooldown, just to summon a brand-new sandstorm, with the cycle endlessly repeating if the stick is present in player's primary build.
* Sticks used to have health and damage stats themselves, but they were removed.

'''Magic Stick,''' shortened in-game to Stick, is a [[Magic Petals|magic petal]] in [https://florr.io florr.io]. It summons Sandstorms like normal [[Mysterious Stick|Stick]], but at a cost of Mana. Magic Stick is dropped by [[Sandstorm]]s with {{Rarity|4}}+ [[Mana Orb]] equipped.

==Appearance==
Magic Stick looks like a cyan Stick.

==Function==

Magic Stick will spawn friendly [[Sandstorm]]<nowiki/>s when you have enough mana for some seconds. It can spawn up to 15 Sandstorm at once as long as you have enough mana. Sandstorm rarities always are always 1 rarity lower than Magic Stick, except Unique Magic Stick (you obviously can't spawn boss, like old Super Egg and Stick). Summoned Sandstorm lasts for 10 seconds if alive, and they always follow the player current moving direction.

Spawn cost is doubled throughout the rarities. Unique Magic Stick is like Super Magic Stick but cost less mana, like how old Super Egg and Stick take less time to summon Mythic mobs.

==Obtainment==
===[[Sandstorm]]===
{{:Sandstorm/Drop Stick}}

===Purchasing===
On certain days, Magic Stick can be purchased from the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''{{Petal Price|5}}
==Strategies==
*Magic Stick thrives wherever normal Stick does but with some differences.
**This petal is immensely powerful only when paired with magic petals close to its own rarity as it consumes much more mana than other magic petals like [[Stinger/Magic|Magic Stinger]] or [[Third Eye/Magic|Magic Eye]] to balance out the fact that it essentially acts like 7 [[Mysterious Stick|Sticks]] in 1 inventory slot.
**Similar to normal Stick, avoids melee combat at all cost as the petal itself has garbage stats like the normal Stick and will be instantly destroy by high rarity mobs. Swapping loadout is very inefficient just like normal Stick but Magic Stick provides more self survivability when you solo farm (which is 99% what you will be doing when you are using this petal) as left over mana from the petals you use to maintain summoned Sandstorms will come in handy when the Magic Stick gets destroyed at close range, allow you to use Magic Eye or Magic Stinger do defend yourself or bubble away from mobs.
**For farming in general, the Sandstorms' unpredictable movement when the user is stationary doesn't really harm you. If you can maintain all the Sandstorms, 15 Sandstorms are no joke, especially with {{Rarity|Super|Super+}} Magic Stick which spawns Ultra Sandstorms combine with [[Talents|Summoner Talents]], are insanely good at shredding everything apart including your Mana. ''From here are just speculations as no detailed info on Super M Stick usage has been known to me:'' To consistently maintain a {{Rarity|Super}} Magic Stick you would need 1 {{Rarity|Ultra}} Mana Orb, 3-4 {{Rarity|Ultra}} [[Leaf/Magic|Magic Leaf]], 1 {{Rarity|Mythic}}/{{Rarity|Ultra}} [[Cactus/Magic|Magic Cactus]], all of these petals by themselves are pretty hard to obtain already but supposed you have all the needed ingredients, having 7 Super Stick with just 1 Super craft attempt is still great nonetheless as {{Rarity|Super}} Sticks are still rarer than the Ultras you need to have to maintain all Sandstorms.
===Obtaining===
*This petal can be obtained by killing {{Rarity|Epic|Epic+}} [[Sandstorm|Sandstorms]] in Desert while having {{Rarity|Epic|Epic+}} [[Mana Orb]] in your current loadout. This will replace the normal Stick drop of the Sandstorm with Magic Stick of the same rarity as the Mana Orb you have even if you aren't supposed to get that drop at high rarity, like other Magic Petals.
*The most efficient method of farming Sticks will also applied to Magic Stick, specifically:
**The top left box, this spawns pretty much only {{Rarity|Legendary}}/{{Rarity|Mythic}} Sandstorm so is really suitable for mid-games who desperately need {{Rarity|Ultra}} Magic Stick to farm other petals. Rate is pretty high (for mid-game obviously) at probably 300~400 L/h and 2 or 3 M if you are lucky so expect maybe 3-4 days for 1 Ultra Magic Stick if you already have some Ultras. For {{Rarity|Ultra|Ultra+}} players this zone will not cut it as Mythic spawn rate here is quite low for the only pure Sandstorm zone in the game, even actively farming for those players in this zone would only brought the rate up maybe 2k-3k/h. For a estimate of roughly 150k+ L for 1 {{Rarity|Super}} (that's if the RNG is with you), you don't want to farm here late-game.
**The {{Rarity|Ultra}} zone of Desert, which is the bottom right box is where you want to farm Magic Stick late game. Despite being a general Ultra zone, the rates should still be better here compared to Sandstorm zone as {{Rarity|Super}} Sandstorms spawn here and with a good enough Squad you should be getting around 75 M Magic Sticks/h and a 5% Ultra drop from Super Sandstorm (if you have Ultra Magic Orb which you probably don't) so still much better rates than other zones. As Sandstorms have basically no mob AI to aggro on you AFK farming is a terrible if you are only here for the Sticks but this zone serves a very good purpose of having very good rates of everything else so even actively farming for just Sandstorms might gave you more of other petals good petals like [[Iris]], [[Pincer]], [[Beetle Egg]], etc. (especially [[Cactus/Magic|Magic Cactuses]] which helps with using Magic Sticks) than Magic Sticks themselves.
*This petal can also be bought from the shop, see price above. This method should only be considered for those who farms for actual money.
{{Zone|Garden}}
*
{{Zone|Desert}}
*Summoned Sandstorms are very good meatshield for Ultra zone because Desert mobs are pretty tanky and has high body damage compared to mobs of other are except [[Hel]]. Take {{Rarity|Super}} Magic Stick which spawns Ultra Sandstorms for example, with max [[Talents|Summoner Talent]], each Sandstorm has 5686200 HP and 45489.6 DMG each.
{{Zone|Ocean}}
*Sandstorms annihilates every single mob in [[Ocean]] due to their low stats generally speaking
**[[Bubble (Mob)|Bubbles]] get popped as they should.
**[[Leech|Leeches]]' low health means that with just a few Sandstorms, they will be shredded before even getting close to you. Even if they did, the healing wouldn't be enough anyways.
**[[Crab|Crabs]] can still poses a threat at high rarity as now, instead of charging in and get stopped by Sandstorms half way, they phase through summons and come straight at you.
**[[Jellyfish|Jellyfishes]] at {{Rarity|Ultra|Ultra-}} gets destroyed if you are able to maintain the Sandstorms and keep a distance. Super
{{Zone|Jungle}}
*Strategies exclusively for the Jungle zone.
{{Zone|Hel}}
===PvE===
*Strategies exclusively for fighting Hel mobs.
*PvE stands for Player versus Enemy, which is used to refer to players fighting mobs (like the Hel Wasp, which is present in Hel). This section has to include petals fighting mobs, and MUST NOT include petals fighting other players.
===PvP: Using===
*This petal doesn't have much use offensively for PvP as Sandstorms don't aggro on players and move unpredictably so you can't have them to both attack other players and defend yourself. Though its defensive uses are superior to every other summon provided that you can maintain the summoned Sandstorms.
**Sandstorms are extremely good as a large wall to sponge massive amount of damage that other summon will otherwise be annihilate way to fast to have any value against other players, especially {{Rarity|Super|Super+}} squads which will '''shred''' through Ultras like nothing. Being able to consistently maintain 15 buffed {{Rarity|Ultra}} Sandstorms makes you basically invincible. This pair extremely well with Magic Eye as you can zap other players from a distance without having to worry they can [[Lightning]] or [[Battery]] you back as Sandstorms will absorb all bounces if they are close to each other.
**With the Feb 6th, 2025 [[Updates|update]], Sandstorms now phase through non-flower entities so generally PvP aren't affected beside summoner interactions.
===PvP: Against===
*You can use Magic Stick to fight against Magic Stick user, be aware that Sandstorms will pass through each other and can damage you without expectation. Do not use Egg against it as Sandstorm is very durable and capable of dealing high damage upon contact, unless the enemy use Mythic- Magic Stick.
*[[Salt]] has almost no use against the user as you will reflect the damage back to the mobs instead of the user.
*[[Battery]] can counter the enemy, but be aware that Sandstorms can deal massive amount of damage to you and kill you in no time. [[Lightning]] can be used too, as it can chain on the user, but it's very risky as Lightning itself provide no defense.
*[[Third Eye/Magic|Magic Eye]] can be used to damage the user through the Sandstorm, but the petal needs mana to deal sufficient damage.
*[[Jelly]] is a good counter against Sandstorm as it can knock the Sandstorm far away from you. However, do not hit the user with Jelly as you may give them more time to prepare for the fight.
{{Zone|Ant Hell}}
*Strategies exclusively for the Ant Hell zone. This section also should include [[Ant Hole]]s from Garden, [[Fire Ant Burrow]]s from Desert and [[Termite Mound]]s from Jungle.
{{Zone|Sewers}}
*Strategies exclusively for the Sewers zone.
==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">
File:Magic Stick (Common).png|{{R|1}} Magic Stick (Unobtainable)
File:Magic Stick (Unusual).png|{{R|2}} Magic Stick (Unobtainable)
File:Magic Stick (Rare).png|{{R|3}} Magic Stick (Unobtainable)
File:Magic Stick (Epic).png|{{R|4}} {{PAGENAME}}.
File:Magic Stick (Legendary).png|{{R|5}} {{PAGENAME}}.
File:Magic Stick (Mythic).png|{{R|6}} {{PAGENAME}}.
File:Magic Stick (Ultra).png|{{R|7}} {{PAGENAME}}.
File:Magic Stick (Super).png|{{R|8}} {{PAGENAME}}.
File:Magic Stick (Unique).png|{{R|9}} {{PAGENAME}}.
File:Magic Stick.png|{{PAGENAME}}.
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*Magic Stick used to summon a total of 30 Sandstorms before being halved to 15 on December 19th, 2024.
*Magic Stick is currently the only magic petal to start at {{Rarity|Epic}} rarity. It's also one of the two petals to start at {{Rarity|Epic}}, with the other being Stick.
*Magic Stick is known as the best summoner petal along with [[Pharaoh's Crown]], as multiple {{Rarity|Ultra}} Sandstorms are good at dealing massive amount of damage, providing good defense wall, farming, PvP-ing and {{Rarity|Super}} hunting.
**On September 12th, 2025, the spawn cost of Magic Stick was increased by 50% to substantially nerf its performance.
*Magic Stick used to have an attribute called '''Maintenance cost''', which is the upkeep of the summoned Sandstorms. This was removed in an unknown update.
*{{Rarity|Super}} Magic Stick is the most purchased petals due to its powerful ability to spawn multiple {{Rarity|Ultra}} Sandstorms.
*Pehiley is the first and the current owner of {{Rarity|Unique}} Magic Stick.
*bbaillies is the first player to craft a {{Rarity|Super}} Magic Stick.
*The petal is nerfed (cost more mana to spawn) along with Magic Stinger nerf.

'''Nazar Amulet''' is a [[Petals|petal]] in [https://florr.io florr.io]. Its function consists of reducing some instances of damage taken by the flower by 98%. Nazar Amulet is dropped by [[Beetle/Nazar|Nazar Beetle]].

==Appearance==
Nazar Amulet consists of a blue amulet with a concentric circle pattern in the middle, with a white, light blue, and black circle within that decreases in size. It is very similar to the [https://emojipedia.org/nazar-amulet 🧿] emoji.

===Purchasing===
On certain days, Nazar Amulet can be purchased from the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|5|Mythic}}


==Strategies==

* Do not use this petal with [[Sponge]] because that will cause the petal to only affect a tiny fraction of the damage you took from one attack.
* This petal can be useful if you want to block hits that might kill you. Be aware that low rarity Nazar have a lengthy reload time when all charges are used. This wasn't a problem back then, but it got nerfed on an unknown date.

===Obtaining===
*[[Beetle/Nazar|Nazar Beetles]] are somewhat uncommon occurrences in Desert. You should farm in [[Pyramid]] to find Nazar Beetles since they spawn far more frequently there. 
*Mythic+ [[Beetle/Nazar|Nazar Beetles]] are very likely to drop Epic Nazar Amulet, which reloads faster then Common - Rare Nazar Amulet. 

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">

</gallery>

==Trivia==
*Nazar Amulet is now one of the rarest petals in the game, likely just more common than [[Square]].
*Nazar Amulet is the only petal that cannot be dropped at certain rarities. {{Rarity|Unusual}}, {{Rarity|Rare}}, {{Rarity|Legendary}} and {{Rarity|Mythic}} rarities can only be obtained by Crafting.
*Nazar Amulets are one of the two Amulets in the game, with the other one being the normal [[Amulet]].
*Nazar Amulets used to block single damage instance taken by the flower, but this was changed in March 2025 to affect multiple based on Charges stat. 
*If you look carefully, you can see the outermost blue part of the petal moving and shifting around, similar to the Blob skin from [https://digdig.io digdig.io].
*If Nazar was stackable, it would be too overpowered as they can help tank lots of damage.
*Nazar is the only petal to start with the letter "N."

title: Oracle
content:
{{Background|图片=Oceanb.png|大小=2000px}} {{Mob Template Infobox | name = Oracle | image = Oracle.png | rarity = {{Rarity|1}} | health = Invulnerable | move = Yes (Random Curves) | status = Passive | spawn = [[Ocean]] | special = *Changes your normal crafting menu into the blue Oracle crafting menu. *Craft a single petal instantly at certain amount of petals. *Predicts petals if you (or anyone) says a specific phrase. }} The '''Oracle''' is a [[:Category:Special Entities|Special Entity]] that can be found in [[Ocean]]. == Appearance == The Oracle appears as a large yellow Digger equipped the Tentacle cutter skin and the All-Seeing face skin. It is quite large, approximately the size of a {{R|3}}+ [[Sponge]]. When the Oracle moves, it slowly drifts around in a sine wave motion, periodically switching directions like [[Jellyfish]]. It often flees away at a certain direction at a fast speed. === Function === When you get close to it, your crafting menu will turn blue. Then you can insert a number of petals and you will get at least one guaranteed craft. The amount of petals inserted will increase with the rarity of petal crafted. It has a cooldown of 28 minutes after crafting. The Oracle speaks of a certain "price" for each craft. {| class="article-table" |- ! Rarity Crafted ! Required amount of previous rarity |- |{{Rarity|Common}} | Not Craftable |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |7 |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |11 |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |19 |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |34 |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |65 |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |128 |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |253 |- |{{Rarity|Eternal}} |2,503 |} The petals required for the guaranteed craft are the same as the petals required to craft a higher tier petal on average. These are not random numbers, because they are based on calculations. === Quotes === There are a lot of lines that Oracle speaks periodically from time to time. Much like [[Titan|the titan]], [[Trader]] or [[Gambler]], the Oracle utilizes the chat feature to communicate. The quotes are below.
'''Click "Expand" to show every quote.'''
===Idle=== * A vague anomaly when one considers this particular future. Troubling * Again, again, and once again * And what if I do? * And what if I don't? * Beware of stairs * Beware what you wish for * Can one ever be so sure? * Choose wisely * Commit! Or don't * Do not fear to commit * Don't mind me * Fate cannot be denied * Ho ho! Imagine the outcome * I appear to be suffering from a blind spot * I can see the future * I cannot be held responsible for all the outcomes * I have a prophecy to deliver * I scry with my little eye * I see you dying of extreme old age. Or perhaps a very old person will kill you. One or the other * If is not a word I use lightly * If not now, when? * If only it were all this simple * If that is what one wants * Ignore all previous prophecies * Ignore all prior utterances * Interpret this! * It is ever thus * Let if be all that we see or seem * Precisely as planned * Prepare for unforeseen consequences * So many destinies * So many paths to choose * Some fates are indeterminate * Some futures cannot be bought * That is one possible course * The cost of certainty * The Oracle sees all * There are no guarantees * This prophecy supersedes all previous prophecies * We can make a fair exchange * You will die in a house fire ===When a {{Rarity|s}} petal is about to be crafted=== * Ah yes, the prophecy... it should happen any time now... * It was foretold, this is the moment... * I was waiting for this moment...
== Interface == The Oracle's interface is dark teal, contrasting the warm color themes of the menus it replaces, like the [[Crafting|Crafting menu]] and the [[Trader|Trading menu]]. It also has the ability to see all of your petals, including the ones that you have equipped. One singular crafting slot appears. Upon clicking a petal that can be crafted through the Oracle, exactly the required amount of petals to craft the same petal of a higher rarity (refer to the table above). === Overview === Crafting using the Oracle is completely guaranteed (so long as you have enough petals). However, you can only craft one petal at a time: for example, to craft a Mythic petal, you can only use 65 Legendary petals, as it won't accept more: furthermore, it has a cool down of 28 minutes. To activate it, move within range of the Oracle and press '''C''' or touch the Oracle.
'''The amount of petals required to craft a single petal are below, alongside the average amount of petals received from crafting.'''
{| class="article-table" |- ! Rarity ! Required petals to craft next rarity through Oracle ! Statistical average required petals to craft next rarity |- |{{Rarity|2}} |7 {{Rarity|1}} petals |6.04625 {{Rarity|1}} petals |- |{{Rarity|3}} |11 {{Rarity|2}} petals |10.3125 {{Rarity|2}} petals |- |{{Rarity|4}} |19 {{Rarity|3}} petals |18.125 {{Rarity|3}} petals |- |{{Rarity|5}} |34 {{Rarity|4}} petals |33.75 {{Rarity|4}} petals |- |{{Rarity|6}} |65 {{Rarity|5}} petals |65 {{Rarity|5}} petals |- |{{Rarity|7}} |128 {{Rarity|6}} petals |127.5 {{Rarity|6}} petals |- |{{Rarity|8}} |253 {{Rarity|7}} petals |252.5 {{Rarity|7}} petals |- |{{Rarity|Eternal}} |2,503 {{Rarity|8}} petals |2,502.5 {{Rarity|8}} petals |} When the specific number of petals is inserted, as shown above, the crafting chance is 100%. This indicates that crafting with the Oracle is not worth it, unless crafting from Legendary to Mythic, in which case crafting with or without the Oracle give the same average amount of petals.{{Game Info}}
== Wish == Every 20 hours, one may ask the Oracle to predict when a petal will appear in the [[Shop]], by sending the following message in [[Chat]] near the Oracle:
'''''Oracle, oracle, make me a prediction about [rarity] [petal]'''''
 Note that there is no period at the end, and there are spaces between commas. Also, the first letter of the rarity and petal can be either uppercase or lowercase, but when sent it will be automatically changed to uppercase. The Shop works though changing its petals with each cycle, each individual cycle lasting 20 hours before the petals rotate. There is a system to predict when a petal appears in the shop, though it resets whenever a new petal is added. If you want to make a prediction about {{RarityTest|Legendary}} [[Yggdrasil]], say "'''''Oracle, oracle, make me a prediction about Legendary [[Yggdrasil]]'''''" {| class="article-table" |- ! Distance ! Response |- | 0 | It is imminent |- | 1 | Soon |- | 2 | Eventually |- | 3 | Perhaps some futures are just indeterminate |- | 4+ / still in 20h wish cooldown | The future is too cloudy to tell |}{{Game Info}} == Trivia == *The lines of Oracle are directly referenced from a hero named [https://dota2.fandom.com/wiki/Oracle/Responses Oracle] on most of his responses from Dota 2. *You can get in total of 2 [[achievements]] from Oracle: The All-Seeing One (craft a petal with Oracle) and It Was Destiny (craft an {{Rarity|Ultra}}+ petal with Oracle). * As of 5th January 2025, the Oracle and Target dummies are the only flower-like NPC not to have petals orbiting itself. This contrasts other NPCs, like Trader and Titan: Trader has 8 [[Coin|Coins]] of rarities {{Rarity|Common}} - {{Rarity|Super}} surrounding itself; Titan, which uses a combination of Unique petals. * This is the second NPC to not be able to be attacked (represented by the yellow health bar). The first was [[Trader]]. ** Some mobs also have a yellow health bar, which signifies invincibility and the inability to take damage. The first was [[Target Dummy]], and the second was [[Fire Ant Burrow]] (once all the mobs it spawns are defeated, it despawns and and drops loot to those eligible.) * The blue color of the Oracle's interface resembles [[Ocean]] and the dark blue theme of Ocean. This can be compared to how the Trading menu is yellow, resembling the light yellow/orange theme of [[Desert]]. * When you craft a {{Rarity|Super}} with the Oracle, it will say "'''I was waiting for this moment'''". This may suggest that it always wanted to see a {{Rarity|Super}} petal being crafted. *The Oracle's '''28 m'''inutes (28m) cooldown may be a reference to [[M28]]. * The Oracle has by far the most quotes out of every NPC. * The Oracle can't be pushed by mobs. As such, it can get in front of {{Rarity|Super}} Mobs and block their way, specifically the {{Rarity|Super}} [[Crab]], although it will not go to where high rarity Mobs spawn. * The Oracle seems to be a reference to the "All Seeing" face and the "Tentacles" spinner from [https://digdig.io/ digdig.io], or maybe vice versa. * If the Oracle has been corrupted, its body will turn pink with its eyes elongated. * The Oracle can be found in the bubble zone beneath the spawn point, although it used to be found sometimes very far away. * In an update during November 2025, all Special Entities couldn't have their menus' opened by touching it. They only way is to click "c" or the button in the bottom left corner. == Gallery == File:Happy happy happy.jpg|An Oracle. File:Sgtj dfuggbsfugjhv.jpg|An Oracle says “Some fates are indeterminate.” File:Corrupted-oracle.png|M28 Event (it looks a bit weird...) {{Mobs}} [[Category:Special Entities]] [[Category:Interactive Entities]] [[Category:Non-stationary mobs]] [[Category:Florr.io]] [[Category:Ocean]]

'''Pearl''' is a petal in florr.io introduced in the [[Ocean]] release. When left-clicked, it will throw its petal away, and when left click is released, it will return to the flower, hitting anything in its way. The '''Pearl''' is dropped by [[Shell (Mob)|Shells]].

==Appearance==
The Pearl is just like a [[Heavy]], but instead of having black color, it is a faint yellow color, along with a darker color as the outline, and a white shine at the top right.

==Function==
This petal works like [[Jelly]], but has to be moved manually and it moves far from the flower and possibly toss the mob towards the flower.

''Note: The "A + B" respawn format means that the petal takes A seconds to respawn and B seconds after respawning to do its special action/have its special action enabled.''

|}Pearl can be thrown by the flower on left-click, follows its owner when it is too far and goes away from the owner when it is too close. When the left mouse button is released, it will fly back to its owner.

==Obtaining==
===[[Shell (Mob)|Shell]]===
{{:Shell (Mob)/Drop Pearl}}

===Purchasing===
On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|1}}

==Strategy==
The '''{{PAGENAME}}''' has the same health as rock, but it's not good for survival as there is an additional delay for being thrown. In its thrown state, the pearl gets knocked back easily, meaning it will usually take a few seconds for it to deal the entirety of its damage. Still, it unlocks quite a few tactics and is a menace if one is always on defense, as it does not shrink how [[Heavy]] does.

It is advised to spend talents on Petal Health due to Pearl massive damage and health that can severely damage a mob.

===[[Garden]]===
*Try to throw a pearl against a Hornet/Spider to push the mob away, and deal a lot of damage.
*It can destroy every mob in its path if used right.
===[[Desert]]===
*Do not use often at few desert zones, Scorpions will likely poison you, and beetles will try to chase and tackle you
**With higher rarities of full build pearl, you can easily toss anything which is in the path of the Pearl.
===[[Ocean]]===
*It is good at bubble zones, and alright at Shell zones. Unlike lightning, trying to toss it near shells will cause several shells to chase you.
*Having 8-10 pearls are good for several zones, like the weak crabs in Crab Kingdom
===[[PvP]]===
====Using====
*Pearl is one of the strongest melee pvp petals in the game. On flowers, it's dps far exceeds stinger. You should play to pearl's health: you should periodically click the pearl in and out, so that it is constantly moving, and anything that comes close can be hit and destroyed very quickly. If you are defending, you should keep pearl following you in a close trail by clicking rapidly, but do not hold click, as that means pearl will be too far away to attack. Pearl is great against stinger users if you use pollen, and it also does great damage against summoner users. Swapping [[Yin Yang]] continously can help you aim better for your opponent, making the attack even more deadly.
====Against====
*To kill a pearl user, you must outdps them. This can be done with tomato or stinger. However, this is very, very, very difficult to do if they are using sponge. To successfully kill them in this case, you must use an assassin build with bubbles and stingers, so that you can do a large amount of damage in one strike, then quickly run away before they can kill you.

==Gallery==

==Trivia==
*Most people are unaware of the power that Pearls can bring, as it's an uncommon strategy in the PvP area.
*Before the December 29, 2022 update, {{R|6}}+ pearls had a larger size visually while in the map.
*The first player to obtain {{R|8}} Pearl was twang-777.
*At some time around end of November 2025, Pearl was nerfed so it's no longer got attracted to mobs. M28 confirmed this as he stated "Pearl is a shootable petal"..
*If Pearl pathway is totally blocked from the player, it will just simply despawn and respawn in the petal orbit.

''This page is for normal peas. For the [[Magic Petals|magic petal]] variant, see [[Blueberries]].''

'''Peas''' is a petal in Florr.io. It is released into four peas that are launched in a straight line when the player attacks or defends.

==Appearance==
Four small green circular petals with a dark green outline. They are arranged in a square-like formation with a slight overlap and a small empty space in the center, similar to [[Grapes]] and [[Sand]].

==Function==
''Note: The "A + B" respawn format means that the petal takes A seconds to respawn and B seconds after respawning to do its special action/have its special action enabled.''

''Note: Health stats are for the whole petal health. Each'' ''pea have 1/4 of the full health.''

Upon attacking or defending, fires peas in four directions at 90 degree angles to each other. These peas travel for 15 tiles or until they hit something. The direction the peas fire is random.

==Obtaining==
===[[Centipede]]===
{{:Centipede/Drop Peas}}

===[[Mantis]]===
{{:Mantis/Drop Peas}}

===Purchasing===
On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|1}}

==Strategy==
Despite heavy nerfs, peas are still a good petal for dealing ranged damage against swarms of mobs. Peas damage increases in mid range combat but decreases overall in close quarters. Single target damage will increase, but since the peas won't fire, it removes group damage.

To make the most of peas, get as close to a mob as possible without letting the peas touch it prematurely before firing. Peas are best used when spammed since it mitigates their RNG factor.

===[[Garden]]===
*Being a ranged petal, Peas are great against [[Hornet|Hornets]]. They can deal moderate damage to large swarms from far away.
**Peas do best when mobs are more spread out, so users should try to prevent the Hornets from clumping by staying fairly still, only moving slightly to dodge missiles.
*Peas are great against swarms of weak mobs. They're useful for clearing out an area after stronger mobs have been killed. Ironically, peas are strong against [[Centipede]]s, the mob that drops them.

===[[Desert]]=== 
*Peas are worse in desert since mobs deal all their damage with their body, forcing pea users into close combat. They are still useful against [[Sandstorm]]s and [[Desert Centipede]]s since they're best taken out from a distance and tend to spawn in swarms.

===[[Ocean]]===  
*Peas are decent against ocean mobs since range is useful. However, it lacks burst damage that petals such as the [[Missile]] have, so it's not the most optimal petal to use. It is good at thinning out an advancing rush of mobs.
*Peas are near useless against [[Starfish]] since their DPS can't match the Starfish's health regen.

===[[PvP]]===
====Using==== 
*Peas struggle to deal damage against a single target. However, when spammed, they're almost guaranteed to make a hit on nearby players every time.
**Peas are also decent at inflicting knockback on opponents, keeping them further away from the user.

====Against==== 
*Keeping petals in the defensive position, especially with [[Light]], [[Pollen]] or high health petals, can prevent peas from hitting the flower.
*[[Bubble]]s can be used to quickly approach a peas user without taking much damage. Once in close range, it'll be difficult for the pea user to survive.

== Gallery ==
<gallery>
File:Peas (Common).png|{{R|1}} Peas.
File:Peas (Unusual).png|{{R|2}} Peas.
File:Peas (Rare).png|{{R|3}} Peas.

File:Peas (Unique).png|{{R|9}} Peas.
File:Peas.png|Peas.
File:Peas with Duplicator.png|Peas when applied with Duplicator talent.
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*Before the release of the [[Duplicator|Duplicator Talent]], its original description was "4 in 1 deal". This also applied to the [[Grapes]] petal.
*As of the 30th August 2022 update, higher rarity peas are no longer poisonous and the poisonous ones are now [[Grapes]].
*Before the 29th December 2022 update, {{R|Mythic}} and {{R|Ultra}} Peas used to have a larger size visually.
*It is one of the 3 petals that are based on a vegetable, others are [[Carrot]] and [[Corn]].

title: Petal

title: Petals
content:
'''Petals''' are the main tools and weapons used by all players in [https://florr.io florr.io]. All players start out with five petal slots and can gain up to 5 additional slots (adding up to 10) with the Loadout [[Talent]]. Players also have an equal number of secondary petal slots. A petal in a secondary slot can be switched with the petal in the primary slot directly above. There are currently '''117''' petals; however, [[M28]], the developer, is going to add more in the future. When colliding with [[Mobs]], flowers and other Petals, they deal damage. Petals also receive damage from collisions, which lowers their HP. When a petal's HP is depleted, it will be destroyed temporarily, and it will reappear after a certain amount of time (the petal's cooldown time). When swapping and equipping a new petal, the minimum reload time is 2.5 seconds. The minimum reload time can be decreased by 0.5 seconds 5 times, to a minimum of 0.0 seconds, by upgrading the Reload [[Talents|Talent]]. This rule only applies when first equipping a petal to replace another, not when reloading the equipped petal. Petals with an original reload time of 2.5 seconds or more are unaffected by this rule. The "2.0s + 1.0s" cooldown time format means that the petal takes 2 seconds to reload, and 1 second after being reloaded to perform its special action (if it has one). If the minimum reload time is 2.5 seconds: *The petal will take 2.5 seconds (instead of 2.0 seconds) to spawn when it is first equipped, and will take 1.0 seconds to perform its special action (e.x. heal for rose) *When the petal is destroyed, it will go with the normal 2.0 second reload time until the petal is unequipped or swapped out. Players can switch petals by using the number keys. Each slot corresponds to a number based on its location with the number appearing below each slot. Pressing this number switches the active petal and the secondary petal. Petals can also be switched by clicking on either or the petals that the player would like to switch. Pressing R completely switches the inventory. All petals obtained by the player during game play will have their icons saved in their Inventory and [[Crafting]]. All petals range in [[Rarity]], but players can craft higher Rarity petals. Some petals don't have lower rarities (e.g. Antennae starts at Rare rarity, while Basil starts at Mythic rarity). Petals are dropped by mobs once they die. Petal drops are shared in PvE, meaning everyone can pick the drops up if they do at least 5% damage to the mob. Almost every petal can also be purchased from the [[Shop]]. You cannot steal drops from someone else, even in PvP, where drops are not shared. Magic variants of certain normal petals can also be dropped by mobs once you have a Mana Orb equipped. They have improved stats and/or abilities compared to their base version, but they need mana to be spawned or even maintained. They are called [[Magic Petals]]. == Petal List ==

Prevents revival for a certain duration afterwards.'' | On death, the flower turns undead for some time and cannot be revived. | [[beetle/Mummy|Mummy Beetle]], [[Beetle/Pharaoh|Pharaoh Beetle]] | [[File:Bandage.png|100px]] | 102 |- |[[Pharaoh's Crown]] |''Allows the wearer to summon Undead Beetles at your side.'' |Summons undead Mummy Beetles every second that lasts for a number of seconds as duration. |[[Beetle/Pharaoh|Pharaoh Beetle]] | |103 |- |[[Totem]] |''Grants health regen for a certain duration after killing a mob.'' |Upon killing a mob, the flower will receive health regeneration for a certain period of seconds. |[[Tomb]] | |104 |- |[[Triangle]] |''Each copy of this petal equipped grants additional damage to it.'' |Each copy of this petal of the same rarity equipped grants extra damage to the petal. |[[Soldier Fire Ant]] | |105 |- |[[Electric Web]] |''It's a little less sticky, but a bit more shocking.'' |Acts like the normal [[Web]], but it deals lightning damage to mobs inside of it. |[[Spider/Mecha|Mecha Spider]] | |106 |- |[[Blood Sacrifice]] | ''On death, summons a mob to fight again''.
''Mob type depends on where the sacrifice happens.''
''Cannot be used in low mob rarity areas.'' |Summons random mobs of the same rarity on death. It cannot be used on places with difficulty scale 20 or less. |Natural spawn in [[Hel]] | |107 |- |[[Sawblade]] |''This looks like it'd hurt.'' |A petal that deals a lot of damage and has a lot of health. |[[Assembler|Assembling]] | |108 |- |[[Clay]] |''It sticks to dust in the air to become bigger and stronger.'' |Its damage and hitbox size is increased based on the player's total travel distance. |[[Silverfish]] | |109 |- |[[Dust]] |''Sometimes it sticks together, sometimes it doesn't.'' |Clumps into a [[Sand]] while on defensive or neutral mode and expands as a [[Light]] when on offensive mode. |[[Silverfish]], [[Tomb]] | |110 |- |[[Broccoli]] |''Explodes when it hits something. For reasons unknown.'' |Explodes into missiles when it loses all of its health. |[[Baby Fire Ant]], [[Garbage]] | |111 |- |[[Lentil]] |''Increases petal attraction to enemies.'' |Increases petal attraction range, allowing petals to gravitate towards targets a larger distance. |[[Beetle/Mummy|Mummy Beetle]], [[Beetle/Pharaoh|Pharaoh Beetle]] | |112 |- |[[Dead Leaf]] |''Increases petal health but makes them reload slower.'' |Increases all petal's health while also increasing their reload |[[Soldier Ant/Diver|Soldier Diver Ant]] | |113 |- |[[Soul Splitter]] |''Fractures your soul into two (three for {{Rarity|Unique}}'') |Splits your flower into multiple parts, which lets you control them individually. |[[M28]]??? | |114 |- |[[Goggles|Paranormal Goggles]] |''Allows finding some paranormal activity.'' |Allows you to see and interact with Ghosts up to the petal's rarity, but decreases your vision. |[[Spider/Mecha|Mecha Spider]] | |115 |- |[[Bean]] |''Deals more damage if there are no allied flowers around you.'' |Its damage is increased by 5 times when you are not in a squad. |[[Beetle/Nazar|Nazar Beetle]] | |116 |- |[[Champion's Crown]] |''Gives flowers around you a {{Rarity|Super}} Basic.'' ''You receive credit for damage dealt using it.'' |Gives 25 players around you a {{Rarity|Super}} Basic. every damage dealt by the Basic count towards looting. |Winning a Battle Royale in [[Rift]]. | |117 |- |} ==Gallery== File:Alphabetical petals 2.png|Petals arranged alphabetically in the inventory, showing the highest Rarity versions of them (Stack display). File:Orderized rarity petals new.png|Petals categorized Rarity and alphabetically from top (Super) to bottom (Common). ==Trivia== *A total of 118 petals can be currently found in the game. **30 of those petals are from legacy florr.io, while the other 88 were added ever since florr.io was revamped. However, the 30 legacy petals have some differences from their legacy states: ***The starting rarity of some petals may not match with their legacy rarity; ***Certain petals are missing, such as [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Heavy Heavy]; ***The {{Rarity|Eternal}} and {{Rarity|Unique}} rarity is missing; ***[https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/{{Rarity|Rare}} Rose], [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Peas_(Epic) {{Rarity|Epic}}] and [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Peas_(Legendary) {{Rarity|Legendary}} Peas], [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Heaviest Heaviest] and [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Fast Fast], [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Twin Twin] and [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Triplet Triplet] were renamed to, respectively: [[Dahlia]], [[Grapes]], [[Heavy]] and [[Light]]; ***Multiple rarity variants of a certain petal (such as [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Cactus Cactus] and its [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Cactus_(Epic) {{Rarity|Epic}} variant]) are now in the same lineage of petals (e.g. [[Cactus]]); ***Some petals are dropped by different mobs, and only of a certain rarity or above; ***[[Dandelion]] debuffs flowers by 20% for 15 seconds while stacking, and is not shootable anymore but goes farther away on offensive mode than most other petals, and receives one extra Dandelion at both the {{Rarity|Mythic}} and the {{Rarity|Ultra}} rarity variants; ***[[Heavy]] slows down petal rotation speed; ***[[Honey]] can be dropped to the ground, disappears after some time, attracts mobs to its location while it's on the ground, has a mob rarity limit and mobs can develop immunity to its attraction; ***[[Salt]] reflects 20% damage to flowers, 50% damage to mobs of the same rarity, (n*3) 50% damage to mobs of (n-1) rarity below it (whereas n is the number of the rarities the mob is below the petal, and 1 being the same rarity) and less damage to mobs above its own rarity; ***[[Stinger]] is now 3 petals in 1 at {{Rarity|Mythic}} rarity, and not {{Rarity|Legendary}}, and finally; ***[[Yin Yang]] changes the petal rotation orientation at 1, 3, 5, 7 and 9 Yin Yangs in one build, while increasing the petal orbit layers at 2, 4, 6, 8 and 10 Yin Yangs in one build (layers start at 1 with zero Yin Yangs, then 2 layers at 2, 3 at 4, 4 at 6, 5 at 8 and all 10 at 10). **There are 56 petals starting at the {{Rarity|Common}} rarity, 21 at the {{Rarity|Unusual}} rarity, 10 at the {{Rarity|Rare}} rarity, 2 at the {{Rarity|Epic}} rarity, 2 at the {{Rarity|Legendary}} rarity, 5 at the {{Rarity|Mythic}} rarity, and 5 at the {{Rarity|Ultra}} rarity. *Petals, while heavily influencing almost all of them, are directly the subject of 54 [[Achievements]]: **The "All-seeing" {{Rarity|Unusual}} Achievement, granted to you once you equip [[Antennae]] of any rarity; **The 2 "Be gone" Achievements of {{Rarity|Legendary}} and {{Rarity|Ultra}} rarities, granted to you once you mark, respectively, 1 player and 100 players with [[Dark Mark]]; **The "Breath in, breath out" {{Rarity|Epic}} secret Achievement, granted to you once you rapidly equip and unequip [[Air]] of any rarity multiple times; **The "Collector" {{Rarity|Super}} Achievement, granted to you once you grab 65 different petal drops in one playthrough, which means without dying; **The 4 "Consolation prize" Achievements of the {{Rarity|Common}}, {{Rarity|Ultra}}, {{Rarity|Super}} and {{Rarity|Eternal}} rarities, granted to you once you lose, respectively, 4 petals of any rarity, 4 {{Rarity|Mythic}} petals, 4 {{Rarity|Ultra}} petals and 4 {{Rarity|Super}} petals from a fail in [[Crafting]]; **The 7 "Crafter" Achievements of all rarities starting at {{Rarity|Unusual}}, granted to you once you successfully craft a petal of the rarity corresponding to the Achievement's own from Crafting; **The "Digger" {{Rarity|Unusual}} Achievement, granted to you once you equip [[Cutter]] of any rarity; **The 3 "Evasive" Achievements of the {{Rarity|Rare}}, {{Rarity|Legendary}} and {{Rarity|Ultra}} rarities, granted to you once you evade, respectively, 1k, 100k and 10m damage values with [[Talisman of Evasion]]; **The "Gambler" {{Rarity|Legendary}} Achievement, granted to you once you insert 1000 [[Poker Chip|Chips]] of any rarity at [[Plinko]]; **The 7 "Gear Up" Achievements of all rarities starting at {{Rarity|Unusual}}, granted to you once you equip, respectively, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10 and 10 petals of the same rarity in one build (the rarity of the petal depends on the Achievement); **The "Go to WHERE?" {{Rarity|Unusual}} Achievement, granted to you once you get marked and successfully sent to Hel by a Mark from another player; **The "High roller" {{Rarity|Super}} Achievement, granted to you once you insert an {{Rarity|Ultra}} or a {{Rarity|Super}} Chip in Plinko **The "High volume crafter" {{Rarity|Ultra}} Achievement, granted to you once you successfully craft, in all of your playthroughs, 200k petals of any rarity from Crafting; **The "JACKPOT!" {{Rarity|Mythic}} Achievement, granted to you once you hit the 500x Chip multiplier bucket in Plinko; **The "Magician" {{Rarity|Epic}} Achievement, granted to you once you equip a [[Mana Orb]] of any rarity; **The "Return to sender" {{Rarity|Rare}} Achievement, granted to you once you hit a mob with [[Jelly]]; **The "Rolling in dough" {{Rarity|Legendary}} Achievement, granted to you once you grab 100 petal drops from different [[mobs]] in one second or less; **The "Sacrifice" {{Rarity|Mythic}} Achievement, granted to you once you die one second (or less) after you revive someone with a Yggdrasil; **The "Saved" {{Rarity|Legendary}} Achievement, granted to you once a player revives you with [[Yggdrasil]]; **The 2 "Savior" Achievements of {{Rarity|Legendary}} and {{Rarity|Ultra}} rarities, granted to you once you revive, respectively, 1 player and 100 players with Yggdrasil; **The 2 "Sound barrier" Achievements of {{Rarity|Mythic}} and {{Rarity|Ultra}} rarities, granted to you once you move a lot of tiles in a very short amount of time, usually achieved with [[Bubble]]s or [[Rubber]]s; **The 4 "Summoner" Achievements of the {{Rarity|Rare}}, {{Rarity|Epic}}, {{Rarity|Legendary}} and {{Rarity|Mythic}} rarities, granted to you once you spawn, respectively, 1, 100, 1k and 10k friendly mobs; **The "Termites…?" {{Rarity|Rare}} Achievement, granted to you once you spawn a friendly [[Soldier Termite]] from using both [[Ant Egg]] and [[Relic]] in the same build; **The "The all-seeing one" and "It was destiny" Achievements of {{Rarity|Legendary}} and {{Rarity|Ultra}} rarities, which, despite having different names, share almost the same completion method as if they had the same name: they are granted to you once you, respectively, craft a petal of any rarity and an {{Rarity|Ultra}} Petal with [[Oracle]]; **The "The lucky one" {{Rarity|Legendary}} Achievement, granted to you once you craft 10 petals in a row without fail from Crafting; **The "The unlucky one" {{Rarity|Ultra}} Achievement, granted to you once you fail 100 petal crafts in a row without success from Crafting; **The 3 "Trade" Achievements of {{Rarity|Epic}}, {{Rarity|Mythic}} and {{Rarity|Super}} rarities, granted to you once you trade, respectively, a petal of any rarity, a {{Rarity|Mythic}} petal and a {{Rarity|Super}} petal for [[Coin]] from [[Trader]], and finally; **The "Wasteful Crafting" {{Rarity|Mythic}} Achievement, granted to you once you lose 1m petals from fails in Crafting, in all of your playthroughs. {{Wiki Guide}} {{Template:Petals}} [[Category:Gameplay]] [[Category:List]] [[zh:{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Florr.io]] [[Category:Florr.io Community Wiki]]

'''Plank''' is a [[Petals|petal]] in [https://florr.io florr.io]. It deals 20x damage to projectiles and petals. Plank is dropped from [[Worker Termite]]s.

==Appearance==
Plank looks similar to a wooden plank. It has a brown rectangular body with some scratches and a hole.

==Function==
Plank is a defensive petal, meaning it cannot expand when attacking. If the petal is hit by projectiles or other players' petals in PvP, it will deal 20x its normal damage.

==Obtaining==
===[[Worker Termite]]===
{{:Worker Termite/Drop Plank}}

===Purchasing===
On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|2}}

==Strategy==
*Plank is useful in [[Garden]], [[Jungle]] and [[Factory]] where projectile mobs are present. Although [[Scorpion]]s in [[Desert]] could be a reason to use Planks there, it is more advisable to use [[Lotus]]es instead.
**Plank can tank two projectile hits from [[Hornet]]s, [[Wasp/Mecha|Mecha Wasps]] and [[Wasp]]s of the same rarity, and five projectile hits from [[Mantis]]es of the same rarity (except {{Rarity|Super}} and {{Rarity|Unique}}). Since projectile health scales exactly like mob health, Plank is vital to survival in high-rarity areas.
**Using two or three Planks spaced evenly should provide enough protection against projectiles.
*Plank could be used to fend off petals in [[Hel]], but this usage is less common.

==Trivia==
* This is the second petal that appears to be from a tree. The first is [[Mysterious Stick|Stick]], and the third one is [[Root]].
* This petal used to deal only 5x damage upon hitting projectiles, but now, because of the mob projectile health update, it was buffed to deal 20x damage.
* It is currently one of two petals that do extra damage to certain things, the other being [[Claw]].

'''Pollen''' is a petal in florr.io. It is much like a [[Light]] petal, except that it drops and remains on the ground for 5 seconds upon attacking or defending.

==Appearance==
It looks much like the Light, as it is the same shape and size, but it is yellow instead of white. The outlines of each pollen are dark yellow.

==Function==
''Note: The "A + B" respawn format means that the petal takes A seconds to respawn and B seconds after respawning to do its special action/have its special action enabled.''

|+Petal Stats

An {{Rarity|Unusual}} Pollen has 1 petal, {{Rarity|Rare}} Pollen has 2 petals, and a Pollen of rarity {{R|Epic}} or higher has 3 petals. If there are multiple petals, they do not occupy the same spot, so the space among all petals decreases, similar to [[Light]].

Upon attacking or defending, all Pollen will drop from the Flower and stay stationary, damaging enemies and projectiles that make contact in it. Upon being dropped on the ground, Pollen will stay there for 5 seconds before disappearing.

==Obtaining==
===[[Bee]]===
{{:Bee/Drop Pollen}}

=== [[Bumble Bee]] ===
{{:Bumble Bee/Drop Pollen}}

=== Purchasing ===
On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|4}}

==Strategy==
Pollen DPS is lower than DPS based petals, but unlike other petals, its DPS is constant. This is because it can lay petals down at a constant rate; it does not need to immediately hit a mob to deal damage, as a mob may wander over the petal later. This is useful in creating a defensive trail or shield. It is very difficult for players and mobs to pursue a fleeing pollen user, though the front side will be left vulnerable.

Compared to the [[Light]], a petal similar to it, this petal excels at defense while the Light is better on offense and as a general purpose petal.

===[[Garden]]===
*[[Hornet]]s stray far away from the flower, so taking them out with Pollen requires the user to flee. This can cause them to run into hornets on their front side where they will be left vulnerable.
*Pollen is near useless against non aggressive mobs. With no way to control their movement, the user must hope the mob runs into the pollen or get extremely close to the mob, risking body damage.
*Pollen is extremely useful against [[Spider]]s. The player can stand still and create a defensive ring that is nearly impossible to penetrate, especially since spiders have low HP. This is more effective if other players do the same. It is wise for players to keep several Pollens in their inventory, as they will come in handy when spiders swarm.

===[[Desert]]===
*Beetles deal damage using their body, so a pursuing beetle will have to pass through a bunch of pollen petals.
**However, if the mobs overwhelm the pollen and force the user to flee, they will be left wide open to head on attacks where they have little protection.
*Poison don't work for petals, so a wall of pollen stalls the scorpions' pincers effectively.
*Pollen is not very useful against Sandstorms due to their random movement.

===[[Ocean]]=== 
*Pollen is still effective in ocean but is not as dominant as in other game modes.
**[[Jellyfish]] can be taken out using pollen, but the user must flee due to the jellyfish's range. Thankfully jellyfish are slow, so a head on attack can be avoided. 
***Since jellyfishes are slow, it might not hit the pollen before it expires.
**Pollen is good at depleting a [[Starfish (Mob)|Starfish]] to half health, but it cannot kill the Starfish afterward since it will then flee from the player. Other petals must be used to finish a starfish off.
**Pollen is good against [[Crab|crabs]].
**Pollens can have its maximum DPS against [[leech]], where it don't have to be laid down. Be wary of your health though.

===[[Jungle]]===

*Pollen is riskier and more effective against wasps due to the curvy movement of their missiles. They aren't destroyed as easily as at hornets.

=== [[PvP]]===
====Using====
*Pollen can be used to slow down enemies that try to chase you. While a single Pollen does not do much, enemies that consecutively touches them will eventually have their health wear down.
*This is a great defensive petal allowing the user to run away from players while the pollen left behind on the ground will damage them. 
**Although pollen's knockback is low, repeatedly running into it will hinder a pursuer's movement, allowing the user to get away. The pursuer can also try to dodge the pollen, but this will slow them down.

====Against====
*It's a good idea to follow a different pathway than the trail of pollen on the ground to avoid taking damage.
*Pollen itself is quite weak, though the chaser's petals will not be able to destroy a trail of it. It is, however, useful in close range combat where pollen is not as useful.
*Salt will reflect damage dealt by pollen back to the user.

===[[Hel]]===

*Pollen is not as effective since [[Hel Wasp]]<nowiki/>s always tend to destroy them with ease.

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">
File:Pollen (Common).png|{{rarity|1}} Pollen. (obtainable in waves for a very short time)
File:Pollen (Unusual).png|{{rarity|2}} Pollen.
File:Pollen (Rare).png|{{rarity|3}} Pollen.
File:Pollen (Epic).png|{{rarity|4}} Pollen.
File:Pollen (Legendary).png|{{rarity|5}} Pollen.
File:Pollen (Mythic).png|{{rarity|6}} Pollen.
File:Pollen (Ultra).png|{{rarity|7}} Pollen.
File:Pollen (Super).png|{{rarity|8}} Pollen.
File:Pollen (Unique).png|{{rarity|9}} Pollen.
File:Pollen 7.webp|Old {{Rarity|7}} Pollen.
File:Pollen.png|Pollen.
File:Pollen with Duplicator.png| 3 parts of Pollen when applied with Duplicator talent.
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*In legacy florr.io. Pollen couldn't be dropped by mobs. It could only be dropped by flowers that had {{Rarity|Rare}}+ petals. 
*This, along with [[Orange]], and [[Dahlia]], is the petal tied for third with the most individual petals.
**[[Light]] and [[Stinger]] tie for the first, with up to 5 individual petals. [[Grapes]], [[Peas]], [[Egg (Ant)]] and [[Sand]] tie for second with 4 petals each.
**It, along with [[Light]] is also the only petal where the individual petals do not occupy the same spot, thus decreasing the distance between petals
*Pollen is one of the five petals that drops itself on the ground. Others are [[Honey]], [[Uranium]], [[Wax]] and [[Web]].
**Pollen is the only droppable petal to deal damage.
*{{R|Common}} Pollen used to be obtainable in waves, however it is no longer obtainable, however if you had a {{R|Common}} pollen and still have it now, it still exists.
*Before the December 29th 2022 update, {{Rarity|Mythic}}+ Pollens used to have a larger size visually. This also applied to other petals such as [[Grapes]], [[Leaf]], [[Peas]] and [[Rice]].

'''Poo''' is a [[Petals|petal]] in [https://florr.io florr.io]. It reduces aggro range of mobs and does not stack. Poo is dropped by [[Fly|Flies]].

==Appearance==
'''Poo''' consists of a brown, cartoonish poop, having a brown paste shaped into a cartoonish poop-shape with an outline that is darker and bigger.

==Function==

Poo reduces the range within which an aggressive mob can target the user (aggro range). It does not stack with other Poos, and only works if the petal is alive, so if it gets destroyed, all nearby mobs will instantly aggro on the user.

==Obtainment==
===[[Fly]]===
{{:Fly/Drop Poo}}

===Purchasing===
On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|1}}

==Obtaining==
*Defeat [[Fly|Flies]] to obtain Poo. They can be easily defeated with petals that inflict lightning or poison damage, but one should also be cautious about the other [[Sewers]] mobs, especially [[Roach]]es and [[Spider]]s.

==Strategy ==
*Poo is a useful utility petal in a number of cases.
**Poo is generally very useful when playing solo, as it allows user to pick off mobs slowly in high density {{R|7}} zones like [[Garden]] Spiral without drawing aggro from everything in the area. Beware though, as Poo is a non-extendable petal, it is highly suggested to unlock {{Rarity|Mythic}} [[Talents/Reach|Reach talent]] as soon as possible such that the user can keep a safe distance from the mobs while attacking them to prevent them from destroying it.
**In early-game farming, especially [[Ant Hell]] farming, Poo can protect the user from being swarmed by large numbers of [[Soldier Ant]]s when the chamber is in lack of other players. It also allows the user to take them down one by one, although the rates will be much slower.
**Poo is also useful if one farms with a bubble DPS build, which typically consists of burst or heavy damage petals at the front, healing and utility petals on the sides and [[Bubble]]s at the rear. Poo should be placed opposite to the damage petals and next to Bubbles to minimize the chance of it being destroyed. While Poo can also be used in a conventional rotation build, players should keep an eye on projectile mobs when farming certain biomes.

<gallery>

File:Poo.png|({{Rarity|9b}}) Poo.
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*Poo and [[Light Bulb|Bulb]] are the only petals that alter aggro range.
**Poo is the only petal that can decrease mobs' aggro range, and Bulb can increase mobs' aggro range.
**[[Honey]] is the only petal which can influence the aggro of a mob.
*On December 26, 2024, [[M28]] made all variants of [[Beetle]]s (called Buttles during that time) drop Poo as a joke, before being reverted several days later.
*Mob aggro range stats can be calculated with the formula <math>0.75^{r+1}-1</math>, where r is the rarity index ({{Rarity|Common}} starts at 0, {{Rarity|Unusual}} starts at 1, etc.).
*Corrupt X is the first person to forge the {{Rarity|Unique}} Poo.

The '''Queen Ant''' is a mob that was added to florr.io on February 3, 2020. It has a lot of HP, and comes out of damaged [[Ant Hole]]s.

==Appearance==
It's a larger version of a Soldier Ant with an extra circle on the back, larger than the first two. This circle is also grey with a dark grey outline.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Average, but stops periodicaly to lay eggs.

'''Behavior:''' Aggressive, {{R|Rare}}+ Queen Ants lay eggs that hatch into [[Soldier Ant|Soldier Ants]] of a rarity below the Queen Ant's.

==Spawning==
The Queen Ant spawns from [[Ant Hole|Ant Holes]] below 40% HP and throughout [[Ant Hell]]. 

{{Rarity|Rare}}+ Queen Ants can spawn Ant Eggs that hatch into Soldier Ants one rarity below it.

==Drops==

===[[Basil]]===
{{:Template:ID 5 - M}}

=== [[Egg (Ant)]]===
{{:Template:ID 4}}

==Strategy==  
*Queen Ants have average speed but briefly stay in place every time they lay an Ant Egg.
**Luring them in circles is a bad idea as the Soldier Ants they spawn will end up covering her body from flower attacks.
*You can outrun Queen Ants by moving in a straight line. This is also considered to be the best way to lure them, as the Soldier Ants she hatches will stay behind her, leaving the Queen Ant wide open for attacks. If she's from an Ant Hole, she will stop chasing you past a certain distance from the Ant Hole. Bubbles can help with this. If another flower is nearby, they can attack her while you run. You can also use [[Shovel]] while near someone else so that it will aggro on the other person instead.
*It's a good idea to attack Queen Ants if you see one. They can drop good loot, especially higher rarity Queen Ants, meaning that they're good for getting petals you need or for farming XP.
*The Ant Eggs she lays will hatch [[Soldier Ant|Soldier Ants]] that only stay near her and attack targets close to the Queen Ant that hatched them. If they are separated from their Queen Ant, they will attempt to return to the Queen Ant's location, ignoring mobs and flowers in their way to it. The Ant Eggs she hatches are unique in that they're the only Ant Eggs to hatch Soldier Ants. They will instantly hatch the Soldier Ant should they be destroyed before the hatch timer ends, and do not drop [[Ant Egg]], however they still count towards [[Daily Challenge|Daily Challenges]]. 
*Her circular hitbox does not match her physical appearance.

=== Super Queen Ant ===

* The Super mob itself is quite dangerous as it spawns ultra mobs, but the other Ultra mobs around the mob are also a threat. When bubbling to the mob, it is advised to use Sponge and multiple Ultra healing petals. It is advised to use sponge when actually fighting the mob, so you don't die if anything hits you.
* If the mob spawns deep in Normal Ant Hell, it is advised to form a "megasquad", where a large group of players stay near each other and clear mobs. This increases the chance for everyone to survive.
* The Super Queen Ant is one of the hardest mobs to loot, since numerous people want to loot. So you need high DPS to loot.
**The others mainly consist of [[Termite Egg]], [[Ant Egg (Mob)|Ant Egg]], [[Fire Ant Egg]], [[Queen Fire Ant]], [[Golden Leafbug]], and [[Ladybug (Desert)|Shiny Ladybug]].

==Trivia==
*On the May 8, 2024 update, the [[Wing]] petal was made to no longer drop from {{PAGENAME}}.
**This caused its XP reward to lower.
*Queen Ant is tied with [[Queen Fire Ant]] for third highest health.
* Before the January 1, 2025 update, the Queen Ant could only spawn as {{R|Ultra}} or above in Ant Hell, and only spawned in {{R|Mythic}}- rarities in Garden. After the Ant Hell map update, they can now spawn in any rarity inside of Ant Hell.
*The Soldier Ants spawned by the Queen Ant will try to be near her at all times, even after the death of the Queen Ant.

title: Rarity
content:
'''Rarities''' are a key element of [[florr.io]]. The rarity of a [[Mobs|mob]] or [[Petals|petal]] determines what stats it has: for example, a petal/mob's health, damage, armor, etc. There are currently ten rarities in the game: {{Rarity|1}}, {{Rarity|2}}, {{Rarity|3}}, {{Rarity|4}}, {{Rarity|5}}, {{Rarity|6}}, {{Rarity|7}}, {{Rarity|8}}, {{Rarity|9b}} and {{Rarity|9}}. The hotkey to see the rarity and hitbox of a petal is the Alt key on Windows, or the Option key on Mac OS. ==Petals== Petals are affected by its rarity. Not only does the background color of the petal change, but its statistics, primarily health and damage, are also affected, as well as its special ability: for example, how much health a healing petal can heal. Some petals have duplicate copies of itself, like [[Light]], [[Sand]], and [[Stinger]]: do note that some petals will only have duplicate copies at higher rarities, like Stinger. High-rarity petals have particles trailing off from the petal as seen in game. {{Rarity|Super}} petals used to have lime green particles trailing from their appearance when surrounding the flower. {{Rarity|9b}} petals and {{R|Unique}} petals will have dark gray particles trailing off from them as well. The color of the particles matches the color of the petal tooltip. Petals that are dropped will stay on the ground until despawned: {{Rarity|Common}} petals stay for 15s, {{Rarity|Unusual}} petals stay for 30s, {{Rarity|Rare}} petals stay for 40s, {{Rarity|Epic}} petals stay for 50s, {{Rarity|Legendary}} petals stay for 60s, and {{Rarity|Mythic}} and {{Rarity|Ultra}} petals stay for 120s. The time that {{R|Unique}} petals stay ([[Pharaoh's Crown]]) is currently unmentioned and possibly unknown. As of November 17, 2025, petals on the ground have particle effects coming off of them, corresponding to their Rarity. In addition, {{R|Unique}} and {{R|Eternal}} petals will have their corresponding particle effects applied to them. Through the crafting system, the player is able to obtain higher-rarity petals, besides {{Rarity|Unique}}. {{Rarity|Unique}} and {{Rarity|9b}} petals have the same stats, but while {{Rarity|9b}} petals are extremely costly to craft and are permanent, {{Rarity|Unique}} petals use 5 {{Rarity|Super}} petals per forge and are lost when someone else also forges it, destroying one {{Rarity|Super}} petal as a result. ===Crafting === {| class="fandom-table" !Rarity ! % Chance of success |- |{{Rarity|1}} '''→''' {{Rarity|2}} |64% |- |{{Rarity|2}} '''→''' {{Rarity|3}} |32% |- |{{Rarity|3}} '''→''' {{Rarity|4}} |16% |- |{{Rarity|4}} '''→''' {{Rarity|5}} |8% |- |{{Rarity|5}} '''→''' {{Rarity|6}} |4% |- |{{Rarity|6}} '''→''' {{Rarity|7}} |2% |- |{{Rarity|7}} '''→''' {{Rarity|8}} |1% |- |{{Rarity|8}} '''→''' {{Rarity|9b}} |0.1% |- |{{Rarity|9}} |Must be crafted from [[Titan]].
100% |} [[Square|Squares]] will have a 100% craft chance, regardless of rarity. The Health (except [[Coral]], [[Cotton]], [[Fang]], [[Bubble]] and [[Stinger]]) and Damage of most petals scale with rarity: how much it scales by is described in the chart below. {| class="fandom-table" !Rarity !Compared to {{Rarity|1}} petal !Compared to previous rarity |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |1x | - |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |3x | rowspan="8" |3x |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |9x |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |27x |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |81x |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |243x |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |729x |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |2187x |- |{{Rarity|9}} and {{Rarity|9b}} |6561x |} The healing stats of most healing petals, Root's armor stat, Cotton's health stat and Shell's shield stat scale like the chart below: {| class="fandom-table" !Rarity !Compared to {{Rarity|1}} petal !Compared to previous rarity |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |1x | - |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |3x | rowspan="5" |3x |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |9x |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |27x |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |81x |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |243x |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |243\sqrt{3}x (approx. 420.89x) | rowspan="3" |\sqrt{3}x (approx. 1.73x) |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |729x |- |{{Rarity|9}} and {{Rarity|9b}} |729\sqrt{3}x (approx. 1,262.67x) |} The mana cost/generation of most magic petals scale like the chart below: {| class="fandom-table" !Rarity !Compared to {{Rarity|1}} petal !Compared to previous rarity |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |1x | - |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |2x | rowspan="8" |2x |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |4x |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |8x |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |16x |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |32x |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |64x |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |128x |- |{{Rarity|9}} and {{Rarity|9b}} |256x |} ==Mobs== It changes the backgrounds' color, its statistics (health and damage), its size (it may be hard to differentiate between {{Rarity|1}} and {{Rarity|2}}), its drop chances and rarity of its dropped petals, and mobs with higher rarities' cards are shown above those of lower rarities in game. The rarity of mobs affects (or adds) some Mobs' specials, such as [[Hornet]] missile damage or [[Scorpion]] poison. Many mobs' behavior also changes from passive to neutral. {{R|Super}} mobs do not have designated spawn zones. Instead, they appear in place of one out of every 500 {{R|Ultra}} mobs, with a spawn cooldown of 20 minutes. '''All Mobs will always show a health bar, including friendly mobs.''' The health stats of most mobs scale like the chart below: {| class="fandom-table" !Rarity !Compared to {{Rarity|1}} mob !Compared to previous rarity |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |1x | - |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |3.75x |3.75x |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |13.5x |3.6x |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |54x |4x |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |324x |6x |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |3159x |9.75x |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |145800x |\frac{600}{13}x (approx. 46.15x) |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |4374000x |30x |- |{{Rarity|9}} and {{Rarity|9b}} |26244000x |6x |} The damage stats of most mobs scale like the chart below: {| class="fandom-table" !Rarity !Compared to {{Rarity|1}} mob !Compared to previous rarity |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |1x | - |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |3x | rowspan="8" |3x |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |9x |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |27x |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |81x |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |243x |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |729x |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |2187x |- |{{Rarity|9}} and {{Rarity|9b}} |6561x |} The projectile stats of most mobs scale like the chart below: {| class="fandom-table" !Rarity !Compared to {{Rarity|1}} mob !Compared to previous rarity |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |1x | - |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |5x | rowspan="8" |5x |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |25x |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |125x |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |625x |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |3125x |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |78125x |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |390625x |- |{{Rarity|9}} and {{Rarity|9b}} |1953125x |} The armor stats of most mobs scale like the chart below: {| class="fandom-table" !Rarity !Compared to {{Rarity|1}} mob !Compared to previous rarity |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |1x | - |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |2x |2x |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |7x |3.5x |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |22x |\frac{22}{7}x (approx. 3.14x) |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |65x |2.95x |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |194x |2.98x |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} | rowspan="3" |583x |3.01x |- |{{Rarity|Super}} | rowspan="2" |1x |- |{{Rarity|9}} and {{Rarity|9b}} |} ==Trivia== *Before the release of {{Rarity|Mythic}} petals, the highest mob rarity was {{Rarity|Old6}}. This rarity had a slightly darker color than the current {{Rarity|Ultra}} rarity, and it has ever since been replaced with {{Rarity|Mythic}}. Only two petals had this rarity: Basic and Square. **However, {{Rarity|Unique}} petals were notably bad and only cosmetic/collection petals, as {{Rarity|Old6}} Basic was a Basic with particles/Wing's ability and Square was nothing but a reskinned Basic. **When waves first came out, all mobs could spawn in {{Rarity|Old6}} rarity, however {{Rarity|Old6}} petals did not exist. On July 13, 2022, {{Rarity|Old6}} was replaced with {{Rarity|Mythic}}, which did have petal variants. **The name was reused for the {{Rarity|Unique}} rarity, which has better stats than the {{Rarity|Super}} rarity. *Before the release of {{Rarity|Ultra}} mobs, {{Rarity|Ultra}} petals first had dark gray backgrounds. After that, they were changed to a yellowish orange, and then changed to their current color. **{{Rarity|Ultra}}'s original color is {{Rarity|Unique}}'s current color. *At first, no mobs showed their health bars, including {{Rarity|5}}+ mobs. Next, it was changed to {{Rarity|5}}+ mobs showing their health bars when damaged. Then, it was changed to all mobs showing their health bar regardless of being damaged or not. Then, {{Rarity|Ultra}} mobs gained another huge boss health bar that shows on the top of the screen. After that, {{Rarity|8}} mobs also got this big health bar at the top of the screen. Finally, {{Rarity|Ultra}} mobs were demoted to non-boss mobs and lost their boss health bar. *In the florr.io discord server, M28 stated that there would be a new temporary {{R|Unique}} petal, similar to [[Pharaoh's Crown|Crown]], that would be given to whoever wins the Battle Royale mode. *There was a rumor that {{Rarity|Ultra}} Talented Crafting would be added to the game. **This did not happen as all the Crafting Talents were removed. *On December 14, 2024, the rarity names were temporarily changed to {{Rarity|1|Ummon}}, {{Rarity|2|Unusual}}, {{Rarity|3|Ure}}, {{Rarity|4|Upic}}, {{Rarity|5|Ugendary}}, {{Rarity|6|Uthic}}, {{Rarity|7|Ultra}}, {{Rarity|8|Uper}}, and {{Rarity|9|Unique}}. **{{Rarity|2|Unusual}}, {{Rarity|7|Ultra}} and {{Rarity|9|Unique}} rarities retained their normal names. ** Later that same day, the rarities were reverted to normal, except every rarity from {{Rarity|1|Common}} to {{Rarity|8|Super}} had been swapped around. For example, a {{Rarity|8|Super}} Baby Ant would say it was {{Rarity|1|Common}}. {{Rarity|9|Unique}} was the only rarity not affected by either of these changes, as it had not been released yet. *** These changes were also reverted the same day. ** [[M28]] did the temporary change as a joke regarding the problem people having with shortening {{Rarity|Ultra}} petals to U[petal name], as U could also mean {{Rarity|Unusual}}, whereas people who used U[petal name] were called lazy by people who used Ul[petal name]. The debate got more intense once {{Rarity|Unique}} got rereleased, as U[petal name] could mean 3 rarities at that point. *{{Rarity|Unique}} petals can be obtained from the [[Titan|Titan.]] **Anyone that got {{Rarity|Unique}} petals before the NPC was added used hacks to get them. *As of January 19, 2026, not one player has an {{R|Eternal}} petal. However some may have wasted a few {{Rarity|8|Super}} petals attempting to get the {{R|Eternal}} [[Achievements|achievement]]. === Hex codes === {| class="article-table" ! Rarity ! Hex code |- | '''{{Text shadow|Common|color=#7EEF6D}}''' | #7EEF6D |- | '''{{Text shadow|Unusual|color=#FFE65D}}''' | #FFE65D |- | '''{{Text shadow|Rare|color=#4d52e3}}''' | #4d52e3 |- | '''{{Text shadow|Epic|color=#861FDE}}''' | #861FDE |- | '''{{Text shadow|Legendary|color=#DE1F1F}}''' | #DE1F1F |- | '''{{Text shadow|Mythic|color=#1fdbde}}''' | #1fdbde |- | '''{{Text shadow|Ultra|color=#ff2b75}}''' | #ff2b75 |- | '''{{Text shadow|Super|color=#2bffa3}}''' | #2bffa3 |- |{{Rarity|9b}} |#EEEEEE |- | {{Rarity|9}} | #555555 |- |} ==Gallery== File:Player with Super Sand Equipped.png|Player with a {{R|8}} Sand equipped, notice the particles coming out of the Sand. Note that {{Rarity|Super}} petals no longer have particle effects. File:Hackerflorr.jpg|Player hacking in {{R|9}} petals. Note that {{R|9}} petals can be legitimately obtained from the Titan NPC. File:Zorat with unique sand.jpeg|Player with a {{Rarity|9}} Sand equipped, notice the black particles coming out of the Sand. {{Game Info}} [[Category:Game information]] [[Category:Gameplay]] [[Category:Rare Mob Category]] [[Category:Rare Petals]]

'''Rice''' is a legacy [[Petals|petal]] in [https://florr.io florr.io] added on the 11.2.2020 update. It has weak damage stats, but reloads almost instantly. Rice is dropped by [[Baby Ant]]s.

== Appearance ==
A white petal in the shape of a jellybean with a light-gray border, slightly larger than a light.

==Function==
