Rice has a cooldown time of 0.1 seconds, which can be brought down to 0.05 with all of the Reload [[Talents]] (Rounded to 0.1 in-game).

==Obtaining==
===[[Baby Ant]]===
{{:Baby Ant/Drop Rice}}

===Purchasing===
On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|4}}

== Strategy ==
Rice is the petal with the fastest respawn, not counting petals worn on the flower like [[Third Eye]] and [[Disc]]. It has the highest DPS in the game, even surpassing [[Claw]], but making full use of it is tricky, as you would need to keep it focused on the same target and you would also need to stay as close to the target as possible to ensure its maximum DPS (as the petal reloads so fast that it loses a significant amount of DPS from the travel distance to the orbit).

[[Yin Yang]] also can and should be used with Rice. Put one in your secondary slots and constantly switch it with another petal (which is not Yin Yang) so your rice petals hardly move from their place. This will further increase the DPS of Rice. Using lights to squish multiple rice together with [[Rice]] also boosts DPS by a lot.

With the newly added [[Lentil]] petal, The optimal method would be to equip 2-3 [[Lentil|Lentils]], a [[Bur]] and as many Rice as possible. Put your [[flower]] in the defensive position get close to the [[Mobs|Mob]]. All Rices will hit at the same time causing massive damage, surpassing the damage of [[Sand]], [[Light]] AND [[Dust]] with the same build. (Confirmation needed)

=== [[Garden]] ===
* The rice is useful against [[Centipede]]s. Since they spawn in groups, it can instantly respawn on the centipede many times, dealing heavy damage. 
* The player can use [[Heavy]] petals and [[Light]]/[[Pollen]] to create a wall of rice. By aiming this in a certain direction, the user can create a spot with insane DPS that mobs will have to pass through.

=== [[Desert]] ===
* The wall strategy mentioned above is more effective in desert since all mobs deal damage via contact(except for the scorpion stinger attack). The user should position themselves on an edge and have the rice face outwards. Rapidly switch Yin Yangs in and out to adjust position. Ideally, a teammate will use [[Web]]s to slow down incoming mobs.
* Just as it is useful against normal centipedes, rice is good against [[Desert Centipede]]s.

=== [[Ocean]] ===
* The wall strategy can be used to follow a [[Starfish (Mob)|Starfish]] to quickly kill it, and it works against other mobs too. Yin Yangs should be switched to adjust position, thus this strategy requires high skill.
* Highly useful for greeding bubbles. {{R|8}} Bubbles can easily be killed by using {{R|7}}+ [[Rice]]. 

=== [[PvP]] ===
==== Using ====
* It's best to use these with multiple [[Faster|Fasters]] as they'll have more effect on quickly respawning.
* The wall strategy is not useful in PvP since players can move around it, though a skilled player may be able to track the opponent by switching Yin Yangs in and out.
=== Against ===
* Counter the rice with higher DPS by using [[Light]]s and Fasters.
*[[Wing|Wings]] are also a good weapon because when you're in range, the petal can pop up and hit you faster than you can blink.
* Aside from respawn, its stats are similar to a Basic so similar strategies apply.
* Rice is best used with ultras and supers to get loot easily, as it is easier to aim your rice when the mob is aggroed on someone else.

== Gallery ==
<gallery spacing="small" widths="185">
File:Rice (Common).png|{{R|1}} Rice.
File:Rice (Unusual).png|{{R|2}} Rice.

File:Rice 7.webp|Big Rice. (Pictured at {{R|7}} rarity). Used to visually exist during Waves if a Rice had {{R|6}} or {{R|7}} rarity, though the hitbox wasn't bigger.
</gallery>

== Trivia ==
* This is one of four petals to not have a visible reload after being equipped, the others being [[Antennae]], [[Cutter]], [[Disc]], [[Relic]] and [[Third Eye]].
** This is the only one that orbits the flower with this trait.
** It has it after 1st May update, though subtle
* It was added alongside its opposite twin, [[Corn]].
* For its legacy version, the description states it has no respawn timer, it actually takes 0.04 seconds to respawn (which is just 1 frame). This is faster than any other petal that orbits you ([[Antennae]], Cutter, and Third Eye technically have no respawn).
*Before the December 29th 2022 update. {{R|Mythic}}+ Rice used to look large visually. This was referred as "fat rice" or "big rice".
*Before the 1st May (year?) update, Rice reload used to be less than 0.1 sec (0.04 to be exact)
*Now with full reload upgrades (50% reload reduction) Rice is almost as good as before the 1st May update (0.05 seconds compared to 0.04 seconds).

The '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is a mob in florr.io. It spawns in the [[Sewers]].
==Appearance==
Roaches look like a brown oval with 2 spots and 2 stripes on its abdomen. Its head protudes from the abdomen, visibly darker than the body, with two Antennae protruding from it. 

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed: ''' Slow when normal, extremely fast when provoked. A Roach's movement is directly proportional to its health, moving faster with more health and slower with lower health.

'''Behavior:''' When provoked, chases and attacks the player by ramming.

Roaches (while unprovoked) move a few tiles in one direction, before turning around and moving in another direction. If they take any damage from a player, they will move much faster than the player until killed or reaching low health. As stated previously, movement speed is faster at high health. Going out of view of the Roach can remove its aggro.

==Spawning==
The {{PAGENAME}} spawns anywhere in the [[Sewers]].

==Drops==
===[[Antennae]]===
{{:Roach/Drop Antennae}}

===[[Lotus]]===
{{:Roach/Drop Lotus}}

== Strategy ==
*Provoking it without any petals that can hinder the roach movement is a bad idea, so [[Web|Web,]] [[Jelly|Jelly,]] and [[Pincer|Pincers]] can help as they slow mobs down. And if you don't have high-rarity [[Bubble|Bubbles]] while the high-rarity Roach is charging at you, you are likely doomed. [[Claw]] is also a good choice to quickly reduce the health of a Roach, and with it its movement speed. Using [[Mysterious Powder|Powder]] to escape from any mob is impractical since if you can outrun it with powder it's likely not posing any risks to you anymore.
*When encountering one, proceed with caution. It doesn't have much health, so it's recommended to wait until all your petals have reloaded before proceeding.
*Ramming is good when dealing with Roaches. People with mostly {{Rarity|Mythic}} petals can also beat {{Rarity|Ultra}} roach. 
*You can use its speed as an advantage to yourself by aggravating the roach and letting it chase you and bounce you towards wherever you want to go; if you turn and go towards a certain direction the roach chases and bounces you in that certain direction, until you or the roach dies.<!-- This strategy was made by a user called ApexShadow; he called it "roach surfing"--> (WARNING: this method only works if you have high health, high regeneration or high body damage, otherwise, you will die in mere seconds while using this strategy.)
*[[Beetle Egg]]s that are 1 rarity higher can also kill the Roach, as the [[Beetle]] is stronger than the Roach.
*[[Moon Rock|Moon]] can give you enough time to kill the Roach before it gets destroyed.
*Roaches have a habit of crushing you into walls so petals such as [[Heavy]] and [[Corn]] can help because of their tankiness and their ability to keep mobs back. If all else fails, use [[Shovel]] to lose its aggro and go somewhere safer.

==Trivia ==
*The position of the Roach in the gallery used to be shifted a bit to the top left.
*It is the only mob in which its movement speed is proportional to its health.
*Roach is currently the fastest mob in the game.
*Roach used to be called Cockroach, but its name was changed during March 2023.
*If four {{R|Ultra}} Roaches attack you at the same time, they may create a little room between them where they can't hit you because they are all pushing equally.

'''Root''' is a [[Petals|petal]] in [https://florr.io florr.io]. It gives the user a layer of [[Armor]] each second (up to 10 layers), and one layer is removed every time the flower takes physical damage. Root is dropped by [[Leafbug]]s and [[Golden Leafbug]]s.

==Appearance==
It is brown like the stick, with the shape of a simple mountain which has bumps.

==Function==
{|class="article-table"
|+Petal Stats
![[Rarity]]
!Obtainable?
!Respawn
!Flower Armor
!Interval
!Limit
|-
|{{Rarity|Common}}

|2.5
|rowspan="9"|1 second
|rowspan="9"|10
|-
|{{Rarity|Unusual}}
|7.5
|-
|{{Rarity|Rare}}
|22.5
|-
|{{Rarity|Epic}}
|67.5
|-
|{{Rarity|Legendary}}
|202.5
|-

|607.5
|-

|1052.22
|-

|1822.5
|-
|{{Rarity|Unique}}
|3156.66
|}
Root gives the user a layer of armor every second until the limit of 10 layers is reached, and one layer is removed whenever the user receives physical damage. For example, if a player is using a {{Rarity|Mythic}} Root and has accumulated 10 layers of armor, then the user possesses 607.5 × 10 = 6,075 armor. When the user hits an {{Rarity|Ultra}} [[Soldier Ant]], which does 7,290 physical damage, then the user receives 7,290 - 6,075 = 1,215 physical damage, and a layer of armor is removed, leaving the user with 6,075 - 607.5 = 5,467.5 armor.

==Obtaining==
===[[Leafbug]]===
{{:Leafbug/Drop Root}}

===Purchasing===
On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|2}}

==Strategy==

===For obtaining===
For beginners with only {{R|Legendary}} petals, grind [[Leafbug|Leafbugs]] in the bottom left or bottom middle section of the [[Jungle]] to get {{R|Epic}} and {{R|Legendary}} roots. For late-game pros, grind as far into Jungle as possibke without dying, and hunting after Leafbugs.

===For using ===
This petal works extremely well with [[Disc]]. Disc reduces the amount of damage taken, which when at high amounts of stacks, the user is virtually immortal until the layers get used up by being hit by an attack.  

====[[Garden]]====
*The layers can be used to take as little damage from [[Hornet]] Missiles, which makes it extremely valuable. Take note to not get hit by it though, as it reduces the layers for Root. Additionally, [[Spider]] is a problem since it completely ignores armor with its poison damage.
====[[Desert]]====
* This petal is best combined with [[Disc]] in desert, since many mobs here have high body damage.
====[[Ocean]]==== 
*This petal is valuable in Ocean as [[Crab|Crabs]] and [[Starfish|Starfishes]] deal a lot of body damage, however, take note as [[Jellyfish]] ignore armor.

====[[Centralia Sewers|Sewer]]====

*The large amounts of [[Spider|Spiders]] make it almost useless here, however, it can reduces damage taken by [[Fly]] and [[Roach]].

====[[Ant Hell]]====

* Due to the mob's low damage, it is recommended here. However, take note that the density may just crush you if you are not careful.

==== [[PvP]] ====

===== Using===== 
*Max root stacks can greatly reduce the damage done by petals like [[Stinger|Stingers]], although {{R|Mythic}}+ Stingers have multiple copies that will drain your layers of armor quite quickly.

=====Against=====
*Root can be countered using armor reducing petals like [[Bur]], or huge amounts of quick reload petals like [[Light]] to drain layera of armor quickly.

==Gallery==
<gallery>

File:Root.png|Root.
</gallery>

==Trivia == 
* After the update in January 12, 2024, this petal doesn't have the stats of its durability and damage, meaning that this petal is unbreakable.
* In real life, Roots create a firm foundation for the plant. This is probably why the petal reinforces them in the old days.
* Root used to stack a long time ago.
* In November 3, 2025, Root got massively buffed, as it can now gives Layers of armor that are extremely helpful, even considered to be better than [[Sponge]] itself.
* Both lightning and poison damage don't remove a layer of armor of the player upon get damaged.
* Root used to be considered the worst petal in the game. After the October 23th Build, this petal is no longer useless. 
{{Petals}}

title: Rose
content:
{{Petal Template Infobox | image = File:Rose (Common).png|C File:Rose (Unusual).png|U File:Rose (Rare).png|R File:Rose (Epic).png|E File:Rose (Legendary).png|L File:Rose (Mythic).png|M File:Rose (Ultra).png|Ul File:Rose (Super).png|S File:Rose (Unique).png|Uq | description = Its healing properties are amazing. Not so good at combat though. | first-rarity = {{Rarity|Common}} | damage = {{Petal Scale|5}} | health = {{Petal Scale|5}} | heal = {{Infobox Colors Empty|8|23|68|203|608|1822.5|3156.7|5467.5|9.5k}} | reload-time = 3.5s + 1.5s | stackability = Stackable | dropped-by = * [[Ladybug]] * [[Ladybug (Desert)]] | special = Heals flowers }} '''Rose''' is a petal in florr.io. It is able to heal the user and nearby teammates. ==Appearance== A pink, circular petal with a dark pink outline, roughly the same size as the [[Basic]] petal. ==Function ==
''Note: The "A + B" respawn format means that the petal takes A seconds to respawn and B seconds after respawning to do its special action/have its special action enabled.'' {|class="article-table" |+Petal Stats ![[Rarity]] !Obtainable? !Damage !Health !Respawn !Heal |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |rowspan="9"|Yes |5 |5 |rowspan="9"|3.5 + 1.5 seconds |7.5 |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |15 |15 |22.5 |- | {{Rarity|Rare}} |45 |45 |67.5 |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |135 |135 |202.5 |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |405 |405 |607.5 |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |1215 |1215 |1822.5 |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |3645 |3645 |3156.7 |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |10935 |10935 |5467.5 |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} |32805 |32805 |9.5k |} Each players starts with a {{Rarity|Common}} '''Rose''' in their 5th spot in the primary loadout. If the user is not at full health, the Rose will spawn, orbit the flower for a second, then go towards the flower to be absorbed by it, at which point the flower will heal and the Rose respawn timer will start. If the user has full health, it will simply behave like other petals, orbiting the flower. If a nearby player is not at full health, the Rose will heal them and go on respawn after being absorbed. This petal can't go outward when attacking but can go in when defending. If a Rose is healing someone but they obtain full health before they absorb the Rose, the Rose returns to its orbit. Likewise, if multiple roses are healing someone but not all of them are needed, unnecessary ones will return to their orbit. ==Obtaining== ===[[Ladybug]]=== {{:Ladybug/Drop Rose}} ===[[Ladybug (Desert)]]=== {{:Ladybug (Desert)/Drop Rose}} ===Purchasing=== On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]]. ''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]'' {{Petal Price|2}} ==Strategy== Base healing in florr is low, so Roses are an essential petal to have. They are one of the most powerful healing petals, having a faster heal per second than most other healing petals. In addition, they are the only healing petal other than [[Dahlia]], [[Yggdrasil]], and [[Monstera]] able to heal teammates, so players can quickly heal dying teammates saving them. It is strongly recommended to always have at least one Rose active at all times. More Roses can be kept in the inventory for when more healing is needed. The main disadvantage of the Rose is that healing is not constant. It comes in a burst every 4.5 seconds, which may be too slow if being chased by mobs or when poisoned. The Rose is also vulnerable in the second between respawning and healing. If it dies, it won't be able to heal. The rose has extremely low offensive capabilities, barely dealing damage and not going out upon attacking. ===[[Garden]]=== *Roses are great for healing against hornets. It is likely that players will be far enough way for rose petals to not get destroyed before they heal. Be aware that hornet swarms can shoot rapidly enough to take down roses before they can heal! * Petals that expand upon attacking can keep dangerous mobs away from rose petals, allowing them to heal. === [[Desert]]=== *Almost every mob in desert uses body damage, which puts roses in great danger. However, they are still likely to heal. If you want to decrease this body damage by a percentage, you can use [[Disc]]. === PvP=== ==== Using==== *Roses heal players the fastest but also provide the least offense. They're great for healing when away from battle, but roses leave a weak spot in any build that another player can attack for massive damage. **Using [[Light]]s or [[Pollen]]s, which squeeze petals closer together, can counteract this weakness. *Users should be wary when their weak spot is vulnerable. ====Against==== *Attackers should go in for a kill when the other player's rose is facing them as this is an obvious weak spot. *[[Bubble]]s can be used to do this quickly and catch players off guard. *If a user needs to sync their own rotation to that of another player, so as to best take advantage of their weak spot, rotation modifiers ([[Faster]] or [[Yin Yang]]) can be used. *Destroying another player's rose(s) is an easy way to cripple them. **Using a [[Dandelion]] has the same effect. ==Gallery== File:Rose (Common).png|{{Rarity|1}} Rose. File:Rose (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|2}} Rose. File:Rose (Rare).png|{{Rarity|3}} Rose. File:Rose (Epic).png|{{Rarity|4}} Rose. File:Rose (Legendary).png|{{Rarity|5}} Rose. File:Rose (Mythic).png|{{Rarity|6}} Rose. File:Rose (Ultra).png|{{Rarity|7}} Rose. File:Rose (Super).png|{{Rarity|8}} Rose. File:Rose (Unique).png|{{Rarity|9}} Rose. File:Rose.png|Rose. ==Trivia== * '''Rose''' and [[Basic]] are the first petals a player can obtain, as they are your only efficient way of getting another petal ([[Basic]]) and healing you ('''Rose''') before you progress through the game. * This is one of the nine healing petals, the others being the [[Leaf]], [[Yucca]], [[Starfish]], [[Dahlia]], [[Fang]], [[Yggdrasil]], [[Totem]] and [[Monstera]]. ** This, along with [[Dahlia]] are the only petals out of the bunch that don't have a consistent healing rate (not counting [[Fang]] since it doesn't heal naturally) * This petal has the most heal per second that does not require certain circumstances to be used. * Originally, Rose did not return to its orbit if the user was already fully healed before it got absorbed, so it would still be absorbed and go on respawn as other features were not implemented during that time. {{Petals}} [[zh:玫瑰]] [[Category:Petals]] [[Category:Health-Based]] [[Category:Basic Variants]] [[Category:Common+]] [[Category:Healing Petals]] [[Category:Garden]] [[Category:Desert]] [[Category:Droppable Petals]] [[Category:Stackable Petals]] [[Category:Assembling]] [[Category:Ladybugs]] [[Category:Plant-based petals]]

title: Rubber
content:
{{Petal Template Infobox | image = File:Rubber (Common).png|C File:Rubber (Unusual).png|U File:Rubber (Rare).png|R File:Rubber (Epic).png|E File:Rubber (Legendary).png|L File:Rubber (Mythic).png|M File:Rubber (Ultra).png|Ul File:Rubber (Super).png|S File:Rubber (Unique).png|Uq | description = Increases knockback received. Also absorbs lightning damage dealt to the flower. | first-rarity = {{Rarity|Common}} | damage = {{Petal Scale|1}} | health = {{Petal Scale|30}} | knockback = {{Infobox Colors Empty|+0.3|+0.6|+0.9|+1.2|+1.5|+1.8|+2.1|+2.4|+2.7}} | reload-time = 2.5s | stackability = Stackable | dropped-by = * [[Termite Mound]] | special = Increases knockback.
Absorbs lightning damage. }} '''Rubber''' is a [[petal]] in [https://florr.io florr.io]. It increases the amount of knockback received by the flower and absorbs incoming lightning damage. Rubber is dropped by [[Termite Mound]]s. ==Appearance== It is a square with curved sides, resembling a marshmallow. The petal uses the same colors as Basic. ==Function == {|class="article-table" |+Petal Stats ![[Rarity]] !Obtainable? !Damage !Health !Respawn !Knockback |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |rowspan="9"|Yes |1 |30 |rowspan="9"|2.5 seconds | +0.3 |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |3 |90 | +0.6 |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |9 |270 | +0.9 |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |27 |810 | +1.2 |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |81 |2,430 | +1.5 |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |243 |7,290 | +1.8 |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |729 |21,870 | +2.1 |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |2,189 |65,610 | +2.4 |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} |6,561 |196,830 | +2.7 |} It increases knockback received from all collisions (except collision with other players) and is stackable. The increased knockback will still apply if the petal is broken. It also absorbs lightning damage until it is destroyed, after which any excess lightning damage will be absorbed by other absorber petals or dealt to the user. ==Obtainment== ===[[Termite Mound]]=== {{:Termite Mound/Drop Rubber}} ===Purchasing=== On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]]. ''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]'' {{Petal Price|3}} ==Obtaining== *Defeat [[Termite Mound]]s in [[Jungle]] to obtain Rubber. Use [[Bone]]s of at least one rarity above the Termite Mounds to quickly take down the Termites. [[Web]]s, [[Pincer]]s and [[Jelly|Jellies]] may also be helpful. ==Strategy== *Rubber's ability to absorb lightning damage makes it an effective petal for countering [[Jellyfish]]es. It can be paired with [[Sponge]] to avoid burst lightning damage depleting your health instantly. *Rubber is also useful for escaping mob swarms whenever you are completely surrounded. Using multiple Rubbers with [[Bandage]] allows you to travel across the map at high speeds in mob-dense areas in order to load the {{Rarity|Ultra}} zone of the map and possibly spawn {{Rarity|Super}} mobs. **You can obtain the "Sound Barrier" {{Rarity|Mythic}} and {{Rarity|Ultra}} [[Achievements]] with this method. ==Gallery== File:Rubber (Common).png|{{R|1}} Rubber. File:Rubber (Unusual).png|{{R|2}} Rubber. File:Rubber (Rare).png|{{R|3}} Rubber. File:Rubber (Epic).png|{{R|4}} Rubber. File:Rubber (Legendary).png|{{R|5}} Rubber. File:Rubber (Mythic).png|{{R|6}} Rubber. File:Rubber (Ultra).png|{{R|7}} Rubber. File:Rubber (Super).png|{{R|8}} Rubber. File:Rubber (Unique).png|{{R|9}} Rubber. File:Rubber.png|Rubber. ==Trivia== *This is the only petal that increases knockback received. *This is the only petal that used to directly reduce lightning damage, but now it was more likely to behave as old [[Magnet]] (absorbs lightning strike). *There used to be a bug where Rubber stopped working when destroyed. *Rubber is by far the easiest way to get the Sound Barrier Achievements. *On an unknown update, if a player collide with another player using Rubber, Rubber won't work. *Rubber's ability no longer work when the player is in undead state. {{Petals}} [[zh:橡胶]] [[Category:Petals]] [[Category:Common+]] [[Category:Stackable Petals]] [[Category:Buffs]] [[Category:Termite Mound]] [[Category:Jungle]] [[Category:Absorber]]

title: Salt
content:
{{Petal Template Infobox | image = File:Salt (Unusual).png|U File:Salt (Rare).png|R File:Salt (Epic).png|E File:Salt (Legendary).png|L File:Salt (Mythic).png|M File:Salt (Ultra).png|Ul File:Salt (Super).png|S File:Salt (Unique).png|Uq | description = Reflects some of the damage you take back to the enemy that dealt it.
Higher rarity mobs have higher resistance to damage reflection. | first-rarity = {{R|Unusual}} | damage = {{Petal Scale|10|lowest-rarity=Unusual}} | health = {{Petal Scale|10|lowest-rarity=Unusual}} | damage-reflection = 100% (see below for mob resistance) | reload-time = 2.5s | stackability = Unstackable | dropped-by = * [[Desert Centipede]] * [[Starfish (Mob)]] | special = Reflects damage }} '''Salt''' is a petal that was secretly added into florr.io on the 11th January 2020. It reflects enemies damage by a certain percentage. Salt reflects 100% damage from equal rarity mobs, 3x more for lower rarities and x1/3 for higher rarities. Also it reflects 20% damage against other flowers. Salt is dropped by Desert Centipede and Starfish. ==Appearance== A white heptagon with a light-gray border, resembling the cracked and rugged shape of a [[Rock (Mob)|Rock]]. ==Function== {| class="article-table" |+Petal Stats ![[Rarity]] !Obtainable? !Damage !Health !Respawn !Damage Reflection |- |'''Common''' |No |10 |10 |rowspan="9"|2.5 seconds |100% (vs. '''Unusual''' |rowspan="8"|Yes |30 |30 |100% (vs. '''Unusual''') |- |'''Rare''' |90 |90 |100% (vs. '''Rare''') |- |'''Epic''' |270 |270 |100% (vs. '''Epic''') |- |'''Legendary''' |810 |810 |100% (vs. '''Legendary''') |- |'''Mythic''' |2430 |2430 |100% (vs. '''Mythic''') |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |7290 |7290 |100% (vs. {{Rarity|Ultra}}) |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |21870 |21870 |100% (vs. {{Rarity|Super}}) |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} |65610 |65610 |100% (vs. {{Rarity|Unique}}) |} Reflects some incoming damage back at the entity that dealt it. For each rarity below the listed rarity, it will reflect triple the damage received: for example, {{Rarity|super}} Salt reflects 100% against {{Rarity|super}} mobs, 300% against {{Rarity|ultra}} mobs, and 900% against {{Rarity|mythic}} mobs. It reflects 20% damage against other flowers. Doesn't stack with other salts. ==Obtaining== ===[[Desert Centipede]]=== {{:Desert Centipede/Drop Salt}} ===[[Starfish (Mob)|Starfish(Mob)]]=== {{:Starfish (Mob)/Drop Salt}} ===Purchasing=== On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]]. ''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]'' {{Petal Price|1}} ==Strategy== The best defense is a good offense. Salt serves as a useful defensive petal, countering enemy attacks should they damage the user. Not only does this work against collisions, this also defends against ranged attacks. However, using Salt effectively requires the player to get hit often, which is usually a risky strategy and works best with builds full of healing petals. Against higher-rarity mobs, the damage likely won't be enough to kill the mob before you are killed, and Salt returns to being a supporting petal rather than the star of the show. Salt is best used with [[Cactus]], [[Soil]] or [[Relic]] (recent) to maximize the amount of damage enemies take, and healing petals to recover lost health from taking damage. Salt should NOT be used with petals that minimize/delay the damage you take, including [[Talisman of Evasion|Talisman]], [[Sponge]] (delays damage), [[Nazar Amulet|Nazar]] (98% reduction damage), Disc (reduces collision damage), Cotton (absorbs damage) or Root (reduces damage taken) and Lotus (absorbs poison damage) as they will also reduce the damage you reflect back. === [[Garden]] === * Salt is powerful against [[Hornet]] since they can be damaged without getting into close combat. However, dealing damage also requires taking damage, so healing petals must be used. === [[Desert]] === * Salt is not as useful since there are not many ranged mobs and the main spawns all have very high body damage. This means that salt can only deal damage if the user makes contact with the mob, which would most likely kill the user anyway. * The user can't reflect poison to dead scorpions, making it more useless. === [[Ocean]] === * Same as garden, but with the [[Jellyfish]]. The jellyfish can be harmed from a distance if the user lets the lightning strike them. Since this attack never misses, it is a more reliable source of damage. === [[Jungle]] === * In jungle, the major enemies are the [[wasp]] and the [[mantis]]. On one hand, Mantis's peas can reduce your [[armor]]; on the other hand, they can improve salt's appearance, reflecting more damage to your enemies. However, you should pay attention to avoid being damaged instantly. === [[PvP]] === ==== Using ==== * Salt's damage against flowers is significantly reduced (20%). Careless players can end up taking lethal damage from attacking a salt user. The salt petals themselves can also deal damage, so the damage reflection does not need to be 100% to threaten death. * Use [[Cactus|Cacti]] or Soil to cause the attacker to take more damage and healing petals to recover damage. ====Against==== * 2 to 3 Cacti/Soil should do the trick. With much more health, you will have a chance to kill the Salt user before your own reflected damage kills you. However, this leaves the attacker open to other builds, so it's best to make this a secondary build tailored to salt users. * Using a sponge build might work. * Avoid dealing too much damage at once - this is what the salt user wants. ** Use [[Dandelion]]s to prevent the salt user from healing, which would prevent the attacker from killing them. ==Gallery== File:Salt (Common).png|{{R|1}} Salt. (unobtainable) File:Salt (Unusual).png|{{R|2}} Salt. File:Salt (Rare).png|{{R|3}} Salt. File:Salt (Epic).png|{{R|4}} Salt. File:Salt (Legendary).png|{{R|5}} Salt. File:Salt (Mythic).png|{{R|6}} Salt. File:Salt (Ultra).png|{{R|7}} Salt. File:Salt (Super).png|{{R|8}} Salt. File:Salt (Unique).png|{{R|9}} Salt. File:Salt.png|Salt. ==Trivia== *This is one of two petals to affect flowers without physical contact to the petal, the other being [[Cutter]] (since it is worn on the flower and thus can't be touched). *It was added on the same day as [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Peas_(Legendary) Peas (Legendary)] and [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Basic_(Unique) Basic (Unique)]. *Its legacy version only worked against enemy flowers and was unstackable. **It used to be stackable, up until an update made some time after Salt was made droppable by Soldier Ants. It also used to have 50% of the effect. {{Petals}} [[zh:盐]] [[Category:Petals]] [[Category:Basic Variants]] [[Category:Buffs]] [[Category:Unstackable Petals]] [[Category:Ocean]] [[Category:Droppable Petals]] [[Category:Unusual+]] [[Category:Desert]] [[Category:Starfish]]

'''Sand''' is a petal in florr.io that contains 4 small petals bunched together. Similar to [[Light]], it has relatively low damage but fast recharge.

==Appearance==
The Sand petal consists of 4 small hexagons. Each hexagon is yellow with a dark yellow outline. The hexagons are arranged in a square shape.

==Function==
{|class="article-table"
|+Petal Stats

Unlike [[Light]], all of the petals occupy the same spot. This applies to some petals like the Stinger.

==Drops==
===[[Crab]]===
{{:Crab/Drop Sand}}

===[[Sandstorm]]===
{{:Sandstorm/Drop Sand}}

===[[Starfish (Mob)|Starfish]]===
{{:Starfish (Mob)/Drop Sand}}

=== Purchasing ===
On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|4}}

==Strategy==
Sand is a DPS-based petal similar to the [[Light]], having low damage and health but fast respawn. Compared to the Light, the Sand deals more burst damage but less consistently. Sand also doesn't decrease the distance between other petals.

{{Zone|Garden}} 
*The sand is useful when used with the [[faster]], dishing out high DPS.
*It suffers from having short range which can be a problem against [[Hornet|hornets]] that can shoot from a distance. Players using this must get close to mobs in order to deal damage.
**Stinger is more reliable against Hornets since the player can use a hit-and-run technique and take less damage.
*Sand's clumping means it serves as better defense when hit. However, the space between clumps of sand petals is further, so the shielding effect is less reliable. It also has lower health.

{{Zone|Desert}}
*Sand excels in desert against Beetles. Anything that gets close is quickly decimated by a ring of sands. It should be paired with the [[faster]] to increase DPS.
*Against [[Scorpion|Scorpions]], Sand struggles more, especially with Legendary+ ones. Just like when fighting Hornets, Stinger is better at getting quick hits in to drain lots of health.
* Players must be careful since the sand has low range. It is best to have a teammate use [[Web|webs]] to immobilize enemies making them vulnerable to sand.
*Due to Sandstorm's random movement, using Sand to take them down can be difficult, but skilled players can do it.

{{Zone|Ocean}}
*Sand has high DPS, so it's decent against [[Starfish (Mob)|Starfish]]. However, due to its lack of range, sand struggles against many mobs in Ocean. 
** In particular, [[Jellyfish]] will be able to deal damage to the player before sand has the chance to deal damage to it.

{{Zone|PvP}}
====Using====
*The sand is a solid DPS petal with decent burst damage potential. However, its DPS is lower than the [[Light]]'s and its burst damage is lower than the [[Stinger]]'s. Sand serves as a middle ground between the two, but is typically less effective.
*Sand can be used to wear down the HP of high health petals or disable [[egg]]s before they have a chance to hatch. 
*Sand's petal density can serve as a shield despite each individual petal being weak. This also gives it good knockback.
*Sand can deal extreme amounts of damage combined with some [[Faster]] and most importantly, Mythic+ [[Bur]]. For example, 2 Mythic Sand and 1 Mythic [[Bur]] can deal atleast 25k damage.

====Against==== 
*A few sand clumps will usually not be enough to deal lethal damage. Quick attacks can make work of a sand user.
*Sand also serves as a shield, potentially blocking ranged attacks
**Players should take advantage of this, using [[stinger]]s or [[missile]]s to deal damage faster. Using [[Bubble]]s can help with this.
*[[Lightning]] can bypass the sand's shielding effect.
*A [[Rock]] or [[Heavy]] can cleave through the weak Sand petals and hit the player.

<gallery>

</gallery>

== Trivia==
* Sand ties with [[Egg (Ant)|Ant Egg]], [[Peas]], and [[Grapes]] for second for the petal with the most individual petals, after {{Rarity|Mythic}} [[Light]] and {{Rarity|Ultra}} [[Stinger]].
* It's one of the petals that has no changes from the time it was released (unless you're using [[Talents/Duplicator|Duplicator talent]]).
* Now, Sand can outclass Light DPS due to being affected by Duplicator talent and the mobs armor update, as well as better damage.

[[File:Helmets.png|thumb|Two summoned Sandstorms from different players summoned using [[Mysterious Stick|Sticks]] colliding into each other. Notice the red color as they take damage.]]
'''Sandstorm''' is a passive mob added when the desert scenario was added. It is found in the [[Desert]] area.

==Appearance==
It has the appearance of 3 hexagons, the biggest one is on the outside and is bright yellow. It spins clockwise. The second one is more dark and spins counter clockwise. The third, which is in the middle, spins clockwise and is the darkest.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:'''  Faster than the player, but in unpredictable directions.

'''Behavior:''' Moves randomly and 100% unpredictable. Beware of its high body damage though, if you aren't careful, the Sandstorm might run into you and kill you. Take it out from a distance if possible and be careful when getting close to it. Super Sandstorms pull in nearby mobs and players, so attack with ranged weapons. It phases through mobs.

==Spawning==
The {{PAGENAME}} spawns in Desert. it spawns most commonly in the top left part of desert.
==Drops==

===[[Sand]]===
{{:Sandstorm/Drop Sand}}

===[[Glass]]===
{{:Sandstorm/Drop Stick}}

===[[Stick]]===
{{:Sandstorm/Drop Stick}}

==Strategy ==
*Don't get too close to the Sandstorm, as it might end up moving straight at you without you even realizing it, And it's lethal damage will most likely kill you. [[Third Eye]] and [[Wing]] can kill the Sandstorm from a distance.
*Long ranged petals like [[Missile]] and [[Uranium]] are best because you don't have to get up close. Using ram builds is not advisable against sandstorms due to high body damage and health. If there are any other mobs besides the sandstorm around you, kill those first before dealing with the sandstorm.
*As higher-rarity Sandstorms are huge and can phase through mobs, they can conceal mobs from view, which could ambush you if you get too close to the Sandstorm. Be cautious.

=== Super Sandstorm ===
*The Super Sandstorm itself isn't as dangerous, but the Ultra mobs beside it are a threat. When bubbling to the mob, it is advised to use [[Sponge]] and multiple healing petals so that when something hits you, you don't die. 
*It is advised to form a "megasquad". That is the group of people that come together to kill the mob. It increases the chances of everybody surviving.
*It is strongly advised to clear the surroundings. This is because it is hard or almost impossible to lure sandstorm, resulting in people having to fight the mob where it is. People are going to use  [[Air]] or [[Soil]]; this makes everyone to easily get hit by Ultra mobs.
*Super Sandstorm's special ability (pulling nearby entities except for petals inwards) can be very hard to deal with, since if you are close to the Sandstorm, it will be nearly a guaranteed death. 
**Using Soil helps as it makes you "heavier" (slower) so that you are harder to pull. Keep moving away and it won't be able to reach you.
**Air also applies to this strategy, however, be careful with other players pushing you to the Sandstorm as they may use higher rarity of the Air.
**Another way is to keep some Bubbles in your secondary slots, so when the Sandstorm enables the ability, you can escape its pull with Bubbles.
**Cutters do not work against this anymore.
**This is very limited, but [[Pharaoh's Crown]]'s undead beetles get sucked into a {{Rarity|Super}}+ sandstorm and do a lot of damage.

*Sandstorm's description is a reference to the music title "Sandstorm", made by Darude.
*Before the map texture update, sandstorms used to have a more yellow color.
*In an unmentioned update, Sandstorms have its health nerfed.

The '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is a mob that spawns quite frequently in [[Desert]].

==Appearance==
A brown, egg shaped mob with a dark brown outline. Four dark brown stripes can be found going sideways across its body not quite reaching the ends. It has six small black legs and two small black pincers in the front. A stinger of similar shape to the scorpion can be found near the end of its back. It is much smaller and only has two stripes. On its front end is a large black triangle.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Medium

'''Behavior:''' Aggressive

When passive, it wanders around randomly, moving a few tiles forward before stopping and changing direction. When aggressive, Scorpions will move towards the player but won't immediately charge in. {{Rarity|Common}}-{{Rarity|Epic}} Scorpions will shoot their missile once, and then charge towards you at a much higher speed than even a [[Beetle]]. {{Rarity|Legendary}}+ Scorpions can regain their missiles and therefore will keep shooting them at you (like a [[Hornet]], but slower). If a missile hits you, it will inflict poison damage. The base damage along with the poison damage per second increases with higher rarities.

==Spawning==
Scorpions spawn in [[Desert]] commonly, like [[Beetle|Beetles]]. Scorpions do not have their own spawning area but the frequency at which they spawn normally should be enough to grind them.

==Drops==

===[[Iris]]===
{{:Scorpion/Drop Iris}}

===[[Pincer]]===
{{:Scorpion/Drop Pincer}}

==Strategy==
*Scorpions are stronger versions of [[Hornet|Hornets]], having more health with quicker missile speed and dealing poison damage to any flower the missile touches. 
**Due to this, scorpions are both hard to avoid and annoying so make sure you have good petals (see below) to help neutralize the scorpions.
**Bringing another player or a squad can also help deal with scorpions due to the amount of DPS you could inflict on them.
**Their missiles are a lot faster than [[Hornet|Hornets]]' but they shoot less missiles. Scorpions shoot once every 5 seconds, so try counting before getting ready for the shot.
***Like [[Hornet|Hornets]], Scorpions can predict where you will be when they shoot. So, try moving in the opposite direction from where you think it will shoot.
*For lower concentrations of Scorpions, using high DPS builds that include [[Faster]], [[Light]] and/or [[Sand]] can alone suffocate an attack.  
**For rarer scorpions, DPS alone will not kill it in time and the poison will mean almost certain death. It is essential that slowing petals are used such as [[Pincer]] or [[Web]].
**For high concentrations of scorpions, [[Web]] is essential since it can affect multiple mobs at once. Unlike [[Spider]]s, Scorpions are vulnerable to [[Web|Webs]]. It is best to have one teammate spam webs while the rest use high DPS builds since the webs themselves can't deal damage.
*If you're facing a single {{Rarity|Ultra}} Scorpion, which is quite powerful, use a strategy good for ranged mobs: have one player keep its aggro while others attack it. For Scorpion, a healing build with Lotus is recommended if you're the one it's attacking. If you're alone, swerve back and forth so its missiles don't hit you.
*Using [[Lotus]] is quite useful as it absorbs the poison damage, which can be lethal at higher rarities because of how broken Poison is.
*Plank could also be used to destroy the missiles, but use at least two because Scorpions are very dangerous. It's hard to rely on your Planks to save you.

=== Super Scorpion ===

* The Super mob itself isn't as dangerous, but the Ultra mobs around it are. When bubbling to the mob, it is advised to use [[Sponge]] and multiple healing petals so that if something hits you, you won't die immediately. 
* When killing the mob, it is a good strategy to form a "megasquad", where everyone groups together to kill the mob. It increases everyone's chances of surviving.
* If you arrive there early, you could lure the Super mob to easier areas. This can be done rather by clearing the area or just rush in to the crowd of mobs with poo on and use rubber and bulb to lure it out into easier areas.
* If you arrive a bit late, look for where people are gathered. The Super mob is probably already lured or trying to be lured. 
* It is advised to use [[Talents/Second Chance|Second Chance Talent]] in case the scorpion hits you.  
* When killing it, use a high DPS build and attack it from it's head or the bottom. Do not attack it from the side as it could move sideways and accidentally hit you.  It is recommended to attack it from behind so that it can't hit you. 
* If the Scorpion is attracted to you, you could bubble away and hit behind a wall or just hide behind where there is lots of people. If you are skilled enough, just move back and forth to doge the missiles 
* No need for [[Lotus]] or [[Sponge]] if there are barely other mobs around, but just beware about the Super Scorpion.

==Trivia==
*This mob used to ram its enemy and deal poison. Now the [[Spider]] has this ability.
* This mob, [[Barrel]], [[Hel Spider]], and [[Spider]] are the only mobs that can deal poison damage.
*Its missile can be destroyed before it is actually shot out.
*While the [[Hornet]] and [[Wasp]] don’t get affected by [[Honey]], Scorpion gets affected by it. Also unlike other mobs, it instead shoots its poison missiles trying to destroy the [[Honey]], while the other mobs usually eat it.

title: Secret Areas
content:
{{WIP}}
{{Removed}}
{{Removed Areas}}
There are some areas in [[florr.io]] that are secret and not normally accessible, these areas are known from the game's files and events that [[M28]] hosted.
== Current ==

=== [[PvP]] ===
This is a group of three areas in florr.io that rotated every 20 hours. They were added on March 17th, 2023. They were made inaccessible in the April 12th, 2024 update, but they still exist in the game files, so they are listed here.

For more info on them, see [[PvP]].

=== Battle Royale ===
This is a secret area in florr.io. It was added on March 17th, 2023, alongside the other [[PvP]] areas. It was used for events before. No mobs spawn in it. This area has a minimap and a name below it ingame.

=== a.tmj ===
This is a secret area in florr.io, it was used for events. No mobs spawn in it. It has a minimap but no name below it, so its internal name is used here.

== Removed ==

=== unnamed_0.tmj ===
This was a secret area found in the game files, It was used for events before, no mobs spawn in it. It had a minimap but no name below it, so its internal name is used here.

It can be assumed that this area was removed as it is no longer in the game files.

=== Jungle Testing Area ===
This was another secret area found in the game files. It was likely used to test Jungle mobs. <!-- IOMAN mentioned this in the florr.io discord, so this is most likely true. --> [[Mantis]] could spawn in it. It was removed and most likely replaced with unnamed_0.tmj.

=== Christmas garden map chamber ===
This was an area that was only accessible if M28 teleported you there. Existed in the center of the Christmas garden map and this part didn't spawn any mobs.

title: Sewers
content:
{{Stub}}

 |distance_from_spawn = 1 zone away from Garden spawn.
 |mob_diversity = about 2.857
}}
'''Sewers''' is an area in Florr.io. It includes all types of Sewers mobs. 

== Location ==
'''Sewers''' is in the top left region of the world map, and can be reached from [[Garden]], which is the standard spawn location. The map features several interconnected tunnels and large rooms, as well as a huge open area at the end.
{| class="article-table"
!'''Area'''
!'''Connection location'''
|-
|Garden
|Mid-left
|}

=== Mobs ===
A total of 6 mobs spawn in this area.

'''[[Spider]]''': Aggressive mob that wanders around until it detects a flower and then starts chasing it. All Spider rarities are faster than you and {{Rarity|Legendary}}+ ones lay down webs in its tracks. Drops [[Web]], [[Faster]], and [[Third Eye]] or [[Third Eye/Magic|Magic Eye]] with {{Rarity|Mythic}}+ [[Mana Orb]] equipped.

'''[[Roach]]''': Neutral mob that chases you at a very fast speed when hit. Its speed is proportional to its health, so it goes slower as its health decreases. Drops [[Lotus]] and [[Antennae]].

'''[[Moth]]''': A shy mob that has low health and runs away from you when hit. Drops [[Wing]] and [[Bulb]]. Having a Light Bulb of any rarity equipped makes Moths run toward you instead.

'''[[Fly]]''': Aggressive mob that chases you when you get close enough. It also has a 90% chance to evade hits and not take damage from them. Lightning and poison damage ignore evasion and are hence useful against flies. It does not spawn naturally, and instead comes out of Garbage. Drops [[Wing]], [[Talisman of Evasion]], and [[Poo]].

'''[[Silverfish]]''': A threatening mob that can grab [[Garbage]] the same rarity of itself and ram the player with it, running away to grab more when the garbage is destroyed. It always spawn with a garbage held. Drops [[Clay]] and [[Dust]].

'''[[Garbage]]''': A a stationary mob in Sewers, sometimes used by [[Silverfish]] to ram players. It can also spawn flies the same rarity as itself. Drops [[Poo]] and [[Broccoli]].

=== Special Zones ===
The top right zone is the box with a lot of  {{Rarity|Ultra}} Mobs.

The bottom left box spawns a lot of Garbage.

The bottom middle box spawns a lot of Roaches.

The small box above the bottom right corner spawns slightly more Moths.

The box at the end of the long passage at the Center contains a lot of Garbage and some Silverfish.

== Strategy ==
*This area has no checkpoints, so be very careful, especially in the end zone. If you die and respawn, you will go right back to the Sewer Pipe, the very beginning of this area.
*If possible, try not to aggro [[Roach|Roaches]] unless you have high rarity slow petals as they are extremely fast and dangerous.
*The main box at the top right of the map gives a lot of drops, but needs a very powerful squad.
*A ram build can be used in sewers due to the mobs having low damage. Ram builds are best used in Sewers because all the mobs that spawn here are melee mobs. However, due to the sheer amount of {{R|Mythic}} and {{R|Ultra}} mobs you'll find in here, ram builds may fall short. Only use them if you're in a big crowd of players that can revive you if you die.
*Use [[Wing|Wings]] combined with [[Third Eye]], because their high range will help against [[Fly|Flies]] and [[Roach|Roaches]].
*Only go to the right part at {{Rarity|Mythic}} and bottom part/the box of Sewers when you are {{Rarity|Ultra|Ultra+}} or with a lot of {{R|Ultra}}+ players around.
*For builds suitable for this area, see [[Builds - Sewers|Build - Sewers]].
*[[Lightning]] is very consistent at looting mobs thanks to the high density and averagely low health. This may aggro Roaches though.
*If you were to have [[Moon Rock]] (preferably {{Rarity|Ultra|Ultra+}}), you can use it to travel deep into the Sewers, which then you can use [[Ankh]] to teleport into. Watch [https://youtu.be/uV4VLo-_W2o this video] for more details. This can be used to create a strong Sewers squad on the corners without bringing anyone else.
**Due to the density update, spawners (areas that spawn mobs) will despawn all mobs except {{Rarity|Super|text=Super+}} belonging to them when no players are close (about the range of {{Rarity|Super}} [[Antennae]]). This explains why the Moon does not take much damage and consumable petals do not get destroyed, which is crucial to locate the position of the Moon.
*Grapes are extremely useful at dealing with Flies, although you may unintentionally aggro roaches

== Gallery ==
<gallery>
File:Screenshot 2025-07-28 at 18.06.03.png

</gallery>

== Tiles ==

</gallery>

== Trivia ==
* Prior to the April 12th 2024 update, this area used to be split into 4 individual areas before being merged and adding a rectangle part at the bottom.
** After that, the old areas were merged to a big area with a new part which is nicknamed as Centralia Sewers 5.
* This was the only area beside [[Hel]] to not have stationary mobs until the overhaul.
* The extreme density update has made Sewers Box almost impossible to farm. Now to get there, you need a megasquad, or a squad with Super Eggs, [[Mysterious Stick|Stick]], [[Mysterious Stick/Magic|Magic Stick]], [[Moon Rock|Moon]] and {{Rarity|Ultra|Ultra+}} [[Wax]].
* On November 9, 2025, the Sewer map got an overhaul.

:''This page is about the petal. For the mob, see [[Shell (Mob)|Shell mob]].''

'''Shell''' is a [[Petals|petal]] in [https://florr.io florr.io]. Its function consists of giving the user or allies shield, which depletes over time. Shell is dropped by [[Shell (Mob)|Shell mobs]].

== Appearance ==
Shell consists of the mob with the same name, but without the tail.

== Function ==

Shell adds a shield to the user or allies. This shield is seen as a white bar covering the health bar. Shield is depleted before health does, which decays at a rate proportional to the amount of shield the player has.

==Obtaining==
===[[Shell (Mob)]]===
{{:Shell (Mob)/Drop Shell}}

===Purchasing===
On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|2}}

== Strategy==

* Shell's shield is too low to replace healing by itself, so it should be used in tandem with actual healing petals.
* Shell should not be used with [[Sponge]] given its insanely low shield per second rate.
* In many cases, it's outperformed by [[Amulet]].
* Since Shell can affect other players near you, it can be helpful for group healing.

''Not to be confused with the petal, [[Shell]].'' 

The '''Shell''' is a mob in florr.io. Normally, it is immobile, but it darts towards you once every couple seconds after the player damages it. 

== Appearance ==
Shell has a hand fan shape with beige color and a darker outline, with two pairs of two lines, Each pair are shaped as a "V" shape and are placed in left-to-right position.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' When passive, doesn't move, When hostile, dashes every second. 

'''Behavior:''' Neutral. When provoked, lays down "giant" pearls (it shoots the pearls at {{Rarity|Super}} Rarity). It will then shake a couple times and suck the pearls back to itself. It also darts at you every couple seconds, blasting you backwards or potentially crushing you into a wall.

Note: The Shell has a shield that you must destroy first before bringing down the Shell's health.

==Spawning==
It spawns in pretty much everywhere at certain rarity in [[Ocean]]. It spawns the most in the area linked to the entrance of Crab Kingdom.
==Drops==

===[[Magnet]]===
{{:Shell (Mob)/Drop Magnet}}

===[[Shell]]===
{{:Shell (Mob)/Drop Shell}}

===[[Pearl]]===
{{:Shell (Mob)/Drop Pearl}}

==Strategy ==

* Mythic+ Shells are quite tanky due to its high health and shield, not to mention its pearl stats. You will need petals that utilize in DPS to kill it easily.
*{{Rarity|Ultra}} Shells are one of the most powerful Ultra mobs. They're tanky, damaging, and their shield makes them hard to kill. Use [[Iris]], [[Lightning]], or other petals that deal Poison or Lightning damage to override its armor. [[Pincer]] has the added benefit of slowing down the Shell, but because it only does this for 0.8 seconds, it's likely the Shell will stay immobile for that time and not be affected.
*Shell has the most powerful missile health. Others being [[Hornet]] & [[Wasp]].
*When [[Bur]] is paired with fast reload petals like [[Light]], the shells can be killed faster than before.
*Make sure to fight Shells in large, open areas so it's much harder for it to crush you against a wall.
*[[Bone|Bones]] one rarity above the shell were good for dealing with Shells, but after their armor nerf, they can only completely tank Shells two rarities below. Bones are still very good against shells.

==={{Rarity|Super}} Shell===
*{{Rarity|Super}} Shells can shoot pearls sometimes when aggressive, which places the pearl. Counter this with a ranged build with Third eye, Wing, Missile, or Carrot.  
**Be sure you aren't in the path of the pearls. Eventually the Shell will call them back and hit anyone in the way.
**The best way to counter the {{Rarity|Super}} Shell was by hiding behind a wall at a very specific location near the walls and attacking it with Third Eye or Wings. The Shell would've been be unable to aggro on you there.
***This no longer works because it will now run away if it gets attacked behind a wall.

<gallery widths="185">

</gallery>

==Trivia== 
*Shell used to have a lot of armor, the same amount as [[Leafbug]]. Eventually, the armor was nerfed and later replaced with shield.
*The super variety has the highest amount of health of any ocean mob; this makes it the 5th highest health of all mobs, besides the [[Hel Beetle]], [[Queen Ant]] and [[Queen Fire Ant]].
**As of now, [[Ant Hole]] has the most health of all mobs, beating Termite Overmind.
*The description is a reference/advertisement to [https://www.shell.com/ ''Royal Dutch Shell'']'','' a car-gas manufacturing company.
*The Shell (specifically the {{Rarity|8}} one) used to not shoot pearls as a form of attack.
*At an unknown update, Shell has ability to gain shield overtime if it is at full health and not being damaged.

'''Shovel''' is a petal in florr.io. It enables you to dig into the ground. While [[underground]], the flower will be immune to all kinds of damage and can pass through entities.

==Appearance==
It looks like a poorly drawn shovel with a wooden hilt.

==Function==
''Note: The "A + B" respawn format means that the petal takes A seconds to respawn and B seconds after respawning to do its special action/have its special action enabled.''
{|class="article-table"
|+Petal Stats
![[Rarity]]
!Obtainable?
!Respawn
|-
|{{Rarity|Common}}
|rowspan="10"|Yes
|15 + 2 seconds
|-
|{{Rarity|Unusual}}
|13.1 + 2 seconds
|-
|{{Rarity|Rare}}
|11.8 + 2 seconds
|-
|{{Rarity|Epic}}
|10.1 + 2 seconds
|-
|{{Rarity|Legendary}}
|8.5 + 2 seconds
|-
|{{Rarity|Mythic}}
|6.9 + 2 seconds
|-
|{{Rarity|Ultra}}
|5.2 + 2 seconds
|-
|{{Rarity|Super}}
|3.6 + 2 seconds
|-
|{{Rarity|Unique}}
| rowspan="2"|2 + 2 seconds
|-
|{{Rarity|9b}}
|}
After reloading for a few seconds, the shovel will stay intact until you right click or shift, and it burrows the player underground for 2.0 seconds (the higher rarity, the quicker the time on reloading). While underground, the flower will be immune to all kinds of damage (except worm while burrowed) and can pass through entities. The flower will be shown as a dark grey dot and their movement speed will be decreased (while underground). Soil affects the size of the dark grey dot. Magnets will stop working, but Antennae will still work like usual and the player can pick up petals by touching them.

==Obtaining==
===[[Ant Hole]]===
{{:Ant Hole/Drop Shovel}}

===[[Worm]]===
{{:Worm/Drop Shovel}}

=== Purchasing ===
On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|3}}
==Strategy==
===For obtaining===
Garden is the place where you can obtain it through Ant Holes. You can also obtain this petal in Ant Hell by killing [[Worm|Worms]].
===For using===
Shovels can be used to escape a mob that is chasing you by having it target someone else. Shovels can also be used to survive longer in the event that you are surrounded by mobs, allowing you to find a safe spot. Use this petal with [[Iris]] or [[Pincer]] to take advantage of the fact that mobs will run away from the last direction they were attacked at if there is no target (to avoid being cheesed.)
====[[Garden]]====
* Helps to pass through hornet missiles and get close enough to do damage, if you don't have third eye. 
====[[Desert]]====
* Use this to outrun beetles, especially Ultra ones.
* Watch out for submerged desert centipedes while your shovel ability is activated because they can hit you.
====[[Ocean]]====
* Leeches stay stuck on players even if they use Shovel. Can't use this petal to escape.
* Useful for swarms of mobs. If you have a quick reaction time it will be easier to survive ocean.
* Shovel can be used to avoid {{Rarity|8}} Jellyfish's shockwave.
====[[Centralia Sewers|Sewers]]====
* For Ultra Roach, hit the roach then equip Shovel to lose aggro. Keep doing this to kill the roach or lead it to a corner.
====[[Ant Hell]]====
* Shovel can be used to pass through [[Air]] and [[Soil]] users blocking the entrance.
* Do not use Shovel against worm, the worms which are also underground will follow your user and deal lethal damage on most occasions. Proceed with caution if you have the second chance talent.
* Shovel helps against swarms of ants closing in on you or your squad, but due to the fact that worms spawn here, it's not a good idea.
====[[Ellne Jungle|Jungle]]====
* Use shovel when mobs are closing in. Try to get on the other side of the wall of mobs. Keep doing this until you can reach a safe area or are now in a position to kill the mobs. 
====[[PvP]]====
=====Using=====
* Strategy for using this petal in pvp. 
=====Against=====
* Use an ant egg and put the summons over the area where the player will appear at.

==Gallery==

File:Shovel (Unusual).png|{{R|2}} Shovel.

</gallery>

==Trivia==
* Shovel used to be applied instantly after cooldown, but now it can be used after cooldown by right-clicking or shift-click.
* Shovel is the only petal that makes the player invulnerable.

'''Soil''' is a petal in florr.io that increases the user's maximum health, sacrificing movement speed, and makes the user larger in size (and thus, increasing their hitbox).

== Appearance ==
The '''Soil''' is a heptagon with a brown color, and with a dark brown outline.

== Function ==

Increases the user's maximum health when equipped, more than [[Cactus]], however, also decreasing movement speed and increases flower size.

==Drops==
===[[Ant Hole]]===
{{:Ant Hole/Drop Soil}}

===[[Termite Mound]]===
{{:Termite Mound/Drop Soil}}

===[[Worm]]===
{{:Worm/Drop Soil}}

=== Purchasing ===
On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|6}}

==Strategy== 
===[[Garden]]===
*Soil allows the user to soak up more damage from incoming attacks, particularly [[Hornet]] swarms. However, by increasing your hitbox, it also makes you more vulnerable.

===[[Desert]]===
*Because of the fact that Soil can reduce your movement speed, this is useful for fighting Ultra/Super [[Sandstorm|Sandstorms]], as slower movement speed can help resist the Sandstorm's pull.

===[[Ocean]]===
*Soils are not that good against some mobs.

===[[PvP]]===
====Using====
*You can pair some Soil with some Salt to kill anyone who does enough damage to you. Players will also run away from you. Feel free to use a Cutter if you plan on ramming players.

===Against===
*Since Soil makes flowers slower, you can attack your enemy from a distance without them attacking you as much, or run away. They will also be easier to hit, due to Soil making them larger.

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">

</gallery>

==Trivia==
* The Soil is one of many petals in florr.io that increase your flower stats. In this case, it increases your size and flower health with a cost of movement speed.
** Soil is one of the three petals that increases flower health. The others are [[Cactus]] and [[Relic]].
*** If 10 {{Rarity|8}} Soils are equipped in your inventory, you will get a 1,093,500 health increase, allowing the player to out-ram the {{Rarity|7}} [[Rock (Mob)|Rock]].
** Soil is one of the two petals that increases your size. The other is [[Air]].
*** Using 10 soil would enlarge the flower to around legendary digger size.
*** It is possible to mimic/cosplay as a [[Digger]] by using couple Soils and a [[Cutter]].
**** After the Air granted its size increase, soils are still used to cosplay as a Digger.
* ViciousOogie was the first person to obtain the {{Rarity|8}} Soil.
* Players can block entrances in [[Ant Hell]] with Soil and sometimes Air to prevent players from entering to where they're supposed to farm for. This can be very annoying for newer players.
** [[Shovel]] and/or [[Bubble]] work against this.
*If you equip Soil or [[Air]] when next to a wall or a flower, your flower's size won't change until you move away from the hitbox.

''For other versions of the Soldier Ant, see [[Soldier Termite]] or [[Soldier Fire Ant]].''

The '''Soldier Ant''' is a mob in florr.io that was added on February 3rd, 2020. This ant is hostile toward all flowers regardless of being attacked or not.

==Appearance==
Looks exactly like a [[Worker Ant]] but has two wings on its back. The wings are semi-transparent, white ovals attached to the back behind the head. The wings flap back and forth when aggressive but are still when passive.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Slow when passive, medium when aggressive.

'''Behavior:''' Wanders around randomly, moving a few tiles before stopping and changing directions. Becomes aggressive when a flower gets close, at which point it will chase the flower. It's aggressive range is smaller than other aggressive mobs like beetles and hornets, allowing you to get closer. Soldier ants also stop chasing you if you're at least a moderate distance away. Soldier ants that spawn from an ant hole are aggressive upon spawning and will chase the attacker. One spawns around an ant hole when the ant hole spawns and 26 more spawn as the ant hole is being attacked. They are also aggressive towards Termites and Fire Ants.

Soldier Ants spawned from [[Queen Ant|Queen Ants]] tend to follow the Queen Ant that spawned them (even when the Queen Ant is dead).

==Spawning==
The Soldier Ant spawns in Garden, Ant Holes and Ant Hell.

==Drops==
=== [[Clover]]===
{{:Soldier Ant/Drop Clover}}

=== [[Glass]]===
{{:Template:ID 5}}

==Strategy==
*Lone Soldier Ants pose little to no threat considering they are fairly weak. The problem is that the ants almost never come alone.
*As they have low body damage, it is recommended to use [[Bone|Bones]] of one higher rarity or more to kill them effectively.
*When attacking [[Ant Hole]]s, be very careful since five ants or even more can come out at a time, overwhelming you and causing damage. Be aware that worker ants will also become aggressive and watch for the queen ant, which can block hits that could kill the soldier ants.
*Soldier Ants are useful if you need [[Glass]] or [[Clover]]s. Glass is a good beginners' petal as they are easy to farm.
*Consider [[Lightning]], it is a multi-purpose petal that will very briefly stun the ants and allow you to kill or loot multiple at a time.

=== Super Soldier Ant ===

* In Ant Hell, the main danger when fighting the Super Soldier Ant is not the Soldier Ant itself, but the many Ultra mobs that spawn around it. When bubbling to the Soldier Ant, it is advised to use [[Sponge]] and multiple Ultra healing petals. It is also advised to use Sponge when actually fighting the Soldier Ant, so you don't die if anything hits you.
* If the mob spawns deep in Normal Ant Hell, it is advised to form a "megasquad", where a large group of players stay near each other and clear mobs. This increases the chance for everyone to survive.
* It is advised to use a high DPS build when killing the Super to ensure loot. 

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185">

==Trivia==
*On May 8, 2024, [[Wing]] drop rates are removed from the Soldier Ant, and after a few days since the update, the drop was replaced with [[Salt]], but on June 20th 2024, it was replaced back with Glass.
*This is the first legacy mob that does not drops a petal it used to drop on legacy.
*This mob has the smallest aggro range of mobs with one along with the queen ant.
*This ant has the exact same shape and health of the [[Soldier Fire Ant]], the only differences being the color, aggro range and damage. 
* This mob holds ''the record'' for most variants in game being the normal Soldier Ant, [[Soldier Fire Ant]], [[Soldier Termite]], [[Ant Egg|Soldier Ant (spawned from Ant Egg)]], [[Ant Egg|Soldier Termite Spawned by player)]], Normal Soldier Ant spawned from normal [[Queen Ant|Queen Ants]], [[Soldier Fire Ant]] spawned from [[Queen Fire Ant|Fire Queen Ants]], and [[Soldier Ant/Diver|Diver Ant]]-8 variants in total.
*In real life, soldier ants do not have [[Wing|wings]]. Only [[Queen Ant|queen ants]] and male ants have wings during certain periods of time.
*A common experience is that lots of {{Rarity|Legendary}}+ '''Soldier Ants''' will trap you against the wall, where not even the strongest of flowers survive.

The '''Scuba Soldier Ant''' is a rare variant of the Soldier Ant. It rarely spawns in the [[Ocean]].

==Appearance==
It's a [[Soldier Ant]] with a Snorkel.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Slow when passive, medium when aggressive.

'''Behavior:''' Wanders around randomly, moving a few tiles before stopping and changing directions. Becomes aggressive when a flower gets close, at which point it will chase the flower. It's aggressive range is smaller than other aggressive mobs like [[Beetle|Beetles]] and [[Hornet|Hornets]], allowing you to get closer. Soldier Diver Ants also stop chasing you if you're at least a moderate distance away.

The Soldier Fire Ant will always come in big army because they mass spawn from [[Fire Ant Burrow|Fire Ant Burrows]]. Soldier Fire Ants have one of the highest aggro ranges of any mob, but they only spawn from their burrows so these aren't the biggest threat in the [[Desert]]. ''For other versions of the Soldier Termite, see [[Soldier Ant]] or [[Soldier Fire Ant]].''

'''{{PAGENAME}}''' is a mob in florr.io that was added in the February 27th 2023 update. It's an aggresive mob which chases the player or summoned mobs on sight, and it has Psyonic Connection, which will shares damage taken among the Termites. It drops Bone and Mysterious Relic.
== Appearance==
Looks exactly like the worker termite but has two wings on its back. The wings are semi-transparent and look like white ovals attached to the back behind the head. The wings flap back and forth when aggressive but are still when passive.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Slow when passive, medium when aggressive.

'''Behavior:''' Wanders around randomly, moving a few tiles before stopping and changing directions. Becomes aggressive when a flower gets close, at which point it will chase the flower. It's aggressive range is smaller than other aggressive mobs like beetles and hornets, allowing you to get closer. Soldier ants also stop chasing you if you're at least a moderate distance away.

It has a psyonic connection with other termites, taking damage if any other termites within a certain radius of itself takes damage.
==Spawning==
The {{PAGENAME}} spawns in Ant Hell 3 and in termite mounds in jungle.
==Drops==
===[[Mysterious Relic|Relic]]===
{{:Soldier Termite/Drop Relic}}

===[[Bone]]===
{{:Ladybug/Drop Light}}


==Strategy==
*Soldier Termites often come in swarms, therefore it is advised to stay away from the edges of the map to avoid getting smothered
*These ants, having high health and damage sharing, are extremely difficult to defeat without ultra bone (specifically Legendary+). Try to make sure that you take on different swarms one by one in order to reduce the damage spreading.
*Use [[Lightning]] to damage several termites at once. This is very effective as termites tend to die together. 
*Try to keep away from Worker and Baby Termites while fighting the soldiers, as they act as meat shields that spread the damage that you deal to the soldiers

<gallery widths="185">

File:Soldier Termite.png|{{PAGENAME}}.
</gallery>

==Trivia ==

*Soldier Termites can be spawned from [[Ant Egg]] by using [[Relic]].
*Soldier Termites, along with other Termites got a health change during the following years.
** On October 22, 2024, its HP was nerfed by 50% when its original base health is 200, before being reverted on October 23, 2025 update. M28 previously explained that since Termites share its HP, they don't need a buffed HP. However, this caused some bit of controversy when spawning it from Eggs and Relic when it was nerfed.
** On January 22, 2026, its HP was nerfed by another 50% again.
*On 2/24/2025, Soldier Termite and Soldier Fire Ant switched their drops: Privet to Termites and Bone to Fire Ants.
*On April 1st, 2025 (or April Fools day), Soldier Termites now drop a new petal called Triangle. In the next day, it no longer drop Privet, but now normal Beetle drop it and it replaced the Ankh drop. That made AH3 a lot more targeted than before and that also accidentally turned [[Ankh]] into a petal which is exclusive to [[Pyramid]].
*Soldier Termite used to drop both Privet and Triangle at the same time before Privet drop was swapped with Beetle's Ankh drop.
*The drop for Solider Termites got reverted to bones, and now Fire ants drop triangle.

'''South Desert 1''' was an area in florr.io.

==Appearance==
Just like any other South Desert area, the walls are dirt and the background is sandy. Its appearance on the map kind of resembles a curvy "H".

==Location==
South Desert 1 is located in the bottom left region of the world map. The zone is 2 zones away from [[Centralia]] and 6 zones away from [[Ellne]], the spawn locations. Assuming no interruptions, it takes around 1 minute and 44 seconds to reach this zone, without using [[Bubble|Bubbles]] or [[Powder]].

===Connections===
The area connects to 4 areas.

==Spawning==
The average rarity in South Desert 1 is {{Rarity|Common}}. Mobs typically spawn from {{Rarity|Common}} to {{Rarity|Rare}}. {{Rarity|Epic}} and {{Rarity|Legendary}} Sandstorms and/or Desert Centipedes can be seen near the South Desert 5 portal occasionally.

A total of 7 mobs spawn in South Desert 1.

===Special Zones===
There are no special zones in this area.

===Ultra Mobs===
{{Rarity|7}}/{{Rarity|8}} Sandstorm used to spawn mostly in this area.

==Strategy==
*If you have a couple of Legendary petals you shouldn't worry too much about this zone unless you want Ultra Sandstorm.
*For Ultra Sandstorm, use [[Soil]] to resist its pull.
*A Legendary Fire Ant Burrow has been spotted here before. Chances unknown.

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*It is the first area where you would enter the Desert area.
*This area was removed on April 12, 2024 update along with other South Desert areas and replaced with [[Desert]].

title: South Desert/Northwest
content: {{Removed}} {{Outdated}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:South Desert 5}} {{Area Template Infobox |name = South Desert 5 |image = SouthDesertNW.png |caption-image = |mobs = *[[Beetle]] *[[Cactus (Mob)|Cactus]] *[[Sandstorm]] *[[Scorpion]] |special_entity = |connections = *[[South Desert/Northeast|South Desert 1 (Northeast)]] *[[South Desert/West|South Desert 4 (West)]] |map_size = Medium |distance_from_spawn = * 3 zones away (Centralia) * 6 zones away (Ellne) }} '''South Desert 5''' was an area in florr.io that was added on January 21st 2023. It features high rarities of Desert mobs. ==Appearance== Just like any other South Desert area, the walls are dirt and the background is sandy. ==Location== South Desert 5 was located in the bottom left region of the world map. The zone is 3 zones from [[Centralia]], the standard spawn location. Assuming no interruptions, it takes around 3 minutes and 3 seconds to reach this zone, without using [[Bubble|Bubbles]] or [[Powder]]. ===Connections=== The area connects to 2 areas. {| class="article-table" |+Connections !Area !Connection Location |- |South Desert 1 (Northeast) |Top right |- |South Desert 4 (West) |Bottom right |} ==Spawning== The average rarity in South Desert (Northwest) is {{Rarity|5}}. Mobs typically spawn from {{Rarity|4}} to {{Rarity|6}}. If applicable, mention locations where mobs spawn at higher or lower rarities. A total of 4 mobs spawn in South Desert 5. {| class="article-table" |+Mobs !Mob !Spawn Rate |- |[[Beetle]] |High |- |[[Scorpion]] |Moderate |- |[[Cactus (Mob)|Cactus]] |Moderate |- |[[Sandstorm]] |Moderate |} ===Special Zones=== There are no special zones in this area. ===Ultra/Super Mobs=== All {{Rarity|Ultra}}/{{Rarity|Super}} Desert Mobs used to spawn in this area randomly. Originally, {{Rarity|7}}/{{Rarity|8}} Beetle used to spawn mostly in this area. ==Strategy== * In this area, you may need to have a Mythic [[Stinger]] and have the Health Skill at Legendary because [[Scorpion|Scorpions]] with high poison damage may spawn and can kill you. You may need an Epic+ [[Lotus]] to survive the poison. * Mythic Powder is a very useful petal here as it allows you to outrun mythic/ultra beetles without any slowing petals, allowing you to equip more DPS petals. However 1-2 Mythic Bubbles can work if you don't have Mythic Powder. * DO NOT enter this area if your level is low. Because you're likely gonna die by the mobs. * Something that can slow the beetles down is a good idea. Even if you have powder, you can still get surrounded by beetles if you don't kill them fast enough. Jelly, Web and Pincer are useful for this job. ==Trivia== *This is the only "new" zone in South Desert (Not counting [[Ellne Pass]]) as every other South Desert zone was added when the World Map came out, while this zone was only added 2 days after. * Before this zone was added, Mythics were hard to find in South Desert. *This area was removed on April 12, 2024 update along with other South Desert areas and replaced with [[Desert]]. ==Gallery== File:D5 spawning.png|Spawn zones in this zone. {{Removed Areas}} [[Category:Old World Map]]

'''{{SUBPAGENAME}}''' was an area in florr.io. It was the so-called "Shiny Zone", where (usually only low-rarity) [[Ladybug (Desert)|Yellow Ladybugs]] spawned, it was added on December 28th 2023.

==Appearance==
Just like any other South Desert area, the walls were made of dirt and the background was sandy. Its appearance on the map resembled a donut. The donut hole was represented as a lake.

==Location==
'''South Oasis''' was located in the South Desert region, to the right of South Desert 1 (NE). It was 3 areas away from [[Centralia]] and 6 areas away from [[Ellne]], the spawn locations.

===Connections===
The area connected to only 1 area.
{| class="article-table"
|+Connections
!Area
!Connection Location
|-
|[[South Desert/Northeast|South Desert 1 (Northeast)]]
|Left
|}

==Spawning==
The average rarity in South Oasis was {{Rarity|2}}. Mobs typically spawned from {{Rarity|1}} to {{Rarity|3}}. The higher the rarity, the rarer it spawned. But you shouldn't have just farmed here, as {{Rarity|5}} mobs almost never spawned, and if they did, you still needed to pay attention to the mob drops.

This area consisted of [[Ladybug (Desert)|Yellow Ladybugs]] (or Shiny Ladybugs) and [[Beetle]]s.
{| class="article-table"
|+Mobs
!Mob
!Spawn Rate
|-
|[[Beetle]]
|High
|-
|[[Ladybug (Desert)]]
|Low
|}

===Ultra/Super Mobs===
{{Rarity|7}}+ Yellow Ladybugs could spawn here, but it was extremely rare because {{Rarity|6}} Yellow Ladybugs could only spawn with a 0.00013% chance or higher.

==Strategy==
*Typically, nobody would've grinded here for [[Yggdrasil]] because there was a very small chance of {{Rarity|5}}+ Yellow Ladybugs spawning here, so grinding here was pretty much pointless, and there was only a 0.00013% chance of a {{Rarity|6}} Yellow Ladybug spawning there without usage of [[Clover]]s or the Luck [[Talents|Talent]], with 4.0% extra luck it was 0.00064%. This made it better to grind on higher-level areas.
* It was difficult to die here, because there weren't many hostile mobs and they had a low rarity. However, if you were just starting with the game, you would've had to watch out for [[Beetle|Beetles]] since they are dangerous, and you would've had to stay away from the higher rarity [[Ladybug (Desert)|Yellow Ladybugs]] because they could aggro on you and ram you if you were not careful.

==Gallery==
<gallery>
File:Screenshot 2023-12-28 18.01.42.png|Flowers relaxing in the South Oasis.
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*This area was one of the smallest areas in the entire game.
*Before another update, this area only spawned one mob, which was the [[Ladybug (Desert)|Shiny Ladybug]], but another update made [[Beetle|Beetles]] spawn as well.
*This area was removed on the April 12th 2024 update along with other South Desert areas and replaced with [[Desert]]. However, the spawn of the new area is inspired by this map.
*This was the last main area to be added to the World Map prior to its removal.
{{Removed Areas}}
[[Category:Areas]]
[[Category:Old World Map]]

==Appearance ==
Just like any other South Desert area, the walls are dirt and the background is sandy.

==Location==
South Desert 2 is located in the bottom left region of the world map. The zone is 3 zones from [[Centralia]], the standard spawn location. Assuming no interruptions, it takes around ?? minutes and ?? seconds to reach this zone, without using [[Bubble|Bubbles]] or [[Powder]].

*Sometimes, a mythic spawns here, but very rarely. Be aware, so it's recommended for antennae
