'''South Desert 4''' was an area in florr.io. It is also a popular map for many players as it is the only map in the game that can spawn {{R|Legendary}}+ [[Ladybug (Desert)|Shiny Ladybugs]].

==Appearance==
Just like any other South Desert area, the walls are dirt and the background is sandy.

The map looks like pincers facing left.

==Location==
South Desert 4 was located in the South west region of the world map. The zone is 4 zones from [[Centralia]], the standard spawn location. Assuming no interruptions, it takes around 4 minutes and 13 seconds to reach this zone, without using [[Bubble|Bubbles]] or [[Powder]].

===Connections===
The area connects to 2 areas.
{| class="article-table"
|+Connections
!Area
!Connection Location
|-
|South Desert/Northwest
|Top
|-
|South Desert/Southwest
|Bottom
|}

==Spawning==
The average rarity in South Desert 4 is {{R|Legendary}}. Mobs typically spawn from {{R|Epic}} to {{R|Legendary}}.

A total of 6 mobs spawn in South Desert 4.

A lot of players grind here for a [[Yggdrasil]], from a {{R|Legendary}} [[Ladybug (Desert)|Shiny Ladybug]]. However this mob is very rare, and it only has a 9.8% chance to drop the item.

'''Note for finding Shiny Ladybugs:''' as with all zones, there is a mob cap at which new mobs stop spawning, so it is important to clear other mobs and not simply wander around.

===Special Zones===
The top left is an area where many [[Fire Ant Burrow|Fire Ant Burrows]] can spawn. The corridor to the left spawns lots of [[Cactus|Cacti]].

===Ultra/Super Mobs===
{{Rarity|7}}/{{Rarity|8}} [[Scorpion]] used to spawn mostly in this area.

==Strategy==
*Be wary of [[Petals|Sandstorms]] since they can ram you with random movement.
*Be aware of {{R|Mythic}} mobs that rarely spawn because of people's [[Clover]].

==Gallery==
<gallery>
File:Mythic Shiny Ladybug Encounter in D4.png
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*[[Digger|Diggers]] are fairly common here as there are many {{R|Epic}} burrows.
*Its nicknamed the "ygg farm" since it spawns {{R|Legendary}} to {{R|Mythic}} [[Ladybug (Desert)|Shiny Ladybug]]. Or even {{R|Ultra}} Yellow Ladybug at some point.
*This area was removed on April 12, 2024 update along with other South Desert areas and replaced with [[Desert]].

title: Spider
content:
{{Mob Template Infobox | name = {{PAGENAME}} | image = File:Spider (Common).png|C File:Spider (Unusual).png|U File:Spider (Rare).png|R File:Spider (Epic).png|E File:Spider (Legendary).png|L File:Spider (Mythic).png|M File:Spider (Ultra).png|Ul File:Spider (Super).png|S File:Spider (Unique)|Uq | description = Spooky. | drops = * [[Faster]] * [[Web]] * [[Third Eye]] | health = {{Mob HP Scale|62.5}} | damage = {{Mob Damage Scale|15}} | armor = {{Mob Armor Scale|0.8|0}} | poison = {{Infobox Colors Empty|40 (15/s)|120 (45/s)|360 (135/s)|1080 (405/s)|3240 (1215/s)|9720 (3645/s)|29160 (10935/s)|87480 (32805/s)}} | xp-reward = {{Infobox Colors Empty|1|1|5|41|378|5.3k|43.2k|4.9m}} | move = Medium | status = Aggressive | attack = Body + Poison Damage {{Rarity|8}} + lays down very large webs | spawn = * [[Sewers]] * [[Garden]] | special = Has a poison damage. }}
''For its special variant, see [[Hel Spider]] or [[Mecha Spider]].''
'''Spider''' is a [[Mobs|mob]] in florr.io added on February 20, 2020. It spawns in both [[Garden]] and [[Sewers]]. ==Appearance== A small, brown circle with a dark brown outline. It has eight dark gray legs with four on each side and small gaps in the front and back. When moving, the legs move back and forth in a wave-like motion. ==Stats & Behavior== '''Speed:''' Slightly fast. '''Behavior:''' Wanders around randomly, moving a few tiles forward before stopping and changing direction. When a flower gets in range of it, it will start chasing the flower. Spiders are unaffected by [[Web|Webs]]. {{Rarity|Legendary}}+ Spiders lay down Webs while moving, slowing down players and allied mobs that go into them. {{Rarity|Super}} Spiders have an ability to lay down very large webs. They deal [[Poison Damage|Poison]] Damage when colliding with enemy entities (that is friendly entities to Flowers or Mecha Mobs). They also have a much larger detection range compared to most mobs. {| class="article-table" |+Mob Stats ![[Rarity]] !Health !Body Damage !Poison !Armor !XP Reward |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |62.5 |15 |40 (15/s) |0.8 |1 |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |234.375 |45 |120 (45/s) |2.4 |1 |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |843.75 |135 |360 (135/s) |7.2 |5 |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |3,375 |405 |1,080 (405/s) |21.6 |41 |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |25,312.5 |1,215 |3,240 (1,215/s) |64.8 |378 |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |151,875 |3,645 |9,720 (3,645/s) |194.4 |5.3k |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |1,822,500 |10,935 |29,160 (10,935/s) | rowspan="2" |583.2 |43.2k |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |82,012,500 |32,805 |87,480 (32,805/s) |4.9m |} ==Spawning== Spiders will spawn both in [[Sewers]] and [[Garden]]. Their density is high at the left middle part of Garden, below the portal to Sewers. ==Drops== ===[[Faster]]=== {{:Spider/Drop Faster}} ===[[Web]]=== {{:Spider/Drop Web}} ===[[Third Eye]]=== Third Eye drops are replaced by [[Third Eye/Magic|Magic Eye]] when [[Mana Orb]] is equipped.{{:Spider/Drop Third Eye}} ==Strategy== Spiders are slightly faster than you. Use knockback/slowing petals such as [[Pincer|Pincers]], [[Jelly|Jellies]], or [[Heavy|Heavies]] to outrun or slow a spider as they might attack you if you don't kill it quickly. Due to the new slowness update,it is advisable to carry multiple pincers if the spider overcomes their rarity. It is tested that 2 {{Rarity|Mythic}} Pincers can keep an {{Rarity|Ultra}} Spider at bay. Do not use web as they don't work on spiders. [[Bubble|Bubbles]] can be also used to outrun them. They are very poisonous, especially the higher rarities so be extremely cautious. Summoning friendly mobs are useful, as they can slow the spider by knocking it back, but they might kill your mobs before it can deal enough damage, so if your summons aren't strong enough, try using other attacks. You can also use high-knockback petals like [[Corn]], [[Jelly]], or [[Heavy]] to knock the spider away from you. Do not use [[Lotus]] to tank it as it will kill you if not careful. An effective strategy is to use ranged petals, especially in Garden, as range is effective against [[Hornet|Hornets]] as well. When it's paired with [[Roach|Roaches]], don't attack the spider using ranged petals or eggs as it might accidently hit the roaches which might be a problem. Instead, bait them away from the roach and attack them. ===Super Spider=== *The Super Spider can create giant webs and slow down all players caught in it. Use Bubbles to escape the web if you get caught in it. You can notice this is about to happen if the Spider starts to move in a circle. *To avoid accidentally touching the spider it is advised to use [[Third Eye]] to kill it. ==Gallery== File:Spider (Common).png|{{Rarity|1}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Spider (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|2}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Spider (Rare).png|{{Rarity|3}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Spider (Epic).png|{{Rarity|4}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Spider (Legendary).png|{{Rarity|5}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Spider (Mythic).png|{{Rarity|6}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Spider (Ultra).png|{{Rarity|7}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Spider (Super).png|{{Rarity|8}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Spider (Unique).png|{{Rarity|9}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Spider.png|{{PAGENAME}}. File:Spooder.png|2 {{Rarity|7}} Spiders in the {{Rarity|4}}-{{Rarity|5}} area of Sewers,which is rather unusual. ==Trivia== * There are another versions of Spider being the [[Hel Spider]] and the [[Spider/Mecha|Mecha Spider]]. *The drop rate of {{Rarity|Mythic}} [[Third Eye]] from {{Rarity|Mythic}} Spider is actually 0.025~0.026% but it's rounded to 0.03%. *Spiders used to be in Garden before, but they were removed on an unknown date, but they were re-added to Garden again on April 12, 2024. **Spiders are one of the only two mobs that is not an ant mob that can spawn in 2 areas, with the other being the [[Crab]]. *In early June 2024(?), {{Rarity|Epic}}+ spiders were changed to be faster than the player, like their {{Rarity|Rare}} and below counterparts. {{Mobs}} [[Category:Mobs]] [[Category:Non-stationary mobs]] [[Category:Spiders]] [[Category:Sewers]] [[Category:Garden]] [[Category:Poison]]

'''Hel Spider''' is a mob in florr.io. It is a [[Spider]], but much tougher and it deals more [[Poison Damage|poison]] damage over a longer period of time.

==Appearance==
The Hel Spider appears as a Spider, but it is slightly red and has the [[Hel Beetle]]'s mouth.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Fast

'''Behavior:''' Agressive at very far range

Hel Spider acts just like a regular spider, but has 2.4x the health and 2x poison damage. It also has 2.67 seconds of poison duration instead of 2 seconds like normal [[Spider]].

==Spawning==
Hel Spider spawns in [[Hel]]. It's the third most common mob there, beside [[Beetle/Hel|Hel Beetle]] and [[Wasp/Hel|Hel Wasp]].
==Drops==
===[[Dark Mark|Mark]]===
{{:Hel Spider/Drop Dark Mark}}

===[[Card]]===
{{:Hel Spider/Drop Card}}

===[[Third Eye]]===
{{:Hel Spider/Drop Third Eye}}
==Strategy==

* Same as regular [[Spider]], but try not to let [[Centipede/Hel|Centipedes]] push it towards you.
* Hel Spider's poison is extremely annoying because it can kill you in seconds. Even a {{Rarity|Mythic}} Hel Spider's poison can kill a Level 76+ player with no [[Talents/Health|Health talents]].
* To counteract this, use a bubble build with multiple [[Lotus]]<nowiki/>[[Lotus|es]], which you can put into your main build in [[Hel]] to keep you safe. Generally, it's best not to even touch them, so try using 2 or 3 [[Bubble|Bubbles]] so it can't get to you. Remember you need 2 Lotus of '''the same rarity''' as the Spider to keep the Poison to a minimum.
* Aside from that, treat them as tanky, speedy Spiders with worse droprates and different drops. [[Pincer]] is helpful against them, but they're small enough that they'll eventually make it through the area where Pincer can hit them (assuming you're using [[Talents/Reach|Reach talents]] and/or a [[Third Eye]], which you should if you're in Hel.) and, by consequence, not be affected by it and kill you.
* Due to having the same body damage as normal [[Spider]], [[Jelly]] will do its job against it.
* Be aware of other users while fighting {{Rarity|Super}} Hel Spider, as they may bring summoned {{Rarity|Ultra}} mobs or {{Rarity|Ultra}} [[Uranium]], which may kill you in seconds. You can use [[Jelly]] to knock back the summons as they have little mass, but Uranium can be a threat if you don't bring enough Lotuses or you turn off Hide other petals in the game settings.

==Gallery==
<gallery>
File:Hel Spider (Common).png|{{Rarity|1}} Hel Spider.
File:Hel Spider (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|2}} Hel Spider.
File:Hel Spider (Rare).png|{{Rarity|3}} Hel Spider.

File:Hel Spider (Unique).png|{{Rarity|9}} Hel Spider.
File:Hel Spider.png|Hel Spider.
File:Hel spider 1.jpg|A Hel Spider being attacked.
</gallery>

==Trivia==
* Hel Spider does not drop [[Web]]s, despite laying them down.
* Hel Spider now moves even faster from its [[Spider|normal counterpart]]. Its speed is even faster than [[Hel Beetle]]'s speed.
* Somewhere during mid-2024, the Hel Spider's [[Dice]] drop was replaced with [[Card]].
* This, [[Gambler]], [[Beetle/Hel|Hel Beetle]], [[Centipede/Hel|Hel Centipede]] and [[Wasp/Hel|Hel Wasp]] are the only mobs to spawn in [[Hel]].
* Hel Spider is the toughest mobs to loot in [[Hel]] beside [[Gambler]], because it has high speed, tough poison damage, has a large aggro range, and the current density in [[Hel]].

''This is about the Mecha variant of the Spider. For the regular version, see [[Spider]].''
'''Mecha Spider''' is a mob from florr.io. Instead of dealing poison damage, they deal lightning damage. == Appearance == '''Mecha Spider''' has a similar body to a [[Spider]], but has a gray metallic body and there's a small red circle in the center of the body. Its legs now have straight segments and angles instead of the curved legs of the regular Spider. They spawn in [[Factory]] and [[Garden]] in front of Factory’s entrance. ==Stats & Behavior== '''Speed:''' Same as [[Spider]]. '''Behavior:''' Aggressive, like Normal [[Spider]]. {{R|Legendary}}+ Spiders leave a trail of [[Electric Web|Electric Webs]]behind them. Instead of dealing poison damage and physical damage, they deal lightning damage. {| class="article-table" |+Mob Stats ![[Rarity]] !Health !Body Damage (lightning) !Armor !XP Reward |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |62.5 |30 |3 | rowspan="2" |1 |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |234.375 |90 |8 |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |843.75 |270 |25 |3 |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |3,375 |810 |76 |28 |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |25,312.5 |2,430 |227 |256 |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |151,875 |7,290 |680 |6.7k |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |1,822,500 |21,870 |2,041 |93.9k |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |82,012,500 |65,610 |4,957 |10.3m |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} |1,230,187,500 |196,830 |13,705 |103m |} At {{R|Super}} rarity, Mecha Spiders can spin and lay a very large [[Electric Web]], similar to normal [[Spider|Spiders]] and [[Hel Spider|Hel Spiders]]. Unlike other Spider variants, the Web will not just slow down the flowers, but it will also deal immense lightning damage. This can make the fight much longer as flowers have to stay away from the web. Summoned mobs are harder to use as they could die from the large web before touching the Mecha Spider. == Drops == === [[Battery]] === {{:Firefly/Drop Battery}} ===[[Fragment]]=== {| class="article-table" !Mob Rarity !{{Rarity|Mythic}} !{{Rarity|Ultra}} |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}}''' |3.1% | |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}}''' |46.0% |0.2% |- |{{Rarity|Super}}''' | rowspan="2" | 35.6% | rowspan="2" | 64.4% |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} (10x) |} === [[Electric Web]] === {| class="article-table" !Mob Rarity !{{Rarity|Rare}} !{{Rarity|Epic}} !{{Rarity|Legendary}} !{{Rarity|Mythic}} !{{Rarity|Ultra}} |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |16.5% | | | | |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |81.0% |12.3% | | | |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |7.2% |87.7% |5.0% | | |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} | |7.5% |91.7% |0.5% | |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} | | |85.6% |14.3% |0.05% |- |{{Rarity|Super}} | | | | rowspan="2" |77.2% | rowspan="2" |22.8% |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} (10x) | | | |} == Spawning == Mecha Spiders spawns in [[Factory]] very frequently. == Strategies == * [[Rubber]] is a good petal for against Mecha Spiders, as it can absorb its high body damage. This is even better when used in tandem with [[Sponge]]. * Since Mecha Spider is the same as the normal version in many ways, a lot of the strategies that apply to normal [[Spider]] also apply to Mecha Spider. However, there are a couple differences which need new strategies. ** Mecha Spiders should not be rammed. They have the same body damage as [[Leafbug]] and [[Beetle]], which is the same as that of a regular [[Soldier Ant]] of the next rarity. Therefore, you will take a ton of damage per hit and die much faster. On top of that, this damage is lightning damage, which bypasses armor. This makes [[Root]] have no effect. ** Mecha Spiders have an armor of 3 at {{Rarity|Common}} rarity compared to 1 like regular [[Spider]]. Additionally, this armor value is multiplied by 3 per rarity, more than most other mobs. This makes them harder to kill and makes deathwheels less efficient. === Super Mecha Spider === * The Super mob itself is not as dangerous, but the Ultra mobs and the opposite team around the mob that is a threat. The large web it spins makes some people flee, and possibly luring more Ultra mobs. When bubbling to the mob, it is advised to use Sponge and multiple Ultra healing petals, and have Rubber equipped in case a Spider hits you or a Crab It is not advised to use sponge when actually fighting the mob, because the lightnight damage from the mob and other mobs will just go too high and the healing cannot keep up. * If the mob spawns deep in Factory, it is advised to form a "megasquad", where a large group of players stay near each other and clear mobs. This increases the chance for everyone to survive. * In most cases, players will lure Super Mecha SPider to slightly easier areas because they are nearly impossible to kill while surrounded by Ultra mobs. This means that you do not need to rush into the ultra zone area to attack it. * A good way to kill it is rather to use Rubber and a sponge and burst damage petals to deal large amount of damage and then retreating to heal. Also be aware that the other team could use lightning damage or uranium to kill the mob. Their damage will affect you. == Gallery == File:Mecha Spider (Common).png|{{Rarity|1}} Mecha Spider. File:Mecha Spider (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|2}} Mecha Spider. File:Mecha Spider (Rare).png|{{Rarity|3}} Mecha Spider. File:Mecha Spider (Epic).png|{{Rarity|4}} Mecha Spider. File:Mecha Spider (Legendary).png|{{Rarity|5}} Mecha Spider. File:Mecha Spider (Mythic).png|{{Rarity|6}} Mecha Spider. File:Mecha Spider (Ultra).png|{{Rarity|7}} Mecha Spider. File:Mecha Spider (Super).png|{{Rarity|8}} Mecha Spider. File:Mecha Spider (Unique).png|{{Rarity|9}} Mecha Spider. File:Mecha Spider.png|Mecha Spider. File:UniqueMechaSpider.jpeg File:Capture1.png|A forgotten Super Mecha Spider in Factory. == Trivia == * Unlike normal Spider, Mecha Spiders don't deal poison damage upon collision. ** Instead, Mecha Spiders deal '''Lightning''' damage, making [[Rubber]] a better pick than [[Lotus]]. *On September 13, 2025, the [[Web]] drop has been replaced with the newly added [[Electric Web]]. *On September 14, 2025, shortly after Electric Web's release, Developer M28 spawned in 3 {{R|Unique}} Mecha Spiders which despawned due to closing servers. M28 spawned in another 3 Spiders hours after. The fight itself was pretty long due to its large webs dealing huge lightning damage. *Mecha Spiders webs now are the same color as Electric Web being shooted from flowers, instantly change from blue to yellow and so on. *Mecha Spider back then was kind of useless as they used to drop only Batteries and Fragments. {{Mobs}} [[Category:Mobs]] [[Category:Spiders]] [[Category:Mecha Mobs]] [[Category:Battle Ground]] [[Category:Factory]] [[Category:Garden]] [[Category:Lightning]]

title: Sponge
content:
{{Petal Template Infobox | image = File:Sponge (Unusual).png|U File:Sponge (Rare).png|R File:Sponge (Epic).png|E File:Sponge (Legendary).png|L File:Sponge (Mythic).png|M File:Sponge (Ultra).png|Ul File:Sponge (Super).png|S File:Sponge (Unique).png|Uq | description = Spreads damage taken by the flower over a period of time.
If destroyed, will deal remaining damage all at once.
Does not delay poison damage. | first-rarity = {{Rarity|Unusual}} | period = {{Infobox Colors Empty||6s|9s|12s|15s|18s|21s|24s|27s}} | reload-time = 2.5s | stackability = Unstackable | dropped-by = *[[Sponge (Mob)|Sponge]] | special = Deals received damage over time }} ''This page is about the petal. For the mob, see [[Sponge (Mob)]].'' '''Sponge''' is a petal in florr.io. It absorbs damage and returns it to the flower slowly over time. It does not stack, and set player max health to 50% of it. It hovers over mobs instead of colliding with them, rendering it invincible. ==Appearance== Similar to a cartoon style puff of smoke, but the inside is cream colored and the outline of it is much darker. ==Function== {|class="article-table" |+Petal Stats ![[Rarity]] !Obtainable? !Respawn !Absorb Period (seconds) !Flower Health |- |{{R|Common}} |No |rowspan="9"|2.5 seconds |3 |rowspan="9"|-50% |- |{{R|Unusual}} |rowspan="8"|Yes |6 |- |{{R|Rare}} |9 |- |{{R|Epic}} |12 |- |{{R|Legendary}} |15 |- |{{R|Mythic}} |18 |- |{{R|Ultra}} |21 |- |{{R|Super}} |24 |- |{{R|Unique}} |27 |} Absorbs all of the damage taken by the flower and then slowly returns the absorbed damage to the flower. For example, a flower gets hit by an Ultra Stinger: Instead of taking the huge amount of damage at once, the flower takes damage slowly (similar to an [[Iris]]'s poison effect), until the flower dies or the absorption period runs out. If the petal is switched out before that, all remaining damage it has absorbed is immediately dealt back to the flower. Sponge is a somewhat difficult petal to grind, due to there being no frequent mythic sponge spawn area. However, after a update that adds all ultras, ultra sponge can spawn. ==Obtaining== ===[[Sponge (Mob)]]=== {{:Sponge (Mob)/Drop Sponge}} ===Purchasing=== On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]]. ''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]'' {{Petal Price|4}} ==Strategy== It's best when combined with healing petals, as you will still take all the damage the sponge absorbs. If you use powerful, consistent heals, like Starfish, you will be pretty much invincible. These heals must be combined with a basil or more to increase the healing. Sponge can be used with second chance to survive ramming high damaging mobs multiple times. ram the mob firstas much as you can, then take off the sponge before second chance invulnerability runs out to change to a healing build. DO NOT use sponge with uranium, as it will have little effect on the self-damage of the uranium but deal massive damage to the user when taken off. To maximize mythic starfish healing (calculated): * 4 basil * 6 starfish Whenever you feel you are about to die use sponge, even though sponge got nerfed it can prevent you from getting instantly killed. Even though sponge got nerfed, the current ultra sponge is really good(as strong as pre nerf mythic sponge) . You can lure an enemy thats hard to defeat over to a portal, equip sponge, and when you start taking massive amounts of damage, go into the portal to negate damage, heal up on the other side, and fight the enemy again. Sponge goes extremely well with cotton, as it would still absorb the damage sponge delays. 2 cotton of the same rarity makes the flower hard to kill, especially with reload talent. However, it does have a limit on how much damage it can absorb ===[[Garden]]=== * While using [[Rose|Roses]] + [[Leaf|Leaves]], you can take damage when the sponge absorbs. Best with weaker [[Hornet|hornets]]. * Do not equip weaker sponges while with weaker healing to stronger mobs. as they will kill you in no time. * Petals with consistent, HP/s healing (e.g. [[Leaf]], [[Yucca]], [[Starfish]]) are best when paired with Sponge since its damage is spread out as DPS similar to an [[Iris|Iris's]] poison effect. ===[[Desert]]=== * Same as Garden, Sponge is best when combined with healing petals. ===[[Ocean]]=== * Sponge is okay against most of the [[Ocean]] mobs. The damage of the [[Crab|Crabs]] could cause problems without any good healing with this petals. ** Sponge is actually quite good against [[Jellyfish]] since, while its attacks cannot be dodged, it deals lower damage that can easily be outhealed with the help of this petal. * Never use against Super [[Jellyfish]] as its new shockwave attack will oneshot the sponge (and all other petals) making it useless. (The absorbed damage will be dealt to you immediately) * Paired with Second Chance, when the player realises that the damage would cause eventual death even with the Sponge, it can be switched out. All damage will instantly be taken in, activating the talent and saves the player from any additional damage after the duration of Second Chance. === Ant Hell === * Don’t use it against Super [[Termite Overmind]] because it can randomly disable your sponge, dealing all the damage it has back to you. ===[[PvP]]=== ====Using==== * Pair it with other healing petals to out heal pretty much everything. You can use it to survive Ultra [[Stinger|Stingers]]. ====Against==== * Sponge cannot delay poison damage, so use [[Iris]] to kill Sponge users easily. * [[Claw]] can eradicate sponge users (very useful), if victim is consistently over 80% health. A bunch of [[Faster|Fasters]], [[Claw|Claws]], and perhaps a [[Third Eye]]plus some healing will do a great job in pvp. * [[Dice]]'s critical hit will ignore the Sponge. They will receive the full damage output, rendering Sponge useless against it. * [[Moon]] paired with [[Bubble]]s can also take out a Sponge user, due to it's extremely high health. It can bubble towards that player and constantly keep ramming them, accelerating the damage they take, and eventually killing them. Sponge is the solution to salt users. Sponge tank cannot be killed; 3 starfish 3 basil 2 rubber 2 sponge with mythic petals will never die to mythic petals. This is actually not true prior to the massive salt nerf. == Gallery== File:Sponge (Common).png|{{R|1}} Sponge. (unobtainable) File:Sponge (Unusual).png|{{R|2}} Sponge. File:Sponge (Rare).png|{{R|3}} Sponge. File:Sponge (Epic).png|{{R|4}} Sponge. File:Sponge (Legendary).png|{{R|5}} Sponge. File:Sponge (Mythic).png|{{R|6}} Sponge. File:Sponge (Ultra).png|{{R|7}} Sponge. File:Sponge (Super).png|{{R|8}} Sponge. File:Sponge (Unique).png|{{R|9}} Sponge. File:Sponge.png|Sponge. ==Trivia== *When you get hit by lightning while using Rubber and Sponge, rubber will also take damage overtime, making the flower's health base immune to damage. * After the update in January 12, 2024, this petal doesn't have the stats of its durability, meaning that this petal is unbreakable. ** After the changes, [[M28]] didn't even bother to remove the second line of the description, making new players more confused. *** This is actually because the Sponge can still be destroyed by unequipping it. * Sponge is one of several petals that drops from a mob of the same name. The others are Square, Bubble, Cactus, Dandelion, Rock, Shell, Starfish and Ant Egg. * At some point, people thought that sponges were useless, but they are not. They are useful for healing. If paired with Basils and Leaf/Yucca, then you're almost pretty much invincible (unless you ram into mobs with super high damage such as super cactus, for some reason) *On August 27, 2024, Sponge was nerfed so that it no longer absorbs/delays Poison damage, probably to give more usage to [[Lotus]]. *Around early 2026, Sponge was given the ability to cut players health in half, causing more risk while using this petal. {{Petals}} [[zh:海绵]] [[Category:Petals]] [[Category:Unstackable Petals]] [[Category:Ocean]] [[Category:Droppable Petals]] [[Category:Buffs]] [[Category:Sponge]]

title: Sponge (Mob)
content:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Sponge}}{{Background|图片=Oceanb.png|大小=2000px}}{{Mob Template Infobox | name = {{PAGENAME}} | image = File:Sponge (Mob) (Common).png|C File:Sponge (Mob) (Unusual).png|U File:Sponge (Mob) (Rare).png|R File:Sponge (Mob) (Epic).png|E File:Sponge (Mob) (Legendary).png|L File:Sponge (Mob) (Mythic).png|M File:Sponge (Mob) (Ultra).png|Ul File:Sponge (Mob) (Super).png|S File:Sponge (Mob) (Unique).png|Uq | description = Bob. | drops = * [[Sponge]] * [[Coral]] | health = {{Mob HP Scale|100}} | damage = {{Mob Damage Scale|10}} | armor = {{Mob Armor Scale|0.8|0}} | xp-reward = {{Infobox Colors Empty|1|1|5|33|297|4.2k|31.1k|3.4m}} | move = Stationary | status = Passive | attack = Body Damage | spawn = * [[Ocean]] | special = }}
''Not to be confused with the petal, [[Sponge]].''
The '''Sponge''' is a mob in florr.io that spawns in [[Ocean]]. It is a stationary mob. ==Appearance== A larger cartoonish smoke with circles grouped together with a pentagon shape. The edge is dark cream and the fill is cream. Sometimes, the mob can be colored slightly purple. == Stats & Behavior== '''Speed: '''N/A '''Behavior:''' N/A {| class="article-table" |+Mob Stats ![[Rarity]] !Health !Body Damage !Armor !XP Reward |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |100 |10 |0.8 |1 |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |375 |30 |2.4 |1 |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |1,350 |90 |7.2 |5 |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |5,400 |270 |21.6 |33 |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |40,500 |810 |64.8 |297 |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |243,000 |2,430 |194.4 |4.2k |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |2,916,000 |7,290 | rowspan="2" |583.2 |31.1k |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |131,220,000 |21,870 |3.4m |} ==Spawning== Sponge spawn much everywhere in Ocean, most of them spawn in the corridor at middle bottom of the map. ==Drops== ===[[Sponge]]=== {{:Sponge (Mob)/Drop Sponge}} ===[[Coral]]=== {{:Sponge (Mob)/Drop Coral}} == Strategy== *Killing Sponges are very easy due to its quite low health, but it has some risks while trying to kill this. **The {{Rarity|6}} variants can be big to spawn in the narrow part, causing serious vibrations and can be fatal if you do not move away or use petals that rely on knockback. **Also, {{Rarity|Ultra}}+ Sponges have enough health to be annoying to kill, allowing other mobs plenty of time to kill you while fighting the Sponge. *This is used to escape hostile mobs from killing you, but sometimes they may probably escape from the mob. *{{Rarity|Ultra}} or {{Rarity|Super}} [[Bone]] is an important petal to clear multiple Sponges quickly, due to Sponges having low base damage and Bone having good armor. === {{Rarity|Super}} Sponge === * The Super mob itself isn't as dangerous, but the Ultra mobs around it are a threat. When bubbling to the mob, it is advise to use sponge and multiple healing petals so that if anything hits you, you don't die. * If spawns deep in ocean, it is advised to form a "megasquad" so that mobs can be cleared. It increases the chances of everyone surviving. * When killing the Sponge, it is advised to use sponge so that if a jellyfish deals lightning damage or a Crab or leech randomly hits you, you won't die and be able to loot. * Use a high DPS build to secure loot. ==Gallery== File:Sponge (Mob) (Common).png|{{Rarity|1}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Sponge (Mob) (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|2}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Sponge (Mob) (Rare).png|{{Rarity|3}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Sponge (Mob) (Epic).png|{{Rarity|4}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Sponge (Mob) (Legendary).png|{{Rarity|5}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Sponge (Mob) (Mythic).png|{{Rarity|6}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Sponge (Mob) (Ultra).png|{{Rarity|7}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Sponge (Mob) (Super).png|{{Rarity|8}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Sponge (Mob) (Unique).png|{{Rarity|9}} {{PAGENAME}}. File:Sponge (Mob).png|{{PAGENAME}}. File:Common Sponge ingame (10).png|A common sponge and common bubble with flower in game ==Trivia== *Sponge's description is "Bob" and combined with Sponge, going into the first order will make a reference to SpongeBob. *This mob used to be the only [[Ocean]] mob that did not spawn as an [[Ultra Mobs and Super Mobs|{{Rarity|7}}]], but as of the [[Changelog#January 5th 2024|January 5th 2024]] update, any naturally spawning mob can spawn as an {{Rarity|7}}. *Sponge is one of the mobs that has its exclusive drops, currently are [[Sponge]] and [[Coral]]. *The small tunnel leading up to Crab Kingdom spawns mainly {{Rarity|Legendary}} and {{Rarity|Mythic}} Sponges. {{Mobs}} [[Category:Mobs]] [[Category:Stationary mobs]] [[Category:Ocean]] [[Category:Sponge]]

:''This page is about the petal. For the mob, see [[Square (Mob)]].

'''Square''' is a [[petal]] in [https://florr.io florr.io]. It spawns a [[Square (Mob)|Square mob]] of the rarity equal to the rarity of the petal. Square is dropped by Square mobs.

==Appearance==
Square looks like a yellow square with a darker yellow outline, which resembles the square from [https://diep.io diep.io], another game previously owned by florr.io’s developer, [[M28]]. {{Rarity|Super}} and {{Rarity|Unique}} Squares are green in color with darker outlines, resembling the shiny squares from diep.io.

The petal shown in inventory and hotbar is identical to the Square Mob that is spawned. This differs from [[Mysterious Stick]], [[Beetle Egg]] and [[Ant Egg]], which show their spawner (i.e. the egg or stick) rather than the mob that they spawn.

==Function==
{| class="article-table"
|+Petal Stats
![[Rarity]]
!Obtainable?
!Respawn
!Spawns
|-
|{{Rarity|Common}}
| rowspan="10" |Yes

Square spawns a Square mob the same rarity as the petal. It takes 60 seconds to respawn at {{Rarity|Common}} to {{Rarity|Mythic}} rarities, making the petal useless for any practical use. The mob spawns once the petal has finished its cooldown phase. Unlike [[Ant Egg]] and [[Beetle Egg]], there is no petal phase in between of cooldown phase and mob phase. When in cooldown phase, Square takes up space between petals, though during mob phase, it does not. 

Like the square in diep.io, the Square mob slowly drifts and spins. It randomly changes its movement direction when it collides with a wall, like [[Digger]]. It cannot be attracted by [[Uranium]] or [[Honey]] and damaged by other flowers or their petals (except their summons if they are not in a squad with the player that spawned the Square mob) unless they equip [[Corruption]]. However, it can be destroyed by other players in [[Hel]].

The mob drops nothing if it was spawned by a player.

==Obtaining==
===[[Square (Mob)]]===
{{:Square (Mob)/Drop Square}}
Only {{Rarity|Common}} Square can be dropped. {{Rarity|Unusual}}+ Squares have to be [[Crafting|crafted]].

{{Rarity|Common}} Squares used to be given as a reward to Discord users who reported a valid bug in florr.io. Players could use the bug report channel to report a previously unreported bug. If the bug was valid and considered major enough, they would be rewarded with a {{Rarity|Common}} Square.

Before the update that made {{Rarity|Ultra}} petals permanent, players who had {{Rarity|Ultra}} [[Basic]]s were given a {{Rarity|Common}} Square in exchange for each {{Rarity|Ultra}} Basic, as the reward for reporting bugs used to be {{Rarity|Ultra}} Basics until it changed to {{Rarity|Common}} Squares. This resulted in kt6543, the winner of the PvP background contest, having 50 {{Rarity|Common}} Squares, which he then crafted into what would be the first {{Rarity|Rare}} Square.

Squares can spawn any biome, so it is recommended to farm high density biomes such as sewers in order to see squares more often

===Purchasing===
Unlike other petals, Square is not available for purchase in the [[Shop]].

==Strategy==

* This petal is effectively useless unless you use [[Mimic]] to get a high rarity Square.
* You can also use it for several player interactions. Players are often attracted to these, which provides a distraction in PvP or as a trolling technique in PvE modes.

==Talents==

* The petal may have an accelerated recharge rate
* The petal can be split (using the super duplicator talent, and it is located on the left side of the inventory)
* A square can gain more health from the summoner talent

==Gallery==
<gallery>

==Screenshots==
<gallery>
File:A player pushing a common square.jpg|A player using Ants to push the Square.
File:0362B8A4-99EA-4BE5-AE4E-DE2A3D5B0889.webp|The dead flower on the death screen. Note that the petal is still shown as it was before it was changed to a summoning petal.
File:ADF649EE-280F-4B7E-AF65-4F91F0BDB3BD.webp|The developer’s (the flower with 8 {{Rarity|Mythic}} Basics and Antennae) {{Rarity|Mythic}} Square.

File:2 square.png|2 {{Rarity|Common}} Squares.
File:Csquare.png|An event was made by [[M28]] where he spawned {{Rarity|Common}} Squares and gave a Square petal to each player. It goes away when you equip a petal, or unequip the Square.
File:Screenshot (347).png|{{Rarity|Common}} Square in its reloading state with UI shown. It takes 42.4s for {{Rarity|Common}} Square to spawn as seen here with upgrades.
File:65.png|Player using {{color|#ffe869|Square}} petal. (legacy)
File:Square Start Page.png|Notice the square icon on the start page on the right of the screen (Credits to Pro_Gold)
File:Screenshot 2025-10-20 12.01.25 PM.png|{{R|2}} Square craft.
</gallery>

==Trivia ==  
*This petal is an obvious reference to [https://diep.io diep.io]'s square.
** The {{Rarity|Super}}, {{R|9b}} and {{Rarity|Unique}} variants of this petal are a reference to diep.io's shiny square. Diep.io shiny polygons are extremely rare and are highly valuable, explaining why this trait exists for {{Rarity|Super}}, {{R|9b}} and {{Rarity|Unique}} variants only.
* This petal used to be like [[Basic]] in function and strength.
* Unique [[Compass]] does not point to summoned Squares.
*This, [[Basic]], [[Poker Chip|Chip]], and [[Coin]] are the only petals that never appear in the [[Shop]].
**In Square's case, it is because it would make Square easier to obtain, thus finding the naturally-spawned Square mob very time consuming and also impractical.
*In [https://static.florr.io/old/ legacy florr.io], the petal was one of the two {{Rarity|Old6}} Petals, with the other being [https://florrio.fandom.com/wiki/Basic_(old) Basic]. 
**{{Rarity|Old6}} Square mob used to drop {{Rarity|Old6}} Square at 100% chance if there were no other Square petals on the server. Square mob used to not have drops for a long time. Now Square mob has drops again.
*Before {{Rarity|8}} mobs were added, {{Rarity|8}} square was confirmed to spawn an {{Rarity|7}} Square.
*This is the second petal added to spawn a mob, with the other ones being (in order) [[Beetle Egg]], [[Stick]], [[Ant Egg]], and [[Mysterious Stick/Magic|Magic Stick]]. 
**Square, Stick, and Magic Stick are the only summoning petals that are not eggs.
*Square has the same reload as the [[Moon Rock]] unless it is of {{Rarity|7}}+ rarities.
*This petal can only be obtained if you find a natural [[Square (Mob)|Square]] in the wild and kill it. Anyone else's square (which is peach in color) will not drop the petal.
*Unlike other petals, oracling Squares only needs 5 Squares of the same rarity, likely due to its rareness (cannot be bought from shop and are dropped from [[Square (Mob)|the rarest mob in the game]].
*It is the only petal to have a 100% craft chance for all rarities.
**Multiple people are having Epic Squares (like Mnesia and Corrupt0), with Missile having 4 {{Rarity|Epic}} Squares making him the closest to legendary square so far. However the amount of squares Manfred has is unknown and he could have a large amount of Squares but he simply didn't craft them before he quit.
***Any "{{Rarity|Ultra}}+ Squares" are [[Mimic]]s, ultra squares themselves cannot exist yet.
**Each rarity of the Square Petal requires this amount of {{Rarity|Common}} Squares at '''minimum''':
***{{Rarity|Unusual}}: 5.
***{{Rarity|Rare}}: 25.
***{{Rarity|Epic}}: 125.
***{{Rarity|Legendary}}: 625.
***{{Rarity|Mythic}}: 3,125.
***{{Rarity|Ultra}}: 15,625.
***{{Rarity|Super}}: 78,125.
***{{Rarity|Eternal}}/{{Rarity|Unique}}: 390,625.
****{{Rarity|Eternal}}/{{Rarity|Unique}} Square is the most tedious petal to get, given the absurd amount of {{Rarity|Common}} Squares it requires.

:''Not to be confused with the petal, [[Square]].

The '''Square''' is an original [[Mobs|mob]] from florr.io that has a 0.001% (1/100,000) chance of spawning instead of any another mob. It has a 100% chance to drop a {{Rarity|Common}} Square upon being killed, and you will get the {{R|7}} achievement "[[Achievements#Wrong_game|Wrong game]]."

==Appearance==
The Square resembles the [[w:c:diepio:Square|Square]] from diep.io, consisting of a yellow square with a darker yellow outline. At {{Rarity|8}} rarity, the square is colored lime green (#32CD32) like a shiny square, which is a rare polygon in diep.io.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Very slow

'''Behavior:''' Like the Square in diep.io, it slowly drifts and spins. It randomly changes its movement direction when it collides with a wall. It is passive to players and mobs.

It cannot be attracted by [[Uranium]] or [[Honey]]. Summoned Squares cannot be harmed by other flowers. It can be destroyed by other players in PvP. Summoned Squares can be killed by other summoned [[mobs]]. 

If spawned by a player, it will have a slightly darkened peach tone, just like in diep.io.

==Spawning==
The Square mob has 0.001% (1/100000) to replace a spawning mob.

==Drops==
The Square mob drops the [[Square|Square petal]], but only at the {{Rarity|Common}} rarity.

===[[Square]]===
{{:Square (Mob)/Drop Square}}

==Strategy==
* This mob is extremely rare to find. Use [[Uranium]] to secure kill credit for {{Rarity|Legendary}}- rarities.
* You can't really hunt for these. Most people find them by just wandering around and spotting one randomly.
* The best area to farm for Square is the in the Cactus Zone in desert, which is right above spawn. Here, the mob density is very high and bubbling around with Uranium is the best way to get Squares to spawn faster. Keep in mind that the rate is still only around one Square per two hours. The pathway to [[Pyramid]] is even faster, so it is recommended to farm both at once, or with a squad.
* {{Rarity|9}} [[Compass]] can point at Squares, making it easier to find a Square if one has spawned.

==Trivia==
*This mob is an obvious reference to [https://diep.io diep.io]'s square.
**The {{Rarity|Super}} Square variant is also a reference to the Diep.io's Shiny Square, since this variant is extremely rare.
**In diep.io, the square is the most common polygon, but however in florr.io, it is the rarest mob in the game. This can be debated to have its rarity opposite to each other.
**It's stated that Squares do not belong to florr.io, as in the Unique Compass description.
*This is one of the two only mob that gives a single XP in every rarity, with the other being [[Ghost]].
*On April 2024, Square was temporarily removed from the game due to the squad and map update.
*On an unknown update, {{Rarity|Super}} Squares no longer have a spawn message, or kill message. It was later reverted in late November 2025.  {{Rarity|Ultra}} and {{Rarity|Super}} [[Compass]] do not point to {{Rarity|Super}} Squares, but {{Rarity|Unique}} Compasses do.
*It's the only mob that always has a 100% chance to drop a specific type of petal, that being the [[Square]].
*On December 12th, 2023, [[M28]] spawned a {{Rarity|Super}} Square Mob in [[Ellne Jungle/Ellne Pass|Ellne Pass]], the mob appears as green instead of yellow and chases the player.
*Since the [[Square|Square petal]] spawns a Square of the same Rarity, a {{Rarity|Super}} Square could theoretically be spawned. However, it would take 10 minutes to load it, assuming without [[Talents/Reload|Reload talent]] or [[Golden Leaf]].
*Skware is the first player to get a {{Rarity|Rare}} Square. 
**There are notable a few people who have obtained the {{Rarity|Epic}} Square. 
*Most {{Rarity|Ultra}} player-spawned Squares are just Mimics.
*This is the only non-[[Gambler]] mob that can be targeted by [[Hel]] mobs.
*This makes Hel a bad location to farm for Squres, as the mobs there will kill it in a matter of seconds.

:''This page is about the petal. For the mob, see [[Starfish (Mob)]].''

'''Starfish''' is a petal in florr.io. It heals if the user is below 75% health.

==Appearance==
The Starfish petal is long and fairly skinny. It tapers slightly toward the top of the petal. The tip is rounded. The petal is red with a dark red outline and three pale red spots in the middle of the petal.

==Function==

The Starfish heals the user at a constant rate, but only when the user is below 75% HP. This effect applies even when the petal is in cooldown.

==Obtaining==
===[[Starfish (Mob)]]===
{{:Starfish (Mob)/Drop Starfish}}

===Purchasing===
On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|3}}

==Strategy==
It is good against many mobs, because it's heals so fast but only when the flower has 75% HP or below.

===[[Garden]]===
* Keep in mind that pairing petals like Sponge, Salt and Basil makes saltbombing even more greater, using Sponge or Cotton would absorb the damage, salt would reflect the damage from the mob and basil increase the healing so it becomes so speedy
* You can use this petal to quickly protect you from tanky mobs like Spiders, Hornets.

===[[Desert]]===
* It's a little bit recommended in here, because Beetles are strong and use some petals to attack it.
** Be careful with the Scorpions, because they shoot out poisonoius triangles.

===[[Ocean]]===
* Treat it like being a Starfish if you are using this petal.

===[[PvP]]===
====Using====
* Starfish heals at a very fast rate but only with 75% health or lower. Combined with [[bubble]], a quick escape can be made to recover and get back into the fight faster than you would with [[Yucca]] or [[Leaf]].
* It can also be used to avoid [[Claw]]'s damage gimmick, as it only does increased damage if you're 80% and the Starfish can only heal up to 75%.

====Against====
* [[Dandelion]] can nullify the healing from this.
* High burst damage petals such as [[Stinger]], [[Blood Stinger]] and [[Dice]] can be used to kill the enemy before they get the chance to heal

==Gallery==
<gallery>

</gallery>

==Trivia==
* The Starfish is one of three petals to heal the user at a constant rate. The others are [[Leaf]] and [[Yucca]].
* Starfish is one of several petals that drop from a mob of the same name. The others are [[Bubble]], Cactus, Dandelion, Rock, Shell, Sponge, and Square.

''Not to be confused with the petal, [[Starfish]].'' 

The '''Starfish''' is a [[Mobs|mob]] from florr.io. The Starfish is unique, as it is one of the only enemies that have a defensive state, the other being the [[Moth]]. 
==Appearance==
The Starfish has a similar look to its real-life counterpart. It is star-shaped, which means it has 5 limbs, and each limb has 3 little dots on it, summing up to 15 dots. When it takes a certain amount of damage, it will starts to lost some of its limbs. However, the limbs are regenerated when the Starfish is on low HP and is regenerating itself.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Same as the flower's base speed.

'''Behavior:'''
*Starfish rotates and moves around the map, until a target is in sight. 
**When a target is found, the Starfish locks in, rotating faster and moving at a more moderate speed as they slam into players.
*A unique element to Starfish is that each time they lose a certain amount of health, they lose a limb.  
**When they lose a certain amount of limbs, or lose 50% of their max health, Starfish flee, and run away. 
***This defense mechanism also lets the Starfish regenerate health, which allows them to re-enter combat.
***Starfish heal maximum 2.5 seconds.
***If they lose all their health regeneration from [[Dandelion|dandelions]], they will fight back regardless of their remaining health.

==Spawning==
The Starfish spawns everywhere in [[Ocean]]. It spawns the most in the top right corner.

==Drops==

===[[Sand]]===
{{:Starfish (Mob)/Drop Sand}}

=== [[Salt]]===
{{:Starfish (Mob)/Drop Salt}}

=== [[Starfish]]===
{{:Starfish (Mob)/Drop Starfish}}

==Strategy==
The starfish can heal in its defensive state. However, if one gets hit by a dandelion, the starfish would go back to its attacking state as it can't heal. Thus, it's easier to kill Ultra+ Starfishes this way (Mythic Starfishes if you don't have enough damage/burst). If no one brought at least one dandelion, players can try to use death wheel builds to kill the starfish more quickly and efficiently. Stingers and other high damage petals are also very effective as they can deal high amounts of damage in an instant.

When you're surrounded with multiple Starfishes around you (specifically Mythic Starfishes), don't use Dandelion to kill them, as they may go towards you and get squashed with the other Starfishes around you while damaging them. Instead, kill them without Dandelion and also with high damage petals, because they would heal and retreat themself one by one, so we can escape from the surrounding of the Starfish crowd easily.

The starfish is more easily killed at a corner of the map where it will not aggro. Third Eye is needed for Dandelions to hit it from afar.

=== {{Rarity|Super}} Starfish ===

* The Super mob itself isn't much as a threat as the {{Rarity|Ultra}} mobs around it. When bubbling to the mob, it is advised to use sponge so that if something hits you, you don't immediately die.
* If the mob spawns deep in ocean, it is advised to form a "megasquad" where people group together to clear mobs. It increases everyone's chances of surviving.
* It is also advised to lure the mob into easier or cleared areas so that other mobs won't be a distraction.
* Since it has a normal and not fast speed, you could attack if from any side. If you attack it from the front, use a pincer or something so it doesn't hit you on accident. You could use [[Third Eye]]. If you attack it from the side, beware that it could change aggro anytime and kill you. If you attack it from behind, beware that it could comeback to heal and you could collide into it.
* Make sure that when it almost reaches half health, there is at least one person using dandelion. This prevents the mob from healing and wasting your time.
* Use a high DPS build to secure loot.

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185">
File:Starfish (Mob) (Common).png|{{Rarity|1}} {{PAGENAME}}.

File:StarfishZone1.png|An image of the Starfish zone, located by the Jellyfish zone in [[Ocean]].
File:IMG 2341.webp|Game is perfect, no bugs.
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*In real life, Starfish could actually heal/regenerate their wounds if they lost one of their arms. However, the result were sometimes not perfect and the process time is far longer than in the game.
*One the May 18, 2024 update, Starfish's health was nerfed by 3x.
*The Starfish's description is a reference to a character called Patrick, from the show ''Spongebob SquarePants.''
*The Starfish along with [[Desert Centipede]]s are the only mobs that can drop [[Salt]], but even though Desert Centipedes have a higher drop rate for Salt and more segments than Starfish, Starfish are much better to farm Salt as they have a much higher {{Rarity|6}} spawn rate.
**It is the only mob to drop Salt in the [[Ocean]] scenario.

:''This page is about the normal Stinger. For the [[magic petal|magic]] and [[Blood Stinger|blood]] variant, see [[/Magic/|Magic Stinger]] and [[Blood Stinger]].''

'''Stinger''' is a legacy petal in florr.io. It has high damage, but low health and a long reload time.

==Appearance==
A small, black, triangular shaped petal with an even darker outline. If the petal is {{Rarity|6}}, the Stinger will turn into 3 Stinger petals that rotate around each other. {{Rarity|7}}, {{Rarity|8}} and {{Rarity|9}} Stingers have 5 Stinger petals, forming a pentagon.

With the Duplicator Talent, {{Rarity|6}} Stingers will have 4 Stinger petals, and {{Rarity|7}}, {{Rarity|8}} and {{Rarity|9}} Stingers will have 6 Stinger petals.

==Obtaining==

{{:Bee/Drop Stinger}}

===Purchasing===
On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|4}}

==Strategy==
Stingers deal devastating damage to anything they hit. Higher rarities will one shot most enemies. However, their very long respawn keeps the DPS fairly low, except for the {{R|Mythic}} and {{R|Ultra}} varieties. Still, without a high durability higher levels cannot multi-hit mobs unlike other petals, meaning high level stingers have less of an edge. Their low health means that they can be destroyed very easily. High-rarity stingers have been heavily nerfed (the reload used to be 2.5s, then was nerfed to 7s and in March 2023 to 8s). 

===[[Garden]]===
* Stinger's low respawn means that it struggles against hordes of enemies. However, it is perhaps the best petal at dealing with a single mob quickly. Stinger spam can take down moderate groups of mobs and is great for dealing with a single mob. 
** Pairing stingers with [[Faster]]s lowers the overall damage but increases how quickly it is dealt, making it a useful combination.
** It is effective towards Termites.
* Stingers are good against [[Hornet|Hornets]] because they have high burst damage. However, be aware that the Hornet's missiles may destroy the Stingers. 
** Because of this, Stinger is one of the best petals to use in high rarity zones to farm  petals.

===[[Desert]]===
* Similar strategies as garden, it is great for dealing damage against a single mob or handling moderate swarms. 
* It is especially useful against [[Sandstorm]]s due to their high health. Stingers can kill a sandstorm before it has the potential to cause problems.

===[[Ocean]]===
* Due to their health regen, Stinger is useful against [[Starfish]]. They can one shot most of them, preventing regen.
** Having multiple stingers can counter rarer varieties that may be able to tank a hit.
* Stinger is good against [[Jellyfish]] since it can quickly kill them before they have the chance to deal too much damage.
* Stinger is good against [[Crab]]s since it only needs to land one hit to kill it or deal serious damage, making the crab less likely to dodge as they tend to do.

===[[PvP]] ===
====Using====
* Since the beginning of florr, stingers have been a powerhouse in PvP. A few of them will make quick work of any flower and all players fear them. A {{Rarity|6}} stinger deals over 24.3k damage at once, killing any player in one hit unless they have opted to spam [[Cactus|Cacti]] and/or [[Soil]], but if they decided to do that, they lack adequate damage.
* The goal of a stinger user is quite simple - land a stinger on the flower to deal lethal or near lethal damage. 
** [[Faster]]s can make this easier by causing stingers to move faster, making them harder to dodge or destroy
** One of the most popular builds involves stingers and [[Bubble]]s. The stingers are placed on the opposite side of the bubbles, so when the user uses their bubbles, their stingers are thrusted into any opponent with almost no way to dodge.
* High health petals such as the [[Heavy]] and [[Corn]] can be used a shield against fragile stingers.
** Stinger users can also use these petals to shield their stingers.
* A stinger can be paired with an [[Iris]] for an inescapable combo. The stinger deals a quick hit of damage while the iris slowly deals the rest. This is an almost guaranteed kill if successful, but the attacker must land a good hit and dodge counterattacks for a few seconds.
** Hitting the player with a [[Dandelion]] beforehand slows down their healing.

====Against ====
* High petal density, achieved through [[Light]] or [[Pollen]] spam, can serve as a shield against stinger attacks too.
* If a player's stingers are killed, they are left vulnerable for a few seconds. Players can take full advantage of this, attacking the stinger user before their petals recharge. Stinger users should be wary of this and be ready to flee battle if needed. Bubbles can help with fleeing.
* [[Salt]] can reflect the Stinger's very high damage back to the user.
** Note that they could be using [[Sponge]].

==Trivia==
* {{Rarity|6}} Stingers with no Duplicator talent are frequently referred to as '''Tringers''' due to there being three and them forming a triangle shape. {{Rarity|7}} Stingers with no Duplicator talent are frequently referred to as '''Pingers''' due to there being five and them forming a pentagon shape.
** When the Duplicator talent is purchased, Tringers may be referred to as '''Tetringers''' or '''Quadringers'''/'''Quadingers''' and Pingers as '''Hexingers'''.
* They were originally most frequently obtained from [[Hornet]]s, but this was changed on February 19, 2020, when Hornets now drop [[Missile]]s instead.
* The Stinger used to have the highest single-target burst damage potential of any petal that does not require certain circumstances. Now the [[Dice]] beats its burst damage with its crit hit. 
* In the Legacy version of florr.io. {{Rarity|5}} Stingers used to have 3 Stinger petals in one.
* On January 13, 2023, Stinger's damage was buffed due to many other {{Rarity|6}} petals outclassing its damage.
* There is a variant of this petal called the [[Blood Stinger]], which has three quarters the reload at the cost of dealing you some damage when you spawn it.
* In the modern era, many high DPS petals and hard hitting petals have outclassed Stinger heavily.
* Stinger has 1 health at all rarities.

:''This page is about Magic Stinger. For the normal petal variant, see [[Stinger]].''

'''Magic Stinger,''' shortened in-game into just '''Stinger,''' is a petal in florr.io. It's like Stinger but better damage and reload, just cost mana to spawn. Its damage is just less damage than [[Dice]]'s critical hit per hit. Magic Stinger is dropped by [[Bee]] and [[Wasp]] with Mana Orb equipped, regardless of rarity.

==Appearance==

Magic Stinger is a recolored version of Stinger, being cyan instead of black.

==Obtaining==
===[[Bee]] (with [[Mana Orb]] equipped)===
{{:Bee/Drop Magic Stinger}}

===Purchasing===
On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''{{Petal Price|4}}

==Strategy==
Due to its high damage, Magic Stinger can nuke most mobs and deal extreme damage to Supers if used correctly. However, this is a risky strategy because of its low health, it can consume your mana very fast and waste your time to regain mana, especially against ranged mobs and high-speed mobs.

There is a useful strategy for Magic Stinger but only apply it for Super hunting. Magic Stinger is bad at farming. Firstly, get M+ [[Cactus/Magic|Magic Cactus]] and M+ [[Leaf/Magic|Magic Leaf]], as well as [[Mana Orb]] in order to increase the mana capacity that you can store, generate mana faster. Remember to replace it with another petal to prevent it from consuming mana. And then, if you reach enough distance to the Super mobs, swap the other petal with it and rapidly switch [[Yin Yang]] with another petal to keep Magic Stinger in place to focus the damage on the enemy.

===[[Garden]]===
*The magic stinger, unlike normal stinger, can respawn quickly if you have enough mana. It is recommended to only use one in your loadout and hold it in your second hotbar until you get close enough to your target while there are little projectiles flying towards you so that you don't waste mana on irrelevant entities.

===[[Desert]]===
*Just like above, you can use the magic stinger against sandstorms do deal major damage. You can use it against projectile-shooting mobs when they're not targeting you.

===[[Ocean]]===
*The magic stinger is great to use against starfish. You can easily kill them with the high damage stingers before they even heal. They can also be used against shells but is better when used against starfish.

=== [[Sewers]] ===

* The magic stinger excels at destroying [[Roach|Roaches]] due to them being neutral mobs. However, tuck the magic stinger away as soon as a [[Fly]] targets you, as Fly evasion can nullify magic stinger's damage quickly, draining your mana.

=== [[Jungle]] ===

* The magic stinger is great to use against [[Leafbug|Leafbugs]] due to the petal's high damage per hit. You can use it against projectile-shooting mobs when they're not targeting you.

===[[PvP]]===
====Using====
*If used well, the magic stinger can have very effective use. The high rarity magic stinger has high damage and can even kill the opponents in one hit. However, you need to use it carefully and wisely because of its low health. In order to kill other flowers effectively, you need to make sure the stinger hits the flower rather than its pedal or other mobs. You can try to use [[Yin Yang]] for better control of the magic stinger.

====Against====
*Even though the magic stinger has high damage, the magic stinger has 2 vital disadvantages: its low health same as a kind of stinger, and its mana consumption. As long as the stinger cannot hit you, it cannot deal damage to you. Therefore, you can use your pedal to defeat the other flower's stinger until it does not have mana left.

==Trivia==
*Unlike the normal variants of Stinger and Blood Stinger, its petal count doesn't increase per rarity.
*Unique Magic Stinger has potential to deal very insane damage, about 1.6m damage per hit. 
*Magic Stinger used to have no reload timer. Now Magic Stinger has 0.5s reload. This update heavily affects the Mana bombing strategy.
*Magic Stinger was used to be dropped from Bumble Bee, now from Bee and recently Wasp.
*On 10th of September, Magic Stinger was updated to double its reload, along with Bandage 3rd nerf. This don't affect its total damage, but damage output and DPS.
*Missile crafted the first and second ever super magic stinger. Boner_Alert crafted the third ever super magic stinger.
*Magic Stinger health has been nerfed to 1 on all rarities.
