title: Super and Unique Mobs
content:
{{LongPage}}

{{R|8}} Mobs are special mobs that have a chance of spawning from an {{R|7}} mob. Super Mobs have special boss attacks and better drop rates. 

If you are level 75 or above and when a {{R|8}} mob spawns in biome you are in, you will receive a green message saying "'''{{Text shadow|A Super [mob name] has spawned!|class = super}}'''", but if you are not in the same biome as the mob, it will instead say "'''{{Text shadow|A Super [mob name] has spawned somewhere!|class = super}}'''". 

When you approach a {{Rarity|Super|Super+}} mob, a large health bar shows up for a clearer view to determine how much health it has. 

If a {{R|8}} Mob has been destroyed, it will globally send a green message saying "'''{{Text shadow|A Super [mob] has been defeated by [user]!|class = super}}"''' with the user's name if they dealt the highest damage'''.''' If 2 players defeated the {{R|8}} mob, it will instead say "'''{{Text shadow|A Super [mob] has been defeated by [user] & [another user]!|class = super}}"''' with three being "'''{{Text shadow|A Super [mob] has been defeated by [user], [2nd user] & [3rd user]!|class = super}}"''' and four being "'''{{Text shadow|A Super [mob] has been defeated by [user], [2nd user], [3rd user] & [4th user]!|class = super}}"'''. 

Many different {{R|8}} Mobs are able to summon another mob: for example, [[Hornet|Hornets]] that spawn hornets, [[Bush|Bushes]] that spawn random kind of Ultra jungle mob, [[Wasp|Wasps]] that spawn wasps and etc. 

There are also '''{{Text shadow|Unique|class = unique}}''' mobs, which their special {{R|8}} attacks are the same but have more stats and they can only be spawned by M28 and they drop 10 times the loot. 

==Mobs==
''Mobs marked with N/A don't have special {{R|8}} mob attack. Diggers, Target Dummies, NPCs and Squares do not include in this list due to being an ally (cannot be attacked by flowers except for unfriendly mobs).''

===Garden===

*[[Bee]]: N/A
*[[Bumble Bee]]: N/A
*[[Centipede]]: N/A
*[[Dandelion (Mob)|Dandelion]]: N/A
* [[Hornet]]:
** When the Hornet begins to wiggle its Missile, it will shoot a cyclone of {{Rarity|7}} hornet missiles and spin around. The hornet may also push itself when it is shooting a barrage of missiles.
**When the Hornet begins to wiggle its antennae, it will summon a couple of {{Rarity|7}} Hornets.
* [[Ladybug]]: N/A
*[[Rock (Mob)|Rock]]: 
**Spawns {{Rarity|6}}-{{Rarity|7}} rocks based on its health loss.

===Desert===

*[[Beetle]]: N/A
*[[Cactus]]: N/A
*[[Centipede (Desert)|Desert Centipede]]: N/A
*[[Sandstorm]]:
** Pulls in nearby [[Flower|Flowers]] and Mobs, but not Petals. It will pull until the flower or mob gets in the center of the Sandstorm. You can use Air or Soil to resist the effect.
*[[Scorpion]]: N/A

===Ocean=== 

*[[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble]]:
**Spawns {{Rarity|6}}-{{Rarity|7}} [[Bubble (Mob)|Bubbles]] based on its health loss.
*[[Crab]]: N/A
*[[Jellyfish]]:
**Jellyfish releases a shockwave that damages flowers, friendly pets and petals. The shockwave can also disable your petals for a certain period of time.
* [[Leech]]: N/A
*[[Shell]]: 
**Shoots a gigantic pearl. The pearl shooting attack is also persistent on all rarity. It pulls back the pearl after some time and repeats over and over till it's dead.
*[[Sponge (Mob)|Sponge]]: N/A
*[[Starfish (Mob)|Starfish]]: N/A

===Jungle=== 

*[[Bush]]:
**Spawns {{Rarity|7}} random kind of mob in Jungle (excluding Baby and Worker Termites, Termite Mounds, Magic Firefly and other Bushes) when attacked.
*[[Firefly]]: N/A
*[[Leafbug]]: N/A
*[[Mantis]]: N/A
*[[Wasp]]:
**Same as Hornet attacks, but instead of summoning Hornets and Hornet missiles, it spawns Wasps and Wasps missiles. The missiles shot from Wasp moves sinusoidally (zig-zag). This movement is also persistent on all rarity.

=== Sewers ===

* [[Fly]]: N/A
* [[Moth]]: N/A
* [[Roach]]: N/A
* [[Spider]]:
** Lays a giant web and a trail of webs(This attack starts from {{R|Legendary}}+ Spiders).
* [[Silverfish]]:
** Carries a {{R|Super}} Garbage. It has this function at every rarity.
* [[Garbage]]: N/A

===Ant Hell=== 

*[[Baby Ant]]/[[Baby Fire Ant|Fire Baby Ant]]/[[Baby Termite|Termite Baby Ant]]: N/A
*[[Worker Ant]]/[[Worker Fire Ant]]/[[Worker Termite|Worker Termite Ant]]: N/A
*[[Soldier Ant]]/[[Soldier Fire Ant|Solider Fire Ant]]/[[Soldier Termite|Solider Termite Ant]]: N/A
*[[Queen Ant]]/[[Queen Fire Ant]]:
**Summons {{Rarity|7}} Ant/Fire Ant egg that hatches an {{Rarity|7}} Soldier Ant/Fire Ant. This is persistent for other rarities.
*[[Termite Overmind]]:
**Disables player's petals for a certain period of time. Can disable up to 7 petals.
*[[Worm]]:
**Able to eat players into [[Worm (Area)|its zone]]. Then it applies a lot of poison damage every second.

=== Factory ===
* [[Mecha Flower]]: N/A
* [[Wasp/Mecha|Mecha Wasp]]: 
** Same as Hornet attacks, but instead of summoning Hornets and Hornet missiles, it spawns Mecha Wasps and Mecha Wasps missiles. The missiles shot from Wasp homes and this movement is also persistent on all rarity.
* [[Spider/Mecha|Mecha Spider]]: 
** Same attack pattern as Spider. However upon contact with Spider, it does lightning damage instead of poison damage.
* [[Crab/Mecha|Mecha Crab]]: N/A
* [[Barrel]]: Constantly releases poison damage starting from its center and expanding out.

=== Pyramid ===
* [[Beetle/Mummy|Mummy Beetle]]: N/A
* [[Beetle/Nazar|Nazar Beetle:]] N/A
* [[Beetle/Pharaoh|Pharaoh Beetle]]:
** Summons undead Mummy Beetles of 2 rarities lower than the Pharaoh Beetle's rarity. Its function is the same as [[Pharaoh's Crown|Crown]]'s function.
* [[Tomb]]: Summons a Mummy Beetle upon death.

==Strategy==
The recommended requirements for hunting a Super is:

#4 {{Rarity|7}} Bubbles
#Compass ( a must need, since you won't know where a {{Rarity|8}} mob is unless you follow the mega ). 
# {{Rarity|7}} Bur
# Discord Server with super mob-pinging bot. Guilds can also work but only if it is an active one.

===Hunting Guide===

*{{Rarity|7}} petals are recommended to get loot from a Super mob, since many people have {{Rarity|8}} petals that can easily steal the loot from people with couple of Ultras.
**Specifically, full {{Rarity|7}} build is mostly recommended to obtain 100% of the loot from a Super mob. Super petals can also include there, but that will make people with less Ultras not able to get loot.
*If there are less than 25 people, you should stay alive and damage it.
*Refer the recommended requirements above to maximize your DPS and time.

==== DPS builds====
''See [[Builds - General]]. Recommended builds are shown below.''

'''''NOTE:''''' ''Petals must be {{Rarity|7}}+ rarity unless if it is specified. Mythic is also recommended but you won't get the loot mostly.''

'''Light WoD''' 
*Build: 5 [[Light]], 3 [[Lentil]], [[Bur|Bur,]] ''{{Rarity|Legendary}}''+ [[Nazar Amulet|Nazar]] ( lower levels have a really low reload, and it is recommended to get mythic+ or you will take a lot of damage quickly.)
*Obtainment: Medium
AFK in the ladybug zone (top right of garden). Good petals are not needed to farm there. It is recommended to have a couple eggs or good super petals.

For lentils go to {{R|Super}} [[Beetle/Mummy|Mummy Beetles]] or look for squads in [[pyramid]] to grind it out.  
*DPS: ~300-500k , can loot most of the supers easily and can carry others if you are really early ( if mega isn't too big)
*Summary: Light WoD is not bad to get, even though it is kind of difficult, it's DPS output isn't too bad.  Nazar also prevents you from taking damage.
*This can be replaced once you have a couple supers from shunting.
'''Sand WoD'''
*Build: 7 [[Sand]], 2 [[Faster]], [[Bur]] 
Iris can be used as replacement for only 1 sand. Needs investment in the poison talent to yield a better DPS output.
*Obtainment: Medium/Hard
The quickest way to obtain 7 sand is in ant hell (by getting 7 glass and assemble them in factory). However, strong eggs are required to afk here. Sand can also be obtained passively through ocean or desert farming. Starfish zone can be farmed too for sand, though not as quickly.*DPS: ~185k, enough for most supers if you get there quick enough except for difficult supers such as Queen or Ant Hole
* Summary: Sand WoD is a very viable option for looting supers. It is the easiest build to obtain which can consistently loot supers which makes it very popular.
'''Claw'''
*Build: 8 [[Claw]], 2 [[Faster]] (any rarity)
Petal rotation needs to be adjusted with Q/E for optimal rotation 9 Claw can be used too. 9 claw can give up to 650k dps burst and then it drops down to 400-500k. But it can do a solid 2 million damage in a couple seconds. 
*Obtainment: Medium
By far the quickest way to obtain this is through AFK in crab zone. You can also grind a Sand WoD here too, as well as bubbles. Full claw can be obtained far faster than full sand due to the better drop rates
*DPS:~350k, keep in mind this only works above 80%, so switch to another build once the super drops below that.
*Summary:Claw builds can provide a nice amount of extra damage if you get to supers early, which can help you loot them.
Comment: need 3.58 rad/s for full reload talent so use no faster. Has 137k on mythics so around 400k for ultras.

'''Chip'''
*Build: 8 [[Poker Chip|Chip]], [[Faster]](''{{Rarity|Rare}}''+), [[Bur]]
Petal rotation needs to be adjusted with Q/E for optimal rotation
*Obtainment: Hard
The only way to obtain Chip is by farming Hel. The most optimal way to farm hel is by targeting gamblers in the ultra zone (on the right). You should bubble around the ultra zone using burst dps petals such as [Stinger] opposite to the bubbles, and sponge with healing for other free petal slots. Very good petals are required to farm Hel. Despite this, rates are still low.
* DPS: ~186k 
This is not too RNG based as the chance of the petal breaking is low and doesn’t have much consequence when it happens.*Summary: Chip is a great DPS build which can consistently loot almost all supers. However, it is fairly difficult to obtain.

'''Dice'''
*Build: 8 [[Dice]], [[Clover]], [[Faster]] (any rarity)
Needs full Luck talent. 
*Obtainment: Hard
See Chip obtainment above. Same strategies apply.
* DPS: ~210k (with full luck talent, varies HIGHLY)
Highly RNG based and requires Luck talent
*Summary: Dice can do insane levels of DPS. However, sometimes it fails to crit, causing you to get no loot. Also requires 14 TP of investment to be good.
'''Rice'''
*Build:[[Yin Yang]] (any rarity),5 [[Rice]], [[Bur]], [[Sponge]] (Mythic+), 2 [[Light]]
Bur should be in between the rice.*Obtainment: Medium/Hard
There are 2 ways to get this: 1. Farm normal ant hell, very easy to survive though the rates are absolutely awful due to it bring only ~8% [[Baby Ant]] and low rarity. 2. Farm garden, baby ant spawn rates are lower, however, there is a possibility for far higher rarities spawning. Optimal zone to farm is spiral (top left). You can also leech off a sweaty NF squad but be prepare to get bullied (it’s NF).
*DPS: Varies HIGHLY
If you are close to the super mob, rice can do insane amounts of DPS (250k+). However, if you are far away, rice does extremely mediocre damage*Summary: Rice offers a high-risk high-reward playstyle. It requires you to get extremely close to super mobs, meaning you may risk being pushed into them. It can yield extreme DPS outputs.

'''Privet'''
*Build: SUPER [[Iris]], 5 [[Privet Berry]], [[Sponge]], 2 [[Light]], [[Yin Yang]]
Iris must be super rarity, don't even try with ultra iris. Needs max poison talent.
*Obtainment: Easy (excluding super iris)
Farm bottom right of fire ant hell using an egg build, or leech off a squad there.
* DPS: 550k
*Summary: If you are lucky enough to get super iris, this build is extremely overpowered. Use it like how you would use rice.

'''Triangle'''
*Build: 9 [[Triangle]], [[Faster]]
*Obtainment: Easy
Grind fire ant hell, very fast
*DPS: 220k
*Summary: This build is high-risk, high-reward. In a triangle build, you cannot use petals like [[Sponge]] or [[Third Eye]] as it will lose you to much dps, effectively making you a glass cannon.

'''Coral'''
*Build: 7 [[Coral]], [[Bur]], 2 [[Faster]]
*Obtainment:Good Luck
The only feasible method of obtaining 7 ultra coral is by killing super Sponges, which have a 9.8% chance of dropping ucoral. On average you will need around 70 ''{{Rarity|Super}}'' sponge kills to complete this build. Farming coral is absurdly slow, with trench giving around 3 mcoral/hour after crafting legendaries.
*DPS: 250k
*Summary: Enough DPS to loot almost any super, if you somehow manage to get this.

'''Ram'''
*Build:
There are 2 main types of ram builds: rice ram and heal ram.

Rice Ram: 4 [[Rice]], [[Bur]], [[Cutter]] (''{{Rarity|Mythic}}'' will do but ''{{Rarity|Ultra}}''  is recommended), [[Disc]] (''{{Rarity|Mythic}}''  works but ''{{Rarity|Ultra}}'' is better), [[Sponge]], 2 [[Light]] (''{{Rarity|Mythic}}''  works just as well as ''{{Rarity|Ultra}}'' ), [[Bandage]] (in secondary loadout)

Heal Ram: [[Cutter]], [[Disc]], [[Sponge]], 5 ''{{Rarity|Ultra}}''  Healing petals, 2 [[Basil]] (''{{Rarity|Mythic}}''  works)
The Sharp Edges talent is a must for heal ram and highly recommended for rice ram.
*Obtainment:

Rice: Can be obtained by actively farming normal Ant Hell (top left). 

Bandage: The only feasible way to obtain  ''{{Rarity|Ultra}}''  Bandage is to kill a pharaoh beetle, as literally no-one farms pyramid. Luckily, pharaoh beetle’s have an extremely high chance to drop Ultra Bandage (98.4%), so it shouldn’t be an issue.

Cutter (''{{Rarity|Ultra}}'' ): Farm termites for either 1280 mythic triangles or 10 ultra triangles. Then find an assembler in the [[Factory]] which will convert 10 triangles into a cutter (if you have ultra triangles, it might take a while to find). If you grinded mythic triangles, oracle ultra cutter.

Disc: Go to super fire ant burrows, as they have a 43% chance to drop ultra disc. Make sure not to go too far away from the burrow as it could cause you to not loot.

Sponge: Either get lucky from a ''{{Rarity|Super}}'' sponge or farm sponge zone (middle bottom of ocean) if you have bad petals or trench if you have decent petals.
*Total Ram Damage: (with bandage)

Rice Ram: Around 4m

Heal Ram: idk
*Summary: Should be used when a super is low on health a source of extra damage. Only equip bandage once second chance has activated as bandage will still reduce body damage even if its effect is not active.

==== Other Notes ====

* It is ALWAYS worth using compass on a super. Even if it seems a super always spawns in the same zone, you never know when the game may surprise you (a Super Scorpion once spawned in the area left of spawn).
* Super Coin is not worth using in DPS builds sometimes. Calculate the DPS before using it.
* Hit dummies for at least 1 minute for accurate results.
* When a super mob is low hp, you can switch to a ram build for extra damage.

=== Mobs ===

* Garden:
** Bee: Since they have the highest damage in the game along with [[Hornet]], [[Wasp]], and [[Hel Beetle]] (65.6k at Super, enough to one-shot), but low health, it's best to attack it with high-rarity [[Stinger|Stingers]] with [[Third Eye|Third Eye,]] or use [[Wing]] for extra range, as melee petals are extremely difficult to use against a mob with erratic movement. The [[Bee]] is also faster than the player, so it is wise to use [[Bubble|Bubbles]] against it. Using Pincers or Web is ill-advised because of Super mob resistance to these petals debuffs.
** Bumble Bee: They run fast, and are always passive. Using mythic+ [[Bubble|bubbles]] against them are a good idea because their movement is fairly random at times such as hitting a wall.
** Centipede:
**#Use [[Lightning]] to deal damage to multiple segments.
**# If you do not have high enough rarity Lightning, use a high DPS build to cause enough damage to a few segments for loot.
**#If you struggle to run from fast mobs, use [[Bubble]] or [[Rubber]], if you are using Bubble be careful to where you bubble to.
**Dandelion:
**#Destroy one of the Dandelion's petals first before attacking the Dandelion itself so you would not be hit by a launched petal, or stay in between 2 petals to avoid hitting it.
**#Attack it with high DPS petals. Deathwheel ([[Light]]/[[Sand]] + [[Faster]]) builds are best at countering ultra dandelions as they have no other defence once the missiles are gone.
**Hornet:
**#The missiles from Hornet's cyclone attack are a little smaller and slightly less damaging than super missiles. You need to go far away where the missiles despawn or use multiple planks far enough from it. Use heals as you will hit its missiles.
**#Minions (Ultra rarity) will be harmless unless aggroed. Using [[Poo]] or good healing + health petals would combat these minions.
**#It is difficult to dodge Super missiles, and punishing if you are hit by them.  Blocking line-of-sight using walls and [[Shovel]] will make the hornet target another player. Moving away during Super Hornet's default attack will also work.
**Ladybug: They are slow and easy to outrun. Use a regular DPS/Deathwheel build to counter these. Be careful of other mobs, especially [[Hornet|Hornets]], as they can push you into the Ladybug.
**Rock:
**#Use a DPS / Deathwheel build. Rocks fling smaller rocks at the players depending on how much damage they dealt.
**# [[Jelly]] can stop small rocks from hitting you.
**#Lightning / Battery will deal with smaller rocks, but you would only need one or two as they deal lower damage and have less health than the Super Rock.
**#Hiding behind a wall can also shield you from flying rocks.
*Desert:
**Beetle:
**#Generally, using bubbles is the only way to be faster than the beetle.
**#Use [[Shovel]] to lose aggro so that the beetle pauses its movements for a few seconds, it might rotate 180 degrees while being passive, giving you extra time to outrun it because it makes turns slowly.
**Cactus:
**#Do not ram it. The thorns body damage would insta-kill most players.
**#Use a DPS/Deathwheel build.
**Desert Centipede:
**#Use a DPS/Deathwheel build. Beware of the attack, as it might kill you even when you are in a position far away. It's recommended to use Ultra+ [[Bubble|bubbles]] if you feel things can go wrong.
**# Make sure that the centipede does not target you. Even while underground, it still follows you.
** Sandstorm: You can use [[Air]] in order to not get pulled easily by the Sandstorm, since it doesn't slow down the movement. However its very vulnerable to most of the nearby since it only increases the flower size. [[Soil]] users are less affected by this, so you can use a [[Soil]]. Note that the pull of the Super Sandstorm is stronger, so you have to use 2 Mythic Soil or 1 Ultra Soil. Another counter to this ability is to quickly [[bubble]] away if you get too close. [[Cutter]] doesn't work anymore. Make sure you use [[Wing|wings]] to attack from a safe distance, because you can die from one ram. Otherwise, use [[Third Eye|third eye]] with a Deathwheel build.
** Scorpion:
**#Scorpions MUST be handled with dodging skills and [[Lotus]]. For dodging, a quick tip is that scorpions shoot every 5 seconds.
**# Quickly strafing left and right makes the scorpion more likely to miss its shot.
**#[[Lotus]] tanks poison, which might help you survive if you are hit.
*Ocean:
**Bubble: The key to looting the Bubble is speed, as players will greed it before you can even get close to it. The Bubble has extremely low HP. Despite their buffed Armor and ability to spawn lower rarity bubbles like the Super Rock, they will be greeded '''very quickly.''' try use a nuke build (build that is suitable to kill mobs with massive armor) (although it is usually better to share the Bubble with other players to prevent them from hating you).
**Crab:
**#Make sure you have an ultra+ [[bubble]] to run away if needed. They might still be avoided without bubbles with enough distance.
**#Attack with ranged petals like [[Third Eye]] or [[Wing]].
**#If the [[Oracle]] happens to wander into the Crab's location, it can get in front of the Super Crab and block the way, preventing it from reaching the players since it can't be pushed by mobs.
**Jellyfish:
**#It releases a shockwave that damages all nearby players and destroys all their petals. This shockwave can be dangerous if you do not have enough base HP.
**#It can disable petals temporarily with its shockwave. The only effective method is to [[bubble]] near it, hit with some high damaging petals like [[stinger]] or [[tomato]], [[bubble]] away, and heal. Do this in between each shockwave.
**#Alternatively, you can use [[Carrot|carrots]] as they travel further than the shockwave, but this is not recommended.
**#There is a spot close to the Jellyfish (no more than 5% of a Basil should be on the jellyfish) where the shockwave does not affect your petals.
**Leech:
**#If you are unable to tank, switch to a [[bubble]] build and wrap the leech around. Then attack. If it is a short leech (rare variant of leech), this option will not work, and you are screwed. Try to outrun while the megasquad is coming.
**#Tanking is the most reliable option, however, damage may be a problem if you require many slots to tank. You should use Fangs, Starfish, or Yucca with Basil to outheal a Leech, as you should have at least 6k heal (in case there are mythic leeches). Rose and Dahlia do not work because they are destroyed before they can heal you. Leaf heal is usually not enough. Spread out fangs to give more consistent heal. Battery is the best source of damage against an Ultra leech, followed by Lightning (which is easier to obtain). Be careful of Mythic Crabs or Starfish pushing you into the Leech.
**Shell:
**#Shoots a huge [[pearl]] to the player that its aggro is on. It has high health and average damage like the [[Pearl]] petal. It also has a similar movement to the Pearl petal. The Shell can shoot 5 pearls, and it will predict where the player will go. (like how Epic+ hornets shoot its missiles) After it shoots a pearl, it will dash twice before shooting another. When all pearls are shot, it will stay still, shake its head, call its pearls back one by one, and immediately shoots more pearls after. If any pearls are stuck outside, it will keep shaking until it gives up, and continue firing.
**# To counter this, use bubbles to stay a safe distance away from the shell. If the shell blasts you directly, you may die. If it hits you, but the shot is not direct, you can survive. [[Wing|Wings]] and [[Third Eye|Third eye]] is a great combo to counter the shell.
**# Another strategy is to have one player using a [[Rubber]] + [[Salt]] tank [[Builds|build]] while the rest of the players use DPS builds suitable for killing mobs with high armor. As the tank, be careful to not travel too far from the Shells or the DPS/Deathwheel build players will be at risk.
**#If you are alone, try wall cheese it where you can safely hit it without it seeing you, then killing it. Another method is to predict the pearl trajectory. Dodging the pearl is, in some ways, similar to dodging the hornet/wasp/mantis attacks.
**#Ant Eggs and Beetle Eggs will not penetrate the Shell's Armor while Sticks only deal little damage to it.
**Sponge: Use a Deathwheel build. It is easy to kill and poses no threat. It is consider to kill it with many damages since sometimes it would spawn in the area full of Ultra Mobs.
**Starfish:
**#Use [[Dandelion]] to counter its healing factor. You may want to use [[Bubble|Bubbles]] or a knockback petal like [[Heavy]], as they are quite fast. In small areas like in the maze area, the other mobs can be a problem. In other areas, it is still fine to deal with, UNLESS THERE'S A LOT OF MOBS NEARBY.
**#The Starfish is cheesable, but unless you're in a nook and can hit the starfish using melee, Third Eye is required for Dandelion to hit it.
*Jungle:
**Bush: Use a Deathwheel build. Use [[Lightning]] to kill the spawned mobs. You need some heal petals because spawned mobs deal a lot of damage.
**Firefly:
**#Use a Deathwheel build.
**# Don't ram it as it can release [[lightning]] when you do so, which can kill other players close to you and you with low health. You may ram it when it is low on health.
**#Use [[Rubber]], [[Cactus]], and especially [[Sponge]] (against super firefly) to counter lightning.
**Leafbug: Use petals that deal more damage than its amount of armor, like [[Tomato]], [[Wing]], or [[Stinger]] (Armor: 7.9k at Ultra, 23.8k at Super). If you do not have any, use [[Lightning]] and [[Iris]] because they bypass armor. For Super, not many petals can damage it, aside from Tomato, Claw, and Dice's burst damage, only lightning/poison petals can (lightning: Lightning, Battery, some Magic Petals; poison: Grapes, Pincer, Iris)
**Mantis:
**#The Mantis rapidly shoots [[Peas]] that have an armor debuff. To dodge these, move to the sides of the Mantis back and forth to confuse the Mantis. Be careful that the peas are very big, and hitting it will almost immediately result in death if there are a lot of projectiles nearby.
**#[[Wing|Wings]] have high range and decent damage. This can be helpful when you are being targeted as that extra range lets you hit the Mantis from further away. [[Missile]] works too, but can be easily destroyed by the Mantis' peas.
**Wasp: Strategy is the same as Hornet, but you should watch out for the sidewinding missiles, since it can hit you easily.
*Sewers:
**Fly: Counter the fly with [[Jelly]]. It moves faster than you, so use knockback petals, such as Jelly.
**Moth: 
**#You can corner the moth, or you can use [[Bulb]] + [[Jelly]]. If using [[Bulb]] + [[Jelly]], treat it like a [[Fly]].
**#Disable Bulb when it is near, unless the squad is strong enough to kill in one go.
**Roach:
**#Shoot a [[carrot]] or [[missile]] or use [[Bulb]] to gain its aggro, then use Mythic+ [[Bubble|bubbles]] (ultra bubbles preferred, 2-4 should be enough) to bubble away along with Mythic+ [[pollen]] to chip its HP (note that this only works if the roach is in a low difficulty area)
**#The preferred way is to lure the Roach to a corner, then kill it with Wings + Third Eye (optional).
**#If you're near a Super roach (especially those with high HP percentile), beware as it can target you when you don't expect it. The best way to approach it is when someone else has aggro, you can come in from behind with burst damage petals (stinger, tomato)
**# Eggs might slow the Roach down as you run, making it more manageable. Alternatively, you can wait for Eggs to reload then attack, once all the eggs are used up you can Ankh back to a safe spot around the corner.
**Spider:
**#Counter these with [[Jelly]]. They are poisonous, so equip [[Lotus]] in case you are hit.
**#Use [[Bubble]]s to escape it's giant webs if you get caught in it.
*Ant Hell:
**Queen Fire Ant: Treat it like a regular [[Queen Ant]]. Be careful with the Ultra [[Soldier Fire Ant|Soldier Fire Ants]], as they can jam you, which is especially deadly since they do 2x damage. If there are not enough players to kill it effectively, lure it to the top chamber where Mythics spawn less frequently.
**Queen Ant: Its eggs aren't too big of a problem. [[Queen Ant|Queen ants]] have a weird hitbox on the edge protruding out, so it is recommended to use [[Wing|wings]] and stay a safe distance from the Queen Ant.
**Termite Overmind: 
**#The main problem with the Overmind is how tanky it is. Use Deathwheel builds against it. If the Overmind spawns in a Mythic chamber, you can lure it away to kill it faster and safer. It's best to use petals that function well alone and not with other petals (e.g. use Wing, Bone, Pearl, Leaf, Iris instead of Sand, Faster, Bur)
**#The Termite Overmind only disables your loadout slots, but not targeting at any of your specific petal. When it is possible, swap petals between slots to ensure that your major attacking and healing petals are on their places.
*Hel:
**Hel Beetle:
**#Be careful at encountering the Hel Beetle, they may teleport onto aggroed players and cause massive damage from Hel Beetle's body damages.
**# There are lots of players in Hel trying to loot this mob at super rarity.
**# Even the strongest players can still struggle, so good luck. It is recommended to have at least 3 ultra [[Bubble|bubbles]] and multiple ultra [[Stinger|stingers]]/tomatoes, as well as mythic+ [[sponge]] before attempting to loot it.
**#A Super Hel build may look similar to a Super Jelly build, however Sponge is necessary due to PvP being on, as well as heals.
**# Counter these like a normal beetle, except with damage same as [[Bee]] and PvP mode.
** Hel Spider: Treat it like Spider, but avoid hitting its body and other players. If you hit any Iris, you are screwed.
** Hel Centipede:
**#It is not recommended to try and loot this, because of the low drop rates (73% chance for legendary mark)
**#If you are going to loot it, treat it like a normal centipede but use sponge and extra heals
**Hel Wasp:
**#Try to keep your distance to it if it is aggravated on you
**# Try to let the mob chase another player, then use high damage petals on it.
**#Beware, as it does a lot of damage
**Gambler: 
**#Use sponge, cotton and a lot of healing petals
**#Super Gambler can be sometimes crowded due to a somewhat high drop rates compared to other Hel mobs and XP.
**#The gambler's petals might pose a bit of a problem, so use petals with a decent amount of health.

*

==Super Spawn Messages==
On 5th July, 2023, a change was made that some {{Rarity|8}} mobs will have unique spawn messages: mobs marked below as bold have special spawn messages.

*Beetle: A Super Beetle has spawned somewhere!
*Bubble: A Super Bubble has spawned somewhere!
*Bush: A Super Bush has spawned somewhere!
*Cactus: '''A tower of thorns rises from the sands...'''
*Centipede: A Super Centipede has spawned somewhere!
*Hel Centipede: '''You sense ominous vibrations coming from a different realm...'''
*Crab: A Super Crab has spawned somewhere!
*Dandelion: A Super Dandelion has spawned somewhere!
*Dark Ladybug: A Super Ladybug has spawned somewhere!
* Desert Centipede: A Super Centipede has spawned somewhere!
* Fly: '''A buzzing noise echoes through the sewer tunnels'''
* Firefly: '''There's a bright light in the horizon...'''
*Gambler: '''You hear someone whisper faintly... "just... one more game..."'''
*Hel Beetle: '''You sense ominous vibrations coming from a different realm...'''
*Hornet: '''A big yellow spot shows up in the distance...'''
*Jellyfish: '''You hear lightning strikes coming from a far distance...'''
*Ladybug: A Super Ladybug has spawned somewhere!
* Leafbug: A Super Leafbug has spawned somewhere!
*Leech: A Super Leech has spawned somewhere!
*Mantis: A Super Mantis has spawned somewhere!
*Moth: A Super Moth has spawned somewhere!
*Queen Ant: A Super Queen Ant has spawned somewhere!
* Queen Fire Ant: A Super Queen Fire Ant has spawned somewhere!
*Roach: A Super Roach has spawned somewhere!
*Rock: '''Something mountain-like appears in the distance...'''
*Sandstorm: A Super Sandstorm has spawned somewhere!
*Shell: A Super Shell has spawned somewhere!
* Spider: A Super Spider has spawned somewhere!
*Hel Spider: '''You sense ominous vibrations coming from a different realm...'''
*Starfish: A Super Starfish has spawned somewhere!
*Shiny Ladybug: A Super Ladybug has spawned somewhere!
* Termite Overmind: A Super Termite Overmind has spawned somewhere!
*Wasp: A Super Wasp has spawned somewhere!
* Hel Wasp: '''You sense ominous vibrations coming from a different realm...'''

==Trivia==
*When Super Mobs were added, they did not have boss health bars, and they were affected by movement speed reducing petals such as web and pincer.
*For some unknown reason, Super Fly's spawn message doesn't have an ellipsis ("...") symbol at the end of it unlike other special super spawn messages.
*Players would consider them as real boss mobs after Super and Unique mobs replaced Mythic/Ultra mobs as the two highest rarities of mobs.
*Unique Mobs do not spawn naturally, instead they can only be spawned by [[M28]].

title: Talents
content:
{{LongPage}} '''Talents''' (formerly Skills) are a mechanic that allows players to unlock and upgrade their flowers' abilities with Talent Points (abbreviated as TP, formerly Skill Points / SP), which can be obtained by leveling up. ==Obtaining==
''Main Article: [[Level]]''
Talent Points are obtained through leveling up. Defeating mobs or [[Absorbing|absorbing]] petals awards a certain amount of experience (XP). Each level has an XP threshold: when this threshold is passed, a specific amount of TP is given: *Reaching a level ending with 5 gives 2 TP; *Reaching a level ending with 0 gives 10 TP; *Reaching a level ending with other numbers gives 1 TP. ==Talents== The Talents menu can be accessed by pressing 'X' on keyboard or the Talents button at the bottom left corner. In the menu, holding the Talent icon unlocks it and all precedent Talents, provided the player has enough TP to afford them. For an unlocked Talent, holding the icon removes it and all subsequent Talents. There is also an option to reset all Talents, which is free of chargeIt used to be free every 6 months. During the 6-month cooldown, players were required to use [[Stars]] to reset Talents.Before the December 8, 2023 update, Talents could be reset by sacrificing 5 levels.It used to be free every 30 days. Then, the formula was changed to ( 2 + level / 3 ) days.. When Talents are reset, all TP spent on the Talents will be refunded. If an update changes the Talent costs, causing a player to result in negative TP, their Talents will automatically be reset and their TP will be refunded, as this was the case with the updates on October 12, 2022, October 2, 2023, June 4, 2024, October 23, 2025, and January 15, 2026. NOTE: Talents with costs below are its base. In game, the cost of the Talent after the rarity higher than the latest Talent rarity increases based on the previous rarity price and the current rarity price, and then this continues until the maximum rarity. For example, if one wants to purchase {{R|8}} Health while having the highest rarity of that talent purchased as {{R|5}}, the cost is shown as: 17 TP (for {{R|6}}) + 20 TP (for {{R|7}}) + 21 TP (for {{R|8}}) = 58 TP. This is used to calculate how many TP is required to purchase rarities of up to the selected. === [[Talents/Petal Health|Petal Health]] === {| class="article-table" width="100%" |+ Talent Stats ! width="12.5%" |Rarity ! width="12.5%" |Icon ! width="50%" |Function ! width="12.5%" |Cost ! width="12.5%" |Requirements |- |{{R|1}} |[[File:Petal Health (Common).png|75px|link=]] |Increases '''{{Text shadow|Petal Health|color=#66ff66}}''' from 100% to 115%. |2 TP | |- |{{R|2}} |[[File:Petal Health (Unusual).png|75px|link=]] |Increases '''{{Text shadow|Petal Health|color=#66ff66}}''' from 115% to 132.25%. |4 TP |{{R|1}} Petal Health |- |{{R|3}} |[[File:Petal Health(Rare).png|75px|link=]] |Increases '''{{Text shadow|Petal Health|color=#66ff66}}''' from 132.25% to 152.09%. |6 TP |{{R|2}} Petal Health |- |{{R|4}} |[[File:Petal Health (Epic).png|75px|link=]] |Increases '''{{Text shadow|Petal Health|color=#66ff66}}''' from 152.09% to 174.9%. |8 TP |{{R|3}} Petal Health |- |{{R|5}} |[[File:Petal Health (Legendary).png|75px|link=]] |Increases '''{{Text shadow|Petal Health|color=#66ff66}}''' from 174.9% to 201.14%. |10 TP |{{R|4}} Petal Health |- |{{R|6}} |[[File:Petal Health (Mythic).png|75px|link=]] |Increases '''{{Text shadow|Petal Health|color=#66ff66}}''' from 201.14% to 231.31%. |12 TP |{{R|5}} Petal Health |- |{{R|7}} |[[File:Petal Health (Ultra).png|75px|link=]] |Increases '''{{Text shadow|Petal Health|color=#66ff66}}''' from 231.31% to 266%. |14 TP |{{R|6}} Petal Health |- |{{R|8}} |[[File:Petal Health (Super).png|75px|link=]] |Increases '''{{Text shadow|Petal Health|color=#66ff66}}''' from 266% to 305.9%. |16 TP |{{R|7}} Petal Health |- |{{R|9b}} |[[File:Petal Health (Eternal).png|75px|link=]] |Increases '''{{Text shadow|Petal Health|color=#66ff66}}''' from 305.9% to 404.56%. |18 TP |{{R|8}} Petal Health |} Cumulative cost: 90 TP === [[Talents/Loadout|Loadout]] === {| class="article-table" width="100%" |+ Talent Stats ! width="12.5%" |Rarity ! width="12.5%" |Icon ! width="50%" |Function ! width="12.5%" |Cost ! width="12.5%" |Requirements |- |{{R|1}} |[[File:Loadout (Common).png|75px|link=]] |Enables a 6th loadout slot to equip petals. |3 TP | |- |{{R|2}} |[[File:Loadout (Unusual).png|75px|link=]] |Enables a 7th loadout slot to equip petals. |6 TP |{{R|1}} Loadout |- |{{R|3}} |[[File:Loadout (Rare).png|75px|link=]] |Enables a 8th loadout slot to equip petals. |9 TP |{{R|2}} Loadout |- |{{R|4}} |[[File:Loadout (Epic).png|75px|link=]] |Enables a 9th loadout slot to equip petals. |12 TP |{{R|3}} Loadout |- |{{R|5}} |[[File:Loadout (Legendary).png|75px|link=]] |Enables a 10th loadout slot to equip petals. |15 TP |{{R|4}} Loadout |} Cumulative cost: 45 TP ===[[Talents/Antennae|Antennae]]=== {| class="article-table" width="100%" ! width="12.5%" |Rarity ! width="12.5%" |Icon ! width="50%" |Function ! width="12.5%" |Cost ! width="12.5%" |Requirements |- |{{R|3}} |[[File:Antennae Talent (Rare).png|75px|link=]] |Increases vision range by 25%. |1 TP |{{R|1}} Loadout |- |{{R|4}} |[[File:Antennae Talent (Epic).png|75px|link=]] |Increases extra vision range from 25% to 50%. |3 TP |{{R|3}} Antennae |- |{{R|5}} |[[File:Antennae Talent (Legendary).png|75px|link=]] |Increases extra vision range from 50% to 100%. |5 TP |{{R|4}} Antennae |} This talent does not stack with the "Extra Vision" attribute of [[Antennae]] and [[Antennae/Mecha|Mecha Antennae]], effectively making this talent a permanent Antennae, though players can still adjust their FoV with the scroll button. Cumulative cost: 9 TPAntennae now requires {{R|1}} Loadout in order to obtain it. It used to be obtained through {{R|3}} Loadout. It is also now much cheaper to obtain all of them. ===[[Talents/Reach|Reach]]=== {| class="article-table" width="100%" ! width="12.5%" |Rarity ! width="12.5%" |Icon ! width="50%" |Function ! width="12.5%" |Cost ! width="12.5%" |Requirements |- |{{R|3}} |[[File:Reach (Rare).png|75px|link=]] | rowspan="3" |Increases petal Attack Range by 50. |3 TP |{{R|2}} Loadout |- |{{R|6}} |[[File:Reach (Mythic).png|75px|link=]] |6 TP |{{R|3}} Reach |- |{{R|8}} |[[File:Reach (Super).png|75px|link=]] |9 TP |{{R|6}} Reach |} This talent stacks with "Attack Range" attribute, which [[Third Eye]] also includes. width="12.5%" |Rarity ! width="12.5%" |Icon ! width="50%" |Function ! width="12.5%" |Cost ! width="12.5%" |Requirements |- |{{R|2}} |[[File:Crafting (Unusual).png|75px|link=]] |Unlocks the ability to craft {{R|2}} petals. |1 TP | |- |{{R|3}} |[[File:Crafting (Rare).png|75px|link=]] |Unlocks the ability to craft {{R|3}} petals. |2 TP |{{R|2}} Crafting |- |{{R|4}} |[[File:Crafting (Epic).png|75px|link=]] |Unlocks the ability to craft {{R|4}} petals. |3 TP |{{R|3}} Crafting |- |{{R|5}} |[[File:Crafting (Legendary).png|75px|link=]] |Unlocks the ability to craft {{R|5}} petals. |4 TP |{{R|4}} Crafting |- |{{R|6}} |[[File:Crafting (Mythic).png|75px|link=]] |Unlocks the ability to craft {{R|6}} petals. |5 TP |{{R|5}} Crafting |- |{{R|7}} |[[File:Crafting (Ultra).png|75px|link=]] |Unlocks the ability to craft {{R|7}} petals. |6 TP |{{R|6}} Crafting |- |{{R|8}} |[[File:Crafting (Super).png|75px|link=]] |Unlocks the ability to craft {{R|8}} petals. |7 TP |{{R|7}} Crafting |} Cumulative cost: 27 TP ===[[Talents/Talented Crafting|Talented Crafting]]=== Previously named Skilled Crafting, and changed to Talented Crafting when the Skills menu is renamed to Talents. {| class="article-table" width="100%" |+Talent Stats ! width="12.5%" |Rarity ! width="12.5%" |Icon ! width="50%" |Function ! width="12.5%" |Cost ! width="12.5%" |Requirements |- |{{R|5}} |[[File:Talented Crafting (Legendary).png|75px|link=]] |Increases craft chance of all rarities to 1.1x of the base chance. |5 TP |{{R|3}} Crafting |- |{{R|6}} |[[File:Talented Crafting (Mythic).png|75px|link=]] |Increases craft chance of all rarities to 1.2x of the base chance. |10 TP |{{R|5}} Talented Crafting |} Cumulative cost: 15 TP ===[[Talents/Rigid Body|Rigid Body]]=== {| class="article-table" width="100%" |+Talent Stats ! width="12.5%" |Rarity ! width="12.5%" |Icon ! width="50%" |Function ! width="12.5%" |Cost ! width="12.5%" |Requirements |- |{{R|7}} |[[File:Rigid Body (Ultra).png|75px|link=]] |Removes knockback from collisions between mobs. | 5 TP |{{R|5}} Sharp Edges |} ==Strategy== * Talents must be spent very wisely because of the XP required to attain a level growing exponentially. **This means that obtaining TP via Levels becomes harder if you reach higher levels, however if you reach a level that ends with 5, you get twice the amount of TP and reaching a level with 0 gets you to 10 TP, allowing you to save up for future talents. ** If you made a mistake while you are spending Talents, you can remove the talent by holding the talent you accidentally purchased, refunding your TP back. ** If you want to spend your TP immediately without clicking it one by one, there's a feature where you can immediately upgrade your talents instantly depending on how many it requires. If you already spend the parts of it, it will decreases depends on how many you spend on it. *Some talents may affect your gameplay in the long run. These contrast talents such as [[Talents/Loadout|Loadout]] and [[Talents/Body Toxicity|Body Toxicity]]. For more information about these talents, visit one of the talent's subpages. ==Trivia== Talents and TP will be referred as Skills and SP for historic purposes. *Skills were renamed to Talents on December 8, 2023. *When Skills were first released, the only available skills were Loadout, Crafting, Health, and Luck. * Before February 10, 2023, you would gain 3 Skill Points and 5 Skill Points respectively every 3 and 5 levels. Since then the price of upgrades has reduced. * As of October 23, 2025, the maximum TP you can obtain is 620. At level 1, you have no TP so if you leveled up all the way to 311, which is the maximum level you can have, you will get: (8-1)+(8*30)+1+(2*31)+(10*31)=620, which means that you can get 2 TP by reaching 31 unique levels that have numbers ending with a 5 and 10 TP by reaching 31 unique levels that have numbers ending with a 0 and the rest of unique levels ending with other number you get 1 TP is 240. This means that you can buy all Talents in the game as they cost a total of 576 TP. * There was used to be a confirmation popup when you reset your Talents and it would tell you about the consequences of resetting. * Before October 23, 2025, each level only gave you 1 TP each, and the others were gained by achievements. * There used to be {{Rarity|Unique}} talents before being replaced with {{Rarity|9b}} talents after January 15, 2026. ==References== {{Wiki Guide}} {{Talents}}{{Game Info}} [[Category:Game information]] [[Category:User Interface Menu]] [[Category:Gameplay]] [[Category:Features]]

title: Talents/Antennae
content:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Antennae Talent}}
''Not to be confused with the [[Antennae|petal of the same name]].''
{{Talent Template Infobox | name = Antennae | image = File:Antennae Talent (Rare).png|R File:Antennae Talent (Epic).png|E File:Antennae Talent (Legendary).png|L | first-rarity = | description = * Increases vision range by #% * Increases extra vision range from #% to #%. | special = Increases vision range by a number of percentage. |lowest_rarity={{Rarity|3}}|rarities={{Rarity|3}}, {{Rarity|4}} and {{Rarity|5}}|cost={{Infobox Colors Empty|||1 TP|3 TP|5 TP||||}}|requirement=Common Loadout}} '''Antennae''' is a [[Talents|talent]] from [https://florr.io florr.io]. Its function consists of increasing vision range. Antennae can be purchased with its base cost of 1 TP and having purchased {{Rarity|1}} [[Talents/Loadout|Loadout]]. ==Appearance== Antennae's icon looks the same as the [[Antennae|petal]], though white. ==Function== {| class="article-table" !'''Rarity''' !'''Effect''' |- |{{Rarity|3}} |Increases vision range by 25% |- |{{Rarity|4}} |Increases the previous rarities' effect to 50%. |- |{{Rarity|5}} |Increases the previous rarities' effect to 100%. |} Antennae's effect consists of increasing vision range by a number of percentage. This talent does not stack with Antennae, meaning the effect will override lower rarity Antennae. ==Purchasing== This table will show the base cost. This cost is increased based on what rarity up to you want to buy. {| class="article-table" !'''Rarity''' !'''Cost''' !'''Requirements''' |- |{{Rarity|3}} |1 TP |Common Loadout |- |{{Rarity|4}} |3 TP |Rare Antennae |- |{{Rarity|5}} |5 TP |Epic Antennae |} Total cost: 9 TP ==Strategies== *Since this talent uses the [[Antennae]]'s effect, it is recommended to refer to its strategy. **It can be like a permanent low rarity Antennae. **Since the effect doesn't stack with Antennae, do not equip a lower rarity Antennae because not only it will be a waste of slot, but also it won't work due to the talent overriding its effect. ==Gallery== File:Antennae Talent (Rare).png|{{Rarity|3}} Antennae. File:Antennae Talent (Epic).png|{{Rarity|4}} Antennae. File:Antennae Talent (Legendary).png|{{Rarity|5}} Antennae. File:Screenshot 2025-02-10 162623.png|Antennae in the Talents tree. ==Trivia== *Antennae is the only talent to share its name with a petal. *Antennae, [[Talents/Luck|Luck]], [[Talents/Magnetism|Magnetism]] and [[Talents/Summoner|Summoner]] are the only talents that don't use icons from Game-Icons.net {{Talents}} [[Category:Talents]][[Category:Antennae]]

title: Talents/Body Toxicity
content:
{{Talent Template Infobox
| name = Body Toxicity
| image = Body Toxicity (Ultra).png
| first-rarity = {{Rarity|7}}
| description = When colliding with an enemy, deals additional damage equal to 0.6x Body Damage as Poison over 1s.
| special = Touching an enemy with this talent causes the enemy to receive Poison equal to the player's body damage, multiplied by 0.6 times over a second.
|rarities={{Rarity|7}}|cost=10 TP|requirement=Mythic Sharp Edges}}

'''Body Toxicity''' is a [[Talents|talent]] from [https://florr.io florr.io]. Its function consists of dealing poison damage equal to 60% of your body damage to an enemy with a duration of a second. Body Toxicity can be purchased with its base cost of 10 TP at the lowest rarity and having purchased Mythic Sharp Edges.

==Appearance==
Body Toxicity's icon consists of a white angry skull with white fumes.

==Function==
{| class="article-table"
!'''Rarity'''
!'''Effect'''
|-
|{{Rarity|7}}
|When colliding with an enemy, deals additional damage equal to 0.6x Body Damage as Poison over 1s.
|}
Body Toxicity's effect consists of dealing additional damage equal to the player's 60% body damage as Poison for over a second.

==Purchasing==
This table will show the base cost. This cost is increased based on what rarity up to you want to buy.
{| class="article-table"
!'''Rarity'''
!'''Cost'''
!'''Requirements'''
|-
|{{Rarity|7}}
|10 TP
|{{Rarity|6}} [[Talents/Sharp Edges|Sharp Edges]]
|}
Total cost: 10 TP.

==Strategies==
*Body Toxicity is sometimes useful with ramming builds, which deals extra poison damage to mobs. However, it has a small effect, making it useless if you do not have [[Bandage]].
*Using this talent with Bandage can be a great combo.

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">
File:Body Toxicity (Ultra).png|{{Rarity|Ultra}} Body Toxicity.
</gallery>
==Trivia==
*Body Toxicity uses the Poison gas icon from https://game-icons.net.
*Its original effect was dealing additional damage as 10x Body Damage as Poison, but was nerfed to 5x, then 3x and now 0.6x, along with duration being only a second.
**The complete nerf to 60% is because of Bandage.

title: Talents/Duplicator
content:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Duplicator}}
{{Talent Template Infobox
| name =Duplicator 
| image = Duplicator (Legendary).png
| first-rarity = {{Rarity|5}}
| description = Adds extra petals to petal that has at least 2 copies.
| special = Provide additional copies on petals that have more than one copy of itself.
|rarities={{Rarity|5}}, {{Rarity|7}} to {{Rarity|9b}}|cost={{Infobox Colors Empty|||||10||20|30|40|}}|requirement=Epic Loadout}}
Duplicator is a [[Talents|talent]] from [https://florr.io florr.io]. It adds extra copies to petals depending on the rarity of the talent.

==Appearance==
Duplicator's icon consists of two circles, connected by several vein-like structures that hold each other together.

==Function==
{| class="article-table"
!'''Rarity'''
!'''Effect'''
|-
|{{Rarity|5}}
|Adds an extra copy for petals that have at least 2 copies.
|-
|{{Rarity|7}}
|Adds an extra copy for petals that have at least 2 copies.
|-
|{{Rarity|8}}
|Adds an extra copy for the left-most petal in the loadout.
Does not affect {{R|9}} and {{R|9b}} petals.

Does not affect '''{{Text shadow|Unstackable|color=#d2eb34}}''' petals.
|-
|{{Rarity|9b}}
|Allows the previous talent to affect {{R|9}} and {{R|9b}} petals.
|}This talent gives additional petals to petals that have at least 2 copies (e.g. [[Sand]], [[Light]], [[Stinger]], etc). for example: {{Rarity|Mythic}} Stinger has 3 copies without Duplicator and 4 with.

When {{Rarity|8}} Duplicator is purchased, any left-most petals (except {{R|9}} and {{R|9b}} ones) get an extra copy.

When {{R|9b}} Duplicator is purchased, the last 2 rarities are affected by the previous talents.
==Purchasing==
This table will show the base cost. This cost is increased based on what rarity up to you want to buy.
{| class="article-table"
!'''Rarity'''
!'''Cost'''
!'''Requirements'''
|-
|{{Rarity|5}}
| 10 TP
|Epic [[Talents/Loadout|Loadout]]
|-
|{{Rarity|7}}
| 20 TP
|Legendary Duplicatior
|-
|{{Rarity|8}}
| 30 TP
|Ultra Duplicator
|-
|{{Rarity|9b}}
| 40 TP
|Super Duplicator
|}
==Strategies==
*This talent is very useful in increasing your overall DPS with petals like Sand, Light, Stinger, etc. [[Dahlia]] is also affected by this talent and essentially heals 25% extra which is pretty good for survival. At higher rarities, this talent can be good.
*It is generally not recommended to purchase this talent at low rarities if you rely on a build that doesn't have petals with multiple copies. This is a waste of TP until the {{Rarity|8}} rarity, where it allows you to add an extra copy to the petal at the first slot of your loadout that can stack and isn't an {{Rarity|9}}.
*This talent amplifies DPS the most utilizing [[Orange]] and [[Dust]].

==Gallery ==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">
File:Duplicator (Legendary).png|{{Rarity|5}} Duplicator.
File:Duplicator (Ultra).png|{{Rarity|7}} Duplicator.
File:Duplicator (Eternal).png|{{Rarity|9b}} Duplicator.
File:Duplicator Tree.png|Duplicator talents from {{Rarity|Legendary}} to {{Rarity|Eternal}}. 
File:Duplicated Ultra Bone.png|A Duplicated {{Rarity|Ultra}} Bone. It is in the first petal slot and the {{Rarity|Super}} Duplicator talent is upgraded.
File:Duplicated Ultra Claw.png|A Duplicated {{Rarity|Ultra}} Claw.
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*This talent's release caused [[Peas]] and [[Grapes]] to change their descriptions: originally, their descriptions were '''4 in 1 deal. [Now with a secret ingredient: poison.]'''.
*There used to be an {{Rarity|Epic}} rarity of this talent, but was removed shortly after it was released.
*SizzleGames designed the icon for Duplicator.
*{{R|Eternal}} Duplicator used to be {{R|Unique}}.
*{{R|Super}} Duplicator only affects the petal that's in the first slot. 
*There used to obly be {{R|Legendary}} Duplicator. 
*{{R|Super}} Duplicator doesn't affect Coral, despite it not being unstackable.
*[[Bone]] and [[Plank]] are the only two petals that don't eventually form a ring shape from {{Rarity|Super}} Duplicator if continued, instead forming an X.
{{Talents}}
[[Category:Talents]][[Category:Unique Talents]]

'''Health''' is a [[Talents|talent]] from [https://florr.io florr.io]. Its function consists of increasing base flower health. Health can be purchased with its base cost of 2 TP.

==Appearance==
Health's icon consists of a medical cross.

==Function==
{| class="article-table"
! '''Rarity'''
!'''Effect'''
|-
|{{Rarity|1}}
|Increases base Flower Health by 30%.
|-
|{{Rarity|2}}
|Increases base Flower Health from 130% to 169%.
|-
|{{Rarity|3}}
|Increases base Flower Health from 169% to 220%.
|-
|{{Rarity|4}}
|Increases base Flower Health from 220% to 286%.
|-
|{{Rarity|5}}
|Increases base Flower Health from 286% to 371%.
|-
|{{Rarity|6}}
|Increases base Flower Health from 371% to 483%.
|-
|{{Rarity|7}}
|Increases base Flower Health from 483% to 627%.
|-
|{{Rarity|8}}
|Increases base Flower Health from 627% to 816%.
|-
|{{Rarity|9}}
|Increases base Flower Health from 816% to 1060%.
|}

==Purchasing ==
This table will show the base cost. This cost is increased based on what rarity up to you want to buy.
{| class="article-table"
! '''Rarity'''
!'''Cost'''
!'''Requirements'''
|-
|{{Rarity|1}}
|2 TP
|N/A
|-
|{{Rarity|2}}
|5 TP
|{{Rarity|1}} Health
|-
|{{Rarity|3}}
|8 TP
|{{Rarity|2}} Health
|-
|{{Rarity|4}}
|11 TP
|{{Rarity|3}} Health
|-
|{{Rarity|5}}
|14 TP
|{{Rarity|4}} Health
|-
|{{Rarity|6}}
|17 TP
|{{Rarity|5}} Health
|-
|{{Rarity|7}}
|20 TP
|{{Rarity|6}} Health
|-
|{{Rarity|8}}
|23 TP
|{{Rarity|7}} Health
|-
|{{Rarity|9}}
|26 TP
|{{Rarity|8}} Health
|}

==Strategies==
*Health is an important talent for survival, as it allows you to survive mob swarms or attack. Pairing this with [[Talents/Sharp Edges|Sharp Edges]] can be extremely good.
*Some areas have mobs with high body damage. To survive, higher rarities of this talent can be purchased and this lets you survive dangerous attacks.
*Some mobs might kill you even if you have this talent. Use petals like [[Rubber]] (for [[Jellyfish]]).
*It is useless against higher rarity mobs as they can easily overwhelm you.
*Sponge can put you in risk since it reduces your max health to 50%, so be careful while using this petal.

== Gallery ==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">

File:Screenshot 2025-02-10 162436.png|Health in the Talents tree
</gallery>

== Trivia ==

* Health uses the Health normal icon from [https://game-icons.net/ https://game-icons.net].
* Health used to increase your HP by 25% per upgrade, but it was changed to multiply your base health by 1.3x.
* Having Eternal Health talent on can help you tank 1 hit from Super Wasps, Hornets and Mecha Wasps (124k health without any health petals)

'''Luck''' is a [[Talents|talent]] from [https://florr.io florr.io]. Its function consists of increasing your Luck by 0.2. Luck can be purchased with its base cost of 2 TP at the lowest rarity.

==Appearance==
Its appearance is similar to the [[Clover]] petal.

==Function==

{| class="article-table"
!'''Rarity'''
!'''Effect'''
|-
|{{Rarity|4}}
| rowspan="4" |Increases Luck by 0.2.

Luck is used by specific petals to change their stats.

Luck also increases the rarity of mobs that spawn around you.
|-
|{{Rarity|5}}
|-
|{{Rarity|6}}
|-
|{{Rarity|7}}
|}
Luck talent increases your Luck by 0.2, which is used by specific petals to increase the chances of doing an action: for example, [[Dice]].

Luck also increases the rarity of mobs that spawn around you. For instance, more Ultra mobs will likely spawn in Mythic zones.

==Purchasing==
This table will show the base cost. This cost is increased based on what rarity up to you want to buy.
{| class="article-table"
!'''Rarity'''
!'''Cost'''
!'''Requirements'''
|-
|{{Rarity|4}}
|2 TP
|
|-
|{{Rarity|5}}
|3 TP
|{{Rarity|4}} Luck
|-
|{{Rarity|6}}
|4 TP
|{{Rarity|5}} Luck
|-
|{{Rarity|7}}
|5 TP
|{{Rarity|6}} Luck
|}

==Strategies==
*Luck is recommended if one has a high rarity [[Dice]], to increase the chances of dealing crit damage.

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">
File:Luck (Epic).png|{{Rarity|4}} Luck.
File:Luck (Legendary).png|{{Rarity|5}} Luck.
File:Luck (Mythic).png|{{Rarity|6}} Luck.
File:Luck (Ultra).png|{{Rarity|7}} Luck.
</gallery>

==Trivia==
* The Luck skill was originally removed on December 26th, 2022, before being re-added on December 8th, 2023 with the renaming of Skills to Talents. Originally, each point of luck gave +0.3 luck: when it was re-added, it was nerfed to +0.2.
** Luck used to increase spawn rates of mobs, before increasing Dice crit chance. However, on October 14th, 2025, the ability of Luck to increase spawn rates of mobs was re-added.
*This, [[Talents/Antennae|Antennae]], [[Talents/Magnetism|Magnetism]] and [[Talents/Summoner|Summoner]] are the only talents that don't use icons from Game-Icons.net.
*Luck used to only have Ultra being the highest rarity of the talent.
*[[Card]] and [[Poker Chip]] are not affected by Luck despite being RNG-based petals.

'''Magnetism''' is a [[Talents|talent]] from [https://florr.io florr.io]. Its function consists of increasing petal pick up range by 1000 when alive. Magnetism can be purchased with its base cost of 30 TP at the lowest rarity and having purchased Legendary [[Talents/Loadout|Loadout]].

==Appearance==
It uses the same icon as the [[Magnet]] petal, however it is completely white.

==Function==

{| class="article-table"
!'''Rarity'''
!'''Effect'''
|-
|{{Rarity|6}}
|Increases petal pickup range by 1000 when you are alive.
|}
Magnetism's effect stacks additively with [[Magnet]], meaning it will provide an extra 1000 petal pick up range but only when the flower is alive.

==Purchasing==
This table will show the base cost. This cost is increased based on what rarity up to you want to buy.

!'''Rarity'''
!'''Cost'''
!'''Requirements'''
|-
|{{Rarity|6}}
|30 TP
|{{Rarity|5}} [[Talents/Loadout|Loadout]]
|}

==Strategies==
*Magnetism is useful if you do not want to use up a slot for your Magnet for collecting drops.
**Since it stacks with Magnet, pair this with a high rarity Magnet to get drops further away than you.
*Do note that this talent only functions while alive.

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">.
File:Magnetism (Mythic).png|{{Rarity|6}} Magnetism.
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*Magnetism's effect used to automatically pick up all petals dropped in a small map view range. However, due to it being overpowered, M28 decided to nerf the effect to 1000 extra pick up range.
*This, [[Talents/Antennae|Antennae]], [[Talents/Luck|Luck]] and [[Talents/Summoner|Summoner]] are the only talents that don't use icons from Game-Icons.net

title: Talents/Petal Health
content:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Petal Health}}
{{Stub}}{{WIP}}{{Talent Template Infobox|name=Petal Health|image=<gallery>
File:Health (Common).png|C
File:Health (Unusual).png|U

File:Health (Super).png|S</gallery>|first-rarity={{Rarity|1}}|description=Increases Petal Health from #.#% to #.#%|special=Increases health of petals by percentage.}}'''Petal Health''' is a [[Talents|talent]] from [https://florr.io florr.io]. Its function consists of increasing Petal Health by a certain percentage. Petal Health can be purchased with its base cost of 2 TP at the lowest rarity.

== Appearance ==
Petal Health consists of a white circle (appearing as a [[Basic]]) and a cross symbol.

== Function ==
{| class="article-table"
!'''Rarity'''
!'''Effect'''
|-
|{{R|1}}
|Increases petal health from 100% to 115%.
|-
|{{R|2}}
|Increases petal health from 115% to 132.25%.
|-
|{{R|3}}
|Increases petal health from 132.25% to 152.09%.
|-
|{{R|4}}
|Increases petal health from 152.09% to 174.9%.
|-
|{{R|5}}
|Increases petal health from 174.9% to 201.14%.
|-
|{{R|6}}
|Increases petal health from 201.14% to 231.31%.
|-
|{{R|7}}
|Increases petal health from 231.31% to 266%.
|-
|{{R|8}}
|Increases petal health from 266% to 305.9%.
|-
|{{R|9}}
|Increases petal health from 305.9% to 404.56%.
|}
Petal Health talent increases the Health stat of a petal (if it exists) by a certain percentage, depending on the talent rarity. Petals that are designed to have one Health, including [[Bubble]], [[Coral]], [[Fang]], [[Stinger]], [[Blood Stinger]] and [[Stinger/Magic|Magic Stinger]], are not affected by this talent.

== Purchasing ==
This table will show the base cost. This cost is increased based on what rarity up to you want to buy.
{| class="article-table"

!'''Cost'''
!'''Requirements'''

|{{R|1}}
|2 TP
|N/A

|-

|{{R|7}} Petal Health
|-
|{{R|9}}
|18 TP
|{{R|8}} Petal Health
|}
''Total cost: 90 TP''

== Strategies ==
*Petal Health talent is mostly beneficial to petals with high base Health stats, for example [[Corn]], [[Pearl]], [[Rock]] and [[Sawblade]], as it offers a percentage increase rather than a fixed amount of increment.
*When choosing between Petal Health and [[Talents/Reload|Reload]] talents, always prioritize Reload talent and spend the excess TP for Petal Health, which should be regarded as a secondary means to improve a petal's damage output.

'''Petal Rotation''' is a [[Talents|talent]] from [https://florr.io florr.io]. Its function consists of increasing the petal rotation speed. Petal Rotation can be purchased with its base cost of 1 TP.

==Appearance==
Petal Rotation's icon consists of a windmill.

==Function==
{| class="article-table"
! '''Rarity'''
!'''Effect'''
|-
|{{Rarity|1}}
|Increases petal Rotation Speed from 2.5 to 3.1 rad/s.
|-
|{{Rarity|2}}
|Increases petal Rotation Speed from 3.1 to 3.7 rad/s.
|-
|{{Rarity|3}}
|Increases petal Rotation Speed from 3.7 to 4.3 rad/s.
|-
|{{Rarity|4}}
|Increases petal Rotation Speed from 4.3 to 4.9 rad/s.
|-
|{{Rarity|5}}
|Increases petal Rotation Speed from 4.9 to 5.5 rad/s.
|}
Increases the petal rotation speed by +0.6 rad/s each rarity from 2.5 rad/s to 5.5 rad/s.

==Purchasing ==
This table will show the base cost. This cost is increased based on what rarity up to you want to buy.
{| class="article-table"
! '''Rarity'''
!'''Cost'''
!'''Requirements'''
|-
|{{Rarity|1}}
|1 TP
|
|-
|{{Rarity|2}}
|2 TP
|{{Rarity|1}} Petal Rotation
|-
|{{Rarity|3}}
|3 TP
|{{Rarity|2}} Petal Rotation
|-
|{{Rarity|4}}
|4 TP
|{{Rarity|3}} Petal Rotation
|-
|{{Rarity|5}}
|5 TP
|{{Rarity|4}} Petal Rotation
|-
|}

==Strategies==
*Specific combinations of [[Faster]] and Petal Rotation can result in the perfect rotation speed to deal max DPS. Anything too much or too little will result in overrotation or underrotation. Combine this talent with Reload talent to generally increase your DPS.
*Petal Rotation can negatively affect Bubble/Yin Yang builds so it's recommended to decrease the effect by pressing Q.

*Petal Rotation uses the Twirly flower icon from https://www.game-icons.net.

'''Reach''' is a [[Talents|talent]] from [https://florr.io florr.io]. Its function consists of increasing petal Attack Range by 50. Reach can be purchased with its base cost of 3 TP at the lowest rarity and having purchased {{Rarity|2}} Loadout.

== Appearance==
'''Reach'''<nowiki/>'s icon is a circle with two crescent shapes around it.

==Function==
{| class="article-table"
!'''Rarity'''
!'''Effect'''
|-
|{{Rarity|3}}
| rowspan="3" |Increases petal Attack Range by 50.
|-
|{{Rarity|6}}
|-
|{{Rarity|8}}
|}
Reach increases petal Attack Range by 50.

==Purchasing==
This table will show the base cost. This cost is increased based on what rarity up to you want to buy.
{| class="article-table"
!'''Rarity'''
!'''Cost'''
!'''Requirements'''
|-
|{{Rarity|3}}
|3 TP
|Unusual Loadout
|-
|{{Rarity|6}}
|6 TP
|Rare Reach
|-
|{{Rarity|8}}
|9 TP
|Mythic Reach
|}

==Strategies==
*Reach can be useful for replicating effects of a [[Third Eye]], since this petal is hard to obtain. At full Reach talents, being 150 extra petal Attack Range, it is possible to replicate the effects of an {{Rarity|7}} Third Eye.
**However, the talent's effect stacks with Third Eye, making it more useful. However, the petals that are further away than you may cause some mobs to go right through your petals.
*This is useful when handling high body damage mobs, unless if you are using a ramming build.

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">

File:Reach (Mythic).png|{{Rarity|6}} Reach.

</gallery>

==Trivia==
*Reach uses the Concentration orb icon from https://game-icons.net.
*Reach is one of the only talents that skips the main order of Rarities: skipping {{Rarity|1}} to {{Rarity|2}}, then {{Rarity|4}} to {{Rarity|5}}, and {{Rarity|7}} rarities. It's only obtainable at {{Rarity|3}}, {{Rarity|6}} and {{Rarity|8}} rarities.

content:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Reload}}
{{Talent Template Infobox
| name = Reload
| image = <gallery>

| first-rarity = {{Rarity|1}}
| description = * Decreases petal reload time from -#% to -#%.
* Decreases petal reload time by -#%.
* Decreases minimum reload time when swapping petals from #s to #s.
| special = Makes your petals reload faster.
}}

'''Reload''' is a [[Talents|talent]] from [https://florr.io florr.io]. Its function consists decreasing the petal reload. Reload can be purchased with its base cost of 2 TP.

==Appearance==
Reload's icon consists of an arrow that loops almost fully around to the place it started.

==Function==
{| class="article-table"
! '''Rarity'''
!'''Effect'''
|-
|{{Rarity|1}}
| Decreases petal reload time by -10%.
Decreases minimum reload time when swapping petals from 2.5s to 2s.
|-
|{{Rarity|2}}
|Decreases petal reload time from -10% to -19%.
Decreases minimum reload time when swapping petals from 2s to 1.5s.
|-
|{{Rarity|3}}
|Decreases petal reload time from -19% to -27.1%.
Decreases minimum reload time when swapping petals from 1.5s to 1s.
|-
|{{Rarity|4}}
|Decreases petal reload time from -27.1% to -34.39%.
Decreases minimum reload time when swapping petals from 1s to 0.5s.
|-
|{{Rarity|5}}
|Decreases petal reload time from -34.39% to -47.51%.
Decreases minimum reload time when swapping petals from 0.5 to 0s.
|-
|{{Rarity|6}}
|Decreases petal reload time from -47.51% to -58.01%.
|-
|{{Rarity|7}}
|Decreases petal reload time from -58.01% to -66.41%.
|-
|{{Rarity|8}}
|Decreases petal reload time from -66.41% to -73.13%.
|-
|'''Eternal'''
|Decreases petal reload time from -73.13% to -81.19%.
|}
The Reload talent decreases the time it takes for your petals to reload. For example, if you have {{Rarity|8}} reload, a [[Moon Rock]] will load in 26.87s, rather than 100.0s, which is its normal reload time.

==Purchasing ==
This table will show the base cost. This cost is increased based on what rarity up to you want to buy.
{| class="article-table"
! '''Rarity'''
!'''Cost'''
!'''Requirements'''
|-
|{{Rarity|1}}
|2 TP
|
|-
|{{Rarity|2}}
|6 TP
|{{Rarity|1}} Reload
|-
|{{Rarity|3}}
|10 TP
|{{Rarity|2}} Reload
|-
|{{Rarity|4}}
|12 TP
|{{Rarity|3}} Reload
|-
|{{Rarity|5}}
|16 TP
|{{Rarity|4}} Reload
|-
|{{Rarity|6}}
|20 TP
|{{Rarity|5}} Reload
|-
|{{Rarity|7}}
|24 TP
|{{Rarity|6}} Reload
|-
|{{Rarity|8}}
|28 TP
|{{Rarity|7}} Reload
|-
|'''Eternal'''
|32 TP
|Super Reload
|}

==Strategies==
*Reload is useful for petals if you do not want to wait long for a petal to spawn. This also increases DPS on certain petals.
**By using [[Golden Leaf]], you can effectively reduce a delay of your petals to spawn, causing a DPS boost.
*At lower rarities, it will reduce minimum reload time when you are swapping petals, increasing time for yor petals to spawn.
*Use it for your bubbling build, removing not-so-needed stuff like Range talents, Magnetism, and Petal Rotation. Remove other talents if you want, but you should probably prioritize keeping your health.

*

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">
File:Reload (Common).png|{{Rarity|1}} Reload.
File:Reload (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|2}} Reload.
File:Reload (Rare).png|{{Rarity|3}} Reload.
File:Reload (Epic).png|{{Rarity|4}} Reload.
File:Reload (Legendary).png|{{Rarity|5}} Reload.
File:Reload (Mythic).png|{{Rarity|6}} Reload.
File:Reload (Ultra).png|{{Rarity|7}} Reload.
File:Reload (Super).png|{{Rarity|8}} Reload.
File:Quick Hands.png|Old Reload, also known as Quick Hands.
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*This talent used to be called "Quick Hands" and had the icon of a hand reaching towards a bag. It, instead of reducing reload speed, reduced the time taken to load in petals initially. Its original icon used the Robber hand icon from https://game-icons.net.
*Around when Eternal rarity was added, the base cost of this talent was 6 TP, but then it was quickly dropped to 2 TP and now its cost drastically increases over rarity, making it practically useless for your main build, especially because the reload decrease decreases by ~1% every level.
*At maximum tier, Reload is the most expensive in Talent Points to buy, at 150 in total.

|rarities={{Rarity|5}} and {{Rarity|6}}|cost={{Infobox Colors Empty|||||5 TP|10 TP|||}}|requirement={{Infobox Colors Empty|||||Rare Health|Legendary Second Chance|||}}}}'''Second Chance''' is a [[Talents|talent]] from [https://florr.io florr.io]. Its function consists of ignoring a single fatal attack for a number of seconds of invincibility, but with a cooldown of number amount of seconds. Second Chance can be purchased with its base cost of 5 TP at the lowest rarity and having purchased Rare [[Health]].

==Appearance==
Second Chance's icon consists of arrow with the outline of a heart.

== Function ==
{| class="article-table"
|+Talent Stats
!'''Rarity'''
!'''Effect'''
|-
|{{Rarity|Legendary}}
|Prevents death for 0.3s upon taking fatal damage. Has a 60s cooldown.
|-
|{{Rarity|Mythic}}
|Increases duration to 1.5s, decreases cooldown to 30s.
|-
|}Second Chance will grant you a short invulnerability period after you take damage that exceeds your current Health. However, there is a cooldown once this Talent is triggered.
==Obtainment==
This table will show the base cost. This cost is increased based on what rarity up to you want to buy.
{| class="article-table"
! '''Rarity'''
!'''Cost'''
!'''Requirements'''
|-
|{{Rarity|5}}
|5 TP
|{{Rarity|Rare}} [[Health]]
|-
|{{Rarity|6}}
|10 TP
|{{Rarity|Legendary}} Second Chance
|}

==Strategies==
*Second Chance can be paired with [[Sponge]]: when Second Chance is activated, swap out the Sponge for a different petal to avoid taking potentially fatal damage from the Sponge spread. This tactic is extraordinarily useful, making you almost invincible if you know how to properly use Sponges. {{Rarity|Mythic}} Second Chance is highly recommended for this, as it has half the cooldown and it provides immunity to damage for 1.5s, so you'll have 1.5s instead of 0.3s to unequip your Sponge.
*This can help you survive for longer when dealing with high instant damage, but it is less useful when dealing with poison.
*It is good for mob swarms, as you will have more time to kill them before they will overwhelm you. Using reliable petals might also keep you safe.

== Gallery ==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">
File:Second Chance (Legendary).png|{{Rarity|5}} Second Chance.
File:Second Chance (Mythic).png|{{Rarity|6}} Second Chance.
</gallery>
==Trivia==
*Second Chance uses the Self love icon from https://game-icons.net.
*Second Chance is one of the three talents that branches from a talent that is NOT one rarity lower than it ({{Rarity|3}} -> {{Rarity|5}}).
**The other talents that do this are Reach ({{Rarity|3}} -> {{Rarity|6}} -> {{Rarity|8}}) and Antennae ({{Rarity|1}} -> {{Rarity|3}}).

}}
The '''Target Dummy''' is a [[Mobs|mob]] in florr.io that was added on December 22, 2023. Currently the only target dummies are in [[Jungle]], surrounding the hut spawn zone. 

==Appearance==
It looks like a [[Flower]], except that the color is grey, and the white pupils in the eyes are squares. The mouth is a straight horizontal line. {{R|Super}} Target Dummies have no large health bar on the top of the screen for health destroying reasons (it'd be useless as you can't kill it, which is the whole point of the bar --- tracking when it'll die / tracking HP). 

== Stats & Behavior ==
{| class="article-table"
![[Rarity]]
!Health

!Body Damage
!Armor
|-
|{{Rarity|Common}}
| rowspan="8" |10,000,000,000 HP
|10 HP
|0.8
|-
|{{Rarity|Unusual}}
|30 HP
|2.4
|-
|{{Rarity|Rare}}
|90 HP
|7.2
|-
|{{Rarity|Epic}}
|270 HP
|21.6 
|-
|{{Rarity|Legendary}}
|810 HP
|64.8 
|-
|{{Rarity|Mythic}}
|2,430 HP
|194.4
|-
|{{Rarity|Ultra}}
|7,290 HP
| rowspan="2" |583.2
|-
|{{Rarity|Super}}
|21,870 HP
|}The Target Dummy seemingly is invulnerable and has higher health than any other mob in the game. In addition, M28, the developer, confirmed that the Dummy heals itself back to full health every time it gets damaged or a tick of the game has passed, even when turned pale by [[dandelion]]. Damage received by the Dummy is marked as "non-lethal" by the game, and so it can't kill it. The Dummy is immobile, and is not affected by heavy or jelly. Petals within a certain distance of the dummy will automatically go toward the dummy. 

If a total of 10 billion damage was inflicted into a Target Dummy, it'll say these following lines, in the following order: 

* Ouch
* I mean
* Beep boop
* I'm a robot

==Spawning==
There are 8 Dummies in [[Jungle]], each one spaced out equally in a semicircle-like pattern {{Rarity|8}} Target Dummies are on right, and the rarities will decrease in a counter-clockwise direction.

==Drops==
M28 has confirmed that killing a Dummy will grant you a [[Square]].

==Strategy==
So far there is no known way to kill the Dummies, unless you count M28 deleting the dummies. As the Dummy heals every time it takes damage even when bombarded by [[Dandelion|dandelion petals]], so the only way to kill it is by one-shotting it with something that has over 10,000,000,000 damage, which can only be done with hacks Usually, players use the Target Dummy to find their DPS and update their build, which is the whole point of the mob.

==Trivia==
*M28 has confirmed that killing a Dummy will grant you a [[Square]].
*Very few players have Target Dummies in their gallery, due to M28 killing the dummy accidentally within their loot range and them having dealt enough damage to loot it.
*Target Dummies are one of the only mobs that is not a Special Entity to have quotes, the others are [[Gambler]].
*Being killed by a Target Dummy gives you the “You’re the dummy” achievement.
*Since Target Dummies talk when you deal 10 billion damage to them, they technically count as NPCs.
*Target Dummies do not have the highest health out of any mob. The {{Rarity|Unique}} [[Ant Hole]] takes on this title (14,762,250,000 HP).

The {{PAGENAME}} is a mob in florr.io. It was released in the 25th December 2023 update, Like all other ant eggs, it is stationary, and has a chance of hatching Baby Termites upon death.
==Appearance==
The Termite Egg shares the appearance of [[Ant Egg (Mob)|Ant Egg mob]] without any changes, looks similar to [[Pearl]].
==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Stationary

'''Behavior:''' When defeated, has a 33% chance of summoning a hostile Baby Termite of the same rarity as the egg. When a termite egg is damaged, it will aggro nearby termites onto the player.

==Spawning==
The {{PAGENAME}} spawns randomly in the right section of [[Ant Hell]] (Termite Hell/AH3). Termite Eggs may spawn all across the Termite Hell map.
==Drops==

{{:Termite_Egg/Drop_Ant_Egg}}

===[[Amulet]]===

{{:Termite_Egg/Drop_Amulet}}

==Strategy==
* The same as other ant eggs, but be prepared to fight off soldier/worker/baby termites that become aggroed when you attack the termite egg.
* [[Bone]]s of the same rarity can kill Termite Eggs without losing health. (Example: A {{Rarity|Mythic}} Bone can kill a {{Rarity|Mythic}} Termite Egg.)
* All types of Ant Eggs can easily be rammed because of their low mob damage value.

==Trivia==
*Termite Eggs were supposed to make their appearance in the termite update, but however was postponed.
**People used to joke about the non-existent Termite egg.
*In 26th December 2023, M28 accidentally spawned 1,000 Ultra Termite Eggs in Ellne.
*Termite Egg is the only mob that shares another mob's design without any changes.
*Termite Eggs do not seem to share a psyonic connection (damage) with other termites (egg included) but if you attack one, the termites would chase you.
*On an unknown date, a super termite egg randomly spawned in the legendary tunnel zone (shown above). 
*If you attack a termite egg, soldier and worker termites will run away if you attack behind a wall or underground. This does not apply to baby termites as they are passive.

The '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is a mob in florr.io. It behaves as an entrance for the [[Termite Mound (Area)|Termite Mound area]].

==Appearance==
A faded light brown variant of an Ant Hole. Its healthbar is yellow (invincible) due to it not being able to be damaged.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed: ''' Immobile

'''Behavior:''' Behaves as an entrance for every rarity.

Termite Mounds can be entered, unlike Ant Holes and Fire Ant Holes. The size of the map inside the Termite Mound depends on the rarity of the Termite Mound.

==Spawning==
[[File:Mythic Termite Mound ( bottom left).png|thumb|'''Mythic Termite Mound ( bottom left)''']]
The {{PAGENAME}} spawns in [[Jungle]] as a rare spawn. It spawns more frequently in the corridors branching out from the bottom, opposite to the Ant Hell portal.
== Drops==

=== [[Relic]] ===
{{:Termite Mound/Drop Relic}}

===[[Soil]]===
{{:Termite Mound/Drop Soil}}

===[[Rubber]]===
{{:Termite Mound/Drop Rubber}}

==Strategy==
*Termite Mounds are pretty hard to find because of [[Wasp|Wasps]], [[Mantis|Mantises]], [[Leafbug|Leafbugs]], [[Firefly|Fireflies]], [[Ladybug (Jungle)|Dark Ladybugs]] and [[Centipede (Jungle)|Evil Centipedes]].
*Termites have the same health and  same attack damage as garden ants, so [[Bone|Bones]] 1 or more rarities higher than the Termite Mound work the best against them.
*Do not use overleveled [[Blood Stinger|Blood Stingers]] In Termite Mounds. Instead, use [[Lightning]].
*Be careful as you can sometimes spawn right next to or even on high level Termites. For higher-rarity Mounds, this is more unlikely because of how big they are, but it's still possible and you can find yourself dead in seconds.
*Try not to enter these during a {{Rarity|Super}} fight, because you'll likely have to bubble all the way to the super again, wasting precious time and causing you to not loot the super.

==Trivia==

*Termite Mound is the only mob that can be entered at every rarity,
**Others that used to behave as an entrance were [[Ant Hole]] and [[Fire Ant Burrow]].
*Termite Mound is the only Ant Hole to have a leader spawning inside of the mob to not be a queen: it is [[Termite Overmind]] instead.
**Termites are also only ant families that don't have a queen variant, they have an Overmind variant.
**Termite Mounds used to say "Ant Hole" under the minimap.
