title: Termite Mound (Area)
content:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Termite Mound}} {{Background|图片=AHb.png|大小=2000px}} {{Area Template Infobox |name = Termite Mound |image = File:Ant Hole (C, U, R).png|C/U/R File:Ant Hole (Area) (Epic).png|E File:Ant Hole (Area) (Legendary).png|L File:Ant Hole (M, U, S).png|M/Ul/S/Uq |caption-image = |mobs = Termite Mound: *[[Baby Termite]] *[[Worker Termite]] *[[Soldier Termite]] *[[Termite Overmind]] *[[Digger]] |special_entity = |connections = None |map_size = Changes every rarity (From smaller to larger sizes) |distance_from_spawn = Depends on where the termite mound is. }}
''Not to be confused with the [[Termite Mound|Termite Mound mob]].''
The '''Termite Mound''' is an area in florr.io. It can be entered by entering through [[Termite Mound|Termite Mounds]]. ==Appearance== The background and walls are dirt. The map size increases each time the rarity gets higher. ==Location == The Termind Mound is not an area in the standard sense. Instead of existing in a certain location, a new Termite Mound area opens up whenever a [[Termite Mound]] spawns, and you can only enter it through them. ==Connections== This area doesn't have any [[Portal]] connections. It can be entered by touching all rarities of [[Termite Mound]]s. It can only be exited by killing all of the mobs inside, which sends all players inside back to the location that they came from and giving them their drops on the outside. If all of the Termites are not defeated within 5 minutes, the Termite Mound 'expires' and all Termites and players are sent outside, but without receiving the Termite Mound drops. {| class="article-table" |+ Connections !Mob !Connection Location (Entering) !Connection Location (Exiting) |- |[[Termite Mound]] |Any area that the mob spawned in. |Exits to original location
after clearing area. |} ==Spawning== {| class="article-table" |+Amount of mobs per Termite Mound !Mob !Overmind !Soldier !Worker !Baby |- | [[Termite Mound]] |1 |26 |8 |5 |} A total of '''40''' mobs spawn in the Termite Mound area. The [[Termite Overmind]] always spawns as the same rarity as its mound. Diggers have a 10% chance of spawning when the area is entered. The rarity of the Digger matches the rarity of Termite Mound. ===Special Zones=== There are no special zones in this area. ==Strategy== *[[Lightning]] can be very effective to clear every Termite in this location without wasting time trying to get to every Termite. **Lightning can damage many mobs at once, but since Termites have Psyonic Connection, they can create groups that aggro other Termites to kill you. **Additionally, the map size of the area becomes too big, making it difficult to locate where the Termites are. *[[Uranium]] could also kill Termites easily, but this can be risky due to self poison damage and Psyonic Connection. **Uraniums are fatal if you do not use [[Lotus]] so it is highly recommended to use few of them when using it to locate Termites. *[[Bone|Bones]] can be useful for dealing Termites, as they can have high DPS to kill them. *Use [[Bulb]] or [[Honey]] to make Termites go to you or to the Honey especially in high rarities of Termite Mound. ==Gallery== File:Ant Hole Rarities.png|Ant Hole area rarities. File:Ahole.png|A map of different ant hole rarities. (outdated) File:Antholeraritiesv2.png|The different shapes of Ant Hole areas. (outdated) ==Tiles== File:dirt2_c_0.svg File:dirt2_c_1.svg ==Trivia == * This area, [[Hel]], [[PvP]] and [[Worm (Area)|Worm]] are the one of the only areas that don't have any [[portal]] connections. *Before the April 29, 2024 update, [[Ant Hole|Ant Holes]] and [[Fire Ant Burrow|Fire Ant Burrows]] used to be enterable and would teleport you to this area, though the areas were called "Ant Hole" and "Fire Ant Burrow" respectively. Now, Termite Mound is the only type of ant hole that can be entered. *{{Rarity|1}} to {{Rarity|3}} Termite Mounds are the smallest areas in the game. {{Areas}} [[Category:Areas]] [[Category:Ant Areas]] [[Category:Jungle]]

The '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is a mob in florr.io. It is one of the bulkiest and most durable mobs.
==Appearance==
It looks much like a [[Baby Termite]], but a lot larger. The pincers at the front are also more curved inward compared to that of a [[Baby Ant]].
==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Very slow.

'''Behavior:''' Passive until it is attacked or other termites spread damage to it through Psyonic Connection. Will lock onto the nearest player and move toward them very slowly. Any damage taken will be spread evenly to other termites if nearby. While passive, it rotates in clockwise circles.

Like other termites, it has psyonic connection that shares damage with termites. Super Termite Overminds can disable your slots randomly, making any petal in the disabled slot inactive for a period of time. It can disable up to 7 of your slots at once.
==Spawning==
The {{PAGENAME}} spawns in the termite section of [[Ant Hell]]. It also spawns inside of [[Termite Mound|termite mounds]] found across [[Jungle|Jungle,]] with its rarity being the same as the mound it is found in.

==Drops==
=== [[Tomato]]===
{{:Termite Overmind/Drop Tomato}}

===[[Relic]]===
{{:Termite Overmind/Drop Relic}}

===[[Compass]]===
{{:Template:ID 1 - Ul}}

==Strategy== 

*The Overmind has a lot of health compared to all other {{Rarity|Ultra}} mobs but is very slow as compensation. Stay at a distance from it as it has body damage. If any termites or Worms spawn nearby, take them out as fast as possible to as the real danger when fighting the Overmind are Soldier Termites sharing their damage with the Overmind and overwhelming you. Worms are also a threat because they are fast and always burrow underground.
*Be careful not to get crushed if it's passing a tunnel. You can use [[Bubble|Bubbles]] to get a distance away.
*When the Termite Overmind disables one of your slots containing a strong petal, you can swap out a weaker petal that hasn't been disabled with the stronger petal.
*Termite Overminds also have Psyonic Connection, which allows to damage other Termites around it to deal damage to the Overmind, and vice versa
*Do not hide in corners or dead ends as though it is slow, a Termite Overmind can easily trap you when it is closing in on you and does not aggro on someone else.
*Termite Overminds are weak if they are alone. Kill mobs around it first and then you can treat it almost like a stationary mob. All the same, try to fight it in an open area.

==Trivia==
*In May 10th 2024 update, the description of the word "sees" was changed to "oversees".
*A lot of people refer this mob as just "Overmind".
*This mob has the largest size of every mob in the game, with slightly being larger than Queen Ants.
*It used to have the most health of any mob in the game, however [[Ant Hole]] takes that rank now.
**[[Digger|Diggers]] of the same rarity can still kill this mob and survive due to having higher damage.
*This is probably one of the slowest non-stationary mobs in the entire game, along with the [[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble]]
*On Ringo's Map in the old World Map, there used to be a yellow text in the top-right corner of Ant Hell 3. It said "chubby boi", which referenced Termite Overmind.
**It was added in March 5th 2023, but was removed in April 11th 2023.
*Before the 1st January 2025 update, the Termite Overmind could only spawn as {{R|Ultra}} or above in Ant Hell. After the Ant Hell map update, they can now spawn in any rarity.
*This is the first mob that can tamper with your petals, and deactivate them for longer than its reload cooldown. Second was [[Jellyfish]], but instead, it disables all of your petals after getting hit by the shockwave ability.
*Termite Overminds have 1,000 times higher base health than of a [[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble mob]].
*Overminds are often used as a florr joke similie, as fat as a overmind, as these mobs are very large.
*The community jokes about calling it [https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC63anZxfVGHUEmfBAf5w7pw Caseoh] Termite, beacuse [https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC63anZxfVGHUEmfBAf5w7pw Caseoh] is known to get jokes about how they are both chubby.
*On 24th September 2025, XP reward was buffed and the [[Tomato]] drop was buffed to be the same as [[Air]] from [[Bubble (Mob)|Bubble]]. Now none of the mob drops is not below that 1% (at Ultra rarity). Compass drop rate become 2% again.

title: Titan
content:
{{Background|图片=Jungleb.png|大小=2000px}} {{Mob Template Infobox | name = {{PAGENAME}} | image = Titan.png | rarity = {{Rarity|Unique}} | health = Invulnerable | move = No | status = Passive | attack = | spawn = [[Jungle]] | special = Allows you to forge 5 {{Rarity|Super}} petals into a {{Rarity|Unique}} petal. | damage = Very high (unknown) }} The '''Titan''' is a [[:Category:Special Entities|Special Entity]] in [[florr.io]]. It appears in the bottom right of [[Jungle]] and can be used to forge {{Rarity|Super}} petals into {{Rarity|Unique}} petals. ==Appearance== The Titan appears as a gray Flower with a dark gray outline, as well as a bevel that is coloured the same colour as its outline, of which bevel has sharp bumps not unlike the [[Trader]]'s. It also looks angry because it is consistently in the offensive position. The Titan has ten randomly selected {{Rarity|Unique}} petals (besides square, beetle egg, ant egg, or corruption) equipped that orbit around it: if the equipped petal is wearable, it will appear on the Titan instead of around it: for example, it can wear a [[Cutter]], [[Antennae]], [[Third Eye]], [[Third Eye/Magic|Magic Eye]], etc. It can also move faster when equipping [[Powder]], and gets poisoned when it equips [[Uranium]], but doesn't take damage. When it wears a [[Disc]], instead of a thin black circle appearing around the Titan, its entire outline turns black. Interestingly, while other NPCs are {{Rarity|Common}} rarity, the Titan is of the {{Rarity|Unique}} rarity. ==Interface== When you get closer to the Titan, the Crafting menu will turn into the Forge menu. The Forge menu allows you to insert {{Rarity|Super}} petals to forge into a {{Rarity|Unique}} petal: but only one {{Rarity|Unique}} petal of this type can exist. Once someone else crafts another {{Rarity|Unique}} petal of the same rarity, you lose the {{Rarity|Unique}} petal and have all but one of your {{Rarity|Super}} petals refunded. To reforge, someone must wait a certain period of time depending on how frequently the {{Rarity|Unique}} is forged, 1 hour for something frequently forged, and 2 weeks for something almost never forged. This strict reforge duration sometimes leads to forge fights, where the person to click the fastest gets the {{Rarity|Unique}}. For example: Manfred crafts a {{Rarity|Unique}} [[Pincer]] with 5 {{Rarity|Super}} Pincers. Luai2 now can craft another {{Rarity|Unique}} Pincer with 5 {{Rarity|Super}}: once Luai2 does this, Manfred loses their {{Rarity|Unique}} Pincer and receives 4 {{Rarity|Super}} Pincers back. To re-forge the Pincer, Manfred must now use 5 {{Rarity|Super}} Pincers again, and wait the appropriate duration before reforging. The interface is similar to the [[Trader|Trade]] interface: however, it is gray in colour and it only displays your {{Rarity|Super}} petals. If you have no Supers, no petals will display and the text reads: You do not have any super petals to attempt this. The text below reads:
'''{{Text shadow|Unique petals will last until another player forges the same petal.}}
{{Text shadow|When that happens, you will receive your Super petals back,}}
{{Text shadow|but one will be permanently destroyed.}}'''
{{Game Info}} ==Lines== Much like [[Trader]], [[Gambler]], and [[Oracle]], the Titan uses the [[Chat]] system to periodically speak, its lines are: *As the Shaper wills *Formed of the forge *I stride the terrene plane *I was the hammer at the Founding *The forge of creation burns hotter *The Worldsmith wanders *There can be only one *What is weak must break ==Gallery== [[File:Screenshot 2025-03-27 5.29.05 PM.png|thumb]] File:Forge Menu with no supers.png|[[File:Titan with new froge menu while don't have.png|thumb|A New forge menu]]The Forge Menu UI when you don't have any {{Rarity|Super}} petals. File:Forge Menu.png|Like before, but without the red text. File:Forge Menu with Supers.png|Forge Menu with the available {{Rarity|Super}} petals. File:Old Forge Menu.png|An old forge menu. File:Older Forge Menu.png|An even older forge menu. File:2024-12-20 (1).png|Titan appearance with {{Rarity|Ultra}} [[Antennae]] File:2024-12-20 (3).png|Titan appearance compared with the flower normal POV. File:2024-12-19 (5).png|Old Titan File:Corrupt titan.png|Titan when corrupted using {{Rarity|Unique}} [[Corruption]] or a {{Rarity|Unique}} [[Gambler]] is nearby. Notice its different pupils. File:BRUHYOU.jpg|Titan’s health bar, showing it as an NPC. File:Magic Titan.png|Titan equipping a {{Rarity|Unique}} Magic Eye. File:Titan Shell.png|A player being healed by Titan using a Shell. File:Titan.png|Titan - transparent image File:Uniques.png|2 players trying to get and keep {{Rarity|Unique}} [[Yucca]]. File:Twotitans.png|A server crash or bug caused two Titans to spawn. File:IMG 2454.webp|Like the previous. File:Fourtitans.png|Another server crash or bug caused four Titans to spawn. File:Common Titan Spotted In Jungle Event.jpg|{{R|1}} Titan spotted in an event. ==Trivia== *Titan has many traits that are related to the {{Rarity|9}} Rarity. *The only petal the Titan doesn't forge is Pharaoh's Crown and [[Champion's Crown]], as it can be dropped, while {{Rarity|Super}} one cannot be obtained normally. *Titan is currently the third NPC to be added in game. Others were [[Oracle]] and [[Trader]]. *If the Titan comes in contact with a {{Rarity|Super}} or {{Rarity|Unique}} [[Gambler]], it will become a Gambler, though the only visual change is its eyes. **It is unknown if it can get aggressive to players or not upon corruption. *The Titan can interact with players with its petals. **If the Titan has {{Rarity|Unique}} [[Yggdrasil]]/[[Mark]] equipped, it can revive players or send players to [[Hel]]. ***If you are marked by the Titan, you won't see "You were marked by ..." in Chat. ****If you use "/print-players" near the Titan without any flowers nearby, you won't see "No flowers close enough to you." in Chat. ***You can no longer be marked by Titan, as a player named Craft_Super has forged it, effectively stealing the petal. **If the Titan has {{Rarity|Unique}} [[Rose]]/[[Dahlia]] equipped, it can heal you with them. *One of its quotes "There can be only one" is a reference that there can only be one {{Rarity|Unique}} petal of each type in the game. **Its quote is also a reference to the description of the "Cyclops" skin from digdig.io. *Titan is the only NPC that can deal damage, as it kills nearby hostile mobs. Its petals do not deal damage, but it has lots of body damage. **Titan is also known for having massive body damage, it can kill an {{Rarity|Ultra}} in second. *There was a bug when Titan equips {{Rarity|Unique}} [[Laser]], {{Rarity|Unique}} Laser could damage mobs. *When Titan equips {{Rarity|Unique}} Mimic, it will copy the petal that is on the left of it, thus making a interesting visual: 2 {{R|Unique}} petals are equipped. *Titan is unable to equip any petals that summon mobs, like [[Ant Egg]], [[Beetle Egg]], [[Mysterious Stick|Stick]], and [[Square]], even if no one forged these petals. It can also only equip {{Rarity|Unique}} petals that have not yet been forged by other players: once a {{Rarity|Unique}} petal is forged, the Titan can no longer equip it. It will have no visible petals once all the {{Rarity|Unique}} petals it can equip are forged. **This is likely due to the fact that the player that last forged the {{Rarity|9}} petal still has that petal. *On August 20, 2025, the game servers crashed or bugged, causing two Titans to spawn in Asia [[Jungle]] server. **Three days later, another major server crash or bug occurred, which caused four Titans to spawn in Europe Jungle server. *On October 25, 2025, a number of {{Rarity|Unique}} petals had their costs reset due to former owners being permanently banned in-game. The table above will be updated to reflect those changes. *Before November 25, 2025, the cost of a {{Rarity|Unique}} petal would increase by 1 based on how many that petal has been forged globally. After this, the Titan was temporarily removed and then it was changed to have static cost of 5 Supers. This likely reduced farming times but also meant that your {{Rarity|Unique}} can easily be stolen unless if it is an extremely rare one. *Players often lure {{Rarity|Super}} mobs to Titan because it deals infinite damage. This will take less time to defeat. {{Mobs}} [[Category:Mobs]][[Category:Unique+]] [[Category:Non-stationary mobs]] [[Category:Special Entities]] [[Category:Interactive Entities]] [[Category:Giant Mobs]]

'''Tomato''' is a petal in florr.io. It can increase in size and damage. In other words, the bigger its size is, the more damage it does. Its health always stays the same no matter the size.

==Appearance==
It looks like a tomato. Has a red round body with green calyx on top.

This petal that increases in size and damage after reloading, until it reaches its max size/dmg.

==Obtaining==

===[[Termite Overmind]]===
{{:Termite Overmind/Drop Tomato}}

=== Purchasing ===
On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|3}}

==Strategy==
if you have enough Tomatoes, then it can be useful for surviving with them dealing damage in an ascending order. Both the reload stats benefit from skills. It can be used to kill stronger mobs if you have weak petals and ultra-supers if used well with the right rotation speed. As the hitbox size increases with damage, it can be utilized to block petals/projectiles.

High tier tomatoes, unlike stinger, it can be used to multi-hit enemies, which can prove to be overpowered. Most tomato users already have a bone of the same level, which limits its potential. Still, it is a deadly force against most mobs and usually out classes bone due to powerful burst dmg.

Without Ultra Reload talent, Tomato is stronger than Stinger if both break in the same amount of hits, but it will be outclassed after you obtain Ultra Reload Talent due to the 3 second growing time being unaffected.

===[[Garden]]===
* Tomatoes are useful in the garden because of the fact that they can be used to destroy missiles from Hornets, it is a expensive versip. of plank that also has great offensive value.
* Use with healing petals. Even if you use sponge or cotton.

===[[Desert]]===
* It's slightly useful here. to say the least. It can block scorpion missiles properly, but it is a generally good petal.

===[[Ocean]]===
* Using Tomatoes in bulk can actually prove helpful in defeating Crabs and aggravated shells, mostly due to its hitbox increasing widely.

===[[Centralia Sewers|Sewer]]===
* This petal is useless at the lower-tier Sewer areas, even if you don't have one of them.
** Roaches might easily destroy them if you don't have them in higher tiers, but these can slightly slow them down.
** Flys are annoying for tomato users as flys evade tomatos and break them too.

=== [[PvP]] ===
* If 10 ultra tomatoes are used, the owner will be able to survive a lot of petals before dying as when 10 tomatos are used, the tomatoes have such large hit boxes they block most petals, after their petals are on respawn, spam your tomatoes on your enemys and even M tomatoes can destroy even max hp super hp talent people.

====Using====
* It is rather useful at close range since it is less likely to be destroyed when it is weak, and it has more chances of hitting at maximum damage, being useful for both destroying projectiles and dealing damage. Its ideal tactic somewhat resembles iris with a factor of luck.

====Against====
* There are many options since tomato requires perfect timing and skill. Usually deathwheels, ranged petals and eggs should do. It leaves a short lasting weakness in the orbit's defense that can be exploited.
* Third eye and players with supers can take the user down.
* Also, just pearl the guy...

==Trivia==
* Tomatoes used to destroy themselves upon reaching maximum size, but due to a recent unmentioned buff, the tomato doesn't "spoil" anymore. Its petal description was changed accordingly. 
* Rarities below {{Rarity|Legendary}} used to not exist but now is obtainable after the release of the [[Jungle]] Biome.
* It has the Third largest damage potential per single petal (super [[Stinger]] is 5 stingers), only behind [[Claw]] and [[Dice]] (assuming that the Dice were to pose a critical hit, increasing its base damage by 35 times)
* This is the only petal to change properties over time.
* Kr9ilf3b was the first player to obtain the {{Rarity|Super}} Tomato.

'''Totem''' is a [[Petals|petal]] in [https://florr.io florr.io]. Its function consists of granting health regeneration for a certain duration after killing a mob. Totem is dropped from [[Tomb]]s.

== Appearance ==
'''Totem''' consists of a brown shape that is somewhat a rectangle fused into a circle, and it has three dark brown dots going down vertically as well as what looks like dark brown [[Beetle]] pincers at the top of the circle.

== Function ==
{| class="article-table"
|+Petal Stats
![[Rarity]]
!Obtainable?
!Respawn
!Heal
!Duration

|No

|}
Totem grants a certain amount of health regeneration to the user for 10 seconds after killing a mob. Mob rarities don't affect this petal. Killing multiple mobs in quick succession or using multiple Totems grants the user multiple stacks of health regeneration. For example, killing 5 mobs with a {{Rarity|Mythic}} Totem results in a maximum of 243/s x 5 = 1,215/s health regeneration.

Totem is stackable no matter the amount of it or the petal rarity, make it much more useful if you are able to kill multiple mobs quickly.

== Obtainment ==

=== [[Tomb]] ===
{{:Tomb/Drop Totem}}

=== Purchasing ===
On certain days, Totem can be purchased from the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''{{Petal Price|2}}

== Obtaining ==
* Defeat [[Tomb]]s to obtain Totem. Use multi-hit petals such as Corn paired with bur since it has good damage against Tombs.
* While Tombs do not pose any threats to players, they can spawn [[Beetle/Mummy|Mummy Beetles]] upon being destroyed which can be troublesome to deal with. It is thus recommended to use slowing or repellent petals like [[Jelly|Jellies]], [[Pincer]]s, [[Wax]]es and [[Web]]s to immobilize them.
* Whenever a {{Rarity|Super}} mob spawns in the [[Pyramid]], it is the best opportunity to farm this petal.

== Strategies ==
* Similar to [[Fang]], Totem is an active petal which players have to attack mobs to gain heal. It is best advised to use Tomb in areas like [[Ant Hell]] and [[Sewers]] where mobs are more tightly packed, reducing the time needed to kill another mob to gain healing effect.
* Healing is only granted if the player does the last hit to a mob, which means Totem doesn't activate if someone else does the last hit. Therefore, it is advised to only use Totem when you can guarantee you will be killing lots of mobs in a squad. 
* It will work the best when player is using a build with high durability petals like [[Mjölnir]] and [[Rock]]s or AoE petals like [[Lightning]]s which kills one mob shortly after another.
* Players should avoid using Totem when hunting {{Rarity|Super}} [[Super and Unique Mobs|mobs]] or using a ram build.

== Gallery ==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">
File:Totem (Common).png|{{Rarity|1}} {{PAGENAME}}. (unobtainable)

</gallery>

== Trivia ==
* Totem is one of the two petals to heal when damaging something, with the other being Fang.
* In the balance update, Totem health stat was removed, thus making it invincible.
* When Totem was first released, it had twice the heal compared to the Totem's heal today.
* There was an exploit, where players could use [[Moon Rock]] with many '''Totems''' and [[Basil|Basils]] and other players (not of the same side as the Moon player) using {{Rarity|Epic}} [[Ant Egg|Ant Eggs]] to trick the game into thinking that the Moon player was killing mobs, therefore gaining a massive healing. Pairing this with [[Sponge]], players could enter deep areas to lure {{Rarity|Super}} mobs out without dying easily. This was fixed shortly
* This could be the reason that some deep {{Rarity|Super}} mobs were defeated very long after their spawn time without knowing why.
* ZinChlorideR was the first person to ever get Super totem.

}}
The '''Trader''' is a [[:Category:Special Entities|Special Entity]] in [[florr.io]]. It appears in [[Desert]] and allows you to trade petals for a [[Coin]] of the same rarity.

==Appearance ==
The Trader looks like a normal [[Flower]], but with a white outline with bumps, reminiscent of a real-life [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bellis_perennis daisy]. Eight [[Coin]]s orbit it, with one for each rarity.

== Behaviour==
The Trader drifts in a random pattern and moves like a typical passive mob in the game. It can access every part of the Desert.

== Interface==
Once pressing C, the Trade menu pops up. In the menu, when you click a petal and click the 'Trade' button, you lose that petal and will be replaced by a [[Coin]] with the same rarity that got traded. You cannot trade Coins themselves, Uniques, [[Fragment|Fragments]], [[Blood Sacrifice|Sacrifices]] or [[Basic|Basics]]. Trading has a cooldown of 24 hours and you can only trade a single petal at a time.

The Trade menu consists of a yellow background, a title that says 'Trade' on top, and a slot and a button that says 'Trade'. Your petals are shown below, not unlike the [[Crafting]] interface.

Unique [[Rarity]] petals cannot be traded.{{Game Info}}

==Lines==
Trader currently has 3 lines. They come via the chat feature. It is a green speech bubble.

*"I have the coin, if you have wares"
*"I'm looking to expand my collection..."
*"May I interest you in a little trade?"

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185">
File:Trader.png|Trader.
File:Trader UI.png
File:Trader speech.png|Trader says one of its lines: "May I interest you in a little trade?"
File:Trader's Coins.png|Trader's Coins
File:New trader.png|New version of trader.
File:Image all petals.png|This new trader has all rarity [[Coin|coins]].
File:Image of trading.png
File:Image coins.png
File:After-trade trade menu.png|[[File:Image Before trade.png|thumb|This is what the trading menu UI looks like.]]
File:A trader in the epic zone.jpg|The trader
File:Florrio trader brainrot.png|Trader during [[M28]]'s Brainrot Event
</gallery>


==Trivia ==
*The Trader is the first Special Entity (SE for short) in the game, with the second being [[Oracle]], third being the [[Titan]], fourth being the [[Assembler]] and fifth being the [[Crystal]].
*It is located in the [[Desert]], anywhere is viable. It has even been seen at the top of the map near the portal to [[Garden]].
*It cannot be killed, like the other SEs, and mobs or summoned mobs don't damage it, although they can push it.
* You can earn 3 [[achievements]] with it which are Trade (Acquire a [[Coin]] through trade), Trade (Acquire a {{R|6}}+ [[Coin]] through trade), and Trade (Acquire a {{R|8}} [[Coin]] through trade). You can achieve 3 at once by trading a super petal for a Super Coin.
* The Trader has the least amount of lines out of every SE in the game.
*Like [[Oracle]], it only spawns in {{Rarity|Common}} rarity.
*You can trade any petal with it except [[Basic]], [[Fragment]] and [[Coin]].
*Trader's coins used to deal damage to mobs.
*When {{Rarity|Unique}} petals were released, the Trader used to have {{Rarity|Unique}} Coin equipped, but it was removed after its release. This probably is because someone forged the {{Rarity|Unique}} [[Coin]]. 
*In November 2025, all NPCs was updated so that touching them won't automatically open the menu.

''This page is about the normal Wasp. For the [[Wasp/Mecha|mecha]] and [[Wasp/Hel|hel]] variant, see [[Wasp/Mecha|Mecha Wasp]] and [[Wasp/Hel|Hel Wasp]].''

The '''{{PAGENAME}}''' is a mob in florr.io. It spawns in the [[Jungle]] areas.

==Appearance==
Wasps looks like a [[Hornet]] but it has many differences: its [[Antennae]] are larger, its body is colored orange and it has 2 stripes on its body instead of usual 3. The stripes are slightly curved and are larger.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Moves like a Hornet. It is the same speed as a [[Flower]], but you will outrun it as it slows down while firing.

'''Behavior:''' On sight, the Wasp will pursue you, with rapid-fire flying homing [[Missile|Missiles]] that fly in a wave pattern and deal the same damage as the Hornet. Other than that, Wasps' missile's movement is similar to that of [[Hornet|Hornets]]. {{Rarity|Epic|Epic+}} Wasps have the ability to predict your movements like the Hornet.

The Wasp is essentially a Hornet with rapid fire (doubled fire rate), a wider Missile, and a curving wave pattern (Wasp missiles move similarly to [[Bee|Bees]]). Also, it's drop rates for [[Missile]] and [[Antennae]] are better than Hornet drop rates, however not dropping [[Orange]], instead dropping [[Blood Stinger]])

==Spawning==
The {{PAGENAME}} spawns in the [[Jungle]] areas.

==Drops==
===[[Missile]] ===
{{:Template:ID 3 - U}}

===[[Antennae]] ===
{{:Wasp/Drop Antennae}}

===[[Blood Stinger]]===
{{:Wasp/Drop Blood Stinger}}

==Strategy==
* Use [[Corn]] or stronger petals to destroy the missiles, then use damage-heavy petals to destroy the Wasp.
* Its missiles are so slow that even at {{R|Epic}}+ rarities, you don't have to use prediction dodging if you are far enough from the Wasp.
* If aiming for another, hit the stinger so it can’t shoot.
* {{Rarity|Super}} Wasps have 2 abilities:
** When it shakes its Missile, that means it will spin around and rapid fire missiles. You will die instantly either to its missiles or its body damage.
*** Make sure you bubble away when you notice the missile is shaking.
*** If you are skilled enough, you can stand on the right of the Wasp and spin with the Wasp when it spins. Thus you won’t be hit by any missiles.
** When it shakes its Antennae, that means it will spawn 5 {{Rarity|Ultra}} wasps.
*** Try to clear the {{Rarity|Ultra}} wasps surrounding the {{Rarity|Super}}. They are just as dangerous.

==Trivia==
*There are two another variants of Wasp called [[Hel Wasp]] and [[Mecha Wasp]].
**If you count [[Hornet]] as one of its variants, it would be tied with [[Centipede]] for having 4 variants. [[Beetle]] has 5 variants.
*The {{R|Ultra}} Wasp used to be able to spawn normal [[Hornet|Hornets]]. This confirms that M28 probably was far too lazy to give {{R|Ultra}} Wasp its own unique abilities, and therefore, he decided to simply copy the code used for [[Hornet]]'s special moves until they could be reused properly. Currently, only {{R|Super}} Wasps have special abilities.
*It was based on @NerdyAuk#9395's hornet reskin in the discord server.
*It is the only mob with a [[Hel]] variant that doesn't has its health buffed.

The '''Hel Wasp''' is a [[Hel]] variant mob that was added in May 13, 2024. It resembles the regular [[Wasp]] from the [[Jungle]].

==Appearance==
It looks like the actual [[Wasp]], but with recolored reddish body color without the big missile at its back. Similarly to other Hel mob variations, the front part was replaced by [[Hel Beetle]]'s mouth with messy teeth inside that behaves as its missiles. Unlike a Wasp or Hornet, Hel Wasps actually shoot from the front of their bodies, but without the curvature that a Wasp's missiles have.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed: ''' Medium.

'''Behavior:''' Aggressive, shoots five small missiles in various directions with a recoil on the wasp, which behaves like a shotgun. It shoots depends on the players position, using its own mouth, the teeth are made with missiles, those missiles attacks flowers on their position as when the flowers came closer to them.

At the Super Rarity, there is no ability for Hel Wasp since the back of it does not generate missiles, and also could be chaotic by swarm of Ultra Hel Wasps if there is even an ability for it.

==Spawning==
Hel Wasps mostly spawn in [[Hel]] with dense rate, only being outclassed by Hel Beetle.
==Drops==
===[[Dark Mark]]===
{{:Hel Wasp/Drop Dark Mark}}

===[[Missile]]===
{{:Template:ID 3 - U}}

===[[Poker Chip]]===
{{:Hel Wasp/Drop Poker Chip}}

==Strategy==
* [[Jelly]], with the combination of [[Wing|Wings]] and [[Leaf|healing petals]] can take down Hel Wasps.
* Eggs such as [[Ant Egg]] and [[Beetle Egg]] can also be used to approach the Hel Wasp head on and tank its missiles.
* Standing still will also, mostly allow you to dodge its missiles, though beware of other mobs as they may interrupt your standing.
* [[Third Eye]], [[Yin Yang]], and [[Wing]] can help with {{Rarity|Ultra}} Hel Wasps, but this can be time consuming. Beware of other ultras.
* Using [[Missile/Mecha|Mecha Missiles]] can easily outrange the Hel Wasp. Make sure you lure it to a low rarity area.
* For {{Rarity|Mythic}}/{{Rarity|Ultra}} Hel Wasps, a combination of bubbles and burst damage can be effective, although this is risky and can lead to you taking heavy damage.
* {{Rarity|Ultra}} Hel Wasps, which are rather common in Hel, are extremely annoying because they shoot small but damaging missiles which, not only pushing you back, sends them flying out of your petals' reach. This means it's much easier if you have friends to attack the Hel Wasp while it's targeted on you.
* Because the missiles appear smaller, players may underestimate how strong they really are.
* {{Rarity|Ultra|Ultra+}} [[Plank|Planks]] are needed to tank Hel Wasp missiles, thus making it easier to deal with.

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185">

File:Hel waspp.png|There must be some mistakes with this one...
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*Hel Wasp's [[Dice]] drop was replaced with [[Poker Chip]] in the May 28, 2024 update.
*It is the first Hel mob that originates from the [[Jungle]]. The other being [[Centipede/Hel|Hel Centipede]].
*This mob has the same stats as the actual [[Wasp]], with the only difference that Hel Wasp has half of the missile stats.
*The missiles when fired looks like a [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shotgun shotgun].
*Hel Wasp shoots 5 missiles at a time, which is the amount of missiles at the front of its body.
*The Hel Wasp, along with other Hel variants, has had its appearance changed, going from just a red recolor of the Wasp to added missiles/teeth at the front of its body. However, it is the only Hel mob that acts in a different way from its normal counterpart, firing 5 normal missiles instead of 1 curved missile.
*Hel Wasps' and Wasps' Missiles used to have the same stats. However, this has been changed in January 2025. Now they have half the stats.
*Hel Wasp is totally vulnerable in its bottom and both sides, but be aware that when shooting missiles, Hel Wasp will push itself back. It's best to hit it in its sides.
*Hel Wasp used to share Chip drop with Hel Centipede, but later become its exclusive drop due to the addition of Domino petal.
*Within mobs health buff, Hel Wasps are just more annoying than ever.
*It is the only mob Hel variant not to have its health buffed.

The '''Mecha Wasp''' is a [[Mobs|mob]] in florr.io. They act like [[Wasp|Wasps]], but shoot [[Missile/Mecha|Mecha Missiles]]. They drop [[Battery]], [[Antennae/Mecha|Mecha Antennae]], [[Fragment]] and [[Missile/Mecha|Mecha Missile]] ({{R|6|Mythic+}} only).

==Appearance==
Mecha Wasps have a similar body shape to [[Wasp|Wasps]], but they have a grey metallic body with black-yellow stripes. They have red dots on their antennae and a red dot on their missile.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed:''' Same as Wasp.

'''Behavior:''' Shoots homing missiles when close to the player, not directly aiming at the flower, however.

CLICK THIS LINK TO SEE RATES

[[Fragment]]

==Strategy==
* Circle around their missiles to avoid them hitting you. [[Rubber]] also helps as it allow you to bounce away from them, preventing taking more hits. However, be careful not to stand between their missiles and themselves or you will take heavy damage from both their body damage and their missiles.
* Since it is impossible to dodge the missiles, use [[Plank|Planks]] (with the same rarity as the mobs) to destroy them instead. Make sure you have at least 2 as 1 isn't enough to cover your flower.  [[Jelly|Jellies]] can be used too but they are less effective because they are affected by attacking state.
* Use Eggs to distract the Wasp and their missiles so you can survive for longer.
* Their missiles will not lock on if they can't track down you (hiding behind walls, using [[Shovel]]...), so use it to your advantage. You can use Mecha [[Missile/Mecha|Missiles]] to attack the Wasp when you're behind a wall.
* Otherwise, treat them like regular [[Wasp|Wasps]] with more armor.

=== Super Mecha Wasp ===

* The Super mob isn't as dangerous as the Super Hornet or Wasp. This is because It mainly just focuses on one person. Other ultra mobs are also very dangerous. When bubbling to the mob, it is advised to use Sponge and multiple Ultra healing petals, and have Rubber equipped in case a Spider hits you or a Crab. It is actually optional to use sponge when actually fighting the mob, because the wasp won't attack other people even during the missile attack.
* If the mob spawns deep in Factory, it is advised to form a "megasquad", where a large group of players stay near each other and clear mobs. This increases the chance for everyone to survive.
* In most cases, players will lure Super Wasp to slightly easier areas because they are nearly impossible to kill while surrounded by Ultra mobs. This means that you do not need to rush into the ultra zone area to attack it
* It is advised to use [[Third Eye]] or other long ranged petals to kill it. It is also advised to attack the mob rather from where there are not ultra mobs (the ones that mob spawns). But don't go in front of the mob since it is where they shoot their missiles. 
* If it is attracted to you, It is advised to use shovel or bubble away behind a wall when it does its missile spin attack.

</gallery>

==Trivia==
*Mecha Wasp's name on the health bar used to be "Mecha Wasp", but later changed to "Wasp" like [[Wasp/Hel|Hel Wasp]].
*Its missile fire rate is less than normal Wasp.
*Mecha Wasps don't rotate 180° while firing missiles, instead it will rotate itself on many different angles, due to its missiles always homing in on its prey.
*Sometimes you can see that some {{Rarity|Ultra}} Mecha Wasps keep facing in one direction and not moving until it targets a player or a summoned mob. That's because a {{Rarity|Super}}  Mecha Wasp has been lured out of its spawn place, the spawned {{Rarity|Ultra}} Mecha Wasps also tried to follow it, and then they kept facing in a direction even if the {{Rarity|Super}} Mecha Wasp died.
**Its missile can lock on anything it targets.
**The Missile will stop targeting about a second after the Wasp that fired it dies, most likely due to the destruction of the [[Antennae/Mecha|Mecha Anntennae]] controlling it.
**The Missile will also fly to a different direction when Mecha Wasp loses aggro on a player.
**Its direction can change if Mecha Wasp loses aggro on a player and aggro on an another player. So do the summons.

'''Wax''' is a [[Petals|petal]] from [https://florr.io florr.io]. Its function consists of dropping a large block of wax to block mobs. Wax is dropped by [[Bumble Bee]].

==Appearance==
Wax consists of a large golden hexagon that resembles [[Honey]], but is much larger than the mentioned petal and has sharper edges. Wax is larger at higher rarities. 

==Function==

Wax blocks projectiles and other mobs. Players can push the wax block around. Wax does not appear as a petal that revolves around the player, as it instantly spawns where the player is once its cooldown timer ends. Higher rarities of Wax are larger and have more health. Is sometimes also used for trolling in narrower ant hell passages, however with the aid of bubbles these are easily passed. 

==Obtainment==

===Purchasing===
On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''{{Petal Price|2}}

=== Assembling ===
Wax can also be obtained by assembling 3 Honeys of the same rarity.

==Strategies==
*Wax is nearly useless because it deals no damage.
*Wax can act as a defensive petal, as it has very high health. However, it does no damage and has a very high respawn time.
*Wax can be used to afk, paired with eggs and sticks. However, it's reload time and lack of damage makes it quite weak.
*Wax is essentially budget [[Moon Rock|moon]], but with a couple differences:
**Wax has only 40% the health of a Moon (of the same rarity). It does no damage.
**Players can hide inside a Moon (because it phases through flowers and petals.) They cannot hide inside Wax. 
**Wax has half the reload of Moon.
**Moon can and will move around and, because your petals orbit it, can bubble around and tank through mobs. Wax stays stationary and your petals don't orbit it. The only way to move a Wax is by pushing it around.
**It goes without saying, but Wax is far easier to obtain than Moon and has a minimum rarity of {{Rarity|Rare}} rather than {{Rarity|Ultra}}.
===Obtaining===
*Farm Bumble Bees to obtain this petal.
*It is advised that you should farm Wax in the main Spiral and the mini Spiral.
*You can also farm in [[Bee]] zone for Wax.
{{Zone|Garden}}
*Wax can be used to block Hornet missiles, you can also push it to saw down other mobs
{{Zone|Desert}}
*Wax can be used to defend against Scorpion missiles.
{{Zone|Ocean}}
*It's not very useful here, but can block crab.
{{Zone|Jungle}}
*Can block [[Wasp]] missiles.
{{Zone|Hel}}
===PvE===
*Probably the worst petal to use besides square unless you have [[Mimic]], but blocks [[Wasp/Hel|Hel Wasp]] Missiles.
===PvP: Using===

* Wax is not recommended to use as they are fragile due to having no damage and can be ignored as it will just stays at its place.
* However, Wax can distract the enemy in a short time.

===PvP: Against===
*Jelly can be used to counter Wax as it can be bounced very far away.
*Don't worry if you ram a Wax block, as it deals no damage. So does your summons. Use Stingers as they are really good at destroying Wax blocks.
{{Zone|Ant Hell}}
*Maybe Farming. Wax + Stick might be a good way to farm.
*You can farm in a small passageway in here with L+ Wax (so it's big enough to cover you), Carrots, and Sticks. Carrots can bounce around, aggroing mobs and the sandstorms will protect you.
{{Zone|Sewers}}
*Maybe Farming. Might good at blocking Flies and Roaches cause they’re fast.
*Slightly better at protecting you from sewers mobs due to their less than average body damage.
==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185" spacing="small">
File:Wax (Common).png|{{Rarity|1}} {{PAGENAME}}. (unobtainable)
File:Wax (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|2}} {{PAGENAME}}. (unobtainable)

File:TheWaxeningBegins.png|More players have come by with Wax, starting a Waxening.

</gallery>

==Trivia==
*Wax can be used to block the entrance to Pyramid, in Desert. 
**However, it can be pushed out once a player uses a Shovel to burrow underneath.
*Wax is one of the petals that was added unmentioned in the [[changelog]].
*This is the first and so far the only petal to have an "x" in its name.
*Upon addition, Wax was stackable, which led to lots of trolling using Wax to block corridors and paths.
**Additionally, Wax wasn't movable by players.
**After 2 days of releasing, Wax was updated so it no longer stacks but it has twice the original health and deals no damage.
*This petal is similar to [[Moon Rock]], but it has no damage, less health but a faster spawn.
*This petal is tied with [[Air]], [[Poo]], and [[Bur]] for the shortest petal name (3 letters each.)
*Zorat is the first player to craft a Super Wax.
*This petal has the second highest health among the petals (4.4m at Super), with the first being [[Moon Rock]] (11m at Super).

'''Web''' is a petal in florr.io. It shoots out and spreads on the ground, creating webs that slow enemies down by 75%.

== Appearance==
It looks like a 5-pointed white star with white curves, with the size being near to a [[Basic]] petal.

Webs on the ground appear like a [[Spider]]'s web but with a yellow tint.

==Function==
''Note: The "A + B" respawn format means that the petal takes A seconds to respawn and B seconds after respawning to do its special action/have its special action enabled.''

Shoots away (much like [[Missile]] and [[Dandelion]]) for a short distance and turns into a web, at which point it goes on respawn. The web slows enemy flowers or mobs (except [[Spider]]s, [[Hel Spider]]s and [[Shell (Mob)|Shells]]) on it down by 75% but does not deal damage. The slow effect can't stack with other webs but can stack with other slowing petals.

==Obtaining==
===[[Spider]]===
{{:Spider/Drop Web}}

===Purchasing===
On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|2}}

== Strategy ==
Webs are essential at slowing down incoming enemies. They make the enemy vulnerable and increase the amount of time there is to attack. They also allow the user to flee if needed.

===[[Garden]]===
* Webs are useless against [[Spider]]s.
* Webs are not useful against many other mobs.
** A lot are either stationary or have low movement speed, making webs less useful
** [[Hornet]]s stray far from the user anyway, so they won't wander into a web trap the user has laid. Missiles are also unaffected.
* Webs are still useful to temporarily escape a mob swarm to heal or gain a better position.

===[[Desert]] ===
* Webs are essential in Desert, as almost all mobs deal damage via contact, so they must pass through a sea of webs to hit the user.
* Webs greatly increase the time available to kill mobs and make them easier to hit.
* For [[Beetle]]s, [[Desert Centipede]]s and [[Soldier Fire Ant]]s, progression is greatly slowed if they have to pass through webs. Be aware that the mobs may push each other, slightly increasing their speed.
* For [[Sandstorm]]s and Desert Centipedes, their fast speed is nullified if they are trapped in webs, rendering them harmless.

===[[Ocean]]===
* Webs are useless against [[Shell (Mob)|Shells]].
* Webs can slow down the progression of other mobs, giving others more time to kill them. This is especially useful with [[Jellyfish]] to avoid being stung.
* [[Starfish (Mob)|Starfish]]'s retreat as well as its advance is slowed. However, health regen remains the same.
* [[Crab]]s lose their tendency to dodge (by circling) if trapped in webs.

===[[PvP]]===
====Using====
* A perfectly-timed hit with a web can fully slow down a chasing player or mobs (especially with [[Beetle]]s and Hornets), and thus can be a breather.
* The web will form only when shot, and not when destroyed while still orbiting the player. Keep this in mind when having to deal enemies at close range.

====Against====
* When chasing someone with this, be careful and dodge the webs on the ground.
* Bubbles are also useful as the speed boost can help the user escape faster. However, they are not as useful as in legacy florr.
* Use projectiles which can bypass webs with no slowdown and kill players attempting to hide in them.

File:Web.png|Web.
</gallery>

==Trivia==
* It is one of four petals to stick to the ground, the others being [[Pollen]], [[Pearl]], and [[Honey]].
** However, Pearl can be retrieved by the player after going on the ground. The others go on respawn.
* Before the {{rarity|6}} and {{rarity|7}} rework, {{rarity|6}} Webs were as large as {{rarity|7}} Webs. Additionally, {{rarity|7}} Webs used to last for 30 seconds instead of 10 seconds.
* Before an unknown date, this petal was used to not spawn Webs if get destroyed by a mob or flower, but now it does (spawn web immediately after destroyed).
* On July 16, 2022, {{rarity|5}} Web was changed from 1 to 2 webs, this was reverted shortly after.
** Also on July 16, 2022, {{rarity|6}} Web was changed from 1 to 3 webs. On July 23, 2022, it was changed from 3 to 2 webs. And on July 26, 2022 it was changed back to 1 web.

'''Wing''' is a [[petal]] in [https://florr.io florr.io] that moves away and back toward the flower while orbiting it. It drops from [[Fly]], [[Moth]] and [[Firefly]].

==Appearance==
A white crescent with a light gray outline. It constantly rotates around its center while simultaneously revolving around the player.

==Function==
{|class="article-table"
|+Petal Stats
![[Rarity]]
!Obtainable?
!Damage 
!Health
!Respawn
|-
|{{Rarity|Common}}
|rowspan="9"|Yes
|20 HP

|-

| 810 HP

|-
|{{Rarity|Ultra}}

|7,290 HP
|-
| {{Rarity|Super}}

|21,870 HP
|-
| {{Rarity|Unique}}
|131,220 HP
|65,610 HP
|}
When attacking it moves outward very far, then back to the normal attack distance. This movement effectively gives the petal more range. This makes the petal very useful against ranged mobs like [[Hornet|Hornets]], especially when combined with [[Third Eye]]. This combo also can be used to hit mobs through walls.

==Obtaining==
===[[Fly]]===
{{ID 6}}

===[[Moth]]===
{{ID 6}}

===[[Firefly]]===
{{ID 6}}

===Purchasing===
On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|2}}

==Strategy==
Wing has strong damage and acceptable durability. Its key advantage is its long range, which means that it can attack mobs from a safe distance. However, the Wing's movement pattern also makes it hard to use; it is very hard to hit mobs when the Wing is constantly flying back and forth.

Wings pair well with [[Faster|Fasters]], since the rotation speed increase makes the Wings more likely to hit something.

Do not [[Trader|Trade]] Wings for [[Coin]]s because Wings are generally much better than Coins in every aspect, unless you're desperate for an {{Rarity|Unique|Unique Coin}}.
===[[Garden]]===
* This petal is a stronger version of the [[Light]] and [[Sand]] petals, but it has increased range and reload.
** This range is especially useful against [[Hornet|Hornets]], as it allows the user to stay further away from their missiles while still dealing damage.

=== [[Ant Hell]] ===

* Due to the confined space and high mob density of Ant Hell, using Wings here is generally not a good idea

=== [[Sewers]] ===

* Wings can be use to attack [[Roach|Roaches]] from afar, allowing for more window to react and use slow petals like [[Web]] and the Roach won't be able to reach you in an instant.
* Wings are also pretty useful for killing [[Moth|Moths]] that ran away from you in case you don't want to use [[Light Bulb|Bulb]] and attract a gazillion mobs to you.

===[[Desert]]===
* The extra range is useful for staying away from [[Sandstorm]]s, avoiding their high body damage, along with their unpredictable movement. 
* It can also be use to kill [[Desert Centipede|Desert Centipedes]] more easily by using the longer range of the petal.

===[[Ocean]]===
* The extra range is useful for dealing with [[Crab]]s which become tricky to dodge when up close. It is suggested to use with fasters and [[Pincer]] to kill Crabs.
* Wings of the same rarity as [[Bubble|Bubbles]] have enough damage to bypass the bubble's shield and, due to Wings' increased range, it can be use to quickly clear large amount of Bubbles.
* It can also damage [[Shell (Mob)|Shells]] from further away, allowing the user to avoid damage.
* It is advised not to fight [[Leech|Leeches]] with Wings due to the lack of DPS.

=== [[Jungle]] ===

* Wings are pretty good counter to Mantises for similar reasons to other ranged mobs.
* While it is harder to kill Wasps with Wing compare to [[Mantis|Mantises]], it is still much less risky than normal.
* Wings of the same rarity as [[Leafbug|Leafbugs]] have over double the amount of armor they have, making this petal pretty good against Leafbugs.
* It is also a decent petal for Fireflies idk.

=== [[Hel]] ===

* Don't bother fighting anything else but [[Gambler|Gamblers]] with Wings.

=== [[Pyramid]] ===
* Use it and [[Yin Yang]] to extend your attack range, thus reaching Super Tombs over the wall.

===[[PvP]]===
====Using====
* Wings allow the user to attack enemies from a distance. When using Wings, you should try to stay away from your opponent, so that not only will you be able to damage them, they won't be able to damage you too.
* Pair with Faster, which will make your wings fly around quickly and erratically, making them very difficult to dodge.

====Against====
* It is fairly low in single-hit damage, so an attacker may opt to tank the hit.
* The fact it goes out can be taken advantage of. While it usually makes it harder to dodge, one can memorize the pattern and use this to effectively dodge a wing.
* Try to get close to the player with Wings. If you get close enough to your opponent to hit them with your petals, you will likely be able to defeat them.
* Use [[Lightning]] against players with Wings. If their Wing hits your Lightning petal, it might be able to damage them.
* Use [[Salt]]. if a Wing user hits you, the Salt will reflect the damage right back to them.

==Gallery ==
<gallery spacing="small" widths="185">
File:Wing (Common).png|{{Rarity|1}} Wing.
File:Wing (Unusual).png|{{Rarity|2}} Wing.
File:Wing (Rare).png|{{Rarity|3}} Wing.

File:Wing (Legendary).png|{{Rarity|5}} Wing.
File:Wing (Mythic).png|{{Rarity|6}} Wing.
File:Wing (Ultra).png|{{Rarity|7}} Wing.
File:Wing (Super).png|{{Rarity|8}} Wing.
File:Wing (Unique).png|{{Rarity|9}} Wing.
File:Wing.png|Wing.
</gallery>

==Trivia==
* Wing is the only petal along with [[Orange]] and [[Missile]] to have above average range (and [[Dandelion]] currently).
* During legacy, Wing did not have its oscillating movement.
* Currently Pehiley owns {{Rarity|Unique|Unique Wing}}, as of August 8th, 2025, with the current price of 14 (Prior to Unique forging rework).
* Wing used to be dropped by Ants and Fire Ants, but this was removed in an update, and were replaced by [[Glass]] and [[Clover]] for [[Soldier Ant|Soldier Ants]], [[Privet Berry]] formely and [[Bone]] for Soldier Fire Ants. Wing drop simply removed for Queen Ants/Fire Queen Ants. Now, they are only obtainable in [[Sewers]] or [[Jungle]].
** It also used to be dropped by [[Ladybug|Ladybugs]], [[Bee|Bees]], [[Hornet|Hornets]], Soldier [[Soldier Ant|Ants]]/[[Soldier Fire Ant|Fire Ants]] and Queen Ants/Fire Ants
** For some reason Soldier Ants/Fire Ants don't drop Wings anymore despite having visible wings. This might be due to them being extremely easy to farm and Flies/Moths already having good rates which prompt a loot table rework.
** [[Soldier Termite|Soldier Termites]] have never dropped Wings despite also having visible wings like their Ant counterparts.

The '''Worker Ant''' is a mob in florr.io that was added on February 3, 2020 in the old version. It's a neutral mob, only attacking if hurt, from damaged [[Ant Hole|Ant Holes]], or if you use [[Honey]] or [[Light Bulb|Bulb]].

==Appearance==
Exactly like the [[Baby Ant]], but with an extra small circle at the back. This circle is smaller than the first but otherwise looks the same. The jaws move back and forth slightly if provoked.

==Behavior==
Wanders around like a passive mob until provoked, in which case will chase the flower with a normal speed.

==Spawning==
The Worker Ant spawns in [[Ant Hell]] (in the top left area) and anywhere the [[Garden]]. Ant Holes can also spawn them.
==Drops==
===[[Leaf]]===
{{:Worker Ant/Drop Leaf}}

===[[Corn]]===
{{:Worker Ant/Drop Corn}}

==Strategy==
*Although passive, this mob is not super threatening, as it has lower hp then a soldier ant.
*Pincer, Jelly, and Web can slowdown the mob, making it easier to kill. 
*Due to having low body damage, Higher health petals and high rarity bones are good petals to use.

=== Super Worker Ant ===

* The {{Rarity|8}} mob itself is not as dangerous, but the {{Rarity|7}} mobs around the mob that is a threat. When bubbling to the mob, it is advised to use Sponge and multiple {{Rarity|7}} healing petals. It is advised to use Sponge when actually fighting the mob, so you don't die if anything hits you.
* If the mob spawns deep in Normal Ant Hell, it is advised to form a "megasquad", where a large group of players stay near each other and clear mobs. This increases the chance for everyone to survive.
* If there are less people, the {{Rarity|8}} Worker Ant would usually be lured out to easier areas. If there are a large amount of people, players could lure it only to a cleared area and kill it. Builds are the same as other mobs.

Just like the [[Worker Ant]], it will wander around aimlessly until it is attacked, it will then become aggressive and start chasing the attacker until either, it dies, the attacker dies, or it loses sight of the attacker. Everything down from the health and is the same as the [[Worker Ant]] (although the damage is twice as high), so treat this as one when you see it.

==Spawning==
The Worker Fire Ant spawns in the [[Ant Hell]] zone (the bottom left area), and also outside of it's spawn portal. Common and Unusual Worker Fire Ants can rarely spawn in the egg zone near the main exit portal.

==Drops==
===[[Yucca]]===
{{:Worker Fire Ant/Drop Yucca}}

===[[Corn]]===
{{:Worker Fire Ant/Drop Corn}}

==Strategy==
Avoid attacking it unless you want [[Corn]]. If you do attack it, it's generally easy to just move around in a circle and kill it just like the other ants. Make sure that you don't get surrounded into the wall.

The {{PAGENAME}} spawns in [[Ant Hell]] (the middle right area), and in [[Termite Mound|Termite Mounds]] in the [[Jungle]].

{{:Worker Termite/Drop Relic}}

===[[Plank]] ===
{{:Worker Termite/Drop Plank}}

==Strategy ==

*Although semi-aggressive, this mob is not threatening on its own but becomes deadly when paired with termite soldiers as they act as meatshields that keep the soldiers alive for longer.
*It has more health compared to fire and normal ants and it's psyonic connection makes it take even less damage if there are other termites so do not get overconfident and ram it.

==Trivia==

* It is the only mob that drops [[Plank]]s. 
**In real life worker termites are responsible for digging tunnels in wood, which is probably why they drop planks.
*Worker Termites, along with other Termites got a health change during the following years.
** On October 22, 2024, its HP was nerfed by 50% when its original base health is 125, before being reverted on October 23, 2025 update.
** On January 22, 2026, its HP was nerfed by another 50% again.

'''Worm''' is a [[Mobs|mob]] from [https://florr.io florr.io]. It burrows [[underground]], following anything it detects, then pops up from underneath its prey to attack. Worm spawns in [[Ant Hell]].

==Appearance==
The Worm looks like a brown circle with triangular shaped teeth on it. Its teeth are placed randomly on Worm mouth. When burrowed, its body elongates into a larger body, much like [[Leech]]: however, the entire structure lies underground, like [[Centipede (Desert)|Desert Centipede]] or a flower using [[Shovel]] to burrow underground.

==Stats & Behavior==
'''Speed: ''' Worms are substantially faster than the player, moving at the same speed as [[Leech]].

'''Behavior:''' Worm is an aggressive mob which stays underground as if it were using a [[Shovel]] until it sees a player. It then chases the player until it is underneath it, then pops up, dealing extreme damage.

{{Rarity|Super}}+ Worms can swallow flowers, teleporting them [[Worm (Area)|inside of the worm]], where they will need to kill the {{Rarity|Super}}+ Worm by attacking the [[Worm/Worm Guts|Worm Guts]] to leave. Worm Guts don't break, which means that they do not drop any petals, and damage dealt to them will be transferred to the Worm's health. Damaging Worm Guts increases the chances of you receiving loot, because it counts as damage to the Worm. While inside the Worm, everyone takes immense poison damage.

==Spawning==
Worms of any rarity spawn often in [[Ant Hell]].

==Drops==
===[[Shovel]] ===
{{:Worm/Drop Shovel}}

===[[Soil]] ===
{{:Worm/Drop Soil}}

== Strategies ==

* Avoid fighting Worms in corridors and avoid fighting them in swarms.
* Never stop moving when fighting Worms. They do a lot more damage then you think.
* Worms target you over your summons, so if you use them, consider also using [[Poo]].
* Using Shovel is a bad idea, because Worms can attack you while you're underground, and because you can't use your petals while underground, they can kill you in seconds.
* Using [[Bubble]]s is the only way to avoid {{Rarity|Super}}+ Worm attacks.
* Don't try to get eaten by Super Worms unless you have minimum {{Rarity|Ultra}} [[Lotus]] to keep yourself alive.
* make sure to have minimum mythic health talent to survive the poison better. note:this is optional.

==Gallery==
<gallery widths="185">

</gallery>

==Trivia==
*This is the second mob that is able to teleport you to a separate region. The first was [[Termite Mound]].
*The first {{Rarity|8}} Worm spawned in [[Garden]] in the circle zone at the center of the map. Then a {{Rarity|9}} Worm spawned near the Ant Hell portal, and it moved up to near the Garden spawnpoint. Another {{Rarity|8}} and {{Rarity|9}} Worm were also spawned in Ant Hell.
*On 02/02/2025, M28 spawned a Unique Worm at the Ant Hell portal in Garden.
*The entire Worm area is poisonous, likely due to Worm's stomach in real life having acid.
*Every Worm has different amounts of teeth and has different locations for each of them (But they are always either on the top or the bottom of the Worm).
*Worm's description is a reference to real life worms which eat dirt.
*Despite Worm looking long like [[Leech]] when [[Underground]], only it's head has a hitbox. This affects how [[Uranium]] damages worms

'''Worm Guts''' are mobs that only spawn [[Worm (Area)|inside a worm]]. Players are required to attack them if eaten by a {{Rarity|Super}}+ [[Worm]] in order to damage and kill the Worm to escape.

Worm Guts do not drop any petals and do not show up in the [[Mob Gallery]]. They are purely correlated to the Worm's health.

==Appearance==
Worm Guts appear as a light brown, pulsating mass. There is a slightly darker blob that pulsates in the middle.

==Stats & Behavior==
Worm Guts are stationary. All damage done to them is redirected to the Worm.

== Spawning ==
Worm Guts spawn in the Worm area, which can only be accessed by being swallowed by a {{Rarity|Super}}+ Worm.

==Strategy ==
*Finding Worm Guts can be made easier by moving around using [[Bubble]] or [[Bubble/Magic|Magic Bubble]] while using [[Antennae]].
*A minimum of 8 {{Rarity|Ultra}}, 7 {{Rarity|Ultra}} and 2 {{Rarity|Mythic}}, or other rarity combinations of [[Lotus]]es which give the same or higher poison negation are required to completely negate the poison inside a {{Rarity|Super}} Worm.
*In order to loot Worm inside, use petals with high DPS by themselves or petals that can multi-hit the Worm guts, for example [[Rock]], [[Pearl]], [[Sawblade]], [[Bone]] and [[Wing]].

==Gallery==

File:Worm Guts.png|Worm Guts.
</gallery>

==Trivia==
*Worm Guts are one of the mobs which can spawn inside another mob, like termites inside of a [[Termite Mound]].
*Worm Guts' description is a reference to how it cannot be shown in the Mob Gallery. It is only accessible via memory editing.
*If one Gut is damaged, all other Guts are damaged.

''Not to be confused with the mob, [[Worm]].''

'''Worm''' is an [[World Map|area]] in [https://florr.io/ florr.io]. Upon being swallowed by a {{R|8|Super+}} Worm, players will be teleported to a random spot in this area.

==Location==
There are no portal connections in Worm. It can only be accessed by being swallowed by a {{Rarity|Super|Super+}} Worm.

==Mobs==
Only one mob can spawn here: Worm Guts.

'''[[Worm/Worm Guts|Worm Guts]]''' spawn in Worm and are required to be killed to leave the Worm. They are directly correlated to the Worm's health.

==Strategy==
*As poison is the main source of damage inside Worm, it is advised to use [[Lotus]] to counter it.
**For {{R|8}} Worm, a minimum of 7 {{R|7}} and 2 {{R|6}} Lotuses are required.
**For {{R|9}} or {{R|9b}} Worm, a minimum of 7 {{R|8}} and 2 {{R|7}} Lotuses are required.

==Gallery==

File:WormInside.webp|Inside the Worm

File:Worm colorized.png|The view of the old Worm Area (old).

==Trivia==
*Worm is one of two areas that can only be accessed through a mob. The other one is [[Termite Mound]] and [[Termite Mound (Area)|its respective area]].
*The map is hidden in Worm, much like [[Centralia Fields/Maze|Centralia Maze]], a removed area that was in florr.io before April 2024, and [[Pyramid]], which was added in early 2025.
*On an unknown date, the area's appearance was changed, making it smaller.
*This area used to have a red overlay like [[Ocean]], which has a blue overlay.
*On January 2, 2026, {{R|8}} Worm's poison damage was increased from 32,805/s to 76,545/s.

title: Yggdrasil
content:
{{Petal Template Infobox | image = File:Yggdrasil (Legendary).png|L File:Yggdrasil (Mythic).png|M File:Yggdrasil (Ultra).png|Ul File:Yggdrasil (Super).png|S File:Yggdrasil (Unique).png|Uq | description = A dried leaf from the Yggdrasil tree.
Rumored to be able to bring the fallen back to life. | first-rarity = {{Rarity|Legendary}} | health = {{Petal Scale|10|lowest-rarity=legendary}} | revive-heal = 100% | reload-time = 5.0s + 5.0s | stackability = Stackable | dropped-by = * [[Ladybug (Desert)]] | special = Revives a dead flower (cannot revive anyone in [[Hel]]) }} '''Yggdrasil''' is a [[Petals|petal]] in [https://florr.io florr.io]. It revives dead players, restores their health to 100% and grants them 5 seconds invulnerability, but prevents them from being revived again for a certain time. Yggdrasil is dropped by [[Ladybug (Desert)|Shiny Ladybugs]]. ==Appearance== Yggdrasil looks like a short, brown leaf with a darker brown outline and lines through the middle. ==Function== ''Note: The "A + B" respawn format means that the petal takes A seconds to respawn and B seconds after respawning to do its special action/have its special action enabled.'' {|class="article-table" |+Petal Stats ![[Rarity]] !Obtainable? !Health !Respawn !Revival Heal !Unrevivable |- |{{Rarity|Common}} |rowspan="4"|No |10 |rowspan="9"|5 + 5 seconds |rowspan="9"|100% |13.5 hours |- |{{Rarity|Unusual}} |30 |4.5 hours |- |{{Rarity|Rare}} |90 |1.5 hours |- |{{Rarity|Epic}} |270 |30 minutes |- |{{Rarity|Legendary}} |rowspan="5"|Yes |810 |10 minutes |- |{{Rarity|Mythic}} |2430 |3.3 minutes |- |{{Rarity|Ultra}} |7290 |1.1 minutes |- |{{Rarity|Super}} |21870 |22.2 seconds |- |{{Rarity|Unique}} |65610 |7.4 seconds |} Yggdrasil can revive a dead flower. It works like a [[Rose]], but instead it revives any defeated [[Flower]]s and also heals the revived players by 100% of their health. The defeated flowers normally stay on the ground until they press continue on the death screen, but they can be revived with this petal. Lay it on a Flower, like a [[Dark Mark|Mark]], and they come back to life after a certain duration and are invincible for 5 seconds. After being revived, the player is unrevivable for a certain amount of time, depending on the Yggdrasil's rarity. ==Obtaining== ===[[Ladybug (Desert)]]=== {{:Ladybug (Desert)/Drop Yggdrasil}} ===Purchasing=== On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]]. ''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]'' {{Petal Price|100}} == Strategy == Yggdrasil has the extremely powerful and useful ability of being able to revive a dead player. If a player dies, they will be left as a corpse, and they must travel back from the last checkpoint, which can take a long time and may be hard to do in some cases. For this reason, Yggdrasil is considered a necessity in squads. ===Obtaining=== * They can only be obtained by [[Shiny Ladybug]]s, which are very sought-after and very hard to find. You will need to deal with {{Rarity|Mythic}}/{{Rarity|Ultra}} [[Beetle]]s, [[Scorpion]]s, [[Sandstorm]]s and [[Desert Centipede]]s while looking for them. * Shiny Ladybugs are usually greeded for Yggdrasil if it is a {{Rarity|Legendary}} or above. {{Zone|Garden}} * Teammates should crowd around the corpse since there is a range for the effect of Yggdrasil. * Do not revive corpses that have mobs on top of them, as they could possibly die again. * Revive teammates immediately after they die, especially if they themselves have a Yggdrasil so they can revive you as well when you die. * If you are being chased by a hornet swarm, use jelly and one Yggdrasil, and go to the corpse, and revive it. This, however, is a risky strategy since you can get killed by the hornets, or it may spawn inside of a missile, which is a problem. {{Zone|Desert}} * Use [[Web|Webs]] or [[Pincer]]s to slow down mobs so that they can be killed before they touch the corpse. * Apart from that, the strategy is like garden. {{Zone|Ocean}} * Same as garden apart from the hornets. {{Zone|Hel}} ===Using=== * Yggdrasil is fully useless in Hel, as you cannot squad there. * But you can use it to flex that you can waste a slot on it. ===Against=== * Yggdrasil is useless in Hel, so ignore it. * Be warned that the opponent probably has other very good petals if they are wasting a petal slot on Yggdrasil. * But you can see if they have good petals by simply holding Alt, to see the rarity of the petals. == Gallery == File:Yggdrasil (Common).png|{{R|1}} Yggdrasil. (unobtainable) File:Yggdrasil (Unusual).png|{{R|2}} Yggdrasil. (unobtainable) File:Yggdrasil (Rare).png|{{R|3}} Yggdrasil. (unobtainable) File:Yggdrasil (Epic).png|{{R|4}} Yggdrasil. (unobtainable) File:Yggdrasil (Legendary).png|{{R|5}} Yggdrasil. File:Yggdrasil (Mythic).png|{{R|6}} Yggdrasil. File:Yggdrasil (Ultra).png|{{R|7}} Yggdrasil. File:Yggdrasil (Super).png|{{R|8}} Yggdrasil. File:Yggdrasil (Unique).png|{{R|9}} Yggdrasil. File:Yggdrasil.png|Yggdrasil. File:Yggrework.jpg|Yggdrasil after the rework. File:cygg.png|[[M28]] Event where players got {{Rarity|1}} Yggdrasil. ==Trivia== * This petal now is useless in [[Hel]] due to squads no longer being available there. This also applied to players of different teams in [[Factory]] as you can't squad with them. * The referred names for the Yggdrasil are "ygg", which is an abbreviated version of Yggdrasil, and "why gee gee" or "Eeeg", which is the pronunciation of the abbreviated term. * Yggdrasil is one of the only two petals to start at {{Rarity|Legendary}} rarity, the other being [[Monstera]]. * {{Rarity|Common}} Yggdrasil was given Lasercat offering to wipe his inventory in exchange for it to [[M28]]. Unfortunately, M28 later took it away to have people see "someone really mad for losing a common petal", as he said. It was also given to Skibidi by M28 in exchange for locking him in [[Hel]] for a year. * Yggdrasil used to be the rarest petal in the game when rarity is not taken into account, but has since been dethroned by [[Square]], [[Moon Rock]], [[Golden Leaf]], [[Mimic]] and [[Mjölnir]]. ** {{Rarity|Unique}} Yggdrasil is among the rarest, if not the rarest, of all individual petals. {{Rarity|Unique}} Uranium, Cutter ([[Digger]] drops), Basic, Third Eye, Dark Mark, Moon Rock, Nazar Amulet, Golden Leaf, Mimic, Mjölnir and Square are mostly considered to be even rarer. * [[Dark Mark]] acts like an Yggdrasil in [[Hel]], reviving you and teleporting you to a random location on the map. * Yggdrasil used to have a 30s cooldown for reviving players, but was later reverted. Now, it only applies to players who died with the Undead effect. * Yggdrasil used to be easier to get from {{R|Legendary}} Shiny Ladybugs since they had a 15.3% chance to drop. However, the drop rate is now 9.8%. ** Players hunting the petal's best bet is {{R|Mythic}} or {{R|Ultra}} Shiny Ladybugs, which are practically guaranteed to drop the petal. ** Before Yggdrasil got reworked where it started to act like a mark, there was a 30 second cooldown for another revival. * Yggdrasils could be placed down before January 2023. Players could be teleported and revived to the said location of Yggdrasil. After this, the Yggdrasil could no longer be placed to revive squad members and instead give the Yggdrasil to the dead player. * According to a user (namely Stonesausagewebhorn), the {{R|Super}} Yggdrasil used to sell a total of 200m stars, $50,000 in real money, and due to the research, this used to appear to be the 5th most expensive purchasable item in all of video game history. As of now, the {{R|Super}} Yggdrasil now only costs 75m stars, making it a 37.5% price drop. * Yggdrasil used to have 20 seconds base reload, divided 2x for each reached rarity and time to revive a dead flower was 0.5 second. Also, it used to have health set upon revived higher than now (30%, 50%, 80%, 100% from {{R|Legendary}} to {{R|Super}}). * Only 3 people have crafted {{R|Super}} Yggdrasil, namely GODKING, _vOwOv_ and CakeyEgg. However, GODKING was banned, so only _vOwOv_ and CakeyEgg currently have a {{R|Super}} Yggdrasil. * Yggdrasil got a nerf which makes it unable to revive dead flowers for a limited time. {{Petals}} [[zh:世界树]] [[Category:Petals]] [[Category:Leaf]] [[Category:Legendary+]] [[Category:Droppable Petals]] [[Category:Stackable Petals]] [[Category:Healing Petals]] [[Category:Health-Based]] [[Category:Desert]] [[Category:Ladybugs]] [[Category:Rare Petals]] [[Category:Plant-based petals]]

'''Yucca''' is a petal in florr.io. It is similar to the [[Leaf]] petal except that it only heals the user when the user is in the defensive position, and heals quicker but the damage is lower than leaf.

==Appearance==
Yucca looks like a thin, yellowish green leaf. It has a dark green outline and a dark green stripe through the middle. Unlike the Leaf petal, it has two pointed ends, and no stem. Common Yuccas cannot be dropped (though they have coded stats) and Super Yuccas have to be crafted.

Heals the user at a constant rate, but only while they are in the defensive position. Stacks with other healing petals.

==Obtaining==
===[[Soldier Fire Ant]]===
{{:Soldier Fire Ant/Drop Yucca}}

===[[Worker Fire Ant]]===
{{:Worker Fire Ant/Drop Yucca}}

===[[Baby Fire Ant]]===
{{:Baby Fire Ant/Drop Yucca}}

===Purchasing===
On certain days, the petal can be purchased in the [[Shop]].

''Note: This table shows the amount of Stars that can be spent. Also, the stars can be awarded through challenges or purchases. For more information, go to this page: [[Stars]]''
{{Petal Price|1}}

==Strategy==
* The Yucca petal itself is rather weak. The Yucca only heals the user when they are in defensive position, so Yuccas are best used alongside petals that work fine when in the defensive position. For example, [[Pollen]] and [[Beetle Egg]]s function well in the defensive position.
* If using a build that requires attacking position (such as DPS builds, peashooter builds), Yucca will not be useful in it. As such, players should use [[Rose]]s, [[Dahlia]]s, [[Leaf|Leaves]], [[Starfish]] or [[Fangs]] instead of Yuccas. The main advantage of Yuccas compared to Roses is that they provide better defense against mobs. Because of this, a full Yucca build is superior to a full Rose build, since a full Rose build has virtually no defense.
** A strategy is for players to keep a full Yucca build in their secondary slots and put it on when at low HP. It will heal nearly as fast as a full rose build, but players will be more able to defend themselves. Another strategy is to use a build with all Yuccas except for one Salt. This build can quickly regenerate, has decent defense, and can kill ranged mobs through reflected damage.

===[[Garden]]===
* The Yucca-[[Salt]] build is good at surviving deeper in the Garden maze. With enough Yuccas, players can outheal even fairly large swarms of [[Hornet]]s, especially if the player is skilled at dodging Hornet missiles. The Salt deals enough damage back to Hornets to eventually kill them.  
** One player in a squad can use a Yucca-Salt build and attract the fire of Hornets, while another can use a high DPS build to easily destroy the distracted Hornets.
* The Yucca-[[Lightning]] build is also very good against Hornets, since when a Lightning petal is hit by a Hornet [[Missile]], it can damage several Hornets at once. Yuccas are useful in this build because players can damage Hornets even while in the defensive position.

===[[Desert]]===
* Yucca doesn't have major strengths in Desert. Ironically, Desert and Ant Hell are the only places where Yuccas can be obtained.
* [[Scorpion]]s and [[Beetle]]s may have a hard time with the Yucca-Salt build. [[Desert Centipede]]s and [[Fire Ant]]s will have a hard time with the Yucca-Lightning Build.
* [[Scorpion]]s pincer are poisonous.If you get poison,right click and hold quickly.It may heal very fast.To prevent another poison,you have to do it.If the situation can't do it.Dodge all as possible.

===[[Ocean]]===
* If a player is using petals that work well in the defensive position, Yuccas could be used instead of Roses when dealing with [[Jellyfish]]es. Roses can be destroyed by Jellyfish lightning, which would prevent their healing, but Yuccas will heal regardless.
** The Yucca-Salt build could be used to kill Jellyfish as long as the user avoids a large Jellyfish swarm.

===[[PvP|PvP(Outdated)]]===
====Using ====
* Yuccas can be used as a defensive petal and a healing petal.
* Keep an all Yucca or Yucca-Salt build in your secondary slot.

====Against====  
* Take advantage of the fact that your opponent will need to defend in order to heal. Try to stay at attacking range and force your opponent to choose between attacking and healing.
* The Dandelion can prevent the healing effect of the Yucca.

==Trivia== 
* Yucca's healing are now the same as [[Starfish]].
* The Yucca was unexpandable after the Termite update, but was reverted in an unknown date.
* Yucca is very similar to the Leaf, in both appearance and function.
** Yucca essentially plays as a Fire Ant variation of Leaf.
** Yucca is stronger than leaf, but requires the user to be in defensive position to use.
* The Yucca is one of the only petals to activate its ability in a specific condition, like [[Claw]].
